name: Gabby Hancher
clientText: I work best with: *Collaborative individuals *Folks who are curious about mindfulness *Folks who are creative and/or highly sensitive *Folks who identify as care-givers *Activists looking to process and integrate their experiences *Adult parentified children *Perfectionists *Queer folks who want to explore their identity more deeply *Neurodivergent individuals *Folks who feel they've gotten what they can from talk therapy and need something else *Folks who want to feel more embodied
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In a typical session, we will slow down and listen to the wisdom of your body. Together, we can discover what it is that you need to process in order to experience more integration in your life. We may work to support your own innate resilience by developing a felt sense of safety based on your own true sense of that or use the therapeutic relationship as a container to explore attachment wounding patterns through our present moment relationship. All of these experiences can offer fundamental shifts in your overall mood, the way you show up in the world, or even in your relationships. Whether you are experiencing difficulty with grief, relationships, anxiety or any number of concerns, together we can help you orient towards your inner knowing and personal agency.
My Values as a Therapist
I approach all of my work through a social justice lens and believe deeply that when we transform on an interpersonal level, social change ripples outwards. Accessibility, embodied experience, and trauma-informed care are important pillars of my practice. Persons of all races & ethnicities, genders, sexual orientations, and abilities are welcome. I am also affirming of kink, non-traditional relationship structures such as polyamory and non-monogamy, and sex work. Some of my identities hold varying levels of privilege which I always invite conversation around in session.

name: Christa Cummins
clientText: Are you unsatisfied with your relationships or work? Are you looking for support with chosen or unchosen transitions? Are you having trouble making changes in your life? I help people explore their needs and values, and to feel empowered to set healthy boundaries with significant others, family members, managers and colleagues. I love helping others clarify their values and find direction, and I'm here to support you at a pace that feels right for you.
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I took a nontraditional path to become a therapist; after over 20 years in corporate positions, I found myself more passionate about helping my colleagues work through challenges in their personal and work lives. I’d volunteered for trauma- and grief-focused agencies for years, so it felt natural to get an MA in Mental Health Counseling from Lewis & Clark's graduate school. My focus draws upon 3 approaches: Relational-cultural constructivism, which suggests that our challenges are influenced by our sociocultural environments, and that recognizing our authentic selves is important to create change; Attachment Theory, in which our experiences, relationships, and interpretations from the past might be reflected in how we relate to the world now; and Cognitive Behavioral Theory, which emphasizes using these understandings of our constructions and ourselves to practice new ways of thinking, feeling, and behaving.
More About the People I Treat
I help people get in touch with their authentic needs and values, and feel empowered to set healthy boundaries in relationships with significant others, family, and in the workplace. We may take a look at ways of coping that no longer serve you, and identify changes you want to try in your life. I’m here to help you practice change in a place where you feel heard and supported, and your intuition is valued without judgment. I especially love the opportunity to support people at transition points in their lives: career and workplace difficulties, life changes, and the anxiety, depression and 'stuckness' those can bring about. I am queer-affirming and welcome a diversity of clients.

name: Jed Rendleman
clientText: I help my clients transcend cycles they feel stuck in. Together we find ways to reconnect to joy, connection, and spontaneity. My ideal client values building stronger relationships - with themselves, with the people that matter most to them, and with me.
approaches:
My Background and Approach
The profound change I experienced through therapy was the core motivation for me to become a therapist myself. My approach is grounded in the belief that we all deserve happiness, purpose, and connectedness. Through our work together, we’ll discover new insights, experiment with novel ways of being, and say or hear things that have gone unsaid or unheard. Let’s build the life you want - one full of connection, groundedness, and love. I hold a Master's in Marriage and Family Therapy from Lewis & Clark . I have specific training in helping couples create more equitable relationships and satisfying intimacy, addressing the unique needs of men and boys, and acting as a facilitator and witness to help clients find deeper purpose and meaning in their lives. I have extensive training in emotionally focused therapy (EFT), Internal Family Systems (IFS), Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR), mindfulness, anti-racism, and trauma-informed care.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
Throughout our lives, we learn behaviors to cope and feel safe. Perhaps a part of you became a perfectionist to protect a part that fears rejection. Over time, patterns like this can become entrenched, inhibiting our growth and even causing the very pain they were meant to prevent. I can help you identify these patterns and the parts of yourself they protect. Through curiosity and compassion, we can loosen their grip and even befriend them. Emotions signal what’s important for us to notice. Acknowledging your own emotions and the emotions of others promotes healing and connection. Easier said than done! I use techniques that help individuals and couples slow down, sit with feelings, and gain language to better understand/communicate them. I believe that it takes practice to really create lasting change. I will often assign simple experiments to further the growth you experience during sessions and provide us more data to work with in future sessions.

name: Elizabeth Knutsen
clientText: I work with individuals who are interested in building a deeper level of understanding of limiting beliefs held about themselves and the world that keep them from living the fully expansive lives they deserve. My clients are sex workers, those with eating disorders/disordered eating, non-monogamous, kinky and queer populations and those who are involved in community organizing. Please note I only provide telehealth sessions; I no longer live in OR. Please inquire about sliding scale rates.
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Mind and body connection is one of the main portals to gaining a deeper understanding of ourselves. I utilize Somatic Experiencing and Mindfulness to help support my clients build safety and connection to their bodies to support their healing and growth process. I am deeply committed to obtaining consent and feedback from clients to ensure they are getting their needs met in our time together. It takes courage to show up as one's authentic self and to be vulnerable. I will validate the hell out of you and also provide reality checks when needed. Your feelings are real and they need space AND we can explore new ways of being in the world. Have you ever had feelings of being too much? I promise you, you're not.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
We'll be a good fit for one another if you appreciate direct feedback that has a softness to it. If you are ready to consistently show up and feel. I actively strive to dismantle beliefs that have been socialized in me that are harmful to others and myself. This is an ongoing task as, globally, we continue under late stage capitalism. I will often "punch up" at systems of oppression that hurt us all. We all live in a world that doesn't always support expansion and safety. Explore building safety in ourselves and our world and look at some of the ways your beliefs and actions may not be aligning with your values so we can come back home to those values. I take identity into consideration in my work with clients and will support you in separating your identities from the identity politics; a tool of the ruling class used to keep us separated.

name: Brandon Bressi, MA, LPC
clientText: The clients I work with are individuals who come from varying cultural backgrounds, with unique life experiences and personal stories. They are determined and dedicated to exploring themselves openly and authentically while seeking to engage in a collaborative, therapeutic journey towards deeper self-awareness, greater self-confidence, and personal healing.
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is eclectic, person-centered, and tailored to your needs. I believe individuals are motivated to discover solutions to the questions, struggles, uncertainties, and lack of meaning they are presently experiencing in their life. I am responsible for helping you identify, develop, and apply these solutions to your life in order to resolve current issues and challenges you are facing. I provide services to individuals who are struggling with an array of emotional health concerns including but not limited to anxiety, stress, grief, loss, trauma, and depression. I will work with you to find healthier solutions to enhance your wellbeing and live a more authentic life by creating a safe environment so you feel comfortable and confident with exploring yourself. Practicing this type of approach helps open the channels for us to create an authentic space where you can fully explore yourself as a whole person, which assists in facilitating growth and healing.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
My career journey has allowed me to compile over two decades of experience working with diverse individuals and communities across the lifespan throughout the social services, behavioral health, mental health, and healthcare fields spanning the east and west coasts. These experiences allowed me to gain invaluable skills, perspectives, and training which prepared me to become a therapist. I deeply enjoy collaborating with clients and helping them live lives with more awareness, intention, and meaning. It’s an honor to witness healing and liberation occur during our therapeutic journey together.

name: caroline sabi
clientText: I am a big fan of psycho-spiritual growth work. I think we are as happy or unhappy as the quality of our relationships. Clients who feel lost on their path and wondering how they can find meaning and purpose in their lives are clients I deeply enjoy working with. Anybody who sticks to our work long enough will experience a deeper sense of homecoming in their own bodies and a deeper connection to the values that bring peace and contentment in their lives.
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My philosophy as a therapist is primarily focused on core transformation rather than merely symptom management, and needless to say, I strongly believe in the centrality of the therapeutic relationship as a significant healing agent. Moving towards our wholeness necessitates the courage to first move into the wounded parts that we all have as members of the human species. I call the psychotherapy that I do “healing into wholeness” because I view our wounds as the stepping stones taking us back to our wholeness through a process of integration. Therefore, there is sacredness to our wounded parts.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am an EMDR specialist. This is a very potent and experiential therapeutic technique designed to resolve various traumas. I am also an Emotionally Focused trained couple counselor, EFT.

name: Fionn Fortune
clientText: Although I enjoy working with people of all backgrounds, I am particularly passionate about working with children, families, teens, and emerging adulthood clients. I have a strong background working with LGBTQ+ children and teens and supporting folx with other marginalized identities. I strive to be relational, collaborative, and client-driven in my work.
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a therapist, I strive to create a space where clients of all backgrounds and identities feel welcome and safe. I truly believe that my clients are the experts on themselves and their experiences. I prefer to follow the lead of my clients and work at their pace as we navigate the path of healing and growth together. You are in charge! If we use a car metaphor, I will always let you be the main driver, while I’m in your passenger seat. I have great experience as a co-pilot and love that job! My approach emphasizes the importance of building a relationship and connecting as humans first. Some of our time together may look like taking steps towards meeting goals, while some sessions may focus on navigating the challenges that arise in everyday life and celebrating the small victories. I love finding new ways to deepen our relationship and connect to support this work. This can include learning about your pets or favorite people. You’ll likely hear about my quirky dog at some point!
My Values as a Therapist
I practice from a humanistic approach and incorporate interventions from ACT and CBT to help clients meet their goals. I often incorporate mindfulness and other evidence-based intervention as necessary—again, you’re the driver. In my practice as a therapist, I enjoy harnessing clients’ personal values to help them work towards living their most authentic lives. This can look like helping you assess what your needs are and work towards choice points that support your long-term goals, or looking at past experiences to better understand where you want to go. I value collaboration with your important people to support our work.

name: Aaron Buchholz
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
People usually come to therapy because life isn’t working, they feel stuck, or because they want something to shift. Having a non-judgmental, supportive space to think through what feels wrong can be helpful. We are able to make new meaning when someone listens, helps us focus in on what is important, understands our challenges, and grasps who we really are. We begin to understand our own stories and become able to shed new light on older, outdated narratives. We untangle from the repetitive pain we impose on our selves, family, partners, friends, and begin to take charge of our lives. I help people to understand and change long standing behaviors, recover hope and self-confidence, become more self reflective, work through difficult feelings, come to terms with past losses and traumas, find new independence, and improve relationships with their selves and others.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I work with each of my patients as individually as possible because our culture, race, personal history and unique attributes shape who we are. Every person is different. A successful therapy works best when designed to fit you and your needs. Prior to starting my private practice I worked with a wide diversity of people in a variety of mental health settings including high schools, a suicide hotline, a mobile crisis team, adolescent foster care, a prison facility, and in medical clinics. I also have experience in residential construction/home restoration, as a musician, and a lifelong athlete. These different experiences all inform my approach as a therapist.

name: Megan Langworthy
clientText: You may not even believe anymore that full recovery is possible, struggle with motivation, and your moods may be compounded by insomnia, weight gain, gut health issues or chronic pain, AGAIN. You are not alone. If you have hit a wall with lifestyle hacks, and have made the decision to get well again but don't know how, I may be a good fit for you. Learn , hormone replacement, supplements and medication in combination with Ketamine to not only make changes with ease, but crave the life you have
approaches:
How I Can Help You
A functional approach to psychiatry takes into account the whole system including hormones, inflammation, sleep, exercise, and nutrition. Knowing the source of your discomfort is essential to providing the right care in the right order. All the ketamine in the world is not going help if you have low testosterone, for example. For those of you with gut health issues, side effects of many medications may be intolerable so we'll start there. Add to that all the time you spend going in circles and it's hard to trust that anyone will be able to help. Not only that, western medicine lab values may have you testing normal, delaying essential care. Using the latest science, I will help you get to the root cause of your symptoms, to increase energy, and to feel like yourself, maybe for the first time.
My Approach to Helping
I do not believe in treatment resistant symptoms but instead, that symptoms are often not being treated at the source. If you are active, eat fairly well, and generally have a healthy relationship with alcohol and other substances, but are not performing at your peak, I can help.

name: Marny Moore
clientText: I work with individuals, couples, and families. Ages I treat are 12 years old to adults. My specialties are: Mental Health, Substance Use, Trauma, ADHD, Anger, Anxiety, Attachment Issues, Behavioral Issues, Grief, Co-dependency, Depression, Divorce, Family Conflict, Infidelity, LGBTQIA2S+, Marital Issues, Parenting, School Stress, Self Esteem, Self Harm, and Work Stress.
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We are all born with the capacity to heal and reach our fullest potential and I am grateful to be allowed to step into your world with compassion and empathy to assist you in discovering and working through the concerns that brought you to reach out. I am an eclectic practitioner who uses elements of Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT), Motivational Interviewing (MI), Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT), Acceptance and 0Commitment Therapy (ACT), Attachment Theory, Humanistic Therapy (HT), and Internal Family Systems (IFS) in order to find what works best for my clients.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
My helping philosophy lies in meeting each person where they are at and in focusing on what resonates most in your healing journey. I offer a safe space for each person to explore their thoughts and feelings without fear of judgment or a negative outcome. I believe by building a collaborative and empathetic alliance, we can work together to overcome obstacles and promote personal growth and autonomy.

name: Corey Griggs
clientText: I am passionate about working with couples and families who are struggling. Coming from a military background I look forward to serving the veteran community. Having lived through the culture shock of returning home I hope to help individuals reassimilate as well as help improve communication lines between veterans and their families. I hope to work with teens, individuals, couples, and families from diverse backgrounds. I am excited to help, learn, and grow as a person and as a counselor.
approaches:
My Background
I was raised in a military family, who moved often, all over the world. This time in my life cemented the need for strong relationships within the family system. Later in life, I enlisted in the Marine Corps and returned as a combat veteran. I understand how difficult it can be to reintegrate and how hard it can be to ask for help. I believe that there is always a chance to grow stronger more meaningful relationships with one another. I am excited to use my knowledge, military background, and empathy to connect, work and pilot services toward providing care with teens, adults, couples, and groups. I hope to promote healthy relationships and help others with their struggles involving PTSD, depression, anxiety, trauma, ADHD and other issues while building a positive sense of self. I am passionate and empathetic, positive yet realistic.
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a therapist to help others. Which may sound a bit obvious, but it’s true. Not long after I graduated from high school a close friend talked to me about his troubles since returning home from the service. I disliked the fact that I could not help more than simply listening, I decided I needed to do something to help others struggling with mental health. After my enlistment I returned home and started my bachelor’s degree and finally leading me to where I am now, working on my master’s degree. It has been a long and incredibly rewarding journey; I look forward to listening and helping others any way I can.

name: Veronica Bangs
clientText: I work primarily with clients affected by trauma, anxiety and depression. Some of my “niche” populations have included the transition age group (I consider this to be 18-30 years old), creatives of all varieties, the neurodivergent community, and anyone interested in exploring their connection with their body through the lenses of Polyvagal Theory and Somatic Experiencing.
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach involves exploring body-based cues and how to use that information to cultivate more emotional wellness and values-alignment in our day-to-day lives. Throughout this process, I use Somatic Experiencing, Polyvagal Theory and Attachment Theory alongside questions formed to promote self-exploration and a widening of perspective. Though I offer coping techniques and psychoeducation, I believe that you are the best authority on your life, and will defer to you as the driving influence during our sessions. My lens is always nervous-system based, trauma-informed, and feminist.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
At heart, I am a creative and a feminist. I'm deeply passionate about transformative justice. My work in counseling is fueled by my desire to contribute to community practices that are rooted in dignity and care for all people. I believe that all of us are intuitive beings with lifelong capacity for change. Outside of my work, I spend most of my time playing the piano, soaking in time outdoors, and reading fantasy novels.

name: Katelin Harrington
clientText: It is a deep honor to have the opportunity to walk beside my clients as they seek healing and wholeness for their heart and soul. Therapy can feel scary and hard and my ideal client is one who is courageous enough to step into this unknown space in order to have a life marked by healing. If you are walking through grief, loss, a traumatic event, a painful relationship, struggle with anxiety, depression, or feel stuck, I would love to offer a safe and connective space to help facilitate healing.
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer a collaborative, person-centered approach while using attachment-based therapy, polyvagal theory and Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT) when working with my clients. My client is the expert in the room and has everything within them to create a life of healing and wholeness amidst the feelings of defeat, frustration, overwhelm or confusion. My role is to come beside my clients and help create a safe environment for healing to occur and empower them to believe in the strength within themselves. Through various tools and strategies, self-awareness and attachment, a new life can be forged and your heart can come back to life from its dormant, painful, or empty state to one full of life, vigor and transformation.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe people are wired for connection and therefore we are most often hurt within the context of relationship or lack thereof. This also means we can find healing through positive, safe and empathetic connection. During the hardest seasons of life, when more support is needed to navigate the trenches, the times that take us off guard and we feel unequipped to handle life, people need safe connection, empathy, tenderness and care. These are the sacred spaces that I have the privilege and honor to step into and be a support when it feels like you are walking alone. I have a deep and unique passion for supporting mommas, couples or families who've lost a baby or child. It is one of the most painful and devastating experiences you can walk through as a parent. As a mother who has had to say goodbye to my own baby boy, and as a therapist, I bring a unique perspective. It would be an honor to support you and be a safe space to process the grief and trauma you have had to live through.

name: Abbie Nederhoed
clientText: Folks I often work with: non-monogamous relationships whether you are exploring opening a relationship or have been practicing non-monogamy for years, Kink and BDSM, relationship conflict and attachment wounding, painful sex, desire discrepancy, sexual trauma, gender and sexual identity. I enjoy working with complex trauma and have a background in mental health crisis work, I approach this work with understanding that the systems we navigate can at times be helpful, but can also cause harm.
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Harm reduction is my unifying foundational perspective. I utilize narrative and humanistic frameworks and strive to be authentic and compassion driven, approaching each situation from an anti-oppressive, sex-positive, and intersectional perspective. Approaches I like to currently work with are: attachment, internal family systems, emotionally focused therapy. Shame and judgment are often inherent in the ways we cope, and change can be a difficult process. Mitigating any potential harm in how we cope, while continuing to build on existing skills is often an entry point into the work we can do together. One of my roles is to hold space for you to explore your most authentic self. I will also hold space for the ways you wish to navigate relationships and communities, while honoring that you may have to mask your identities to keep yourself safe within these systems.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As an agender, and queer individual who identifies within the kink and non-monogamous communities, lived experience within these identities helps me to understand that we do not exist on a binary. I am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker and I have training in sex therapy. Originally a Midwesterner, I moved from Michigan to Oregon in 2015. I have three tuxedo cats and more recently have adopted a 16 y.o grumpy grey boy (cat), I love coffee and cute spooky things.

name: Ashleigh Zoerb
clientText: Do you find yourself constantly overwhelmed, anxious, depressed, and/or numb? Do you feel stuck and unable to create the changes you desire in your life? Maybe you feel disconnected from your relationships and completely alone. I know how scary and hopeless this can feel; I am here to help you navigate these feelings and find another way, a way which gives you greater peace in your life.
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work from an attachment and somatic based perspective meaning I look at the relational experiences you've had over your life and how these have translated into survival strategies which your body continues to hold onto today. Together we will untangle these, learn to tune into the wisdom of your body, and begin to learn new ways of living which allow you to thrive instead of simply survive. I will help you to re-gain a sense of safety and security within yourself and in your relationships giving you the ability to be more resilient.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Life is difficult to say the least, and often gives us experiences which leave us hurting. I believe we are all doing our very best in any given moment and that change is possible even when it seems impossible. I believe every person matters and every aspect of what makes each person who they are, matters. Having safe, healthy, and supportive relationships in our lives, makes navigating the greatest challenges, more bearable. And I believe dogs to be some of the greatest healers here on Earth.

name: Alex MacLeod
clientText: When we adapt to an oppressive environment, we are often left feeling scattered, anxious, depressed, numb, disconnected, and misaligned. What’s really tricky is that we’re often not even aware of how we ended up there, we just know that it hurts. Maybe you've been hurting for a while without anyone in your life really aware of how hard it is for you, or maybe something's shifted recently and you're in need of new tools.
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In therapy, you can learn how to widen your awareness of your experience and to encounter and even befriend the parts of yourself that cause tension and conflict in your everyday life. In our work together, I will invite you to notice aspects of your experience that may have fallen from your conscious awareness. This can be especially helpful if you're someone who struggles to know what you're feeling, or finds it difficult navigating tense conversations with people you love. We will also talk about concrete tools to help you get unstuck from unwanted behavioral patterns, set and stick to compassionate boundaries, and ask for what you need. I incorporate Somatic Experiencing in my approach to helping, which is a trauma treatment modality that focuses on helping you feel safe and grounded before renegotiating the painful and wounding things you've experienced in this life. If you're someone who is turned off by traditional talk therapy models, this may be a welcome change for you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that we are living in a distinctive and difficult time. I also believe that at its best, therapy can be a revolutionary act. Where prevailing societal currents push us away from each other and ourselves, the therapeutic relationship can be a place to drop anchor, resist, and flourish in the way that is your birthright.

name: Julie Sliga
clientText: You might be overwhelmed by anxiety, worn down by chronic pain or illness, or simply exhausted from holding too much for too long. You’ve learned how to push through—but maybe you’re ready for something else. Therapy can be a place to exhale. Together, we’ll make room for what’s difficult, reconnect with what’s possible, and move at a pace that honors your nervous system.
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I believe healing happens when we feel safe enough to explore what hurts—and supported enough to imagine something different. My work is rooted in trauma-informed, relational care and grounded in mind-body approaches that support the nervous system, challenge internalized beliefs, and honor lived experience. I work with people navigating chronic pain, illness, anxiety, and the complex grief that often comes with these experiences. I understand that access to care is shaped by larger systems of oppression, and I strive to practice in ways that are both ethical and sustainable. I hold a number of sliding scale spots and offer referrals when I’m not the right fit. Therapy with me is collaborative, curious, and compassionate. I welcome all the parts of you that are ready to heal—tender, protective, uncertain, and wise.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe healing happens when we feel safe enough to explore what hurts—and supported enough to imagine something different. My work is rooted in trauma-informed, relational care and grounded in mind-body approaches that support the nervous system, challenge internalized beliefs, and honor lived experience. I specialize in working with people navigating chronic pain, illness, anxiety, and the complex grief that comes with these experiences. I understand that access to care is shaped by larger systems of oppression, and I strive to practice in ways that are both ethical and sustainable. I hold a small number of sliding scale spots and offer referrals when I’m not the right fit. Therapy with me is collaborative, curious, and compassionate, and I welcome all the parts of you that are ready to heal.

name: DeLyn Griffin
clientText: I welcome individuals, couples, and families dealing with relationship conflicts, parenting, behavioral challenges, depression, anxiety, developing sexual health, life stress & balance, or personal identity. I was a teacher for many years and welcome children ages 3 and up for play therapy to help them communicate their inner experiences as they learn new skills and practice new ways of coping. I recognize the impact of trauma (big and small) and provide EMDR as a treatment option for all ages.
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
The most important aspect of counseling is for you to experience a therapeutic environment that is warm, accepting, empathetic, encouraging, and patient. In that environment, you are empowered to know your inherent value and worth, and find ways to access the inner wisdom to guide you toward your goals. I believe people are relational beings with physical, emotional, social, and spiritual needs. Increasing awareness of and integrating these needs can lead to a richer, more meaningful life. While I believe psychological insight is important, it is also essential to be action oriented. I help clients acquire new tools and experiment with new behaviors and ways of thinking to create the desired changes in their lives and relationships. I have additional training to work with children & teens (play therapy, sandtray, & expressive arts) couples (Gottman, Emotionally Focused, & RelateStrong) & trauma (EMDR with infants, children, adolescents & adults).
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My favorite things are spending time with my husband, our four children and their partners, and snuggling our 2 grandkiddos. In my free time I love all sorts of books, quilting, knitting, and gardening.

name: Chynna Springer
clientText: Do you want to improve your quality of life? Are symptoms of stress, anxiety or depression getting in your way? Do you feel trapped in an unhealthy or abusive relationship? Are you overwhelmed, burnt out, or juggling multiple roles? Counseling can support you through these challenges!
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I will create a safe therapeutic environment where you can be heard, feel understood, and work towards positive growth. You will learn practical tools to reach your goals based on your individual needs. I approach counseling from a client-driven and strengths-based perspective. I infuse and encourage the use of humor in therapy and believe it can be a beneficial part of the process and positive coping mechanism. ALLIED COMMUNITIES: Body Positivity, LGBTQIA+ Allied, Open Relationships Non-Monogamy Allied, Racial Justice Allied, Sex Worker Allied, Women's Rights Allied
More About the People I Treat
I'm skilled in treating the different varieties of anxiety and I really enjoy being a part of people's healing process. I'm experienced in working with people struggling with multiple symptoms since conditions such as ADHD/ADD, trauma, grief, panic, depression etc. can often accompany other issues. I provide guidance through life transitions, balancing multiple roles, and life stressors. I'm also passionate about empowering survivors of abusive relationships. I'm a Licensed Professional Counselor in Oregon, a Licensed Mental Health Counselor in Washington, and a Certified Alcohol and Drug Counselor in Oregon. With 16 years of experience, I've worked in crisis centers, outreach programs, residential treatment facilities, domestic abuse programs, etc.

name: Sarah Fawcett
clientText: Sometimes, we just need a little help to touch ground long enough to hear the sound of our own voice, gain insight, and remember what it is that makes us feel truly alive. Therapy can be that grounding help, offering a mirror to see ourselves from a new angle, uncovering unconscious patterns, and seeing all of our parts from a new and accepting perspective. Therapy can uncover the “why” behind our actions or choices that don’t seem to be working for us anymore and help us change.
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach, drawing from depth psychology and attachment theory, centers around first creating a safe and compassionate space for clients to consider their inner world and uncover unconscious patterns and beliefs. I believe therapy (at its best) is a safe place to repair how we interact with the outer world, i.e., relationship patterns and intimacy issues, as well as a safe place to uncover and repair our relationship with our inner world, i.e., negative self-image, addiction, etc. Therapy is your time; I am here to help guide and reflect and offer you about 50 minutes to touch ground, feel heard, and perhaps, spin a little less. My work with children and adolescents is similar in approach, with the addition of art and play-based therapy, family systems, and developmental theories. As a designer and artist, I love to work with other creatives and incorporate creativity and art-making into sessions with kids as well as adults.
Why I Became a Therapist
I have always been drawn to understanding what makes people tick. Psychological or otherwise, symptoms are cues for where to look but aren’t the true problem to be solved. Our symptoms (anxiety, depression, etc.) are the outward manifestations of our inner wounds and where the “work” begins. I love helping people uncover their own personal blocks to their path toward happiness and help them feel like the truest version of themselves.

name: David Hanna (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
When seeking a therapist you want someone who'll be able to recognize who you are and what you're needing. No one wants to waste their time or have the setback of meeting with someone and leaving the experience more discouraged than before you came in. You're looking for someone to see you clearly and support you with whatever you're struggling with, wherever you're stuck, however much (or little) pain or confusion you're in - whether you're single, married, struggling as a step-parent, dealing with the loss of someone close, feeling lonely or dissatisfied. If you've ever felt like you're "too much" for those around you or if you're depressed, anxious and not getting what you want out of your relationships, work, or dating life, then counseling may be for you. I understand these challenges and together we can help you find a better way forward. We all need to be seen and no one's been seen enough. Let's build a therapeutic relationship that encourages your growth & health.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I’ve been working as a therapist since 2006. I received my master’s degree in applied behavioral science with a focus on systems counseling from The Leadership Institute of Seattle at Bastyr University. I hold a bachelor’s degree in electronic communication arts from San Francisco State University. I'm a Licensed Mental Health Counselor in WA and a Licensed Professional Counselor in OR. I come from a blended family, with full, half and stepsiblings. I have lost a parent and come through a challenging divorce stronger and more able to connect to others with similar challenges. I am empathic to the complex emotions and binds of being a sensitive person in relationships with friends, colleagues and family members who don’t always value those traits. As Hemingway said, "The world breaks everyone, and afterward, some are strong at the broken places.”

name: Cory Anton
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use humor, compassion, and a soft but direct approach to helping my clients find freedom and heal from anxiety disorders, depression, and life transitions. I use effective treatment methods (CBT, ERP) that help you get back to living your life faster and spend less time on my couch! I understand how scary and overwhelming living with anxiety can be. Often, the things that we are worried about are things that have happened before (like a panic attack or social judgement) and are strong in our memories. Challenging these fears can seem daunting, to say the least, and it can feel like a losing battle. Together, we will raise your hopefulness and get you back to living life in control.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have been working in mental health for over 10 years, starting out in psychiatric residential treatment and inpatient programs. I received my Masters in Professional Mental Health Counseling from Lewis and Clark and am licensed in both Oregon and Washington. I love working with adolescents and families as well as emerging adults. As a therapist, I have worked in residential treatment, the school setting, outpatient and private practice. I have expertise in treating OCD and anxiety disorders and have trained with the BTTI (Behavioral Therapy Training Institute) through the IOCDF.

name: Brooke Kaufman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is somatic, based on the cultivation of mindful awareness by using the body and breath as tools to come into the present moment. These practices can assist you in becoming more aware, centered, grounded and better equipped to face the past, to engage in the present and create a more fulfilling, integrated future. Inner child work, parts work, depth psychology, transpersonal psychology, and eco psychology are all frameworks that I use to support you in your healing. I offer attuned, consistent support to help you feel more resourced and resilient. I support and welcome people of all backgrounds and orientations. I look forward to supporting you on your path!

name: Caretia Silva (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I am a certified Somatic Experiencing Practioner (SEP). I have been trained in Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Internal Family Systems (IFS), Collaborative and Proactive Solutions (CPS), Psychodynamic Approach and Whole Brain Child, ARISE intervention model and Motivational Interviewing. I specialize in childhood development, anxiety, neurodiversity(such as ADHD and ASD), trauma focused and trauma informed care. I have advanced training in working with first responders and armed forces members and their families. I aim to practice with humility, inclusivity and welcome and encourage ongoing feedback.
My Values as a Therapist
I have been a therapist for over 20 years and, all these years later, I still enjoy being a therapist and supporting people in their journey. I have supported folks through exploring their self-identities, healing from trauma, working through anxiety, depression, grief, loss, life stages and building connections within their communities. I value transparency, humor, humility, integrity, honesty and mutual respect. I maintain a schedule that allows for flexible scheduling and regularly scheduled appointments with my clients. I believe in healing and ultimately, I believe people find their way through even the most difficult, dark times in their lives when they have support. We all need fresh perspective and guidance from time-to-time and therapy is a great way to show up and grow. I hope we get to meet and I am excited to go on this journey with you.

name: Midori Ferris Wayne (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I do therapy differently than most, because I trust that you already have all the inner resources to deal with stuck patterns in your life. I also won't minimize you. I know the struggle is real and is compounded by systemic oppression that isn’t going away soon enough for any of us. But you have so much more coping potential than you realize. Rather than approaching therapy as treatment for mental illness, I can help you see yourself as fundamentally healthy and whole. This isn't woo woo stuff, it's brain-science and evidence-based, and you'll see the difference. I've been trained in trauma-informed, body and sex positive/kink/polyam/neuroqueer approaches for individual and relational therapy with a specialization in intimacy/sex therapy. I've also worked with neurodiverse families for almost two decades. I integrate Internal Family Systems, narrative therapy, and interpersonal neurobiology with a focus on equity by balancing independence and compassion for self and others.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My identity as a person and as a therapist is based in a neuroqueer framework, which is really more of a verb than a noun. Let's neuroqueer this therapy process! Neuroqueering honors a diversity of minds as well as bodies and recognizes that rigid expectations around ability, gender, sexuality, etc. are simply age-old products of societal oppression. This world is not designed for anyone who doesn't fit neatly into societal norms for experiencing the world or expressing themselves, and it's harder than we think to unlearn inherited beliefs about what is "normal". I'm passionate about tracking the impacts of normative ideology directly in sessions and hold myself accountable to untangling these relational patterns for each of us. It's an exciting process which has also become a life practice for me, as I become more aware of vulnerabilities and show up in my relationships more courageously. We can collaborate to create safety for this transformative work in your life as well.

name: Jenny Larson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
You want to reduce the discomfort you feel, but it can feel really daunting and scary to reach out for therapy. Asking for help can put us in a vulnerable place, which can be challenging. I recognize this and work to build a therapeutic relationship that feels safe and trusting. I focus on meeting my clients where they are and consider their unique life experiences, skills, and needs when working with them. My approach is compassionate, collaborative, relational, pragmatic, and non-judgmental. I’ll support you while also encouraging you to do the work you’re capable of doing. If it feels like there’s a lot of work to do, you’ll never get to the end of it, and it feels overwhelming and scary to stir things up, we’ll go slowly and at a pace that feels comfortable for you. You deserve to feel better and you’re worth it.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe that everyone has the capacity to heal. When we encounter traumatic or otherwise challenging life situations, we often develop strategies to help us get through those times. Unfortunately, those strategies can impact us later in life. Maybe we've developed a critical inner voice, or we have a hard time connecting with others. Maybe we struggle to set boundaries that we need or we might not even be sure of what we need. We might find ourselves wanting to please everyone around us and feel that we have to be perfect and it's exhausting. Maybe we're showing up in a way that doesn't feel good and isn't how we want to show up, but we don't feel like we know any other way. I value my clients as the people they are and the experiences that shaped them, no matter what those experiences are. I believe that therapists have an obligation to keep learning and growing in order to best serve their clients. I value my training and experience and work every day to keep building on these.

name: Lucas Wenker MS LPC (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My primary approach to therapy is offering a compassionate, non-judgmental and strength based dynamic within our relationship. I meet my clients where they are at. I employ an integrative mix of person-centered, cognitive behavioral, and motivational interviewing counseling techniques. I help my clients with goal-setting, effective communication, self-care, self-exploration, and accountability. Creating positive and healthy relationships with clients is a powerful experience that I am forever grateful for.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I decided to become a counselor after noticing that all of my other choices of work were in the helping professions. I worked for residence life departments at Oregon State University and Southern Oregon university, and have over a decade of experience working at both day and residential youth summer camp programs. I have worked in the mental health field since 2009, when I began working with high school students in rural Oregon as well as Rogue Community College counseling center in Medford, OR for my graduate school internship. I became clinically licensed while working at a large counseling agency in the Portland area in 2014, then spent two years working as a substance use and abuse counselor in South Dakota. In 2016 I moved to the Oregon Coast and began a private practice, which I transitioned to Portland in 2019. In addition to private practice, I am currently the lead field therapist for a wilderness therapy program based on the Olympic Peninsula, where I work with at-risk youth

name: Karla Wolf, LPC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a Certified IFS therapist, I believe that our wholeness is not achieved by cutting off a portion of ourself but by inviting in ALL parts of ourself, which all want to play a positive role in who we are. To help you reach this goal of wholeness I work primarily from the Internal Family Systems (IFS) model (parts work). This model utilizes both the client’s & therapist’s inner capacities for curiosity, compassion & courage to support your journey. I use Non Violent Communication (NVC) & IFS (IFIO model) to assist you & your partner in developing the skills to problem solve well together. As Marshall Rosenburg (NVC) said, “Every criticism, judgment, blame & expression of anger is the tragic expression of an unmet need.” We will work together so that unmet needs can be expressed & met. I will also invite you to notice the ways you are already supported. I find that gratitude can be a wonderful antidote to resentments.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I am drawn to counseling because I love working with people. I think each person has their own abilities & wisdom to find their way, develop the tools they need to be an anchor for themselves & their loved ones. I bring a social justice perspective to my work & will support you in recognizing the ways oppression & related traumas have affected your sense of self & well-being. In my own life, I continue to learn about myself, & my vulnerabilities & strengths, through partnerships & parenting, & through embracing the demons in myself & finding healing. I love being with friends & family, being outdoors, finding solace in nature & playing with my 3 cats. My counseling style is compassionate, down to earth, casual, full of humor & warmth. I love working with people & I learn from you every day. “Compassion is not a relationship between the healer & the wounded. It’s a relationship between equals. Only when we know our own darkness can we be present with the darkness of others." P.Chodron

name: Sean Callahan (HE/THEY/SHE)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am passionate about helping people feel at home in themselves. I integrate Somatic IFS and Gestalt therapies to support others in staying radically present with their experience, going beyond the stories that keep us stuck in our heads and disconnected from ourselves and each other. The intention of this work is to deepen your relationship and trust in yourself, which leads to more authentic, meaningful, and vibrant relationships with others and the world around you. Showing up as your self can be terrifying, but I believe that a meaningful life requires being valued for who you truly are. My approach is: Relational, because we come to understand ourselves through our relationships with others. Somatic, because our bodies are capable of processing information and experiences that we can’t access through thinking. Spiritually engaging, because when we don't include the human spirit, we ignore important aspects of who we are. Grounded in IFS, because it is both powerful and respectful.
Why I Became a Therapist
While completing a master’s degree in forest ecology, I felt overwhelmed by the disconnect between the research I was doing and the change that I hoped to see in how we care for our planet, our non-human relatives, and one another. I felt strongly that we already had enough information to know that we need to make changes, and yet everywhere I looked I saw how trapped we were in our systems and ways of being that rely on exploitation and extraction, and how many of us seemed to be struggling in those same systems. So my focus shifted towards understanding people, and what became clear is that when we experience trauma and do not have the opportunity to heal and make sense of what we’ve gone through, we pass that trauma on to others and to the world around us even as we continue to suffer ourselves. This is how I understand the way we’ve arrived where we are today, and it is why I decided that the most meaningful thing I could do is find a way to heal and help others do the same.

name: Dr. Wilson, DNP (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Your treatment will encompass your holistic health to improve your overall well-being and support the path to healing. Medication management services consider your symptoms, treatment goals, and physical health, as well as developmental considerations for children and teens. We know that finding the right medication or combination of medications and dosage can be a challenge. But with over a decade of experience and clinical expertise in complex patients with multiple diagnoses, we can help you discover the most effective and well-tolerated treatment.

name: Ryan Leiderman (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background
Credentials I hold a MA in Counseling and am a Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC) as well as a Registered Clinical Counselor (RCC). Since 2014, I have been dedicated to supporting individuals on their journey toward mental and emotional well-being. Experience With a decade of experience, I have had the privilege of working with a diverse range of clients in various settings, including private practice, EAPs, and community health centers. Specialties I specialize in helping individuals navigate the challenges of anxiety, trauma, and addictions. These areas of focus are close to my heart, and I am committed to providing collaborative, evidence-based therapies that meet your unique needs.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I’m an advocate for animal rights and deeply committed to environmental conservation. My love for animals extends into my vegan lifestyle, where I am passionate about plant-based cooking and nutrition.

name: Katrina Clark (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My practice is informed and enriched by my personal journey, my professional trainings and my experience in the fields of social justice, mental health and holistic wellness. I offer a unique, integrative approach that goes beyond symptom management. Using a blend of evidence-based and experiential modalities — including EMDR, IFS, and somatic interventions — we’ll work together to gently unwind the effects of trauma, release deep emotional blocks, and rewire your nervous system for lasting transformation. In addition to individual therapy, I offer EMDR Intensives and Therapy Retreats for those seeking immersive, focused healing experiences. If you’re ready to reconnect with your authentic self and create meaningful, lasting change — know that it’s never too late to rewrite your story. I would be honored to walk alongside you.

name: Gemma Baumer (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Our earliest experiences formed our nervous systems. We adapted to situations outside of ourselves to meet our inherent need for connection, often before we were even conscious. There is good reason for why we do what we do! Most importantly, we get to continuously reorganize and redefine our experience, and find more connection to ourselves and others. I practice from an Existential-Humanistic framework in which I view building a genuine therapeutic relationship as essential. From this place, I walk beside my clients in their journey of integrating and exploring all aspects of experience--psychological, interpersonal, and emotional. In attuning to our inner experience--needs, boundaries and desires, we are able to connect more to life itself, creating more freedom around how we want to live. Starting therapy with a new therapist can feel vulnerable. I will do my best to honor the inherent bravery it takes to engage in these ways.
Why I Became a Therapist
I hold a Bachelor's degree in Psychology and a Master's in Mental Health Counseling from Lewis & Clark College. I worked with Philip Zimbardo on curriculum for promoting a Growth Mindset. I completed my internship at the Mindful Experiential Therapy Counseling Center (M.E.T.A.), where I received training in the Hakomi Method and Body-Centered awareness. I currently serve on the board of Existential Humanist NW, where I curate the blog. I became a therapist because I am inspired by the way we humans make meaning of and transform our experiences. I too have benefited from the non-judgmental and embracing space of a good therapeutic relationship in which to explore my own being, and I feel passionate about holding this space for my clients. Because I believe therapy has so much to do with cultivating a genuine connection, and feeling free to show up as oneself, I encourage you to trust your intuition. If you think we might be a good fit, feel free to reach out for a free consultation!

name: Matthew Geraths (THEY/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I use a combination of Narrative Therapy, Emotionally Focused Therapy, and a Systems approach. My speciality is sexualities, gender, and relationship therapy
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have worked in the field of gender, sexualities, and identity for the last 12 years. I split my time between teaching at a local university and counseling. As a therapist, I have a strong specialization in sexual intimacy/trauma work, couples/family therapy, as well as grief therapy.

name: Sam Trillo (HE/THEM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
The most important tools I possess are my listening ability and empathy. The main therapeutic effect then becomes the real relationship you and I create. Through my work as a counselor, I provide a space for you to explore and work through feelings you've never put a name to. I believe Western society has left us detached from our emotions. I can help you learn to trust your emotions. By letting you be yourself and say whatever comes to mind, you can access unknown parts of yourself that deserve to be heard. Talking is just one of the tools we can utilize to find those parts of you that may be hidden; through art, music, and movement, we can communicate with the whole of you. My approach is integrative, meaning I pull from different techniques catered to you. Using humanistic, person-centered, and psychodynamic techniques, we can build a trusting relationship where you feel safe to explore. I believe everyone possesses an unconscious that is ready to be listened to.
Social Justice Issues I Care About
I am invested in helping people find their self that has been covered over by capitalism and societal expectations. I believe social media has exacerbated the loss of community and intimate relationships that neoliberal policies helped erode over the last five decades. I believe in community activism and solidarity. Through my counseling work, I hope to help LGBTQ2SIA+ communities, People of Color, BIPOC individuals and organizations, and low-income individuals. I am deeply concerned by the effects of climate change. I'm willing and able to explore the feelings of grief that may be associated with climate change. I have worked with many people who have experienced unexplainable or paranormal events. I am non-judgemental and open-minded to all types of spiritual and metaphysical beliefs.

name: Noha Mostafa (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Using a well rounded approach to wellbeing, I co-craft with each client every counseling session as a "1 hour retreat" from daily stressful cycles and an opportunity to experience meaningful presence and real aliveness. I hope that when strengths and inner resources are more intentionally accessed; and when patterns of relating to self and others as well as identity related aspects are compassionately and honestly confronted, sustainable therapeutic outcomes can be facilitated. I see humans as parts of larger structures and systems that constantly impact their health. I am a Licensed Professional Counselor Associate (LPC) with the Oregon Board of Licensed Professional Counselors and Therapists, a National Certified Counselor (NCC) and registered in the EMDR International Association as an emdria certified therapist who also offers consultation hours to EMDR trained clinicians working towards their certification and to clinicians completing their EMDR basic training requirements.
More About the People I Treat
I enjoy working with people who hope to optimize their health and increase the capacity to be present for what they care about. Prior to becoming a therapist, my work as a "master certified" health coach focused on educating and coaching professionals and caregivers on sustainable nutrition, movement, lifestyle and mindset habits for optimal health. As a certified teacher of mindfulness based somatic practice since 2010, my work with clients navigating chronic imbalances has been focused on actualizing their hopes to expand their potential and feel free from unhelpful patterns related to undetected past memories and environmental stressors. While each therapeutic journey is meant to patiently meet clients' minds, bodies and hearts where they are at, the majority of the clients I work with can refine the regulation skills and wellness practices learnt during sessions and conclude their therapeutic experience feeling confident in their capacity to sustain their therapeutic gains.

name: Mary Knoblock (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
My Qualifications are as follows: 1. Licensed Clinical Rapid Transformation Therapy Practitioner 2. Emotion Code Practitioner 3. Duke Trained Integrative Holistic Health Coach 4. Reiki Master 5. Spiritual Counseling (ordained minister) 6. Homeopath Student of two years with Dr. Faulkner, MD
Social Justice Issues I Care About
I have had the wonderful opportunity to work with clients from all religions, and genders, and am an advocate supporter of the LGBTQIA+ healthcare community after many LGBTQIA members found my sessions so welcoming and helpful. I have many clients who have worked with me who felt comfortable reaching out to a non biased, welcoming holistic hypnotist. I also support families from very diverse cultures and advocate for equality and inclusion and fair treatment in client care.

name: Eight Matallana (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I combine mindfulness, somatic techniques, and parts work to help you regulate emotions, build confidence, feel more embodied, and shift long-standing patterns. I use IFS-Informed therapy, trauma-informed care, Restoration Therapy, Brainspotting, and the Gottman Method to support emotional healing and relationship growth. I also assist clients in processing psychedelic-assisted therapy experiences and navigating life transitions in a thoughtful, holistic way.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
In our work together, I help you navigate anxiety, ruminating thoughts, personal changes, relationship conflicts, and creative blocks in a non-judgmental and supportive way. I maintain a trauma-informed lens that integrates mind-body-spirit connection in order to help clients build self-trust, confidence and emotional resilience. My approach is collaborative: in the first few sessions, we’ll assess if we’re a good fit, and together, we’ll create a treatment plan tailored to your unique goals, supporting lasting personal growth and emotional well-being. Feel free to book a free 20-minute consultation with me.

name: Alana Ogilvie (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I hold a Master's Degree in Couple & Family Therapy, with a specialization in Sex Therapy, from Thomas Jefferson University in Philadelphia, PA. I am a licensed marriage and family therapist in both Oregon and Washington. With over a decade of experience as a couples counselor, I bring a wealth of knowledge and a compassionate approach to my practice. I utilize a variety of models and evidence-based practices, including emotion-focused therapy (EFT) and intersystems theory. As a systemic-oriented therapist, I believe that people are connected to each other through a system of intertwining relationships. Sometimes, as people encounter changes, problems arise that individuals and partners cannot handle on their own. My role is to support you in building positive, secure relationships that will improve your well-being and interpersonal experiences.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in the healing power that comes from dismantling internalized systems of oppression (racism, misogyny, ableism, etc.) and I am passionate about helping my clients break free of previously identified constraints and negative beliefs that keep them, their loved ones, and society at large stuck and unable to reach their full potential. My clinical interests include working with couples, relationship groups, and individuals on communication issues, a lost sense of connection, and sexual dysfunction in the context of their relationships. My passions are addressing sexual desire differences in couples, working with neurodiverse partnerships, and helping clients explore or renegotiate open relationships. When I am not in the office, you can find me practicing yoga, wine tasting, skiing Mt. Hood, or camping with my partner and friends.

name: Mimosa Constans (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My role as your therapist is to provide you space and support with attunement, compassion, and empathy so that you can put burdens down, and rest your weary heart. My goal is for you to feel safe to express your needs and vulnerabilities that you've buried for so long. In the process of uncovering the foundation you were built on, we can identify the spaces of your mind and heart that need healing. Highlighting your natural strengths and drawing from your inner compass to help us navigate our therapeutic journey together.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I'm a fully inclusive therapist and support all communities. I know how it feels to not quite “fit in”. I've lived in a world of many cultural influences and learned that we are all longing for love, acceptance, meaningful connection, and autonomy no matter where started or how we got here. Outside of therapy I am a wife, mother, and self proclaimed foodie. I love quality time with my husband, four amazing daughters and our pack of (4) dogs.

name: Janel Ruehl (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I am a Level I Certified Restoration Therapist and have studied with Dr. T. Hargrave, the founder of the Restoration Therapy Model. I hold a master’s degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from Oregon State University- Cascades, and a bachelor’s degree in English from the University of Oregon. Prior to transitioning into the Mental Health field, I worked for five years in education, and more than a decade in the non-profit and local government sectors.
Why I Became a Therapist
I'm a human too! After years of struggling to understand my own anxiety and depression, I began to develop healthy coping skills through my relationships with skilled counselors and therapists. This impacted every aspect of my life, including my career in community development. Although I enjoyed working toward positive change at the community level, I realized I can make an even bigger impact by working with individuals and couples one-on-one. Now, I love to combine my belief in the power of community with my work as a counselor, helping my clients find healing connections all around them. In addition to counseling, I also continue to support a variety of non-profit organizations through grant writing, organizational development, and facilitation services. In my free time, I volunteer with several organizations focused on food security, LGBTQ+ community building, and animal welfare. I also enjoy gardening, cooking, hiking, and skiing and find deep peacefulness in nature.

name: ⚚DR.SANJAY KUMAR⚚ ⚚PSYCHOTHERAPY LLC⚚
approaches:
My Background
Starting my private ⚚Clinical Practice in 2023, I am provide services in the Portland, Oregon area, as well as throughout the state of Oregon, with a wide range of Mental Health services. I provide both Individual & Couples Therapy. I am currently registered as a Professional Counseling Associate with the State of Oregon. Clinical Psychotherapy has always been my strong passion since my early childhood. I have a ⚚Doctor of Medicine, M.D., degree, and later specialized with my Master of Science in Clinical Mental Health. I have chosen the latter M.S. degree and my Professional Counseling Associate for my current credentials for providing dedicated Mental Health for so many clients throughout this wonderful state of Oregon. Life can be very overwhelming, and sometimes even exhausting, but I’m here to guide you through any obstacles you may face. My sincere goal is to help you to better cope with change, to help you with self-discovery. Dr. Sanjay Kumar
My Approach to Helping
I implement effective eclectic Psychotherapy methods such as Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) combined with the Mindfulness techniques of Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT) to instill self-understanding and encourage my clients to adopt new attitudes and feelings towards life situations. It is essential to keep in mind that therapy is not a magic pill or a quick fix: therapy is a healing process that necessitates participation and investment from the patient and caretaker alike. But in the end, making small changes to self-defeating behavior and coping with feelings of sadness, fear, and pain can bring life-altering results. I hope to serve as a dedicated facilitator to help you reach those goals of Self-Empowerment.

name: Becca Flatt (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am an eclectic therapist who practices from a foundation of attachment theory. The most important part of therapy is developing a solid therapeutic relationship with clients. My goal is to help you understand yourself in a safe space free of harmful judgment and full of compassion and kindness. I hold space for all emotions and hope you bring all of them in with you even the ones that might feel not okay to have.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a licensed clinical social worker. I have worked with people from every stage of life. My favorite populations to work with are adolescents 15+, young adults, adults, and older adults. My focus of practice includes adoption-related complex trauma, racial identity development, grief, secondary rejection, search & reunion, estrangement, and parenting as an adoptee. I am an adoptee-competent therapist and specialize in working with adoptees and transracial adoptees 15+. As both an adult adoptee and adoptive parent, I have the understanding clinically and through lived experience of the complexities, difficulties, and mixed emotions that punctuate adoption. I specialize in working with marginalized communities with complex identities, specifically: people of the global majority and queer women/fems, identity, racialized trauma, and the deconstruction of internalized racism and homophobia.

name: Forage Wellness Collective
approaches:
Our Background and Approach
At Forage Wellness, our treatment team has hundreds of combined training hours in the Restoration Therapy approach. Our Clinical Supervisor, Justin Little, LMFT (RT Certified Level II), is heavily involved in every aspect of your care. Student interns and pre-licensed clinicians receive top-notch attention, training and supervision in order to provide you with the best treatment possible. Justin will often participate in therapy sessions in addition to supervising and training in real-time.
My Approach to Helping
Restoration Therapy (also known as “RT”) uses the latest developments in Attachment Theory, Emotional Regulation and Mindfulness in an understandable and effective approach that empowers clients to clearly identify and name fight/flight reactions in order to facilitate growth. All of our therapists receive extensive training and supervision in RT in order to maximize positive clinical outcomes for you.

name: Angie Arledge (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I can provide a safe space for you to share your story and how that has made you who you are. I offer support for adults, adolescents, and couples looking for a place to work through what they are experiencing. While you process the things that are coming up, I will walk alongside you.
My Values as a Therapist
I am here to walk with you as you discover or rediscover that place of contentment. Mental wellness is one the hardest things to reach out and ask for support with. We will work together to develop ways to decrease those symptoms or life struggles through self-investing.

name: Michael Morales (HE/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
People often ask me–after sharing about their situation–if I have any hope for them. The answer is: I always have hope! But it’s not unfounded. It comes from years of experience and the success I’ve seen using research-based treatment methods. I prefer to use the Gottman Method, Emotion-Focused Therapy, and Interpersonal Neurobiology as our foundation. As we do the work together, we may find that additional tools, techniques, or outside professional support are needed. I expect to work with couples for an average of 3-4 months (10-15 weekly sessions). As you put in the effort, you will begin to see shifts in your relationship within the first two months (8 sessions) of treatment. This is when the "average couple" begins to make and maintain meaningful changes. As a result, their hope for sustaining change and their confidence in getting "unstuck" from past and future problems grows. And just like that–we’re talking about how we want to continue or conclude our work together.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
Every couple is different, and we’ve all got our unique preferences and communication styles, so I can’t promise I’ll be the best fit for you. Just like shopping for a car, you can do all kinds of research, but you never know which one is right until you take it for a test drive. That’s why I offer free 15-minute consultations, so you can get a feel for what therapy will be like with me. There are a few things I can promise if you choose to work with me: to remain neutral and create space for you and your partner to talk through your issues. I commit to doing my best work in every session and to be honest when I have not. I will support you in the work of exercising productive communication skills in the moments that matter. Flexibility and responsiveness to your unique needs will be the hallmarks of the work I do with you. We’ll definitely get along if you also hail from California, are a covert coffee snob, shed tears for bygone pets, and wish Ted Lasso was a real person.

name: Joaquin Lopez (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Our bodies have an inherent ability to heal themselves. Our minds do, too. All clients can tap into their own healing, and with counsel, cultivate their strengths and personhood. Counseling provides a time to understand our state of mind, affect, or mood. Counseling offers a time to be seen and listened to with undivided attention, I offer compassion and listen to understand. I bring my whole being and imagination into the work to offer an authentic connection to create a working relationship based on trust, openness, and possibility. I incorporate tools garnered from psychodynamic approaches, humanistic/existential psychology, motivational interviewing, and CBT. I believe reflecting on life’s past and present relationships offer insight to help us navigate our current and future relationships, experiences, and self. Together we can help you achieve your goals, make new choices to create new experiences that ultimately forge a new understanding and a more satisfying, fulfilling life.
My Values as a Therapist
Pain, stress, and trauma can cause restlessness, sleepless nights, loss of pleasure, difficulty concentrating, low self-esteem, and the sense of not being fully present. The body expresses these symptoms accompanied by feelings of anger, guilt, anxiety, and shame. These feelings separate us from ourselves and from the people we love. These feelings prevent us from forming loving relationships and hinder our ability to enjoy life. In counseling, the client and the counselor enter a mindful and healing professional relationship. This connection is cultivated through the exploration of the client's presenting concerns, feelings, and mental disposition. The counselor listens with a clinical understanding while offering insight, therapeutic awareness, and tools for personal growth. The client works with honesty, vulnerability, and openness. A desire to change, self-awareness, and regular reflection by the client generate an environment for personal healing and growth.

name: Luke Allen (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I primarily provide individual therapy with late teens and adults. In addition to depression and anxiety, I also specialize in working with transgender and non-binary youth and their families on matters related to gender identity. Transgender clients and family members seeking assessment or counseling services can expect a safe space, and respectful, non-pathologizing, collaborative care. I am a Certified World Professional Association for Transgender Health Care (WPATH) GEI SOC8 Member. I have extensive training, research, and clinical experience working with transgender and gender-diverse youth, adults, and families.

name: Iris Torres Garcia (SHE/HER/ELLA)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Over the past 10 years, I’ve had the privilege of serving parents, youth, and families in the Portland Metro area through my work in juvenile justice, education, and supporting survivors of domestic violence. My approach draws from a diverse set of therapeutic methods, including Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), narrative therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), strength-based and trauma-informed practices, and culturally responsive frameworks. I believe in an integrative perspective, as no single theory can fully capture the vast complexity of human experience.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
As a Chicanx clinician, I specialize in mental health counseling for Q/BIPOC, who may have experienced sexual assault, domestic- violence, and intergenerational trauma. Maíz y Sol Counseling Services is an offering dedicated to family, community, and the ancestors. It’s an act of love committed to decolonizing mental health services where we believe that Black Lives Matter, We are pro- immigration, and Love is love.

name: Emma Jewell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I bring a powerful empathetic presence with focused support for your personal journey. I help you weave a way back to connection through movement exercises, sound, and expansive wisdom. Whether it's heart break, anger, terror, insecurity, anxiety and so much more, sessions will help you show up in your life with more agility and ease. Meeting everyday challenges with more capacity and space rather than reaction. I am always continuing on my path of learning and unlearning.
