Unwinding the oppressions passed to me and my ancestors and the people around me and of the land: Indigenous people. I bring forth this work for the liberation of the people and the whole world.

name: Melissa Yeary (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe everyone has an innate capacity to grow and thrive. Therapy offers a safe space to explore the depth of your inner life and identify what may be holding you back from becoming the person you want to be. Together, we’ll work to understand your unique emotions, thoughts, body, and history as the process of healing unfolds. By gaining clarity about yourself—individually and in relationships—you can make thoughtful, meaningful choices. Exploring long-held thought patterns can free you from emotional limitations, opening the door to greater engagement, passion, and purpose. I also believe in the power of creativity to enhance growth and self-expression. Integrating creative practices into therapy can deepen insight and healing. I enjoy supporting clients who wish to explore their creative process in any form. Additionally, I facilitate SoulCollage®, an experiential art practice that brings profound meaning and insight, helping you connect with your inner wisdom.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have extensive experience working with adults of all ages, specializing in anxiety, depression, self-awareness, resolving childhood trauma, and nervous system regulation. As a certified Hakomi therapist, I support clients in achieving meaningful transformation by exploring their thoughts, emotions, body sensations, and memories. My approach is guided by a diverse background as a parent, artist, coach, and former software designer and manager. This blend of experiences allows me to offer a unique combination of creativity, intuition, and practical insight. I believe creativity plays a vital role in nurturing the spirit and often encourage clients to explore their creative interests as a pathway to self-renewal and emotional balance. In sessions, I frequently incorporate parts-of-self work, which helps clients connect with and understand the many layers of their inner world, fostering deeper self-awareness and lasting healing.

name: Nicole Denney (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Ace Therapy strives to provide a safe environment that encourages growth for you as an individual, couple or family unit. I specialize in empowering clients to explore their strengths, develop coping strategies and achieve growth in reaching their goals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Nobody can make you feel inferior without your permission. - Elanor Roosevelt Lets feel empowered together. I specialize in seeing those who are struggling with anger, depression, anxiety, relationships, behaviors and addiction. Let's walk this path together, towards a brighter, healthier, stronger you!

name: Michael Wilson, MA
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
You may have lost the ability to hope that your relationship can improve, but a better life awaits you. I work with couples in distress, and it is a privilege and an honor. I help my clients reconnect with parts of themselves they feel too afraid to acknowledge and certainly don’t feel comfortable sharing with others. I then work to help you and your partner better understand one another, to truly hear one another and meet one another’s deepest needs. My hope and my belief is that when you can communicate your deepest fears and needs with each other, you can begin to understand one another and love one another in a new, more fulfilling way.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Since becoming a counselor I’ve developed a passion to help couples regain connection and a deep sense of contentment and fulfillment with one another. I’ve often been told by couples that I work with that I have a way about me that helps them feel comfortable and capable to be open and deal with the real problems in the relationships. I have extensive experience helping clients with identity formation, largely because I think it is vital to being in a relationship to understand oneself before you can reach out to another. I also have experience working with depression, anxiety, and self-criticism.

name: Ashley Ward (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have a positive and direct approach when working with my clients. I'm empathetic and enjoy using motivational talk to help my clients feel motivated in sessions. I am passionate about teaching my clients effective techniques and strategies to meet their mental health needs. I also hold my client's accountable to do therapeutic homework assignments and practicing effective techniques in order for you to meet your personal mental health goals. Let's work together to make the positive change you are looking for.
More About the People I Treat
I specialize in treating OCD Obsessive Compulsive Disorder and Anxiety Disorders. Most client's that have OCD need a therapist that is specialized well versed in ERP and Mindfulness; as these are the two modalities that can assist with treating OCD. Most places only specialize in one modality. Working with me, I can provide an array of therapeutic modalities as I am certified in Exposure and Response Prevention Therapy (ERP), Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) , Mindfulness, and Trauma Focused -Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (TF-CBT). I keep the focus on treating the OCD while making sure I don't give my client's reassurance. In addition, my client's can talk about trauma, daily stressors, and other life events in sessions while I use a CBT approach to help treat additional concerns. I have invested in my training and am passionate about providing quality and diverse treatment for my client's mental health needs. Making it easier for my clients to have options in therapy.

name: Adam Marx (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My counseling degree is from a depth-oriented counseling psychology program, and I have been coaching clients for over 25 years. This broad experience gives me a wide ranges of tools and approaches to use with clients, completely dependent on what each client needs. I can empathically listen and support client insight into difficult issues, I can collaboratively explore dreams and memories, and I can help clients achieve specific behavioral goals. Most often, I use multiple approaches with each client, as they move from one aspect of an issue to another, or one stage to the next. I draw from expertise and experience with somatic therapies, NeuroLinguistic Programming (NLP), Gestalt techniques, and parts work.
Why I Became a Therapist
Two events set me on the path to become a psychotherapist and coach, and they both involve loss. The first was the death of my younger brother, and the second was the death of my son. The combination of these two experiences (25 years apart) and the support I received to deal with those losses set me on the path to counseling. I experienced first-hand how devastating it is to lose a loved one, and how much of a difference a caring and compassionate professional could make as I traveled through my grieving process. I realized that I wanted to help others navigate that grieving process. In addition to grief work, I have benefited from working on healthy masculinity. Involved with men's groups for over ten years (as both a facilitator and a member), I have found them invaluable in working through many of the negative, toxic, and counterproductive beliefs and unconscious training men integrate in our culture.

name: Alexa Adams (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We all bring our unique selves, our pasts, and family histories into each relationship. It can be challenging to find balance and harmony with so many different factors interacting with and against each other. I absolutely believe that everyone can benefit from relationship therapy. I love working with and discovering the intricacies each relationship holds. I hold a special focus of working with complex trauma, both on the individual level and with how trauma interacts with the relationship system. I do not hold tight to one particular model due to the complexity and uniqueness that each client brings to the therapy experience. I tend to pull from narrative, structural, strategic, and emotionally focused therapy approaches. I love experiential therapy techniques and will often bring these into the room. I also utilize my intuition and energy that each client brings into the space.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that everyone can benefit from therapy. I maintain the deepest appreciation and hope for my clients. Life can be hard, lonely, and overwhelming, and having someone who is simply there to listen without bias and judgement can be instrumental in the healing process. I believe that each therapist should engage in their own intensive therapy to explore their personal biases and judgements in order to be aware of how these may be entering the therapy space. I maintain a social justice framework and am constantly learning and growing in my awareness and understanding. I am a strong ally for all communities who have been and continue to experience oppression and marginalization. I believe in grassroots organizing and feel that the only way that we can impact change is to stand as a solid community against hate and violence. I believe that Buffy the Vampire Slayer is one of the best shows, ever, and often use this show to provide examples during sessions. :)

name: Mitch Bacon, MA, LMFT
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My therapeutic approach draws from Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT), Ecopyschology, and Narrative therapy. I support my clients in building awareness, finding strategies for solutions, and becoming more engaged with the people you love in the process. I've practice in a variety of settings, such as wilderness therapy in the desert, a yurt for an office in a garden, medical setting, and private practice. I hold a Bachelor of Arts in Psychology from The Colorado College and a Master of Arts in Marriage, Couples, and Family Therapy from Lewis and Clark College.
Why I Became a Therapist
My first job out of college was as a guide in a wilderness therapy program in Idaho. I quickly learned that growth can happen when people developed connections with others, had someone believing in them, and pushed themselves to their limits. I felt honored to walk alongside people making changes in their lives. I have since spent my professional life working together with countless people who have made powerful, sustainable changes.

name: Jennie Towne
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I support clients in increasing positive communication skills, creating healthy boundaries, improving relationships, and in processing past challenging experiences. I have extensive experience in working with individuals who have experienced sexual abuse, domestic violence, depression, and anxiety. My practice centers on creating a safe space through being inclusive and affirming to all humans by paying particular attention to each individual’s experiences and life story. I embrace working with people from diverse backgrounds and who identify on the LGBTQ spectrum.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My personal experiences combined with my love of learning, teaching, and my desire to better understand the human experience guided me to becoming a therapist. I completed both my Master’s of Education and Master’s of Counseling in Clinical Mental Health from Portland State University. Many of my professional experiences help to provide a broad foundation for my work as a therapist. My experience as a yoga instructor continues to connect me to the importance of having a holistic approach in working with each individual. My work, spanning from working with survivors of domestic violence to being in a community mental health agency, provided me with direct experience in managing crisis, creating safety, and, supporting healing. I honor the resilience in us all.

name: Teresa Stroup (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
As a Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW) with 15+ years of experience, I offer specialized trauma therapy, PTSD treatment, and relationship counseling in Portland. My background includes leadership at Good Samaritan Ministries, providing Christian counseling while honoring diverse beliefs. I'm certified in anger management, couples therapy, and evidence-based parenting programs. My approach blends Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT), and Solution-Focused Therapy to address immediate concerns and deeper patterns. I specialize in life transitions, neurodiversity challenges, and family conflict. With a Master's from Portland State and experience as a clinical supervisor, I uphold the highest practice standards. My background in hospice social work and crisis intervention equips me for complex emotional needs, creating a safe space where healing flourishes through trauma-informed care and authentic connection.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
My clients find our work together transformative because I blend professional expertise with genuine compassion. As your therapist, I create a uniquely safe environment where trauma healing and personal growth naturally unfold. My integrated approach combines evidence-based therapy with heartfelt understanding of your unique journey. If you're seeking couples counseling to rebuild connection, PTSD treatment to reclaim your peace, or Christian counseling that honors your faith while addressing real-world challenges, my practice offers specialized support. For those navigating neurodiversity, family therapy needs, or anger management, I provide both practical tools and deeper emotional understanding. My background in crisis intervention and hospice care has refined my ability to meet you exactly where you are—whether in acute distress or subtle dissatisfaction. Through CBT techniques, DBT skills, and solution-focused methods, we'll address both symptoms and underlying patterns.

name: JoAnne White (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My social work history with teens, young adults, and families has given my a plethora of experience in numerous life situations. I aim to be the person that can assist you in defining goals, ways to reach them, and strategies to maintain them. I meet where you are at and our time could be brief or long, but my goal is solution focused therapy with cognitive techniques.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe everyone can benefit from therapy if you find the right fit. Therapy is work, but the rewards outweigh the struggle. I have been in social work and worked alongside first responders. I have a good foundation in trauma care.

name: Care Nimble
approaches:
Our Approach to Helping
At Care Nimble, we make mental health medication simple. Our board-certified psychiatrists and psychiatric nurse practitioners provide you with same-day care through an easy-to-use, online platform. No scheduling needed. Get personalized prescription treatment for depression, anxiety, ADHD, bipolar disorder, PTSD, and more. We ask the right questions to understand your unique needs, and get you the right care when you need it - today. Start your journey to better mental health today with effective, affordable mental health medication management that works for you.
Why We Started this Group Practice
At Care Nimble, we make mental health medication simple. Our board-certified psychiatrists and psychiatric nurse practitioners provide you with same-day care through an easy-to-use, online platform. No scheduling needed. Get personalized prescription treatment for depression, anxiety, ADHD, bipolar disorder, PTSD, and more. We ask the right questions to understand your unique needs, and get you the right care when you need it - today. Start your journey to better mental health today with effective, affordable mental health medication management that works for you. Psychiatric care is often frustrating, expensive, and overwhelming, with long waits, inconvenient scheduling, expensive bills, complicated insurance coverage. Care Nimble makes it simple. We provide personalized, affordable, quality psychiatric medication management, on your terms. Right now. Mental health med management made easy.

name: Jaimie Lusk (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My expertise is grounded in evidence-based therapies, including Cognitive Processing Therapy (CPT), Prolonged Exposure (PE), Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), and Emotion-Focused Therapy, all of which I am certified in. My approach is holistic, integrating these therapies with practices tailored to each client's unique healing journey. With over 10 years of experience as a psychologist in the VA Portland, I have developed a profound understanding of the challenges faced by veterans, first responders, and survivors of abuse. This extensive background has equipped me with the skills to address a wide range of trauma-related issues effectively. My dedication to ongoing professional development ensures that I bring the most effective, innovative treatments to those I serve. My contributions to the field, through national trainings and published research, reflect my commitment to not just healing but empowering my clients to reclaim their narratives and thrive.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
At the core of my practice, I believe in the the human capacity for healing. I am passionate about empowering individuals to reclaim their narratives, viewing each person's journey through a lens of strength and potential rather than pathology. My personal interests in mindfulness, wilderness exploration, and the arts deeply influence my therapeutic approach, emphasizing the mind-body connection and the importance of holistic well-being. I am enthusiastic about incorporating animals and nature into our work together, as humans can find connection and purpose not only in our human relationships but in our relationships with the non-human world. My commitment to trauma recovery extends beyond the therapy room; I have actively built and supported two veteran surf communities on the Oregon coast where Oregon veterans and friends can find camaraderie, connection and challenge in the ocean. Check out my blog here: https://www.psychologytoday.com/us/blog/threshold

name: Carrie Farrell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Humans have a unique opportunity: the ability to shift attention intentionally from automatic and habitual thought patterns to awareness of sensation in the present moment. By strengthening our ability to accurately perceive reality through experiential awareness, we untangle outdated threat responses so that we can respond with clarity to what we are experiencing in the present moment. Caring for and updating signals from the body can be a powerful approach to lasting therapeutic change. Clients often report a greater sense of integration and wholeness, as well as increased ability to make empowered choices, rather than feeling like life is happening to them.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I aim to see clients in their wholeness, rather than any individual fragmented wounds that may present themselves. I am always cheering for each client’s innate wisdom and whole self to emerge in our work together. I am trauma-informed, sex and body-positive, and committed to increasing my social awareness. I believe it is essential to consider how systems of power and privilege relate to each person’s experience in the world and welcome these influences and inquiries into my counseling office.

name: Dr. Tee Mayfield (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
Dr. Tee is a (LPC) licensed professional counselor in the state of Texas, Arizona and Oregon. She has been practicing mental health treatment in a variety of settings such as Inpatient, PHP, IOP and private practice. She has Bachelor's degree in Psychology and a master's degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from Texas Woman's University and is currently a Doctoral Candidate in Marriage and Family Therapy from NCU. She is skilled in different modalities such as DBT, CBT, EMDR as well as Existential therapy. Tee is also resourceful and helpful with navigating through minor to major crisis in life. Tee is an essential therapist who helps with FMLA, Medication Management, CPS cases and is savvy with utilizing different resources to assist in day to day obstacles. She enjoys meeting clients where they are at in their treatment and in their crisis. Her schedule is very flexible and will work with clients based on their availability and finances.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
Here at Center of Cultivating is dedicated to the mission of partnering with patients to improve their quality of life. We promise to be there for you every step of your journey. Our goal is to create a healthy and reliable patient and professional partnership in which is the heart of our organization. We are committed to providing high quality care. Let’s us work together to get you feeling your best!

name: Sam Skye (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I focus on helping my clients making positive lifestyle choices that support living with fullness and satisfaction. My goals and objectives for our therapeutic work are built upon a foundation of mutual respect, safety, and clear communication. In therapy, we may explore the impacts of discrimination, trauma, and oppression on mental health and well-being. My practice is informed by an anti-oppressive, sex-positive, and feminist systems approach that seeks to bring empowerment and respect to the individual. 
My Values as a Therapist
I am a trained art therapist with a love for a variety of creative processes! Art making is always available, but never required! I have expertise in transgender issues in children and adults, exploring lesbian, gay, bisexual, polyamorous, and queer identities, and intersectional feminist family systems theories. I specialize in adoption and foster care issues including support with healing from trauma, abuse, loss, grief, and neglect.

name: Nathan Becker (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am committed to creating a collaborative space in which you and I can work together to cultivate change. My approach is informed by my values and qualities of empathy, authenticity, openness, and social justice. I believe in gently challenging, pushing you outside of your comfort zone while focusing on and acknowledging your sources of strength and resiliency. I incorporate Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) into much of my work, where we will focus on changing maladaptive behaviors and disengaging from negative thought patterns.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I became a therapist because I know what it’s like to feel overwhelmed, misunderstood, and unsure how to move forward. As someone who identifies as neurodiverse, I bring not only clinical training, but lived experience to the work I do. My practice is rooted in authenticity, compassion, and a belief in the power of small, meaningful change. I specialize in working with adults navigating ADHD, Anxiety, OCD, Insomnia, BFRBs, and the heavy self-criticism that often comes with them. I use evidence-based therapies like CBT, ACT, and ERP, and I balance those tools with warmth, curiosity, and respect for your unique experience. I care deeply about creating a space where all parts of you are welcome—including the messy, scared, or uncertain ones. Whether you're seeking clarity, healing, or simply space to breathe, I'm here to walk with you, not ahead of you.

name: Stace Parlen (THEY/THEM/THEIRS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My framework centers on the belief that we are constantly being shaped by our environment and experiences, and we are co-cretors of reality. My work with transgender and neurodivergent individuals has shown me that we need to feel there is room to expand/edit our stories in order to be able to grow and heal. Each person has a unique story. I use a variety of techniques and interventions to support the retelling of these stories using cultural sensitivity, attachment theory, parts work and trauma- informed care. I am specifically trained in working with family systems, which includes looking at relational dynamics and patterns that may be impacting growth and connection. My practice also incorporates psycho-educational trainings for families, clinicians, schools, businesses, and other community members wishing to know more about transgender identities.
Why I Became a Therapist
I received a BA in Psychology from the University California Santa Cruz. I then pursued a degree in Exotic Animal Training and Management through the EATM program at Moorpark College in 2009. My work with animal attachment and bonding influenced my perspective on human interactions and relationships and sparked my journey to becoming a marriage, couple, & family therapist. I received my Masters degree in Marriage, Couple, & Family Therapy from the Lewis & Clark College in 2015. Throughout my graduate studies I participated in workshops and trainings to educate mental health clinicians and students about LGBTQ concerns. My past work at TransActive Gender Center included counseling transgender youth and their families in both personalized counseling and group counseling settings.

name: Bree Koenig (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy involves person-centered, psychodynamic, and relational modalities that enable us to explore lifelong, underlying patterns of thinking and behavior in a safe and connected environment. My therapeutic style allows me to hold sensitivity towards your emotional and cognitive state while empowering us to access insights into what experiences and beliefs have impacted your wellbeing. With these insights we will explore the most effective forms of coping and change that will be most beneficial to your personal needs.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe that everyone deserves an opportunity to foster an understanding of their own mental health and create insight into what is needed in order to build healthy, personalized approaches to self-care, coping, and communication. Building this type of self-awareness and reaching the point of implementing these skills is often daunting, so connecting with a therapist to support you in processing through these experiences will help lead to feeling genuine change.

name: Patrick Hyland
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I specialize in childhood trauma, including abuse, latch-key, abandonment, and parental rejection. I also am experienced in other traumas, such as combat, workforce, or other significant trauma, having PTSD myself from Vietnam and multiple divorces. I empower adults, both men and women, to become the person they truly want to be, their individual and "True Self."
Why We Started this Group Practice
I started the group practice named Oregon Directions, to find, keep, and teach all counselors the importance of agency support, group recognition for all counselors and therapists, and the value of personally interacting with all other counselors, regardless of their present or future employers, in ways that exemplifies and raises each of us to higher standards of care and capabilities for the benefit of all clients. The Vision and Mission Statements provide clear guidance to all counselors that our clients deserve only the best and our daily work and on-going intentions are to work together to achieve that goal.

name: Yael Kievsky (SHE/HER/ELLA)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My style is engaged, directive, and sometimes humorous, and always tender and reverent of all of who you are. I believe in people being the experts of their own past and their many possible futures, and this orientation has me showing up with a radical sense of hopefulness and creativity to our every encounter. I value cultivating transparency and collaboration in our work together. I am trained in expressive arts for trauma processing. Via simple, non-intimidating arts prompts we may find new points of entry into old stories, and source surprising or compelling meaning - on your terms. I use neuroscience to ground our understanding of what you may be going through. I am trained in NET, a radical and affirming trauma processing modality that empowers us to revisit your story on your terms. In my work with couples I draw directly from restorative and transformative justice & EFT, a deeply effective attachment based approach for transforming relationships.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My background is as an artist and educator, and I am rooted in anti-oppression and liberatory practice. As a Latinx-Jewish immigrant, I come from deeply collectivistic cultures, and the value and sacredness of reciprocal human connection drives my work. My lived experience of immigration and acculturation led me to specialize on the many griefs and traumas of displacement, present or intergenerational. My own experiences in the area of fertility/infertility, queer family making, and perinatal complications showed me firsthand the ways our society does not champion these powerful journeys fully or well. I am dedicated to supporting folks throughout pregnancy-ing, birthing, and parenting; including the joys, fears, grief, and trauma which very often intersect. I am queer-allied, kink-allied, sex positive, trans affirming, anti-ableist (with a disability justice and neurodiversity informed lens), HAES-informed, and explicitly anti racist in my therapeutic work.

name: Julian Montanez (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I'm committed to patient education and shared decision-making. I explain medications thoroughly, discussing potential benefits and side effects, and ensuring you feel confident in your treatment plan. My background in nursing brings a unique perspective to psychiatric care, allowing me to address both biological and personal aspects of mental health.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
If you're considering medication management or looking to optimize your current treatment plan, I welcome you to reach out. I offer both in-person and telehealth appointments, providing flexible options for ongoing care. Together, we can work toward finding the right medication approach to support your mental health goals.

name: Ryja (Rylan Jay) Testa (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My combined personal and professional paths uniquely prepare me to guide others through times of unknown. I offer counseling based on: - over a decade professional experience as a clinical psychologist - over two years of full-time residential training at a Zen monastery - certification as a Nature and Forest Therapy Guide (anft.earth) - certification in ministry and Depth Hypnosis (developed by Isa Gucciardi, PhD.; sacredstream.org) - personal experience healing my own nervous system from burnout, mold toxicity, and dysautonomia, through neural reprogramming, guided imagery, dreamwork, and creative practices - countless hours listening to the teachings of the more-than-human world - lived experience as a transgender man I walk alongside you as fellow human, having gone through my own transformations of gender, health, and spirituality. I offer you all the gifts that have been gathered through my own evolution and healing. Please see my full CV on my website, including publications.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I personalize treatment for you, using a combination of insight oriented work, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, guided meditation, dream work, forest therapy, hypnotherapy, art, movement, and music therapies. I help you connect with your deepest intentions and inner wisdom, and heal from any trauma or stuck patterns that block you from moving forward. You don't have to do the unknown alone. I walk alongside you in the mystery until you find your way to the other shore. I have particular specialized expertise the areas of gender and sexuality, spirituality, and health. If you are experiencing distress related to any of these areas, please don't hesitate to reach out for a free consultation! I would love to hear more about your journey and see how I might be of support.

name: Caitlin Keitel (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I believe everyone has expertise from lived experience. Everyone has profound gifts to offer. Part of my work with clients is to shine a light on the wisdom they already possess, and support them to bring those gifts fully into the world. I use the theories and methods of Process Work in order to customize interventions and bring awareness to the client’s internal process. Process Work helps us to trace our deepest personal patterning through our bodies, behaviors, dreaming, and relationships. It allows us to unfold the mythic power and meaning of our lives. Some methods of awareness expansion include: role play, earth-based meditation, sensory-grounded experience, and creative movement. These methods invite new insights and fluid ways of dealing with painful situations.
My Values as a Therapist
For years, I have worked in the field of community based social work, supporting dialogue and action for racial, economic, housing, and food justice. As a survivor of my mother’s suicide, I understand the urgency of breaking the silence and stigma that surrounds our mental health struggles. I know there is a relationship between the distress of mental illness and the social traumas we endure. No matter the horrors one has experienced, I sincerely believe healing is possible. I find inspiration from the people-led social movements that show us that revolutionary change processes begin with those on the margins. We all deserve healing, and we are stronger together. For many of us, there is a profound element of healing that is only accessible through relationship. Sharing personally with a caring witness can be a powerful step to healing traumas and heartbreak - especially when we have been hurt in relationship. As a therapist, I feel honored to connect with my clients in this way.

name: Congruent Therapy: Danie Meyer (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I work with adults, couples, and individuals navigating all of life's challenges. My clients often include LGBTQ+ folks, those in ethically non-monogamous and polyamorous relationships, and kink-positive. My extensive experience supporting those facing homelessness, addiction, and incarceration further enriches my practice, allowing me to offer a comprehensive and empathetic perspective to all clients, regardless of their background or history. I firmly believe that the therapeutic relationship between therapist and client is the most crucial aspect of therapy. To foster effective therapy, I prioritize engagement, connection, attunement, and rapport. My approach is rooted in a person-centered, trauma-informed, and attachment-based perspective, with an emphasis on harm reduction, which focuses on collaboration between therapist and client. I believe that clients possess the answers to their problems, and it is my job to work alongside them to find solutions.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
My unique blend of life experiences and professional education gives me a distinct outlook on the complexities surrounding identity, mental health, and resilience. I hold a special interest in aiding individuals grappling with attachment traumas, particularly those exploring non-monogamous relationships. The ideal client for me is someone conscious of their challenges and in search of a therapist willing to accompany them on the sometimes uncomfortable journey towards healing and self-discovery, focusing on building healthy attachments and reevaluating personal beliefs and life narratives.

name: Becca Dorn-Medeiros (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My style has been described as approachable and real. I use humor (when appropriate of course) and strive to be aware of the influences of culture, power, and privilege on relationships including the therapeutic relationship. I use a client focused approach; in other words, the client is the true expert on themselves and my role is to help focus that expertise towards a client-set goal. My work pulls from several therapeutic theories including mindfulness practice, solution-focused approaches, collaborative problem solving, cognitive behavioral therapy, skill building, narrative theory, and motivational interviewing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My work has uniquely prepared me to support parents of teens, particularly teens dealing with anxiety, depression, ADHD, ASD, developmental disabilities, as well as parents of kids who identify as LGBTQIA+. I have extensive training in educational psychology, educational disabilities, and educational and disability laws. Additionally I have worked extensively with adolescents and have a keen understanding of the pressures and struggles unique to that age group. As a member of the queer community, it's always been important to me to be an ally and advocate. As an LPC, I am trained to work with any number of life stressors.

name: Jessica Butler (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
When I work with couples and relationships, it is important to me that we create a space where you can show up fully and authentically. I want to support you both in having healthy conflict, feeling like your partner really gets you, working through a break in trust or jealousy, and in having a fulfilling sex life. When I work with individuals, I am passionate about supporting you in navigating the often chaotic world of dating, understanding and advocating for your needs, and cultivating an empowering relationship with your sexuality. I believe that everyone has the right to live their happiest life, and it is my goal to assist you on this journey.
My Approach to Helping
I believe that every person has a unique story, and because of this, unique needs. There is not one correct way to be, and I strive to create a space where you feel safe to be your most authentic self. Whether you are monogamous or poly, kinky or vanilla, straight or queer, I am affirming and celebratory of your story. I look forward to discovering with you, your strength, your resilience, and your capacity to heal and grow.

name: Carissa Ponting (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Therapy should be a place for you to feel safe and cared for. A therapeutic relationships efficacy depends on solid rapport. I use humor, skill and a deep curiosity about life to help develop this important connection. I will provide an authentic lens and specialize in working with parents, those experiencing significant life change, and trauma. As a trained licensed clinical social worker who has lived around the world and engaged in many different systems, I am well versed in how systems and social structures can impact a persons mental health which is something we will likely discuss! Peoples mental health is often pathologized for having a very natural response to systems that create oppression and this is something we will likely discuss. You are not crazy for having a response to life being hard!
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a therapist for many different reasons! I initially entered this field working with pregnant and parenting teens in the custody of the state. I have worked within state systems, in Oregon and in Japan, am a parent, and a person in the world who has navigated difficult circumstances. I have worked with individuals of all ages and specialize in working with parents and couples. Anxiety and depression can be identified on a spectrum and our experiences with these labels can and will fluctuate depending on external circumstances that are not always within our control. I look forward to getting to know you better to provide you with the tools you need to live the life you want to live!

name: Derrick Radford (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
As a psychotherapist, my approach is deeply rooted in depth psychological and integrative perspectives, reflecting a blend of relational, psychoanalytic, emotionally focused, and trauma-informed methods. Grounded in empathy, understanding, and safety, I help clients navigate the complexities of life by promoting the freedom of selfhood, inner strength, curiosity, and communal relations. Additionally, I incorporate a psychospiritual and holistic perspective, acknowledging that wellbeing encompasses the totality of ones being which includes the mind, the body, and the soul. My background and approach offer a therapeutic experience that honors the complexity of each individual's story while fostering profound growth and transformation. As a tribal member of a sovereign Indigenous nation, my life path is rooted in Indigenous culture and knowledge. My practice is ideal for Indigenous peoples or those seeking to reclaim their relationship with their Indigenous roots.
Why I Became a Therapist
I am passionate about helping others reach their fullest potential in life through fostering empowerment and resilience. In particular, I am fond of helping others rewrite their stories so they may live the life they have always wanted to live. It was through rewriting my own story that allowed me the privilege to guide others in doing the same. I would not be where I am today if it were not for those around me who deeply care about my life. I believe that if it weren’t for others in our lives, our potential would diminish. A loving hand is vitality casting itself through generations. I truly believe that growth can come from places we least expect it and through even the darkest of challenges, transformation can be found.

name: Michaela Christopher (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I was privileged to grow up in a culture of sex positivity, which has supported me in forming a secure relationship with exploring sex and sexuality. I view sexuality as something that is uniquely ours to define and honor the dynamic, fluid nature of our sexual identities and experiences. I strongly believe in the importance of a consent based approach, amplifying sexual autonomy, and encouraging curiosity and open mindedness in sexual exploration. I utilize models such as Sensate Focus and the Good Enough Sex, to support individuals and partners in creating unique and fulfilling connections with their sexual identities and in their sexual relationships. I especially enjoy working with partners who are new to parenthood or are experiencing chronic illness, and are looking to reconnect and rediscover sex and intimacy in their relationships.
My Values as a Therapist
At my core, I believe that we are all emotional beings who are influenced by our connections to the people and world around us. My own upbringing was shaped by valuing social justice, understanding the importance of my personal relationships, and creating meaningful connections to my community. Not only as a therapist but as a human being, I enjoy connecting to others through the power of storytelling. Whether our stories are similar or different, I believe sharing them furthers human connection and understanding. It is through the fabric of our relationships that we are able to make larger impacts on our communities and world.

name: Dore Everett, LCSW (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My method of service is eclectic, strengths based and is a mindfulness-centered, somatic approach that is relational and empathic. I meet people where they are without judgment or expectation to explore: limiting personal beliefs/habits and ways to step into your strength and potential to become your best self. I trust in the wholeness of every human being to aid in the discovery of our natural state of thriving and balance.
Why I Became a Therapist
My MSW training and degree was received at the Hunter School of Social Work, NYC. I have post graduate training in the use of Gestalt, CIMBS (Complex Integration of Multiple Brain Systems) as well as extended training with the M.E.T.A. Institute: includes- Attachment work, Mindfulness, Trauma, Hakomi and RC- S. I have extensive experience as a grief/ bereavement counselor and hospice social worker as well as working in drug and alcohol treatment. Prior to my career as a social worker I worked as a freelance artist in New York and San Francisco.

name: Steve Harper (HE)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use a combination of evidenced based therapeutic practices along with holistic approaches. Clients may enter therapy with anxiety, depression, family issues, relationship/dating, and addictions, and leave with the practical tools to cultivate a deeper sense of "aliveness" - experiencing greater peace, freedom, and personal meaning. I find fulfillment in helping my clients merge therapy with their real life goals and challenges. I am happy to talk over a free 15 minute introductory call so you can make sure we are a good fit together.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold Master's Degrees in Clinical Mental Health Counseling (OSU) and Educational Leadership (U of O). I am relatable with considerable life experience, plus 20 years of collective occupational background in business sales, public education, and addiction recovery. I have worked with a variety of people from differing ethnic, social, and economic backgrounds in San Diego, Chicago, and throughout Oregon.

name: Mark Pechovnik
approaches:
The emphasis on therapy is always on what is happening now, here in session. Even when thoughts of the past arise, they are being metabolized in the present. We work to acknowledge and allow our reality to fully express itself and then make choices about what is the most skillful way to move forward. Whereas we often think we are broken, we are always less broken than uncomfortable with our own complexity. We all have darkness inside of us. Therapeutic work helps us to familiarize ourselves with the rich landscape of our life experiences. In the end, we build a set of tools to help us navigate our lives well and with integrity. In so doing, build our self-confidence and self esteem.
I am both a Licensed Clinical Social Worker and a Rinzai Zen Priest. I am committed in both fields to help others find freedom in their lives. I have extensive experience in various cognitive, emotional and somatic modalities as they relate to relationships, trauma, mood disorders and addiction. I have worked in youth facilities, addiction treatment centers, community mental health, mindfulness clinics, and most recently in private practice. Currently, about a third of my case load is couples.

name: Kendra Smith (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am trained in Gestalt therapy with a focus on Attachment theory. This means that I use curiosity to approach how you have evolved to survive in and respond to your past and current environments, including your relationships with others. Sometimes, we find that the strategies that were effective in navigating the world in the past don't meet our needs in our present day. I am here to walk alongside you as a support as you experiment with ways to respond more authentically to yourself and others as you go through your life. While my primary position is to let clients lead their own journey, I will from time to time drop in a gentle challenge or question if I see an opportunity for growth. This sounds like a serious task, and it is, but there are lots of moments for humor and levity in the counseling process.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a white, cis woman who is currently able-bodied. I am from the Pacific Northwest. I obtained my graduate degree in Professional Mental Health Counseling from Lewis and Clark's Graduate School in Education and Counseling. I have received training in Gestalt Therapy with an Attachment focus from the Gestalt Therapy Training Center Northwest. These two training backgrounds have given me a place to explore how oppression can show up at various social levels most of us interact with, and attending to those factors is a prominent part of my client work. I strive to be anti-racist, am LGBTQIA+ affirming, neurodiversity affirming, sex positive, and engage in continued training to support my ability to work with people with different identities from my own. I welcome all clients to my practice regardless of their backgrounds, what will determine if we can work together is a goodness of personality fit, and if my training supports your needs.

name: Mary Bruce-Owenby (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have a BA from Wellesley College, an MA in clinical psychology from Antioch University, couples certifications from the Developmental Couples Institute and the PACT Institute, and a deep interest in helping couples thrive. Together, through a flexible but structured process, we identify pain points in the relationship and work through them using focused experiments and experiences that are similar to the difficulties you are experiencing. Throughout the process, we are strengthening emotional safety and the couple bond.
My Approach to Helping
My approach is experiential. I am well versed in attachment, developmental, phenomenological, and psychoanalytic theory, and I believe that each person's earliest relational dynamics with their first caregiver makes a deep, formative impression on them that shows up in their relationships later in life. Buddhist Psychology, Gestalt Psychotherapy, and the psychobiological model (PACT) inform my somatic approach, which focuses couples on an awareness of their thoughts, behaviors, and emotions, without judgement.

name: Katherine Lindsay (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapeutic style is rooted in warmth, creativity, and directness. I blend affirmation with clear guidance, helping you build new, healthy dynamics in your relationships and daily life. Therapy is not just about addressing difficulties—it's about creating a space where you can grow, heal, and discover a stronger version of yourself. I believe deeply in the power of therapy to transform lives, and my approach reflects that belief. Together, we will work to help you understand your past, navigate your present, and build the future you deserve.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have worked in the behavioral health field since 2014. I have a Master of Social Work from Portland State University and a Master of Global Public Health from New York University. I also hold a Certified Drug and Alcohol Counselor in Oregon. I am a mother of four, wife, and co-parent. I love cooking, travel, dancing, and raising guide-dogs. I am often accomponied by a guide-dog-puppy in training. I am currently raising a Golden Retriever named Clara and a yellow lab named Vada.

name: Gregory Kaplan (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We will go beyond relieving symptoms to unpack the sources of your life's pain and purpose. I listen attentively, with curiosity and no judgment, allowing your experience to unfold so we can sense into its uniqueness. I help you revise outdated patterns and repair old wounds that break through coping mechanisms and defenses into symptoms such as anxiety, depression, uncontrollable anger, toxic shame, unresolved grief, mood swings, and insecure attachments. You can adjust behaviors, feelings, and thoughts that were once adaptive and are no longer. You will rediscover flexibility and adaptability, resilience and grace—appreciating how to track and shift emotional states, what to say to a loved one, why to connect more deeply with or cut someone off (e.g., an abusive relationship), etc. I help adults, adolescents, families, and groups. By dismantling oppression together, we can hear ancestral voices and unpack intergenerational trauma that resonates in us all collectively.
My Values as a Therapist
My orientation to counseling is psychodynamic, feminist, multicultural, anti-oppression, neuroscience-aware, and trauma-informed. Before counseling, I had careers in academia and book publishing that prepared me to nurture people's growth in an adaptive way. My academic focus on philosophy of religion encouraged me to explore how people have made very different senses of their lives in the world. I experience my own privilege as a White upper-middle-class assigned-male-at-birth. I identify as a member of non-Zionist Jewish ethnicity and the LGBTQIA+ community. I am educated in psychodynamic psychotherapy and trained in EMDR, somatic therapy, group work, trauma recovery, anger management, White anti-racism, and Internal Family Systems. I earned a PhD in Comparative Religious Studies from Stanford University and a MA in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from the Family Institute at Northwestern University. I serve as adjunct faculty at Lewis & Clark.

name: Courageous Couples Counseling (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Welcome to Courageous Couples Counseling, where I blend the Gottman Method and the Psychobiological Approach to Couples Therapy (PACT) to help you build stronger, more resilient relationships. With the Gottman Method, we focus on enhancing communication, deepening emotional connection, and resolving conflicts effectively. PACT adds a rich understanding of attachment theory, neuroscience, and arousal regulation to address underlying patterns and create lasting change. Together, these approaches provide a comprehensive, compassionate framework for navigating challenges and fostering intimacy. Whether you're facing recurring issues or seeking to enhance your bond, I am here to support you in creating a fulfilling and enduring partnership. I also work well with couples exploring ENM, Polyamory, and Kink dynmaics in thier relationships.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I embrace all identities and relationship structures. As a queer therapist, I love working with other members of the LGBTQIA+ family and provide a broader perspective to my many and varied heteronormative couples. I believe that the human experience is both universal and highly individual. My primary values are kindness and transparency. I play an active role and bring my humanity into my sessions with clients. Most of my clients rarely leave therapy thinking "I wonder what Kevin is thinking". I love personal developlement and I'm a thearpy nerd. You can often find me outside of work reading a new personal development book in the Rose Test Garden or walking my dog Quentin with my partner Spencer. I love checking out the food scene here in Portland or the occational night out dancing.

name: Nadya Mirza (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Every client has a unique and valuable story that offers a glimpse into their journey through life. It is a privilege for me to listen to and to try to understand these experiences, whatever they may be – stress, anxiety, depression, struggle with finding one's identity, trauma, shame, or difficulty with relationships. As you make important decisions in navigating those challenges, I would be honored to support you in exploring your inner strengths and finding additional resources that foster hope, resilience, and a sense of purpose, including but not limited to family bonds, community, meaningful activities, past accomplishments, and spirituality.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Life is full right now, but I love every minute of it. I enjoy spending time with my family and pets, traveling, camping, playing board games, listening to audiobooks, and cross-stitching in my free time.

name: Xan Scott (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Xan has multiple degrees in Theatre Arts and yoga and has decades of experience working in home caregiving. Working with various populations like incarcerated women, domestic violence, and vulnerable youth Xan has life experience that you cannot go to school for. Through studying the arts Xan knows the power firsthand of sharing your story. Therapy sessions with the right fit of client to counselor can be full of fun and emotional exploration. Sharing stories of deep-seated secrets frees our internal pressure of "doing things right" and we can start showing up for ourselves including regulating our bodies and nervous systems. Xan herself experienced wanting to be seen as "normal," whatever "normal" is since she can remember. Everyone is unique and deserves to be listened to deeply in a judgment-free space.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I became a counselor after years of fellow students and clients generously sharing all of their grief and heartache on the stage. Looking around the room at my fellow teachers and performers I thought to myself, "Who is in charge here?" How does the healing continue after they leave the stage, workshop, and classroom? I am a firm believer in arts in education and the power of play! In a world with building pressure and high expectations, we need more time to slow down and connect with ourselves and others. It is easy to become isolated and stuck in an anxiety/depression cycle and realize that we are not living but merely surviving and watching time go by. I truly love helping people learn to find what they love to do that involves community. Learning to love yourself and find more compassion and curiosity is the recipe for success. Xan lives with her adopted shelter cat Merlin who teaches her daily the power of love can heal the most traumatic circumstances.

name: Elizabeth (Liz) Ash (THEY/ SHE)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I like to start with a building a trusting relationship/therapeutic alliance where your voice and opinions on problems and care are equally important in the room. My approach is person-centered, meaning we focus on you as an individual. Other ways we pursue understanding is to explore with intersectional feminist theory, which considers how different aspects of your identity (like gender, race, and class) affect your experiences and nature-based techniques (using the healing power of nature). Other conversations we might have would involve Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) which encourages accepting your thoughts and feelings, Motivational Interviewing which helps you find the motivation to change, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) which helps change negative thought patterns. I highly encourage bibliotherapy which involves reading/listening to materials to supplement the changes being made.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Before I became a therapist, I know when I needed help that I needed someone to sit through the storm of it with me, to accept me a human being (flaws and all), and to listen to me carefully. My hope to is to hold this understanding with you and continue exploring the infinite realm that is the human experience. My values now are to facilitate the space for you to establish your understanding of your whole self and to see what creative paths it will take to create the life you desire.

name: KZ Therapy
approaches:
Our Background and Approach
Our team's diverse training and experience uniquely position us to support clients from many walks of life, searching for a better way forward. We offer in-person and virtual options. To find out more about members of the KZ Therapy team please visit us at kztherapy.com/meet-the-team and send us a note. There, you will find information about Katherine, Jake, Evelyn, Keli, Sean, Rory, Natalie, and Nate that will help you determine the best therapeutic fit for you. You will also find actually pictures of us - unfortunately, we are not a cute puppy - that's Pippa, one of our emotional support dogs.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Our sincere hope is to help you find ways to heal and learn from the past, find peace and contentment in the present, and embrace the future with optimism. We strive to take care of ourselves and each other so we can approach clients with curiosity, humility, compassion, and enough wisdom to know there is always more to learn. We look forward to hearing from you!

name: David Ju (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background
My journey to becoming a therapist has been deeply shaped by my own transformative experiences as a client. With an M.D. and engineering degree, I navigated various careers while battling worsening anxiety and depression until a wise and compassionate counselor helped me understand the impact of my childhood traumas, accept my true feelings and needs, and stop living for others. Since then, as a therapist, I have had the honor to offer the same support and guidance to others, helping them discover and embrace their true selves, break free and heal from trauma, and experience a life that is no longer about surviving but living authentically.
My Approach to Helping
My practice draws from a variety of approaches—such as person-centered, psychodynamic, neuroscience, trauma-informed, and culturally sensitive modalities—but at the heart of my work is a deep and sincere respect for your personal journey. I am here to walk alongside you as you navigate the distinct complexities and challenges of your life. Together, we will explore your deepest thoughts and feelings to foster a greater understanding of yourself within your unique circumstances. We will cultivate self-compassion and acceptance, learning to see and love yourself even amidst difficult challenges and suffering. We will work to gradually break free from the shackles of past wounds and traumas and reclaim what they have painfully stolen from you, finding peace and becoming whole. This journey toward self-discovery, freedom, and wholeness is often not an easy one, but I'm here to support and guide you every step of the way.

name: Aletha Eastwood (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have been a Certified Hakomi Practitioner since 2013. I invite you into a place of curiosity and nonjudgment about your experience. Using mindfulness and experiential approaches drawn from attachment theory, Hakomi, AEDP, EFT, and interpersonal neurobiology, we can identify and untangle old patterns of behavior and process unhelpful beliefs and old or ongoing wounding or trauma. These old patterns and beliefs are based in our experiences of the world, often from our earliest experiences in childhood or through trauma. Over time, they continue to function and can create a barrier to living a more satisfying life. In our work together, I offer a safe space to explore and heal patterns that are no longer working for you and to help you uncover and move in alignment with your authentic self. Our relationship becomes a foundation for exploration, acceptance, and growth from which you learn new ways to create greater fulfillment and connection with yourself, others, and your world.
My Values as a Therapist
I see therapy as a relational process. Hakomi is a practice of active, deep listening that invites you, as the client, to experience your own inherent wisdom. With my presence and guidance, I help you to anchor into this wisdom. Holding an attachment-based lens and recognizing how trauma disrupts our natural attachment system, I adjust and attune when difficult feelings, thoughts, and experiences arise. I help you to find your deeper connection to yourself once again. From Hakomi and my Buddhist meditation practice and studies, I believe that even the parts of ourselves we would rather get rid of have wisdom. No part of our experience is without wisdom. It is in the listening that we discover what is needing to be heard. In my work with couples, I draw on Nonviolent Communication and Emotion Focused Therapy to strengthen communication skills and uncover rutted patterns that have become barriers to intimacy and connection.

name: Kaia Stamiris (THEY/SHE)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My work blends Person-Centered Therapy, Internal Family Systems (IFS), Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), and mindfulness techniques to help clients increase awareness of patterns and inner dynamics that may be holding them back, and increase motivation to intervene in those patterns to create new outcomes. Relational boundaries, self-compassion, and cognitive flexibility are topics that frequently arise in my sessions. I hold a Master of Arts in Professional Mental Health Counseling from Lewis and Clark College (2020), and a BA in Psychology from the University of Oregon (2003). I have done extra study and training in ACT and mindfulness skills, and I have completed the official Level 1 training in IFS.
My Values as a Therapist
I’m grateful to live in a time where work is being done to de-stigmatize mental health conditions, as they have long been a natural part of the human condition. Shame, self-judgement, and comparative thinking regarding inevitable inner experiences like anxiety and negative mood states are habits of mind that can be unlearned. I am aware of the privileges afforded by my white, able body and am committed to furthering my awareness of systematic oppression.

name: Nicole McDonald (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
My approach to counseling is relational, client centered and non-judgmental. I can be with you in hard things, offer my presence, and be an active listener and thoughtful questioner. I’m invested in building a trusting relationship with you and helping you to explore.
Why I Became a Therapist
I was born and raised in Utah and coming out as a queer person surrounded by Mormon culture I often felt repressed and like I didn’t belong. Because of my queerness, introversion, and depression, I felt I could never be accepted as I was. However, through therapy, finding queer community, and the growth and connection that I experienced in school, I found that there was a place I belonged and a place where I could be known. I know the difference it has made in my life to have compassionate and supportive mentors and counselors and I'm grateful to have the opportunity to now be in that role for others who need support.

name: Peter Ragen (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that participating in therapy is collaborating in a safe and accepting space where you can learn practical skills that facilitate meaningful, long-lasting positive change in yourself and your relationships. I believe that it is through a process of self-discovery and practice that we are able to empower ourselves to skillfully make choices that lead us toward our goals. Through a greater awareness of ourselves and how we relate to the world, we can begin to make small but impactful changes that compound over time. I practice from an integrated lens, meaning I pull from multiple therapeutic approaches to best address your particular therapeutic needs. As someone who has grappled with my own mental health struggles, I know that change is possible but not always easy. I also know that when we take responsibility, hold ourselves accountable, and do the good work, we can achieve our goals. If you or a loved one are ready to begin this journey, reach out!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My fiance and I are Oregon natives, and we live in Portland with our two dogs. When I’m not in session, I enjoy spending time with my significant other, exploring the city’s rich foodie culture, walking our dogs, and skiing.

name: Rebecca Brown Schroeder (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to helping is holistic, compassion-focused, nature-based and experiential, including animal-assisted therapeutic activities. I have specialized training in Child Parent Psychotherapy - compassionately addressing complex issues impacting young children and their caregivers. I also specialize in treating youth and adults experiencing grief, loss, trauma, chronic and acute illness/pain, mood disorders and neurodivergence. My life experience with parenthood, grief/loss and caregiving for aging parents inform my practice and my connection with clients. I continuously cultivate a special interest in supporting grievers including those experiencing traumatic grief, end of life, and eco-grief. I have experience in supporting young grievers and their caregivers through my work at The Dougy Center for Grieving Children and Families, a place with so much heart and deep understanding.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I understand that play is a window into the internal world of the child and that holistic healing occurs across the lifespan in emotionally attuned relationships. I am passionate about the power of nature to awaken us to the present moment and spark the mind-body's innate capacity to heal. I believe that experiential and animal-assisted therapies are especially effective at deepening each client's awareness and understanding of themselves, their relationships and their environment. I have worked previously as a licensed special education teacher, a parent support coach, and an education policy advocate. I hold a B.S. in Human Development and Social Policy from Northwestern University and advanced degrees in Public Policy, Special Education, and Professional Mental Health Counseling. I am honored to walk alongside my clients in open-hearted connection as they grow toward healing, wholeness and vitality. I look forward to meeting you.

name: Michael Ianello (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I help clients reduce their anxiety and develop strategies to reduce suffering. I am direct, with a light touch, to ensure both comfort and authentic connection are achieved in every session. Through the development of renewed vitality, curiosity, and joy, my clients rediscover core life values; learn and develop concrete skills to find relief from sticky or painful thoughts; cultivate empathy; connect compassionately, and deeply, with their own true heart. I have worked in the developmental and mental health fields as an educator, researcher, and counselor for over 15 years. I have a bachelor's degree in Psychology and a Master's degree in Professional Mental Health Counseling. I have received professional training in DBT, ACT, and mindfulness based behavioral interventions as well as depth focused, process oriented modalities.
My Values as a Therapist
The core values of my practice are: safety, honesty, and curiosity. A feeling of safety and confidentiality is paramount to the profound work of self-discovery that occurs in a therapeutic practice. I seek to model honesty, directness, and vulnerability with clients so that they can experience the unique freedoms that arise from honest and open dialogue. Cultivating curiosity can provide relief from rote ways of thinking or perceiving. A simple practice of reframing an issue, might provide tremendous relief from unnecessary suffering. I value diversity and strive to treat all clients with dignity and respect, no matter their beliefs or background. I also commit my time away from work to health care reform and community mental health outreach with the hopes that quality mental health care is no longer out of anyone's reach due to social, cultural, or financial position

name: Grace Cantor (SHE, HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My style is warm and collaborative. As an existential-humanistic counselor, I believe that exploring meaning and authentic self-expression contribute to fulfillment and a sense of well-being. My intention is to facilitate a process where clients can receive support and mindfully address whatever aspects of their life may need attention and healing. I have extensive training in issues related to trauma, attachment and sexuality. I am passionate about creating a space where folks can ultimately feel empowered.
More About the People We Treat
I became a counselor because growing up I struggled with anxiety and depression and counseling helped me tremendously. I believe in the transformative effect that counseling can have on people's lives. I consider it a great honor and privilege to get to do this work. Getting to witness others on this journey of healing and transformation in whatever form it may take is what makes me so dedicated to this work.

name: Andrew Friede
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use structured counseling and the latest medications (including Suboxone). Together we will find practical ways to enhance mindfulness, sound sleep, nutrition, and exercise. If you already have a counselor, I can offer medication-management in collaboration with them. I only see patients in-person (no video or phone calls), ages 16+. Please find more details at Bluestone-Clinic.com.
My Approach to Helping
I treat the whole person because psychological pain has its origins in the complex interactions of personal and cultural history, physical health, individual neurochemistry, and life struggles. Together we will help you find meaning and peace in the context of complex family issues, your own history, and work and school challenges. Not everyone needs medication, but if you do, we identify medications for anxiety, depression, bipolar disorder, ADHD, personality disorders, PTSD, addictions, and weight management. You may have found that psychiatric medications either do not help or have bothersome side effects. However, these issues can be caused by drug or alcohol use, sleep deprivation, or untreated medical conditions. I will help you sort this out. Sleep, exercise, nutrition, and mindfulness have a profound impact on mood, productivity, addictions, and relationships. We will help you find ways to sleep and eat better, and learn relaxation techniques, including meditation.

name: Meg Farrell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to psychiatric and medication management services is holistic in nature and explore all aspects of lifestyle when determining treatment planning. When appropriate, I apply cognitive behavioral and dialectical behavioral therapy skills to medication management services. I offer a conservative approach to prescribing psychotropics; however, I offer information and resources for alternative forms of treatment, including ketamine and TMS.

name: Lisa Sacker (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Similarly to my choice of approach, the ultimate objective of our time together will differ between individuals, and we can work together to identify your personal goals. I have experience in providing counseling, assessments, and crisis intervention to individuals struggling with mental health, trauma, and addiction issues. I am well versed in the area of trauma and I bring an understanding of the effects of trauma into my work for the therapeutic benefit of my clients. Eye Movement Desensitization Reprocessing (EMDR) is just one healing modality I can utilize with clients.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My professional experiences have given me a deep understanding of mental health, trauma, addiction issues and their impact on clients. The attitude I bring to my work conveys both dignity and respect and I hold the belief that people can change. My goal is to create a safe space in which clients can explore their life narrative and navigate moving forward.

name: Kat Aguilar (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
There are many ways to experience life, and everyone's journey is different. I hope to grow our therapeutic relationship from a place of openness, trust, gentleness, and compassion. I take a collaborative approach towards counseling, blending techniques to best serve each person and welcoming feedback to increase trust and collaboration. I integrate several orientations to achieve a well-rounded perspective to address and explore the stressors that life has brought onto you. I am happy to meet with you for a free consultation to discuss your goals and determine if I am a good fit for your needs.

name: Jeffrey C. Peyton
approaches:
My goal is to support you and your teen identify and minimize self-limiting patterns that no longer work, while accessing and encouraging innate strengths and resilience. My approach is based on respect and compassion, while drawing on tools of mindfulness practices, family systems, adolescent development and other evidence-based practices. I am an experienced addictions counselor, and view substance use as only one aspect of an individual. With compassion and encouragement, I support my clients to restore the life balance they seek. A professional symphony musician, I also specialize in the management of performance and social anxiety with musicians, dancers, actors and in daily life.
I have assisted hundreds of adolescents through academic challenges, anxiety, depression, emotional dysregulation, substance use, ADHD, traumatic experiences, social anxiety and divorce. While I specialize in working with adolescents, I enjoy working with individuals of all ages, with families, and with single and coupled parents navigating the challenges and rewards of parenting. I hold a Masters degree in Counseling Psychology from Prescott College, with additional professional training in addiction therapy, EMDR and the treatment of trauma, and hold extensive experience in parent coaching. Prior to opening my private practice, I served as a lead wilderness therapist for Catherine Freer Wilderness Therapy Programs, and continue to work with adolescents and families returning home from residential treatment. In addition to my counseling practice, I am a masters degree graduate of the Juilliard School of Music and perform professionally as a percussionist and conductor.

name: The Portland Attachment & Trauma Healing Center The PATH Center
approaches:
What We Specialize In
We provide cutting-edge mental health related services to those seeking a way to walk the PATH to a more balanced, peaceful and happy life.
Our Personal Beliefs and Interests
Most of us think of trauma in terms of losing a loved one, being in an accident, natural disasters and assault. Trauma includes but is not limited to being raised by parents who were emotionally, verbally and or physically abusive, mentally ill, physically and or emotionally neglectful, incarcerated, had dependency concerns, physically or emotionally abandoned you, and any type of sexual abuse. People are resilient and many of us have some of the above mentioned traumas in our history. We are still able to create and keep long term intimate loving relationships. However if you have several of the above mentioned concerns you may be struggling in the areas of interpersonal relationships, job performance and play (your down time).

name: Casey Campbell, LPC (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I approach my work with five things in mind. I strive to develop a strong therapeutic relationship with you. I meet you where you are in life, welcoming you to bring your authentic self to session. I help you identify and clarify problems and challenges. I help you build and hone new skills, patterns of thought, and ways of being. Finally, we bring it all together so you can solve problems and make meaningful and lasting changes. I find value in using a variety of counseling theories and techniques, but specialize in Internal Family Systems (IFS). Through an IFS lens, I'll help you get to know your internal world, specifically your core self and your parts. I also utilize cognitive behavioral, and existential modalities in my practice.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have studied psychology and counseling since 1993. I earned a Bachelors Degree in psychology in 2001, a Masters degree in psychology in 2006, a Masters degree in Counseling in 2013. I have been a therapist for over ten years and am currently a Licensed Professional Counselor.
