I’ve focused my scholarly and professional interests on self-conscious emotions such as shame and guilt, anxiety, depression, chronic illness and disability, academic success, career exploration and decision making, human creativity and problem solving, and positive psychology and optimal experience.

name: Lisa Headings (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
Words are powerful—but sometimes they’re not enough. Expressive arts therapy weaves together visual art, movement, writing, and creative exploration to help you process emotions, reduce stress, and rediscover joy. No art skills required—just curiosity and a willingness to explore. Therapy with me is deep, playful, and liberating. We quiet the overthinking brain, tap into your inner creativity, and uncover what’s been keeping you stuck. My approach is nonjudgmental and intuitive. Together, we create a safe, non-clinical space where you can show up fully and reconnect with who you truly are.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I know what it’s like to feel like you don’t quite fit in—I’m a lifelong, card-carrying member of the Island of Misfit Toys. That’s why I create space for honest, imperfect expression—where you can show up exactly as you are and feel seen. If talk therapy hasn't quite clicked, expressive arts therapy offers a creative, intuitive path to healing. You don’t need to be “good at art”—just open to exploring. I work best with deep feelers, creatives, and those ready to ditch the script and find themselves again.

name: C.J. Sanders (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
Finding your person is only the beginning—learning how to love your person is a lifelong endeavor. I offer research based skill building and interactive dialogue on topics like conflict + communication, finances + household matters, sex + intimacy, bonding + friendship. We also go deeper into how to navigate differences, form a secure attachment, and address concerns that you may have. After over a decade of working with couples, I have the inside track to help you learn what you didn't know you needed to know! Most couples come to premarital or exploring commitment counseling not really sure what they should talk about. This is why my guided program makes sense! I have compiled what I think are the best ideas, skills, and tools for modern romantic relationships. You and your partner get to explore and grow together in collaboration with an experienced relationship therapist!
My Approach to Helping
This is a fun and meaningful process that will help you feel more confident and connected in your partnership! You will build on your strengths as a team and develop ways to impact your challenges. You will get a resource toolkit that contains what you learn in each session as well as suggestions for further exploration to keep you two growing together.

name: Autumn Farrara (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My work is informed by IFS, Attachment theory, EFT, and Relational Life Therapy created by Terry Real.

name: Tyne Zewadski Clifton (THEY/SHE)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
You know you want to make a change, and that desire scares you. I’ve been there! Even small changes can be hard for the little parts of our animal selves that adore consistency. These parts hope to protect us by sticking to familiar routines– even painful or exhausting routines can feel “more safe” to these protective parts than the uncertainty of transformation. Changes we desire within our most intimate relationships often feel especially scary, because these relationships are so meaningful to us! I support my clients in developing deep and healing relationships with the protective parts of themselves that come forward in times of challenge. As we learn to work with our internal protectors, we move through change with greater ease and become more confident in choosing and enacting our desired lives. My approach to therapy creates space through which vulnerability and change may enter our lives with the permission of the protective gatekeepers guarding the path to transformation!
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a therapist to help others facilitate the type of radical change I have been able to enact in my life with therapeutic support! I deconstructed the roots of my panic attacks, disarming them at the source. I learned to understand and communicate my needs, and assert boundaries in relationships. I’ve built a secure, supportive relationship with my spouse, and discovered the deep joy of gender euphoria. I feel drawn to supporting those seeking this type of transformation in their lives. Whether that’s unmasking as a neurodivergent person, opening your relationship, navigating a faith deconstruction, or exploring your gender. I bring a radically curious and always-compassionate energy into this work, focusing on supporting you in creating sustainable paths to enact the change you seek. I’m eager to collaborate with you so that you can: embrace your authentic, multifaceted self; find confidence in what you want & who you are; have more satisfying relationships with your people!

name: Naomi Baker, LMFT (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My intention is to create a collaborative, safe and empathic space to explore thoughts, feelings, musings, dreams and behaviors to promote change and healing. I believe that we all have the capacity to repair, grow, and transform our human experiences.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a neurodivergent, wounded healer with a love for writing, stories of all kinds, and walking. I am also a single cat lady and have an interest in web design and coding. I deepen my feminism, antiracism, understanding of the human condition, and insights into the evolution of our species through my work with diverse clients. I am consistently impressed by the strength and intelligence of people from all backgrounds and continually amazed by our resilience and capacity for growth and healing after trauma. In my opinion, post traumatic growth might include the wisdom we need to change the old western stories that have led to the planetary crisis we are currently facing.

name: Jeri Parks (NONE; JERI/JERI'S)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Intentional change includes acceptance, respect, and gratitude for adaptive psychological and behavioral patterns. Resistance to resistance only multiplies tension. I will accompany you in cultivating clarity and choice about the ways you meet needs for survival, safety, belonging, and love, through presence and compassion.
My Approach to Helping
My holistic power-with approach to therapy is founded upon my background and training in Hakomi mindful somatic therapy, Mindful Compassionate Dialogue, and Vipassana meditation. Access and re-inform the implicit material that shapes present experiences and relationships, and find liberation from chronic patterns. Develop a vocabulary for integrating experiences and self-responsible expression. Gain competence with concrete relational skills like life-serving boundaries, self-empathy, and working with reactivity. Understand interpersonal systems and patterns, and develop the fundamental framework and skills necessary for effective, satisfying communication.

name: Kirby Orr (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer medication management and psychological testing for adults struggling with mental health conditions. As a Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner with 20 years of experience in mental health, I specialize in depression, anxiety, ADHD, trauma, and substance use disorders. My approach focuses on evidence-based treatment.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
If you're feeling overwhelmed by mental health challenges or uncertain about your diagnosis, know that seeking help is a brave first step. I understand how difficult it can be to find the right treatment approach. With two decades of experience, I offer a supportive, judgment-free environment where we can work together toward your wellness goals.

name: Rani Gupta (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
My clients are sensitive millennial professionals who often describe the following to me: 1) Even though they're making a great salary, they often wonder if they're in the right job. They notice their bosses or co-workers willing to be cutthroat, and that's just not how they want to be, 2) They swore they wouldn't become workaholics, because that was never aligned with their values, but here they are...and it's been months since they've hung out with friends, been on a date, or had a relaxing day to themselves, OR 3) They feel disconnected from their family, not just because they haven't seen them in a while, but because they have a gut feeling something intense or traumatic happened to their parents, grandparents, or somewhere else in their lineage *Are you ready for a change? I'm now also offering a 10-week accelerated 1:1 therapy experience. Visit my website for more information about my practice and to schedule your free 15-minute video meet-and-greet!*
Why I Became a Therapist
My training in EMDR, Flash Technique, and somatic parts work means I have the expertise you need and deserve when processing work stress, generational wounds, and healing your relationships. But at the end of the day, you want to know you’re working with someone who gets what it means to be an ambitious, sensitive-hearted young professional struggling with "doing it all", right? I get it because I’ve lived it myself. I know that sometimes you wonder, “Who am I outside of this job?” I know you grapple with trying to have hobbies again. So when we work together, you won’t just get a trained therapist. You’ll get someone who truly understands what you’re going through.

name: Anchi Howitz (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My mission is to create a collaborative environment, grounded in warmth, authenticity, and compassion, nurturing your innate abilities to heal and grow. Throughout life’s journey, unhelpful patterns, cultural messages, and oppressive systems may have promoted disconnection within yourself, in your relationships, with community, and to the Earth. I believe that through reflection, insight, and deepening connections both internally and externally, we can achieve healing, liberation, and expanded possibilities. I am currently finishing my Master's Degree in the Marriage, Family, and Couple Therapy program at Lewis & Clark Graduate School. In addition, I am completing a specialty certification in Ecopsychology. I primarily draw from Narrative Therapy, Emotionally Focused Therapy, and Ecotherapy. Attending therapy is a vulnerable and courageous act, and I feel honored to be able to hold space for you as we embark on this journey.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a biracial Asian-American woman, I have observed how my intersecting identities have shaped relational power dynamics. I bring a systemic perspective, which takes into consideration issues of equity and justice. I value and look forward to working with individuals and families of all identities and configurations, including the LGBTQIA+ and BIPOC communities. I graduated from Cornell University's school of Hospitality Administration, and my first career was in luxury spa management. Quickly I realized that I wanted to step away from the management side and have a more intimate role in people's healing. I spent ten years after this working as a Licensed Massage Therapist. In this time, I also co-founded a farmed animal sanctuary. Currently as a therapist, I see that I have always been drawn to work that is about healing, empathy, and connection. I am honored to do this work.

name: Terra Marzano (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Attachment-oriented, I draw from training in psychodynamic theory to promote resilience, adaptation and emotional freedom. My work is not centered on symptom-reduction, but is focused more on getting to the roots of your turmoil and transforming your relationship to yourself in order to find relief. I bring mindfulness and body-based awareness into nearly every session, trusting and honoring somatic wisdom. I am trained in EMDR with advanced training in Flash Technique, both of which are effective via video visits.
My Values as a Therapist
I am diligent in my efforts to be trauma-responsive, gender and identity-affirming, and anti-racist. I interrogate my own biases and participate monthly in individual and group consultation to ensure my work is consistent with the values I hold as a person and as a professional social worker. I strive for the day where all of us can celebrate seeking care for our whole selves, body, mind and spirit.

name: Josh Wilson (HE / HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
For the first twenty years of my career, I was a creative writing professor at universities where I helped students tell their stories. After earning advanced degrees in creative writing, film studies, and a doctorate in English, I retrained in couple and family therapy to pursue my long-held goal of becoming a therapist. My experience, humor, patience, and insight will help you to identify new plot lines in your story, reaffirm your sense of purpose, and strengthen bonds between you and your loved ones. My therapeutic approach with couples integrates Emotionally Focused Couple Therapy (EFCT) and Gottman Research Methods. With individuals, I draw on Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT), Internal Family Systems (IFS), as well as a psychodynamic approach to better understand how your family of origin and upbringing are impacting you now. With families, I utilize an approach that integrates Emotionally Focused Family Therapy (EFFT) and Milan Systemic Family Therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I love to help couples, families, and individuals catalyze their strengths, remove obstacles, face new challenges, and tap into their resilience. In short, I can help you to summon your inner resources to change. Because I spent many years abroad--in Italy, Spain, South Africa, Turkey, Slovakia, and Ireland--I continue to draw on a wealth of experience working with people of diverse backgrounds, identities, traditions, and perspectives. Finding the right fit in a therapist is essential to forging a healing relationship. If it seems like I could be the right therapist for you, I hope you'll take the next step to reach out for a free 15-minute consultation, where we'll explore what you're seeking help with. Currently, I'm available to see couples, clients in polyamorous relationships, families, and adult individuals.

name: Jonathan Penley
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have a strong background in psychotherapy research at both the undergraduate and graduate level, focusing on attachment, trauma, psychiatric interventions, brain imaging, and self-help interventions. I believe most mental health symptoms originate from early attachment and stressful events, and that revisiting these early life experiences helps people develop more adaptive emotional and cognitive responses to present-day stressors. I specialize in the evidence-based therapies of Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing, Lifespan Integration, Group Therapy, and Neurofeedback for adults. Most of my clients enjoy the eclectic elements of these non-linear right brain therapies, ranging from brief exposure, insightfulness, mind-body awareness, and the unconscious. My approach is a combination of lightheartedness and kindness, alongside being directive and honest.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In my spare time, I enjoy spending time with my partner, pup, family, friends, listening to audiobooks, keeping up with the news, learning, whole-food plant-based cuisine, being physically active, helping my dad restore a vintage car, attempting to forage for mushrooms, and being an imperfect "good enough" human. My most recommended "pop-psy" books related to trauma and stress: "The Body Keeps the Score" by Bessel van der Kolk; "Healing the Shame that Binds You" by John Bradshaw; "Stop Walking on Eggshells" by Paul T Mason and Randi Kreger; "Waking Up" by Sam Harris; "Full Catastrophe Living" by Jon Kabat-Zinn, and many books by Gabor Maté, Julie and John Gottman, and Irvin Yalom.

name: Niki Adams (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a therapist, I strive to create a welcoming environment for all that walk through the door. I aim for the client to feel like the expert in the room, as they are the person that understands themselves the best. I utilize feminist, multicultural, and affirming approaches to help one better understand themselves and how they deal with day-to-day life. During therapy sessions, I enjoy discussing values and how their unique experiences. I like bringing cultural context into the room, as we are not living in a vacuum chamber and the world around us impacts us every day. My therapeutic approach is often through a multicultural lens, meaning that I take each person’s unique experiences and culture into account when coming up with a holistic view of the individual.

name: Sarah Dykes
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Whether you are new to counseling or have experience in therapy, I provide a safe, judgment-free space to explore your inner experience and truth. I use experiential therapeutic tools to sift through the layers. My approach is integrative, humanistic, and strengths-based.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In 2017, I finished my Master’s Degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling at Portland State University. My experience included a yearlong internship at William Temple House working with diverse individuals in transition. As a Registered LPC Intern in the State of Oregon, I am under the supervision of Karen Hixson, PhD, LPC. I finished training as a massage therapist at the Oregon School of Massage in 2003, and practiced for seven years. This experience continues to inform my understanding of how we carry our lived experience in our bodies. I completed a B.A. in Human Services from the University of Oregon in 1993, and for the next twenty-five years worked with children, youth, families and adults in community based organizations, workforce development, and higher education in Portland, Oregon.

name: Haley Collins (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I am dedicated to the mission of partnering with patients to improve their quality of life. We promise to be there for you every step of your journey. Our goal is to create a healthy and reliable patient and professional partnership in which is the heart of our organization. We are committed to providing high quality care. Let’s us work together to get you feeling your best!

name: Jordan Curtis (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
In 2019, I began to study psychotherapy in order to pursue my goals of providing self-empowering holistic services. I graduated from the two-year Hakomi training at the META (Mindfulness Experiential Therapy Approaches) Institute in 2021 and from the University of the Cumberlands Masters Degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling in 2023. From 2022-2023 I studied emotionally focused therapy (EFT) with Dr. Sue Johnson and began seeing couples in clinical practice at Liberation Institute. My approach to psychotherapy is first and foremost: *Relational & connected *Attentive *Collaborative *Humanistic *Person-Centered *Empathic *Compassionate *Validating *Experiential *Attachment theory-based *Trauma-informed *Focused on changing root-cause neurobiological & relational systems (rather than focusing on symptom management) ...and also: *Strengths-based *Empowering *Encouraging *Non-pathologizing *Grounded in scientific research & theory (particularly neuroscience) *Holistic & Somatic
Why I Became a Therapist
In 2015, while taking time off to recover from a work injury, I went on my first 10-day meditation retreat. During this meditation retreat, I discovered the profound connection between body and mind, and the healing power of mindfulness. Further experiences have led me to pursue my deep fascination with psychosomatic processes and the power of relational & psychological healing in psychotherapy. In 2019 I began transitioning out of nursing and started training in somatic therapy. While nursing has been a valuable experience (helping me with clients navigating medical systems, medical trauma, & death), I have found that I have the greatest impact on client wellness through psychotherapy. In the 90s, the US Public Health Service published that 7 out of 10 of the top health risks in the US were rooted not in biology, but in behavioral choices. This understanding, that psychology underlies both physical and mental wellness, has greatly inspired my choice to become a therapist.

name: Wyatt Okeefe (HE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As an experienced therapist my primary goal is to empower my clients to live their most authentic and fulfilling lives. I employ an existential approach, emphasizing the importance of finding meaning in one's daily activities and relationships as essential to thriving. Through our collaborative work, I guide my clients in exploring their identities, values, and beliefs about themselves and others, and how these factors shape their interactions and satisfaction with life. My role is to provide the tools and support my clients need to assert themselves with confidence and self-awareness. By delving into these core aspects of their personal experience, my clients are able to develop a stronger sense of self-understanding and agency, ultimately enabling them to live more purposeful and enriching lives.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I understand the impact of transphobia, homophobia and misogyny on our mental health and the challenges we face as a community. I know the anxiety and fear we face is real and I also know we can persevere and still experience the joy of living authentically in spite of it. I strive to create a safe, validating space where clients can explore their thoughts, feelings, and personal journeys with authenticity. Through an integrative, client-centered approach, I collaboratively develop tailored strategies for managing concerns like depression, anxiety, and trauma. This may involve evidence-based therapies combined with holistic, identity-affirming practices. Underlying my work is a commitment to empowering LGBTQIA+ clients to live their most authentic, fulfilling lives. I believe self-acceptance and a strong sense of identity are essential to thriving, and I help guide clients in cultivating these qualities. Reach out, I'm here to support you.

name: Kip Williams (HE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Mindfulness-Based Somatic Therapy combines traditional mindfulness practice, guided self-inquiry, and body-based awareness to foster healing, integration, and personal growth. Instead of focusing solely on solving problems and figuring things out, this approach invites you to slow down, tune into your body, and allow your experience to unfold in the present moment. By bringing mindful awareness to how your emotions show up in the body, how your nervous system responds to stress, and how past experiences shape your present reality, this approach helps you develop greater self-trust, resilience, and integration. The therapeutic relationship itself is also a key part of the healing process—providing a space of attunement, co-regulation, and relational safety that supports deep transformation. Over time, you may find yourself not just talking about change, but actually experiencing it—in how you relate to yourself, others, and the world around you.
Why I Became a Therapist
Before becoming a therapist, I spent my 20s as a movement organizer for various causes including nuclear nonproliferation, death penalty abolition, racial and economic justice, and LGBTQ+ rights. This was powerful and rewarding work in many ways, but I continued to struggle with overwhelming emotions and deeply rooted feelings of shame and disconnection. I experienced a major burnout circa 2010 that led me to turn inward and begin addressing the complex trauma and grief I had been avoiding. Through therapy, mindfulness meditation, and various somatic and spiritual practices, I developed a more grounded and compassionate relationship with myself. This path led me to pursue education and training as a therapist so I could support others on their journey of healing and transformation. This work is truly my life’s calling. I’m passionate about working with developmental, cultural, and intergenerational trauma through mindfulness, somatics, psychedelics, nature, and spirituality.

name: Jerzy Hubert
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Originally from Canada, my love of nature and the great outdoors recently brought me out to the Pacific Northwest. I have worked in a range of outpatient settings, providing psychotherapy (eg. CBT, DBT, IPT, PDT), medication management, and interventional treatments (eg. KIT, TMS) to adult patients, including treatment resistant cases. Within psychiatry, my passion is psychoanalysis and psychodynamic therapy, though I’m also interested in consciousness models, psychedelics, and neuroscience. I offer both psychodynamic therapy and medication management. I practice with an eclectic psychodynamic framework, drawing on a range of theoretical schools (eg. drive, ego, self, object relations theory) and tailoring treatment to the individual needs of the client. I also incorporate skills based approaches when necessary.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I offer cash pay and superbills for individuals seeking out of network (OON) coverage. I am in network with Regence BCBS and can arrange single case agreements for individuals with Moda Health to see me at an in-network benefit level of coverage.

name: Sarah Sumner (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that people already have exactly what they need to heal. Rather than focusing on what is lacking, I collaborate with clients to remove external constraints that block access to that power, harmonize your inner system, and build a relationship to your core self. As a result, behavior, emotions, and thoughts tend to change naturally. I strive to facilitate healing that includes both interpersonal challenges and how systems of power and privilege are affecting your livelihood. I previously provided individual and group counseling at DVRC and somatic therapy at Harlem Hospital and on interdisciplinary therapy teams. I received a Master’s degree in Clinical Social Work from Columbia University and completed IFS Level 1 from the IFS Institute. In addition to providing evidence-based practices, I’m also a trained Yoga Therapist and enjoy offering somatic and psycho-spiritual approaches bolster holistic healing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I became a therapist because even in the face of the most challenging circumstances, I believe everyone deserves the right to feel fulfilled, safe, and empowered. As a Pleasure Activist, I love to help people move from shame-based to a pleasure-centered relationship to their sexuality and see this work as integral to collective liberation. Accompanying people on their own journey of self-discovery whether that’s solo or in partnership, fills me with an immense amount of meaning and joy. I am an active ally; I’m a queer neurodivergent femme-presenting and white-bodied person who is aware of my privilege and continuously humbling myself. I look forward to bearing witness to your growth!

name: Mary Diorio (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I take a creative and collaborative approach in my practice, researching new techniques and information to help you make the changes you desire. My clinical focus areas include: relationships, anxiety, depression, eating disorders, life transitions, and stress management. I work with adolescent and adult clients. I use cognitive behavioral therapy, psychodynamic theory, and emotionally focused therapy as my main theoretical lenses.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have completed multiple years of training in Emotionally Focused Couples Therapy in Seattle, New York, and Portland. Emotionally focused Therapy developed by Dr. Leslie Greenberg, PhD, helps couples seeking greater intimacy, facilitates healing after an affair, and changes negative relational communication problems/cycles. With this approach, couples communicate their emotions effectively, create an intimate secure bond with each other, and heal from past attachment injuries. I have also studied under Dr. Susan Johnson's, PhD, Emotionally Focused Therapy Center in Seattle, Washington. I have many years of experience and training working with people struggling with eating problems and weight management. I have worked in outpatient, partial day treatment, and a hospitilazation level of care adressing complexities of eating disorders. I also work with clients who are just trying to develop healthier eating patterns and ways to cope with stress more effectively.

name: Kate Reece (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My style is down to earth, warm, and supportive. I believe my clients should control as much of their treatment as possible. I honor an individual's vision of their journey and act as a guide to help them meet their goals. I also offer challenges and strategies to help clients achieve emotional and mental wellness in ways they had not considered before.  My background is in attachment and family structural therapy. I believe individuals are deeply affected by their first attachment relationships, and the relationships they form later in life play a part in both their successes and difficulties. I utilize cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) techniques, mindfulness techniques, dialectal behavioral therapy (DBT), and motivational interviewing to promote change and healing. Therapy is a process of growth. I provide a judgment-free environment that encourages self-expression, cognitive and emotional transformation, and self-awareness.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Helping others discover peace, connection, and confidence in the face of turmoil has long been a passion of mine. I believe everyone is capable of growth and healing if they are provided sufficient encouragement, support, and effective strategies. I encourage clients to identify and communicate their unique needs and concerns in order to improve interpersonal relationships, manage bothersome emotions or behaviors, and develop healthier family connections. My background is in Family Structural Therapy, which places value on and examines an individual’s relationships, both past and present. This approach assesses the ways in which relationships shape people’s self-worth, how they interact with others, how they manage difficult experiences, and their emotional and mental health. My family is mixed race, and I work with a diverse array of clients. I am committed to serving as a support to those exploring their race, gender, and sexual identity.

name: River Delogu-Clark (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I identify as a cisgender, white, able-bodied gay woman of Mediterranean, working-class origin. I grew up around Marseille, a large port city in the South of France, of second-generation Italian/Sardinian and third-generation Spanish immigrants. I was the first person in my family to go to high school. The privilege of a free education system there allowed me to earn a Masters degree in English and my first love was literary translation. I have lived in Portland for over 20 years. Authenticity is a big part of who I am, and it is reflected in sessions. My approach is integrative and relational. I use a combination of several kinds of evidence-based therapies based on your particular needs at any given moment, blending talk-, mindfulness- and somatic-based therapies. I often incorporate specific cognitive-behavioral skills and neuroscience-based education as indicated to help you reach your goals. More info at: https://www.caravancounselingpdx.com/about
Why I am a Good Fit for You
If you are contending with: depression and anxiety; internalized shame and self-criticism, feeling like who you are or what you do isn't good enough; relational or intergenerational trauma (if you feel stuck in the same patterns, whether in your personal relationship or at work, this may be part of the issue); Marginalized identity/ies; or you've felt like a misfit for most of your life, and you feel something is holding you back; If you long to get off the hamster wheel, you long for a connection to *something* you aren't quite sure what it is, but you sense you won't find it within the box of unrestrained capitalism; If you tend to care a lot about things, and maybe you feel a bit isolated... ... I want to invite you to explore with me the patterns and fears that get in the way of reclaiming your inner essence, power and deepest purpose. This means I will also help you explore your values and strengths, so you can inhabit them fully and rewrite the narrative of who you are.

name: Marina Nelson, MC (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I strive to make the process as experiential and whole person integrative as possible to make change and healing as easy as possible. Customization and collaboration are important since you are the expert on you. When people have experienced traumas, EMDR and Sensorimotor Psychotherapy (developed from Hakomi) are effective and gentle ways to process and resolve those experiences. Even if talking about the events is difficult, they can be dealt with using mind-body integration. I am also Certified in Trauma-Informed Stabilization Therapy (an Internal Family systems {IFS} based method that incorporates mindfulness & somatic awareness, too). If you've been afraid to address the past or feel like you're just managing symptoms instead of living life fully, this work may be for you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I use many modalities to support my clients' self-healing in a gentle, self-paced manner. My training includes: * EMDR Certified Therapist * IFS (Level 1) in on-going consultation towards certification * IFS-Informed EMDR Level 3 in on-going consultation towards certification * Sensorimotor Psychotherapy Levels 1 & 2 (somatic AKA mind-body integration), * Hypnotherapy (Level 3) * Mindfulness * Trauma-Informed Stabilization Therapy Certified Therapist (an IFS-based modality that incorporates mindfulness, somatic awareness, etc) ***For the time being, all services are offered in an online format only***

name: Megan Campbell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe healing and growth can happen in an infinite amount of ways, and I believe therapy can look and feel different for everyone. There is no one size fits all for healing, growth and exploration, and this can feel both daunting and liberating. We are often born into a world that defines us before we define ourselves, which can create a sense of disconnection from who we are. Therapy can offer a space where you are given an opportunity to explore and "be with" yourself in new ways as you navigate your human journey in this world and in relationship to others. Exploring ourselves and our journey(s) in life takes courage, bravery, and openness, as we learn how to surrender deeper into our unique being-ness. If we begin by looking within, we may observe our external world shifting as a result, creating more harmony and ease in our lives, as well as in our relationship to ourselves and others.
My Values as a Therapist
I utilize a holistic lens in my work, meaning I acknowledge the complexity of what it means to be human as a mind, body, soul, and spirit. In growth, healing, and exploration, I believe it is necessary to tap into all of the layers that encompass who we are, as well as assess our relationship to the world around us. I pull from a variety of perspectives including both Eastern and Western philosophy, earth-based wisdom, experiential and existential-humanistic practices, somatic and mindfulness techniques, systemic and relational frameworks, and everyday life experience as a human being in this world alongside you.

name: Heather Ackles (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
Relationships (breakups, separation, dating, divorce) bring up anxiety, regret, fear, depression and self-judgment - effecting the relationship we have with ourselves. Resentments, anger, and guilt - toward others and within ourselves - make us feel disconnected and alone. Our therapy sessions help you define what’s missing, unpack who you are, and figure out what you want. As a therapist, my aim is to help you reconnect with yourself. Everyone is different. Our expectations and ideals are influenced by family history, cultural expectations, and unique experiences. My therapeutic 'approach' helps you develop tools to define the relationships you want - and work through anxiety, past regrets, self doubt, negative self-image, and slowly dispose of the negative loud inner-monologue (which comes up, frequently, when trying to navigate the road ahead.)
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I’ve been on all sides of relationships. I am grateful to help people strengthen connection, grow confidence, and develop the foundation of who they are and want to be. Defining our core values is challenging - and yet, needed for empowering growth. As a therapist (and human being), I value barre, spin, dance, art, and music. I value therapy as a source of connection. All humans are interesting, complex, and different - similar to my love for music: Vivaldi (Four Seasons), Otis Redding, and HAIM. I am licensed in Oregon and Washington State. Interested in reaching out? Check out my webpage - or give me a call 503-498-6879.

name: Abby Ernst (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I am here to help you weather the storm of your eating disorder, anxiety, body image distress, and/or whatever else you bring to the room. Through my own eating disorder recovery, and helping many clients find theirs, I have learned that no recovery journey is the same. Recovery is SO worth it, and I would be honored to help you navigate this process. I have experience working in both residential and outpatient eating disorder care, with queer and trans clients, and with both teens and young adults. I utilize techniques including DBT, CBT, and Narrative Therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that counseling involves a relationship of mutual respect and collaboration. I believe that clients are the experts of their own experience and the stories we tell ourselves and others impact how we see ourselves in the world. My role as a counselor is to provide a space where you can show up authentically as your whole self. I am committed to fat liberation, body neutrality, and anti-racist work and operate from a Health at Every Size lens. When you get to know me, you will learn that I love love love my job and am so honored to get to work with amazing, passionate, and brave humans each day. A few other things I love are: rain, floral print, cooking and baking, tending to my vegetable garden, doing jigsaw puzzles, and going out to eat with my friends and family.

name: The DBT Clinic
approaches:
Our Approach to Helping
Simply put, DBT teaches people how to live well. It doesn’t matter the diagnosis, or even if there is no diagnosis. If one has a model of strategies and skills to deal effectively with the inevitable stress that is part and parcel to the human experience, one is better equipped to manage their life more effectively. One can learn to create “a life worth living”. There are four skills sets: 1) Core Mindfulness; 2) Distress Tolerance; 3) Emotion Regulation and 4) Interpersonal Effectiveness.
Social Justice Issues We Care About
We, The DBT Clinic of Portland, stand in solidarity with the fight for racial equality. We continually scrutinize our company’s policies, procedures, and hiring practices. We engage in ongoing conversations around our Mission Statement and Inclusivity Statement that act as our guide to serving our community. And in full support of our statements we also have a multi-point plan of action that guides continuing therapist training and company practices, ensuring that our practice acknowledges the intersectionality of every experience and interaction, decolonizing our therapy and research. We are constantly growing and maturing to better show up for our community and those most underserved.

name: Rebecca X Casanova (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My work with the people who I am privileged to serve is trauma-informed, strengths-based, client-centered, collaborative, and supportive. Many of us have been hurt and have adapted in ways that may keep us safe, but also keep us feeling stuck and disconnected from other people. I emphasize meeting people where they are at and creating a safe environment to grow and try new things. Through our work, you can become more adept at recognizing and responding to difficult emotions (including not feeling anything or nothing but anger or anxiety), and how your past trauma is showing up in your body; building resilience through skills development and practice; and feeling more comfortable in your own skin. It is my hope that everyone I serve as a therapist experiences liberation and/or is able to transcend past difficulties in order to be able to be more fully present in their lives and relationships. I encourage open communication between myself and the people I serve and welcome feedback.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a lifelong commitment to serving people who have experienced many forms of trauma and have specialized training and deep experience in serving LGBTQ+ people and Veterans. I see relationships as the most important element in healing and am also trained in multiple modalities/evidence-based therapy protocols for trauma, anxiety, depression, and related issues. I earned my MSW and MPH from Portland State University in 2013, worked at the Portland VA for nine years, and am a first-generation college graduate. Before graduate school, I was in New York for many years, where I did international and domestic human rights and public health work, including direct action and advocacy. When I’m not working, I ride my bike, hang out in nature, look at art, putter around making art, listen to music, cook, and tease my pets.

name: Natalie Buller
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Bringing your unconscious to consciousness. Jungian or Depth Psychology is applicable to all ages and presenting issues. I have a Master's in Counseling Psychology with an emphasis in Depth Psychology. Teens and young adults are beginning what Jung termed as the individuation process. Together we will identify tendencies of your persona and shadow, what changes you would like to make, and how to cope daily on your own. I have experience as a substance abuse counselor at The Salvation Army Adult Rehabilitation Center for men, Lovejoy Surgicenter, Mother & Child Education Center a non-profit, and currently at Rose Marriage & Family Therapy a private practice.

name: Christopher Raines (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am integrative therapist drawing on the broad theories that support people in healing. Sometimes this is a directive/problem focused support. With other patients it can involve building insight into emotions, relationship patterns, and unconscious areas that contribute to distress. Whatever your goals, I hope to bring my considerable background in psychotherapy to helping you achieve your goals.
My Approach to Helping
Very often people come to therapy when they have exhausted personal resources and feel stuck in persistent patterns and challenges. I find by helping people become aware of the function and dynamics that contribute to their problems they can develop strategies to more flexibly engage with themselves and others. This often can contribute to improvement and increased capacity for self compassion and understanding in the face of the day to day stressors that life can bring.

name: Geraldine Kuphal (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have a background in family therapy, cognitive-behavioral therapy and a strength based-approach. I have worked with families in various settings such as shelters, crisis lines, preschools, mental health clinics and other outpatient settings. Depending on your personal style and goals, I use a variety of tools such as narrative, solution-focused, mindfulness-based, art therapy, family-based interventions. Therapy can be short-term or long-term depending on your personal style and goals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Whether you realize it or not, there are unique aspects of who you are today that work in your favor: your life experience, your skills, your belief system, perhaps even your ethnicity or family history. We will identify these and use them as resources to help you heal. I also believe that therapy is a two-way-street. We will meet each other halfway, each of us actively participating in the process, to reach our shared goal of overcoming your challenges, whatever they may be. A cognitive behavioral approach is one that takes into account how your thoughts affect the way you feel about yourself, other and the world. And how these affect your behavior. Together, we will create a treatment plan that best fits your needs, goals and personal style.

name: Chris Ferrante (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Counseling can be helpful in numerous ways. Some examples include: building skills to manage anxiety and mood, overcoming obsessions and compulsions, working through past trauma, and overcoming fear of social situations. I take a client-centered, collaborative approach and utilize evidence-based practices, such as behavioral therapy, cognitive-behavioral therapy, and mindfulness & acceptance-based approaches.

name: Eden Baron-Williams (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I am a registered Marriage and Family Therapist Associate with a specialization in sexualities. I hold certifications and special training in Somatic Attachment Therapy, EFT, and am a Certified Sexual Assault Survivor Advocate. I have a background in the creative arts, which has allowed me to think creatively with clients and explore artistic mediums in our work together. Being a person who is invisibly physically disabled and neurodivergent, I have a passion for working with folx struggling with chronic pain/chronic illness. I have both experience and specialization in working with, advocating for, and supporting queer individuals and relationships, trans and non-binary individuals and relationships, kink/BDSM, folks suffering from systemic marginalization, and PTSD/complex trauma. I have a special passion for supporting individuals navigating the medical system, be that people needing treatment for chronic pain/illness to folx seeking gender affirming care and surgery.
My Values as a Therapist
I think about mental health through a systemic lens - the understanding that we are all impacted by one another, the sociopolitical systems in which we live, and our social locations. Our bodies are also a system as is the Earth, plants, and animals we live among. The way we communicate within our own system, is a reflection of the way we communicate with other systems and vice versa. Through this lens, I center those impacted by systemic marginalization and emphasize social justice in my practice. I believe therapy can be a building block not only for you and your relationships, but for community change and liberation. I am invisibly physically disabled and neurodivergent, which fuels me toward naming and dismantling ableism on the systemic level. I am an active advocate for the LGBTQ+ community and have served as an advocate and educator teaching students and community members about topics such as systemic racism, the importance of sex-positive education, and trans rights.

name: Tu Tran (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I am a graduate student intern in the Professional Mental Health Counseling Program at Lewis and Clark College. My core areas of interest include queer and BIPOC identities, neurodivergence (ADHD, OCD, autism), trauma, leaving religion, anxiety and depression. My interests reflect my identity as a genderqueer/non-binary and Asian-American immigrant, and I strive to provide understanding and bridge the gaps in care in underserved communities. I also strive to support and create the space of compassion through establishing trust, connection and with gentle challenges, emphasizing collaboration to create goals and strategies that meet you where you are. If you are someone seeking to have a voice in the direction of their treatment and want see non-judgment modeled to foster their self-compassion, I hope to be a part of your journey. If this sounds like something you would be interested in, I welcome your outreach to my program email.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Some theories that influence my approach include mindfulness-based therapies like acceptance and commitment therapy, parts work/internal family systems, and somatic therapy. I am body-positive, sex-positive, poly and kink-affirming. As someone who has dealt with the challenges and complexities of having multiple minority identities, I can relate to the experience of falling through the cracks and feeling lost. I hope to be in your corner to figure out the tools for navigation. Outside of therapeutic training, I like to explore new activities and restaurants in the city, consuming Asian media like Kpop and manhwas/manhuas, and writing fiction novellas.

name: Jona Behrer (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to counseling is trauma informed, humanistic, feminist and sex positive. I utilize techniques from Accelerated Experiential Dynamic Psychotherapy, Internal Family Systems, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, and Mindfulness. So what does all this mean? I collaborate with clients to build genuine and safe relationship. Using moment to moment awareness, I help clients deepen into and process their emotions. (Yes, you're in therapy to feel your feelings.) I will be there to help you regulate, make it feel less overwhelming, and make sense of it all. Through this process you will increase your self awareness, break out of unhelpful patterns, integrate new insights, learn coping skills and build resilience.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I bring my authentic self to my work with clients. My style is warm, attuned, compassionate, direct and passionate. I truly care about my clients, and this work. I care about your pain, and the injustices you have faced. The therapeutic relationship is one of depth and of great vulnerability. I encourage you to be discerning, and make sure you find someone who is the right fit for you. If you think that could be me, please reach out to schedule a free consultation to help you decide.

name: Leif Moa-Anderson (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am only a mirror from which you can see your own mind. I will not tell you what to do, I have no answers, I have no judgements. I am here to reflects the inner world that you show me. I will reflect your light to illuminate your shadows. I will change the angle of reflection at times to show you new perspectives. I will ask you questions to help you see the shadows on the cave wall as shadows they are. I will help you see that you hold the chains that bind you and show you how to let them go. What you do with this from there is up to you. Sometimes you may learn that you like what you see and don't want to change it after all. Some things you may discover you can't change, and I will help you accept those. What you can and want to change will take time, practice, and patience, but it can be done. You're going to change anyway, you might as well be intentional about it.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a person of strong opinions, loosely held.-Unknown: Your resistance to what is, is the cause of your suffering.-Morihei Ueshiba: The only true wisdom is knowing you know nothing. -Socrates: I cannot teach anyone anything, I can only make them think.-Socrates: Take what works, leave the rest.-Bruce Lee: If you see failure as weakness, you will become weaker. If you see failure as opportunity, it can make you stronger. Nothing can't hurt you, for it is nothing, and an empty bucket gives the opportunity to fill it. Therefore, don't be afraid of feeling empty, for there is freedom in it. My answer to the great epistemological question, how do you know that you know what you know, is that you don't; accepting this is very freeing. I am not a nihilist, however, I believe that the scientific method is as close to real knowledge as humans will ever get; and, there is morality, it’s just that morality is a continually unfolding process that we are part of, not something set in stone.

name: Cheryl Lane (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I’m certified in Pain Reprocessing Therapy (PRT). It’s designed to treat chronic psychophysiologic pain, and has been found to be very effective for a wide range of common chronic pain syndromes and other chronic ailments (e.g., dizziness, IBS, and migraines). Most chronic pain is due to the brain’s faulty interpretation of signals from the body. We call this “mindbody” or “neuroplastic” pain. That doesn’t mean your pain isn’t real – ALL pain is real! I combine PRT and EAET (Emotional Awareness and Expression Therapy), along with other therapeutic approaches to help you learn a new way of responding to pain signals. You and I will work together to help you “retrain your brain” in a way that can significantly reduce your symptoms, and in many cases, completely eliminate them. It’s not uncommon for individuals with chronic pain to also struggle with anxiety, depression, obsessive / compulsive tendencies, and / or unresolved trauma. I can help you with those as well!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that everyone has the capacity for positive change, and that whatever challenges or concerns have brought you to therapy do not define you. I strive to create a therapeutic space that feels both emotionally and psychologically safe for every client I see, and treat each individual with unconditional positive regard - accepting you for who you are without judgement. These are necessary elements for healing to take place. My approach to therapy is based on establishing a relationship of mutual trust, authenticity, and respect with each client. I believe each individual embodies a unique combination of life experiences, beliefs, values, challenges, and personality traits. With this in mind, I don’t believe in a one-size-fits-all approach to therapy; rather, I will tailor my work with you based on your individual needs and treatment goals.

name: MEGAN WYCKOFF (SHE/HER, THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My main belief is that relationships are how we heal, and that the best growth is seen when we feel safe and curious to do the real work of healing trauma. Healing can only happen against a backdrop of trust. I use talk therapy, but also somatic interventions such as EMDR and other modalities such as NET (Narrative Exposure Therapy) and EFT (Emotionally Focused Therapy) to resolve longstanding issues and break harmful and maladaptive patterns keeping individuals and couples from authentic relating. As a therapist, I seek to create collaborative, authentic relationships with my clients. I am direct, caring, and not interested in hiding behind fancy terminology or concepts. My most important skill is my ability to see you and affirm you: where you are, where you came from, and where you are going. I'll develop a roadmap for your work and I'll help keep you on the right track.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a queer, non-binary cis-passing white person with chronic illness, I find that I have a lot in common with many of my clients, and I seek to create common ground so we can do our best healing work together as a team. My pronouns are she/her, they/them, and my commitment to supporting trans, non-binary and gender expansive folks is longstanding. I support movements advocating for HAES, and I am aligned with non-traditional relationship styles and sex-positivity movements, and an advocate for the rights of sex workers, women, immigrants, and other minorities. While I am white, I actively work to create an anti-racist practice and space of healing for BIPOC clients, and am happy to speak with you explicitly about my own racial justice experience. I work out of a practice located on unceded land of the Cascades, Stl’pulmsh (Cowlitz), Confederated Tribes of Siletz Indians, Confederated Tribes of Grand Ronde, Cayuse, Umatilla and Walla Walla.

name: Scott LaForce (HE, THEM, HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work intersubjectively through a postmodern lens with an emphasis on empowerment. This means that I remain cognizant of all the crucial elements coming into the room, including your unique experience and how my presence interacts with it. Dogma is not a part of my practice. While past theories have insisted on the therapist as expert, I believe that you are the expert of your life and that my job is to assist you in (re)discovering yourself. There are many options. We just need to find the best one for your needs. Social justice is a primary focus for my practice. I will help you to deconstruct the dominant discourses that have been a disservice in your life while helping you to create new narratives that will allow you to build the life you've always wanted... the life you deserve.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold a master's degree in Applied Psychology from Antioch University Los Angeles. My focus has been on social justice with an emphasis on LGBTQ+Affirmative therapies. I worked as a rehabilitation practitioner with folks who suffer from severe and persistent mental illness, a therapist for people making strides toward eradicating stigma surrounding HIV, and as an intake counselor at various agencies. I am sex-positive. I am led by synchronicity and the teachings of Carl Jung. I support holistic healing, which means that I will encourage you to seek many other avenues toward betterment. Therapy is only one piece to the puzzle that is you.

name: Fiona Chen (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
* telehealth service only *Individual Therapy (no couples therapy) *Oregon & age 18-64 *Office Hours: Tue & Thur 9am-3pm, Fri 9am-2pm

name: Aaron Adamski (HE HIS)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I provide in person services through community outdoor spaces such as city parks. I believe that you, me, and every human on this planet is relational. The way we relate to ourselves, others, and our environment creates meaning in our lives. Often there are patterns across these different ways of relating. Grasping and understanding of these patterns can be therapeutic and provide insight. Through counseling we will work in a collaborative way to address your personal struggles and life challenges because life itself is a struggle. Each one of us needs help from others, reaching out is the first step, so congratulations! Therapy is a process and at times it may be uncomfortable, but I do my best to provide a safe, respectful, and meaningful space where we can work towards your goals and desires.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received a MA in 2019 for Professional Mental Health Counseling from Lewis & Clark College. My B.A. is in Psychology, from University of Montana in 2014. In 2020 I received a certificate in Ecopsychology at Pacifica Graduate Institute. In Montana I lead several wilderness trips and hold a Wilderness First Responder certificate. In addition, I have 8 years of direct support experience with adolescents and adults in a variety of settings.

name: Todd Mercural (HE/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use an existential approach to my work which goes beyond simply alleviating symptoms. My job is to help you find freedom within life's constraints and discover agency where it feels like there are no choices. I want to know who you are by understanding your life experiences and how they have shaped who you are so that we can find authentic meaning, purpose, and connection. I am a Certified Clinical Trauma Professional. I have completed Hatha Yoga teacher training and Mindfulness Based Stress Reduction which I use to incorporate experiential mind-body practices in session. For couples, I use Gottman method to: disarm conflicting verbal communication; increase intimacy, respect, and affection; create a heightened sense of empathy and understanding within the context of the relationship; and develop and strengthen a sense of shared meaning and life vision. It is a combination of skill building and existential therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe healing and change, and authenticity come through the integration of psychological, emotional, spiritual, social, professional, cultural, biological, moral and ethical aspects of life. My counseling perspective is rooted in existential and humanistic philosophies, and deeply influenced by Buddhist and Yogic teachings. I am a lifelong learner and, in the same way I have approached my own life, I approach clients' lives with openness, curiosity and compassion. In my free time I I love spending time in nature through trail running and hiking, canoeing lakes and rivers, cycling, and mountaineering.

name: Jenna Steen (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Studies confirm what I know to be true: the best predictor of therapeutic success is a secure, attuned therapeutic relationship. This means I meet you with compassion and listen without judgment, assumptions, or expectations. I respect your pace and honor the need to feel safe when sharing your story.
My Values as a Therapist
At the foundation of my work is the belief that every human being has an innate ability to thrive. I offer acceptance, empathy, and my own authenticity to enable you towards growth and fulfillment. I believe the capacity to build the life you want is already within you. My role as therapist is to help you clear the way.

name: Jack Marvin (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is centered in a radical feminist framework that understands each of us has been harmed by systems (family, societal, political, among others) and that, depending on our intersectional identities, that harm has varying impacts on our bodies, hearts, minds, and stories. I am particularly interested in the stories that we tell ourselves. Too frequently, these stories come from our external worlds and are integrated as fundamental truths. I believe therapy is a collaborative process of disentangling unuseful, harmful, and deeply integrated falsehoods. In this process of unraveling, we learn new tools and skills that enable us to know ourselves deeper and integrate truths congruent with that deeper knowing. My practice relies on a humanist narrative approach that integrates CBT, DBT, mindfulness, and motivational interviewing techniques as well as attachment, trauma, and polyvagal theories, all in support of the reclamation of control and autonomy of your identity and story.
Why I Became a Therapist
Injury and harm, and the symptomatology that often accompanies, occurs within our relationships to systems, as well as our past and present interpersonal relationships. I believe that healing from these harms also happens in safer and more trusting relationships. I became a therapist to work to create this type of safer, more trustworthy space. It is my great privilege to hold hope that no matter how loud, painful, or uncertain you're feeling now that together in this safer and more trusting space with specific, attainable, and measurable goals, we can work to reduce the symptoms you're experiencing.

name: John Lenkin (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background
I have trained in both Clinical Mental Health Counseling and Marriage Family Therapy. I work from an experiential, somatic, attachment, mindfulness, and compassion focused orientations. My approach is relational, multiculturally-oriented, trauma-informed, and strengths-based. I seek to meet you with empathy, non judgment, and welcome all identities. My graduate work rests on 20 years of previous clinical experience. I am certified as a Somatic Experiencing Practitioner, NARM practitioner for attachment, relational and developmental trauma, as well as, having completed Hakomi, Internal Family Systems, and Emotionally Focused Therapy training. I am a Mindful Self-Compassion (MSC) Teacher, and Somatic Movement Therapist.
My Approach to Helping
I appreciate that you reading this is a reflection of your own curiosity and showing up for yourself! Therapy is a safe, collaborative, and respectful space to work with you towards lessening your struggles and achieving your goals. Our work together is an opportunity to shift from scripts, wounding and habit patterns of your past and more fully enter the present moment with clarity, belief in yourself, and strength to act. Ultimately, our work together can decrease your stress, cultivate a more comfortable relationship with yourself and others, and overall lead to more possibilities and self-empowerment. My aim is to support your natural capacities, as you grow beyond limitations towards self-acceptance, health, and connection. During our time together, I am interested in how to aid you in developing resources of awareness, embodiment, creativity, resilience, and self-compassion to transform moments of challenge towards greater ease and life satisfaction.

name: Sara Goldman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As relational and body based counselor, we will employ many methods of working together, one of which is “mindfulness.” This is a way of paying attention to yourself with curiosity, openness, and acceptance. It often means closing your eyes to focus your attention on your actual experiences in the moment. Don't worry! I won't just be asking you to practice breathing! Together, we will get curious about your experiences, about what you want and how you want to feel.
More About the People I Treat
I worked in K-12 public schools for over 10 years as a counselor. I understand how those systems can be so difficult to work with and try to thrive. I love working with teachers, school staff, and parents to understand how to navigate the system to keep yourself and your kids whole. Please reach out to me and schedule a free 30 minute consultation to see if we might be a good fit for each other. I look forward to talking with you.

name: Anthony Boyd
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Working from a multicultural perspective that employs a Person-centered approach, I am able to gain a holistic perspective of you. Throughout your therapy, we will collaborate as we tend to your inner life; both the light and shadow. I will listen to you closely with care and curiosity, and without judgment. I align closely with Jungian theory and primarily use Psychodynamic Therapy. By helping you find and recognize patterns in your emotions, thoughts, and beliefs, you can gain insight into yourself. I find this approach fosters individuation and integration, allowing for wholeness and becoming the highest version of yourself. I have a deep interest in utilizing expressive arts as a tool within therapy to aid in self-discovery. I work with adults and teens of varying cultural backgrounds struggling with issues including, but not limited to, LGBTQ+ issues, gender identity issues, body image, self-esteem issues, depression, anxiety, grief, sexuality, PTSD, and personality disorder.
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a therapist because I felt like I was wasting my innate gifts by continuing down the 20 year long path of a graphic designer in a corporate world. Throughout school as a child, I was severely bullied for being different. It took many years to understand myself, and to understand that people really did love and care about me. As a member of the LGBTQ+ community, I work to help all that struggle with not only their sexuality, but also their gender. I believe in and support equality for all, and human rights for all. I support Black Lives Matter and am an ally for all BIPOC peoples. I work hard to identify my bias, understand my intersectionality, and do my best to use my position and privilege to benefit those without it. That said, I am also willing to be called out by someone when a micro-aggression occurs in the course of therapy. They happen, and to pretend they don't means I am part of the problem. I'd much rather be part of the solution and help you thrive.

name: Heather Canby (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I work by being an active participant in your process. I believe that increased awareness helps us understand ourselves and the way we are making meaning in the world and gives us the opportunity to try out new ways of being, responding, and coping. No one is broken or in need of fixing, but sometimes we need a listening ear to remind us of who we are, to encourage us when bumps in the road arise, and to help us clarify our goals. I bring a sense of curiosity and practicality to our work. I welcome feedback and adjust accordingly if folks are looking for a more directive, solution focused approach or if your way of processing is less linear. I have been in the mental health field for ten years now, working in a wide variety of settings including community mental health, a university counseling center, and in the EAP space.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that human beings have immense potential for growth and change. Often we develop ways of coping and surviving that serve is in the moment and become less useful later on. Much of therapy is shedding a light on these patterns of beings and looking at what we want to continue on with and what we want to shift or let go of.

name: Becca Newland (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Growth takes place in relationships; I offer a warm, consistent, collaborative, & direct therapeutic relationship. I specialize in supporting my clients explore their internal worlds with compassion and curiosity so that they can improve and deepen their connections with themselves & with others. This will likely include identifying relational and emotional patterns, working to understand how these have come to be, tending to older hurts and wounds, learning and trying out new skills, and more. The primary clinical approaches that I am informed by and incorporate include internal family systems (IFS), attachment theory, psychodynamic psychology, Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT), & mindfulness practices. I integrate somatic (body) awareness into sessions. I am engaged in ongoing training and learning in all of these modalities. I hope to offer couples/relationship therapy by 2026. I am a Level 1 Trained Clinician in EMDR but do not use EMDR in my practice at this time.
My Values as a Therapist
I strive to practice radical acceptance and offer a non-judgmental, welcoming space to individuals of all backgrounds. It is my sincere hope that with time, my clients can feel safe bringing their most authentic selves to therapy. To support this, I engage in my own ongoing self-reflection to understand how my identities and experiences may show up in the therapeutic space. I am also committed to providing a supportive space to freely name/discuss the various societal systems that oppress or exclude.

name: Caroline McGrath LPC, NCC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I support clients in their personal journey to discover tools that resonate with them, explore their sense of self, uncover personal coping strategies, and develop new, healthy ways to express their emotions. As a Trauma-Informed Breathwork Facilitator & Somatic-Based Coach, I incorporate a blend of integrative techniques, which encompass Coaching, Breathwork, Shadow Work, TRE, & Social Neuroscience. I hold a Master's Degree in Counseling Psychology from Lewis & Clark Graduate School. I specialize in working with children, adolescents, and adults, employing a variety of individual and group counseling modalities. My extensive experience includes helping clients who have navigated challenges such as divorce, trauma, grief/loss, anxiety, depression, life transitions, self-esteem, and self-worth issues. Additionally, I have a background in assisting children and teens in shelter and residential treatment facilities who were facing serious to severe emotional and behavioral difficulties.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am deeply committed to destigmatizing mental health issues, especially among children, teens, and young adults. Many young individuals in our communities grapple with the overwhelming stressors and high expectations tied to school, peer relationships, parental pressures, college, sports, and future planning. These pressures can culminate in anxiety, depression, as well as strained relationships with family and peers. My passion lies in helping children, teens, and young adults navigate these challenges and emerge with a strong sense of self-worth, equipped with the coping skills to journey through life's ups and downs. On a more personal note, I cherish spontaneous adventures with my family and friends, and I make it a point to take mental health days to rejuvenate from life's daily demands. Being a mom to my boys is a source of immense joy and fulfillment as we journey through life together.

name: April Slabosheski (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Therapy is a place where you get to be yourself. I draw on various counseling theories as well as my personal experiences, identities, and values of anti-racism and intersectional feminism to support you as you work toward your goals. My life experiences and therapy journey have given me a sense of awe in the power of connection with ourselves, each other, and the land we live on. While we can be wounded and traumatized in relationships that don’t meet our needs, the counseling relationship offers a unique connection that can be a source of strength, consistency, and security. Over time, the therapeutic relationship can help strengthen other relationships in our lives and help us find–or create–our communities, even if we’re not born into them.
More About the People I Treat
I work in person and via telehealth with individuals ages 15+ who are exploring meaning in life, queer identity, the impacts of trauma, relationships, attachment patterns, shame, loneliness, the impacts of white culture and identity, ADHD and other neurodivergence, self-esteem, anxiety, depression, and more. People seek therapy for all kinds of reasons: maybe you’re feeling stuck, not sure who you are anymore, or disconnected from those you care about the most. Perhaps you find yourself not doing the things you really want because of shame or fear. It could be that something big has changed, or maybe you just wish life made more sense than it does right now. No matter what you’re going through, you don’t have to do it alone.

name: Chelsea Kellogg (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As an emotionally focused therapist, I believe that emotion is a powerful agent of both change and connection. Problems tend to happen when the dominant emotional experiences that underlie experience are not fully conscious or felt. Same goes for our thoughts. Change occurs when emotions and thoughts are made more fully conscious and interacted with differently. In couples therapy, we work toward change by accessing (making explicit) the dominant emotional experiences and then creating new ways of expressing and receiving these emotional experiences to allow change to happen. I integrate attachment theory, interpersonal neurobiology, humanism, somatics, experiential therapy, IFS, intersectional feminism, affirmative care, family systems theories, ACT, motivational interviewing, harm reduction, Gottman method, MBNET (Mentalization Based Narrative Exposure Therapy) and psychodynamics in my work.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I utilize an intersectional lens to my work. I am trans-allied, anti racist, anti-colonial, anti-apartheid and genocide, feminist, HAES and body trust informed, and poly and LGBTQIA+ allied (I am personally bi/pan-sexual and demisexual.) I have completed numerous advanced trainings to better support neurodiverse people who experience, ADHD, and/or OCD. I am also trained in assessment of ADHD and autism for adults. I am neurodiverse myself. My practice is secular and non-religious. I have extensive knowledge and experience treating complex trauma (CPTSD) and trauma in general.

name: Barbara Biedrzycki
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My treatment approach draws from the theoretical framework of ACT (Acceptance and Commitment Therapy). This therapy focuses on pursuing a meaningful life through value driven behaviors and reducing the struggle brought on by problematic thoughts, feelings and behaviors in our lives. It is a highly effective intervention for treating depression, anxiety, chronic pain and other distressing conditions.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have an MA from Lewis and Clark college in Counseling Psychology and am Licensed by the Board of Licensed Professional Counselors and Therapists. I have worked in community mental health since 2000 and have been in private practice mental health counseling since 2013. While I specialize in treating depression, anxiety and low self worth, I am also skilled and experienced in treating other mental health conditions that cause concern: Borderline Personality Disorder, existential issues, major life transitions, grief, loss and shame. I work primarily with populations over the age 17.

name: Kelly Rees (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I focus on intimate relationships, sexual expression, disparate desire, shifting intimacy, alternative relating styles, polyamory. This often includes performance issues, the impact of aging and menopause, dealing with grief and jealousy. I have experience working with differing abilities and my clients range in age from mid-twenties to mid-nineties. I aim to engage the mind and heart while centering on the body. Pleasure is important and a great way to learn!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have been an Intimacy and Sexuality coach since 2008, and completed my PhD in Human Sexuality in 2018. I am a board certified Sexologist with a background in neo-Tantra. I have been guiding people into clarity around their sexuality since 2003. I also work with those in recovery from drug/alcohol addiction and their specific issues around sex and relationships.

name: Kelly Arthur, LMFT, LPC, CADC I
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Research shows that the most important predictor of success in therapy is the quality of the relationship between therapist and client. My top priority is to create a safe space for you to be able to be real, to show up fully in your stuckness and your pain. As a systemically-oriented counselor, I recognize that we are all doing the very best we can to survive, but sometimes our ways of going about it may cause harm to those around us, including our very selves. I see therapy as a process of accessing new pathways for interaction; this is freedom. I meet people on the common ground of our shared humanity, in a collaborative partnership designed to empower YOU. While I may hold some expertise in the areas of individual and couples counseling, YOU are the expert in your own life.
