I have deep respect for the human capacity for growth and healing, and I consider it a most high honor to participate in someone else's growth/change process. Basically, I have the best job ever!
My Values as a Therapist
I believe it is important to walk the talk. I've been engaged in my own intensive personal growth work for almost three decades. People who have worked with me appreciate my highly attuned presence, to which I attribute a daily meditation practice that enables me to show up fully present in all areas of my life and work. I'm trained in couples, marriage, and family therapy, so I work holistically and systemically with both individuals and couples. I practice culturally-sensitive counseling with all types of people, including straight, gay, queer, trans, non-binary, poly, and kink. I hold credentials as a Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist, a Licensed Professional Counselor, a Nationally Certified Counselor, and a Certified Alcohol and Drug Counselor. I've been counseling people with a variety of issues since 2015.

name: Lesley Lacny (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I can help you develop a greater awareness of yourself and your actions. Together, we will explore ways to move forward using tools to help you turn off your “auto-pilot” and to deepen your understanding of what is underneath your reactions so you can begin living freely and within your control. We will explore the root of behaviors, thoughts, and emotional responses that pull you into “reactive mode”. We will explore the physiological impact this creates in your body and ways to move into an aware state that lends to calm, presence, and a sense of self-control. We will explore alternative ways to manage overwhelming feelings and cope with experiences including: anxiety, depression, stress, trauma, relationship struggles, separation and divorce, addiction, anger, parenting, and life transitions.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
For the past 25 years, I have been invited to sit with others during some of their most vulnerable moments. The intimacy held in the relationship between myself and my clients has lent itself to a fulfilling career that touches me well beyond my time spent in sessions. Education and training are essential to developing into a skilled therapist; however, for me, it is also what I have leaned in my sessions with clients that has taught me what it means to endure pain, to put our guards down, and to sit with raw emotion. Seeing people work through difficult things and come out feeling more fulfilled and at peace is what encourages me to continue to show up in my own life with curiosity, compassion, and lightheartedness. As I support my clients to find balance and fulfillment, I too strive to live my life in a way that fulfills me. When I am not sitting with clients, I can be found chasing my boys down mountain bike trails, rock climbing, and finding quiet moments in meditation.

name: Carolyn Knutson LPC, LMT (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Welcome to a holistic approach to mental health. I offer individual counseling and embodiment sessions to address developmental trauma, PTSD, stress, anxiety, depression, addictions, chronic pain/auto-immune disorders, and spiritual/energetic issues. My approach uses somatic (body-centered) awareness and mindfulness as a way to enhance traditional talk therapy and draws from the wisdom traditions of yoga and Buddhism, the biodynamic field of the human energy system, and somatic psychotherapy. I look forward to accompanying you on a journey of healing and transformation.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As both a licensed professional counselor and a licensed massage therapist, I deeply value the strength of including the mind, body, and spirit in healing. I have worked in the mental health field since 2001 in a variety of settings, including psychiatric, drug treatment, and youth corrections. It was disheartening at the time to see in many ways how these facilities and institutions provided an environment that continued systems of oppression and traumatic stress, and not necessarily the safety and connection that allowed for deeper healing to take place—there had to be another way. I had a passionate drive to follow the path of yoga, spirituality, touch, and after attending massage therapy school to pursue a career in the healing arts, I realized how powerful both mind and body therapies are for holistic healing. I have specialized training in somatic psychotherapy and craniosacral therapy, as well as an extensive yoga and meditation practice which greatly influences my work.

name: Kelly Reams MSW, BCD Psychoanalyst (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a psychotherapist and contemporary psychoanalyst, I work with both situational, short-term issues or the more chronic concerns depending on your goals for therapy. I have found that establishing consistent meeting times at a particular frequency (once a week or more often) helps create a sense of safety and trust that supports you in making more fundamental changes on the inside and in daily life. We will jointly explore your inner world of feelings, hopes, dreams, fantasies, thoughts, anxieties, fears, secrets and memories to help you understand these profound influences that continue to impact us in the present in ways that we haven't known, before. I will deeply respect your unique self and self-direction based on confidence that with help, you will be able to experience new ways of being and understanding yourself that are liberating and transformative.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Over the past thirty years, I've actively sought out teachers, mentors, supervisors, play therapists, psychotherapists and psychoanalysts to help me develop the skills and knowledge needed to offer a range of treatment options that differ in depth and intensity depending on one's goals for treatment. After completing a M.S.W. (1980), I've worked as a psychotherapist with children, teens, adults and families in residential treatment, the Child & Adolescent Clinic at O.H.S.U. and have had a private practice for over 30 years which has included individual therapy, play therapy, couples and family therapy, consultation/ supervision and now, psychoanalysis. I finished a 6 year psychoanalytic training program (OPC) in 2004, am a certified analyst and a Training-Supervising Psychoanalyst. I also am a faculty member at OPC and continue to learn through study groups, continuing education, consultation, and as a consultant to mental health therapists.

name: Noël Quigley (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I take an integrated, holistic approach to the therapeutic journey. Pulling from talk therapy, working with the body (somatic - including movement, breathing, nervous system integration), meditation and mindfulness, IFS informed parts work, and more, we collaborate on the treatment approach that best suites your needs/dreams/goals. I encourage integrating outside practices and practitioners, knowing that each individual journey requires unique tools. I believe humans are drawn to find ways to find and feel connection. We have a natural ability to connect with others as well as ourselves, and this natural ability it shaped and altered by situations throughout life. Connection can also feel hard, uncomfortable, strained, and uncertain...feelings in conflict with another natural need, to feel safe and comfortable. Whether you are feeling anxious, loneliness and isolation, worry, depression, a disconnection with your own needs or wants or any other emotional experiences, you are whole.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe that we are all naturally connected and wired to connect, and that profound healing happens in relationships. When you experience trauma, whether one large traumatic event or smaller incidents over time, relationships with self and others can become strained and it can feel challenging to connect. In this you are not alone. Additionally, in that I believe we are all unique, I believe we all have a responsibility to one another. I am a white, queer, trans non-binary, chronically ill, neurodivergent therapist from the south living in Portland, OR. I am committed to mutual aid efforts and continue to navigate how I can personally and professionally uphold my values of community, accessibility, compassion, and more. With a mindset of cultural humility, I know that there is much that I can know and can learn about experiences other than my own, but that I will never know what it is like to live an experience other than that of my own cultural background, identity, and position.

name: Ursina Teuscher (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer decision coaching to help you solve problems and achieve your goals. Issues I can help you with include: improving your time management, setting priorities, getting more work done, and establishing healthier habits. For your big decisions, we will use a structured process to clarify your core values and evaluate your options. This process will give you the necessary confidence in your decision and peace of mind, so that you can commit to taking action. Throughout our work together, I use decision-aiding tools that were developed on a scientific background and designed to enhance our ability to think systematically as well as creatively. I employ coaching methods that are evidence-based and have been shown to be effective for supporting decisions, improving performance and achieving goals. (Examples: mindfulness-based, cognitive behavioral, value focused, paradoxical, creative thinking and problem-solving techniques.)
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
After my training as a Career Counselor (professional license and MS in Psychology in 2000), I gained my doctoral degree (PhD) in Applied Psychology in 2004 from the University of Freiburg, Switzerland. I have co-authored the textbook, “Heart and Mind: Mastering the Art of Decision Making,” and have published peer-reviewed research articles in the fields of cognitive psychology, neuroscience, decision-making, and coaching techniques. I have been teaching Psychology and Decision-Making classes at high-school and university levels since 2001 (currently at PSU). My ongoing research and teaching complements and informs my work in private practice. It continues to give me insights into disciplines that are fast-changing and rich with new findings about the way people think, feel and behave. Those insights continue to inform my approach and methods. I’m deeply committed to bringing you well-researched, highly relevant techniques and practices.

name: Dawn Forrester (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In 2000, I earned my master’s degree in counseling with a heartfelt mission to help people create positive change and transformation in their lives. I believe we all hold the power within us to shape our future and live authentically. My goal is to help you identify and build on your strengths, guiding you toward a life filled with purpose and meaning. Together, we’ll work to uncover your power, navigate challenges, and move forward with hope, clarity, and support. Using a blend of positive and cognitive theoretical frameworks, I help clients embrace change and overcome obstacles to living a fulfilling life. I listen with compassion and understanding, fostering a strong therapeutic connection to empower you to take action and create the life you deserve.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
My goal is to support you in uncovering what may be holding you back while highlighting and harnessing your unique strengths. Together, we'll nurture your inner potential, guiding you toward a life that feels authentic, purposeful, and fulfilling. With hope, clarity, and meaningful support, we’ll chart a path forward that empowers you to thrive.

name: Jason Schaefer (HE/HIM)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
As a psychodynamic and depth psychology-oriented therapist, I specialize in supporting adults exploring their own possible autism or ADHD, or who are healing from trauma through teletherapy. Drawing from my personal experience as a late-diagnosed neurodivergent adult, I understand the unique challenges you face. Together, we work to validate your unique perspectives, navigate internal contradictions, and address trauma-related issues like anxiety and emotional dysregulation. My approach emphasizes self-acceptance, building self-confidence, and fostering personal growth. By creating a safe and accepting space, I help you move from seeking external validation to trusting your own insights and decisions. I have immediate openings and offer a tiered, honor-based sliding scale cash fee arrangement. I'm here to support your journey toward a more confident and authentic self.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
My path to becoming a therapist was shaped by my own journey to understand and accept my identity as a late-diagnosed neurodivergent person. Therapy was pivotal in embracing my differences and aligning my career with my core values of honesty, social justice, and acceptance. I am deeply committed to truly understanding how you feel, ensuring that you are always heard and understood. As an empathetic listener who asks clarifying questions, I create a safe and supportive environment where you can freely share your experiences. My personal and professional background allows me to recognize and honor your unique challenges, fostering a relationship built on trust and respect. With specialized training in Jungian psychodynamic and depth psychology, complemented by humanistic and parts work techniques inspired by Carl Rogers and Voice Dialogue, I offer a versatile and compassionate approach. Additionally, I integrate mindfulness and body-centered practices to support your overall well-being.

name: Hadi Farah (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is rooted in deep self-exploration and understanding. I believe that lasting change comes not just from symptom management, but from uncovering the patterns, emotions, and experiences that shape your life. Rather than focusing solely on medication, I prioritize psychotherapy, offering a space for thoughtful conversations that lead to real insight and growth. My goal is to guide you toward clarity, balance, and a greater sense of self-awareness. Every person’s journey is unique, so I tailor my approach to fit your needs, whether that means exploring past experiences, addressing current challenges, or navigating future goals. All sessions are virtual, allowing you to engage in therapy from the comfort of your own space.
My Values as a Therapist
As a therapist, I value the integration of science and compassion in my work, always aiming to take an objective approach to understand your situation without taking sides. I believe in listening deeply without rushing to provide solutions, giving you the time to explore your thoughts and feelings. I enjoy what I do and find meaning in building a collaborative relationship where you feel heard and understood.

name: Alyssa Gursky (THEY/THEM & SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My academic training provides me with an abundance of tools for teaching emotional and nervous system regulation, mindfulness practices, and creative processes for healing and insight. No artistic experience is necessary to participate in art therapy. My extracurricular training in psychedelic-assisted psychotherapy has cultivated passion and expertise around the preparation and integration of non-ordinary state experiences. I also offer Ketamine Assisted Psychotherapy in my practice. My approach is collaborative and non-directive. Curiosity is critical in the healing relationship.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a queer person, I'm passionate about working with individuals who are on the LGBTQIA+ spectrum. With eight years of working in the field of psychedelic therapy and psychedelic research, I am devoted to helping my clients explore their relationship with all substances in a judgment-free zone. This is known as Harm Reduction Therapy. Creativity manifests in many ways and is a powerful force of healing. l I feel a deep resonance in working with artists. When creativity can come into the therapy space, it can offer a profound mirror to witness oneself and their inner multitudes. However, this is not a requirement. In art therapy, we do not look at art for it's aesthetic value. I'm kink informed, BDSM friendly, and am educated on non-traditional relationship structures.

name: Veronica Wilson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I do not believe in one-size-fits-all therapy. Your personal experience and desires are essential in creating a plan that will work for you. Your personality, life circumstances, strengths and history will all drive approach I take when working with you. That being said, I do believe in evidence-based strategies, so I will consider what interventions have shown to be effective for your situation. However, the most important part of therapy is establishing a trusting and open therapeutic relationship, so I vow to attend to our relationship and remain accountable if you ever feel uncomfortable or uncertain about the work we do. I truly believe in my clients' ability to achieve the changes they want, and I am here to guide you in this process. I work with people with a variety of problems, hopes, goals, and dreams and I'm here to support you in making the changes you want to see in your life.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I have worked in the behavioral health field since 2008 in a variety of settings including acute hospital care, residental and outpatient mental health. However, I have found my home doing therapy and this is the work that motivates me and ignites my passion. I love seeing my clients find the strength and insight within themselves that empowers them to create change. I also have personal experience with weight loss and have maintained a loss of over 75lbs for several years. I believe this makes me the ideal person to help you if you have your own weight loss goals. However, weight loss certainly does not need to be a goal of yours to work with me and for some it's not a healthy goal to have, such as for those with a history of eating disorders. I am supportive and accepting, but will also be honest and direct with you. I will not assume I know your story based on how you look or present yourself, and I strive to understand who you are as a person.

name: Travis Wright (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I’m a veteran, and have 20 plus years experience as a therapist with the Department of Veterans Affairs / Vet Center. My work with minority veterans during my time at the Vet Center/VA has given me some small glimpse into the systemic racism of our country and its history. My approach is trauma informed, with extensive training and experience in transpersonal and existential perspectives. Mostly I believe that the awe and wonder of life can be overshadowed with the darkness from our past or the anxiety with our future. Purpose and meaning are a big part of what I am interested in. Particularly through the lens of humor and wonder at our existence. The idea that we could learn to be more comfortable in our own skin underlies much of the work in therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have been married for 30 years and have two teenage sons. I have lived in Oregon for the last twenty five years or so and I am in love with the rain and awesome craft beer. I don’t take myself very seriously and find happiness in the small things in life. I am curious about the world and our existence and enjoy reveling in it whenever it presents itself. Nature and relationships are a big deal to me and I’m kind of dork since I read science fiction and fantasy books a lot. I also have too much vinyl and keep telling myself not to go into the record shops.

name: Sabrina Walters (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a Certified Gottman Therapist, I focus on the research-based approach to helping couples heal. We have you do an in-depth assessment and use the Core Values Index to understand you at your innate, unchanging core level.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have been licensed since 2006 and have been practicing the Gottman Method for the past several years, and became fully certified as a Gottman Certified Therapist in 2017. I am also a Gottman Trainer as well. My husband and I have been doing couples conferences since 2003 and he also offers relationship coaching and co-therapy if that is something you would like to do. I became certified in the EAGALA method of Equine Assisted Therapy in 2014. I use a combination of EFT, EMDR, Intersubjectivity, Gottman, Equine, and Strategic/Structural therapy to serve my clients best. I believe clients need to feel safe and secure in my presence, so that is my greatest strength: the ability to make you feel safe enough to explore your strengths and struggles and secure enough to try something new.

name: Alex Perez (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I provide care with a holistic approach that focuses on healing all aspects impacting an individual's well-being (physical, emotional, mental, social, and spiritual). My specialties are: Mental Health, ADD/ADHD, Anger Management, Anxiety/Phobic Disorders, Bereavement/Grief, Co-dependency, Central Cultural Ethnic Issues, Child Abuse/Neglect, Chronic Illness, Depression, Divorce, Domestic Abuse, Family Conflict, Geriatric Seniors, Infidelity/Affairs, Internet Addiction, LGBTQIA2S+, Life Coach, Life Transitions, Marital and Premarital, Medical, Mood Disorders, Relationships Issues, Parenting, Peer Relationships, Pregnancy, Prenatal, Postpartum, PTSD, Sleep and Insomnia, Self Esteem, Stress, Teen Violence, Thinking Disorders, Transgender, Trauma, Women's Issues, and Work Stress.

name: Blossom Counseling
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Blossom Counseling was founded on the belief that true growth takes time and unfolds in cycles, much like nature's seasons. Rooted in trauma-informed, person-centered, and attachment-based care, we understand that healing is a gradual process requiring patience, trust, and support. By embracing these cycles of change with compassion and understanding, we help individuals bloom into their fullest selves at their own pace. At Blossom Counseling, we care for children, adults, and families, believing that everyone deserves access to support. That's why we provide both in-person and telehealth options to ensure care is accessible to all. We help individuals navigate life transitions, which are often times of increased vulnerability to anxiety, depression, and trauma, by offering guidance and tools to embrace change and promote healing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
We are committed to providing inclusive, compassionate care for individuals and families from all walks of life. We recognize the unique challenges that come with different backgrounds, cultures, identities, and experiences, and we strive to create a safe, affirming space where everyone feels respected and understood. Whether you’re navigating cultural transitions, facing systemic challenges, or seeking support in an environment that honors your identity, we’re here to meet you where you are. Our approach is rooted in culturally responsive, trauma-informed care, ensuring that your unique needs and experiences are at the center of your healing journey. We invite you to book a free 20-minute consultation to discuss your needs and determine if we’re the right fit for you. This is a chance to ask questions, explore your goals, and learn more about how we can support your journey.

name: Matt Newey (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My style is interactive and goal-driven. Clients describe me as professional, personable, engaging, humorous and helpful. Most are able to quickly feel at ease around me and trust my expertise. My goal is to provide a safe and supportive environment where people can confront deeply personal matters even if those matters are difficult to talk about. Sometimes I am the first person my client has risked talking to about an issue; other times a client may have talked to many people but needs expert guidance to determine what to do.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Over 30 years of professional experience on top of two masters degrees in the field I have worked with a wide variety of people and issues. In addition I am certified in Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, and am a certified anxiety and trauma treatment professional.

name: Amanda Holden, LPC, CADC-I (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I'm a trauma-informed therapist trained in Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Emotionally-Focused Therapy (EFT) & the Gottman Method. I specialize in working with couples who are struggling to feel connected, have communication issues, or are having the same arguments on repeat. I also specialize in working with individuals who are struggling with anxiety, anger or irritability, setting healthy boundaries or saying 'no', or dealing with difficult relationships. I use my training & expertise to help you heal childhood wounds, gain practical tools to more effectively cope with anxiety & overwhelm, set better boundaries, and improve communication & intimacy with your partner(s). Some, but not all of my clients are also raising children & struggling to be the parents they want to be. All of my work is done through a feminist, anti-hate, anti-racist, decolonized, LGBTQIA2S+ affirming lens. I don't always get it right, but I'm sure as hell going to keep showing up to do the work.
Why I Became a Therapist
I'm a therapist because I love people. I know I'm going to like you. I always do. If you are coming in for couples' therapy, I'm going to like your partner, too. I'll see the best in you and how damn hard you've already tried before you ever reached out for therapy. I see so much hurting around me & I want to be a healing force in this world. I want to help hurting partners communicate in ways that build trust, increase safety in the relationship, and make people feel like they've got the best, most supportive ally in this often scary and difficult world. I want to help individuals see their innate goodness, to help them feel seen and heard, and to help them cope with the overwhelm, anger, and anxiety they feel too much too often.

name: Elizabeth Sokolowski, PhD (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Background
I am licensed as a Psychologist in the State of Oregon (#3490) by the Oregon Board of Psychology. My interest in mental health started in undergrad during a philosophy class exploring the mind and early psychology. Due to experiences with chronic illness, I learned the importance of caring for both mind and body. I graduated with a PhD in Psychology from Colorado State University. I was trained as a generalist clinician to treat a wide variety of presenting concerns. I often saw clients for anxiety, depression, PTSD, trauma, grief, loss, and other life stressors. I focused my dissertation to benefit the LGBTQ+ graduate student population by identifying climate concerns (e.g., microaggressions, harassment, mental health impacts, efforts to cope) and generating a list of recommendations to improve campus climate. I became a psychologist because I wanted to provide the same safety, validation, understanding, and support for others, just like my first therapist provided for me.
My Approach to Helping
I work from trauma-informed, humanistic, and cognitive-behavioral lenses, which together recognize the influence that past and present traumatic experiences can have upon our mental health, our relationship with ourselves, and our relationships with others. I believe that change can occur for you through exploring your stories, learning about yourself, building skills, challenging your doubts, and recognizing your strength and resiliency. I recognize that our personal and cultural identities often influence our mental health and social interactions, leading to both strengths and traumatic experiences within our lives. I identify as white, genderfluid, queer, a dog mom, an aunt, and living with chronic health conditions. I invite you to discuss how your identities and lived experiences have influenced your mental health. I approach therapy with warmth, empathy, openness, and light-hearted humor to foster spaces for these conversations and identities to grow.

name: Maddie Cameron (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have no theory to prove or agenda to push, I do not work toward “fixing” people or relationships and aim to steer clear of our one-dimensional focus on behavioral health change. Instead, I hope to walk with you as we explore how to live with more alignment, authenticity, and depth, whatever that may mean or look like for you. When we turn our attention toward this, health and wellness follow.
Why I Became a Therapist
I am currently working with individuals and couples through grief, life transition, identity development, and women’s issues. I act as a guide and support in this work of returning to the spirit of self. “For a seed to achieve its greatest expression, it must come completely undone. The shell cracks, its insides come out and everything changes. To someone who doesn't understand growth, it would look like complete destruction,” Cynthia Occelli.

name: Nani Waddoups (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a professional listener. My work is to attune with my clients, and to strive for understanding. I believe that we all want to be understood, and to understand ourselves. My natural curiosity, my training and my work with clients continue to hone my skills for asking the right questions, sharing my perspective, and checking in to confirm we are still on the right course.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a Masters in Counseling from Lewis & Clark Graduate School in Portland, and my private practice includes work with individuals, couples, families and groups. I have also worked as a group facilitator at the Dougy Center for Grieving Children and their Families, and for the Washington County Counseling Center working primarily with inmates in the county residential treatment dorm. Counseling is a second career for me. The messiness of life has been my best teacher. I am 60 years old, and have navigated these years in ways that have helped me appreciate the richness and complexity of what it is to be human, and to celebrate the potential for self-determination.

name: Lindsey Lundin (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is very relational as I believe that long-term growth and healing happens within trusting, healthy relationships. My relational lens also means that I see the therapeutic relationship as the catalyst for shifting inner narratives and working towards lasting change. That said, we live in a world where varying systems impact our lives every day depending on our identities and working within a structure that we can't quickly change can be challenging. That is why I believe it is important to acknowledge these systems and how my client's daily lives are impacted by them. It can be difficult to find spaces where we feel safe enough to be vulnerable and examine all our parts with compassion which is the kind of space I work to cultivate. I use an attachment and systems lens, elements of Internal Family Systems, skill development, and interpersonal awareness. If looking for Mentalization-Based Narrative Exposure Therapy (MBNET) for trauma processing, reach out to discuss options.
My Values as a Therapist
I’m guided by curiosity and a desire to help people on their journey through healing and insight. In acknowledging my varying identities, I work to examine and discuss how my privileged identities impact and intersect with my client's identities and how we navigate building trust. One of my goals is to help clients feel seen and understood which means staying open to their experiences within our larger social systems and structural inequities. I deeply value the relationships I build with my clients, knowing that an honest, reflective therapeutic connection can foster lasting growth and change.

name: Francesca Guglielmi (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a therapist, I believe that each client is the expert on themselves and their life experiences, and I want to spend our time in therapy together developing a deeper understanding of the way your mind works, which can help us uncover any obstacles or patterns that are preventing you from living the life that you envision. I strive to create a safe space for each of my clients to show up as their authentic self, where they feel heard and understood, and comfortable enough to use the space however will best serve them in that moment. My goal for our work together is to examine the different aspects of your life you bring to our space to help you develop a deeper understanding of yourself, your current circumstances, and your potential opportunities. One of my favorite things is talking with my clients about their lives, hearing stories about their experiences and sharing their favorite things, then using that information to create a more personalized therapeutic experience.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I bring an array of therapies and techniques into my work so that together we can develop a uniquely tailored therapeutic plan that allows us to explore how personal, relational, familial, and cultural experiences have shaped your life. Through exploring these areas of you life we will develop an understanding of potential conflicts and connections, that we can then use to help us bridge your past and your future to resolve any internal conflicts, conscious or unconscious. I am most passionate about the psychodynamic and relational approaches to therapy, and will utilize them along with other modalities like CBT to help you achieve your goals. Most importantly, though, I am here to listen and I look forward to getting to know you and embarking on this healing journey together.

name: Melissa Demers (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
ONLY OFFERING TELEHEALTH (ONLINE) SESSIONS. I want to work alongside you, exploring your strengths and values to help you better cope with life's ups and downs. I use an integrated approach to help you achieve your goals. I am an emotion focused therapist and help couples and families from an attachment framework. I specialized in addictions counseling during my internship at Lewis and Clark College where I saw individuals struggling with addictions and co-occurring disorders for nearly 2 years, as well as helped their family members navigate through that process and be a support to their family member. My passion is working with couples, exploring their strengths and values to help them come together as a team and navigate life's ups and downs. I have an additional year of experience working primarily with couples and families.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that change is constant. I always wanted to find a way to give back and contribute something to society, therefore I became a therapist to help foster positive and sustainable change in people's lives. Due to my own upbringing and family background, I knew that I wanted to help others find ways to come together and create a loving and secure community, something I didn't have much of when I was young. Some random tidbits about myself are that I have a strong affinity for dogs and anything cuddly. You may get the chance to see my dog during our telehealth sessions. She is a Border Collie/Australian Shepard mix. I love my plant babies as well, which will be fully displayed in my home office for your visual enjoyment.

name: Alexandra Moskovich (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
With a largely existential focus, incorporating depth-work, attachment work, and somatic work, I take an integrative and holistic approach, believing that true healing occurs when clients are able to confront the core issues that are holding them back. By exploring each client’s unique intersections and circumstances, and delving into the underlying emotions and beliefs that drive our behavior, I help clients gain a deeper understanding of themselves and their place in the world. My approach is relational and grounded in the belief that, even with our unique circumstances, we all have the power of choice. By recognizing our inherent freedom, we can create more meaningful and fulfilling lives.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold certificates in Sex Therapy through CIIS, as well as Psychedelic-Assisted Therapy through IPI.

name: LAUREN MULLINS (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
I have over 10 years in working with people who are recovering from relational abuse. I have worked with various domestic and sexual violence agencies providing individual and group counseling services. I have experience in suicide prevention and crisis intervention. I received my EMDR (Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing) certification in 2019 and enjoy providing EMDR therapy to clients looking to heal from past traumatic experiences and relationships. In my free time I like to garden, kayak, camp and go for walks with my Boston Terrier, Baked Beans.
My Approach to Helping
As a graduate from Portland State University, the majority of my training is centered in trauma-informed, strength-based, anti-oppressive and collaborative perspectives. I utilize a multi-dimensional assessment with individuals, giving special attention to cultural responsiveness and power dynamics.

name: Alannah Purdie (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I’m passionate about helping individuals explore their emotions and core beliefs to foster personal growth. Therapy should be a unique space for vulnerability and self-discovery, free from judgment. Together, we can challenge limiting beliefs, nurture your strengths, and work towards changing your relationship with difficult emotions and experiences.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that every individual has strengths within them that can be used in the therapeutic process to support change. I enjoy helping people discover these strengths to support them in their therapeutic goals. Starting therapy can feel vulnerable and overwhelming when it comes time to speaking our pain out loud. You might be facing overwhelming emotions or navigating challenging situations, and it’s completely normal to feel uncertain. Together, we can create a therapeutic relationship built on warmth, laughter, and a little sass.

name: Daniel Maher, LCSW (HE / HIM)
approaches:
My Background
I have over 17 years of diverse clinical experience, including psychotherapy, medical social work in emergency rooms, ICUs, psychiatric emergency services, addiction services, and working with military veterans and their families. I specialize in treating depression/anxiety, PTSD/trauma, complicated grief, relationship issues, and the impact of physical illness and addictions. I am also sensitive to the particular needs of LGBTQIA people. My training and experience blend psychodynamic, attachment neuroscience, DBT and Mindfulness-based, experiential methods to help you feel better, heal, and be more connected.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
My style is warm, active, forthright and supportive. I consider it a privilege to accompany clients towards healing and greater fulfillment in their lives. My approach is highly informed by Accelerated Experiential Dynamic Psychotherapy. AEDP aims at healing attachment wounds and other kinds of trauma, and is also effective in treating depression, anxiety, addictive behaviors, relationship difficulties, and many other issues. Often session work includes a combination of us attending to present-moment emotional experience and learning skills to regulate feelings, reduce anxiety, and facilitate connection with self and others. I also enlist tools from Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) and Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) when helpful.

name: Heather Mangold
approaches:
Our Background
To schedule an appointment, visit activepath.com

name: Katherina Alexandre (SHE/HER/ELLA)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In the past 20 years, my practice has focused on helping my clients with grief, depression, anxiety, work-related stress, parenting relationships, and violence. My approach involves creating a safe space to feel comfortable with me; I use an integrated relational, cognitive behavioral model and evidence-based techniques. We will work with clients to identify goals and find the best ways to achieve them. As a Compassion Fatigue Professional and Marriage and Family Therapist, I offer treatment for symptoms of compassion fatigue (secondary trauma and burnout) for first responders and anyone in the healthcare, addiction, and mental health fields. I offer workshops throughout the year.

name: Erika Tarjan (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a compassionate and collaborative mental health therapist, I create a safe, non-judgmental space where trust can flourish. I specialize in supporting individuals and couples who feel overwhelmed by life’s challenges, whether navigating anxiety, depression, trauma, or significant life transitions. Together, we will explore your unique strengths, challenges, and goals, working to develop practical coping skills that empower you to feel more in control of your life. My goal is to support your journey toward healing, resilience, and a future filled with confidence and meaningful growth. Drawing from evidence-based interventions, including strength-based approaches, EMDR, Internal Family Systems (IFS), and mindfulness practices, I help create a personalized map to help you facilitate meaningful change.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I deeply value compassion, authenticity, cultural humility, and collaboration. My goal is to create a safe, supportive space where clients feel truly seen, heard, and empowered to heal and grow. I approach therapy with a systems perspective, recognizing the influence of family dynamics, societal structures, and systems of oppression on our lives and well-being. I also emphasize acknowledging power imbalances, being aware of societal and systemic inequalities, and working toward equitable relationships and practices

name: Anchor Within Counseling
approaches:
Our Approach to Helping
We believe that you are more than the issue you bring into therapy. We all have lived experiences, developmental stages, and life choices which have shaped our story this far and they are all important in the therapeutic process. Our therapists work from the theoretical approaches of Brief Solution Focused Therapy (BSFT), Attachment Theory, Cognitive Behavior Therapy (CBT), Person-Centered Theory, Narrative Therapy, Family Systems Theory, Motivational Interviewing (MI), Crisis intervention, Trauma-Informed perspectives, and Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT) skills. We incorporate indispensable practices which include expansive self-reflection, introspective family history review, and cooperative treatment planning. It is our goal that you feel open and safe to share in sessions. Please see our website for more specific information about the individual providers at our office.
Our Personal Beliefs and Interests
Our Providers have a rich blend of experiences in communities across the state and around the globe. We have practiced in residential treatment facilities, child welfare, foster care programs, parenting education, homeless and runaway youth, case management, school social work, sexual exploitation, domestic violence, medical social work, immigrant and refugee supports, community mental health, and disability services. See the MEET US page on our website for specifics about each provider.

name: Lauren Merrell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I specialize in psychodynamic psychotherapy, which is well-suited to people who want to get to the root of long-term problems and patterns that continually crop up and get in the way of living a fulfilled life. I incorporate this expertise into my medication management appointments, as well as with patients who are seeking therapy. As a Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner, sometimes I will recommend medications that I believe will help you to make best use of therapy.
My Approach to Helping
My approach to care is warm, direct, and attentive. I believe in having honest, thoughtful conversations about your mental health while providing a supportive space to explore what’s working and what’s not. When prescribing medication, my goal is always to find the lowest effective dose.

name: Michaela Curtis-Joyce (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am glad you are here and I genuinely would like to help you find the right support. I believe we all already have exactly what we need. I find it quite rewarding to team up with clients and find ways to harness their strengths. I am relationship based and balance compassion with frank communication.

name: Tristan Smith (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have a passion for supporting individuals on their journey toward healing, growth, and self-understanding. Born and raised in Portland, I have always been deeply curious about the world and how we can transform it, both individually and collectively. My experiences working with at-risk youth and individuals struggling with addiction have reinforced my belief in the power of human connection, empathy, and resilience. I earned my Master of Social Work (MSW) from Portland State University, where I was trained in a wide range of therapeutic approaches, but I have always been drawn to holistic methods that address the mind, body, and spirit. I believe that true healing requires more than just symptom management; it involves understanding the underlying emotional, psychological, and physical factors that contribute to one’s struggles. My approach to therapy is integrative and client-centered, focusing on creating a safe, nonjudgmental space where individuals can explore themselves.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I was born and raised in Portland, and for as long as I can remember, I have always possessed a deep curiosity about the world. Why is it the way it is? How can we change it? The love I have received from my family throughout my life has taught me that the human bond is the greatest power we, as humans, have to offer this world. Therefore, it is truly a privilege for me to be of service to you. The education I received in both undergraduate and graduate school has awakened a sensitive and critical-thinking lens within me, allowing me to believe that people who seek to understand both the world around them and the world within them are bound by nothing. My priority as a therapist is to be the best resource I can be for you, and to work together to build a healthier future while striving for more internal and external validation.

name: Kate Degenhardt, PhD (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is compassionate, collaborative, and grounded in evidence-based therapies tailored to each client’s unique needs. I primarily use Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) and Exposure and Response Prevention (ERP), proven methods for treating anxiety and OCD. I believe that real change happens when we gently lean into discomfort with support and guidance, developing new skills to navigate life’s challenges. I strive to create a warm, non-judgmental space where clients feel seen, heard, and empowered. Together, we’ll explore patterns of thoughts and behaviors that may be keeping you stuck, and work to develop tools to reduce distress, face fears, and build resilience. Whether it’s managing perfectionism, intrusive thoughts, panic, or navigating life transitions, I’m here to help you or your loved one gain confidence and clarity. My goal is to empower clients to break free from anxiety and live fuller, more connected lives.
More About the People I Treat
I work with children (9+), teens, and adults who are navigating anxiety, OCD, and the challenges life can bring. My clients are often high-achievers, deep thinkers, or sensitive individuals who feel overwhelmed by worry, intrusive thoughts, or the pressure to meet expectations. Some are facing transitions like starting college, a new career, or adjusting to big life changes. Others may struggle with perfectionism, panic, or a sense of being “stuck.” I provide a warm, supportive space to help clients feel understood and empowered. My approach is collaborative and grounded in evidence-based therapies like Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) and Exposure and Response Prevention (ERP). I’m passionate about helping people build confidence, face fears, and live fuller, more connected lives. Whether you’re a parent seeking support for your child or a young adult figuring out next steps, I’m here to help you or your loved one move forward with resilience and hope.

name: Jordan Hubchik (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is warm, inviting, and sprinkled with humor. I am authentically myself, and direct (when needed), so it feels like we are having an engaging and real conversation. I use a lot of metaphor and analogy when I speak, which I believe makes it easer to understand and relate to important therapy themes. My approach is trauma-informed because I build consent and collaboration into every step of the process (i.e., you set the pace, and you tell me when something is or isn't comfortable for you). As a licensed, certified art therapist, I can help you express and externalize your inner thoughts and feelings through spontaneous and/or prompted art-making and creative interventions. The art provides a degree of separation, so troubling thoughts and feelings become less overwhelming, enabling you to reflect and witness yourself more fully. Art can be integrated as much or as little into our therapy encounter as is desired. No artistic experience is required to benefit.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a bisexual, cis-femme woman, in a monogamous, queer relationship. In my leisure time, you'll find me escaping into a romantasy book, watching one of my favorite shows (e.g., Queer Eye, Schitt's Creek, Game of Thrones, Great British Baking Schow), traveling around Oregon with my wife, daughter, and our three-legged dog, or making art. I believe art is a basic human need. Art has the power to nurture creativity, stimulate intellectual growth, and provide emotional solace in ways thinking and speaking cannot. For me, art has the power to make the world slow down and get quieter. When I'm creating something, I am able to ground and reorient with myself and my surroundings, while also losing myself at the same time. I almost always end an art-making session feeling lighter and more embodied, which gives me a greater capacity for new experiences and connecting with the other hobbies, interests, and people I love. Art is a gift I enjoy sharing with my clients.

name: Iphilosophy Psychotherapy
approaches:
Our Background
We are a diverse, multicultural group of providers with extensive experience in serving individuals and families from various cultural and ethnic backgrounds. Our team is dedicated to delivering culturally responsive care, understanding the unique needs of each community we support. We prioritize inclusivity, ensuring that language and cultural differences are respected and integrated into our approach to care.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
We have extensive experience in trauma-informed care, culturally responsive services, and working with diverse populations, including refugees and immigrants. Our team specializes in integrating mental health services with a whole-person approach, ensuring comprehensive support for individuals with complex needs.

name: Oregon Directions
approaches:
Our Background and Approach
We provide kind, thoughtful, and nurturing solutions for a healthy life. We treat anxiety, depression, eating difficulties, loss and grief, stress, trauma, anger, and symptoms of PTSD. We help you navigate life's challenges with respectful and attentive support in a safe environment. We make it easy to start becoming the true you. Counseling is based on the research that people can become more in balance with themselves and in their relationships by understanding themselves, their needs, and their particular issues. People can change! Life changes can be confusing to most of us. We find ourselves doing old things in the same old way and expecting new results, or we do new things and wonder if we’re doing it “right.” Counseling is the opportunity to work with a trained and experienced professional in a safe and trusting environment to help you become the person you truly want to be!
Why We Started this Group Practice
Our vision is to 1) provide our counselors with the environment that enhances their professional development; 2) seek clients in need and provide enlightened, inspired, efficient and encompassing therapies; 3) build a community of thoughtful, kind, nurturing and healthy individuals to implement solutions. We started this group practice because we felt that counselors need a support system that is healthy and strong. Our counselors need to focus on their number one priority: the client. As an agency, our focus is on the counselor and providing them with everything they need. This includes financial, intellectual, and emotional support. We believe all this support flows downstream to you, the client.

name: Emily Overland (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to healing is specific to you, yet I follow the guidelines of Dialectical Behavioral Therapy thus start with building the relationship and stabilizing before moving to trauma work through depth psychology and/or trauma informed yoga. I have been a therapist for 14 years and have worked in hospitals, crisis call centers, community mental health and group practices. I am comprehensively trained in CBT, DBT, and am a trauma informed registered yoga teacher. I highly value the therapeutic relationship and work on growing it by teaching you coping skills and mindfulness to increase self awareness. Healing is beautiful, messy, and sometimes silly process and I welcome all aspects as I welcome all aspects of you. I am here for the long haul and want to help you learn how to heal you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
We are all first time humans, and we all need grace. I believe that we heal in community and you do have everything you need within you, you just do not have the skills to get there. I do not believe in hopeless cases. We can all heal. Shame does not fit in my therapy room; we are here to move forward. I work from a systems perspective and understand that we live and work in a system that is actively working against us and impacting how we enjoy this little life. I have always been an advocate and have attracted advocates to my caseload; helping them manage the difficulty and emotional strain of advocacy work. My office is a safe space, for everyone. I am a yogi and have practiced for 20 years, it is part of my identity and guides my philosophy in life. I may also tell you about what the planets are doing or what phase the moon is in or offer to talk about Tarot if you are interested.

name: Congruent Therapy: Dana Stroud (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
Dana specializes in a holistic approach to therapy, drawing from systemic therapy, narrative therapy, polyvagal theory, and emotion-focused therapy. With a focus on systemic therapy, Dana examines the interconnectedness of individuals within their families and larger systems, aiming for relational healing and empowerment. They utilize narrative therapy techniques to help clients rewrite their life stories, fostering resilience Polyvagal theory informs Dana's understanding of the nervous system's role in emotional regulation and trauma recovery, integrating practices to support nervous system regulation. Emotion-focused therapy enriches their work by exploring and validating emotions, facilitating emotional processing and transformation. Through this integrative approach, Dana creates a safe and empathetic space for clients to explore their identities, heal from past wounds, and cultivate deeper connections with themselves and others.
My Approach to Helping
Narrative therapy, Emotion-Focused therapy, Poly Vagal theory, EMDR & Nature-Based therapy. Dana's interventions are both trauma-informed and client-driven. Dana likes to support clients in choosing what is best for themselves, supporting and shining the light on intervention and diagnostic options that they have from a place of evidenced-based best practices.

name: Casey Black (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to helping is to let whatever is happening in the present moment lead us deeper into your self. If you arrive with a strange feeling, we sit with it. If you arrive with a thought, we unpack it. If you arrive with a numbness, we’ll be with that too. The more we can just be with a thing, the more we’ll understand your suffering. Curiosity and compassion will guide us. Meaning, if we’re curious about your pain and kind to yourself while tending to it, change will happen. Being with yourself in this way may not feel natural at first. That’s okay. We'll work with an eclectic approach that works for you: This may include somatic mindfulness, Internal Family Systems, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, or psychodynamic therapy. Non-clinically speaking, it just means we're going to use whatever we need to to help you feel better. My experience: Before opening a private practice three years ago, I worked for three years as a therapist at the Portland VA.

name: Niko Delgado-McGee (HE/HIS/HIM)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I provide culturally-informed trauma and addiction services to adults and teens (15+) in Oregon and Washington. I offer IFS informed EMDR, psychedelic integration, and somatic focused mindfulness to help you calm your mind and reconnect to your body. I also specialize in addiction counseling so we can address coping mechanisms that no longer serve you as we get to know each other.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I am one of the few mixed race pacific island counselors and am passionate about helping marginalized communities and allies. I have years of experience working with veterans, mental health clinicians, teens in crisis, and families who have been affected by addiction. I value the importance of connecting with nature and am happy to meet you at a local park for our sessions.

name: Katelyn Bessette (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
People show up to therapy in various stages of change, and the only way to facilitate that is through meeting client's where they are at. The importance of mind-body connection is so often forgotten, and my passion is awakening this essential element of life to help people reconnect with themselves and their community. I believe sensuality and exploration of desire is essential to the full expression of who we are as human beings. I received my Master's of Counseling Psychology from Lewis and Clark College. I am a licensed professional counselor (LPC), Certified Sex Therapist (CST) and a certified alcohol and drug counselor (CADCIII). I am also formally trained in DBT and have extensive training in substance use disorders. I am also accepting LPC interns for supervision
Why I Became a Therapist
I began this journey wanting to provide sex therapy and start a sexuality resource center. My path diverged from this into addictions, where I have seen all the same challenges and questions arise in terms of our physical and emotional wellbeing. I have seen how sexual trauma shows up in many different areas of life, how negative coping skills can destroy our hopes and how people have endless capacity to heal. I am excited to rejoin the path to provide therapy to those seeking a deeper understanding of their relationships, their sexuality and their desires. This may include addressing past trauma, relationship difficulties and negative sense self. Whatever it is that is creating barriers for you, I am ready to start that journey with you! I know that there is a huge gap in understanding when it comes to kink and non-monogamy and because of that I am particularly passionate about providing a safe space for people to process without having to educate or feel judged.

name: Tahlia Harrison (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am dedicated to bringing you an open, supportive, space and a willingness to challenge you (gently) when needed. With a collaborative approach, our work together will be responsive to your care needs while helping you connect to the tools you need to move through life with more fulfillment and peace. I am committed to providing a culturally and contextually affirming practice and approach each session with curiosity, warmth, compassion, and respect for your unique lived experience. As a therapist, I feel honored to regularly witness the transformation that occurs when people retune to themselves through the therapeutic process.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am grounded by eco-informed systems thinking and I incorporate attachment theory, existential-humanistic, somatic, and narrative approaches; this combination places value on relationship and experience as foundational to healing. In the presence of substance use, I am dedicated to the principles of harm reduction, a set of practical strategies and ideas aimed at reducing negative consequences associated with drug use. My approach involves working together to help you examine how your inner and external relationships may be supporting or inhibiting your present life and future visions. I am dedicated to culturally affirming practice and approach each session with curiosity, warmth, compassion, and respect for your unique lived experience. Our work together will be collaborative and responsive to your care goals.

name: Pivot Behavioral Health
approaches:
Our Approach to Helping
Pivot offers holistic management of medical and mental health conditions, prioritizing care for the whole person, not just a diagnosis or symptom. Treatment plans are collaborative and didactic. The clinician role is to provide access to resources and information so that the individual can make an informed decision about their care after hearing all of their choices. The decision is always in the hands of the individual, if, they wish to proceed, and how to proceed. We support decision and care autonomy. Our services include medication management, psychotherapy (CBT, ACT), academic and work accommodations, FMLA, short term leave, ESA letters, gender affirming care letters, and much more. Pivot offers a judgment-free, empathetic space to share. Your story will always be recognized as your truth and respected.
Why We are a Good Fit for You
Our providers offer an array of specialities because they are all unique and bring their varied and rich lived experience to their speciality service. While all of our providers specialize in ADHD, they also offer much more. Jonathan May PMHNP member of the LGBTQIA+ community person with ADHD Brea Di Dato FNP PMHNP Person with diabetes (type 1) parent and partner of persons with ADHD specializes in chronic illness burnout and diabetes distress Gloria Sim Le FNP PMHNP partner of persons with ADHD specializes in Asian intergenerational trauma Jody Pauwels PMHNP person with ADHD certified perinatal and postpartum mental health provider Julia Jansen PMHNP parent and partner of persons with ADHD foster parent

name: Kir Rian (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
These beautiful, unpredictable, messy moments of life shape us, and I've learned that defining who we are through these moments helps us build the life we want. It's hard work. I help people navigate and sift through it all, and I believe in a collaborative, mindfulness-based approach. I am fully licensed in OR and WA, hold a Master’s in Clinical Mental Health Counseling, am trauma certified, EMDR trained, and hold extensive training and experience in grief and trauma counseling. Previously, I worked in community mental health, and led processing groups and classes internationally, and with a variety of nonprofits for over 20 years. I am an advocate for equality and social justice, and an ally for marginalized groups. I utilize an integrated theoretical approach based in person-centered, mindfulness, somatic, ACT, EFT, and CBT. I work with a wide range of individuals and diagnoses including anxiety and codependency, and I specialize in grief, illness, and stress management.
Why I Became a Therapist
We hold the capacity to grow and move toward a life of health and happiness. Social, cultural, and family systems influence our perspective, as well as how we communicate with ourselves and within relationships. Sometimes our bodies betray us. Sometimes we need to learn how to carry past traumas or losses. In other words, context matters. The mind, heart, and body are all interconnected, and therapy and mindfulness strategies can significantly affect our stress responses and quality of life. I worked for years with marginalized and trauma-affected individuals through various nonprofits, and that work, combined with chronic illness issues and a sudden death in our immediate family all combined to lead me to do this work. There are tools for coping, and insights to be gleaned from what we've been through. There are connections to be made within ourselves, our community, and even our DNA through things like epigenetics. I help my clients see their light.

name: Justin Little (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I take 3-6 sessions assessing individual or family clients to get an in-depth understanding of what you are experiencing. If we together decide to continue working together, I then help clients begin to gain both insight and skills regarding their mental health and behaviors, in order to empower you to understand yourself and your relationships more clearly and then take concrete steps to control what you can and improve your wellbeing with a lot of support along the way!
Why I Became a Therapist
I have benefited so deeply from my own therapy, as well as becoming more aware and educated about mental health and the ways my own feelings, thoughts and actions impact myself and others. Because I have found great meaning and have benefited from therapy, I am passionate about working with clients like you to help you carve your own path towards healing and thriving in your mind, body and relationships. I also believe that quality mental health care is a human right, and I am passionate about making therapy affordable and accessible to all.

name: Eric Loya (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapeutic practice is rooted in a humanistic approach, incorporating a client/person-centered focus and maintaining a therapeutic space that fosters empowerment and healing. My focus for individual and family therapy is maintaining a therapeutic environment that is safe, egalitarian, and of high cultural awareness. I value adhering to your personal goals for treatment, specific to your needs, as well as pulling from your own personal insights and experiences to build tools for a healthy life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a Master's degree in Social Work (MSW) and License in Clinical Social Work (LCSW). I have provided individual and family therapy to adolescents and adults for over 8 years in Oregon and Washington. I specialize in treating individuals dealing with depression, OCD, anxiety, grief and PTSD.

name: Jennifer Rocks, MA, MS, PsyD (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am interested in where you are coming from, where you are headed, and simply where you are in this moment. Working from a Gestalt therapy approach, and integrating Buddhist psychology, meditation, and mindfulness, I bring a passion for human connection and a curiosity about you to our work together. This will look different for each individual, as each of our processes is unique. Therapy can open us up to new experiences and so the therapeutic relationship is essential to the work. Together we have the potential to increase awareness of your needs and discover what might be preventing you from getting your needs met. Gaining this awareness offers you the possibility to make different choices. With compassion, messiness, courage and laughter, we can find new ways of approaching old "stuff." YOU are the expert on YOU! My job is to offer curiosity, skills and information to help you reach your goals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
PsyD. Clinical Psychology, Pacific University; MA. Counseling Psychology, Pacific University; MS. Speech Communication, Portland State University; BA. Communication & Theatre, Goucher College. I celebrate and honor all aspects of diversity and identity. Using WPATH standards and models, I offer support, assessment and letter writing for trans* and gender non-binary individuals.

name: Grace Forrester (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach stems from an Existential-BIPOC-Humanistic lens. Thus, finding and valuing your authentic narrative is essential in my practice. So, if find, you have goals, dreams, and aspirations, but you are uncertain whether you will ever make everything in your mind work out the way you planned. Here’s to meeting your goals!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a Trauma-informed Therapist, I work from an empathetic, strength-based lens. I strive to create a generous, welcoming, judgment-free, and secure space. Along with you (the client), the aim is to form an equitable collaboration for however long it is needed. My work as a social justice therapist understands how various institutionalized parameters may impact a healthy-wellbeing: such as Anti-Blackness, Gender-Based Oppression, Anti-Climate change, and other forms of systemic injustice. Therefore, my therapeutic praxis is inculcated from an anti-oppressive, gender-affirming, and Mindful-radical acceptance perspective. Additionally, my work has highlighted but is not limited to: Anxiety, Trauma, LGBTQ+, non-monogamy, sex workers, depression, relational conflict, addiction and dependence, Somatic theory, and geropsychology

name: Courtney Boyd
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am excited and prepared to work with clients from a wide array of backgrounds. As a member of the queer community, I have a deep understanding of the unique experiences and challenges faced by LGBTQIA2S+ individuals and am committed to creating a safe and affirming therapeutic space for all. With a background as a registered nurse, I bring a unique perspective and skill set to address the interplay between physical and mental health. Whether you're managing a chronic condition, navigating life transitions, or seeking to enhance your overall quality of life, I believe therapy can be a valuable tool.
Why I Became a Therapist
I was drawn to the field of therapy by a deep admiration for the human spirit's resilience. Witnessing people overcome challenges inspired me to support others on their healing journeys. I believe everyone possesses the capacity for growth and a fulfilling life, and consider it an honor to accompany individuals on this path.

name: Melissa Tomecek (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have a background in mental health counseling, working with individuals facing severe and persistent mental illness, depression, anxiety, attachment issues, and disordered eating. Over time, my focus has narrowed to complex trauma, helping clients navigate the deep, often unseen wounds that shape their emotions, relationships, and sense of self. I understand that trauma is not just about the events that happened but also about the way those experiences continue to live in the body and mind. My approach to therapy is integrative, relational, and deeply client-centered. I draw from psychodynamic therapy to explore unconscious patterns, humanistic therapy to foster self-acceptance, and Internal Family Systems (IFS) to help clients build a compassionate relationship with all parts of themselves. I also use EMDR to help process traumatic memories in a way that reduces their emotional intensity and promotes healing. Above all, I believe in the power of the therapeutic relationship.
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a therapist because I believe in the power of understanding, connection, and healing. From an early age, I was drawn to the complexities of human emotions and the ways our past experiences shape who we are. I saw how unprocessed pain and trauma can impact every aspect of life, and I knew I wanted to help people find clarity, self-compassion, and growth. My passion for this work deepened as I witnessed how transformative the therapeutic process can be—how feeling truly seen and heard can open the door to profound change. I am especially drawn to working with complex trauma because I understand that healing is not just about coping but about reclaiming a sense of self that may have felt lost. Becoming a therapist was never just a career choice for me; it was a calling to create a space where people feel safe enough to explore, process, and ultimately find the strength to move forward.

name: Allison Strausberg (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My collaborative approach integrates evidence-based therapy methods with compassion, humor, and attuned listening. My practice is Internal Family Systems informed and incorporates mindfulness-based somatic work, interpersonal effectiveness skill building such as mutually respectful boundary setting, Emotionally Focused Therapy, and Somatic and Attachment Focused EMDR. I am proactive, provide structure, and welcome long silences, fast-paced intensity, and everything in between. I find that most people who come to see me are ultimately longing for a sense of inner peace, personal agency, and meaningful connections. My focus is on creating a safe and authentic space where you (or you and your partner) can heal your nervous system, expand your emotional capacity, learn to relate more effectively, and begin to build a life that resonates deeply with your dreams and values.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received my Bachelor of Arts from Juniata College with a double major in International Relations and Theater. I have worked as a process facilitator of restorative justice in Maine and a housing access case manager in rural Colorado. I practiced applied arts and theater for social change across many settings including rural Ecuador and Portland, ME before receiving my Master of Social Work with a certificate in Applied Arts and Social Justice from the University of New England in 2016. As a clinician, I worked in residential treatment programs with adults struggling with co-occurring mental illnesses and houselessness and residential treatment programs with neurodivergent teens and teens struggling with complex trauma, before transitioning to outpatient therapy settings in 2019. I am originally from the east coast and relocated to Oregon in 2020. My life outside of work is guided by a love of adventure, play, and connection.

name: Ron Fox (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background
I am a Portland based therapist with an MA counseling psychology and a PhD in clinical psychology. I have over 30 years of experience working with individuals, relationships, and families. I have worked as a therapist and supervisor in community mental health, and I have also taught graduate counseling and psychology courses for students on the path to becoming professional therapists.

name: Caitlin "Caity" Lynch (THEY/SHE)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapy style is collaborative, integrating wisdom from your experiences and from counseling theories rooted in empowerment and equity. I consider your family influences and how systemic "isms" impact you & your relationships. *NOTE: I do not provide ADHD or Autism Assessments. Please seek this through your PCP or a psychologist.* I am a sex-positive therapist and welcome all relationships, whether you're seeking pre-marital therapy, are a poly, kink, BDSM, or LGBTQIA+ relationship. I use Emotion-Focused Therapy to help you get curious about how your attachment styles (the way you relate to significant people) impact your relationships. Together we'll note relational patterns aren't working and strengthen the ones that are. With Narrative Therapy, I'll encourage you to consider your strengths and creative solutions. We'll use Internal Family Systems to help you reconnect to your core, authentic self and parts you may have ignored or been afraid to look at.
Why I Became a Therapist
Therapy has been an important part of my healing work in both relationships and self-discovery. I've also encountered therapists who were unhelpful -- even harmful. I want to be a supportive, healing, decolonized space for individuals, families, and couples/relationships to explore uncomfortable, interesting, difficult, freeing, healing patterns and ideas. My healing journey includes formal therapy, body awareness practices, meditation, spiritual exploration, relational boundary setting, self-education, and lots of trial and error. I expect many of my clients will come to therapy with a similarly rich, complex, and varied healing path. I welcome you to bring your whole selves to therapy sessions. I became a therapist so I could meet you where you are, bring your strengths to light, and send you on your way into this brave, beautiful, scary, strange, and transformative world.

name: Congruent Therapy: Madison Paskett (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a queer individual who experiences ADHD, I understand firsthand how life’s quirks can shape our experiences. I’d describe myself as someone who feels deeply, loves hard, and shows up with an open heart and approachability. I’m all about curating an inclusive therapy space where laughter and lightness are welcome, proving that personal growth doesn’t have to be scary or even that serious. I firmly believe that you are the expert in your own life and the journey you find yourself on is your’s. My role is to walk alongside you in your process, ensuring that you are the one in the driver’s seat and I am with you. I believe the therapeutic relationship is rooted in transparency, respect, trust, and unconditional positive regard. I'm an eclectic clinician who leans into trauma informed, strength-based, and skill building interventions. My work often includes DBT, CBT, motivational interviewing, Parts work, Ecosystemic Structural Family Therapy, Emotionally Focused Therapy, and many more!
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I am most passionate about supporting individuals with intense emotions and dysregulation, helping them navigate these experiences with compassion and resilience. My focus includes young adults, LGBTQIA+ individuals, and those who are neurodivergent. I also specialize in working with couples, polyamorous relationships, kink, and sexuality, recognizing the unique dynamics and challenges they present. Using evidence-based interventions and a holistic approach, I collaborate with individuals and their support systems on a journey of self-discovery. My goal is to help you view your emotions as valuable insights and create a therapeutic space where you feel heard, understood, and validated, equipping you with the skills to build the life you want. I work to cultivate a safe therapy space that meets you where you are at and invites collaboration, compassion, and connection.

name: Sophia Jarvis (SHE/HER)
approaches:
