My Approach to Helping
I use Emotionally Focused Therapy to support couples in building real understanding and repair. For deeper wounds, I also offer EMDR and Flash Technique when talking alone isn’t enough. My style is laid-back and conversational, with a steady focus on what’s happening beneath the surface. We’ll explore the cycles between you, how each of you makes sense of them, and what it would take to feel more seen, safe, and connected. I work collaboratively—I’m not here to fix you, but to help you understand yourselves and each other in deeper, more honest ways so you can create the relationship that's right for you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I come to this work with the understanding that we all live within systems that shape how we relate to ourselves and each other, with some carrying more weight than others. For me, relational healing is inseparable from social justice—it’s about dignity, care, boundaries, repair, self-expression, and the ongoing practice of humanizing ourselves and each other. This work requires deep reflection on both how we’re harmed and how we perpetuate harm, and a commitment to creating space for healing through connection and authenticity.

name: Mary Russell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
All parts of you are welcome- even the messy ones. Therapy with me is an opportunity to get curious about yourself, reflect on relationships, be tender with traumas, and learn to feel and heal through the depths and lightness of life. I work through a trauma-informed lens and craft each session alongside you, being present with your unique wants and needs as we go. We will explore your emotions, patterns, family history, societal impact, inner narrative, and cultural values. We will practice sitting with feelings, learn concrete tools, and use our safe relationship as a home base. I believe that each of us has the innate ability to heal when we have the right support.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I honor you as an individual, your experiences, and your commitment to change. I offer a safe environment to explore your thoughts, feelings, and behaviors. If you struggle with anger, self-doubt, self-critical thoughts, self-destructive behaviors, a lack of motivation, or repeatedly find yourself in unfulfilling relationships, let’s work together toward a more resilient, more peaceful you.

name: BBH Psychiatric Services
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
**We are psychiatric providers. We incorporate elements of psychotherapy into my sessions when appropriate, but we do not conduct therapy-only sessions.** Are you struggling with irritability, anxiety, low mood, poor focus, substance use, or lack of motivation that is impairing your relationships, your career, or your overall quality of life? Have you perhaps tried therapy, but found that your problems persisted? Have you tried medications, but found that psychiatric appointments were too impersonal or that your concerns about possible side effects or worsening symptoms were met with indifference?
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
**We are a psychiatric providers. I incorporate elements of psychotherapy into my sessions when appropriate, but we do not conduct therapy-only sessions.** We believe primarily in a listening-oriented approach to care, as well as incorporating your preferences into our treatment plan as much as possible. We provide evidence-based mental health treatments, which may include medications, nutritional supplements, sleep pattern modifications, referral to talk therapists or treatment centers, and more.

name: Korina Jochim
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach with my clients is one of collaboration and strong teamwork. I favor a directive, psychoeducational way of working with my clients and believe in empowering them with knowledge and tools. As a therapist I join each of my client's in their subjective world, endeavoring to understand them deeply and support their process, whatever their healing or life goals may be. Because we are all different, I don't work the same way with everyone, and adapt my approach to the needs of my clients. What therapy is to each person is a unique experience. Some need to resolve a crisis or grapple with a life transition or loss. Others want to focus on perceived blocks to moving forward into a state of greater mental wellness. Still others need to re-work their difficult attachments to early caregivers and require a longer therapeutic relationship. After a therapeutic relationship has been established, I make myself available to my clients for booster sessions when the need arises.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Prior to my psychotherapy career I was a journalist and professional writer. People's stories have always been what compelled me and drove me to understand and connect on a deeper level. In graduate school I focused on equity and inclusion, addictions and trauma, and my earlier career experience was in residential and outpatient treatment. Gradually I began to focus on intersectional approaches to mental health and have experience in treating mood disorders, abuse recovery, addictions, grief and loss, and negative relationship patterns. As a Marriage and Family therapist I view individuals as inextricably linked to the systems and groups of which they are a part. I am trained in trauma-informed care and mindfulness modalities such as third-wave CBT. In my free time I blog about intimate partner violence and other systems of control and advocate for abuse survivors.

name: Kristin Schuchman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer a mix of counseling skills and patient yet clear-headed insight, helping individuals experiencing anxiety and overwhelm navigate personal and professional obligations. I can offer empathy and understanding as well as tools to help clients overcome self-limiting beliefs, look inward for sources of motivation and self-confidence, build better boundaries, and cultivate a sense of mindfulness and inner calm. I love helping people unlock inner potential and build fulfilling lives while prioritizing self-care and personal relationships. I excel in supporting clients overcome the common threats to personal angst -- indecisiveness, lack of confidence, fear of risk, toxic workplaces, bullying, people-pleasing tendencies, and social anxiety. I offer a safe place as well as perspective and tangible tools for helping you stay on tops of life's demands without sacrificing your personal well-being and physical health.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Believing you need to keep the spark alive in both home and work life, I challenge clients to create lives that provide meaning and self-expression while maintaining balance. I work well with individuals who identify as creative or purpose-driven (or a combination thereof). I am committed to women's economic empowerment and am an active supporter of LGBTQIA+ and Black Lives Matter. I offer expertise for remote workers wanting to feel more engaged with colleagues and a sense of purpose, parents and caregivers balancing family and professional obligations, re-launching after an absence, and anyone who believes Brené Brown is right when she says, "When we have the courage to walk into our story and own it, we get to write the ending." I meet clients over Zoom and in-person in a COVID-safe, routinely sanitized office.

name: David Hanna (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that change through counseling stems from the connection built between two people, providing a sense of security that allows for exploring new ways of thinking, feeling, or living your life. As your therapist, I want to be someone who you can speak freely with, who can be there for the good and the bad as you pave a new path in your life. My goal is to build a trusting relationship that supports you in navigating complex life experiences. Utilizing an integrative therapeutic approach, I use various methods to help clients explore underlying patterns in their lives, develop psychological flexibility, and create meaningful change. Along with these clinical approaches, I engage with my clients in a manner that is both sensitive and analytical. My goal is to create a compassionate, collaborative space where individuals can understand their experiences, resolve internal conflicts, and move toward more authentic and fulfilling lives.
More About the People I Treat
I work with people as young as 14, and adults throughout the lifespan. You might be coming to counseling with something very specific that you would like to work on, or you might be unsure why things are not feeling right in your life. Perhaps you are feeling like your life is not going the way you had hoped, and you are missing a sense of purpose, excitement, or passion. In all of these cases, I work with clients in a collaborative manner to help figure out the best path forward. I strongly believe in approaching counseling through a holistic lens, considering the ways that your environment, work or school related stress, relationships, social systems, and traumatic or other lived experiences might affect you in the present. With these considerations, I will support you in building a greater sense of self-awareness, allowing you to develop tools to live more closely in line with your values and aspirations.

name: Kasey Serdar (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My work focuses on helping people understand themselves in greater depth so they feel empowered to relate to themselves in a different way. It takes courage to look at yourself in depth, but doing so is necessary for lasting change. Together, we will get to the heart of why you see yourself the way you do. Most clients appreciate my compassionate, yet interactive and straightforward approach to therapy. Though I utilize techniques from a range of approaches in my work with clients, I specialize in psychoanalytic psychotherapy. Psychoanalytic psychotherapy is helpful in identifying and addressing unconscious patterns that often impact the way we see ourselves and live our lives. When you begin to understand these patterns in greater depth, you build your capacity to change what is not working in your life.
More About the People I Treat
Many of my clients struggle with anxiety, self-esteem, perfectionism, eating disordered behavior, and body image concerns. I am also passionate about providing affirming care to LGBTQIA+ individuals and I have specialized experience providing affirmative care with trans, non-binary, and gender expansive individuals.

name: BrightSpire Health
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Our goal is to create a personalized care plan for each of our clients that is tailored to their individual needs and goals. We believe in the power of healing and outcome informed care. Our team is passionate about helping clients reach their fullest potential, and here to provide support and guidance on the path to a brighter future BrightSpire Health will work diligently to connect you with a Therapist who aligns with your wellness journey. We look forward to supporting you on your path to health and happiness!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Therapy must be guided by the most rigorous ethical and clinical standards, and delivered with compassion, authenticity, and respect. Clinical integrity is the core foundation of every organizational, clinical, and administrative decision made. Healthy connection requires honoring the dignity of the other, engaging respectful inquisition, and creating safety for personal expression. Our mission is to promote health and wellness for our clients, our team, and support the pursuit of the greatest life fulfillment possible for all. We aim to be the premier outpatient mental health company within the Pacific Northwest utilizing the most effective standards of practice and providing the highest quality of service to the community.

name: Kate Parker-Shames (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Deciding to enter therapy can be frightening and uncomfortable. I will work to help you feel at ease through this process. Through my calm presence, strong support, and our therapeutic relationship, I can help you move through this time. You can find new connection and healing to help move you towards the changes you wish to see. I offer a nonjudgmental space where we can explore together sources of your pain or distress, while developing new ways of interacting with yourself and others. I offer empathy, experience, and expertise to guide you through this process. Please visit my website at www.parkershamescounseling.com for more details on my practice and approach. Contact me to set up a free initial phone consultation. You do not have to face this alone. I can help!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received my Bachelor's in Sociology from Pitzer College, in Claremont, CA in 2004. I worked for many years as a public health researcher and educator before returning to school to pursue a degree in counseling. I received my Master's of Science degree in Counselor Education, Marriage, Couples and Family Therapy from Portland State University in 2014. I opened my private practice in 2015. I currently hold two licenses in the State of Oregon, as both a Licensed Professional Counselor (License # C5466) and a Licensed Marriage & Family Therapist (License # T2493). I was born and raised in southern Oregon. I have lived in Portland for 20 years. When I am not working, I enjoy travel, cooking, hiking, creating art, and exploring beautiful places in the Pacific Northwest with my family. I believe that therapy is a powerful tool for change, and it is my privilege to provide a space for you to explore the possibility of healing for you.

name: Angelie Karabatsos (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is based on 3 C's: compassion, collaboration, and creativity. Individualized treatment is provided based on each person or couples' need. Interventions include recognizing unhealthy thinking patterns, reframing self-limiting talk, developing behavioral and emotional coping cards, utilizing principles of mindfulness and guided imagery exercises to reduce anxious feelings. Empowering my clients to see new opportunities or directions is the goal in every session.
Why I Became a Therapist
I became interested in human behavior after taking several psychology courses my senior year of high school. My undergraduate and graduate degrees focused on organizational development and marketing, which allowed me to explore behavior from a holistic and analytical perspective. My business career spans 23 years and encompasses relationship, marketing, and project management. I have worked in the Banking, E-commerce and Advertising industries. I have seen the impact of mergers and layoffs. Watched couples’ struggle balancing careers, family, and relationships.

name: Kaley Carpenter (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I specialize in play therapy and EMDR. Past experiences, attachment, and familial relationships are important to therapy work, though my main focus in the therapy room is a strengths-based and present-focused approach. While in the counseling room, I will focus on mindfulness, attuning to both myself and my client and keeping our work focused on the here-and-now and bring awareness to the client’s emotional state. I will utilize different play therapy techniques, along with EMDR. Overall, my approach is integrative and I enjoy tailoring my therapeutic work to the specific needs and development of my client. Overall, my approach is integrative and I enjoy tailoring my therapeutic work to the needs and development of my client.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I was born and raised in Portland and am a big Portland Timbers and Thorns soccer fan! In my free time I also love reading, going on walks, and enjoying coffee with friends and family.

name: Josephine Jardine (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Using a range of adaptive therapeutic modalities, I offer a collaborative and safe space where all aspects of your experience—whether spontaneous, curious, brave, or vulnerable—are welcome. With gentle challenging, perspective shifting, humor, kindness, and deep respect, I support you in exploring, understanding, and integrating all parts of yourself, helping you feel seen, held, and empowered.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My therapy-style is rooted in the belief that individuals shouldn't bear the sole responsibility for creating a quality of life without the support of reciprocal communal care, advocacy, ad accountability. As your therapist, I play an active, and involved part of your larger circle of care. I work closely with multiple supervisors, and clinical consultation groups, so that my practice maintains the ethical and updated standards of our field.

name: Eliot B. Altschul, Ph.D. (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My work is inclusive and queer-affirming. I enjoy working with relationship dynamics of all types, and my approach is interpersonal and depth oriented. *I am able to provide online teletherapy visits for residents of OR, WA, CA, NY, and IL.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received my Ph.D. in Clinical Psychology from the California School of Professional Psychology, Berkeley and my B.A in Psychology from Boston University. I completed my clinical fellowship at Stanford University, and my pre-doctoral internship at Boston University. Prior faculty distinctions include: Columbia and Emory Universities.

name: Harley Steen (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work from a strength-based, depth-oriented, psychodynamic perspective. I provide nonjudgmental therapeutic guidance while fostering interpersonal growth and change through problem solving, cognitive behavioral skills development, and the power of a therapeutically corrective interpersonal relationship with my clients.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Therapy in my view is most effective and valuable when it is a collaboration. In our work together, we will engage in a partnership, because l believe that you hold within you the power to create happiness, contentment, and peace.

name: Dr. Uhunoma Osadolor
approaches:
My Background and Approach
With a background in mental health and psychiatric care, I bring a blend of clinical expertise and compassionate support to my practice. My experience spans across specialized assessments for individuals dealing with ADHD, Autism Spectrum Disorder, and various personality disorders, including Borderline and Narcissistic Personality Disorders. I prioritize creating a safe, empathetic space where clients can explore their mental health challenges and find tailored strategies for resilience and personal growth. My approach is integrative, combining evidence-based techniques like cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT) with a strengths-based perspective, helping clients focus on their goals and strengths to overcome obstacles. I emphasize a collaborative partnership, working closely with clients to understand their unique experiences, and fostering a therapeutic journey that’s both supportive and empowering.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that mental health care should be holistic, compassionate, and accessible to everyone. My approach is guided by the idea that each person deserves to be seen and heard in their unique experience and that effective care combines both scientific rigor and genuine empathy. I’m particularly interested in how lifestyle, personal growth, and community impact mental well-being, as well as how we can incorporate mindfulness and resilience-building strategies into everyday life. Outside of my practice, I enjoy exploring nature, learning about new wellness practices, and dedicating time to creative outlets. These interests often shape my understanding of health as a balance between mind, body, and environment, inspiring me to guide clients toward a well-rounded and sustainable approach to personal growth.

name: Katrina Severin (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Prior to becoming a therapist, I completed a BA in Global Anthropology from University of the Pacific in 2014. My studies encompassed various aspects of culture and the human experience, which continues to inform my worldview and my understanding of human nature. In 2022, I completed a MS in Clinical Rehabilitation Counseling, which focused on the disability experience from a biopsychosocial perspective. I use an integrated multicultural, anti-oppression, feminist, trauma-informed, and person-centered approach that draws from Gestalt therapy, Jungian psychology, Internal Family Systems, Polyvagal theory, somatics, and mindfulness. I also draw personally from various sources of wisdom and creativity, including astrology, Quantum theory, art and poetry, ancestry, breath and movement, plant medicine, and the Chakra system.
My Values as a Therapist
We will all die someday - and yet, we often forget to live while we are alive. We are all born into circumstances beyond our control, and many of us do not get what we need in the crucial years of development. We find beautifully creative ways to adapt in service of our survival, only to become unconsciously stuck in rigid postures and patterns that keep us defended, reactive, and deeply unsatisfied as we move through the ever-changing conditions of our lives. In our work together, I can help you to increase your awareness of these patterns, review the scripts you've been given, and increase your capacity for agency and choice as you learn to listen more deeply to your own voice. In our work together, I will help you to honor your body as a historical record keeper, a deep source of wisdom, and a vessel for transcendence. I look forward to supporting you in living a life of meaning.

name: Ray Nelson (HE/HIM)
approaches:
You don’t have to do it alone! Finding a therapist who gets you can be life changing. I work collaboratively with my clients helping them focus on what they're struggling with in a safe, comfortable environment. I encourage my clients to reconnect with feelings and their bodies so they can stop repeating the patterns that are holding them back from who they really are.
I’m a rain loving, urban-dwelling Portlander. Prior to becoming a therapist, I worked in the trades as a DC power engineer and general contractor. In my spare time I enjoy building things, being snobbish about craft beer, B-grade shark movies, and doing outdoorsie things. My goal as a therapist is to provide a safe and compassionate environment that supports my clients in enhancing their awareness, acceptance, and self-expression as they move towards their best self.

name: Brad Creel (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer a compassionate, non-judgmental, therapeutic environment where my clients can feel welcome and safe. This setting allows clients the opportunity to express themselves deeply, and relate to their authentic self. I utilize an eclectic approach, based on the different needs of my clients. As a trained Depth therapist, I believe that healing can happen through accessing the realm of the unconscious. This process of exploring thoughts and images can be daunting, but also immensely satisfying and life changing. Creativity is an important part of my practice as it is a unique way access material that lies bound up in the psyche. I practice a blend of theories that best serve my clients and help them find their inner voice.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I attended Pacifica Graduate Institute and received a MA in Counseling Psychology with an emphasis in Jungian Psychology. My Master's thesis focused on the process of creativity, inspiration, and overcoming writer' (or artist's) block. I have worked professionally in the arts as an illustrator, woodworker, and performing musician. I also have had past experience owning a small business, and understand the stresses and challenges of running an organization. In my practice, I like to take time to slow down and get to know the parts of yourself that have protected you in the past, but no longer serve you in healthy ways. This journey of self discovery will allow you come into better relationship with your true self and help to create inner wholeness.

name: Kristin Fialko (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We often find ourselves—our bodies, our spirits—navigating the winding path back to health after overwhelming experiences: birth, chronic illness, trauma, grief, or profound loss. Reigniting our inner fire and returning to emotional equilibrium can feel elusive, yet deeply vital. As a somatic psychotherapist, I see my role not as a fixer, but as a sacred collaborator—walking alongside clients as they reconnect with the innate wisdom of their bodies and process the echoes of trauma, anxiety, depression, and grief. My approach weaves together Somatic techniques, EMDR, Parts Work, Transpersonal psychology, mindfulness, and energetic work. Grounded in a trauma-informed, anti-racist, and socially aware framework, I hold space for healing that honors the complex intersections of mind, body, spirit, and lineage. The body is a vessel of profound intelligence—emotional, spiritual, and ancestral—and I am here to help you listen, reclaim, and remember your inherent capacity to heal and thrive.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Rooted in creativity, sensitivity, and a deep reverence for healing, my work as a therapist is shaped by my lived experience as an artist, HSP, mother, chronic illness warrior, and activist. My path—from a childhood in rural PA to studying art in Baltimore, to reclaiming my body and voice in San Francisco—led me to somatic psychotherapy as both a personal and professional calling. I hold a MA in Somatic Psychology from California Institute of Integral Studies and have over a decade of experience working in private practice & community mental health. I move through the world with deep feeling, a vivid imagination, and a relentless curiosity about the human spirit. I am someone who values complexity, who listens closely to the spoken and unspoken, and who is always in process—learning, unlearning, and tending. My work is an extension of who I am: intuitive, grounded, creative, and committed to collective liberation.

name: Congruent Therapy (SHE/HE/THEY)
approaches:
What We Specialize In
We are a group of therapists dedicated to serving a diverse clientele, including LGBTQIA2S+ individuals, couples, and poly systems. Our affirmative approach is grounded in empathy, curiosity, and compassion. We specialize in addressing a range of issues, from trauma and depression to anxiety, ADHD, relational challenges, self-love, and identity exploration. Utilizing a comprehensive toolkit, we draw from modalities such as Emotionally Focused Couple Therapy, The Gottman Method, Narrative Therapy, Internal Family Systems, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, EMDR, Dialectical Behavioral Therapy, and Solution-Focused Therapy. Our goal is to provide tailored support for your unique journey toward healing and growth.
Why We are a Good Fit for You
At our core, we honor your expertise in your own life, fostering a collaborative journey. Our role is to provide steadfast support, akin to a secure attachment, as we engage in the therapeutic process. While we offer insights and a plethora of tools, it's your agency and commitment that remain pivotal in driving meaningful change. Your active participation is the linchpin to the transformative journey we embark on together.

name: Liz Ortland (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
In 2017 I graduated from Lewis & Clark College's Marriage Couple and Family Therapy program with a specialization in Sex Therapy. I gained experience working with refugees and low-income individuals, couples, families, and groups at Lutheran Community Services Northwest. I have recently returned from living in rural Japan where I provided teletherapy for English-speaking expats, repatriates, and Japanese citizens living throughout Japan from 2019-2023. Because of my experiences living abroad and working with immigrants both in the US and in Japan, I developed a deep appreciation for the importance of having a sense of belonging and safety in the world. I have a passion for working across cultural and language barriers to help people explore their identities and values, communicate more clearly with others, and to cultivate a life worth living.
My Approach to Helping
I try to form a unique and genuine connection with every client, which means what I do looks a little different with everyone. Together you and I will co-create the healing space that works best for you. You are the expert on your own experiences. I am here to help you in listen more deeply to yourself to discover stuck patterns, shed light on disowned parts of yourself, identify needs you may have a hard time voicing, and unravel struggles with worthiness and barriers to living authentically. We may practice new coping skills, learn about trauma and the body, explore alternative ways to communicate needs / navigate relationships, and face challenging past experiences together through mutual dialogue. We may also bring in art, music, philosophy, spirituality, or whatever else it is that gives meaning to your world. I aim to empower you to take responsibility for your own growth and healing and emerge with more confidence in navigating your life, relationships, and decisions.

name: Kirah Doerr (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is integrative, combining therapeutic techniques such as Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT), and Motivational Interviewing to meet your unique needs. I’m committed to creating a nurturing space where you feel truly heard and valued. Together, we’ll explore your emotions, identify your strengths, and address the challenges you’re facing. I believe in the healing power of understanding and connection, and I’m passionate about helping you embrace your growth and potential. Our sessions will be a place where your experiences are honored, allowing us to dive into your thoughts, examine the beliefs shaping your self-view, and develop the skills you need to live more fully. Whether you’re managing anxiety, facing societal pressures around body image, or exploring your identity, I’m here to support you every step of the way. Let’s work together to help you feel more connected, confident, and capable of navigating whatever life brings your way.
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a therapist because I understand firsthand the impact of life’s challenges and the importance of having support during difficult times. During my own transitions, I often wished for someone to help me navigate my emotions, which led me to study psychology. I discovered that understanding emotions and the ways we connect with others can bring profound change. I believe in the power of therapeutic connection. I’m passionate about providing a supportive environment where you can freely explore your experiences and challenges. Each person’s path is different, and I’m committed to helping you find what feels right for you. I’m here to help you reconnect with your strengths and embrace your personal growth. My treatment approach aligns best with clients who seek support for anxiety, stress, depression, life transitions, identity development and exploration, disordered eating, trauma, and attachment wounds.

name: Blake Locher (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background
I am a practiced therapist with experience in community counseling, victims' services, and intensive outpatient treatment. I am a Certified Rehabilitation Counselor. I pursued this education because it provided special training in helping individuals with disabilities achieve their social, emotional and career goals. This also means that I have training in helping individuals adjust to their own internal and external barriers to change regardless of ability. Outside of providing individual counseling, I am an active member of the Master’s degree program at Portland State University, providing therapy, training and mentoring to students enrolled in the program. For more information on my background and services, visit my website: blakelocherportlandcounseling.com
My Approach to Helping
I practice pragmatic depth therapy. My approach combines the powerful change that comes from addressing our inner psychological world and more practical behavioral change that takes place outside ourselves – in our actions, our relationships and our workplace. After collaborating with you to increase your hope, resiliency, and self-awareness we can begin to untangle the beliefs and behaviors that restrict wellbeing and ownership of your life. Then, with this freedom, I work with you to create a life where who you really are is aligned with the choices you make out in the world. Together, we uncover the way to more satisfaction, joy, and meaning in your life. The make-up of my approach includes relational, psychodynamic, and cognitive behavioral theories. As we get to know each other, I tailor the right balance of these theories to best suit your needs.

name: Theresa Ferrante (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I bring over 20 years of experience to my clinical practice and advanced training in evidence-based approaches like EMDR, PACT, AEDP, ACT, DBT, CBT, ART, and CFT, among others. If your first thought is, “What are all these letters?”—you’re not alone. Therapy acronyms can feel like alphabet soup, but don’t worry. What truly matters is the relationship we build, and I believe that’s the key ingredient for meaningful change. I start by getting to know you, understanding your needs, and tailoring my approach to work for you. I studied Psychology and Spanish at the University of Notre Dame and earned two Master’s Degrees (MSW and MPA:HA). I’m passionate about continuous learning to ensure I provide the best therapy possible. My style is down-to-earth, collaborative, and interactive—with a touch of lightheartedness to keep things human.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe non-judgmental curiosity is a far better teacher than self-blame or shame. While harsh self-criticism may be the language many of us learned early on, I believe we can all gain fluency in self-compassion with the right support. I’m continually in awe of human resilience—time and again, I see people draw on their innate wisdom and strength to navigate life’s challenges. Though many of us are conditioned to disconnect from our inner resources, I believe it’s always possible to reconnect. With time, support, and practice, we can tap back into the well of strength and wisdom within ourselves.

name: Ashley Adler (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
When I work with relationships, I take the time to really understand your dynamic—how you interact, where you get stuck, and how your past experiences might be shaping the present. The way we love and communicate is often influenced by what we learned growing up, the relationships we’ve been in, and the unspoken stories we carry. I also bring a focus on equity, exploring how each person’s experiences, identities, and histories show up in your relationship. When I work with individuals, I focus on creating a space of safety, curiosity, and deep listening. I use body-based awareness to help you reconnect with yourself in ways that go beyond words. While I will gently challenge you to step into growth, I trust that you are the expert in your own experience, and my role is to guide and support—not to push.
My Approach to Helping
I am a white, Jewish, queer cis woman, and my identities shape not only how I move through the world but also how I show up as a therapist. I am committed to providing culturally attuned therapy that honors each person’s unique lived experience. I know that healing doesn’t happen in a vacuum—our identities, histories, and environments all shape us. That’s why I approach my work with a deep respect for the complexities of each person’s lived experience, creating a space where all parts of who you are can be seen, held, and honored.

name: Judith McIntosh (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My role as a therapist is to foster psychological health by assisting my clients to identify and utilize their existing strengths. My approach is collaborative and supportive. Symptoms of depression - sadness, loneliness, meaninglessness, hopelessness - can be excruciating. However, each symptom holds the potential for greater self-awareness, the key to moving beyond it. By sharing your story with me, I can help you dig down to the root of the issue and uncover its hidden value. My therapeutic approach combines the practicality of cognitive behavioral therapies with the creativity of depth psychology and mythology by tapping into the realm of the imagination and utilizing a variety of tools that may include storytelling, art, writing, literature, film, and dreams.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In addition to my private practice, I have had the good fortune to work in a variety of mental health settings including a community counseling clinic, an employee assistance program, a non-profit support community for cancer patients and caregivers, county mental health services and a crisis line. I have taught counseling and communication skills courses at California State University Northridge, as well as provide clinical supervision to MFT graduate students. Although I value all of these experiences, I am thrilled that at this point in my life I have to freedom to focus exclusively on my private practice. While my previous experience has provided me with a solid clinical foundation, my passion lies in helping clients tap into the unconscious realm through humanities-based expressive arts. I love how working this way brings an element of creativity and fun to the therapeutic process with less focus on diagnosis and more attention to each person's inherent movement toward health.

name: Jessica Lea (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I frequently help my patients challenge and reshape the thought patterns contributing to their anxiety or depression, cope with chronic pain or illness, and navigate family conflict. During therapy sessions, I listen empathetically and seek to fully understand each person's struggles and needs. I have been told that I have an excellent way of looking at complex situations and helping patients find solutions. I always strive to support and empower my patients. I advocate for equity for members of all marginalized populations, with a particular emphasis on LGBTQIA+ rights. I am trained in Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Psychodynamic Therapy, Collaborative Problem-Solving, Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT), Solution-Focused Therapy, Motivational Interviewing, Acceptance & Commitment Therapy, Trauma-Informed Therapy, Exposure Therapy, Person-Centered Therapy, and Mindfulness.
My Approach to Helping
I earned my BA in Sociology from the University of Portland and my MSW from Portland State University. My background in social work enables me to see how the different aspects of my clients' lives intersect. I am trained in crisis intervention and suicide risk assessment and prevention. I am also very familiar with the resources available in the Portland metro area. I have worked in a variety of settings: outpatient, inpatient, intensive outpatient (IOP), triage, and emergency room (ER). I have led individual therapy, group therapy, and family therapy. I have experience working with adolescents, teenagers, adults, and seniors. I often use Cognitive Behavioral Therapy and have taught CBT to thousands of patients! However, I will incorporate other therapeutic techniques into our sessions as I learn more about you. During your first session, we'll explore what brought you to therapy. Together we'll come up with a plan to make progress toward your goals and the life you want!

name: Jessica Wynant (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I have years of experience in working with folks struggling with disordered eating. Whether you are in recovery for an eating disorder or want to improve your relationship with food and your body, I am here to walk alongside you in this journey. I practice from a Health At Every Size (HAES) approach and believe that we are all deserving of food and body freedom. I help clients identify and break down harmful patterns with food and exercise that lead to undue stress, chronic dieting, and disordered eating. I also have experience working with couples and families. Our relationships are often the most rich and at times challenging aspects of our lives. All couples and families experience times of friction and disconnection and I am here to help. Whether you need guidance in navigating a difficult decision, need help reconnecting, or are simply wanting to strengthen your communication, we can work together to achieve your goals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In my work as a therapist, I aim to provide a safe and inclusive space for all and I believe deeply that we are wildly capable of healing. As someone who experienced the transformative work of therapy in my college years, I instantly became fascinated with mental health and helping others achieve healing in their own lives. I am constantly in awe of the resiliency of the human spirit and feel deeply grateful for the work I get to do. When I'm not in the office, I love cooking and spending time with my wife and our pup. I also love exploring the beauty of the Pacific Northwest and watching lots of women's soccer.

name: Debra Schnack (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I take a holistic approach to therapy, concerned with the overall long-term well-being of the individual and their family. I work with clients on partner relational issues, family of origin conflict, anxiety, depression, low self-esteem, and other issues in a safe, supportive, and non-judgmental environment. I practice EMDR, Brainspotting, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT), and Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), each depending upon the issues we are addressing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Realizing you have a problem that you can't solve on your own is a brave first step for any person. Seeking out help and support is one of the toughest decisions to make. And as your therapist, I want you to know that I will be here to support you and work with you to help you become the person you want to be.

name: Norma Nance (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Cultivating a relationship built on trust with the people I collaborate with is something I value deeply. Having the ability to trust your therapist allows you to bring more of your true self to the table, which directly affects the quality of the work we’ll do together. I’ll work with you to build a mutual and trusting relationship, because a therapeutic relationship models relationships out in the world. Because pain and trauma are realities of our world, our past selves often inform our present relationships, even when we don’t want them to. Together we’ll work to help you understand and identify what parts of yourself show up in your life today. We’ll unpack your history and upbringing to discern how they’re potentially impacting your current relationships. Generally, my style of work is a mixture of collaborating with clients, while also challenging them to dig deeper. But above all, I approach my clients with gentleness, compassion, and I will always keep our focus on healing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I was raised in a conservative Christian environment where sex, sexuality, and gender were defined by my religion. This in turn created internalized feelings of shame about who I was. Since then, I’ve done a lot of educating and relearning to shape my identity into who I am today. As a black, brown, and queer woman, my identities inform my work and the way I show up for my clients and community.

name: Gabriel Trees (HE / HIM)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
Are you struggling with communication issues in your relationship? Reaching out for support is a big step. Searching the internet, you will encounter an overwhelming amount of advice on relationships. Finding someone who can help you discern and apply what is helpful and what is not is where a professional relationship specialist is invaluable. You can expect me to offer straight-forward dialogue, diverse experience, and a compassionate path forward. As a Level-3 Gottman Therapist, I specialize in a wide range of evidence-based approaches. Let's shift disruptive patterns into balance, clarity, and fulfillment - and help you bring joy and understanding to your relationships.
Why I Became a Therapist
Besides being a parent, husband, and friend, I also have extensive experience as a caretaker of children. Relationships in general have always held my attention and curiosity. Having the privilege of becoming a relationship specialist has been one of the most humbling and ever-inspiring focuses of my life. I love this work and I'm here for you. I'm available for In-Person sessions (at our SE office) and Online session. If you have any questions or would like to meet, I offer FREE Consultations.

name: Dr. Michelle Pliske (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Creating an environment of safety means having a focus on relationships. It is impossible to think creatively, problem solve or think through some of the most profound or difficult experiences in life if there isn't a feeling of trust. Relationships matter and I believe it is important to invest in building that trust and safety in therapy. Children find trust and safety through an environment in which they can be authentic. Play therapy offers a child an environment of safety. If children are able to say what they think or how they feel through toys/art/sand/media with someone who can translate their behavior and facilitate the process, they will respond positively and can successfully work through their presenting issues.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a doctoral degree in clinical social work and social policy with a trauma specialization. This includes not only how we support individuals but communities as a whole. I have post-graduate certifications in play therapy, adoption and foster care, trauma informed care, children's yoga and animal assisted human health. I work in partnership with my therapy dog Mercury. I am dual board certified as a supervisor in social work/counseling and play therapy practices.

name: Alli Swift (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to counseling rests on my belief that the therapeutic relationship is central to client growth and change. Creating a space of safety, trust and unconditional positive regard for the client guides all my interactions and interventions.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a Masters degree in Professional Mental Health Counseling from Lewis & Clark in Portland, OR, and have spent most of my clinical hours working with adolescents around issues of anxiety, self image, relational difficulties and life transitions. I believe in social justice for all, and creating an environment that welcomes anyone and everyone.

name: Kristen Seghete (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have 20 years of experience working in women's and infants' mental health, and I am certified in perinatal mental health. My expertise includes postpartum depression and anxiety, trauma, and parenting. In our work together, you will learn how to make space for sadness, anxiety, and fear. We'll work on pushing pause on the endless loop of worry, guilt, and judgment that plays in your mind. Once again, you will learn how to have compassion for yourself, nurture yourself, and ask for what you need. We will figure out how you can become less isolated. My approach includes understanding how feelings and emotions are related to each other and how we can change our behaviors (cognitive behavioral therapy). I also focus on mindfulness-based interventions, meaning using non-judgmental, present-moment awareness as a guiding principle to make space for your inner experiences and emotions.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe in compassionate and collaborative care based in an authentic and non-judgmental relationship. I care about your story, the experiences that have shaped you, and any pain you carry with you. Trauma is something that shapes us, gets passed down, and physically affects us. I am particularly passionate about understanding how women's experiences of trauma affect how the world interacts with them and how they can interact with the world. Therapy takes work. Hard work. And I will honor and respect your unique journey. I am here to meet you where you are at right now and recognize you are doing the best you can at any given moment.

name: Kaiden Craddock
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I’ve worked in the mental health field for 10 years with specialized interest in supporting individuals with intense trauma. In that time I have learned that healing looks different for each person and our approach to treating trauma must be as individualized and the folks we are serving. I operate from a framework that looks beyond the individual and considers the systems and environments that impact their wellbeing I have long witnessed the effects of complex and inter-generational trauma and have had the privilege of witnessing the bravery of individuals with the desire to break cycles. I strongly believe that we are all a work-in-progress, that growth is a lifelong journey, and that every person deserves an opportunity to be seen and heard in a supportive and nonjudgmental space.

name: karen myers (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Did you know that trauma effects 70% of the population? It's the silence and the shame that keeps us stuck or oscillating in all areas of life, including financially. Your bank account suffers as much as your mind or your body. Because by age 35 your decisions are a memorized set of patterns, beliefs and behaviors. So you keep doing the same thing over and over and over again. Year after year. Maybe a different context but the outcome is the same. It's exhausting. I bring my 35 years of training from EMDR, gestalt, NLP, Self psychology and a little known discovery called the window of consolidation. By incorporating the Window of Consolidation, learning theory and other therapeutic processes so you can finally release the resistance, the fear and the overwhelm. We start by training the brain to find and neutralize unconscious blocks and negative memories. This cutting edge method is validated by neuroscience.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I work with my clients in a fluid and dynamic partnership. We will begin where you are and work with techniques that you find useful to embark on a journey to where you want to be. We will work together to apply techniques that are effective for you. I will provide guidance and resources that can remove stumbling blocks to beneficial changes in your life. All change is a journey from an undesirable to a desired state – your outcome.

name: Sara Vetiver (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that every person holds the map to their own healing. Within the therapeutic relationship, I support my clients in re-connecting with their inherent wisdom and the tools needed to further liberate it. To do this work, I like to move slowly to build trust and forge a relationship that feels solid yet flexible. This relationship building occurs through curiosity, patience, and embodiment. In this space, it is then possible to foster comfort in vulnerable exploration and to cultivate mindfulness of one's patterns and constrictions, one's desires for growth, and all the various parts of the self that are waiting to be seen and loved again. Using this mindfulness practice as an anchor, we can begin to explore trauma and process it gradually and intentionally over time. I use this approach with both individuals and couples, co-creating an environment in which we can see our cycles, and from this perspective, feel the agency to make new choices.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My path has been by shaped by somatics, yoga, and wilderness immersion; my writing practice and transformation through arts’ communities; the cohort and coursework of California Institute of Integral Studies; community organizing; deep dives into psychedelic healing; my journey of understanding how trauma has shaped me— the losses and the fortifications; and, most significantly, the relationships that bless my life with ongoing opportunities for mutual evolution. I am a queer, femme, she/they, white, Pacific Islander, first-generation college student, an oldest sibling, temporarily able-bodied, a settler on Indigenous lands, and a lifelong learner. I am a lover of poetry, philosophy, astrology, backpacking, swimming, and pups.

name: Anne Horgan (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is eclectic and person-centered. I utilize EMDR for trauma healing and incorporate elements of Cognitive Behavior Therapy, art and narrative therapy, Dialectical Behavior Therapy, mindfulness and imagery/meditation, discovery of mind-body connection, solutions-focused, and strengths-based approaches. My ultimate goal to create and provide a safe space for connection, empowerment, comfort, trust and engagement. I work to provide a down to earth and non-judgmental environment with a natural and genuine approach to supporting individuals in all walks of life!

name: Bonnie Davey (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
My work as a child therapist is grounded in person-centered therapy and interpersonal neurobiology, with a particular focus on the use of child-centered play therapy, expressive arts, and incorporating nature in the therapeutic process. I've engaged in extensive trainings in nature-based child centered play therapy as well as in child therapy, and have a fully furnished, inviting, and child-centered therapy playroom and outdoor space for nature-based work. I specialize in working with very young children, specifically ages 3-7 years old.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
We are all growing together. We all experience beauty, as well as thorns. I hope to provide a place for you where thriving once again feels possible.

name: Liam Woods (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work through a somatic and transpersonal lens, focusing on the relationship between the mind and body. I specialize in attachment, somatic awareness, processing and bringing meaning to trauma, ecotherapy, and integrating experiences of psychedelic and expanded states of consciousness. My practice is tailored to the needs of each individual, offering a warm and understanding space to share and attend to the parts of our experience that need attention and care. The offerings in my practice are for individuals and couples of all identities. They are not specific to a particular demographic but are oriented toward people who want a holistic approach that brings together attuned connection and the innate wisdom of the body. Our bodies have an inherent healing intelligence, and by letting that guide our work, we can move toward and through difficulties at a pace that is safe for you.
My Values as a Therapist
A part of my practice is supporting clients in deepening their own perspective about where our culture is failing them. This perspective can be an antidote and a counterbalance to what many of us do, which is to take the failures and the toxic messaging of our culture inward and to make it mean that we are in some way damaged, different, unworthy, or simply not trying hard enough. This story we are being told is based in capitalism, patriarchy, and colonialism. In therapy we can create a space to challenge these stories of unworthiness and disconnection. When we share them and the way they impact us in a safe and intimate setting we begin to unravel them and to break their power over us. We as humans are meant to be in connection, to share, and to be seen in both our joys and our wounding. It is my privilege to provide the connection and presence to that allows people to be seen in their wounding and to begin to believe that they are not not damaged, different, or unworthy.

name: Giving Hope Psychiatry
approaches:
Our Approach to Helping
At Giving Hope Psychiatry, our approach is rooted in compassion, collaboration, and clinical expertise. We believe that effective psychiatric care begins with listening—truly understanding each person's experiences, symptoms, and goals. From there, we work closely with clients to develop personalized medication strategies that support long-term stability and growth. We prioritize education and transparency, so clients feel informed and empowered in their treatment decisions. Our care is not one-size-fits-all; we recognize the complexity of mental health and tailor our support to the individual. Through consistent follow-up and thoughtful adjustments, we help clients achieve meaningful progress. Whether someone is new to psychiatric care or has had past experiences, we strive to create a safe, respectful environment where hope and healing meet.
Why We are a Good Fit for You
Giving Hope Psychiatry is a good fit for individuals seeking a supportive, non-judgmental space to explore their mental health needs with the help of experienced professionals. If you’re looking for personalized medication support—whether it’s your first time or you’re seeking a better fit—we take the time to understand your story and goals. We’re especially suited for adults navigating anxiety, depression, ADHD, mood disorders, and sleep challenges. Our team values collaboration and clear communication, ensuring you’re an active part of your treatment plan. Through telehealth, we offer convenient access to care across Oregon, making it easier to get the help you need from the comfort of your home. If you want compassionate, thoughtful psychiatric care that goes beyond symptom management and focuses on long-term wellness, we may be the right fit for you.

name: Eileen Fisher (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In my work, I go beyond traditional talk therapy by integrating somatic therapy (body focused) and Internal Family Systems (IFS)—both evidence-based approaches that support deep, lasting change. IFS helps clients understand and heal inner conflicts by working with different "parts" of themselves, fostering self-compassion, clarity, and emotional balance. Somatic Experiencing focuses on how trauma and stress are stored in the body, allowing clients to regulate their nervous system, release stuck emotions, and build a greater sense of safety and ease. Through these approaches, I support my clients in connecting with their own inner healer, empowering them to cultivate resilience and growth beyond the therapy room. At the core of my practice is a compassionate, nonjudgmental space where clients feel safe to explore their experiences. My work is collaborative and client-centered, meeting each person exactly where they are.

name: Brad Pankey (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My eating disorder and disordered eating training includes time working at an in-patient clinic and courses on the treatment of eating disorders and trauma. We will also leverage my experience traveling and living all over the world, my MBA degree, and my diverse career and personal background while working with people. With that, we can safely explore topics generally seen as "taboo," such as money, sex, spirituality, and politics, all of which impact our worldview and how we relate to others. In the therapy room, I work with elements of Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), RO-DBT, Cognitive Processing Therapy (CPT) for trauma, and Relational-Cultural Therapy (RCT) through a Person-Centered and Feminist lens. My approach is culturally self-aware and humble, LGBTQ2IA+ aligned, and spiritually connected. I take the position of co-explorer as we navigate the treatment journey together. Wherever treatment takes us, we will always move together.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that we are neither helpless nor capable of being broken and therefore shy away from the idea we need to be fixed. Instead, I see suffering as arising when we become disconnected from our values, when we lose contact with the here and now, and when we get swept up in our thoughts and emotions. Each of us possess the capacity for change. I see my clients as already possessing the attributes that serve as the spark for change and growth. Together, we will discover what is needed to reconnect with one’s values and make the shift from doing to being. In the therapy room, I exist with my clients in shared awareness as we seek understanding rather taking on the role of healer, teacher, or expert. Our relationship is held as sacred and is used as a vehicle for awakening to ourselves. Please feel free to connect if my profile resonates with you. It would be a pleasure to join you in healing your relationship with food, exercise, your body, and yourself.

name: Shelby Dobrich (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
“My priority is to create a space where you feel safe and heard.” I believe in patient-centered care, where your needs and goals drive the treatment process. My approach is rooted in actively listening to your concerns, so we can collaboratively create a treatment plan that truly fits you. What motivates me most is seeing the positive changes in my clients’ lives. Together, we will work towards your success—helping you live life to the fullest.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a provider dedicated to exceptional care, I take a holistic approach to healing, addressing both the mind and body. This includes a combination of brief therapy and medication management, tailored to support your overall well-being.

name: Gary Alexander (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
While some of the issues leading us to start psychotherapy might be relieved relatively quickly, I have found as we begin clear away the initial symptoms we find that our issues may be much more complex and require time and the right attention to be effectively worked through. I believe the best work we have ahead of us isn’t merely to alleviate negative symptoms, but to help create real, deep, and lasting change; change that helps elevate us closer to our greatest potential. I strive to create the connection, bond, and sense of care needed for us to do this work together. Clients have described my approach as authentic, real, and interactive. I believe a relatable and engaged interaction with thoughtful feedback and reflection are the foundations to working through such important and big changes. Ultimately my goal is to build a strong, trusting, and reliable relationship together, helping you to further hear your true voice and in turn find direction and balance.

name: Tori Gorman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer compassionate, authentic, and holistic care so you can feel seen and start to move forward. I guide you to start making changes to help you feel empowered and have more control in your life. I am particularly passionate about utilizing ACT and narrative therapy to address symptoms of anxiety and ADHD.

name: Leonie Fischer-Laycock (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My work has roots in person-centered counseling, as well as Internal Family Systems (IFS) therapy. This perspective centers each client and their experiences, perspectives, and interconnected parts in sessions. My goal is to support clients in developing a deeper understanding of their experiences and identities, and to build a stronger internal relationship with parts of themselves that they may not yet be familiar or comfortable with. I utilize Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) to help folks with decision-making and shifting how they relate with anxiety. I operate from a multicultural feminist lens, providing LGBTQ+ affirming and anti-racist care. Clients of all identities are welcome, and our time together will always include space to explore your unique lived experiences and culture.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am well versed in trauma-informed care and prioritize a fat-positive, anti-racist, and queer-affirming approach with all clients. I am grounded in social justice and advocacy, and seek to understand clients individually and within the systems they are in.

name: Family Ties Counseling Center
approaches:
Our Approach to Helping
Here at FTCC, we believe in the strength of healing through relationships. We hold an expanded and limitless view of what family can be; we empower each of our clients to define for themselves what and who family is for them. When you’re a client at FTCC, you’re supported by a team of therapists who are dedicated to enriching the lives of you and your loved ones. As a collaborative group practice, we ensure that your whole family gets the care and resources they need. We provide child and family therapy, intimate relationship counseling, individual therapy, teen therapy, group counseling, art therapy, and ecotherapy.
Why We are a Good Fit for You
Our diverse staff addresses myriad concerns, including: relationship struggles, anxiety and depression, parenting, ADHD and neurodiversity, identity, sexual health, school concerns, life transitions, trauma, cultural and systemic oppression, and eco/climate anxiety, among others. Our clinicians offer a wide range of therapeutic modalities to fit you and your family's needs, always from a trauma-informed, anti-racist, gender-affirming perspective, including (but not limited to): attachment-based therapies, art therapy, cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT), ecotherapy, dialectical behavioral therapy (DBT), intergenerational, internal family systems (IFS), play therapy, and EMDR.

name: Annie McConnell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is psychodynamic, person-centered, and trauma-informed. I believe that human beings thrive off the connections we make and are shaped into our unique selves based off of the feedback we receive from people, communities, and society. Therapy can be an enriching and transformative experience that often requires vulnerability and hard work. My approach is warm, safe, and assertive. My therapeutic work is informed by Narrative Therapy, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), and Internal Family Systems (IFS) as I support clients in unpacking the many parts of themselves and their story.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist and received my Master's degree in Marriage, Couple, and Family Therapy from Lewis & Clark College. I received a Bachelor's degree in Family & Human Services from University of Oregon. Through experience gained in working within the social work field at various non-profits, I realized my passion for working with people on their relationships by providing direct therapeutic support. I am a white, able-bodied, cisgender woman who strives to improve upon ways to listen and learn from others that identify differently than me. I hold my own unique experience of what it is like to move through the world, just as you hold your own experience. I strive to support you and to advocate for greater equity within society. Your lived experience is important and considered within your therapeutic work and also vital towards positive growth.

name: Megan McDavid
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is not one-size-fits-all and is tailored to your specific goals and needs from therapy. I strive to help you feel comfortable and at-ease discussing topics that can sometimes be very sensitive. I strive to create a space free from judgement that can support you on your own individual journey.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am an AASECT Certified Sex Therapist. I received my Master’s Degree in Social Work at the Silberman School of Social Work at Hunter College in New York City and a Master’s Degree in Education with a specialization in Human Sexuality and Sex Therapy from Widener University in Pennsylvania.

name: Mara Sobesky (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Many people experience a tension in desiring both stability & change, surrender & control, freedom & its’ limitations. The distress behind these mutual and conflicting needs is part of what clients want to explore when they choose to work with me. Mapping themes such as identity development, life transitions & grief, can illuminate patterns that support and block your sense of presence. Together, we can build mutual trust and explore historic, cultural and social contexts to make sense of your experience, as we contemplate the question “how are you willing to live?” My approach is relational, collaborative & centered in Existential-Humanism. I also have specialized training in treating disordered eating and body image concerns.
More About the People I Treat
I love working with clients who are LGBTQ+, identify as neurodivergent, or are in mixed-neurotype relationships, communities in which I am a part.

name: Britt Axness (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My career has primarily centered on working with adults who have experienced traumatic events or who have faced other significant challenges like severe mental illness, addiction, and social marginalization. My work with survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault/abuse has heavily influenced my career path and is part of why I now offer individual therapy as a private practitioner. I am skilled at working with people who may be facing depression, anxiety, grief and loss, attachment issues, crisis, or trauma reactions. I am trained in Cognitive-Processing Therapy (CPT), a proven trauma treatment approach, as well as mindfulness, Internal Family Systems (IFS), Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy (CBT), and Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), among other modalities. I strive to be warm and open and look at therapy as a collaboration between myself and the client.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I view therapy as a gift we can give to ourselves and a valuable opportunity for self-growth and healing. Life is full of challenges, loss, and unexpected events, which affects each of us in different ways. Therapy can be a chance to better understand ourselves, our emotions, and our behaviors in relation to our past and current struggles. It is also a chance to process, to learn to cope, and find ways to navigate through these experiences in more effective and helpful ways. We all want to be at peace, find connection, and live fully. Therapy can be part of how we work towards the life we want and find healing. It is my goal to create a welcoming and affirming environment for people of all backgrounds. I have worked with diverse individuals throughout my career and continue to learn how best to support each person with both curiosity and culturally-responsive care.

name: Isaiah Bartlett (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I draw upon interventions from psychoanalytic psychotherapy, attachment focused therapy, emotionally focused therapy, radical acceptance, and health at every size. As an attachment focused therapist, my therapeutic work with clients centers around helping people better understand how attachment wounds and early experiences of caregiving impact relationships later in life, both with ourselves and with others. I have extensive training and personal experience in psychoanalytic psychotherapy and I believe strongly in the use of transference in the therapeutic relationship. Through my own personal and professional work, I have learned that some of the most transformative and healing work can be accomplished simply by exploring our connection as therapist and client.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received my Masters in Social Work from New York University, a program known for its clinical and psychoanalytic focus. My graduate coursework allowed me to explore the intersections between psychoanalysis, queer theory, critical race theory, and their implications on clinical work. I have over ten years of experience providing therapy in community mental health agencies as a child and family therapist and have been in private practice since 2015. Outside of my work, I am a dedicated adventurer and spend as much time as I can bicycle touring, skiing, and surfing. I enjoy bringing this same enthusiasm, energy, and love of exploration into my clinical work.

name: Tamara Randall
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In my therapeutic practice, I prioritize creating a safe and non-judgmental space within the client-therapist relationship, recognizing that trust is the foundation for effective therapy. I understand that the journey of therapy can be challenging, and I use my clinical judgment to balance self-exploration with gentle encouragement to step outside of one's comfort zone. I firmly believe in the potential for every individual to be empowered to self-actualize and overcome limitations. My approach is deeply rooted in a commitment to social justice and equity, ensuring that all clients feel valued and respected as they work towards personal growth and healing.

name: Anjulie Semenchuk
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe we are all moving through life learning new things and that from time to time we are faced with challenges that may require extra assistance. I am happy to partner with you to find the tools you need to deal with these challenges effectively. To learn more about me visit my website alpiniacounseling.com I have a uniquely logical and practical method toward treatment that allows me the capacity to connect with individuals who process information more cerebrally. I believe this balance of being able to meet clients in both an emotional and cognitive space has specifically attuned me to working with populations who are more analytically minded.

name: Serenity Wellness
approaches:
My Background
I have extensive work with a variety of settings from Head Start, Lockdown schools, jail and prison system. I have worked with school age populations from K-12, SPMI, Dual dx, couples and addictions, incarcerated individuals and those that have struggled to integrate back into society. I believe there can be change and leave the past in the past. I believe a holistic approach benefits both clients and clinicians. It does take time to get to know each other, then the work begins. I provide a safe space to be your authentic self. I use a combination of CBT, DBT, and solution focused. I am certified in prepare and enrich marriage and premarital counseling.
More About the People I Treat
I believe that change is within us and can have a positive impact in our lives when we engage in help, whether it is from ourselves, others or books. We just need to take a breath and start working on ourself and know we are worth it and can accomplish anything. Change takes time to implement. None of us are perfect and all we can do is our best. I hold space for everyone in a non judgemental way.

name: Derek Crain
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Derek has a Masters in Social Work from the University of Washington as well as a Bachelors in Sociology and Criminology from Cal State Northridge. After practicing and helping clients for nearly a decade, Derek founded Mindful Therapy Group with his wife, Jessica Crain. Derek thinks big picture, always pushing himself and his staff to think more critically, and throws around the phrase “self-compassion,” every chance he gets. Derek is an auditory learner and works in words. He hears the details and will use them to pivot and teach anyone who will listen how to be a better communicator.
Our Values as Mental Health Practitioners
In today’s fast-paced world, mental health and the need for accessible support is more vital than ever. Making resources readily available not only fosters individual well-being but also strengthens communities and workplaces. Mindful Therapy Group began with one goal: Uniting mental health care providers to make it easier to serve and grow the well being of the communities we live in. By giving as many people as possible access to compassionate, experienced, and forward thinking providers we are doing our part to create a more caring and compassionate world today.

name: Wendy Micheau (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a counselor, I offer undivided attention and nonjudgement. You’ll find that I’m warm, approachable, and easy to talk to. I will listen with patience and compassion, using curiosity and thoughtful questions to examine your concerns. If you're open to it, we may use creative activities like metaphor, drawing, writing, visualization, or projection to approach problems in new ways. I will also challenge you to take small, frequent action steps to help you meet the goals you've set for yourself. In our work together I will honor your wholeness and recognize your inherent strengths. This means that—while I will attend to the challenges you describe in session—I will also acknowledge your knowledge, skills, attitudes, wisdom, capabilities, community, & resiliency. You are so much more than your current hardship! Rest assured that you will come away from our time together feeling heard and seen.
Social Justice Issues I Care About
All people deserve to be treated with dignity and respect. I advocate for the inclusion and basic rights of all marginalized and oppressed populations--both inside the counseling room and in the larger community; however, I'm especially passionate about serving people with chronic illness and disability. As a single mom with a disability, I understand the experience of adjusting to a diagnosis, learning new ways to accommodate limitations, and dealing with disability stigma and discrimination. Getting through a typical day in our culture can feel exhausting and overwhelming. I would be honored to help you address any barriers keeping you from the vibrant, meaningful life you desire.

name: Morgan Huls (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a board-certified Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner with a certificate in Functional Medicine. This specialized education allows me to approach each client’s mental health from a holistic standpoint, looking at all aspects of life to determine the underlying cause and assist the client in achieving optimal mental wellness by addressing the root of an issue! I also have certificates in Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing Therapy and Health & Life Coaching which allow my clients to focus on forward movement towards goals and a life of desire while recognizing and reprocessing the negative belief systems that currently hold them back.
Why We are a Good Fit for You
At MindRx, our mission is to revolutionize mental wellness by embracing an integrative approach that recognizes the profound connection between the mind and body. We are committed to providing exceptional care and empowering individuals to achieve true well-being. Through our innovative programs, delivered by highly trained practitioners, we aim to transform lives and inspire a new era of mental health care. By combining cutting-edge techniques, functional medicine, and personalized approaches, we strive to optimize the mental well-being of our patients, enabling them to lead fulfilling and balanced lives. At MindRx, we believe that everyone deserves the opportunity to unlock their full potential and experience the transformative power of holistic mental wellness.
