name: Alex Harrison (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is to help you explore the unconscious forces contributing to pattern repetition, emotional distress, and disconnection from self and others. I strongly believe we all possess an inner healing intelligence that speaks to us in a variety of ways, and my intention is to facilitate this process by listening deeply and providing space for you to express those parts of you that have been suppressed or discouraged out of fear or out of necessity. I am also trained in somatic therapy and often incorporate techniques like meditation, art, and parts work to help access and integrate difficult life experiences, amplify your intuitive capacity, and remove the blocks keeping you from sharing the gift of your authentic self with the world.

name: Molly Nearman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I approach counseling from an inclusive, strength-based, trauma-informed, and client-centered perspective. I believe that all individuals naturally strive toward growth and wholeness, guided by a nurturing inner wisdom or real self. My counseling practice is rooted in attachment-based psychodynamic theory, meaning that our early life experiences impact the way we view ourselves, relate to others, and understand the world around us. Sometimes these experiences can impair our inner wisdom and prevent us from living as our authentic selves. I also view individuals systemically, meaning that each of us is shaped and impacted by the interpersonal patterns and dynamics of our families and relationships. I integrate these theoretical lenses with Eye Movement Desensitization & Reprocessing (EMDR) therapy to help clients process trauma and move beyond their negative experiences to realize their full potential.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My connection and commitment to this work is deeply personal. Having grown up in a Midwestern culture that discouraged seeking help or even talking about painful feelings or challenges, moving to the more open-minded Pacific Northwest was a revelation in many ways. After experiencing my own transformative growth in counseling, I knew I wanted to help others to process and make sense of the past, gain insight, achieve personal growth, and reach their potential. I hold a Master’s degree in Marriage, Couple and Family Counseling from Portland State University. Before joining Eastside Counseling, I worked in community mental health and outpatient clinic settings with teens and adults. In my free time, I enjoy spending time hiking, running, and swimming in the breathtaking landscapes the Pacific Northwest has to offer. I also recharge indoors by reading, spending time with family and close friends, and engaging in creative projects like painting, baking, and sewing.

name: Marcy Irene Jenks (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer compassionate and heart-centered counseling to individuals, couples and groups, using mindful, experiential, relational and somatic based techniques. In sessions I invite you to discover your authentic self by curiously exploring all aspects of your personhood, helping you to pay attention to yourself from a place of non-judgmental observation, and from this place to study your experience of the world. Providing a supportive and safe container, I will hold space for your deepest wisdoms to unfold; an insightful process that allows for increased awareness, presence and inspiration that is life-changing! I also draw from existential, humanistic and psychodynamic theories based on your unique needs regarding mind, body, spirit and soul. I believe that the counselor-client relationship is at the foundation of a successful self-exploration endeavor and with gentle curiosity, grace and wonder, we will partner on a journey to a place of self-integration and fullness of being.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I feel fortunate to have spent ten years as a stay at home mom raising my three children, then earning a BS in Nursing from the UW in Seattle and becoming Certified as an Ayurvedic Consultant through Kerala Ayurveda Academy. Graduating from Walden University with a MS in Clinical Mental Health Counseling, I finished a year-long internship with the M.E.T.A Counseling Clinic in Portland, completed the Hakomi Mindful Somatic Professional Skills Module, the Re-Creation of the Self Module, and the Primary Attachment Psychotherapy Module. I have explored various aspects of mental health and its relation to physical health through my work as a hospital nurse, and have studied psychology through an eastern perspective, providing assessment, diagnosis and treatment consultations to clients using Ayurvedic medicine. These experiences have seasoned my understanding and reverence of human diversity with a deep respect for the unique healing path that each person discovers for themselves.

name: Palma Corral Sotelo (SHE /HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In counseling I serve individual adults and my process is relational and collaborative. Together we will explore the parts of your life you want to develop, change or grow. We can explore your emotions, thoughts and beliefs, and learn to decipher the messages they bring you from within yourself and from the world around you. We will explore your strengths and preferences as they will be our foundation for what kinds of modalities we will utilize to explore our work together. We can work with your preferred channels of expression, such as talking, visualization, making visual art, writing, movement and sound. We will work relationally and explore also through the lenses of attachment and human development and will incorporate somatic (felt in the body) practices if you feel you’re ready to do deep work. We will go at your pace because I trust your inner wisdom to move towards healing and growth.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that the challenges life presents us with are the fertile soil we can use to grow the depth and character of our being. Our work together begins and is sustained by a foundation of respect and trust. I work at cultivating a therapeutic presence that is heart centered and expressed through openness, curiosity, empathy, authenticity, creativity, and cultural humility. I believe all cultures and people possess wisdom and that includes you. I am influenced by theories on attachment, developmental, existential, feminist, humanistic, multicultural, neurobiological and experiential approaches such as, relational, somatic, and expressive therapies. I am also influenced by my worldview, which includes my belief in evolution, my understanding of human history and linguistics, my belief in the practice of the scientific methods in its broadest expression or praxis, as well as my art and spiritual practices. I am motivated by my own search for human wisdom.

name: Heart Thyself, LLC
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Narcissistic Abuse Recovery is deeply intense personal work that will transform your life. It involves breaking the cycle of generations of abuse and dysfunction. Recovery work is a pro-active process of establishing a treatment plan focused on your identity and relationship goals. Most clients see significant changes after 4-6 months of dedicated weekly therapy. Change is painful, but not nearly as painful as spending an unfulfilled life in a narcissistic family system or relationship. Heart Thyself is dedicated to helping you through this pain into the life you would choose to live. You can do it!

name: Erik Lee (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We begin with the premise that you are the expert of your own experiences. I will listen, gain an understanding of your unique needs, and work with you to create a personalized path to better mental health. My comprehensive evaluation process includes validated assessments, in-depth interviews, medical exams, and thorough record reviews. I may also recommend additional diagnostics like sleep studies or genetic tests when warranted. I offer a range of treatment options, from medication management to naturopathic approaches, psychotherapy, and medication-free alternatives. You'll be fully informed about all options, empowering you to make confident decisions about your care. I use customized tracking methods to monitor your progress and adjust treatment as needed. With commitment and my guidance, you can join the majority of patients who experience significant, lasting improvements in their daily lives. Take the first step towards lasting mental wellness and schedule a consultation.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
As a mental health professional with personal experience in trauma recovery, I offer a unique perspective to guide you on your healing journey. My approach is informed by my identity as a gay man, BIPOC individual, and immigrant, resulting in empathetic and culturally sensitive care. I practice what I preach, maintaining a self-care regimen rooted in mindfulness, gratitude, and acceptance, which not only benefits my own well-being but also shapes the guidance I offer to patients. As a seasoned diagnostician and psychopharmacotherapist, I stay current with psychiatric advancements through ongoing training, ensuring you receive the most up-to-date and effective care. I take great pride in the fact that my clients benefit from: a safe, judgment-free space to explore mental health concerns; culturally informed care that respects all backgrounds; evidence-based treatments combined with holistic wellness practices; and a provider who understands firsthand the challenges you may face.

name: Portland Therapy Project
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Our counselors are a passionate group of graduate therapy students invested in providing personable, caring and empathetic therapy. Traumas, our history, the socio economic and cultural realities we face can make it difficult to connect to ourselves and others. We provide a space where clients can connect with their body and emotions with clinicians dedicated to care and connection. Please check out our website for more info about our counselors: www.portlandtherapyproject.com.
More About the People We Treat
To get started, fill out our intake online: https://www.portlandtherapyproject.com/#new-client-intake We will contact you as soon as we have an opening that matches your availability!

name: Kalpana Krishnamurthy (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I start by listening to build a strong relationship and making sure that the goals and direction in therapy are where you want to go. I focus on learning about you - strengths, growth areas, hopes, family, and fears - to understand the patterns in your life right now. As we get deeper, I use a mix of tools in session - from mindfulness to somatic work to creating maps to exploring thoughts and feelings. For South Asian, API, and other BIPOC clients, exploring your thoughts, beliefs, emotions, and personal story in therapy can be a risk. May be it goes against your cultural or family norms, or may be you’ve had bad experiences with white therapists. As a South Asian therapist, I bring my lived experience to our work together. While I may not share your specific racial or ethnic identities, I will respect and affirm your experiences while holding space for you to explore.
My Values as a Therapist
When I first started therapy, I could label 3 of my feelings: angry, sad, and hungry. And I wasn't even sure that hungry was a feeling? Little did I realize--most people can't name more than three feelings. Therapy helped me slow down and find my own experience of my feelings. That was a gamechanger. After 25 years as a community organizer working primarily with BIPOC and LGBTQ+ communities, I decided to change directions and become a therapist. You’ll always get some organizing flavor in my therapy because I believe that much of the suffering we face is because the systems around us (like schools, housing, or workplaces) weren’t built for us. Therapy can’t solve that–but our work can be a place of individual healing, connection, and radical understanding of your experience(s). Some identities that matter to me—and that might matter to you as you look for a therapist—include being South Asian, a cisgender woman, straight, and a parent.

name: Jessica Tolleson, M.A, LPC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I take an evidence based approach and create effective therapy by tailoring treatment to my client's needs. In using my extensive experience and training in evidence based practice, such as Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Mindfulness, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), and Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT), I can create personalized treatment for each client.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I help adults who have difficulty connecting with others because of daily struggles, such as, extreme sadness, worry, stress or trauma. I have experience working with veterans suffering from PTSD and other symptoms of trauma. Lastly, I specialize in working with individuals who are suffering from depression, anxiety, low self-esteem and assertiveness issues as a result of witnessing and/or surviving domestic violence.

name: ELIZABETH PIERCE-GREEN
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe in treating the mind, body and spirit as a whole person. I am passionate about providing quality, compassionate, and individualized care to patients and families with psychiatric medications and brief psychotherapeutic interventions as well as some alternative therapies in combination with medication management.
Why I Became a Therapist
After years of working with people in health care, I realized that many individuals continue to struggle as they do not have that one special person they can pour out their inner most thoughts, doubts, fears, and anxieties to. I believe that to heal we must have a trusting relationship with someone that understands us. Someone that can give us tools to change and grow but not judge our self-perceived inadequacies. It takes time to build a trusting relationship. I want to be that person for you. I am a mother to four amazing adults and a grandmother to four beautiful independent and spicy littles that have made their forever home within an hour drive to my residence. My husband owns and operates a local family business. My home is here. I plan on staying local; therefore, we will have time to build a relationship and work together to device a collaborative plan that meets your needs.

name: Tamzin Johanson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My own experiences as a queer and neurodivergent person growing up in a turbulent household, sparked my passion for offering others a safer space — a container — to explore their own struggles, experiences, and sense of self. Because of my own experiences, I have a particular passion for supporting sex workers. I bring a unique perspective to my practice, understanding the complexities and nuances of navigating such a dynamic and multifaceted industry. Where we come from matters. The events, relationships, & traumas in our past shape who we are and our patterns; I want to recognize that these patterns likely formed in the past to protect and keep us safe. I am here to guide you in recognizing those patterns & understanding how they may influence your life. I approach our sessions with unwavering dedication, addressing even the darkest & most challenging aspects you may be grappling with. While providing validation & compassion, I won’t hesitate to push you when it’s needed for growth.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a therapist with a focus on relationships and systemic perspectives, I’ll be attentive to how your experiences within relationships and broader societal structures shape your well-being. I am anti-racist, queer and trans affirming, ethical non-monogamy affirming, anti-ableist, neurodivergent affirming, ace affirming and sex worker affirming. I care deeply about your feelings and needs & will make space for feedback. I do my best, but I may not always get it right. I want to create a space where we can openly and honestly discuss what is helpful to you. I’m engaged to a fantastic nerdy and neurodivergent person, and we have two kitty fur babies. I love playing video games, reading fantasy books (especially the spicy kind), and watching movies/shows. I also loveeee doing arts and crafts and have a passion for dance.

name: Nic Sutherland (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a marriage and family therapist, I will always conceptualize your life through a systemic lens, attuning to the systems of power that impact your life, the relationships that surround you, and how your past experiences make their way into the present moment, shaping the future. As an existential humanistic therapist, I will be looking for the tensions and contradictions that you hold, your willingness to face what is true, and your belief systems. I draw on Internal Family Systems in order to access the parts of yourself with different wants and needs. We will connect to the parts of you that are scared, angry, protective, entirely disconnected, joyful, etc. They are all true, an and together they build the tapestry of your authenticity. My goal is to help you find connection to yourself and others, own your capacity for choice in the face of limiting circumstances, and find inspiration and meaning.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In this life, I believe we are all born to connect and make meaning. As your therapist, my goal is to build a safe relationship with you, in which we can explore the complexity of your life; the mundane, the magic, the painful. This looks like curiosity, honesty, laughter, use of metaphor, play and art therapy techniques, naming and feeling emotion, and most importantly, relying on the quality of our own relationship to create new experiences.

name: Christopher Creaturo (HE/HIS)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I specialize in techniques of EMDR for trauma, Internal Family Systems for meeting and healing our exiled parts, Mentalization Based Therapy for Borderline Personality Disorder, Jungian techniques such as dreamwork, and Attachment-informed strategies to improve relationships. These techniques and perspectives compliment a therapy relationship built on deep trust, which can be inherently healing to the patterns of our life and our deep sense of being valid and whole in the world.
My Values as a Therapist
When someone sees the best in us it's easier to see that for ourselves. Therapy at it's best can hold up a healing mirror to ourselves and our lives. In therapy, I work hard to honor the best possibilities of you and support you on a road of healing. Reach out by email with a short description of what you're seeking, and I'll get back to you promptly!

name: Kirsten Kelly Chambers (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I will help you identify the source of your symptoms and help you find acceptance and self-compassion while discovering healthier ways to heal, connect and eventually thrive. I use authenticity, humor, directness, warmth and a strengths-based approach to help you build resiliency and self-confidence. Treatment is an individualized process, which means I meet you where you are and create a plan to support your individual needs and goals. Through my work as a therapist, I strive to integrate social justice and equity, and thus my goal is to make counseling more accessible and less stigmatized. I offer a select number of sliding scale spots for those experiencing financial hardship.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Licensed Professional Counselor registered with the State of Oregon license number C7531. I earned my masters in professional mental health counseling with a specialization in addictions from Lewis & Clark College and my bachelors in communications and psychology from Syracuse University in New York. Prior to joining the counseling field, I spent 15 years as a public relations specialist working in both the corporate and nonprofit sectors. As a therapist, I have 10 years of experience providing individual, group and family therapy to youth, adults and families in residential, outpatient, school-based and community mental health settings. I am a mom to identical twin post-college age daughters, a native Portlander, an active ally to BIPOC and LGBTQ communities and graduate of Portland Public Schools.

name: Dr. Reece Henrichs-Beck (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Being asked and invited to be with someone in the process of healing and change is a true privilege and deeply meaningful to me. As a therapist, I seek to provide a secure and open space in which clients experience being seen, heard, and known. Clients describe me as warm, caring, honest, and authentic. My therapy style is relational, process-focused, and experiential. This means that I will guide us in using the relationship between you and I as a space to support your growth and change. It also means that both the content of therapy (what we are talking about) and the process of therapy (what is happening as we are talking and interacting) are both important for us to engage with. Lastly, it means that I will offer activities ("exploring by doing") that will help you learn more about yourself, develop new choices, and have new experiences in support of your therapy goals.

name: Daniel Roa (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to healing emphasizes collaboration, compassion, and a deep understanding of each person's unique journey. I believe in treating the whole individual - mind, body, spirit - rather than just addressing a single symptom or diagnosis. By integrating psychiatric expertise with primary care and wellness principles, I help patients uncover their inherent strengths and develop personalized strategies for achieving optimal well-being. Medication is judiciously used when necessary, while also considering the role of nutrition, supplements, and lifestyle factors in promoting resilience and long-term health. Above all, I strive to create a safe, non-judgmental space where individuals feel empowered to actively participate in their healing process and reach their full potential.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As an LGBTQ+ BIPOC provider, I'm passionately dedicated to serving my community. I believe that everyone deserves compassionate, affirming care that honors their identities and experiences. My personal journey has instilled in me a deep understanding of the unique challenges and resilience within our communities. I'm committed to creating a safe space where all individuals feel seen, heard, and empowered to thrive. Beyond my practice, I'm an advocate for social justice and health equity. In my free time, I enjoy connecting with nature, exploring diverse cultures, and celebrating the beauty of our community's collective strength.

name: Emily Ross-Johnson (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I work with clients to develop awareness and acceptance in times of psychological, relational, and existential difficulty. I am focused on fostering an inclusive, and body/sex-positive environment for growth. I have experience with neurodiverse clients across the ADHD and Autism spectrum as well as clients in open/polyamorous relationships, and my work is kink aware and affirming, as well as LGBTQ+ and gender affirming. I offer a non-judgmental space for growth. With commitment to authenticity and depth-oriented counseling practices, I work to help provide you with a new perspective on life and help you create room for lasting change.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Being human is unrelenting, but one of the most amazing things about us, is that we have the capacity for change. Neuroscience has shown us that our brains are able to form new neural pathways, allowing us to create healthier patterns for ourselves, and enabling us to heal old wounds. My own journey of self-discovery has cemented a love of music, travel, film, the outdoors, and spending time with those closest to me. I believe we are all capable, through introspection and curiosity, of fostering our own values and improving our relationship with others as well as ourselves.

name: Dr. Udo Chukwuocha
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I take an integrative, patient-centered approach to mental health, blending evidence-based psychiatric care with holistic and lifestyle-based strategies. As a dual board-certified psychiatric mental health and family nurse practitioner, I provide comprehensive care that considers the whole person. With over a decade of experience in diverse healthcare settings, my goal is to empower you with a treatment plan that aligns with your values. My approach includes medication management when appropriate, therapeutic techniques, lifestyle coaching, nutritional guidance, and culturally responsive care tailored to each individual's unique needs. I believe in collaborative healing—working together to help you gain clarity, confidence, and balance. Whether you’ve struggled with finding the right provider or feel like past treatment didn’t fully address your needs, I strive to create a supportive space where you feel seen, heard, and empowered on your journey to mental wellness.
More About the People I Treat
I am deeply committed to providing compassionate, inclusive, and culturally responsive mental health care for individuals who have felt unseen or unheard in traditional treatment spaces. My approach is rooted in understanding the complexities of identity, culture, and lived experiences, ensuring that every client feels valued and supported. As a provider with a background in both psychiatric and family medicine, I focus on integrative care that goes beyond symptom management to address the root causes of distress. I am particularly passionate about working with individuals navigating anxiety, ADHD, depression, stress, and I strive to create a safe space where healing, self-discovery, and growth can thrive. My practice embraces a holistic perspective, incorporating medication, lifestyle modifications, mindfulness, and evidence-based treatments to foster resilience and overall well-being. No matter where you are in your journey, I am here to support and empower you.

name: David Kremmel (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
Our Approach to Helping
My approach is to first address your immediate concerns with empathy and evidence-based interventions from Emotional Focused Therapy, Narrative Therapy, Humanistic, CBT, and Gottman to support your needs. I offer warmth and education throughout the therapeutic process to support my clients. In our time, I like to explore what shapes us the most, our important relationships, and the stories we tell and believe about ourselves. Coming to therapy can be scary and vulnerable. Whether this is your first time or you are seasoned to therapy, I welcome you as you are. We will go at your pace. I will tailor the entire process to your goals and strengths. I have hope and believe the human potential we hold is outstanding and I am eager for the opportunity to join you in this journey.

name: Trish Nicholson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe in a compassionate and collaborative approach to therapy. Our work together will be grounded in empathy and mutual respect. In therapy, you can share your thoughts and feelings without fear of judgment. My goal is to partner with you to reclaim your life through talk and connection as has been done by our ancestors for generations. We will work together to help you get clarity and gain insights on patterns that no longer serve you. Therapy with me is a non-judgmental space where your whole self is welcome. We may explore past experiences, relationships, and the intersectionalities of your identities and communities, which can help us identify roadblocks to change and places to draw strength. Ultimately, we will work together to ground you in your power as you dig deep to identify strategies to empower you, break negative cycles, and foster personal growth.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a mixed-race therapist, with White (Italian & Irish) and Indigenous (Mi'kmaq) ancestors, I know how hard it can be to find time for self growth and care while living in oppressive systems. It can be even harder when we don't fit in a binary or see ourselves reflected in the spaces we live, work, or learn. I am guided by a liberation psychology framework, and I encourage the disruption of systems and beliefs that have perpetuated cycles of harm to our life ways, relationships, and self-concepts. Outside of therapy, you may find me gardening. I am happiest with my hands in the soil. I also enjoy hanging out with my cats and dog (who often make cameos!) and enjoying a cup of coffee on a brisk morning walk.

name: Emalie Coplan (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
In our therapeutic relationship, we will collaboratively create a safe space for exploration, delving into your past and navigating the path between where you currently stand and where you aspire to be. I tailor interventions for each client, drawing on IFS and experiential techniques like EMDR, Brainspotting, and psychedelics (psilocybin and ketamine). My approach is relational, client-led, collaborative, and social justice-oriented. As a white therapist, I am deeply committed to actively learning from those who hold marginalized identities, applying an intersectional lens, and practicing from a place of cultural humility and anti-racism. Additionally, this means I am sex, kink, and fat-positive, and aim to provide neurodivergent and polyamory/non-monogamy affirming care. Our lives are political, and our mental health and well-being is always tied to the systems and structures we live within. As a queer person, I am especially committed to working with queer and trans communities.
Why I Became a Therapist
As someone who overcame a difficult childhood, I’m not too different from you. I come from my own traumas throughout childhood and into adult life. I experienced and survived a dysfunctional childhood, I’m a 10/10 on the Adverse Childhood Experiences (ACES). Despite seeming to “have it together”, I felt detached from my emotions and my sense of self. I had numerous relationships but lacked true intimacy, not realizing this was a common experience for trauma survivors—feeling a painful internal split and deep self-estrangement. When I began my own healing, it became a transformative process for me. It helped me integrate my childhood trauma and reclaim lost parts of myself. With the right support, I stopped avoiding my feelings and started feeling a sense of belonging, finding the courage and safety to reconnect with myself. Therapy profoundly transformed my life and mended my heart. Inspired by my own journey, I am dedicated to offering others the same support and encouragement.

name: Elizabeth Nelson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a therapist, I seek to foster healing in action, play, imagination, wholeness, meaning making, connection, courage, authenticity, a sense of belonging, transformation, agency and resilience in the lives of the individuals I work with. My work is grounded in attachment, trauma informed, and experiential theoretical orientations. Art therapy is a powerful, experiential, expressive arts therapy that helps address that which is difficult to put words to. It focuses not necessarily on the art product, but rather using art in the process of therapy as a lens to access parts of the brain, body and psyche that talk therapy may not fully reach. Humanity has turned to various types of art for millennia to heal, long before therapists in the modern sense existed, and this type of therapy engages in this phenomenon.
My Values as a Therapist
I strive to make space in therapy to examine the impact of my and my clients' social locations and the impact of the systems we are all apart of, knowing this brings a broader and more complete picture in regards to how we all show up in the world.

name: Michelle Robertson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Life can be challenging. At times it can be filled with obstacles that seem insurmountable. Anxiety, depression, stress, relationships, work, low self-esteem and trauma can make one’s life even more difficult to navigate. Learning skills to help you through these challenges can be key to helping you overcome them. I would like to come alongside you on your journey to help you find peace and freedom. I will bring compassion, genuineness, empathy, directness, and humor into our sessions. I will also bring my understanding of how trauma can affect every aspect of your life, including your body. One of my main goals is to create an environment of safety and a place to explore to gain an insight.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In my spare time, I love to cook and laugh with my family and friends. My other hobbies include gardening, reading, cooking, and sewing. The longer I work on my self-care practices, the more I see them intersect with my hobbies and I love this.

name: Rachel McEwen, LPC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I am a relational therapist at heart, which means I strive to build a meaningful therapeutic relationship with you so that healing and growth can occur. Therapy to me is a collaborative experience, and we will work together to find what motivates and energizes you. During therapy, you will be able to process through your struggles and learn practical skills and new ways of being that will help you feel more balanced in life. I will also help you challenge any negative self-talk you might have going on in your mind, that voice inside your head telling you that you're not good enough. I will help you find the voice inside you that tells you that you are enough, just the way you are, and together, we will make that voice louder. As your therapist, I will meet you where you are. I will work side-by-side with you through this challenging time.
My Approach to Helping
I have over a decade of experience working with people who struggle with issues such as anxiety, depression, relationship issues, trauma, and self-esteem, just to name a few. My approach is trauma-informed and integrative. I do my best to be aware and sensitive to your life experience and will adapt my therapeutic approach to best fit your needs. I always strive to provide a safe space where you can be yourself without judgement. As your therapist, I promise to be open-minded and genuine with you. I am committed to being present with you every step of the way.

name: James Thatcher (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In regard to my experience, I have administered, performed, and consulted on over two thousand Autism, neurodevelopmental, cognitive, ADHD, learning disorder, and psychological evaluations on children from 18-months through adulthood using state-of-the-art assessment tools and evidence-based measurements to diagnose even the most complex cases. Furthermore, due to my experience, I am able to test individuals within one day. I write a full report within one week of your in-person visit. This way, you and your family get the answers you need immediately and do not have to wait months for the report, or keep returning for further testing sessions.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
Imagine confidently managing daily tasks and improving your social interactions. With over 10 years of experience specializing in neurodivergence, I offer evaluations to help you see your neurodivergence as a gift and a strength in all areas of your life. Ready to embrace your abilities and thrive? Please click on the link to our website above and schedule a free 15-minute consultation today.

name: Cameron Kemper (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
When you come to therapy, you seek answers to difficult life challenges. Perhaps you lack a sense of purpose, are in a relationship or job that is no longer satisfying or are experiencing overwhelming depression or anxiety. I ground my work in respect and compassion for you because this contributes to the foundation of trust, self-acceptance and courage necessary for you to successfully overcome your difficulties and create the life you want to live. Through the cultivation of mindful awareness and the implementation of proven stress management and emotion regulation skills, I will teach you how to calm anxiety, cope with distressing feelings, access your intuitive wisdom, and review your behaviors and beliefs so that you can commit to those which align with your values. This will help you realize your personal, professional and relationship goals and ultimately experience what all of us want in this life - a greater sense of peace, vitality and happiness.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have provided mental health and substance abuse counseling in private practice, hospital behavioral health and community mental health settings for the past 30 years. I graduated from Stanford University and received my Masters degree in Clinical Psychology from CIIS, a program which integrates Eastern and Western views on psychology. I have received extensive supervision and have expertise utilizing several evidence-based therapeutic modalities as well as mindfulness-based stress reduction which helps me work effectively with anxiety and mood disorders as well as existential and relationship issues. My mindfulness orientation is informed by over 30 years of meditative practice including residency in a Buddhist monastery. I also have extensive experience working with geriatric clients while employed with Providence Behavioral Health.

name: Dr. Shelby Kittinger (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a relational psychodynamic therapist, Dr. Shelby’s approach to therapy focuses on transformational change through the context of relationship. She engages her patients with a balance of acknowledging pain and suffering while also holding compassion and gently moving alongside each patient towards their own unique healing. Dr. Shelby takes this depth-oriented approach to provide her patients with long lasting change. In the context of treatment, Dr. Shelby prioritizes the authenticity of the moment, attending to and unlocking conscious and unconscious patterns of how we relate to ourselves, to others, and to the world around us. She is particularly interested in helping people work through past wounds and shining a light on all the “parts” of a patient to help cultivate a whole cohesive sense of self. She addresses meeting wants and needs, personal values, and increasing agency, compassion, and radical acceptance.
More About the People I Treat
For Relationship Therapy: Dr. Shelby is trained in and utilizes Emotion Focused Therapy which focuses on entering into the depths of dysfunctional patterns, chronic communication issues, and difficulty regulating emotion within the relationship that undermine the security of the relationship as a whole. In treatment, Dr. Shelby focuses on treating the relationship as the patient versus getting stuck in one versus another. She centers valuing understanding versus agreeing, identifying patterns, and increasing compassion to help strengthen the relationships core and build toward transformational long-lasting change. Dr. Shelby is trained and experienced in working with LGBTQIA+ folks and non-heteronormative identities or relationships. Wherever you are at, her hope is to co-create a space open to deep and thoughtful reflection established gradually with trust and a sense of being safe enough to take steps toward healing, transformation, and relational fulfillment.

name: Elise Helms (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapeutic approach is genuine and warm, highly relational, integrative, and grounded in attachment, humanistic, holistic, and existential theories. I honor the connection of mind, body, heart, and spirit, and I incorporate mindfulness and somatic-based interventions to bring greater awareness to one’s whole being. I aim to provide a safe and supportive environment where you will know and feel that you are accepted just as you are. I view my role as that of a mindful companion supporting you on your journey. I will follow your lead, while remaining curious, engaged, and honest in what I notice. I invite you to stay open to new directions, yet grounded in the present moment, as you access your own wisdom and gain new insight and awareness. I will meet you with an open heart and mind, with understanding, patience, encouragement, and compassion as we uncover and explore the underpinnings of your life experiences to facilitate the growth, healing, and change that you desire.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in your wholeness and inherent worth and wisdom. I also believe that you are meant to be truly heard, seen, and witnessed by another. As a client, I have experienced the healing and growth that is possible when one is met with the genuine presence, support, and compassion of an attuned therapist. It is my honor to collaborate with and hold space for others as they reclaim their strength, repair their relationships, and remember their true self. As a therapist, I am committed to my own personal, political, and spiritual evolution, grounded in the belief that compassionate counseling requires a commitment to personal growth, cultural humility, and always striving to genuinely understand the myriad lived experiences of diverse populations. I view humans through a multicultural and relational lens with the understanding that our well-being is impacted by our identities, the social and cultural systems we inhabit, and the state and health of our relationships.

name: Esther Odaibo (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I have been in the clinical field for fifteen years and I am very culturally competent being exposed to different cultures and lifestyles.
My Approach to Helping
I offer a listening ear without judgement or bias understanding that we are all on different paths and have been on different paths. We may carry different trauma from past experiences and may have current stressors affecting our mental health and hindering us from living our lives to our fullest potential. I am trained and experienced in various therapy modalities to overcome challenges and develop new life skills to help my clients live fulfilling lives. I also use medication management were needed to alleviate mental health struggles. I look forward to working together with you to establish and accomplish goals which we create together.

name: La Saechao (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a woman of color, I have experienced the stressors related to living and navigating the complexity of family expectations, culture, and dominant society. I understand the impact of marginalization and systematic oppression. Most importantly, I understand the benefits of finding a non-judgemental space where you can truly honor who you really are while being seen and heard. A space to sort through demands and stressors of everyday life, relationships, and cultural identity/BIPOC-specific challenges. As a therapist I can offer this space to you. Issues I can help with are life transitions, stress, anxiety, self-esteem, cultural identity, personal growth, and depression. I provide individual therapy to adults through virtual counseling sessions. Unfortunately, I do not have in-person options at this time. Please visit my website to schedule a free consultation at www.growingwithinpnw.com.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe we are all shaped by diverse experiences that influence who we are, how we operate in the world, and the lens through which we view life. Each experience holds profound meaning and value, guiding us as we navigate the world around us. When we take the time to hold space for our experiences, we create opportunities to explore, understand, and nurture them. This process allows for healing, self-discovery, and transformation. As your therapist, my goal is to honor all aspects of your lived experiences. I practice from a social justice and anti-oppressive framework, ensuring that our work together is inclusive, affirming, and compassionate. I strive to create a space where all parts of you are seen, heard, and valued.

name: Christopher Boothby (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
As a counselor, I approach counseling with a holistic assumption of the interconnectedness of body, mind, and spirit. I work with subtle awareness through mindful somatic exploration of present-moment experience, accessing places of activation without judgment. Through attention to present moment experience comes the discovery of beliefs, thoughts, feelings, memories, and images that negatively impact our quality of life. We can then work with corrective emotional experiences to facilitate the updating of our relational templates. Drawing from my foundational Hakomi training and recent Internal Family Systems (IFS) training, I offer a mindful, somatic, experiential, and relational approach. By integrating the IFS's focus on internal parts with Hakomi’s emphasis on relational presence and mindfulness, I support clients in identifying patterns that may be limiting them. Together we can transform outdated core beliefs, resolve internal conflicts, and enhance self-leadership qualities.
Why I Became a Therapist
My journey to becoming a therapist was by way of an eclectic professional path and years of therapy as a client. During my youth I worked in diverse hands-on trades, from furniture making to home construction and even auto mechanics. Subsequently I became an Architect, specializing in civic and cultural projects from Libraries, to Arts Centers and Municipal projects with my own firm. Seeking a more kinesthetic balance in my work life I became a massage therapist on the side. When emotional content surfaced in my clients during massage, I became interested in the interface of the physical and emotional bodies and the potential for healing therein. Thus, began my study of psychotherapy, first in the Hakomi Method of body centered psychotherapy, then a Masters of Science in Marriage and Family Therapy and subsequently an Internal Family Systems Level I training.

name: Jamee Puccio
approaches:
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I deeply believe that we all thrive best when we are taking care of one another. However, most of us these days live in cultures that de-prioritize that community care and expect us to suck it up and take care of ourselves, by ourselves, even in the murkiest times. I don't know about you, but I don't feel very successful when I try to move through life without support. I became a therapist because I love being a part of people's support networks, and I think that therapy is a unique and beautiful way to do that. I love helping folks navigate shifting identities, uncover and care for old wounds, and move into the person they know they really are. I am passionate about exploring the role of systemic oppression and collective trauma in my clients’ stories. As a member of the LGBTQIA+ community I am especially drawn to helping fellow queer folks. I also love helping support parents!

name: Daniel Blumenthal (HE/HIM)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
In my experience, most mental health challenges are complex and can benefit from therapy, use of recommended skills between session, and personal focus on physical health. Each therapeutic relationship begins with an assessment and treatment planning, in which I will gather information. We will then work together to establish goals, and determine which type of therapy will be most helpful based on your symptoms and needs. I use the following strategies with positive outcomes: Psychodynamic therapy is a long-term strategy that acknowledges the complex reasons and causes for emotional suffering, and examines subconscious motivations for our behavior and feelings. Research shows long-term alleviation of symptoms with this therapy. EMDR: A structured evidence-based technique that uses eye-movements and talk therapy to alleviate PTSD symptoms. CBT/DBT: Structured step-by-step therapies that reduce anxious negative thoughts and emotional dysregulation.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I may be a good fit for you if: you are dealing with trauma symptoms, anxiety, depression, or anger challenges; are in a relationship with someone suffering from mental health or personality disorders; are seeking long-term therapy to address complex dynamic challenges and reach a point of self-actualization; want to try EMDR treatment; are a couple seeking help in improving communication and addressing resentment within the relationship; or are a guardian of a child with mild to moderate mental health or behavioral challenges. I may not be a good fit for you if (or can not provide the adequate level of care if): you are seeking a magic bullet or brief counseling to fix or solve a complex issue; are trying to treat ADHD for the first time; want treatment for a personality disorder; want a therapist to tell you what to do (I'm happy to provide psychodeducation, but want you to feel empowered to make your own decisions); or want treatment for a child with severe behavioral challenges.

name: Kendall Herr (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I started my career working in the school system as a support to students in elementary school and went on to become a school counselor in a rural high school for the last 3 years. I have experience working with children and teens navigating the challenges of school and life outside of school. As much as I loved being a school counselor, I have transitioned into mental health counseling because I wanted to be able to help on a deeper level. I take time to really get to know my clients to help determine the best course of action to help. I use a variety of interventions ranging from CBT to Person Centered and also work with kids using art and play therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that change is possible with the right support, tools, and the determination to put in the hard work to make it happen. I believe that creating a strong connection with my clients is vital because healing and growth happens when you feel safe and heard. Suicide prevention/intervention have been a big part of my career. I have created prevention programs in schools and train staff on interventions. I became a counselor because I have had a few different counselors help me along my journey and I would not be where I am without them. I hope to be that for you! I have a passion for helping people and it is truly an honor to have people let me in their world and hear their stories.

name: Gina Young (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
One of my favorite quotes is, “everything you want is on the other side of fear”. Fear is often a massive barrier for people to lead the life they truly desire. My goal as a therapist is to help you move though your fears so that you can be your best self. I use an eclectic approach, taking individual values and beliefs into consideration to help the therapeutic process be as approachable and relatable as possible. I mainly use techniques from solution-focused, mindfulness-based, and client-centered therapies. I aim to be an authentic participant in the healing process by walking clients through action plans in addition to emotional processing. If you are looking to make a change, improve relationships, or gain support in any area of your life, let's work together so you can live your best life and embrace what is important to you. You don't have to face change alone, reach out so we can schedule your first session.
Why I Became a Therapist
I have been a lifelong helper and problem-solver, so becoming a therapist felt like a natural step forward. I approach life with an open heart and mind which I believe holds a great deal of value in the therapeutic relationship. Emotional connection and safety is a human need that is easy to overlook for many of us, so I aim to provide that for others while continuing to appreciate the uniqueness of each person.

name: Danae Merrill (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In therapy I aim to help my clients slow down and help them connect with themselves, their body, and their emotions more deeply. Through the process of strengthening our emotional insight and somatic connection, this helps us to foster a sense of control, connection, and confidence that we may have lost sight of. Additionally I am trauma and attachment informed and am trained in EMDR, a treatment that is used to help facilitate healing from trauma. If you are interested in connecting to see if we will be a good match together, please feel free to reach out for a free consultation. I currently have openings for new clients starting in January 2025.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
From my own life experiences, I have come to know the power and magic of therapy and am deeply passionate about the work I do. I welcome all who are seeking space to process, grieve, find deeper connection, heal from trauma, or prioritize their own wellbeing.

name: Daniella Kramer, Keshet Counseling LLC
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I mostly work with youth aged 15-30. I practice holistic and integral psychology, taking the whole body, mind, and spirit into consideration, bringing a somatic, depth, and mindfulness approach to our work. Using mindfulness we will often pay attention to “where” and “how” feelings and experiences are held within your body, work to understand the unconscious stories we tell ourselves, and explore the deeper meanings in your life, as well as the deeper reasons behind your challenges and difficulties. I don't subscribe to the belief that there is something wrong with you, rather "what happened to you".
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold a humanistic perspective, meaning that I believe you are whole, deserving, and perfect at your core. I trust that your inherent intelligence and ability to connect to yourself can be revealed and encouraged through the therapeutic relationship that we will create together, which is why I will often turn to you for answers, as I respect that you are the ultimate authority on your life and experience. I am much more of an engaged and directive therapist than a passive listener, and I will assume you are coming to therapy to make changes. Congrats on taking the first step of reaching out for the help you deserve.

name: Kimberly Randle (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I identify as an integrative and feedback-informed therapist, which means that I use multiple types of evidence-based therapy depending on your need and rely on regular feedback from patients to guide the process. This allows me to individualize therapy to your needs as a whole and unique person. I believe that people and our complex needs can't be solved through a textbook or modality (type of therapy) alone. Relationships, humanity and creativity are also needed to be successful! The modalities that I am formally trained to use include: EMDR Therapy, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, and Narrative Exposure Therapy. The treatment and tools we decide to use on your journey is a collaborative decision that we make together as a team. There is no template to healing!
My Values as a Therapist
I am a member of the BIPOC community as a person who is mixed-race and indigenous. I identify as a feminist, an ally to the LGBTQIA+ community and I believe that Black Lives Matter. I am grounded in social work principles and believe in the advancement of social justice for all communities, not just a select few. I support diversity and understand that most folks who identify as a minority member of a community experience injustice such as racism, classism and misogyny. It is my goal to help people take meaning from these experiences and find peace in a world that feels unsafe.

name: Jess Fox (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I approach therapy holistically & aim to help you connect with your body and emotions so that you can feel more at ease. I will highlight your strengths and inner resources that can help you thrive, while also gently challenging you when you’re stuck in unhelpful patterns. I will work to center your needs, capacity, and feedback so that therapy feels supportive to you. My goal in therapy is to help you access a space of authenticity and connection to yourself, your partner(s), and your communities. I work from an ecosystemic and relational lens; this means that I acknowledge the ways we are in connection with each other and the spaces we occupy, knowing our daily life can be impacted by larger systems such as capitalism and white supremacy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I felt inspired to do this work because of my lived experience. I identify as a bi-racial (Mexican American), second generation, Latinx, queer, gender fluid, kinky person who currently exists in a mid-size body. While these identities are a just fraction of who I am, it’s important to me to share them because they have shaped how I navigate the world. As a person with intersectional identities, I have experienced having to hide parts of myself in order to survive. I have also been in spaces with people who embraced me and made me feel cared for, loved, and able to be true to my authentic self. Because of these experiences, I approach therapy as a way to meet people where they are in their lives and work together collaboratively, so people can live as their most authentic selves. Life can bring us uncertainty and challenges; as a therapist, I believe people deserve to express themselves and be heard in an open, curious, and non-judgmental space.

name: Jess Peterson (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I provide warm, affirming care, in which I aim to empower you to liberate your authentic self by cultivating self-awareness and self-compassion. From this compassionate place, we can begin to explore what lights you up and brings meaning to your life. I believe you know what is best for you, and that we learn to doubt ourselves, diminish our feelings, and tamp down our needs and desires in order to survive. We will find playfulness, humor, and creativity in this process where we can. I enjoy bringing my humanness into sessions, and feel that my authenticity as your care provider helps to build trust and safety between us. I also love bringing in non-traditional and creative interventions: art activities, oracle cards, play therapy, writing activities, and nature-based interventions (but only if you are interested in these things). I center social justice principles in my life, including anti-racism, anti-ableism, anti-capitalism, Health at Every Size, and feminism.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Growing up, I was living with undiagnosed neurodivergence and kept my queerness hidden where it felt safe from my conservative small town in the Midwest. After many years of feeling like there was something “wrong” with me, I eventually found deep validation, understanding, and care in therapy. I experienced the healing power of being seen and heard by another human. As I healed, I realized that it is part of my journey on this gorgeous little floating rock to be this person for others. All of this has amounted to a relentless curiosity about people’s stories and a love for exploring the ways people have found to connect, express themselves, and find joy and meaning in life, including through sex and pleasure, art, time in nature, and more.

name: Jenny Kepler (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My style is down-to-earth, non-judgmental and inclusive. I have a deep faith in the fact that though you may feel lost, the path to wholeness is already inside you. I'm here as a guide to help you find it. I work in a way that is depth-oriented and present-focused. We'll focus on alleviating the hurt that is bringing you into therapy right from the start, with the long-term goal of healing its root causes. I use mindfulness to help you attend to limiting thoughts and how you experience them in the body; and gently address patterns in your life that have you stuck, uncovering strength, resilience and more personal freedom.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My experience as a long-time partner, mother, sister, daughter, friend and traveler add richness to my work. Before my life as a therapist, I worked in publishing, the food and wine industry, the corporate world and as a doula. I received my MA in Integral Counseling Psychology at California Institute of Integral Studies in 2011 and have done extensive training in couples counseling, somatically-informed trauma treatment, infant/parent psychology, family systems, relational psychotherapy, mindfulness and interpersonal neurobiology. I have advanced training in working with clients with disabilities and chronic medical conditions.

name: Lawrence VENASKA (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I help you break down your goals into achievable chunks that move you towards your desired outcomes. We review what got in the way and rework your goals when you get stuck to eventually build momentum in getting past your barriers to success. I help you clearly identify lessons you learned as a child that led to beliefs to adapt to things out of your control that may still be running today. We look at those beliefs still on auto pilot and check out if they are still true for you now and help you connect to your resourcefulness and power more than ever before. From this place new outcomes are available you couldn't achieve when on auto pilot from youthful strategies based on powerlessness.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have an extensive background in working with individuals, couples, families and groups to find their personal power, heal trauma and to achieve their dreams. I have worked in human services field since 1993 and as a psychotherapist sine 1998. I have training in healing developmental traumas and childhood wounds, body centered psychotherapy, systems work, deep listening or attunement and draw on a multitude of perspectives in creating the treatment suitable for your needs. I especially like working with the drama triangle and how to exit it and get increasingly satisfying results in relationships.

name: Ernest Likins (HE/THEY)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
As a clinician, I employ Experiential (Somatic, IFS), ETF & Narrative therapies. Many of my clients come to me to work with themes of boundaries & communication, gender exploration & dysphoria, family & developmental transitions, agency & self worth, ecological connection & the many griefs of white supremacy culture, as well as healing from a spiritual perspective. Whatever approach we take, you’ll find our work together reflecting your healing as a part of our collective healing, & your liberation bound together in the struggle for us all to be free. I recognize that we all hold complexities in our navigations & experiences of the world. I approach therapy from an acknowledgement of the both/and of these experiences – that we all bring areas of wisdom & ignorance to the relationships in our lives. Let’s work together to expand your perspective, deepen your belonging, & explore the ground of your precious humanness. I welcome neurodivergent clients in couple and family systems!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Prior to becoming a therapist, I taught mindfulness in tech spaces with an emphasis on Diversity Equity and Inclusion. I regularly delivered talks on the reality of white supremacy culture in the workplace. I also enjoyed training large groups of people (upwards of 200, mainly white) to use mindfulness and self-regulation to facilitate long-term engagement in the work of undoing white supremacy in the workplace. I've identified as a community organizer for many years, and have worked with Cascade Aids Project, the Nat'l HIV League, Trans Assistance Project, LGBTQ young folx groups both locally & internationally. I'm also an openly HIV+ clinician, and welcome clients who are PLWHIV or those affected by HIV/AIDS. In my down time I enjoy painting with watercolors, hanging out with my cat Red Bean (a regular Zoom guest), goofing off with friends and moving my body, watching Star Trek reruns, touching moss and admiring old trees, and watching the changing colors of clouds at sunset.

name: Sarah Curts (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In Somatic Re-Education Therapy, injury is treated as an incomplete action. The body and mind hold tension patterns out of an effort to "do" something. This approach helps the system complete the action it has been stuck in and thus release the pain that has been held onto. By offering awareness, acceptance, and guiding support, I help clients recognize the unmet needs of their pain, and how to address them so long-held problems release.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a deep spiritual background that informs my practice. Through the study of internal martial arts and mindfulness meditation, I have come to understand how our thoughts and beliefs shape our lives and how by working on the subtle level, big changes can occur.

name: Gabriella Losada (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Your process is important and I don’t believe in changing or taking away coping mechanisms that have allowed you to survive until this point. Instead, the idea is to give you new or different options to sift through complicated situations or feelings and help you evaluate what you feel is no longer serving you. Talking can be a great way to process but sometimes words aren't enough. During sessions, I use a mix of talk therapy, experiential and somatic techniques. Experientially, we might do some writing, make art, use music, or create a tangible coping tool you can use. Somatically, this might look like doing body scans, grounding exercises, small physical movement, guided visualizations, or just pausing and noticing what your body is doing and how it’s feeling. All of my work is rooted in trauma-informed care and intersectional feminist theory to create a collaborative environment where your "no" is honored and where we acknowledge the ways oppressive systems hinder healing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Above all else, I believe in liberation for all marginalized groups. I believe in active mutual aid, radical collective care, and harm reduction. The personal and political are impossible to disentangle and what is happening globally, nationally, and locally often shows up in the therapy room. I hope to create an environment that embodies intersectionality, sex positivity, gender-affirming care, disability justice, and anti-racism. However, I fully acknowledge that this is an ongoing, imperfect process that I have to actively work on. I hold a lot of privileged identities (white, cisgender, thin, formally educated, US citizen) and some largely invisible marginalized identities (Latina, woman, living with chronic illness/pain, queer, neurodivergent). It’s important to me that I understand the impact my privilege has and how that shows up in and out of the therapy room. I always welcome conversations about how our differing or similar identities impact your process.

name: Sarah Moyer (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
My path into therapy began as a personal trainer and basketball coach, where I discovered my love for motivation, mindset work, and helping others reach their potential. I later worked as a professional school counselor in both elementary and middle schools, where I supported students with emotional and behavioral challenges and collaborated closely with educators and families. In addition to my school-based experience, I gained valuable clinical experience working in private practice supporting children with ADHD, anxiety, and emotional regulation, as well as partnering with parents to strengthen the parent-child relationship. As a parent of three myself, I understand firsthand how rewarding—and at times overwhelming—family life can be. That personal experience continues to shape my compassionate, down-to-earth approach to supporting others through the ups and downs of parenting and daily life.
My Approach to Helping
My style is warm, collaborative, and grounded in both emotional insight and practical tools. I use a strengths-based, coaching-informed approach that blends reflection, skill-building, and support for meaningful change. Whether you're navigating the day-to-day or diving into deeper patterns, I’ll walk alongside you—helping you slow down, gain clarity, and take steady, empowering steps forward.

name: Becca Schwartz
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is relationship based, client centered, trauma informed, and at times playful. I utilize several different therapeutic modalities including EMDR, IFS, Mindfulness and somatic practices, Compassion Focused Therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), Narrative therapy, and Anti-oppressive frameworks. My therapeutic work is informed by past experience working in community mental health organizations, addiction treatment, and college counseling. Each of which has guided my understanding of the diverse and complex struggles we face as humans.
My Values as a Therapist
I identify as a sex positive, pleasure centered, queer and gender affirming therapist. Social justice is the foundation of my practice and I will collaborate with you on dismantling and resisting systems of oppression that impact your life.

name: April Taylor (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapeutic style involves assisting clients in processing and gaining better understanding of emotions, habits, or relationships that may be impacting their life in an undesirable manner. This happens through the creation of a safe space that acknowledges intersecting identities, oppressive systems, and generational trauma often add extra stress and turmoil into the lives of women of color. I also aim to empower clients through normalizing help seeking, education about mental health, and believe no one is without skills, we just need a space to discover them. My hope is clients walk away with increased ability to maintain a level of mental health that feels appropriate for them, and life long tools to cope in the face of stressors.
Why I Became a Therapist
I was born and raised in Portland Oregon, a city with little diversity. I have encountered much racism, and discrimination in the mental health field and beyond. This lack of diversity, along with narratives of harm I've come across led me to seek out a career in social work, and continue to pursue my MSW. I engage in therapy, particularly with women of color, with the goal of minimizing inequities, and increasing access to anti-racist mental health spaces. I'm hopeful that by being in practice I am increasing diversity in the field, minimizing one less harmful experience, and ensuring that more voices feel validated.

name: Steven Cohn
approaches:
My Background
I have a PhD in Clinical Psychology from Pacifica Graduate Institute near Santa Barbara, CA. And I have an MA in Clinical Psychology from John F Kennedy University, in Berkeley, CA. Clinically, I specialized in becoming a relationship counselor. I also have an MBA degree from the WP Carey School of Business ("Top 50 Business Schools," US News and World Reports). I have lectured at Stanford University and have been called upon as a relationship consultant, appearing on CNBC.com, KATU 2 TV, and KOIN 6 TV. I have also written and published training materials for graduate psychology students with permission from the American Psychiatric Association, and I'm the author of Your Quick Guide to Relationship Success. My post doctoral education and training is devoted exclusively to relationships. I have trained with some of the leading authorities in the world, including both Dr. John Gottman (of the acclaimed Gottman Institute) and Dr. David Schnarch.
Why I Became a Therapist
I founded The Portland Couples Counseling Center on the guiding belief that with the proper support, encouragement, counseling, and education every couple is capable of having the relationship they truly desire and deserve. My dream, and driving conviction --the passion that gets me out of bed in the morning-- is the belief that couples form the foundation of both the family and the larger community. I also believe that if couples are thriving, then we as individuals, as well as our families, children, and communities will flourish as well. I believe that every couple is capable of co-creating a warm, caring, joyous, loving, affectionate, intimate, lasting, and sexually fulfilling relationship. I also believe that it is possible for couples to work through even the most challenging issues while maintaining a sense of dignity, compassion, and respect. This is my highest hope for you and your relationship.

name: Valenca Valenzuela (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is deeply rooted in existential-humanistic principles, providing a safe and collaborative environment for individuals seeking transformation and rebirth. I employ trauma-informed care and view clients' experiences through a grief-informed lens, embracing a holistic perspective that acknowledges the interconnectedness of emotional, physical, and spiritual well-being. Together, we explore somatic awareness and the nervous system, facilitating an intuitive understanding of self. At the core of my therapeutic method is the cultivation of authentic and meaningful relationships. I view my role as that of a mindful companion supporting you on your journey. I will follow your lead, while remaining curious, engaged, and honest in what I notice. Within this relational space, my goal is to guide individuals towards personal transformation and help them discover their unique path to restoring balance and finding meaning in life.
Why I Became a Therapist
My path to becoming a therapist was driven by a profound personal journey. My own experiences with grief and loss, intertwined with intergenerational trauma, ignited a passionate desire to support others through their unique life challenges. As a therapist, I find immense fulfillment in helping individuals navigate the intricate tapestry of life's transitions. I view each transition as a profound life cycle in itself, a portal of transformation from one chapter to the next. My background in social work, coupled with my deep-rooted passion for grief and loss, is driven by a vision to transform how society engages with grief, fostering a more compassionate and empathetic approach. My existential approach to therapy serves as a powerful tool to shift feelings of isolation, death anxiety, and existential confusion into a journey of meaning-making, balance, and transformation. I'm here to guide you on your path towards greater understanding, resilience, and a deeper connection to life.

name: Ellen Eberhardt (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
Education and Years In Practice: In Practice for 17 years Attended Hawaii Pacific University, Masters in Social Work, Graduated 2008 Additional Credentials Licensed by State of Oregon / L6646 License: License Clinical Social Worker L6646 / 2015 Degree/Diploma from Hawaii Pacific University Masters in Social Work / 2008
More About the People We Treat
Types of Therapy I Provide: Acceptance and Commitment (ACT) Cognitive Behavioral (CBT) Dialectical Behavior (DBT) EMDR Family / Marital Family Systems Feminist Person-Centered Solution Focused Brief (SFBT) Strength-Based Trauma Focused

name: Obi Schlachter (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I hold a MA in Counseling with a specialization in addictions treatment. I am a certified drug and alcohol counselor, and I have been working within mental health for the last 15 years. I began working as a wilderness & leadership guide for youth and have worked in many different roles and areas of the western mental health system. This experience has given me a healthy amount of clinical experience in working with various people and their families amidst unexpected conflict, grief, and chaos that life can hold. I believe each person is a unique ecology, but we all share a desire to connect with our experience of ourselves, others, and the world in a holistic and meaningful way. Integration is a key process of health. My modalities include using mindfulness exercises, somatic narrative, somatic experiencing, breath work, and relationship-focused dialogue to bring balance and acceptance with what is while creating practice and rituals to create change.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
What makes me unique is being a certified movement coach and trainer for the last 10 years and have actively pursued continuing education in somatic depth-psychology to mature my personal practice and understanding of wholeness and balance somatically. Our bodies intelligence is often an unacknowledged resource that a person can tune into to seek insight, expression, release, and healing. Braiding from my experience working in mental health, certified movement coach, and living the hardships of a life changing injury I have created a practice and offering with the aim of supporting clients collaboratively. Authentically receiving, and listening to those I work with, and proactively moving the individual and the collective towards a practice that can create change and wholeness.

name: Maile Sand (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapeutic style is empathetic, honest, collaborative, and creative. I use art directives, reflection, affirmation, encouragement, humor, and observation to help you cultivate your awareness, agency, confidence and healing. My practice primarily involves art therapy, for those clients who choose it—I can assure you that no artistic talent is necessary! I aim to create a safe, non-judgmental, compassionate space for you to explore the things that are causing distress or discomfort in your life. Artmaking is a powerful tool that unlocks areas of ourselves we are unable to access verbally. Whether or not you choose to explore art therapy, all my clients benefit from my experience with evidenced-based practices, including CBT, DBT, IFS, MI and Non-Violent Communication. Change, even from something harmful to something healthy, can be scary. This is where trust comes in; trust in yourself, and trust in me. Together we will cultivate, curiosity, discovery, healing, and beauty.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Seeking help is the first step of a courageous journey toward healing and self-discovery. I use the word courageous intentionally because I know that asking for help is hard, sharing your story with someone new is vulnerable, and change can be scary. But I can tell you from experience, my own and so many others, that it is worth the effort. You are worth the effort! Counseling is an investment you make in yourself, in your relationships and in your future. My career has taken a winding road through child rearing, art education, and fine art. I am returning to counseling with a deeper, broader base of knowledge and experience that will enrich my practice and your experience as a client.

name: Jessica Feinsmith MA LPC (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We all seek meaning and purpose in life and I have found mine in supporting people on their journey. Whether you are looking for support for healing a specific mental health issue or you feel an overall sense of “this is not where I want to be in life,” I understand.
