By getting to know you and your history, and finding out what is stopping you from reaching the goals you have, we work together to unravel the past and the issues that are in your way of achieving the life you desire. I combine years worth of personal growth, world travels, graduate school and several prior careers to support and guide clients to achieve goals and live a fulfilling life. I utilize trainings in attachment theory, mindful self-compassion, solution-focused therapy, trauma-informed yoga therapy, and positive psychology in holistic and creative ways. I see clients in Oregon and accept OHP Reach out for a free consultation. I would be honored to meet with you and see if there is a fit.
More About the People I Treat
I work with individuals over the age of 18 and my specialty is working with young adults. Prior to private practice, I was a college counselor. Combining my young energy with my life experience, I feel a deep connection with my clients. From supporting clients with adulting skills and responsibilities to relationship, dating and family issues to substance abuse and disordered eating behaviors. I've also worked extensively with depression and anxiety, especially during and after Covid. I support clients to practice self compassion as a form of self care. To learn to hear one's own voice and desires and define one's path forward. To heal attachment traumas, identify post traumtic growth and experience loving, authentic relationships. To create a trusting relationship together to support you on whatever healing journey you are on. I accept OHP Healthshare Care Oregon insurance and am OON with United Health.

name: Saren Craig (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
With experience in diverse settings such as inpatient psychiatric hospitals, community mental health, case management, and supported housing, I have gained valuable skills and resources. I understand that external systems often contribute to internal struggles and aim to empower clients by acknowledging the power dynamics in the counseling relationship. I focus on the parts of ourselves that may not have received the affirmation needed along our journey. By offering these younger parts compassion and understanding, healing can occur. I am deeply committed to social justice and continually seek ways to engage in advocacy. I believe that reducing oppression through systemic change and increased awareness fosters healthier, more inclusive communities.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I'm involved in advocacy and activism for the LGBTQ+ Community and support Black Lives Matter. I believe in treating each person with warmth, dignity and respect, while upholding the professional boundaries of the counseling relationship. I work from a broad theoretical background and approach care with a systemic lens. I aim to empower clients, and I understand that each individual has their own pace and path in the growth process. The Portland Metro area rests on traditional village sites of the Multnomah, Wasco, Cowlitz, Kathlamet, Clackamas, Bands of Chinook, Tualatin, Kalapuya, Molalla, and many other tribes who made their homes along the Columbia River. I also acknowledge the systemic policies of genocide, relocation, and assimilation that still impact many Indigenous/Native American families today. I respect the work of Indigenous leaders and families, and pledge to make ongoing efforts recognize their knowledge, creativity, and resilience.

name: Jessica Baker, M.A. LMFT (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have a eclectic approach which is based upon the needs of my client at that time. I believe that the person, or people, that I am working with hold the wisdom to find happiness and healing. My role is to facilitate their goals through use of in depth exploration of the psyche, reflection and behavioral modification within the context of a safe space. I have a strong medical background and believe that people need to be treated as a whole system. This includes mind, body, spirit and relationships.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a background in developmental psychology, nutrition, eastern, western and natural medicine, as well as midwifery. I received my B.S. in Health Psychology from Bastyr University and my M.A. in Counseling with and emphasis in Depth Psychology from Pacifica Graduate Institute. I have clinical experience working with individuals, couples, children and families.

name: Brave Hope Counseling
approaches:
Our Background
Brave Hope Counseling’s mission is to provide accessible mental health care that is representative, affirming and collaborative. We are dedicated to working with folx who experience less access to care due to their race, gender, sexual identity, language, or socio-economic status. We believe that counseling should never be one size fits all and intentionally operate from an anti-oppressive platform. One of our unique strategies is to support social services professionals by providing quality, culturally informative supervision, and a supportive work environment. This strategy ensures counseling services are comprehensive, ethical and appropriate. We’re committed to partnering with our clients and workforce to accomplish our mission by offering unconditional positive regard, honesty and empowering them on their unique journeys. Driving Values Representation Accountability Compassion Honesty Anti-Oppressive Collaboration Advocacy Access to Care Empowerment

name: Kristi Cash White (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have extensive training and experience in various forms of theory and practice, which include treating each individual in a holistic manner, recognizing the importance of family and social systems in a person's growth, building on one's strengths, and seeing people as creative and social beings who need encouragement to thrive. We'll work together on establishing goals for your treatment, and we'll address what is happening in your brain and your body when you are experiencing anxiety and stress in order for you to learn how to calm your systems.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in the healing power of connection, vulnerability, and relationship. It is my desire for everyone to have access to evidence-based, excellent mental health services with a counselor who fits their particular needs. I am passionate about growing diversity within the field, researching and advocating for greater representation of minority populations in counseling and counselor education. As a lifelong Oregonian, I, like the forest mushrooms, thrive in the cool and moist climate of the Northwest. My family embraces the outdoor life of hiking, campfires, and owning more than one Subaru. I am married and actively working to launch my three children into adulthood (we don't talk enough about how difficult this process can be!). Our family practices social justice, participating in marches and community actions that support our Black neighbors, women's rights, and the LGBTQ community. I am an avid fan of the Timbers and Trail Blazers, no matter how often my heart is broken.

name: Steve Manthe (HE/HIM)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I offer In-Person sessions on Wednesdays and Fridays and Telehealth sessions on Tuesdays and Thursdays. "The universe is change; our life is what our thoughts make it." This quote from Marcus Aurelius is over 1800 years old but it's still true today. Your thoughts are making your life and change is guaranteed. If you need someone to help you explore your thoughts, navigate through change, and emerge stronger on the other side, I've a career's worth of experience doing just that. I specialize in anxiety & stress reduction, depression, relationships, marriage, careers, chronic & autoimmune disease, men's issues, and death & dying. I bring an integrative, goal-oriented approach to help you achieve the life you envision.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a student at New York University, completing an M.A. in Counseling for Mental Health and Wellness. Prior to NYU, I enjoyed a 25-year career in various leadership roles where I discovered an aptitude for aiding individuals and groups improve themselves and their relationships. During that time, I also earned an MBA from Auburn University and an executive coaching certification through UC Berkeley's Executive Coaching Institute. I believe the therapeutic journey toward a life well lived starts and ends with trust, empathy, and a shared commitment to your well-being and future success. Now as a counselor, I am passionate about helping individuals, couples, and families navigate anxiety, depression, relationship challenges, grief and loss. I'm also a 22-year husband, a 20-year Dad of 2, and a lifelong runner. Please reach out for a free consultation to see if I’m the right fit. I look forward to hearing from you!

name: L Pearson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My intention is to create an authentic, non-judgmental, safe environment in which I can join you on your journey of self-compassion, exploration, restoration, and growth. I have specialized training and experience in a number of therapeutic modalities and techniques such as: mindfulness, psychodynamic theory, DBT, attachment theory, motivational interviewing, and trauma-informed care. However, there is no one approach that fits all. You and I will work together to develop a therapeutic relationship that provides you with the support you need to actualize your goals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am passionate about living in a world that is safe for all. I strive in my practice to be sensitive to concerns regarding intersectionality, marginalization, and oppression which may include but not limited to: race, ethnicity, gender, sexuality, migration status, acculturation, ability, body size, age, social class, and alternative relationship structures. I affirm relationships of all configurations, gender identities, and sexual orientations. My work is informed by an anti-oppressive, relational-cultural, sex-positive feminist lens. I believe to understand another person one must include the context of culture. Therapy works best when one feels seen, connected, respected, and heard.

name: Robin Perez (HE)
approaches:
My Background
I have worked as an outpatient Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner since 2001. During my carrier as a registered nurse (RN) I worked at inpatient psychiatric units for adults and children as well as other areas of medicine throughout the hospital. The last several years of my RN career I worked as an ICU nurse.
Why I Became a Therapist
I had always wondered what made people "tick" and why some were happy while others sad. In my efforts to understand and learn to help others, I pursued a degree psychology in college but became a nurse at the same time. This dual approach to how the mind and brain/body work only enhanced my understanding and passion to help others. I truly love my work and enjoy working with a very diverse population of people from all genders, backgrounds, races, religions, and lifestyles, etc. I feel that connecting as partners in healthcare has made it easy to understand the uniqueness of each person needs and plan to help them grow and heal.

name: Steph Hawk-Thornehaven
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe healing happens when we honor the whole story. Drawing from my background in family therapy and crisis work, I create a warm, collaborative space where you can explore life's challenges – whether individually or with loved ones. My approach blends systemic thinking with trauma-informed care, always meeting you exactly where you are. I don't position myself as the expert of your life – you already are that. Instead, I'm a curious and caring companion on your journey. Whether you're processing trauma, navigating relationships, or working through daily stressors, we'll work together to understand patterns and discover paths toward meaningful change. I welcome clients from all backgrounds and life stages. Through our work together, you'll find a space where all parts of your story matter. Using collaborative problem-solving and a social justice lens, we'll explore not just immediate challenges, but the broader contexts that impact your well-being.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I deeply believe that every person carries wisdom about their own life, even if past experiences or current challenges make it hard to access. Therapy isn't about fixing what's "broken" – it's about understanding our stories and finding new ways to navigate life's complexities. Change happens when we feel truly seen and heard. This means creating a space where all emotions are welcome, where questioning is encouraged, and where we can explore without judgment. I believe that symptoms often emerge as adaptations to our environments, and what others might label as "problems" are often creative solutions that helped us survive. Systems of oppression, trauma, and societal expectations impact our mental health in profound ways. By acknowledging these larger contexts, we can better understand our experiences and find authentic paths forward. I believe in both/and thinking – we can honor our past survival strategies while learning new ways to thrive.

name: Laura Wozniak (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Though originally trained in family systems with a CBT and Group orientation, I have added from Jungian, self-psychology, internal family systems, and addictions training (including eating disorders and alcohol). I am comfortable working with people who take psychiatric medication or people who have medical conditions. I appreciate collaboration with primary care providers.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am certified in mediation, using the Myers-Briggs Type Indicator, and Gottman couples therapy. I enjoy doing team building with small organizations and have training in DBT/CBT/Mindfulness, PTSD, ADD and critical incident debriefing. My current practice involves handling the special challenges of stress due to the Covid 19 pandemic and social upheavals. I have many years of experience as a clinical supervisor.. I have a limited sliding fee from 50 to 150.00. I can provide a bill for submission to a PPO by the client.

name: Natalie Seibel (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I integrate evidence-based modalities like ACT, DBT, and IFS, along with energy work and spiritual guidance. As a Reiki Master and Spiritual Death Doula, I weave in energetic healing and intuitive insight where it’s desired and aligned. My deepest passion (and most cherished modality) is inner child healing, where some of the most profound shifts occur. Clients I work with often walk away with a deeper sense of self-trust and inner safety; greater clarity and confidence in life decisions; stronger boundaries and more authentic relationships; emotional resilience and the ability to hold discomfort without abandoning themselves; reconnection to their intuition, body, and spiritual path; integration of inner child and shadow parts, leading to wholeness and empowerment. If this speaks to you, I’d be honored to join you on your journey. You don’t have to do this alone.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe we all carry the wisdom to navigate life’s challenges. We already hold the keys to thrive but have likely been made to forget. Systemic & cultural conditioning, rooted in fear and shame, may have led you to disconnect from your truth. It can be hard to imagine another way; I’m here to help you remember. Our wounds aren’t barriers, they’re messengers. Even though you may have been taught to silence or suppress them, they hold deep insight. Shame, in particular, is often the deepest wound. It can whisper that you’re not enough and never will be, keeping you locked in patterns of avoidance. But when you make space for pain, as an inevitable and sacred part of life, you also create space for clarity, healing, and transformation. My practice is rooted in compassion, acceptance, and trust in your inner knowing. I help you build safety within yourself so you can return to your most empowered and authentic expression. I believe this is the heart of reclaiming your personal power.

name: Susan Rosenthall (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Depending on your needs and goals, I offer brief problem-solving therapy and/or longer-term psychodynamic psychotherapy. By design, I have maintained a broad-based practice, welcoming clients with many presenting concerns. However, some of my areas of special expertise include: Adoption (including adoption consultation & work with all members of adoption triad) Anxiety including AlphaStim Assertiveness Attachment and attachment issues Communication Considering parenthood (for single people, same sex couples, older parents) Couples therapy Depression Divorce/separation FASD Family of origin issues Fertility and infertility Gender identity Giftedness Grief and loss Jewish identity Life transitions LGBTQ+ identified Parenting concerns (including one's own issues playing out in parenting) Reactive attachment disorder Relationship concerns Religious and cultural legacies Self-esteem Single parenting Substance abuse Trans-cultural and trans-racial issues Trauma/PTSD Women's issues
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I graduated from Reed College with a BA in English, in retrospect a wonderful foundation for listening to and coming to understand my clients' life stories. Following my BA, I received an MAT (Master of Arts in Teaching), also from Reed. Some years later, I returned to Portland State University and earned a Master's degree in Social Work. I was a social worker in the heart of London before returning to Portland to begin my private practice. (more coming......................)

name: Ryan Brown (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
It is my belief that within each of us lies the potential for personal growth and positive change. I use an Existential-Humanistic approach to therapy, which involves exploring and discovering one's unique and authentic self. In this work, we will explore themes such as choice, responsibility, meaning, purpose, and authenticity so that you may discover a more intentional way of being. I also believe that we experience distress when we struggle to reconcile how things are with the way we want them to be. When this is the case, it can feel like we’ve lost control of our lives, that the world is happening to us, or we’ve lost a sense of who we are. I help clients become empowered to reclaim their lives so that they may live intentionally, authentically, and with integrity, as active players in the creation of their own life experience.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold a Master’s degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from Goddard College with a concentration is Sexual Orientation and specialized training in working with lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, and queer clients. I also serve on the board of Existential-Humanistic Northwest (EHNW). I'm currently a doctoral student at Antioch University studying leadership and change. My education and training have prepared me to work with clients struggling with a diverse range of issues. I am particularly interested in exploring issues that relate to depression, anxiety, lack of fulfillment, life transitions, relationship issues, personal growth, LGBTQ issues, and polyamory & consensual non-monogamy.

name: Jordan Ashford (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Being a counselor for me means being that safe space for somebody who is struggling with the way they view themselves and the world around them. I want someone to walk away from sitting with me and feel like they were truly seen and celebrated. I want them to feel empowered and like they are capable of getting through the things they're struggling with.
Why I Became a Therapist
I always knew I would end up in a helping profession but as I got older I realized I loved talking to people about feelings and why they thought about things the way they did. I loved hearing about people relationships and where they felt like they really flourished and where they felt like their growth edges were. I loved hearing about peoples fears and what scares them, and then what gives them life and fills their cup. Every aspect of our lives is valuable and important and the conversations held in the therapy room are the types of conversations I love having with people. Its an honor to be trusted with these things and to feel that in a place of invisibility, you are seen.

name: Stacey Vallas (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe each of us is drawn toward fulfilling connections with others and with our most life-affirming self, that inner voice that guides us toward what we truly feel and what most deeply matters to us. Sometimes, though, because of hurtful relationships past or present, self-doubt, loss, trauma, or life transitions, we lose touch with that inner wisdom. We feel lost or empty, or get stuck in anxiety, anger, confusion, or low self-esteem, and need support hearing and trusting that inner voice again. Too often we hide from our pain or try to tough it out alone. That you are here means you are open to trying a different path. I am mindful of how I bring my privileged and marginalized identities into our work together. I value collaborating to create a therapeutic alliance in which clients feel safe and honored exploring all dimensions of their identities. I love the work of therapy--helping others connect with a deeper sense of themselves and create more balanced, joyful lives.
Why I Became a Therapist
I am a Licensed Professional Counselor Intern and a Nationally Certified Counselor. I received my Masters in Clinical Mental Health Counseling at Portland State University and also hold a PhD in English and American Literature from Stanford University. Before becoming a counselor, I taught literature and humanities to college students for many years. I’ve always been drawn to the transformative power of stories to illuminate our deepest selves and our links to each other, to history, and to community.

name: Jackie McGivern (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is client-centered and collaborative. Creativity is key; I provide therapy that is shaped to fit YOU - your preferences, your needs, your goals. I believe in doing things differently. Healing and self-discovery is far too complex & personal for there to be a standard practice. I offer 3 modes of therapy: (1) In-Person (office) (2) Virtual (3) Outdoor/Exposure Therapy. Outdoor/exposure therapy consists of an in-person meeting that takes place at a nature or community-based location as determined in collaboration with you. - Better able to access emotions in nature? See you at Mt. Tabor. - Ready to challenge a fear food? I'll join you. - Interested in exploring mindful movement? Let's go. - Want to explore a new resource? I'm happy to help! *I find a mix of these 3 modes can elevate the therapy process by providing a dynamic path for you to explore healing across a wide range of environments & real-life experiences.
More About the People I Treat
Rates vary depending on mode & length of services. Sliding scale available. [ I do not currently accept insurance but I am able to provide a superbill to support you in seeking reimbursement for services from your insurance provider. In some cases, I am able to request a single case agreement for services to be covered under in-network benefits. ] EMAIL @ jackiecool.pdx@gmail.com

name: Kimberly Wilcox, Way of River Psychotherapy
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Rooted in Depth Psychology, I work with a combination of modalities. Together we will discover the approach that feels best suited for you. By meeting in a confidential, honoring environment we will build a trusting relationship that gives space for the work you want to do together. Our sessions will use a combination of insight, action and integration with skill-based techniques to support you on your healing journey. Together we will work to manage and explore the complex depths of meaning and feelings that come with life and relationships. We will work directly with the core elements of thoughts, feelings and experiences to tap into those lost or unknown parts of self that are key to your healing. This work may not only give symptom relief, but bring individual and relationship growth and meaning that sustains you throughout life’s journey. Over time, clients may report feelings of self-acceptance, transformation, authenticity, renewed energy for life and a sense of purpose.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that we as humans are instinctively drawn toward healing and growth with a desire for meaning and soul. This led me to Depth Psychology which believes that healing comes from a deeper level of processing and introspection. It is interested in healing through awareness of those parts of ourselves that have been cast out or not yet able to be known and their impact on our behavior, thoughts and feelings. As I worked with people, I became deeply interested in the dynamics of a person’s relationship with themselves as shaped by their experiences and perceptions of those relationships. I saw many people cut off from their essence, a sense of disconnectedness from self. I believe that healing comes from allowing what is ousted from our awareness to come forward, explore its significance and integrate it; allowing you to become more fully present to yourself and your life resulting in enhancing your relationship with yourself, others, your community and the world.

name: Danielle McWilliams LPC
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a counselor I work from a general theory base of Existential Humanistic and Systems Theory; focusing on the dynamics of creativity, mindfulness, and self-awareness within the context of a person’s worldview. My approach is collaborative & strengths-based, utilizing internal family systems, cognitive, experiential, somatic, creative & relational therapies to encourage clients’ abilities to problem solve, navigate modern life, self-comfort & develop their identity. In sessions clients find a unique space to notice their experiences & observe their place in the world around them. Through this process of individualized, relevant, positive & engaging therapy, clients’ self-expression, awareness & empowerment are cultivated & opportunities for growth & healing can be explored & experienced.
My Approach to Helping
I've been a Nationally Certified Counselor since 2011 & have been providing direct services to youth & families since 2003. I hold a Master’s Degree in Counseling Psychology with a specialty in Children & Adolescent Treatment from Lewis & Clark College, as well as a Bachelor’s Degree in Sociology. My training in both fields helps me see both the individual & the bigger systems (family, culture, world, etc.) they exist within, allowing me to offer my clients a wider perspective & larger framework from which to examine problems & their impact, a particularly useful balance in supporting people of all ages to feel seen & understood around systemic injustices. I’ve worked with children, adolescents, teens, adults & families from a variety of diverse backgrounds, communities, & belief systems; I have experience with issues that span the spectrum of struggles people may face. My work has been done in private practice, elementary, middle & high schools & the juvenile court system.

name: Arwen Huffman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have a Master of Social Work degree and am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker in both Oregon & Washington. I am a Certified Anxiety and Trauma Treatment Provider & LGBTQ+ Affirming: I Specialize in Women's Mental health, focused on perinatal/postpartum, infertility, infant/pregnancy loss (including abortion), menopause; as well as life transitions, divorce, PTSD, life challenges, grief/loss of a loved one & pets. My focus is to partner with you to help identify what issues are interfering with your daily life. I provide a safe, neutral space, while listening to your concerns, focusing on how together we can best work to meet your therapeutic goals and customizing a treatment plan using multiple treatment modalities which are tailored to you and your current needs. I utilize a variety of techniques derived from CPT, CBT, DBT, Solution Focused, ACT, Evidence Based, Mindfulness, Interpersonal and Person-Centered therapies.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in equality for all folks (it's not pie). I believe we all deserve love, respect and support. I believe that no human is illegal and no one human is better than another. I believe that most people are good and decent, trying to do the best they can. I believe that learning is life-long, and reading is fundamental. I believe that you deserve to feel happy. About me... I am a twice divorced mother of 6 grown children, have 3 amazing rescue pups, love reading and enjoy visiting the Oregon coast, watching movies and playing video games. I grew up in Eugene and have been in the Portland area since 2011.

name: Douglas Johns (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Collaborative Couple Therapy (CCT), my primary orientation, structures and models intimate conversations that enable couples to maintain emotional connection during painful interactions. These skills are foundational to healthy relationships. I also incorporate "differentiation" (as described by David Schnarch, Ph.D.): the ability to 'self-soothe' when painful interactions tempt you to angrily blame your partner and/or surrender your innate strength and resiliency. My foundational task as a CCT therapist is in helping you and your partner have intimate conversations about your problems through relating with each other as allies and confidants. In a collaborative conversation I help you and your partner talk with each other as if observing the problem from a platform, suspended high above the fray, looking down at the situation and people with compassionate curiosity, awareness, and reflection. Your experiences and practice in session transfers to your lives outside of my office.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a relationship specialist I devote my entire practice and professional life to understanding what skills make relationships strong and joyful. My primary orientation is called Collaborative Couple Therapy (CCT). I have over sixty hours of training in CCT through direct mentorship with Dan Wile, Ph.D., its originator. I believe so strongly in CCT that, prior to his death in 2020, I invested six years of regular travel down to Oakland, California to train with Dan. I'll bring that same level of commitment to my work with you. John Gottman, Ph.D., arguably the foremost research psychologist on what factors build good relationships, has written that Dan Wile is a “genius” and that CCT comports with his research findings for effective couple therapy. I am also informed by David Schnarch’s description of ‘differentiation’, by depth training in Existential Psychotherapy, by many years of training in and practice of Brief-Solution Focused Therapy, and by Buddhist Psychology.

name: Patrick Petrie (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe therapy is a radical process that hinges entirely on the trust we establish and voluntarily commit to. When else do we get to slow down for an hour and have the opportunity to practice being vulnerable with ourself, let alone a stranger? Despite the sufferings and stressors plaguing our lives, I believe your inner world holds a beautiful hearth that longs to center you and call you home. Over the course of therapy, I believe we will cultivate a practice of trusting this to be true, and a place that can be accessed more readily throughout your day to day life. I trust that what brought you here matters, I trust that change is possible, and I trust that we will make great strides in this work together. My frame of reference is driven by the importance of relationship while also utilizing the wisdom of the body, mind, and spirit to inform us of our best approach to holistic wellbeing.
My Values as a Therapist
The particularity of our stories matter. I strongly affirm the interconnectedness of all living beings, that our struggles are both personal and collective. I welcome your lived experience, your personal beliefs, and your identities without judgment. As a White, able-bodied, and male practitioner, I recognize my areas of privilege and inherent differences in lived experiences. I strive to practice with an open mindset that is culturally sensitive and anti-oppressive. I strive to be a provider who uses my position to empower others, advocate when systems do not serve us, and continuously learn/unlearn for the betterment of our collective liberation and growth.

name: Madeline Ammar (THEY/SHE)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My tools include Hakomi Psychotherapy, Somatic Experiencing, Buddhist Psychology, DBT, Play therapy, Motivational Interviewing, Attachment and Narrative Therapy My style is dynamic, collaborative, and validating. These frameworks and my past life experiences provide a lens through which I’ve learned how to offer compassionate, non-judgemental co-resonance. Building our relationship on a basis of shared respect and consent is a huge part of my practice. If you think we’d be a good fit, let's connect!

name: Jake Cavanah
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Making room for everyone’s uniqueness is essential to creating more harmony in families, couples, and even within oneself. Providing every family member or partner space to be themselves and express themselves promotes more understanding, ultimately leading to more honest and fulfilling relationships. This type of growth helps drive real change. I join clients on a journey that leads them to see how their individuality can thrive in their family, romantic relationship, and/or societal contexts. And much more!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Where we come from, how we’re raised, societal norms, and more help shape us. But we also possess a unique superpower that deserves a voice: individuality. This can create challenges in relationships, but it can also make those relationships more fulfilling and meaningful. I believe finding an optimal balance between one's individuality and their relationships is integral to a quality well-being. Learning how to be one's true self and how to accept someone else's true self is how we experience and maintain the deepest connection possible.

name: Christina Vaughn
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Christina utilizes therapeutic approaches combined with medication management which is vital for improving positive patient outcomes.

name: Couples Clinic of Portland
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Each of us has experienced trials and tribulations in our lives, and we know how restorative and empowering good counseling can be. We are here to collaboratively help you work through whatever you are struggling with and help you find your true, empowered self. Our therapists are trained in Emotionally Focused Therapy, the Gottman Method, Internal Family Systems, Interpersonal Neurobiology, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, and Motivational Interviewing.

name: Megan Dobbs (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
I have experience working with survivors of domestic abuse/intimate partner violence, depression, anxiety, trauma, and life transitions. In our work together, I will utilize evidence-based practices such as Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Narrative Therapy, Emotion Focused Therapy, and self-compassion/mindfulness interventions. It is imperative to me to make sure that you are seen, heard, and understood in all your complexities and experiences. I practice with a critical lens that serves to make sure I’m accounting for your intersectional identities and the power/privilege and systemic issues that affect you, me, and our therapeutic relationship.
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is to be supportive, empathetic, and validating. I hope to provide you an opportunity for growth-whether that is to feel better emotionally, develop as a person, and/or address issues in relationships. I like to use a variety of tools in session depending on what would serve you best. I also realize that your experience is unique to you and is connected to societal systems, cultural family background, gender expectations, and current roles and relationships. My goal is to listen openly and carefully to your experience and to accept you as you are. Our work together will benefit from us being as open and honest in our communication as possible. I focus on strengths and gaining insight over time. Coming to therapy is hard work, and I want to acknowledge the energy, time, and bravery that it takes for you to commit yourself to therapy. I look forward to working with you on your goals and helping you gain insight and positive change and growth for your future.

name: Robert Field (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Before one can receive effective treatment, a better idea of what are the basis of the problems and how to best help need to be established. If there are school related issues, Dr. Field will assist to develop interventions with school staff. Dealing with emotional issues, specific recommendations are made for the best therapeutic approach and if social issues exist, specific approaches will be recommended to fit the issues at hand. After more than 40 years of providing services and developing innovative therapeutic approaches (www.questcamps.com), Dr Field is best suited to provide a correct assessment.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My motivation to become a psychologist was based on my desire to "heal the world". Tikun Olam is the hebrew phrase of putting a priority of helping others and fixing injustices and help reduce pain. I have worked with thousands of families while in practice in Danville, CA and continue to be motivate to continue.

name: Peter Gold (HE/HIM)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
Often times the thoughts and behaviors that got you through difficult and dangerous times in the past work against you in the present. This patterning is in your nervous system--your body, and it is through your body and mind that it is best addressed. I use evidence based, scientifically proven tools and techniques (the polyvagal theory and practice, meditative movement, and others), to help you heal the destructive patterning that is counterproductive to your growth.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have worked as a therapist in a wide span of settings from community mental health to private practice. These include: the National University of Naturopathic Medicine student clinic, the YWCA community mental health clinic, William Temple House community mental health clinic, the Russell Street Counseling Center, and the HumanEx Academy. My first session as a therapist was in 2005 and I've been practicing ever since. I have a Ph.D. in Depth Psychology with an Emphasis in Depth Psychotherapy, and an M.A. in Counseling Psychology with an Emphasis in Depth Psychology from Pacifica Graduate Institute. My B.A. is in philosophy with a minor in sociology from Kenyon College. I have also been practicing qigong for over 26 years.

name: Allison Berl (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I work with adult clients to thoroughly explore and assess current functioning and daily challenges with attention and hyperactivity to determine a diagnosis of ADHD and/or co-occurring disorders. I use a multitude of screening and assessment instruments, as well as an in depth interview of their childhood development and background. At the end of the assessment process, you will be provided with a full diagnostic summary as well as general resources on ADHD treatment to help with your next steps.
My Values as a Therapist
Counseling should never feel like a mystery to anyone. I take care to provide straightforward explanations of the concepts, methods, and processes I use. I offer a safe, transparent, and judgement free space to explore your past and present struggles and success. As a person with ADHD myself, I take great care to provide a neurodiverse affirming experience for my clients. I’m body positive, sex and kink positive, and I affirm disabled, neurodiverse, BIPOC, trans, and LGBTQIA+ individuals.

name: Chris Broussard (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
In counseling, I walk beside clients using proven strategies tailored to each person’s specific needs. By working together to identify the root of the problem, we can efficiently access your felt sense of inner wisdom needed to heal. I use an integrated and holistic approach that draws from internal family systems, somatic experiencing, ecotherapy, transpersonal therapy, humanism, mindfulness, and psychodynamics. Ethical practice is foundational to my work as a professional counselor associate which is guided by the Code of Ethics adopted by the Oregon Board of Licensed Professional Counselors and Therapists.
My Values as a Therapist
My professional approach is informed by my long-term practice and spiritual grounding in Eastern philosophies which hold that all people contain the innate wisdom to break through life's challenges and thrive. I am restored by time in nature and believe in its power to facilitate healing. Play is as important for adults as it is for children. I have a deep vow to work toward the depolarization of society. Suffering can be present in any set of circumstances - I welcome the opportunity to work with people of every race, sex, class, age, ethnicity, religion, national origin, ability, sexual orientation, and gender identity or expression. Cultural sensitivity and antiracism are important to me; respect for each client's cultural background is fundamental to therapy. I am a member of the LGBTQ+ queer community and fully support trans clients during all phases of their journey.

name: Negin Naraghi (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
Life unfolds in messy, beautiful, unpredictable and at times, immensely painful ways. As we make our way through it all, we often need support navigating the terrain of our inner world so that we can show up more fully to all that is happening in our outer world. If you’ve been feeling overwhelmed, disconnected, stuck or alone, then I’m so glad you found your way here. I know from my own life experience that finding the right person to support you in your healing is both a vulnerable and important task. I feel honored to do this work and I am perpetually in awe of our ability as humans to accompany and support each other as we heal and grow.
My Approach to Helping
I operate from the belief that each of us carries a great deal of wisdom, and that our own inner knowing is the key to our healing. However, sometimes the experiences we have been through and the systems we live in can disconnect us from that inner knowing. I also believe that we are relational creatures that benefit enormously from a supportive presence as we find our way back to ourselves. As your therapist, I will work with you to help establish an environment that allows you to tap into that inner knowing. In sessions with me, you can expect that I will work with you to create a state of balance and regulation in your body and nervous system. This will allow us to do deeper work without it being overwhelming. I will listen deeply as we explore things with compassion and care. I will ask you questions to help you process your thoughts, see things from new perspectives and get us closer to your truth.

name: Aly Davis (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
With a counseling philosophy rooted in CBT, narrative therapy, solution-focused therapy, and other therapeutic approaches, I firmly believe in your ability to heal in a safe space. Passionate about overcoming trauma, I offer a straightforward approach to help you reach your goals rapidly while allowing space for exploration, emotions, and healing.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I've experienced firsthand the transformative power of therapy and mental health care, reinforcing my belief that everyone can heal and lead a fulfilled life. I have a passion for helping clients overcome trauma and distressing pasts, employing a straightforward approach, and building trust and honesty in our therapeutic relationship. I stand out as a friendly and warm individual, known for my ability to see all sides in every situation, understanding perspectives deeply.

name: Katherine Chiba, LCSW
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Therapy is pretty simple: two or more people get together in a room and talk. It's not a magic bullet, and it's not pain free. Sometimes, for a while, people in therapy feel worse because they are opening up hurts and traumas that haven't been addressed. Sometimes its painful because we have to face up to things about ourselves or our situations that we would rather ignore. It's worth it because we have the chance to look at things from a different perspective, to ask different questions, to challenge ourselves and, ultimately, get more of what we want out of life. Understanding where we've been and how we got here is important, but in order to feel better, we often have to do something different. In therapy we can learn to identify what the options are, evaluate the risks and benefits of different options, and make the best choice for ourselves. We also have to learn to live with the fact that some choices are not, sadly, on the table.
My Values as a Therapist
I lived in Japan for 17 years, during which time I taught in a community college and did graduate studies in clinical psychology. After the death of my husband I brought our two children back to the US and started my graduate studies over again, this time pursuing a master's in social work at Portland State University. I have been in practice since 1999, first in community mental health and, for the past 15 years, at the VA, where I was hired to help start a Dialectical Behavior Therapy program. For the past three years I have focused on general psychotherapy for clients with a wide variety of diagnosis, including PTSD, depression, and anxiety disorders. I have also trained in Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia, and have utilized this modality in individual and group treatment.

name: Autumn Counseling Services LLC
approaches:
Our Approach to Helping
Autumn Counseling Services, LLC works with clients to reach the goals they have for their lives. We utilize evidenced based practices to ensure the best therapeutic outcomes for our clients. Our therapists each have their own specific techniques and training that they will use to work with their clients. They will ensure they are using the best approaches for each specific client as we understand one size does not fit all. Every client will be met with a person centered strengths based and trauma informed approach to build a trusting therapeutic relationship so you feel comfortable with your therapist. We meet you where you are at, meaning that our sessions are tailored to you and what you feel is important, as well as making sure that you are in control of our sessions and we do not force you when you are not yet ready. Your therapist will help you discuss the things you struggle with, process difficult life experiences, and learn coping skills to help improve your overall well being.
Why We are a Good Fit for You
With our choice of clinicians, we can offer you a therapist who is the best fit possible for you, both personality wise and experience wise. Our therapists have a range of knowledge, experience, and training to treat various presenting problems, populations, and mental health disorders. This allows us to offer a therapist that will be a good fit for you for whatever struggles you may be experiencing. Please reach out to us if you have any questions for us or about our clinicians. We are ready to help you make progress towards your goals and make your life more livable. Also, we have availability now, so don't wait to reach out! Appointments can be made online at our website or by contacting us today by calling or messaging us.

name: Laina Yoswein (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a therapist, my style is approachable and creative. I believe that therapeutic relationships grounded in trust and authenticity foster greater self exploration, growth, and healing. Sessions with me are collaborative and attuned-- meaning I like to work together & am mindful of your needs. Come to therapy one day and really don't feel like talking? I get it, maybe we can draw or find a song to listen to together as a way to get started. I am influenced by a number of evidence-based therapeutic modalities that can be useful tools to help reduce suffering in the moment, grow self-compassion, and help us gain greater understanding of our complex selves. Liberatory, trauma-informed, and anti-oppressive frameworks are central to my practice.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I come to this work because of my own experiences in adolescence and the belief that there is so much to navigate during adolescence and emerging adulthood that is often minimized or misunderstood. I also believe there is wisdom, transformation, and blossoming, that can arise out of the hardship. As a queer mixed race person of Korean and white descent, I offer additional perspectives and lived experience around the joys, challenges (and everything in between), that come with the task of navigating multiple identities during a life stage centered around gaining a greater sense of self. I believe that healing happens within trusting relationships and that we shouldn’t have to go through difficulty alone.

name: Aaron Miller (HE/HIM)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
In times of uncertainty and turmoil, psychotherapy offers a path towards clarity, freedom, and relief. It's not just a tool for navigating difficulties; it's a gateway to self-discovery and growth during life's transitions. With nearly two decades of experience, I've had the privilege of walking alongside individuals, couples, and families as they navigate relationship challenges, work-related worries, personal losses, and the twists and turns of life's journey. One of my areas of expertise lies in guiding individuals through the aftermath of trauma and abuse. I've undergone extensive advanced training to provide the specialized care needed to heal and rebuild after such experiences. Your pain is valid, and together, we can work towards finding strength, healing, and renewal.
My Approach to Helping
We humans have an innate drive towards growth and health and expansion. Over time and throughout life, this natural drive is often hampered through difficult experiences and problematic "lessons" we learn from others. When this occurs, we become cut-off from our experiences, cut off from our hopes, cut-off and cut-off from ourselves. Over the past 20 years in the mental health field, I have worked broadly with children, adolescents, adults, and families. This has included work in psychiatric day treatment programs, community mental health from our needs, cut-off from ourselves, and cut-off from others. Unfortunately, this process is often less-than-conscious and occurs outside of our awareness, leaving us bored, dissatisfied, isolated, stuck, anxious, and depressed. As a therapist, I work hard to co-create an ongoing dialogue with my clients in which they can safely become aware of their experiences, hopes, needs, and desires without fear of judgement.

name: Carol-Joy Brendlinger
approaches:
My Background
I specialize in both group and individual work. As a certified Spiritual Director, I have held contemplative and mindfulness-based spaces for individuals to slow down and connect with their wisdom and truth. My training has grounded me in the awareness that it is our bodies that give us our answers and when we stop to breathe and listen we can find the answers we have been seeking.
Why I Became a Therapist
I am in the field of Counseling because I believe that more mental health support is needed in our world, due to the many challenges and traumatic experiences that individuals face. I think that people are resilient and capable of healing and positive change. My life has been focused on peacemaking, social justice, eco-justice, and equality for all people. It is important for me to continue to listen to and learn from marginalized populations and be active in finding ways to provide resources and care to those who do not have access to many resources.

name: Courtney Watson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
Therapy with me is relational, exploratory, and grounded in curiosity. I integrate multiple therapy models (IFS, AEDP, Relational, and more) to help you develop a more compassionate relationship with yourself — especially with the parts that feel conflicted, hidden, or hard to understand. This work invites depth and emotional honesty, offering space to untangle what’s been carried, question what no longer fits, and reconnect with what feels most true within you. My style is warm and steady — I hold space with care and offer honest reflection when it supports your growth. Our work begins with building a strong, safe therapeutic relationship. The early sessions are a time to slow down and get to know one another. From there, we’ll move at your pace - gently deepening into the work as trust and safety take root.
My Values as a Therapist
I am particularly passionate about effective grief support. I have volunteered at The Dougy Center helping to support children and adults who had a recent death in their family and continue to offer support for the grief community in my therapy practice. I also support those receiving treatment for terminal illnesses. I have training/continuing education in IFS (Level 1), AEDP (Level 1, 2), Grief Therapy, ADHD & Neurodivergence, Religious Trauma, and Trauma-Informed Therapy. For supervisees, my priority is to support your recognition and development of who you are and who you can be as a practitioner. It is my hope that you experience supervision as a safe and supportive place to ask questions, be self-reflective, share mistakes/struggles, and to get the help you need to feel increasingly effective in your work. I strive to elicit ongoing feedback about how the process is working for you and hope that you will find the courage to let me know if you have concerns or unmet needs.

name: Gabriel Boyer
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Conventional therapy treats suffering as a disease--diagnose, dissect, eliminate--rather than an important element of our lives. People feel anxious, depressed, etc. for good reasons: trauma, old wounds, armor or patterns that avoid painful parts of our lives. Meaningful change occurs through connecting to deeper emotions and relational themes that keep coming up. Together we can slow down, explore your experience holistically and connect your history to the present. This expands awareness, softens somatic rigidity and often allows something fresh to emerge. To do this, we cultivate a genuinely safe, supportive therapeutic relationship--tailored to you as a unique person--so you feel more comfortable to explore more deeply and to loosen the knots keeping you stuck.

name: Michael Running (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Counseling is relational: your relationship with yourself in your world, my own relationship to my self, and the relationship we form together. My person-centered and existential orientation to counseling sprouts from the rich soil of Gestalt Therapy, which brings curiosity, exploration, and present-process into the healing you wish to see. From this ground, I also utilize mindful somatic psychotherapy in understanding the wisdom your body and felt experience hold. To help transform painful emotions or traumatic experiences, I also use Emotion-Focused Therapy to access emotions that promote well-being which often lie underneath more painful ones. Bringing mindfulness into our relationship can also be an effective support to access and understand your experience. Curiosity and acceptance are the two pillars my counseling approach rests upon. Most of all, I trust in and rely upon your holistic experience and organic wish for well-being.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Licensed Professional Counselor and a Nationally Certified Counselor in private practice. Much of what I bring to therapy is informed by the near-decade I spent as a Buddhist monk both in the U.S. and overseas in Taiwan. This transformative and dedicated experience continues to unfold in meaning and purpose today. I was born here in the Willamette Valley of Oregon, falling in deep connection with the lands and waters that make this home so special. Before and after my time in the monastery, I worked for a decade with land conservation nonprofits throughout the state. I graduated from Portland State University in 2020 with a master’s degree in counseling for relationships and individuals. Concurrent with the accredited counseling program at PSU, I also received extensive training in Gestalt therapy, somatic psychotherapy (Hakomi), Emotion-Focused therapy, and Acceptance and Commitment Therapy. I also serve on the board of the Oregon Counseling Association.

name: Matthew Beeble (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I operate on the belief that you already have what you need within yourself to experience fulfillment in your life. Unfortunately, there are a lot of things that can get in the way and leave you feeling like you are falling short of who you should be and what you should be doing. This can lead to a sense of being out of sync and other symptoms that are causing you to seek therapy. I believe that the most important task set before you is to make meaning of your life and to remain steadfast in this task. This is done by increasing awareness of your experience and letting a new understanding come forth, one that clarifies your identity, results in a shift in the way you experience yourself and your relationships and a feeling of peace and presence in everyday life. My passion is to help you see your struggles as an opportunity for growth and aiding your search for understanding. I find it rewarding and I'm humbled by the opportunity to witness bravery on a daily basis.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a licensed mental health counselor who has been providing therapy for Clark County residents since 2008. I grew up in Portland and got my training in Seattle. I graduated from Seattle University's Master of Arts in Clinical Psychology in 2005. Before settling in the Portland/Vancouver area, I spent 2 years in London and I worked as volunteer counselor for individuals without insurance.

name: Emily Padilla
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I seek to create a safe space where children feel accepted and heard. I utilize wholistic and evidence based practices to support and guide clients and families through the therapeutic process. I encourage and empower my clients to honor their voice and embrace their uniqueness. I have been working in education and social services for 10 years.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in the power of nurturing relationships and modeling positive habits and coping skills.

name: Samantha Lynes (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My name is Samantha Lynes and I am a Licensed Mental Health Counselor (LMHC) in Washington State and a Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC) in Oregon State. My years of experience in the mental health field is over 9 years. I provide evidence-based best practices and therapies to tailor treatment to meet the specific needs of each unique client in a safe and non-judgmental manner. The therapies I provide are Mindfulness-based Cognitive Therapy (MBCT), Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Cognitive Processing Therapy (CPT), and Trauma Focused Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (TF-CBT). License: State of Washington, Licensed Mental Health Counselor, LH61261913 License: State of Oregon, Licensed Professional Counselors and Therapist, C8126 License: National Board of Certified Counselors 1234738 / 2019
More About the People I Treat
My specialties are: Addiction ADHD Anger Management Anxiety Bisexual Bipolar Disorder Career Counseling Codependency Coping Skills Depression Divorce Drug Abuse Dual Diagnosis Gender Dysphoria Gender Non-conformity Grief Lesbian LGBTQISA2+ Life Transitions Personality Disorder PTSD Self Esteem Sexual Addiction Serious Mental Illness Sleep or Insomnia or Sleep Disorder Spirituality Stress Substance Use Thinking Disorders Trauma Video Game Addiction Bisexual

name: Dr. Kimberly Christensen
approaches:
My Background and Approach
With other a decade of clinical practice, my approach to therapy begins with the interconnectedness of mind, body, soul, and spirit. I support individuals as they feel, digest, and embody the full range of human emotions, leading to the experience of being alive and living in the present. Each therapy session is a step forward, nurturing insights, fostering a deep sense of presence, and unveiling connections previously unexplored. As a licensed marriage and family psychotherapist with a body-oriented approach, my foundational training was completed in Berkeley, CA. Later, I completed my doctorate in Somatics at the Pacifica Graduate Institute, focusing on Jungian depth psychotherapy. My doctoral research delved into the realms of community dream groups, the role of imagination within the body, and the power of archetypal rituals. These areas of study have deeply influenced my therapeutic practice, enriching it with a nuanced understanding of the human psyche.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Through many transformations, much gained, and layers shed, my commitment to mindfulness and inner healing was necessary for my work as a clinician. Living in France, I absorbed a culture that values deep connection, artistry, and the beauty of the everyday, enriching my practice with a sense of presence and appreciation for life’s nuances. My journey continued as I earned a Master’s in Anthropology from the California Institute of Integral Studies, where I explored the vast tapestry of human cultures and rituals. This blend of French cultural immersion and anthropological study deeply informed my approach to mindfulness, integrating a global perspective into my therapeutic work. My journey’s arc brought me back to Portland where I now offer its fruits to others through healing therapy. Together, we will embark on an adventure of self-discovery and healing, leveraging the power of mindfulness, the wisdom of the body, and the depth of the psyche to facilitate transformation and growth.

name: Gina Patrick
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Where there is hope and empathy, there is a way. I enjoy helping clients address and overcome difficult challenges in their lives. I've worked as a trauma counselor, and I've treated clients who struggle with anxiety, depression, OCD, phobias, ADHD, relationship challenges, and life transitions. In addition to my counseling experience, I've worked with teens and adults to help improve their executive functioning. The healing process is a journey and I feel honored to walk beside my clients.
My Values as a Therapist
Achieving optimal wellness is a fluid process. It requires engagement and a desire to expand one's outlook to include new perspectives and possibilities. To that end, I understand that the most effective therapists are those who invest in their clients and make an effort to understand each client's unique culture, needs, strengths, challenges, and goals. Providing empathy, curiosity, hope and support are crucial to a successful client/therapist relationship. When clients have the right therapeutic tools and a trusting therapeutic relationship, including space that is safe and collaborative, growth and healing are both exciting and possible.

name: Jamie Friedman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I help clients using a variety of approaches rooted in emotion, compassion, and curiosity. Emotion Focused Therapy and Internal Family Systems are two modalities that can help you understand, honor, process your internal experiences. I have also received training in interventions (Accelerated Experiential Dynamic Psychotherapy, Neuroaffective Relational Model) that work in present time to support and repair traumatic experiences, explore identity, and strengthen resiliency. Mindfulness and body awareness may also show up in session to release trauma held there and to ground your experience.
More About the People I Treat
I treat adult individuals and couples with an emphasis on LGBTQ+ experiences, ethical separation, divorce and co-parenting, life transitions, religious exploration, grieving, and discernment, family estrangement, moral injury, reconnecting with your body, racial and racialized experiences, misattributed parentage, as well as depression and anxiety issues. Whether you’ve had many counseling experiences, or this is your very first, I look forward to learning about who you are and where you want to go on your path towards healing.

name: Arrival Counseling annie dolle LCSW (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I would like to know the world in which you live. It is only through careful tracking that we can find our way to what is ailing you. I will join you in this journey. It must be safe, accepting, warm, curious and natural. I am not the expert with the wand (unless doing EMDR;) but rather a fellow human working to find meaning, freedom and truth in this lifetime. I have lived, trained, read, meditated, and contemplated throughout my life/education/career to be a better and more skillful vessel for healing. This is both my interest and passion. Mental health issues are a natural response to trauma and a toxic culture and I understand. It is OK. We will find our way through and you are not alone.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a Peace Corps volunteer decades ago, I found my passion listening and learning from others. I saw first hand that much of what we know and believe comes from our unique families, culture and environment. I then began a career in social services drawn to unfair and painful aspects of our society. My work was very strength-based and problem focused. Always growing my capacity to listen with my heart, I developed greater internal space for truth, pain, and beauty. I shifted my focus from crisis response to curiosity, safety and compassion. Amazingly I found that this led to healing all on its own! I have worked with people of all identities and celebrate diversity. I even had the opportunity to work with a team providing mental health therapy to expats in HCMC Vietnam for five years. I listen with my mind, body, and spirit and know that if I track clients closely, they know what needs to happen. As a student of many modalities I know that I will never stop working to hone my craft.

name: Elana Margot
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My therapeutic style is compassionate, open-hearted, and curious. I have a background in Buddhist Psychology and Contemplative Psychotherapy. I am trained in EMDR therapy and I use IFS-Informed and Somatic-based therapies with clients. I'm also a certified Mindfulness Instructor. My training is rooted in the healing possibilities of mindfulness, non-judgmental presence, and growing our awareness of interconnection.
My Approach to Helping
My approach is trauma-informed, client-centered, and somatically-based. We will work together to find and nurture feelings of connection, freedom, belonging, and contentment. I use Mindfulness-based and Internal Family Systems-informed modalities to help clients feel less identified with the challenges in their life and to become more open to the positive changes they are seeking.

name: Lolo Halman (HE/HIM)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
For individuals, I use embodied techniques to help you explore and uncover deep insights into your personal challenges. If you value active engagement and a balance of compassion and critique in counseling, my approach could be right for you. I'm also committed to social justice and skilled in navigating the dynamics of polyamory/ENM, inviting a safer space for exploring diverse identities and relationships. For couples and families, I help you manage recurring conflicts through a mix of clear interventions, conflict resolution tools, and work to do outside of sessions to foster progress and instill a sense of hope. If you're considering polyamory or ethical non-monogamy, I offer guidance to help determine if this path aligns with your relationship goals, supporting healthy and informed decisions.
Why I Became a Therapist
Becoming a counselor was an unexpected journey for me. Initially drawn to the Process Work Institute by my passion for innovative conflict resolution, I yearned to discover methods to transform conflicts from sources of division into opportunities for growth and insight. Throughout my training, especially as I began working more regularly with individuals and couples, I discovered a profound enjoyment and effectiveness in this work that genuinely surprised me. As my practice has evolved, I’ve shifted my focus more towards facilitating relationship conflict. I find relationship counseling particularly fulfilling because it challenges us to confront the intense emotional challenges and pain that can appear most powerfully in intimate relationships. In navigating the intricate dynamics of human connection, I believe my work becomes truly transformative, offering both my clients and myself rich opportunities for insight and growth.

name: Megan Bucknum (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We can approach our work together using a variety of frameworks and I will adapt to meet your needs and preferences. I use frameworks such as Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, and Compassion Focused Therapy. You are the expert of your story and I strive to be a fellow traveler in our time together. To ensure we are moving in the right direction and honoring your experience, I will often seek out your feedback. At this time all counseling appointments will be conducted with a telehealth platform using phone or video conferencing applications.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW) and Certified Alcohol and Drug Counselor I (CADC I). I have been working in social services since 2012 and have been providing direct counseling support since obtaining my masters degree from Portland State Graduate School of Social Work in 2015. My style is person-centered, relational, and collaborative. I also value taking a curious and non-judgmental stance with all of my clients. A little bit about me and how I recharge outside of my therapy role. I enjoy spending time in nature, attending to my plants, playing with my dog, reading novels or comics, enjoying video games, and adventuring in the world of D&D.

name: Evan Gurley (HE/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Therapy will consist of discussing the context of your life, including your relationships, work, interests, and goals as well as your thoughts and feelings and how they are impacted by your present and past. I will encourage you to express emotion and accept your thoughts and feelings in order to build confidence and reach your goals. With families, I will work to help each member to understand the feelings of the others and connect with each other intimately in order to change unhealthy interactions. With children and adolescents, I will help them understand and cope with their painful feelings and encourage connection with friends and family. In my work with men I help in facilitating healthy emotional expression and effective coping strategies to better manage life struggles. With people who've experienced trauma, I will gently encourage them to explore the trauma, the unhelpful beliefs it may have created for them, and encourage them to express their feelings to others.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Therapy is my passion because I believe that expressing your feelings and thoughts allows you to know and love yourself. I believe that it is a miracle that I can help someone feel happier through dialogue. I am strong believer in the importance of diversity, social justice, environmental justice, and representation of all cultural groups in our society. I am inclusive of all people from all walks of life and will strive to understand your differences as well as similarities to me and connect with you.

name: Alicia Banister
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I draw on the mindfulness-based, body-centered psychotherapy of Hakomi. I hold a particular focus on the nervous system, neuropsychology, and the relationship between trauma and the body. My approach involves both holding oneself with radical acceptance and inclusion, while also holding the possibility of change. Claiming Self and cultivating a sense of embodied wholeness informs all of the work that I do. I hold a deep respect for the wounds and scars we each carry and a profound gratitude for the brilliance inherent in each person that helps them to navigate the world with these wounds.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I earned my Masters in Counseling Psychology at Lewis & Clark College in 2018. Prior to that, I completed a year-long internship and extensive study at Mindful Experiential Therapeutic Approaches (M.E.T.A) in Portland, OR, focusing on Hakomi and mindfulness-based body-centered psychotherapy, as well as the Re-Creation of the Self, and Primary Attachment Psychotherapy. I have a broad background in somatic-oriented therapies, including massage therapy, Biodynamic Craniosacral Therapy, and therapeutic yoga. Prior to counseling I ran an Integrative Bodywork practice for 10 years, focusing on mind-body integration and trauma. I have studied extensively the work of Stephen Porges and Peter Levine and find that their work informs much of my approach to counseling.

name: Dr. Tee Mayfield
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
She has been practicing mental health treatment in a variety of settings such as Inpatient, PHP, IOP and private practice. She has Bachelor's degree in Psychology and a master's degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from Texas Woman's University and is currently a Doctoral Candidate in Marriage and Family Therapy from NCU. She is skilled in different modalities such as DBT, CBT, EMDR as well as Existential therapy. Tee is also resourceful and helpful with navigating through minor to major crisis in life.

name: Nicole Frangella (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to counseling is rooted in understanding how early relationships shape current emotional patterns. I use object relations theory to help clients gain insight into their behaviors and relationships. I incorporate Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) to challenge unhelpful thoughts and build healthier coping skills. Accelerated Experiential Dynamic Psychotherapy (AEDP) helps clients process emotions, especially those tied to past trauma, in a safe and nurturing space. I also draw from Family Systems Theory to address how family dynamics influence emotional well-being. Therapy is a collaborative process, where we set personalized goals and strategies. My goal is to empower clients to make lasting changes, build emotional resilience, and approach life with greater self-awareness and confidence.
Why We are a Good Fit for You
Creating a safe, welcoming environment where you feel heard and respected is my priority. I am committed to moving at a pace that feels comfortable for you, ensuring that we take the time needed to establish a strong foundation of trust. I truly believe that therapy works best when it's a collaborative process, where we partner together to identify your unique goals and develop an approach that fits your needs. Whether you're dealing with anxiety, depression, family issues, or significant life transitions, I aim to offer both practical tools and emotional support to help you feel more empowered and in control. My approach is one of mutual respect and open communication, where you are an active participant in your healing journey. I am here to walk alongside you, offering guidance and encouragement as we work together to create meaningful change and help you move forward with confidence and clarity.

name: Meghan Opbroek, MS (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My practice is inclusive and affirming. I’ve done my own work understanding both my privileged and marginalized identities and I’m dedicated to the continuous work involved in knowing one’s biases and beliefs. I’ve worked with folks from all backgrounds, abilities, and cultures. My practice is not tolerant, it’s affirming. Affirming of all relationship types, sexual orientations, religions, spiritual leanings, body types, kink leaning and lifestyles, races, ethnicity, cultures, gender representations and identities, ages, documentation statuses, socio-economic statuses, educational backgrounds, and more.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received my Master’s Degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from Portland State University. If you’re into the technical stuff, my work is rooted in traditional and contemporary psychodynamic theory, specifically Attachment Theory and Object Relations Theory. If you aren’t into the technical stuff, I believe people become who they are in relation to and because of others and their experiences. Understanding who you are as a person, parent, child, friend, partner, or any other identity, is key to happiness and well-being. You’re going to continue to change and move forward in your life. We can work together to help that change be positive.

name: Krispin Mayfield (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I specialize in helping people heal from the lasting impact of high-control religious environments. With years of experience supporting clients through religious trauma, I create space to gently explore the beliefs still shaping your nervous system, even if you no longer agree with them. Together, we’ll make sense of the emotions and needs that were once suppressed, so you can build a life where your identity, boundaries, and values take the lead.
Why I Became a Therapist
As someone who grew up in a high-control religious environment, I understand how deeply early belief systems can shape our sense of self, even long after we’ve left them behind. For the past several years, I’ve researched and written about the psychological impact of high-control religion — work that continues to inform my approach as a therapist. I’m passionate about helping others recover from religious trauma, rebuild self-trust, and reconnect with their emotions, identity and values.

name: Jennifer Gray (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I specialize in working with driven professionals who feel overwhelmed by the demands of their careers and personal lives. My focus is on helping individuals navigate burnout, perfectionism, self-doubt, and the challenges of maintaining sustainable boundaries. As a licensed therapist and EMDR-trained clinician, I provide tailored care designed to meet the unique needs of ambitious, high-achieving individuals.
