Using evidence-based practices like EMDR therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, and Attachment Theory, I guide clients through the complexities of stress, anxiety, and patterns that keep them stuck. Whether it’s addressing money mindset blocks, reducing chronic overwhelm, or improving confidence and assertive communication, my work emphasizes meaningful change and alignment. Together, we’ll create space for what truly matters while building a foundation of resilience, balance, and authenticity.
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is deeply personalized, holistic, and action-oriented. I believe that healing requires not only exploring past experiences but also making proactive changes to create a fulfilling future. From our first session, I’ll guide you in taking meaningful steps toward balance and well-being. I intentionally limit my caseload to provide focused, customized care. Our work together will involve a collaborative exploration of your personal and professional life, helping you untangle challenges and identify opportunities for growth. I use a variety of evidence-based frameworks—EMDR therapy, somatic practices, mindfulness, and cognitive approaches—to tailor a treatment plan specific to your needs. In our sessions, I’ll encourage you to embrace all parts of yourself, even those you might find chaotic or vulnerable. By integrating your strengths and struggles, you’ll develop the confidence and clarity to align your life and work with your values.

name: Jake Bassen (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
It is my goal to help you find hope and empowerment both personally and for the relationships that matter most to you. In our work together, you can expect to find a supportive and safe space to explore and make steps toward changes you would like to see in yourself or in your relationship. I began practicing therapy in 2019 and have primarily focused my practice on working with couples, although I also see adult individuals for a variety of concerns including life transitions, relational challenges, men's issues, depression, and anxiety. I currently see clients at the Couples Clinic of Portland located in NW Portland. As a Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist (LMFT) I am trained in systemic couples and family therapy, Emotionally Focused Couples Therapy, and the Gottman Method. I hold a masters in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from George Fox University and a BA in Psychology from Oregon State University.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that connection to oneself and others is the foundation of one’s sense of security, purpose, and fulfillment. Disconnection from self and others can be distressing because we as humans are wired to be close to others from the moment we enter the world. I believe that the work done in therapy centers around trust, respect, and connection. I am LGBTQIA+ affirming and enjoy working with folks from any racial and ethnic background, although most important to me is that my clients feel I am someone that they can envision trusting and being a safe person to them. In my free time I enjoy recording, producing, and performing music - playing guitar in multiple bands around the Portland area. I also enjoy spending time at home with my wife and my 11-year old Tuxedo cat watching fantasy and sci-fi TV shows.

name: Kitty Carter (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I received my PhD in Social Work and Social Research with an emphasis on children's mental health and caregivers work-life balance in 2007 at Portland State University. I completed my MSW in 2004. I have been working in the field of Children's Mental Health for over 25 years in a variety of settings including residential treatment for youth, as a Child and Family Therapist, Clinical Supervisor, Outpatient Clinical Manager, Division Director, Adjunct Professor, and mental health researcher. I have been licensed in the State of Oregon for 15 years and have worked with all kinds of kids and families with various strengths and challenges. My approach to therapy is eclectic and flexible to best meet your needs. My work is shaped by attachment theory, trauma informed practices, cognitive behavioral approaches, neurobiology, child development, the family life cycle, family systems and mindfulness practices. I attend regular training to stay abreast of the latest advancements in the field.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Native Portlandian, wife, mother of one human and two fur babies. In my free time, I enjoy getting out in the beautiful Pacific Northwest, connecting with friends and family and enjoying this amazing food scene we have here. I have spent my entire adult life dedicated to children's mental health and working with kids and families. I love it! Mental health matters! As a Social Worker, I believe in the dignity and worth of all persons. I believe in keeping families together. I believe recovery is possible. I have been on an Equity Advisory Council, participated in Equity Leadership Training, and have specialized training in culturally responsive practice and equity and inclusion. I believe in trauma informed care and have specialized training and experience in organizational models of trauma informed care and trauma specific treatments.

name: Isaac Rider (HE/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I strive to create a collaborative environment where you can bolster your strengths and feel confident you can navigate through the concerns that brough you to therapy. To help you achieve relevant, personalized therapeutic goals and develop effective coping tools, we may use interventions from cognitive behavioral therapy, EMDR, and solution-focused therapy. Additionally, I incorporate a person-centered stance to ensure you can feel understood, accepted, and confident you are working with a clinician who is genuine. If these qualities become hard to authentically cultivate in our work, I will do my best to provide you with a referral to a therapist who may be a better fit for you. Finally, I regularly incorporate cultural humility and a relational-cultural framework to ensure we can work in a manner that is sensitive to your experiences and background. My goal is not to simply treat you as I would like to be treated, but rather to treat you as you wish to be treated.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe we all have the capacity for healing and growth. I also deeply value cultural humility and regularly strive to learn more about the cultural identities of others and my own. I became a counselor to help channel my innate desire to help others into a positive outlet. Additionally, I have had several positive experiences in my personal counseling that nudged me to this field. I earned my Masters in Mental Health Counseling from Montana State University. I have experience working in such settings as college counseling, community health, and private practice environments. I have received specialized training in CBT-I and TF-CBT, and I am currently in training for EMDR. People have described me as warm, patient, accepting, calming, and insightful. When I’m not working, you can find me spending quality time with family and friends, admiring local bookstores, and enjoying the outdoors. I appreciate you taking the time to learn more about me. I look forward to connecting with you!

name: Kori Kramer (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I see my job as a counselor as being a supporter of your work to know, grow, and use your own wisdom and strength to move toward your goals. My approach is a combination of multiple methods. I tend to think about cases through the lens of biopsychosocial, existential, attachment, and person-centered theories. In terms of skills, I lean toward dialectical behavioral therapy (DBT), cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT), and acceptance and commitment therapy (ACT). More importantly, I like to see what works for you as an individual and adjust the ideas and skills I bring into our sessions to fit your needs. I am also aware of how power, privilege, and oppression can affect our work together. I invite discussion around this and seek to lessen the impact of any imbalance. My goal is to empower you as the client to have choice and autonomy in our work together.
Why I Became a Therapist
Being a therapist is one of the greatest honors of my life. I am continually humbled by the opportunity to witness and support my clients in their courageous efforts to understand, heal, and grow beyond the difficulties they experience. After all of the hard things they have been through, my clients still take the risk to trust me, to open up, to try to do the thing they are not sure they can actually do. I feel fortunate to get to be a part of such incredible work every day.

name: Michaele Dunlap PsyD (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
Reed College, BA Psychology; Lewis & Clark College, MA Psychology; Pacific University, Psy.D. Clinical Psychology. In private practice as a psychologist since 1989. Specialized training in alcoholism, addiction, trauma, couples counseling, family counseling and improving relationships in closely held or family owned businesses.
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a therapist because I am fascinated by human resilience, human relationships and our capacity for growth. I value community, collaboration; people's willingness to be helpful and loving to one another. I cherish people's ability to look into their situations, review their options and revise their choices. Sometimes life can be very difficult; with commitment, persistence and support life's difficulties can be managed. If we can't overcome - we can learn to cope.

name: Amanda Best (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a systemic and relational therapist, I offer a challenging yet nurturing presence to help guide you to your therapeutic goals. Whether it be sexual concerns, resolving trauma, managing anxiety, or changing your relationship structure from monogamy to non-monogamy, I aim to offer support and insight throughout your process. Sex and couples therapy is approached from a queer and feminist framework while issues are contextualized through the systems of patriarchy. As a pleasure activist, I will work with you to deconstruct the impact of patriarchy and individualism to re-route you to your ideal life. Staying pleasure-centered is a value in my therapy, which means there will be fun and hard work. I specialize in the treatment of erotically marginalized folx, queer, trans, non-monogamous, and kink. Modalities for couples and relational therapy are Emotionally Focused Therapy, Relational Life Therapy, and Gottman.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Originally hailing from the east coast, I was raised in both New York and New Jersey. I have resided in Portland for the past 5 years. I am a first-generation college student and was privileged enough to attend Lewis and Clark College where I completed my Masters in Marriage, Couples, and Family Therapy. As someone of Greek and Jewish descent, I have spent much of my free time understanding my own culture and others through travel. I find myself the most comfortable in queer and diverse spaces. I have been a social justice activist since I was 19 where you could find me hopping from one Occupy Wall St. camp to another. As a white, cisgender, hetero-passing, able-bodied woman, I have been able to traverse many spaces with ample privilege. Utilizing that privilege to continuously advocate for my community and my clients is how I remain accountable to them. When I’m not doing therapy, I’m usually spending time with my friends, dancing, hiking, and doing colorful makeup!

name: Evan Monse (THEY/ THEM & HE/ HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My practice is grounded in being compassionate, non-judgmental, and giving you the necessary tools to bring balance to your mental health. Together, we’ll focus on changing life-patterns to improve general wellness, while also addressing the underlying causes. We'll use different therapeutic approaches to focus on the relevant aspects of your life: emotions, problem-solving, relationships, environment, and your mind-body connection.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I view therapy from a person-centered, collaborative perspective. We'll tailor sessions so they address your individual needs and lived experience, and regularly check-in to make sure that therapeutic process is heading in a direction that feels right for you. I also offers a generous sliding scale for those who are trying to find affordable therapy without insurance. No one should have to hesitate on starting therapy because of cost.

name: Rachel Boll (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe in a holistic, body-mind approach to healing. My work integrates EMDR, Cognitive Processing Therapy (CPT), mindfulness, and somatic therapy to help clients process trauma, shift negative thought patterns, and reconnect with themselves. Many of the people I work with carry unresolved pain in both their minds and bodies, and I guide them in releasing stored trauma in a way that fosters long-term healing. Using somatic awareness and mindfulness techniques, I help clients develop a deeper connection to their emotions and nervous system. EMDR and CPT provide structured ways to reprocess painful experiences and reframe limiting beliefs. My approach is gentle but transformative, helping clients feel safe while exploring the deeper layers of their struggles. My goal is to empower each person to move through life transitions with resilience, clarity, and a renewed sense of self.
Social Justice Issues I Care About
I am deeply committed to mental health accessibility, trauma-informed care, and social equity. I work to create a welcoming, affirming space for all individuals, including LGBTQ2A+ communities, BIPOC individuals, and those impacted by systemic oppression. My approach to therapy acknowledges the ways that social and cultural factors shape mental health, and I strive to provide culturally responsive, identity-affirming care. I believe in the mind-body connection as a tool for empowerment and in helping clients reclaim ownership over their healing process. Whether working through intergenerational trauma, societal stressors, or personal identity struggles, I encourage clients to find strength in their story. Through my work, I advocate for self-determination, healing, and emotional resilience.

name: Zachary Cope (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a current graduate student at George Fox University and my primary approach to working with clients is Restoration Therapy. This model uses mindfulness, attachment theory and emotional regulation for a holistic approach to bettering mental health and relationships. I also believe in the importance of the mind-body connection and working with the nervous system. I like to use TRE (tension, stress, and trauma releasing exercises) for those it can benefit. I value work with people of all genders but love to help males create a new lens for viewing the world. Society does not teach the value of vulnerability and emotionality to boys. Men often put on a mask, being competitive, aggressive, rational, tough, and not viewing interpersonal relationships as a true measure of success. Working with individuals with ADHD through a strengths-based approach is also a passion of mine.
Why I Became a Therapist
I have always been inclined towards helping people. I worked at summer camps with kids suffering from life threatening illnesses and coached sports when I was younger. I am a small business owner and have found joy over the years in mentoring young entrepreneurs. I’ve worked to develop community-oriented projects and have traveled internationally for volunteer projects. My views of culture, values and beliefs were in part shaped by these experiences. Most of my background is in the business world but I always had the underlying thought inside of me about having a career helping others work through personal and interpersonal issues.

name: Ira Winograd (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Using mindfulness based practices I focus on supporting clients in addressing issues of depression, anxiety, and identity issues. In session I emphasize bringing the client’s attention out of thoughts/stories and into their present moment bodily experience with the understanding that more immediate change can occur this way. I enjoy experiencing the client and counselor relationship as a microcosm of the client’s greater relational life, and an opportunity to do deep healing. My approach is strongly influenced by Zen Buddhist Philosophy and practice, Somatic Experiencing, Rogerian Therapy, Logotherapy, Transpersonal Psychology, Psychedelic Integration, Ecopsychology and Non-violent communication. My approach is trauma informed and centers on a social justice, and multi-cultural orientation, acknowledging the complexity of identity.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am an active member of the LGBTQ community and believe in the importance of servicing this community, specifically trans/non binary folks. I am also passionate about plant medicine and am in the process of being certified to offer psychedelic therapy, though currently am only offering psychedelic integration.

name: Glenn Goldman, MA, LPC (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Helping my clients clarify their goals for therapy is an important part of the process. Collaborating together on the realization of those goals is the heart of the matter. I will listen to you with openness and compassion, and help you reflect on your life experiences and their emotional impact. We will explore how previous ways of struggling with your concerns may have fallen short, and with steady, consistent support, you will be encouraged to practice new ways that will help you move toward a richer experience of living. I look at my clients as "individuals-in-context," meaning that all of us are part of a greater environment. Pulling back the camera in this way allows us the perspective to become more conscious actors in life, rather than re-actors.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received my BA in Social Sciences from Stony Brook University in New York and my MA from the University of Montana. I have spent years studying the complex terrain of human experience from both within, and outside, the world of professional mental health services. I've developed the skills necessary to help people navigate life's real-world challenges. By starting with the assumption that our problems are our teachers, I have learned the importance of helping my clients orient their outlook toward growth and transformation. Having gone through the necessary formal training, and having directly experienced this process myself, I understand how to help others find their own paths of growth.

name: Deborah Kass MS, LCSW (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
“Why do I feel stuck in my career?” “Why is it so hard to open up with my friends and partner?” “How can I help my child feel confident?” Psychotherapy is an opportunity to reclaim your future as well as help your children and family enjoy productive, loving relationships. Together, we develop a conversation focused upon understanding what interferes with your parenting or sense of meaning in both your personal and professional life. By becoming intimate with your emotional landscape, you will feel more confident in your ability to manage relationships as well as life’s inevitable bumps and bruises. I invite you to arrange an initial phone call so that I can address your questions directly.
My Values as a Therapist
Marcel Proust writes that the "real voyage of discovery consists not in seeking new lands but seeing with new eyes." As a therapist, I work collaboratively to explore and expand your vision of yourself in love, work, and play. Together we develop a dialogue that helps you nurture deeper, more connected relationships and live a confident and creative life. My services include intensive psychoanalytic therapy, as well as psychotherapy for adolescents, adults and couples.

name: Matia Kelly, MA, LPC
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We have the potential to grow through our relationships, but often find ourselves frustrated and suffering. My approach to therapy can help you discover how your unique experiences have influenced the way you relate to yourself, others, and your experience of life. Together we can discover your existing barriers to connection with your true self and the true selves of others. I help individuals and couples increase their compassion and open to what is possible, clearing the way to find new solutions to old problems.
More About the People I Treat
I am a psychotherapist who works with individuals and couples on a wide variety of concerns.

name: Kathryn (Jasmine) Green (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
Why Choose Me? Expertise: Extensive training in evidence-based therapies combined with Spiritual integration. Compassion: A warm and empathetic approach that prioritizes your unique journey. Faith-Based Care: Holistic support for those seeking to incorporate their spiritual beliefs into the counseling process. Whether you are seeking professional guidance or support that honors your values and beliefs, I am here to walk alongside you on your journey toward healing and growth. Together, we can work to create a brighter future built on hope, resilience, and purpose.
My Approach to Helping
I specialize in Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Person-Centered Therapy, and Solution-Focused Therapy. My approach is tailored to meet each client’s unique needs while integrating Spiritual principles for those seeking faith-based support. Whether you are navigating life’s challenges or seeking personal growth, I offer a safe and nonjudgmental space to explore your concerns and discover meaningful solutions. I believe in the transformative power of empathy, active listening, and collaboration. My goal is to empower clients to overcome obstacles, find clarity, and achieve lasting change while nurturing their spiritual growth. As someone committed to creating a supportive environment, I strive to help individuals align their lives with their values and faith.

name: Paige Medina (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
With almost a decade of nursing experience and behavioral health, I possess a deep understanding of the complexities of psychiatric care. My journey to become a Nurse Practitioner was driven by a desire to do more for my patients and their families. My approach to healing is patient-centered and holistic. I believe in empowering individuals to take an active role in their mental health journey. I provide compassionate care, education, and tailored treatment plans with medication management. I strive to create a safe space for patients to explore their concerns and work towards wellness. Through compassion, collaboration, and a commitment to excellence, I strive to make a positive impact on the lives of those I serve.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in the importance of mental health and wellness. I'm passionate about empowering individuals to reach their full potential. I believe everyone has the strength within them to overcome challenges and achieve their goals with the right support and tools. In my free time, I enjoy outdoor activities such as hiking and gardening. I also prioritize self-care through mindfulness and meditation. I'm always looking to learn and grow both personally and professionally. With my expertise in thorough testing, tailored evaluations, and individualized medication guidance, I can create focused treatment plans.

name: Roxanne Ahmadpour (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I am a licensed Naturopathic doctor with a Masters degree in Integrative Mental Health and advanced training in somatic therapies. My approach is rooted in Hakomi mindfulness centered somatic therapy, while also being informed by other approaches, including Attachment theory, 'parts work', yoga therapy, and polyvagal theory. The container of our work is relational, present moment, and embodied. I support folks in building a connection with the present moment, with their bodies, and with the resources that are available in the moment. Together we slowly work to uncover hidden and disowned parts of the self, stories long held in the body, and impulses and beliefs rigidly internalized. We hold these pieces together with spaciousness and tenderness while offering an opportunity for new information and possibilities to come in. While this can be deep and impactful work, I bring a sense of lightness, humor, and ease in to sessions.
My Values as a Therapist
Healing and growth is in our nature and is our birthright. Our personal healing is integral to collective healing. The ever present forces of colonialism, capitalism, and cis-white-hetero-patriarchy have impacted all of us and have resulted in intergenerational trauma that we carry in our bodies and act out unknowingly upon ourselves and others. I value moving beyond examining the personal, and including layers of the interpersonal, the systemic, the cultural, and the historical contexts in which our behaviors and beliefs are rooted into my work with people. I deeply value our interconnectedness with nature, the environment, and world around us. I support my clients in connecting with their own nature, as well as the natural world around them, and to this end am excited to begin offering outdoor and 'walk and talk sessions'. My passion for this work is rooted in the promise that as we all heal and grow, so do our relationships, communities, society, and our planet.

name: Taryn Holm (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I chose to pursue a career in counseling to hold a space for individuals to feel safe in their mind and body. My passions within the therapy world are working through trauma, anxiety, body image, and exploring gender expressions and sexual identities. I integrate internal family systems therapy, interpersonal neurobiology, and somatic/mindfulness practices. I believe that all parts of us are good and deserve to be heard, seen, and loved.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe that reconnecting and healing the relationship with oneself is the most beautiful aspect of therapy. In my experience, therapy is a mixture of cultivating inner love, deconstructing shame, and allowing all emotions to exist without judgment. Laughter and tears are welcome too! I would love to get to know you. Reach out with any questions! :)

name: Cary (Lazara) Coll
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Through the Empowerment Model I use an eclectic, client centered, and strength based approach to finding healing and growth in the midst of challenges. By exploring the origin of symptoms and how they influence thoughts and behaviors we can overcome obstacles to feeling genuine health and wellness. While seeking therapy can be a difficult process, I am committed to working collaboratively in order to reach your goals and change undesired behavior patterns. My mission is to work with my clients to create more of the life they want for themselves.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I concentrate on solutions for present issues with insight into how past events and relationships may still be influencing current thoughts, feelings and behaviors. I offer flexibility in my fees and appointment times including weekend hours. Telephone and video counseling is also available for those who don't have qualified professionals in the area. I utilize a variety of theraputic techniques aimed at empowering the individual including EMDR for Trauma and DBT for emotional regulation.

name: Leland St. Charles (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
An old mentor once told me the purpose of therapy is to increase awareness and increase choice. I’ve often found that as a person becomes more aware of their hidden and neglected inner world, new choices and opportunities, new ways of being a human being in this world open up to them. I’m always excited and honored to play a role in that process for another person. Much of my work involves increasing awareness, and then walking through the doors that new awareness opens. In our work, we may examine questions such as... Are you living by rules that no longer serve you? What emotions are being avoided, and what is the cost of this avoidance? What choices are you making, without realizing that you are choosing? I can help you to face these and other questions, make peace with uncomfortable emotions, revise old beliefs and rules, set healthy boundaries, learn new ways of relating to yourself and others, and move towards a life that feels engaging and meaningful.

name: Taunya Gesner (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I worked in the corporate world for many years in a wide variety of positions that give me a lot of insight into the challenges of being in that environment. Additionally - I have adventured and explored the world living and visiting, through military service and sailing the ocean. I shifted careers into counseling and finally feel like I found the right career for me, because I love connecting with people and understanding your stories. I am very respectful of your needs (you are the expert on your life), while also pointing out perspectives and patterns you may not have previously noticed. My life has been an adventure and I've had to overcome many hardships. I have a wide variety of experiences, (including physical and mental health challenges) that often give me unique insight and understanding. I'm also very much a nerd at heart - being into gaming, story crafting, art, cooking, homesteading and family.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I'm good at asking questions, looking for and noticing patterns that you might not have seen. I'm a very good listener, giving you the space to say what you need. I'm very patient and understanding and compassionate. But the truth is - these skills are probably true of most counselors - and I often find myself wondering what makes me different from anyone else? I dunno - I'm a strange combination of nerdy awkard, intelligent, warm, compassionate, helpful, intellectual - always striving to improve my own life and help you find ways to improve yours. I've studied a variety of techniques that I know can be helpful in the right moment. How much time you want to spend utilizing those skills versus talk therapy, is up to you, as I also believe in client led sessions. Also - I'm knowledgable in a wide variety of cultures because of my extensive travels, studies and work with exchange students. If all this sounds appealing/interesting, we are probably a good fit!

name: Laney Kibel
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I bring a broad array of treatment tools to my work including gestalt, object relations and psychodynamic theory.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have 30+ years of experience as a therapist, mentor and clinical supervisor.

name: Michael Junkins (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background
I have a background in working with children and family from a wide diversity of life circumstances and needs including families experiencing houselessness, and families with children requiring both inpatient and outpatient mental health care.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that change and healing can be found through the bonds we share in our closest relationships. As a therapist, my role is to work with you to find new ways of being with your loved ones as well as new insights and possibilities for yourself. Whatever challenges you are facing, know that you are not alone and that things can get better. All of us inherently have the capacity to grow in order to achieve our goals. I approach therapy from an attachment-based, humanistic, and emotion-focused perspective, and I hope we can work together to bring forth the internal wisdom you already possess. My work with children and families has taught me the value of patience, humor, and creativity when working with others, and I seek to create a safe environment where you can feel free to be your authentic self. I am passionate about working with all configurations of families trying to navigate the complexities of life’s changes.

name: Ben Mosebach
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is straightforward and down-to-earth. If theoretical orientation is interesting to you I am rooted in the humanistic perspectives encompassing aspects of existentialism, gestalt and person centered therapy. But I'm not interested in wasting your time with superficial chatter or jargon-filled language. I'm here to roll up my sleeves and get to work with you, tackling whatever challenges you're facing head-on.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
What sets me apart as a therapist is my empathy and compassion complimented with a no-nonsense approach and a genuine desire to see you succeed. I understand that reaching out for help can be tough, and men especially are conditioned to think they need to go it alone. However, embarking on that initial step might just turn out to be the most transformative decision you ever make. During our sessions, you can anticipate genuine and open dialogue. I'll provide constructive challenges when necessary, while also offering unwavering support throughout your journey. Together, we'll tackle any barriers hindering your progress and strive to create a life that inspires you.

name: Nicole Kennelly (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I view our human experience through an existential lens where you are encouraged to engage with your own "becoming" as you create a life worth living. I welcome all ways that bring awareness and change, including restoration therapy and solution-focused therapy. I take an individualized approach to each client, offering techniques of self-compassion, mindfulness, art exercises, spiritual integration, and somatic regulation. With curiosity and compassion, I am eager to get to know you and create a space where you can find hope for this moment.
Why I Became a Therapist
I believe in the effectiveness of therapy. In therapy, I have felt less alone. I have gained new insights and perspectives. I have found compassion for myself and others. I have explored challenges and learned to manage anxiety. I have dug myself out of the hole of depression and apathy. I have gained clarity and regained a sense of self. I have experienced the gift of being heard, seen, and invited to share more of my yucky thoughts - which is a profound and sacred experience. I am passionate about the therapeutic relationship, and I love being with people in the fullness of their lived experiences.

name: Amal Ortiz (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Clients call me unflinchingly honest, and deeply compassionate. Therapy isn’t easy. But with the right therapist and approach, I believe healing is possible. Specifically I use somatic inquiry, parts work, psychodynamics, and liberation psychology as my theoretical frameworks and modalities. We’ll dig into intergenerational healing and inner-child work, unearthing the roots of trauma and pain and forging pathways to self-discovery and empowerment.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I’m the right therapist for you if you earn for an honest and compassion approach that can aid in deep, transformative work. Most of us are wounded in relationship to others, and I believe we can also heal in relationship with the right therapist. My style is straightforward, no-nonsense, yet deeply compassionate. If you want a therapist who’s fully engaged and committed to your journey, I’m here to help you unlock the life you’ve been striving for.

name: Kasha Woods (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I combine somatic (body-focused), mindfulness-based, and depth (psychodynamic) approaches to explore messages that arise through body symptoms, beliefs, patterns, and/or dreams. I work experientially, which means we include your present moment, felt-sense experience to discover direct insight and avenues for change. I have found that these approaches lead to compassionate self-understanding and positive, lasting change. My somatic therapy training includes 7+ years of study in the Hakomi method of mindfulness-based somatic psychotherapy training, including focus in parts work, neurobiology, attachment, character strategies, and trauma-informed practices. My psychodynamic training includes an M.A. in counseling psychology with emphasis in Jungian depth psychology from Pacifica Graduate Institute.

name: Pacific Psychology & Comprehensive Health Pacific University
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Our therapists operate from an evidence-based treatment philosophy. That is, we have a professional and ethical responsibility to provide our clients with the best possible treatment to match their needs. We are able to work with patients interested in CBT, DBT, Mindfulness - based, Psychodynamic, PCIT and a variety of other therapy orientations.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Our providers include experienced licensed clinicians as well as advanced level graduate students working under the supervision of licensed clinical psychologists. We are able to bill most OHP plans in the Portland metro area (Care Oregon, Health Share Oregon, Columbia Pacific, Trillium Health) for mental health evaluation and therapy services.

name: Sarah Maddox (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is eclectic, relational & person-centered. Your values guide our work together. I utilize creativity, training, empathetic listening skills, supported by research to support your healing & growth. I have experience with diverse groups of women in different stages of life. I have a passion for helping women know themselves, their desires & achieve their goals. While I practice with a feminist lens, I’m open to all gender & sexual identities & find this lens offers space for a variety of experiences. Adolescence is a unique period of development that deserves respect & care. I offer a space where teens can process through the big world they’re taking on & utilize talk therapy & other creative means like art, music, & bibliotherapy. I use Attachment Theory with every client, especially couples. Whether it’s sex, communication, finances, parenting, or who’s loading the dishwasher wrong, I offer tools for couples to navigate the issues they’re facing.
My Approach to Helping
My life has afforded me many opportunities to learn about transitions in various relationships, cross-country moves, and parenthood. Whatever transitions you may find yourself in, I am here to support you. I utilize Restoration Therapy as a framework for identifying pain and peace cycles that can be used to empower teens, adults, and couples of all kinds to live in congruence with their values.

name: Lily Packer (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
As a mental health counselor specializing in neurodevelopmental differences, I provide targeted support across age groups. For children, I create playful environments using play therapy to develop self-regulation skills and process emotions safely. With teenagers, I balance respecting autonomy while providing structure to address teen anxiety, depression, and identity exploration. My approach helps teens build emotional resilience while navigating complex social challenges. For young adults, I offer guidance through transitions, supporting them with executive functioning challenges and creating sustainable coping strategies. Families are essential partners in this work. I provide parent coaching to enhance understanding of your child's needs and develop effective behavior management approaches. Through family therapy, we strengthen communication and create environments where everyone thrives.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
Choosing the right therapist is deeply personal. My clients often tell me they feel truly seen in our work together—something particularly valuable for neurodivergent youth who may have experienced misunderstanding elsewhere. With specialized training in childhood anxiety, trauma recovery, and neurodevelopmental differences, I bring both expertise and heartfelt compassion to our sessions. Parents appreciate my practical guidance for managing challenging behaviors, while children and teens respond to my authentic, gentle approach to building emotional regulation skills. My background as a behavioral specialist and crisis intervention counselor has shaped my ability to remain calm and present, even during difficult moments. The therapeutic relationship we build becomes the foundation for growth. Through CBT techniques, mindfulness practices, and strength-based approaches, we'll work together to transform struggles into stepping stones for development.

name: Bea Hurston (THEM/THEM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
As a queer kid who grew up in a fundamentalist community in Mississippi, my journey has been long and hard. But it's also given me the ability to connect deeply with others, both in their pain and in their longing for healing, pleasure, and joy. Using a blend of parts work, self-compassion, and the collective knowledge I’ve gained from the many healers who have helped me, I will help you learn to see yourself more clearly and to navigate your life more peacefully. It's my honor to help my clients find their way to the life of freedom, joy, and intimacy that we all crave.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe we all have the capacity to not only weather the storms our lives bring us, but also to create deeper meaning and connection in our lives because of our unique storms. I see the work I do with my clients as being intimately connected to social and global justice because I believe that in healing our relationships with ourselves, we begin to heal the world.

name: Ryan Kopyar
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I was so afraid to start my own counseling journey. I delayed for so long. So, I get it. It's a personal decision on when to start. I honor that. All i can say is that starting counseling is one of the best decisions that I have ever made. P.S. counseling can have some fun and laughter with it, and I like to focus on that too! I help individuals struggling with anxiety I feel overwhelmed and can't stay present or focused on the things that's most important to them. I also help individuals to break free from their trauma responses which impact their ability to receive and experience love. Ryan is currently practicing under the supervision of Doug Chapman, LMHC (LH0006800)
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a human being just like you. I don't claim to have life completely figured out, and if you find a counselor that does, please let me know so that I can make them my counselor! What I can say is that I've hit rock bottom, more than once, and for my own counseling and my own healing journey I learned how to pick myself back up and create a life that at that time I never could have imagined was possible for me. I have sense committed the rest of my life, to helping other people do the same. Counseling isn't easy, but it also doesn't have to be ridiculously overwhelming, dry, and constantly painful. Healing can involve laughter just as much as it involves tears, and I embrace both in the counseling sessions.

name: Rachel Baker (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
When working together, I view problems from a structuralism framework. In other words, I believe we are deeply impacted by the society we participate in and by our relationships. Therapy is important, and finding the right fit as a client is even more important. I approach therapy from a highly relational, process-oriented framework.. My first and foremost goal , however, is to prioritize and cultivate a strong and authentic therapeutic alliance between myself and you. It is crucial to me that I approach my clients from a forever curious lens. I believe that, no matter how many hours we log together, I will always strive to better understand you than I did our previous session. It is essential to me that I fully believe your story and experience, while holding it with the utmost gentleness and respect.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe that we are deeply impacted by our relationships. In our individualist culture, it is so easy for us to blame ourselves for where we are today and, to forget, that we are companion-oriented species. This ethos is at the center of my work with clients. Studies show that the number one variable responsible for positive change within a client is the therapeutic relationship. This makes so much sense to me because I believe strong therapy has to include authenticity coming from myself. Clients connect with me because they are drawn to my presence and balanced nature. When we are in session, you will feel that I am with you through the laughs, tears, unanswered questions, and discomfort.

name: Joby Mahto (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I specialize in working from a relational foundation informed by attachment theory while incorporating Internal Family Systems, Mindfulness, Narrative Therapy, and Polyvagal theory. I believe my clients would describe me as animated, engaged, direct, curious, humorous, and down-to-earth in session. I value collaboration and anti-oppressive practices in my approach to therapy.
My Values as a Therapist
Prior to getting my graduate degree in Social Work, I worked in a variety of social service settings including in Juvenile Justice, a Women’s Shelter, and an Arts focused nonprofit working with youth in foster care and those experiencing houselessness in New York City and in Los Angeles, CA. I then moved to Oregon and completed my MSW degree at Portland State University in 2013 and have since worked in many different levels of care- from inpatient hospitalization, day treatment, integrated behavioral health, and now- outpatient therapy.

name: Baylee Bullock (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to helping is rooted in curiosity, collaboration, and non-judgment. I aim to create a safe, supportive space where you can feel seen and valued while navigating your unique journey. Combining art therapy with talk therapy, I integrate creative problem-solving, mind-body connection, trauma-informed care, and humanistic therapies to meet your specific needs. Whether through creativity or meaningful conversation, our work will focus on self-expression, insight, and empowerment, allowing us to explore challenges, rediscover joy, and foster personal growth together.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a therapist, I bring a warm, compassionate presence and a wholehearted dedication to your growth and well-being. I’m passionate about working with people of all ages to help them untangle life’s challenges, build a stronger relationship with themselves, and find moments of light and humor along the way. My training in both art and talk therapy allows me to offer creative and adaptable solutions for a wide range of needs. Whether you’re looking to heal, gain clarity, or simply find a space to pause and reflect, I’m here to support you in discovering your unique strengths and moving toward a more meaningful life.

name: Lindsay Walker, LMFT (SHE/HER)
approaches:
Alert: due to the COVID-19 pandemic I am only offering remote video therapy at this time. I believe strongly that my clients are the experts of their lives and I am here to help guide them to find the answers to their questions. Every person I see has a story which they carry with them and which guides their sexual selves. I help people become aware of their sexual selves so they can begin to heal and grow.
I have my Master's Degree in Couple and Family Therapy from Thomas Jefferson University with a specialization in Sex Therapy. I have centered my training around working with relationships and sexual and gender orientations. I am also a certified Prepare-Enrich facilitator which I use as an assessment tool for couples who would like to iron out some kinks in their relationship. I am a member of the Oregon Counseling Association (ORCA).

name: Caitlin Beckwith-Ferguson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Everyone deserves a space to express themselves authentically and to be accepted for who they truly are. I seek to see my clients fully, guiding them in a process of exploration and reflection, and leading them to deeper trust and understanding of themselves. I believe that healing and growth occur through experiencing safety and trust in the therapeutic relationship, and through this experience, my clients develop new ways of interacting with themselves and others. I bring a grounded presence, compassion, curiosity, and gentle guidance to the therapy room.  In our work together, you will become more able to express your emotions, needs, boundaries and desires. I will support you in challenging narratives or beliefs about yourself that no longer fit. Together, we will explore your past, present and future, examining how emotions and attachment experiences impact your interactions. I will help you find comfort, connection, and belonging both inside and outside of the therapy room.
Why I Became a Therapist
It is an honor to support my clients through moments of vulnerability, to affirm their identities, and to help them improve their relationships. From my own experience, I know that understanding your identity is a lifelong journey. I strive to help my clients create connection, safety and belonging as well as a sense of adventure and exploration in their relationships and in their lives. I work from a trauma-informed, feminist, systemic lens, using emotionally-focused therapy, mindfulness, and narrative therapy. I hold a Master of Arts degree in Marriage, Couples and Family Therapy from Lewis & Clark Clark College and am a Certified Clinical Trauma Professional.

name: Sam Wilson
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is always client centered and solution-focused. I employ narrative, cognitive, behavioral, and strengths-based approaches to helping you identify paths to achieving emotional wellness and satisfying relationships. I strive to create a space that is collaborative and non-judgmental for every client. I favor self-reflective interventions because I believe that most of the positive change that occurs through counseling comes from self-learning. I am happy to meet with you for a free consultation to discuss your goals and determine if I am a good fit for your needs.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I welcome adults, teenagers, and couples to my practice. My work with individuals often focuses on the following issues: -ADHD -Anxiety -Workplace stress -Addiction and Dual Diagnosis -Gaming, electronic, and other behavioral addictions -LGBTA adjustment issues -Bipolar Disorder -Depression (including Treatment Resistant Depression) -Coping with a loved one's mental illness I have worked in a variety of settings including homeless shelters, a foster care facility, a domestic violence treatment center, two outpatient mental health clinics, and two career centers.

name: Kelle DeBruin (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Our practice started with just one clinician in 2018. Fast forward through a pandemic and a couple extra offices here and there - we have a group practice of diverse and talented clinicians dedicated to providing excellent care to our clients. With the original member (Kelle) as practice founder, our love for what we do shows up every day in how we work together and treat each client as the individual that they are. We have grown in numbers and in geography. We hope to have clinicians who are available to serve all parts of the great state of Oregon from bustling P-Town to more quiet out of reach areas in eastern, central, and southern Oregon. Currently we have clinicians who dedicate their services to teletherapy solely, and we have in person opportunities in the Portland metro area.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
With over a dozen clinicians in this practice we have an array of specialties, expertise, life experiences, and individual differences that make us able to fit most of the needs a client may have. Kelle DeBruin Counseling is intentional in our approach of honoring diversity and recognizing the importance of providing inclusive representation in the clinicians that serve our clients.

name: Léah Ferreira (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is holistic, eclectic, and client-centered. I work with clients of all ages, including couples, to bridge new relationships with their pasts so that their futures can be fulfilling and hopeful. Again, thank you for being here. I believe in you and know that you can do hard things!
Why I am a Good Fit for You
As a Queer therapist, my practice is a safe place for all people. I work with adults of all ages and genders. No matter how you identify, you are welcome.

name: Riisa Humphrey (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I provide the tools necessary to cope with and heal from the everyday effects of trauma. I have worked with individuals who have experienced extensive trauma history for over 10 years. My approach is person-centered and trauma-informed using empathy and active listening skills to support clients with their mental health needs. I am trained in Brainspotting, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), and Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT).
My Values as a Therapist
At the core of my practice, confidentiality, empathy, respect, cultural sensitivity and evidence-based practice are values I constantly strive for.

name: Sarah Howeth (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
When I work with couples and relationships, I’m interested in helping support you in navigating tough conversations, building a solid foundation together, and getting your relational needs met. I am passionate about working with couples and relationships around: getting free from the cycle of conflict, communicating more skillfully together, mending and tending to trust issues, finding more sexual satisfaction, healing old hurts, and sinking into more relational security together. When I work with individuals, I'm interested in helping you sink into being fully you while being in relationship with others. That might mean learning more about who you are, discovering your needs in relationship, getting support with boundaries and saying "no," processing your childhood and past, or growing your ability to being more vulnerable with others. I want to support you in being your full, authentic self.
My Approach to Helping
Couples and intimate partners all have patterns in the way that they relate based on their early attachment histories. What this means is that when we get into relationship, we might find ourselves having the same argument about the dishes that doesn't actually feel like it is about the dishes. What I'm interested in helping you do is get to the heart of the matter and communicate about it together. I am Certified in Emotionally Focused Therapy for Couples. In addition to my training in Emotionally Focused Therapy, Nonviolent Communication, Hakomi Therapy and Sensorimotor Therapy, I bring an anti-oppressive lens to my work with clients. I am a white, cis, able-bodied, middle-class woman living and working in Portland, Oregon (occupied land of the Clackamas and Cowlitz).

name: "Paisley" Jen Burrell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I utilize an experiential and holistic approach to Emotion-Focused Couples Therapy (EFT-C) to help couples navigate the diverse challenges of their relationships. Whether it's resolving miscommunications, managing conflicts constructively, or healing from past hurts like infidelity, I offer experienced guidance. My style is relational, direct, supportive, engaging, and kind. My therapeutic approach is deeply informed by my training in EFT, Internal Family Systems Therapy, Existential-Humanism, Family Systems Theories, Mindfulness and Experiential Interventions, Solution-Focused Therapy, and the Gottman Method.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe relationships are among the most rewarding aspects of life, yet they can also be some of the most painful. Our intimate partners often highlight areas where we can grow. Couples therapy provides a unique opportunity for partners to come together as a team to address their most challenging issues. In my practice, I offer a collaborative and compassionate space where we can work together to heal the wounds that affect your relationship.

name: Haku Mease (THEY/HE)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Haku’s therapeutic approach is rooted in a relational worldview that centers storytelling as a means to reclaim personal power and honor people’s unique experiences. He does this through narrative therapy, internal family systems, somatic inquiry, and solutions-focused therapy. It can be hard to find the words and language to understand and explain our experiences which is why they enjoy exploring world-building through sand tray, play, and art therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Haku believes in the radical possibilities of change and transformation that exist when we have the safety, trust, and established therapeutic relationship to build towards the new experiences we want and need for ourselves. He views therapy as a practice of collaboration, in trusting people as the experts of their own lives, and of building towards the self-identified skills and experiences that folks would like to explore together, on their own, and in their communities.

name: Alphonso Nunez (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is one that is client-centered and collaborative. While I am versed in many modalities of MFT treatments, I primarily work from a background that leans heavily into Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT), as well as Humanistic-Existential Therapy techniques. My perspective is that humans are inherently relational beings and those relationships are ultimately maintained or dissolved by underlying emotional inner-workings. In our time together, we will work to name those emotions and identify what those emotions are attempting to direct us towards. As your therapist, it's my priority to co-create a space with my clients that is affirming, inclusive, safe and supportive.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a strong background in working with issues around intimacy and sexuality both with individuals and couples. I also work with members of oppressed populations to navigate a number of issues they may experience as a result of systemic marginalization. This work is always done with an emphasis on social justice and empowerment in a collaborative manner. I believe that therapy is a delicate contract between myself and my clients and one that takes time and trust to fully realize the potential and power of change. At its fullest potential, therapy can be a celebration of the ways in which we are all different while appreciating how we're all connected.

name: Mahina Therapy PLLC (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Mahina Therapy PLLC has a mission to provide outpatient mental health services, ADHD + Autism Evaluations, and Gender-Affirming Care to marginalized communities who have been systematically under-served within the mental health field. The clinicians at Mahina Therapy are trained to help you wherever you - and your relationships - are needing additional support in life. Our mission is to provide outpatient mental health services, ADHD + Autism Evaluations, and Gender-Affirming Care to marginalized communities who have be systematically under-served within the mental health field. Clients can expect conversations around macro-systems and to be challenged when they show up in the room with any clinician with us.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Mahina Therapy PLLC believes that therapy is inherently political, thus our stance within the mental health community and with our clients reflects that. We stand with the LGBTQQIP2SA community, Palestinian and Congolese people experiencing systemic genocide, and the Black Lives Matter Movement. We dedicate ourselves to continuous education to be anti-racist, trans-affirming, the Fat liberation (HAES) movement, and kink/sex affirming. Mahina Therapy is QTBIPOC-owned, thus our politics reflect this dedication to systemically marginalized communities. Mahina Therapy PLLC holds a firm stance against racism, xenophobia, queer phobia, transphobia, misogyny, ableism, fatphobia, classism, and any systems of oppression which discriminate against folx with marginalized identities. Clients can expect conversations around these systems and to be challenged when they show up in the room with any clinician or student intern employed at Mahina Therapy.

name: Esther Gabrielle Rosengarten (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I strive to understand the neurological and psychological connections that make up every individual's unique perspective and work closely with them to find what medications will suit them best and are least likely to cause them side effects, adverse reactions and withdrawal upon eventual discontinuation. My aim is to minimize patients' need for medications; I routinely help patients safely come off medications that are no longer needed, wanted or sufficiently helpful. Thanks to neuroplasticity, the brain’s ability to rewire itself, it’s natural that its need for medication may change during the course of proper treatment, which is why re-evaluating this need regularly is a top priority.I incorporate brief psychotherapy when needed in the care provided to catalyze healing as medication is most often much less helpful when not combined with psychological counseling, however I tend to work with individuals who are either already working with or looking for an individual psychotherapist.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
MIND HUB, which stands for Medical Interventions for Neuroplastic Development: Holistically Understanding the Brain, and is a private Mental Health and Psychiatry Healthcare practice that PRIMARILY OFFERS TELEHEALTH APPOINTMENTS and occasional in-person appointments as needed. We treat a variety of mood, anxiety and psychotic disorders & provide comprehensive diagnostic, medication management, brief psychotherapeutic and educational services to individuals committed to bettering their emotional wellness and/or cognitive functioning in a sustainable manner. To request services, click on the "Schedule Now" link above or visit our webpage at: https://www.shrink-this.com/new-clients

name: Lillian Dunham (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work with individuals, couples, and families from the belief that humans are all inherently good and we are all capable of positive change. The joys and struggles of our lives are relational. I came to this work through personal experience as a partner, mother, co-parent, daughter, creator, and employee. My philosophy is grounded in discovering and understanding our context in the systems we participate in, from our most intimate relationships to our locations in greater society. Illuminating unspoken cultural narratives and finding words for the oppressed, forgotten, and shamed can heal ourselves and society. I use empathy, curiosity, and humility and humor in dialogue to help clients imagine and practice authentic and more fulfilling ways of relating to themselves and others. I am trained in Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT), which draws from attachment theory. I also use techniques from Internal Family Systems (IFS) to help individuals communicate within themselves.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in supporting our communities with our special strengths. I volunteer at the Dougy Center for Grieving Children and Families. I believe meaningful intimate relationships can take many different forms. I have been active in the polyamory/ethical-non-monogamy and kink communities.

name: Erin Fettes (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
As a psychologist specializing in reproductive mental health, with my own experiences of infertility, ART and pregnancy loss, I understand the unique difficulties of this journey. A path marked by cycles of hope and heartache, as the anticipation of new possibilities collides with the anguish of unfulfilled dreams. My mission is to provide a compassionate environment for you to explore feelings, insights, hurts, choices and find resilience, support and care. My areas of focus: 1) Support with Trying to Conceive & Infertility - Psychotherapeutic support for the stressors of trying to conceive and navigating infertility. 2) Assisted Reproductive Technology - Care related to decision making for family building options, emotional support during fertility treatments, and the secondary mental health/relationship stressors that can arise. 3) Pregnancy Loss, Reproductive Trauma & EMDR - Support for addressing failed treatment cycles, pregnancy loss, reproductive trauma, grief and loss.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I am a clinical psychologist practicing in Oregon and Colorado. I work with any individual in their experience of the trying to conceive (TTC) process, whether you are the one to carry the pregnancy or a non-gestational partner. The specific focus of my practice is rooted in support for hardships of TTC. I specialize in the needs of this special phase of family building. In addition to emotional support for the challenges of your TTC journey, I also focus on how these difficulties intersect with your treatment of yourself, relationships, prior/concurrent mental health needs, how early-life and formative experiences are impacting how you particularly are navigating these challenges. The present focus on my therapeutic work has personal roots. My therapeutic work has personal roots, with my own path of TTC experiencing many challenges - including queer family building as a fat person, using donor gametes, many rounds of IUI, IVF, failed embryo transfers, and pregnancy loss.

name: Erin Howe (SHE/HER AND THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that therapy can reveal important insights into our adaptations to trauma and attachment patterns and can guide us to more open-hearted connection and empowerment in our relationships and lives. As a therapist, I utilize a body-centered lens that guides clients through mindful exploration of their experience to access the ways we hold thoughts, beliefs, and patterns. Through this work, we are able to move towards embodiment and choice to create lasting change. In my work as a therapist, I primarily utilize Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT) and attachment models and practice through mindful-experiential and somatic-based approaches. I also believe in the power of clarifying needs and incorporate Non-Violent Communication (NVC) into my work. I am proficient but not fluent in Spanish, and welcome clients who would like to work with me at that level or to explore working in both English and Spanish.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My path toward relational and sex therapy began as a teenager teaching peer sex education in high school classrooms. I believe that access to empowerment in our bodies and in our sexuality has powerful and liberatory potential. Social justice has always been important to me, and in my work as a therapist I continue to humbly commit to work towards anti-racist healing. I also am a student of how our contexts and identities shape our embodied experiences, from experiences of oppression to our greatest strengths. It is important for me to locate myself as a white, cisgendered queer person currently living without a disability and from a middle class financial background.

name: Nancy Pearson, LCSW
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Through active listening, individualized problem solving, and experiential activities, your family will receive the best support for stabilization in transition (school, divorce, relocation, new family member, health crisis), emotional self-regulation for all family members, and skill building to reach maximum potential. I work individually with children and with parents and then work together with the family at large. We build action plans based on your wishes for the outcomes you desire. All families are welcome, regardless of size, composition, race, orientation, or belief systems. My goal is for parents to fall in love again with their children and for children to live joyously and harmoniously within their families. I also offer family mediation for custody and parenting time issues. I am collaborative and focused and can help you find solutions that you are not able to reach on your own. Having done more than 1000 meetings of these kinds, I can offer your experienced support.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have been in practice for more than 30 years, working in client homes, offices, clinics, and schools. I sometimes practice with my Labradoodle, Sophie, who lends lightheartedness and joy to the work. I practice from my home office space which is warm and inviting, not coldly clinical. I work for you and with you, not on you. That means that I honor your goals, priorities, and values and work together to achieve your desired ends. Working with Nancy Pearson, "Families Thrive!"

name: Elyse Conterno (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
When it comes to engaging in therapy, it is important to know what type of treatment your therapist uses and determine if it is the best fit for you. I utilize concepts and approaches of Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) and Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT). These behavioral therapies have robust research designating them as “evidence-based treatments,” for anxiety and depressive disorders. Using these theoretical frameworks and related interventions, like mindfulness and Exposure Response Prevention (ERP), I help my clients learn new skills to more effectively overcome mental health challenges and situational stressors. My goal is to support my clients as they re-shape their thinking and behaviors so that they can more consistently engage with life in ways that reflect their values. Therapy will include evaluating problematic avoidances, making plans to challenge unhelpful thinking, and practice new behavioral patterns, and exploring personal values.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As I have settled into my clinical and professional identity, I continue to strive to provide compassionate, culturally responsive evidence-based behavioral therapy. I seek out up-to-date research and training in my commitment to the ethics and principles as a licensed clinical psychologist. This includes additional training and supervision to uphold values of social justice in my personal and professional life. In treatment, I use a trauma-informed approach to consider how systems of inequity and oppression impact those in marginalized groups including BIPOC and LGBTQ+ persons. I seek to create a space where personal and cultural identities are thoughtfully and deliberately brought into the room as an essential consideration in treatment.

name: Carolyn Peterson (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I’m passionate about helping thoughtful, driven adults who feel overwhelmed and insecure find comfort in who they are and build deeper connections with the people and things that matter. I recognize the weight of unrealistic expectations, both self-imposed and external, that leave you perpetually unfulfilled. Whether stemming from past trauma, intricate learning histories, or the pressures of daily life, these challenges may make you feel like you're merely existing rather than truly living. My approach is grounded in scientific principles, tailored to help you break free from this cycle. Together, we navigate the complexities of your background with compassion and understanding, uncovering the roots of your feelings to cultivate a deeper sense of fulfillment and authenticity in your life. If you're ready to move beyond survival mode and embrace a life that aligns with your true self, I'm here to support you every step of the way.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am multiracial, bilingual in Thai and English, and the proud daughter of a Veteran. My experience of growing up abroad and coming to the United States as an adult allows me to bring a unique perspective to therapy. I am deeply committed to exploring the intersectionality of identities. I strive to create a space that honors and embraces the complexity of our individual background, identities, and experiences. I understand societal context plays a significant role in shaping our perspectives and challenges we face. Throughout my training and professional journey, I've had the privilege of supporting individuals from diverse backgrounds in a range of settings, including university counseling centers, child and family centers, community mental health organizations, and Veteran Affairs Hospitals. You will frequently catch me experimenting with new recipes, watching football, hitting the trails with my furry friend, and giving rock climbing my best shot.

name: Kristin Tand (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
First of all I SEE YOU. I totally get the stressed-out grind of the day-to-day. The good news is there is a path forward to settle your mind, nervous system and start being more grounded inside of the stress. Sessions with me uncover what's overwhelming you, increase your skills to manage stress and heal from old burdens that keep you stuck. Together, we'll create balance in your life. And we'll always go at your pace, never pushing to a place where you don’t feel ready.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe everyone has unique strengths that when realized, can create healing and lasting change from the inside out. Life is hard and our experiences over time can create burdens that don't always serve us. Through guidance, we all have the capacity to let go of these challenges and feel good about ourselves. I'm a therapist who walks my talk, having done (and continue to do) my own therapy to overcome past challenges and feel less anxious in my day to day life. I also believe dogs are magical creatures that were put on Earth to bring greater joy and happiness, but I am also accepting of cats and will never turn down a good cat video. I believe that all people deserve equal rights and that love is love. I am welcoming and accepting of all and make it a point to better understand my privilege and use my unearned power for good. I also engage in ongoing learning to strive toward dismantling the layers of racial injustice.

name: Angie Tanyi (SHE/HER/HERS/ELLA)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
By bringing warmth and reflection, together we re-wire old thoughts and behaviors which aren't serving you and help you find new pathways to living an authentic, self compassionate life. We slow down and look at ways things such as anxiety/depression may have once been protective, but now often sabotage you. We change the question from "What's wrong with me?" to "What happened to me?" going into your world with curiosity and compassion. We slow down and make sense of what is getting in your way and keeps you feeling stuck. Through an attachment lens we'll take care of your adaptive child so that your wise adult can lead.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
The people I work with are amazing! I believe all of my clients bring with them all the wisdom they need to live a rich life. You don't need saving nor fixing, rather someone to accompany you as you vulnerably tell your story & have someone "get it”. I am here to help you sift through the feelings that were dismissed either by you or others (or both) & help you make sense of how they impact you today. We’ll also listen to the parts of you that are longing to thrive. For couples, I work with an Emotionally Focused Therapy lens which looks at our relationship cycles & the signs of safety or unsafely which may arise in relationship. Our history informs us & can show up in our current relationships in ways that tell us to protect or protest, to get louder or to shut down. Then negative cycles may occur. We'll look at these young, adaptive parts that need soothing, allowing our wiser parts to eventually take the lead, helping you to have healthier and more connected relationships.

name: Ladan Radafshar (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In childhood, we are socialized within our families and learn ways of relating to ourselves and others. These patterns are often tailored to feeling safe and secure in our familial environment. As we move into adulthood and our circumstances change, we may begin to recognize that the coping strategies we once relied on no longer serve us in our adult relationships. Our work together will bring awareness to your strengths and what childhood experiences and communication styles we may need to reprocess or relearn. Through the lens of Attachment, Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT), and Eye Movement, Desensitization, and Reprocessing (EMDR) we will guide you toward feeling more secure and fulfilled in your relationship with self and others.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
The resilience of the human spirit inspires me, and it is a privilege to witness the transformative power of human connection. I believe that just as we can be wounded in relationships, we can also heal in them. My training in modern psychodynamic therapy holds the relationship between client and therapist as a space of non-judgment, compassion, and an opportunity to process and make sense of the challenges you face. I have a master’s degree in clinical mental health counseling from Northwestern University and completed an externship and core skills training in EFT, as well as advanced training in EMDR, and Mindful Self-Compassion.

name: colby schneider (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer guidance and unconditional positive regard to help people understand themselves using Psychodynamic, Gestalt, Humanistic, and Trauma Focused Cognitive Behavioral Therapies. Working together collaboratively but acknowledging that people are the experts of their own life, we will work together to end suffering while building self-growth. The last few years have caused us suffering and loss on a scale we have never experienced before. I am offering help to process these losses and create the tools needed to thrive and live life to its fullest once again. I'd truly appreciate the opportunity to work with you on your journey of healing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe everyone has the right to be happy and feel good about themselves regardless of what they have experienced in their own life. I feel that therapy should be something everyone experiences and uses at some point in their life to get that extra support. My hourly rates are typically $155-$185 for individuals and couples on a sliding scale and am happy to work around your budget and what is affordable and fair for you. I am an advocate for human and animal rights and have been a vegan for over 15 years. I feel that compassion for all living beings is essential and I use that as a guide in my professional and personal life.

name: Sarah Jurrens (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
My goal is to help any women struggling with confidence issues to get in touch with their authentic selves.
