If you have issues from your past (ie. trauma, abuse, neglect, lack of support system, etc) causing low confidence, it can contribute to symptoms such as depression and anxiety later in life, creating a cycle that feeds insecurity and can cause more disconnect from your inner-self. Let me help you reconnect. I also have extensive training in ADHD. I love to help people understand how ADHD may have contributed unknowingly to trauma growing up and how it has potentially shaped who they are today. Recognizing these impacts can help one heal from a lifetime of shame and self-confidence issues beyond their control and start to recognize how they can harness their strengths while letting go of trying to control things they cannot. I have seen great change come from this work, and people come out of great depression because of it.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that everyone is capable of change. There is no single right or wrong in my world. Everyone gets to decide what is best for them. I think every individual is unique, and I want to honor the therapy process that works for them. I am open to creativity in growth, and I believe it is important to pull from an individual's culture, interests, hobbies, even fears in the growth process as they get in touch with themselves. I believe that mindfulness, meditation, yoga, breathing exercises are beneficial in this journey of reconnection, self-exploration, and learning to love one's self. I also tend to think out of the box when it comes to applying evidence-based practices in therapy. However, I am also aware that sometimes all one needs is an ear to listen to them, and we can keep our sessions simple, too, if that is preferred. No problem at all. I will meet you where you are and collaborate with you. I really love my job and look forward to working with you!

name: Firefly Institute
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We offer a variety of services including treatment options that are highly specialized such as play therapy, animal assisted therapy, therapeutic art, yoga and EMDR. These services are provided by skilled clinicians who have certification in the treatment program they are offering. This is what sets us apart from other clinics or practitioners. Training for specialized care is rigorous and requires significant commitment to education post-graduation. Firefly clinicians are certified to do the work, receive ongoing supervision and consultation and are accredited by training programs or institutions. Areas of specialty include: relationships, grief/loss, chronic illness, abuse/trauma, PTSD, mood imbalances, child therapy, attachment issues, behavioral issues, anxiety, adoption/foster care, divorce and difficult life transitions or change.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
The Firefly Institute clinicians are multi-disciplinary and collaborative. Clinicians are skilled at supporting individuals and family systems through life transitions, anxiety, depression and chronic illness. They carry expertise and credentials in trauma, play therapy, expressive arts and animal assisted human health. We understand that trauma and mental illness, like physical illness, is a medically treatable condition. We understand that trauma and mental illness can occur in any life and those who experience trauma or mental illness can return to good health. As a result, there is no room for stigma in our society. Our clinic’s model for mental well-being is the direct result of a robust collection of research and treatment options which includes: movement and expressive therapies, education services to clients and the professional community and research to support practice. This portfolio of services continuously evolves to meet the changing needs and priorities of all people.

name: Genevieve Payne, LMFT (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I will work with you in a safe and collaborative way to determine your goals for therapy. I'll also invite you to slow down and turn your attention inward, so you can notice thoughts and feelings that emerge and ultimately learn how to make more choices in the moment rather than simply reacting. I'll help you explore patterns of relating to others, without judgment, so you can decide which parts of your identity feel right and which no longer serve you. You will develop new skills that allow you to feel more at peace, safer in your body, and more conscious of your internal thoughts and feelings. With nearly 13 years of experience practicing EMDR, I am now offering 1-3 day EMDR intensives to help you find pathways to healing in a condensed amount of time without the need to commit to weekly or biweekly therapy. Limited space available for weekend intensives, so please inquire about eligibility and scheduling. Let's get started!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have been practicing since 2009 and hold a license in Marriage and Family Therapy. I specialize in working with anxiety, depression, major life transitions, trauma, and pregnancy, postpartum and parenting issues. I treat a variety of traumas including acute trauma, childhood or attachment trauma, sexual trauma, and medical trauma.

name: Tracie McDowell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a provider in the field for more than 15 years, I have substantial experience helping clients learn new skills for managing stress, anxiety and trauma symptoms. My clients tend to have improved functionality including sleep, employment and in their relationships after our work together. Everyone is unique. I do not use one specific therapy tool. I use a variety of tools and cater therapy to each person depending on individual needs or preference. It takes courage to reach out. I return ALL phone calls and emails within 24 hours, and I'm happy to help.
More About the People I Treat
Abuse and shame often originate in childhood because children believe or may be led to believe abuse is their fault or responsibility. Counseling can help to alter the view of abuse by looking at it though a different lens that might not have been visible from the childhood perspective. An alternate view can provide clarity and may help shed feelings of shame and blame. I also work clients who are struggling with splitting/parts/brief dissociative episodes to help them feel more connected.

name: Amber Caldera (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
Our Approach to Helping
Nestled in the heart of the Pacific Northwest, our therapy practice offers a sanctuary for those who feel misunderstood. At Amber Caldera Therapy, we specialize in supporting queer and neurodivergent individuals through the unique challenges they face. Our approach is deeply empathetic, trauma-informed, and tailored to celebrate your authentic self. Whether you're grappling with late diagnoses, exploring your identity, or seeking strategies for managing daily life, we're here to empower you. We utilize trauma-informed techniques and specialized tools to help you build skills and tools that will last you a life time. From EMDR, SSP, DBT, IFS, somatic therapies, mindfulness, and more, we have got you covered! We are always open to your perspective and opinion on how you want your therapy to go, and welcome feedback and collaboration. This is your therapy, and you deserve to advocate for what you want!
Why We are a Good Fit for You
You’re overwhelmed, exhausted, and somehow feel like a total stranger in your own head. Juggling your neuro-spicy quirks with the anxiety of masking or being labeled “too weird”, “too much”, or “lazy”. With us, you’re securing a safe space to heal and blossom into the vibrant garden you were always meant to be! We pride ourselves on being a safe place to land for anyone navigating neurodivergence, trauma, and LGBTQIA+ issues. And not to toot our own horn, but we’re experts for a reason! We chose this path because we identify with or are strong allies to those facing these challenges. With our training, experience, and a sprinkle of intuition, we’re here to help you find lasting healing and peace. If we are the only place you feel comfort in this season of your life, we are honored to hold this space for you. Picture breaking free from the whirlwind of worries, struggles, and self-conscious thoughts swirling through your head (if only we could drop ‘em with the swish of a wand, huh?).

name: Leah Haas (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use a strengths-based, trauma informed, LGBTQIA2S+ affirming, sex positive, intersectional, and client centered approach. I incorporate body-centered psychotherapy (somatic therapy) and mindfulness into my practice. I work with individuals, couples, and families.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My approach to therapy is collaborative, creative, and compassionate. My goal is to create a safe and warm space, so each client feels comfortable sharing what's on their mind and in their heart. This may include diving into trauma, grief, and other challenging life experiences that prevent a person from feeling at peace and operating at their highest potential. The philosophy that guides my work is that everyone has the inner wisdom needed to thrive. My role is to help clients access this inner wisdom so they can heal, grow and live the fulfilling life they are seeking.

name: Margaret King (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My willingness to stay open to my clients' unique experiences in the space we create together allows me to facilitate safety for my clients. My approach operates in the "here-and-now" from a place of both intuition & professional training. I work with clients using a trauma-informed lens to deepen self-knowing, meaning making, support shifts in perspective, as well as emotional and behavioral support. *I believe the therapeutic relationship created between client and therapist in the present moment is the most essential tool for learning, growth, and deepening.* I use a variety of creative interventions to assist the process: -Internal Family Systems (IFS) -Brainspotting -role play -mindfulness/guided meditation -dream assessments -sand tray
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I moved to Portland from New Orleans, LA about 8 years ago in search of new opportunities and a deeper connection with the natural world. I have a background in embodiment practices, comedy/theater, energy work, creativity, and personal struggles that make me the unique healer I am today. Providing mental health services to the community has been a part of my life for the past 10 years. I received by BS in Psychology from Tulane University and my MA in Professional Mental Health Counseling from Lewis & Clark College. I interned for 9 months on the Post Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD) specialty team at the Portland VA Hospital where I served Veterans with PTSD and a variety of other mental health issues. I worked for 1.5 years with children ages 8-17 in crisis on a children's inpatient psychiatric unit in New Orleans, LA.

name: MindRx Group
approaches:
Our Approach to Helping
At MindRx, we understand that mental health is deeply personal, and no two journeys are the same. That’s why we take an integrative, whole-person approach that goes beyond just symptom management. Our team of board-certified psychiatric and mental health practitioners blends neuroscience, functional medicine, and innovative therapeutic techniques to address the root causes of emotional and mental health challenges. Whether you’re struggling with anxiety, depression, trauma, perinatal mental health concerns, or simply feeling stuck, we work with you to develop a personalized plan that supports healing, resilience, and overall well-being. Our goal is to empower you with the tools, insight, and support needed to regain balance and live a fulfilling life.
Our Values as Mental Health Practitioners
At MindRx, we are more than just a mental health practice—we are a community dedicated to breaking barriers in mental health care. Our foundation is built on curiosity, compassion, and accountability, ensuring that every patient feels seen, heard, and supported. We welcome individuals from all backgrounds and are committed to creating an inclusive, affirming space for LGBTQ+ individuals, BIPOC communities, parents, professionals, and anyone seeking meaningful change. Our practitioners are passionate about reducing stigma, embracing innovation, and continuously learning to provide the best possible care. Whether you’re looking for a fresh perspective, long-term support, or guidance through life’s challenges, we are here to walk this path with you.

name: Michelle Perez (SHE/HER/ELLA)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Change can be difficult and scary, and I want to support you in finding the tools to bring needed change into your life. I believe in our capacity to transform wounds into wisdom and to cultivate self-awareness. I use a holistic approach that recognizes all aspects of a person. I enjoy working with children, teens, adults and with families. I integrate techniques from Psychoanalytic, Dialectical Behavioral Therapy, and mind-body interventions such as mindfulness and breathing work. When working with children I utilize techniques from Play Therapy and Attachment Theory.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
During my free time I enjoy spending time with my family and friends, dancing, traveling and the outdoors.

name: Shauna Raffety Schatz (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a licensed psychotherapist with more than 20 years of experience helping clients make positive change in their lives, whether you are struggling with depression, anxiety, relationship problems, or just day-to-day life stressors. I will assist you in identifying issues that are holding you back, building the coping tools you need, and taking action to move towards the life you want. I will greet you with curiosity, compassion, and care and work collaboratively with you, incorporating your inherent strength and wisdom.

name: Jessica Franco
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to healing focuses on creating a collaborative, empowering, and supportive space where you feel safe to explore your emotions, experiences, and goals. I draw from evidence-based practices, including Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Dialectical Behavior Therapy, and Motivational Interviewing, tailoring each session to your unique needs. Together, we’ll identify challenges, set meaningful goals, and develop strategies that resonate with you. I believe healing happens through understanding, connection, and growth, so I emphasize building a strong therapeutic relationship grounded in empathy, trust, and mutual respect. Whether we’re working through past trauma, managing anxiety or depression, or navigating life’s transitions, I aim to provide tools that not only address immediate concerns but also support long-term resilience. With a person-centered and strength-based approach, I’m here to help you uncover your inner resources and embrace your strengths.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I believe that therapy should be a collaborative and supportive process tailored to your unique needs and goals. With experience working with adolescents and adults facing challenges such as trauma, anxiety, depression, and life transitions, I understand the importance of creating a compassionate and inclusive space where you feel safe to explore and grow. Drawing on evidence-based approaches like CBT, DBT, and strength-based methods, I provide practical tools and insights to help you navigate your challenges and uncover your inner resilience. My experience working with diverse populations, including individuals navigating neurodiversity and significant mental health concerns, allows me to adapt my approach to meet you where you are. If you’re looking for someone who will listen with empathy, collaborate with you, and empower you to create meaningful progress, I’m confident we can work together to build a path toward the life you envision for yourself.

name: Carlyn Glaser (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In couples therapy I discuss interpersonal styles, conflict resolution and communication skills. I help couples better understand how their own and partner's past experiences have influenced their relationship styles. In individual therapy I discuss with the client how one's faulty and unhealthy thinking and beliefs can lead to behaviors that are not helpful--often leading to depression and anxiety. I explore how trauma and upbringing influences current behavior.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have practiced therapy on both the east and west coast for many years. I have worked in the following areas: hospital counseling,clinical work, private practice, domestic violence agencies and college counseling. I have an extensive background in : LGBTQ issues , relationship issues, domestic violence, alcohol and drug abuse, depression , anxiety and trauma.

name: Julie Bloom (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use an integrative approach where I attune to my client’s goals, needs, and the greater systems that may be impacting them. My approach is relational, LGBTQ+ affirming, and trauma-informed. I take into account the whole person as well as the relationships, systems of oppression, and barriers that might influence how they show up in the world. So in sessions with clients, we are not simply looking at symptoms, but striving to understand and excavate the deeper places to get the full picture of what’s happening, where it started, and what a path forward could look like. Additionally, I offer EMDR Therapy (Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing). I completed my Master of Arts in Mental Health Counseling from Regis University in Denver.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
The therapeutic relationship is a microcosm where an individual will get to experience someone honoring their full humanity—a supportive space to identify and heal relational wounds. And, to enhance ones ability to connect with others simply by having the allocated space of therapy to practice having a sense of safety within the context of the therapeutic relationship. My hope is to highlight client strengths and equip and empower them to move about the world with more ease and a sense of belonging. At its best, therapy is a space where your full identity and humanity is held, honored, and cultivated.

name: Madison Carter
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that healing and growth happen within safe, nurturing relationships. As a Marriage and Family Therapist Associate, I create a therapeutic environment where clients can explore their challenges, understand their patterns, and develop stronger connections - both with themselves and others. My approach combines evidence-based practices with deep compassion, ensuring that each person's unique background and experiences are honored and respected.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I create a safe, nurturing environment where you can explore your challenges and work toward meaningful change. Whether you're a couple looking to rebuild connection, a family navigating communication difficulties, or an individual, I'm here to help. What sets my practice apart is my dedication to meeting you exactly where you are. I don't believe in one-size-fits-all solutions – instead, I tailor my approach to your unique needs and experiences. Together, we'll work at your pace to strengthen relationships, improve communication patterns, and help you feel more connected to yourself and others

name: Lindsay Bong, LPC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
For clients experiencing anxiety and depression, my goal is to help uncover areas of strength and employ those strengths to improve quality of life and relationships. Looking for patterns in our thoughts, behavior and our relationships gives us information on what works for us and what doesn’t. Practicing self compassion as we look through these sometimes painful past and current experiences leads to healing and confidence. Achieving a healthy self esteem is foundational in having the kind of relationships we are all craving. Mindfulness is a tool I employ often to help clients make connections with their thoughts and their behaviors and the world around them. I'm also trained in Brainspotting, a powerful tool that helps identify, process, and then release core neurophysiological sources of emotional/body pain, trauma, dissociation and other challenging symptoms. I believe that we all are worthy of healing and enjoying healthy relationships with themselves and others!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received a BA in Psychology at the University of Tulsa in 2002, followed by a Masters in Counseling from Dallas Theological Seminary in 2008. I am currently a licensed therapist with the Oregon Board of Licensed Professional Counselors and Therapists. Outside of my work, you can find me reading at least three books at the same time, making art and playing with my family and friends. As I encourage my clients to live wholeheartedly, I engage with what I believe in and am always learning more and practicing the arts of self compassion and growth.

name: Ashley Vande Slunt (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
You don’t have to navigate healing alone. I offer a grounded, supportive space where you can slow down, reconnect with yourself, and move through what’s been holding you back. Using an integrative, somatic approach—including EMDR, SSP (Safe and Sound Protocol), RRP (Rest and Restore Protocol), mindfulness, and trauma-informed care—I help women work through anxiety, past trauma, life transitions, and burnout. Therapy with me is about real, lasting change rooted in self-awareness, body connection, and nervous system healing. You’re the expert of your experience; my role is to walk alongside you, offering tools, perspective, and presence as you move toward greater clarity, resilience, and ease. Whether you’re feeling overwhelmed, stuck, or simply ready for a deeper kind of support, I’m here to help you reconnect with your inner strength and create meaningful change from the inside out.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe healing happens in safe, attuned, and non-judgmental spaces. As a somatic and trauma-informed therapist, I value deep listening, curiosity, and collaboration. I believe our body caries both resilience and wisdom. I center consent, cultural awareness, and nervous system safety in all of our work together. Therapy with me is about slowing down, reconnecting with your body, and building the internal resources you need to thrive. I honor your pace and your truth. My role isn’t to fix—it’s to offer support and inviting there to be a trusting relationship with oneself. Healing is possible, and I’ll meet you with compassion every step of the way.

name: Dasia Star (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My relationship with counseling began when I entered therapy for an addiction that shaped my early adulthood. Therapy offered me a space where I felt genuinely grounded, supported, and seen for the first time in my life. My inspiration for becoming a therapist was rooted in the love and safety I found in the therapeutic relationship. My theoretical foundation is trauma informed, attachment based, and draws from Emotionally Focused, Person Centered, and Somatic therapies. Additionally, I have completed professional training in Emotionally Focused Therapy, am currently a Teaching Assistant and am in ongoing consultation for this modality. In session I use mindfulness as an instrument to help clients increase self-awareness and self-compassion, move through painful and immobilizing places, and journey within to regain access to concealed or restrained inner magic.
Social Justice Issues I Care About
My Identity: In terms of my own identity, I am a white, queer, polyamorous, non-binary femme. I love working with everyone from monogamous and cis/het folx to people with diverse and fluid relationship styles and identities. As an activist and systemic thinker, issues of equity and justice are always on my mind. Given our current cultural and political landscape, many of us are in real need of de-stigmatizing and non-shaming therapeutic spaces where we can fully be ourselves. My hope as a therapist is to give folx a space where they don’t have to edit or disown any of their parts. I am body positive, sex positive, kink positive, LQBTQIA+ affirming, anti-racist, and spiritually open. I value working with couples and relationships of all kinds, including with non-traditional and non-monogamous relational orientations. Everyone is welcome here!

name: Jonathan Joebgen
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My personal style is highly intuitive, dynamic, and personable. And my approach to therapy is rooted in mindfulness and strategies to build awareness and insight into one’s thoughts, feelings, actions, and personal experience to cultivate growth, self-acceptance, and a sense of agency or choice. With increased awareness you will be able to “slow down” and better understand what it is you are thinking, feeling, and experiencing in the moment so that you can make an informed decision about how you would like to respond. In our work together, I will teach you skills to help you relate to yourself and others in a way that is helpful, balanced and kind. I have successfully helped others to heal from past pain and loss and form new, healthy relationships based on trust, helped people find their voice and build a sense of self that feels authentic and whole, as well as overcome anxiety and all the "what ifs" that don't seem to end.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a licensed clinical social worker/therapist and earned my Master of Social Work degree from Portland State University. I incorporate Western psychology, neuroscience, and Eastern wisdom traditions in my work, and I have advanced training in the application of Buddhist psychology and Gestalt Therapy. That said, my approach is still very secular and really value and appreciate a skeptical mind because it's so important that we each find our own truth and approach that resonates. I am a founding member of a local sangha called the FUTUS (F you, Thank you) and our name honors the complexity of life and sense of both resistance and gratitude that can come with growth and change. I challenge myself daily to deepen my work and practice so that I can act with integrity and walk my talk. I love to foster that same growth in others and to help my clients to fully embrace themselves and own their power and brilliance.

name: Kate McNulty
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My orientation in working with clients is strengths-based and collaborative. Most people find the type of therapy I offer is informative and engaging. I enjoy helping people learn new ways of looking at themselves. Counseling is often referred to as "hard work." Indeed, our discussions may bring up emotions that are quite intense and difficult . However there is also a lot of room for humor, pleasure and the delight of the unexpected in any counseling session. I seek out those moments and call them to your attention. I see counseling as a place to try out new behaviors, receive honest feedback and increase your emotional intelligence. My approach is pragmatic; I provide people with plenty of handouts, information, tools and resources. You will find the way I work with you is tailored to your particular situation and needs. Although I am trained in many methods of counseling, the very best method is always based on your immediate circumstances and concerns.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have extensive training and experience working with relationship issues and sexual concerns. I am certified as a Gottman Relationship Therapist by the Gottman Institute of Seattle, a research institute that has spent 40+ years studying couple and family relationships with true scientific rigor. I also hold certification as a Sex Therapist through AASECT, the American Association of Sex Educators, Counselors and Therapists. Both of these credentials required comprehensive study, direct demonstration of clinical skills and endorsement by other therapists who could attest to my competence. I am proud of my association with these organizations because of their dedication to protecting clients' well-being and to ongoing, engaged critical thinking about the field of mental health. Prior to opening my private practice my professional experience was in local agencies with children, teens and families. I also worked in a hospital ER for 15 years.

name: J.C. Stewart (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I've worked hard to center my practice on well-researched techniques that have been shown to drastically decrease symptoms of PTSD and related diagnoses, whether a client's trauma comes from their family life, romantic relationships or degrading systems within our culture. As such, depending on the needs and mutually defined goals we create in session, I use EMDR, IFS, cognitive behavioral therapies and humanistic approaches in my sessions. In addition to working to heal trauma in particular, I can also help treat depression, anxiety and provide support for neurodivergence, such as autism and ADHD.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe we have a lot of work to do to create a more just and equitable society. The systems that have continued to perpetuate violence towards marginalized groups need to be dismantled in order for new and better systems to be created. And while I know that's easier said than done, I also believe that making a better world starts in our local communities, whether through collective advocacy via protests or by direct mutual aid work.

name: Joey Bloom
approaches:
My approach using various therapeutic trance and guided inquiry does not rely heavily on the narrative details of your story but rather the driving force, the deeper part of you; you could say where the story originates. By gently going right to the core, it is very easy to quickly and effectively break out of negative patterns and heal deep emotional wounds and stored trauma.
For the last 4 years I have been a Leela School student based out of Ashland, Oregon. We are A.C.H.E certified. Using trance, guided meditation and simply an open quiet space, this type of intervention works very efficiently in getting to the root of the problem. Whether it be changing behaviors, stopping addictions or simply being stuck in negative mind patterns, I meet you openly.

name: Laken Nelson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to supporting and helping people on their unique journey of healing is first and foremost collaborative. Therapy is challenging on its own and I strive for each person I work with to feel safe and supported to engage actively in deciding how their treatment goes. I've worked with adults who have experienced complex and chronic trauma that has negatively impacted their relationship with themselves and their ability to experience their emotions. I have experience working with folks who have tried therapy in the past and feel stuck with too much awareness and not enough support to move through and past challenges and problems. I work collectively with each client to identify their current relationship to and with their body when experiencing their emotions and collaborate from there times to weave in breath work, mindfulness, grounding exercises and other somatic interventions to support the relationship between mind and body.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I believe that everyone is capable and deserving of healing and connection. So many different experiences can impact and influence how we think, feel and function. Another impact on us as people are the various and intersecting identities we possess: partner, sibling, friend, student, parent, child, queer, trans, neurodivergent and that is just to name a few. I take all identities and experiences into consideration when working with folks because I believe that therapy cannot be done without that information. I value folks for all that they are and all that they might wish that they weren't with an aim to healing the relationship amongst all the parts of you through integrative healing with the mind and body. Our mind and bodies cannot exist without one another, yet we often operate as if they do. Reconnecting or plugging back into the body is scary and uncomfortable but it is also rewarding and has the ability to cultivate a healing experience for all your parts and identities.

name: Anne-Marie Basso (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Disruptive patterns often derive from early relationships and take on a life of their own, outside of your awareness. I see the therapy relationship as a laboratory, playground and crucible in which you can discover new ways of relating to yourself and your world, in which you can feel both supported and challenged enough to stretch the limits of what you thought possible. You have strengths and abilities waiting to be unearthed, and buried potential poised to bloom. In the privacy of our therapy relationship, I hope to help you think about, move beyond and learn from emotional pain in order to create more choice and possibilities in your life, and find new and more effective ways of coping with life's challenges. Come: let's begin your journey.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
People come to see me when they want to make meaningful changes that will lead them to having more of what they want in life: more loving relationships, more work satisfaction, more insight into themselves, more creative fulfillment, more joy. I believe that people grow in relationships, and that two minds working together on complex problems is the best way to uncover their core and create the conditions for change. My style is engaged, respectful, collaboritive and compassionate. I am interested in grasping the whole of your world, inner and outer, and how they interact. I find that the best way to make sense of your current feelings is to understand them in the context of your life, past and present, and I will listen carefully and work with you to develop a fuller understanding of what you are struggling with and how I can support you in moving through it.

name: Chaviva Greenberg
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
With a passion for supporting families, teens, and kids, I thrive on collaboration. Whether it's home, school, or friendships, let's craft tailored solutions for success. As a fellow parent, of both neuro-typical and neuro-divergent children, I get the challenges. Let's find the right tools for a transformative journey together! Additionally, I use a combined CBT and MBSR approach to helping people manage their chronic pain and/or women's health issues.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe in a strength based approach. Everyone possesses unique strengths that shape their individuality. These strengths empower individuals to navigate life's challenges, fostering personal growth. As a mother and as someone with extensive experience with children and teenagers, I understand the significance of cultivating genuine feelings of competency and success. These are important no matter what stage of life you are in.

name: Jaxon Shaffer (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I consider the therapist/client relationship sacred and personal. I believe that the relationship and dynamics in the therapy room are of the most essential mediums for change. With this, I bring immediacy to my engagement with clients. Using immediacy means that clients can expect for me, at times, to reveal how I am feeling in response to them. This approach focuses attention on the here-and-now relationship of therapist and client with intentional timing to challenge defensiveness and/or heighten awareness of patterns of relating to others outside the therapy room. If you are looking to process trauma, I am trained in and use a modality called Narrative Exposure Therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a non-binary trans-masculine clinician, I am dedicated to helping clients who are transgender, non-binary, agender, gender expansive, or clients who are questioning their gender identity. Understanding that so many of us have been harmed by the medical system (including therapists), I first and foremost strive to create a therapeutic relationship that feels safe and welcoming. Secondly, I am passionate about guiding my clients through their journey with gender: establishing a strong sense of self, coming out, dealing with challenging family responses and dynamics, considering or maintaining physical interventions to treat dysphoria, navigating educational and medical systems or/or workplaces, learning to cope with and exist within a cis and heteronormative society. Thirdly, it is essential that my clients feel as though their gender IS NOT a problem to be solved and is not the sole focus of therapy.

name: Jim Seger (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Working together, we will discuss the challenges you face and consider the therapeutic approaches that seem most likely to benefit you. In some cases, you might decide to focus on how your family of origin or trauma history gets in the way of more fully being your better, healthier self. In other cases, you might want to work through challenging choices or situations in the present.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As important as self understanding is, therapy is much more than just figuring out the what and why of our ways of being in the world. If understanding alone were enough to heal ourselves and our relationships, life would be much easier. But, for many of us, even after we develop some understanding, we still struggle. As understanding is developed we need to use that understanding to cultivate change. I use the term "cultivate" purposefully. We do not simply use our conscious understanding to impose internal change. Rather, like a farmer, we need to establish generative conditions that allow internal growth and shifts to occur. In some cases, therapy provides an opportunity to practice altering how we act in response to certain situations. In other cases, therapy may be more about figuring out how to create conditions in our lives and routines that allow difficult to fathom healing powers to change us in healthful ways.

name: Kathleen Converse (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I'm an anti-oppressive relational therapist and bring that lens into all of my work with clients. I take a personalized, collaborative approach to healing work. I prioritize creating a strong relationship with clients with safety and trust. I am trained in EMDR and Internal Family Systems Theory informed, and draw on somatic mind- body techniques in my work with clients. I use attachment theory to explore relational wounds and use a strengths based approach to build on your existing coping strategies. I am a former sexual assault advocate, and university Director for the Center for Gender Equity. I have 10 years of experience working with survivors of trauma, and draw on this background to help you have healthier relationships with yourself and others.
My Values as a Therapist
I have a background in community organizing and am passionate about social justice. My practice strives to be anti-oppressive, queer and trans affirming, neuro-affirming, and trauma- informed. I'm a queer therapist and passionate about supporting the LGBTQ+ community, . I believe that individual healing creates ripples in the world, and that a more equitable and just world is possible starting by healing ourselves.

name: Natalie Mickola (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My first 6+ years of therapy experience was with adolescents with eating disorders. When people think of eating disorders, they often imagine a stereotypical version, which doesn’t represent the majority of people who experience eating disorders and disordered eating. This is something that can happen to anyone, for many reasons. I view it as a way of coping, similar but different to addiction, that is aimed to help someone but has too many negative consequences. Whether you are looking to change your behaviors and/or mindset, I am here to help you better understand why you’re engaging in your behaviors and to decrease your need to rely on this specific way of coping. My biggest goal is always to help you find better ways to take care of yourself. I use an eclectic mix of therapeutic modalities, based on what seems to best match with my client’s needs. I view therapy as a place to build trust so you can do your most vulnerable and deep exploration to meet your therapeutic goals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I value being that person to hold space for you and your emotions. It’s hard enough managing our life stressors day-to-day sometimes, but as you add up the other challenges that are often out of our control (past trauma, systems of oppression, diet culture, social media, societal messaging, imbalances in the brain, etc.), moving forward and making change can feel impossible. I think everyone could benefit from having that extra support, and I’m proud to be that person. In addition to my beliefs about therapy, here are some of the values and beliefs that inform my practice: I work from a Heath at Every Size (HAES) approach, meaning an anti-weight bias and anti-fatphobia view of people and bodies. Black Lives Matter. Racism is trauma. Trans and nonbinary people have always existed. Everyone deserves to have accessible and safe healthcare. People deserve to be the ones to make decisions (medical or otherwise) about their own bodies. Diet culture and misogyny harm all of us.

name: Kate Sturges, MA, LPC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Believing in our drive to connect through relationships, I work from a space of collaboration. It is my desire to accompany you, to be a witness for you and offer a safe nonjudgmental space for you to look inward and find the answers that lie within you. I strive to provide a space for individuals and couples to be able to find clarity during times of chaos. I seek to create a space that can open up dialogue for people to explore the places they feel unbalanced whether it be in work, relationships, or other forms of transition.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a Master’s Degree in Counseling Psychology from Lewis and Clark College, Graduate School of Education and Counseling. As a Nationally Certified Counselor and a Licensed Professional Counselor with the State of Oregon and Washington, I offer a broad base of knowledge with over 13 years’ of experience spanning the spectrum of the mental health field. Prior to entering into private practice I worked in community mental health and group practice clinics. As a counselor over the years I broadened my experience by working with a wide range of clients experiencing depression, anxiety, relationship issues, transitions and grief in a clinical setting.

name: Cathy Walker, LPC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Therapy is collaborative. Everyone has their strengths, although, at times, it is difficult to recognize them. I use an eclectic approach based on who the client is and what they bring into the session. I draw from person-centered, Gestalt, cognitive behavioral therapy, and mindfulness approaches.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a licensed professional counselor. I started my professional life in a technical field and found myself becoming more and more interested in the people with whom I worked, and, so, I decided to pursue a master's degree in counseling from Portland State University. I have a background in crisis work, suicide bereavement support, and working in elementary schools in addition to my private practice.

name: Dr. Katie Statman-Weil (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I am passionate about Parenting and supporting children's healthy development; I wholeheartedly believe that your relationship with your child is the foundation for health and healing. I am both a Licensed Clinical Social Worker and I hold a doctorate in education focused on supporting students' behavior and mental health in the classroom. I support families and schools caring for children with trauma histories, family conflict, attachment issues, depression, anxiety, ADHD, PDA, and behavioral challenges. I am a foster, adoptive, and biological parent and bring this lived experience into my work. I understand the impact trauma can have on relationships and attachment firsthand, and I know healing is possible. Parenting can be hard and lonely, especially when your child's emotions or behaviors feel bigger than those of their peers. I am here to help you find a way out of the loneliness. I am a collaborator and help you problem solve ways to create more joy and ease in your lives.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe children, and parents, do well when they can. Sometimes it takes some work to figure out exactly what it is that children need to feel better. I work from an attachment and relationship focused lens to support parents raising children with emotional, behavioral, and learning differences. I work with families who could describe their children as: challenging, explosive, difficult, neurodiverse, gifted, different, struggling in school, or having experienced trauma.

name: Izy Leighton (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
Prior to becoming a therapist, I worked for many years in an inpatient treatment setting for kids and teens struggling with their safety, supporting them with emotion regulation, trauma recovery, and family dynamics issues. I left this position to complete my Master's degree in Marriage, Couples, and Family Therapy at Lewis & Clark Graduate School of Education and Counseling, where I earned an additional specialization in Sex Therapy. Since then I have worked with diverse populations in an array of settings - including a community counseling center, a family medical clinic, and a group practice specializing in couples and sex therapy - before starting my own practice, where I provide compassionate trauma-informed therapy for individuals and relationships.
My Approach to Helping
Though I have valuable experience and knowledge to share with my clients, I pointedly do not identify as an expert and do not claim to have all the answers. I recognize my clients' innate wisdom and seek to honor their agency and intuition throughout the therapeutic process. I approach therapy as an active collaborator, a curious explorer, and a loving witness. I believe it's critical to "zoom out" and consider the contexts in which my clients' concerns occur, exploring the impacts of trauma, attachment, culture, and oppression. My therapeutic style is authentic and warm, though I gently challenge my clients to approach their growing edges and take risks that support their healing. I believe a strong therapeutic relationship is a cornerstone of truly transformative therapy and a key change agent in itself; I continually invest in this relationship to provide my clients with reparative emotional experiences and support them in moving toward secure attachment with themselves and others.

name: Kendra Hollingsworth (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I aim to co-create a safe space with my clients to strengthen their relationship to themselves and others by cultivating compassion, tenderness and greater self awareness. It is my hope that through our work together, you will feel more confident and connected with your authentic self, and feel empowered to live congruently in community, while operating from a place of personal alignment. I approach my work from an Existential Humanistic lens (we are all humans trying to learn how to human together), and utilize tools and techniques derived from play therapy, Internal Family Systems, Emotionally Focused Therapy, Experiential practices, as well as a dose of my own healthy humor. Together we will explore what it would be like if we had the courage to live life outside the box prescribed to us, and ask ourselves what would it look like if we constructed our own box, or chose to remain outside the confines of one altogether.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
It is my belief that healing happens in relationship, specifically when you have experienced being witnessed, heard, and seen by others. It is also my belief that we are the architects of our own reality, and that by being able to cultivate a true relationship with our authentic selves, we can create the life we envision for ourselves. Prior to pursuing my graduate studies, I spent eight years working abroad with children, adolescents, and adults from a variety of cultural and social backgrounds in both educational and camp-like settings. In those spaces I developed a passion for exploring communication and care within cross-cultural relationships, as well as a curiosity around alternative ways of being and self expression.

name: Kristine Zappa (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to counseling starts with a belief that healing happens within a safe, compassionate, non-judgmental relationship. I hope to create a space to process overwhelming experiences in order to help you gain more clarity and move forward with new skills and a sense of empowerment in your life. From a somatic perspective, I believe the body and the mind are intimately linked and I safely incorporate body-centered trauma processing tools as a path towards finding stability in your life. I also believe that systems play an important role in our experience and should be taken into account when treating mental health; these systems can include culture, institutions, community, family, intimate relationships and more. Lastly, and importantly, my approach to counseling is not one of being an "expert", but my goal is to help you discover your own inherent wisdom.
Why I Became a Therapist
I believe in the power of relationships and know firsthand how supportive a trusting, safe therapeutic relationship can be. I became a therapist because of my own journey of self-growth and healing through therapy and I wholeheartedly believe people are so much more than their struggles. I am dedicated to helping folks see and feel their innate goodness, beyond their past experiences, and lean into the potential for a life of ease and wholeness. I also became a therapist because I believe that healing as an individual can ripple out and help heal the collective in which we are all apart of, and which we all play a role.

name: Lacey Hawkins (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
Are you in the middle of a divorce or are divorced and want to find healing and health? Has there been an infidelity in your marriage? Are there issues in your family you need a little extra help with? Are you a blended family looking for help blending? Do you need help walking through marital or family trauma? Are you dealing with the death of a loved one? Are you experiencing infertility? Pregnancy or postpartum issues? Are you a Pastor on the verge of burnout? There is hope! Let's get connected.
Why I Became a Therapist
After years of helping youth and families, I realized there were so many times that I wished I knew more so I could be better equipped to help those I served to walk through what they were facing. I decided to attend George Fox University and it was there I confirmed that Marriage and Family Therapy was where I was supposed to be. I am Restoration Therapy focused which means I help individuals, couples, and families to identify the pain cycles in their lives, move those to peace, and help to restore love and trust. My husband and I are Marriage and Family Pastors at our church and we are on a mission to help couples have the best marriage they can. We discovered the "secret" for us is open communication, regular check-ins, date nights, marriage retreats, dreaming together, forgiveness, and laughing a ton together. We have 3 children all under the age of 6 who keep us on our toes and who are the light of our life.

name: Gillian Callahan (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My work is largely influenced by our constantly evolving understanding of neurobiology and central nervous system functioning. I'm also currently working toward certifications in psychedelic assisted therapy modalities. Trauma triggers, anxiety and anger are elevated physical responses that can be met and addressed in the body, where they're happening, so that we can find other ways to think and act on those difficult emotions. Language is a vital way in which we make sense of our experience, but when language is inaccessible ("I don't know how to talk about this") or played out ("I've talked about my trauma a million times but it never seems to get better"), the body gives us information and wisdom about what we need to heal. I utilize curiosity, empathy, humor and compassion to help people feel comfortable and safe, and then we listen to what the body has to tell us.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a proud member of the queer community. I am passionate about racial justice and understand that my privilege and blind spots as a white cis woman require constant checking, learning and humility. I believe that patriarchy is harmful to all genders and that healing the wounds of toxic masculinity is a path to true connection and happiness for all genders. I often bring my dogs to work with me to help people relax and regulate when they're struggling in session, and also because dogs are awesome. I'm an avid learner of history and often find myself bringing the lessons of the past into my work. I'm also a big giant sci-fi and fantasy nerd and have been known to use allegories from early 80's young adult fantasy movies, Tolkien, Dr. Who and Star Trek, among other things, as teaching tools.

name: Kerry Powers, LPC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
I provide a safe, encouraging and non-judgmental therapeutic environment. My style is warm, collaborative and direct. Compassion and humor are vital ingredients for growth and wellness. My approach is integrative; drawing from strengths based, trauma-informed, relational, social justice and anti-oppression perspectives. I place great value on being able to deeply witness, understand and hold space for you in your moments of pain and uncertainty. Some life challenges have the capacity to derail, uproot, and halt us from harnessing our power to live the lives we desire. As a counselor, I place strong emphasis on developing insight & empathy to address and resolve problems, change patterns, improve relationships, heal trauma & navigate hardships. I hope to offer a transformative therapeutic experience that will enable you to realize your goals. Together we will cultivate the skills, insight and draw on your inner strength to benefit you years beyond our time together.
In 2007 I received my M.A. in Counseling Psychology from Lewis and Clark College. I have 22 years of social service and counseling experience serving adolescents, adults and families. My background is in trauma recovery, grief and loss and medical and health psychology. I have served older adults and families through adjustment, chronic illness, end of life issues and caregiving. My community counseling work encompassed a wide range of issues with an emphasis on navigating transitions, relationships and mood disorders. I value existential work, helping clients navigate transitions and deepen in their own process of identity development and self-actualization. I place great value on cultural competency, advocating for social justice and the eradiction of all forms inequality and oppression. I endeavor to deepen in my own personal & professional evolution striving to be congruent with the principles I impart.

name: Claudia Brown (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
While I may have training in mental health, you are the expert in your own life. For me, it is crucial to stay present and curious about my client's experiences. I start all of my relationships by getting to know you deeply. Your history, perspective, point of view, and your feelings. Relationship building and trust is crucial, and some clients start to see transformation in their lives just from feeling fully seen and heard. If and when you become ready to go deeper into real lasting behavioral change, I employ frameworks such as Dialectice Behavior Therapy (DBT) and Mindfulness. I also use Internal Family Systems (IFS), inner child work and somatic work. As a queer person that lives with CPTSD and ADHD myself, I have done a lot of my own healing in therapy and bring many of my skills to you.
My Values as a Therapist
Human liberation is central to my practice, and I strive to create a space where you can challenge oppressive systems and embrace your true self. While the world around us may not always reflect the understanding or support we need, we can work toward a place of inner peace and resilience. My goal is to help you navigate these complexities, offering both compassion and empowerment as we work together to create meaningful change in your life. I am a proud member of the LGBTQ+ community, and have served LGBTQ+ youth in the school system. I also have a background in activism, within the Black Lives Matter movement as well as within immigration reform. As an immigrant child myself I resonate deeply with the feeling of displacement and survival that many of us experience. I am a Soviet Jewish cisgender woman, a mother, a dancer, and an artist

name: Salim Elhalaby
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I’m passionate about providing compassionate, and comprehensive mental health services to those in need by building rapport with patients and families. I follow evidence-based guidelines combined with a wide range of clinical experience, objective observation, and intuition to deliver patient centered treatment.

name: Jireh Miller (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Perhaps you've heard that understanding comes from walking a mile in that person's shoes. I've come to believe that true understanding comes from walking 'a mile' WITH them. Your journey has been filled with a variety of difficult events (and likely many moments of joy), leading you to the place you now find yourself. As we journey together, I bring an understanding and appreciation for your strengths, enabling a kind and caring discussion of where course correction may free you to thrive. I utilize elements from DBT, ACT, and Psychodynamic psychology mixed with beautiful influences from Narrative, multicultural, and person-centered modalities. I maintain an awareness for where systemic influences are asserting pressure, especially within the therapeutic alliance. Additionally, I offer Lifespan Integration.
Why I Became a Therapist
Instigated by personal experiences of childhood, intimate partner, and spiritual abuse, I decided to embark on a journey of healing. It's been a wild ride, and one that I don't anticipate ending anytime soon. However, the skills and understanding that I've gained from life-experience, support groups, and professional training have instilled an invaluable sense of how to proceed in life.... at least, I have that sense more often than I did in the past. What I'm saying is this - being able to read the terrain has gotten much easier, allowing me to implement my skills more effectively and a little more quickly. I look forward to helping you learn and grow into similar emotional dexterity. Additionally, I've not yet had a client from whom I didn't learn as much as they did from me. I look forward to our partnership on this therapeutic journey.

name: Carrie Ivy (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work with individuals and couples to provide support, heighten awareness, and bring clarity to foster understanding and forward movement. I offer in-person sessions at my office in SE Portland, as well as telehealth options to meet remotely. I am especially passionate about relationships, childhood trauma, and spirituality. With a Masters in Process-Oriented Facilitation and Conflict Studies, I use a process-oriented approach, which includes parts work like IFS. We will work together wherever you are at in your own process, exploring unconscious patterns, and pouring positive regard on all the underprivileged parts of yourself. In addition to talk therapy, I offer a variety of methods including dream work, art making, role-play, movement, and constellation work (walking vectors). I offer counseling and guidance navigating boundaries, grief, identity, sexuality, career, self-love, family dynamics, body symptoms, spirituality, plant medicine integration and harm reduction, and more.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that by integrating the parts of us that we marginalize and honoring our hearts, we can heal. (I believe this is true personally and collectively.) I believe all people and all things have a spirit, and that our connection to the spirit in ourselves is our key to contacting the spirit in everything else. I bring my whole self and whole heart to my practice. I have a rich spiritual life, guided by the stars and seen and unseen forces of nature and beyond. I'm an astrologer, I read birth charts (and tarot, and Kabbalistic Tree of Life), and keep my eye on the greater energies at play in our inner and outer worlds. I am a creative with many practices, and encourage others to follow their passions. I am a white queer cis woman, a witch, a partner, a mother, a poet, an activist, a mystic, an artist, a publisher.

name: Guillaume Counseling Services (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Our primarily lens is family system as we provide family therapy and couples therapy. We utilize Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Brief Solutions Focused Therapy, to name a few. Some of our Clinicians also have background in trauma informed care and also incorporate DBT. We have an eclectic approach to treatment, meaning that we first assess and then pull from treatment approaches to help our clients based on their present needs in the moment. We are very client-centered and tailor the service to individual needs. Most of our clients who are getting couples therapy are also receiving individual therapy. Our approach in working with couples is to focus on each person's mental health, which include individual and family (couples) sessions) with the same therapist. Most of our clients utilize their insurance to pay for services. We have a small percentage of clients who just want couples' counseling, focusing on the relationship only and not their mental health.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Clinical Supervisor and Clinician, Nahomie Guillaume is a Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist in the state of California (#48571), Oregon (# T1492) and Texas (#202775). She has 18 years of experienced in the field and has been licensed 14 years. She has a master's in Counseling Psychology, a Master of Business Administration, and a bachelor's in Psychology (emphasis in Christian Behavioral Science) and Communication arts (emphasis in Theater Arts). She also has a certification in life coaching and prayer therapy. She went to California Baptist University for both her undergraduate and graduate studies in psychology. Nahomie understand firsthand the challenges of being a mother, wife, with 4 little ones under the age of 12, juggling a career, and home life. Nahomie understand the challenges of losing a child through miscarriage and stillbirth. She understand the daily struggles of managing a relationship, striving to maintain love and respect, and growing as one. She is compassionate

name: Katya Stark (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a deeply engaged, intuitive, and experiential therapist who thrives on curiosity, emotional depth, and meaningful transformation. My work is dynamic and immersive, fostering deep self-exploration and real-world application. My immersive and interactive approach guides clients toward deep insight and meaningful, lasting change. My clients appreciate an approach that is both structured and fluid—offering the safety of guidance while encouraging self-directed discovery. They seek therapy that is engaging, thought-provoking, and capable of initiating true change. I balance warmth with directness, creating a space where clients can confront deep-seated patterns, reframe limiting beliefs, and cultivate more fulfilling ways of thinking, feeling, and connecting. My therapeutic style is active and experiential. I incorporate somatic awareness, relational dynamics, and emotional processing to help clients recognize patterns and make lasting shifts.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in transformation that arises from deep self-reflection, self-compassion, and the willingness to challenge internalized narratives and external limitations. My values are rooted in equity, autonomy, and emotional integrity, guiding me to create a space where clients feel seen, heard, and empowered. I am committed to anti-racist, anti-oppressive work and strive to dismantle systemic barriers that restrict self-expression and relational healing. I hold a deep respect for human complexity and the power of connection in fostering growth. I value intellectual curiosity, authenticity, and the courage to explore life beyond conventional norms. As a queer immigrant, I have experienced reinvention and resilience, shaping my ability to support those navigating identity, belonging, and personal evolution. My approach encourages exploration, challenges limiting beliefs, and fosters a life aligned with one’s deepest truths and aspirations.

name: Mary Roberts (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My clinical approach is collaborative, integrative, and grounded in relational neuroscience, justice, and posttraumatic growth. I draw from psychodynamic, feminist, and systems-informed models, tailoring the work to your unique needs and goals. Sessions are active and insight-driven, with a focus on connection, clarity, and sustainable change. I work best with clients ready to explore patterns, deepen self-trust, and engage in meaningful, values-based transformation.
Our Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that effective clinical care is rooted in humility, responsiveness, and a deep respect for each client’s lived experience. I value multicultural awareness, acknowledge systemic harm, and approach every person with curiosity rather than assumptions. My work is guided by a commitment to justice, integrity, and relational accountability. I strive to create a space where complexity is welcomed and where healing is tied to both personal and collective liberation. As a supervisor, I'm also very concerned with the state of the profession and take an active role in advocating for issues that affect both our clients and communities, and also the field of Professional Counseling.

name: Matthew Boylan
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that the most important part of counseling is the relationship. I work from an Intersubjective perspective and integrate Gottman Method, Emotionally Focused Therapy, and Equine Assisted Therapy to create safe and accepting relationships which foster growth and healing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold a masters degree in Marriage Couple and Family Counseling from George Fox University. My focus in graduate school was on Couples Therapy, Family Therapy, and Child-Centered Play Therapy. I have specialty in the Gottman Method for Couples Therapy, the Core Values Index for relationships and individual potential, and EAGALA to work with horses in counseling individuals, families, and groups.

name: Angelica Nenchev (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I specialize in substance abuse, eating disorders, relational issues, trauma, and oppression-based difficulties. I utilize an attachment-based, relational approach to explore your struggles in the context of your personal relationships and larger social structures. You can expect our sessions to be collaborative, compassionate, and culturally-affirming. I teach somatic-based skills to help integrate our work into the rest of your nervous system. I am trained in Somatic Experiencing (SE)-- a body-based approach for healing trauma. SE integrates the fields of physiology, psychology, ethology, biology, neuroscience, indigenous healing practices, and medical biophysics to release compressed traumatic energy in the body slowly over time. I supplement my somatic work with interventions from Acceptance & Commitment Therapy (ACT), Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), and Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT).
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I identify as an able, cis-gender biracial female. I am guided by cultural humility and enjoy working with people of all identities. I believe therapy should be a safe space for you to talk about and feel anything that arises at your own pace. My approach is guided by the principles of anti-oppression and social justice, so we will naturally address contextual circumstances that interfere with your sense of belonging and self-worth. I believe individuals grow through their relationships and are doing their best with the tools they have to adapt and respond to their environments. In my work, I help clients understand and appreciate the adaptations that have kept them alive, while also supporting the discovery of other options that promote choice, flexibility and empowerment.

name: Active Path Mental Health
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
At Active Path Mental Health, patients will be paired with a psychiatrist provider to help get their depression and/or anxiety under control using traditional front-line treatments such as medication management or specialty treatments like TMS or Spravato.
My Values as a Therapist
At Active Path Mental Health, providers take the time to understand a patient’s mental health history and current medications to allow for informed decision-making on the best depression and/or anxiety treatment plan moving forward, whether that is TMS, therapy, or medication.

name: Chance Parker (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I take a holistic approach that honors the physical, psychological, and spiritual parts of self to promote deep healing. My practice is grounded in Jungian/psychodynamic theory that integrates somatic experiencing, trauma-informed and cognitive approaches. PMDD can be excruciating. While there is a legitimate biological component to PMDD that differs from PMS, I also see menstrual symptoms as a call to deeply listen to your body's wisdom and challenge patriarchal attitudes and conditioned responses to the menstrual cycle. One way I help people with this is through a practice called Menstrual Cycle Awareness (MCA). This involves learning new methods to chart your cycle and techniques to build self-understanding through a cyclical practice that respects and honors your body's unique rhythms.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe each person has a natural tendency towards healing, and my role is to support your unique path. I find dreams to be invaluable for healing and encourage folks to explore their dreams in and outside of therapy. I use Menstrual Cycle Awareness (MCA) in my practice to help folks develop a deep connection with their bodies. No cycle is the same. Together, we’ll get to know your body’s unique phases and how they influence your body, mind, and spirit. Rather than trying to control your menstrual cycle for relief, we'll listen to your body and learn what it needs to be adequately supported.

name: Gary Conachan III (HE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I tell every client that the most important part of effective therapy is the fit between therapist and client. As proven by research, the therapeutic relationship is one of the biggest contributors to meaningful and lasting change. I want our work together to be focused on what you want and need. Because the therapeutic relationship is so important, I offer time and space for regular feedback. I name my identities and parts and invite clients to do the same, especially where power and privilege might be impacting with our work. Yes, I am the therapist with education and clinical experience - and, clients are the ultimate experts on their own lives. I view us as fellow travelers, bringing all our parts, identities, and humanity into our work together.
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a therapist because I grew up queer in an evangelical church and home. I've experienced first-hand what it's like to deny who you are for your faith and community. In my undergraduate studies, everything I'd known was challenged and deconstructed. Incidentally, it was through my studies that I became affirming of LGBTQIA+ people, which eventually led to accepting myself. I decided to pursue counseling because I want to help those with similar backgrounds. Faith and spirituality have caused tremendous harm and trauma, and healing from those things is possible. Whether you want to reconstruct your faith, create or find a spirituality suited to you and your values, or leave it all behind, you know what's best and I'm here to support you. Additionally, a lack of sex education from my church and upbringing sparked a passion to help people thrive in their sexuality. Together, we can determine your sexual ethics, explore your identities, and increase pleasure in your relationships.

name: Lisa Gascho (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I utilize various therapeutic modalities depending on the person’s needs and goals for treatment, which can include EMDR, Narrative Therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, Dialectical Behavioral Therapy, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, and others. My goal is to help people work through whatever pain they’re experiencing so that they can live in a manner that better supports their well-being and aligns with their values. This can look like helping a person process difficult life experiences in a more effective way in order to reduce the impact to them, teaching skills to grow the person’s ability to soothe and center themselves when feeling emotionally dysregulated or disconnected, and empathically challenging negative beliefs about themselves that do not reflect the full reality of who they are and their worth.
My Values as a Therapist
I’ve learned that whatever a person’s life experiences have been, they can be experienced as a crisis or a traumatic event when it exceeds the person’s capacity to cope. It is there where I like to come alongside and help the person sort out what is needed in order to heal and move forward in their life. As a therapist, I take a strengths-based approach with clients by not only working to address the issues they are seeking treatment for, but also helping them to recognize and access their own resilience, abilities, and resources; using these to address present-day challenges that they face.

name: Lauren Stines (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
The practice of observing and feeling one's feelings can build a sense of aliveness, decisiveness, clarity, compassion, and confidence in tending to the emotions that inform our thoughts, behaviors, wants, and needs. In session, you will be invited to tap into the emotional content that comes up as we discuss the topics of your life. You will be invited to use your imagination and creativity as you explore who you are and what you feel. I find that this provides opportunity for emotional processing, increased insight, developed intuition, developed decision-making skills, overall emotional awareness and wellbeing. It becomes easier to connect with others, when we build a practice of connecting to ourselves.
My Values as a Therapist
I practice from a trauma-informed perspective which enacts physical and emotional safety, choice, and transparency. You are always allowed to say “no”/”not right now” to a check-in or question I ask, though depending on the topic I may ask for clarification on what informs your “no”. So many of us have been taught that our feelings are something to push down, avoid, to apologize for, or hide from others. Feeling Space Oregon Therapy rebels against that belief and practice by creating a space where clients' feelings are valued and cherished as vital information to our lived experience as humans.

name: Congruent Therapy: Italia Hensley (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I have experience with various modalities and work with people from all walks of life. I primarily use Narrative Therapy, Humanistic approaches, Internal Family Systems, and Emotionally Focused Therapy. I also have specialized training in EFT, EFIT, IFIO, supporting LGBTQIA+, polyamorous/CNM relationships, couples, trauma survivors, and neurodivergent humans. A good fit would be someone who has the openness to take on the slow burn that therapy requires. If you’re seeking someone who is both supportive and challenging, that’s me. I'll be a secure attachment in your life that also kicks you in the butt. I work with couples, poly systems, individuals, queer and neurodivergent, using an affirmative approach with empathy, curiosity, and compassion. I am Consensual Non-Monogamy certified through The Sexual Health Alliance. I employ Emotionally Focused Couple Therapy, Narrative Therapy, IFS, and Gottman, and integrate other modalities as needed to meet clients where they are.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I believe that you are the expert of your life, but while I can give you insight and many tools, you're the most important factor to change and success. I’m a cis neurodivergent individual with fibromyalgia, juvenile rheumatoid arthritis, and ADHD. I’m a self-made business owner, supervisor, and PhD student, as well as a cat mom, partner, and a direct and sassy person. Some of the reasons I became a therapist include childhood trauma, poverty, abuse, and feeling unsafe in my body.

name: Allison Batty-Capps (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I will create an environment that enables you to feel safe, so that you may develop trust and can feel heard and seen. I understand the current neuroscience research that discusses the impact that attachment trauma, childhood trauma, and life stressors can have on the body and mind, while also understanding this from a parts perspective. I will use mindfulness and yoga to help you learn to witness your inner world with compassion and non-judgment. I will use reiki to help you care for yourself with loving kindness. I will use depth psychology and internal family systems to help you get to know yourself on a deeper level and integrate your shadow self with your inner divinity. I am a licensed therapist but I currently do not do trauma processing with clients. Please be aware that through this platform I am working as a somatic wellness coach not a therapist. I work as a therapist on Rula (through psychologytoday profile) and BetterHelp.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received my Masters in Counseling from Pacifica Graduate Institute, where I studied Jungian Psychology/Depth Psychology. I am licensed with the Oregon State Board of Marriage and Family Therapists but am currently not practicing as a mental health therapist. I am trained in mind/body therapy, somatic and mindfulness practices, Internal Family Systems informed, meditation, Yoga Therapy, and I am a reiki Master.

name: Laura Marie Separa MA LPC MFT
approaches:
As a depth-oriented psychotherapist, I blend traditional talk therapy with the creative process and invite a deepening into the unfolding story of your life.
