With the exploration of symbolism, imagery, and self expression, opportunities for healing can arise which open new pathways, both internally and externally. I incorporate a model of treatment that includes cognitive modalities, emotional regulation, stress management, coping skills, behavior modification, and ways to address the unconscious processes and how they express themselves.Through this work, a deep level of integration emerges which serves to unleash unlimited potential for optimum growth.
I received my Masters degree from Pacifica Graduate Institute with an emphasis in Depth Psychology. I have also received additional training in Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, Motivational Interviewing, Cognitive Behavioral Skills, Mindfulness Training, and Marriage and Family Therapy. While these theoretical models provide a substantial framework for my practice, my work is largely informed by the personal experience of profound grief. Journeying through these difficult waters allows me to uniquely empathize and assist my clients through life struggles and losses.

name: Hannah Boyd (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
You are multifaceted, and your experiences are unique. Together, we'll take an integrative approach to address the various symptoms and challenges you're facing. While talk therapy can be helpful on its own, I believe in enhancing the process with humor, curiosity, and collaboration to deepen your understanding of your feelings and experiences. Your ideas and creativity are always welcome as we work together. Through empowerment and exploration, you can tap into your internal resources to recognize patterns, solve problems, and cope with life's challenges.
My Values as a Therapist
I’m committed to creating an inclusive, affirming space where you can show up as your authentic self, regardless of background or identity. I recognize the significant impact of systemic and cultural factors on mental health and strive to provide therapy that’s not only supportive but also mindful of diversity, equity, and inclusion

name: Katie Eichner (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Some healing can only happen in the context of a trusting and supportive relationship, and it is crucial to find the right therapist for you. I may be the right therapist for you if you are looking for someone who is warm, transparent, friendly, genuine, and active. I am collaborative and work to tailor our work to your needs, values, and goals. It is important to me that we actively discuss and minimize power dynamics in our relationship so that we can genuinely connect. Do you struggle with self-judgment, self-criticism, or self-blame? We live in a culture that promotes these as paths to self-improvement; instead, they are paths to depression, dissatisfaction, and feeling stuck. I can help you to develop a more compassionate, supportive, accepting, and loving relationship with yourself. Healing and caring for your own wounds and trauma can help you to improve all aspects of your life and your relationships with others.
My Values as a Therapist
People do not have problems outside of context; we are all struggling to exist in a neoliberal capitalist system that is not designed to support our mental health or wellness. Helping my clients to understand how systems of oppression impact their functioning is critically important to shifting from self-blame to healing and action in support of a more just world for all. I have an anti-racist, intersectional feminist lens in my work, and social justice is deeply important to me. I work with a wide range of clients struggling with many different issues, but have a particular focus on working with trans and gender diverse clients, other members of the LGBTQ community, kinky folks, college and graduate students, and people in polyamorous relationship structures. I am also passionate about working with fellow therapists and therapists in training!

name: Jeremy Manalis (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We will explore any relevant past trauma & family of origin contributions to each of your part of the negative interactions. I will teach you new skills so you will have increased positive interactions. Our therapy will ask you to change how you relate to yourself just as much as how you relate with your partner. I use Relational Life Therapy as my primary approach.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I also offer consulting services for family-owned businesses and coaching leaders in organizations. I roamed the Sierra Nevada mountains as an Outward Bound Instructor. I have past training and experience in the healing arts as a yoga teacher and Craniosacral Therapist. I am passionate about men’s work: I have facilitated men's process groups and I have been in my own peer-led men's group for 20 years. My undergraduate degree is in Philosophy and Religion from UC Santa Barbara. I hold a Masters degree in Counseling Psychology from Lewis & Clark College. These days when I’m not working, you can usually find me hanging out with my wife and two daughters, making bad dad jokes, going for camping adventures. I enjoy taking acting & improv classes, making electronic music, cooking up a storm, walking my pups, and mountain biking. My #1 self-care is taking a hot sauna followed by a wickedly long cold plunge!

name: Jennifer Bielenberg (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a bisexual/queer therapist, I recognize that aspects of privileged and marginalized identities are both visible and invisible. I aim to create a safe environment for my clients who have experienced oppression, discrimination, and prejudice based on aspects of their identity. The spirit of my therapeutic approach is deeply relational and egalitarian, and informed by social justice and culturally responsive care. I value the context of your life and learning history, including unique aspects of your identity, adaptive coping skills and strengths, and acknowledging the harmful impacts of hostile, oppressive, and/or discriminatory experiences and systems that may have contributed to your suffering. I bring an embodied, relational presence to our sessions. I will empower you to connect to your deeper sense of knowing, learn to flow with your inner experiences, and live a meaningful life, on your terms.
Why I Became a Therapist
When I started my graduate training, I hoped that my education would teach transcendence - at the time, I secretly hoped that meant not having to feel anything uncomfortable! As my training progressed, and both past and present life struggles appeared, I employed all of my familiar strategies to fight them. The harder I worked to try and achieve my way out of my own discomfort, I experienced how unworkable that effort became, and transcendence began to take on a new meaning - letting go of the struggle for control, being in compassionate presence with difficult emotions, and allowing for deep vulnerability. As I opened up to the flow of my own experience and full humanity, I made changes I never would have thought possible at the beginning of the long road to becoming a therapist - and I'm here to help you do the same!

name: Krysta Snyder
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
With over 15 years of nursing experience, 13 of those in behavioral health, I possess a deep understanding of the complexities of psychiatric care. My journey to become a Nurse Practitioner was driven by a desire to do more for my patients and their families. I am dedicated to providing holistic, individualized care that addresses the unique needs and circumstances of each client. My approach integrates medication management with psychotherapy, empowering individuals to achieve their personal goals and enhance their well-being. I take great joy in educating my patients, believing that knowledge is power in the path towards healing and self-improvement. Through compassion, collaboration, and a commitment to excellence, I strive to make a positive impact on the lives of those I serve.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
My strong suit is providing personalized, holistic Psychiatric care. With my expertise in thorough testing, tailored evaluations, and individualized medication guidance, I can create focused treatment plans. My supportive therapy style and commitment to understanding each patient's unique circumstances position me as a great match for those seeking a psychologist who will partner with them to address challenges and improve well-being.

name: Chris Lehman
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a counselor I assist individuals, couples, and families in their search for personal and relational integration. The client- counselor relationship is designed to facilitate emotional, cognitive, and spiritual growth through open dialogue, and self exploration.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a Master of Science degree in Counseling from Portland State University, and a Master of Arts in Teaching from University of Portland. I also am a credentialed yoga and breath work instructor with thirty three years of experience. I have worked as a professional with children and families in various educational, community, and agency settings for over thirty years.

name: Destiny Bankhead MS LPC
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
The primary focus in my therapy is to provide skills for healing through diverse therapeutic practices. I strive to provide a therapeutic environment that is individually tailored to each person’s emotional, social, and psychological capabilities. I am a multi-cultural therapist with extensive experience working with diverse populations. My approach is a trauma-informed approach understanding how past trauma can have effects on current functioning and overall well-being. I strive to provide an environment of comfort and safety through connection and understanding. This non-bias, non-objective experience allows people to comfortably heal and be in the present.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have 20 years of experience in the mental health field, Licensed since 2015. Working in a variety of settings including community based crisis intensive therapy, educational/school settings, residential/group home settings, inpatient and outpatient clinical services. I have lead treatment groups ranging from sexual abuse treatment to social skills and parenting groups. I am certified in suicide prevention and intervention and have extensive experience working with individuals struggling with depression, anxiety, and social/interpersonal challenges assisting them to happiness and mindful living.

name: Sawyer Salameh (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Therapy can extend beyond the mind by incorporating the body and our experiences. This approach provides us with a more comprehensive understanding, enabling us to make more grounded and intentional choices. It helps us clarify how we want to show up and live the life we’re in.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a white, queer, nonbinary therapist, I recognize that my identity has uniquely shaped my experiences and influences how others perceive and treat me. I understand that privilege extends beyond financial stability; being part of the queer community allows me to create connections and offer support. As a nonbinary individual, I have the privilege of understanding the complexities of both masculinity and femininity. Examining the intersectionality of one's life, culture, and experiences is crucial for me to fully understand you and your place in the world. My whiteness has afforded me significant financial and safety privileges, and I am mindful of the disparities created by Western colonialism that have impacted others.

name: Kasie Gilliland (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is warm, collaborative, and grounded in understanding. I draw from methods like Attachment, Emotionally Focused Therapy, and Internal Family Systems to help you break through barriers and create lasting, positive changes in your relationships and life. I look forward to helping you work towards growth and healing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a relational therapist who values openness and trust. I believe in creating a warm and inviting environment that ensures folks with vulnerable identities to feel safe. I am passionate about working with teens, individuals and couples looking to unpack life's unexpected challenges. I specialize in working with people struggling with anxiety, depression, and ADHD. I see clients Monday-Wednesday in person or via Telehealth and Thursday mornings via telehealth

name: Simon Yugler (HE/HIM)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I specialize in psychedelic and integration therapy, and have extensive experience and training in psilocybin and ketamine-assisted psychotherapy. I have facilitated nearly 500 hours of legal psychedelic sessions with people from a vast variety of backgrounds through my work with MycoMeditations, one of the world's leading psilocybin retreats, and am trained in ketamine-assisted psychotherapy through Polaris Insight Center. I am also a psychedelic educator, and work with Inner Trek and Alma Institute-two OHA-approved Psilocybin Facilitator Training programs based in Oregon. My approach to all of my work is grounded in the tradition of depth and Jungian psychology, orienting towards the archetypal world and the transpersonal dimensions that altered states of consciousness can put us in contact with and from where we can find profound level of healing and growth. I also specialize in working with men and masculinity, relationships, finding your life purpose, depression, and anxiety.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Whether spending time with indigenous communities in Australia and Peru, guiding students on educational adventures abroad, or living on my own in the developing world, much of my life has been a journey of self discovery. I am profoundly grateful for these life experiences and how they changed me. I was led to become a psychotherapist while engaged in an immersive study of traditional plant medicine in the Peruvian Amazon. At a young age I was interested in Jungian psychology and spiritual practices. The rich body work of what is now called the “mythopoetic men’s movement” inspired me to deepen my study of archetypes, masculinity, and initiation, which laid the foundation for approach to psychology. Prior to becoming a psychotherapist I worked with National Geographic Student Expeditions and Carpe Diem Education as an experiential educator and guide, leading groups of young adults across 10 countries.

name: Greg Hertz
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I practice psychoanalytic/psychodynamic psychotherapy, working in-depth over time with careful attention to fundamental growth processes. Recurrent emotional pain is calling for us to reconnect to our true self. It is very uncomfortable to be with all of our experience, yet avoiding feeling cuts us off from meaning and movement that we need to meet suffering with grace and growth. Our defenses against the fullness of our felt experience originate in adaptive strategies to preserve connection in early relationships. Certain feelings were unsafe for us to express and we learned to hide these parts from others and even from ourselves. The psychotherapy relationship helps us question these habitual limited ways of being and find more freedom, satisfaction, and peace.
My Approach to Helping
The safety and stability of this space supports us to turn towards difficult experience with presence and curiosity. Together, we attend to the hidden by listening to intuitive ways of knowing such as feelings, associations, fantasies, dreams, and the body. In a relationship of deep acceptance and attunement, we shine light on what is happening in us at a fundamental level and have new experiences of what is possible. Greater awareness of ourselves brings a sense of wholeness and ease, a freedom to be ourselves and to realize our potential. This work is joyous and liberating. It is also difficult, demanding courage and capacity to be with the uncomfortable and unknown. The right therapy relationship can help us be with ourselves in a new way, reconnecting us with our life.

name: Dani Maier
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I am a board certified Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner (PMHNP) specializing in diagnosis and treatment of anxiety disorders, including OCD. I also treat mood disorders, ADHD, and eating disorders. I provide psychiatric evaluations, medication management, and limited therapy for patients living in Oregon and New Mexico. I believe strongly in translating evidence/science-based mental health care and collaboration to create treatment plans. I also know that people are more than their mental illness and strive to understand how each person exists within their environment and relationships. I have been in practice since 2013 and worked in rural, frontier, and urban settings. I have a Master’s in Nursing from OHSU and Doctor of Nursing Practice from New Mexico State University. I obtained my undergraduate degree from Stanford University, where I majored in Human Biology and minored in Feminist Studies.
My Approach to Helping
Each of us is a collection of stories that come from our past, our experiences, and our loved ones—at times, without our permission. Ultimately, we are the story we tell ourselves and I hope to work with people as they find the story that helps them achieve well-being and meaning. Please go to www.danimaierpmhnp.com to request an appointment

name: Michelle Shelton
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My role as a therapist is to create a safe and judgment-free environment where you feel supported and understood. My approach includes techniques from Cognitive Behavioral Family Systems Theory, Developmental Theory, and Solution Focused therapy. Overall, our sessions allow for flexibility to fit your needs.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As well as having my master's degree in Clinical Psychology, I am also a certified suicide prevention and awareness instructor and feel passionately about speaking on this. My research and experience interests include: stress, burnout, developmental theory, mental health, positive psychology, family dynamics, and suicide prevention. Additionally I have my doctorate of education with research in stress and burnout.

name: Mindful Oregon Clinic
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Your journey toward wellness deserves to be as distinctive as your story. We believe that healing is deeply personal and unique to every individual; That’s why we craft a therapeutic path tailored to your needs, goals, and experiences. ••• At Mindful Oregon Clinic, we specialize in treating a range of mental health challenges, including anxiety, depression, post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), and ADHD, while also addressing many other concerns that impact emotional and psychological well-being. ••• We understand that seeking help can feel overwhelming. Whether you're facing anxiety, trauma, relationship struggles, or navigating life's challenges, we've created a supportive and healing environment to support you. ••• Our team of experienced professionals is committed to creating a safe and supportive environment where you can explore your thoughts and feelings, develop coping strategies, and work toward achieving your personal goals. ••• Read more at https://oregon.clinic/
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
At the heart of my work as a counselor is the belief that everyone has the capacity for growth and change at any stage in their life. I strongly believe that mental health challenges often stem from learned behaviors and patterns that no longer serve us. ••• With the right tools and support, we can unlearn these behaviors and replace them with healthier, more empowering ones. My own journey with anxiety and panic attacks taught me the value of resilience and the transformative power of therapy. Today, I’m passionate about helping others find the same freedom and peace I’ve experienced. ••• I strive to make a positive impact through fostering meaningful connections, supporting personal growth, and advocating for mental wellness. I believe in creating a judgment-free, supportive space where individuals from all walks of life can feel safe, understood, and empowered to take control of their mental health journey. ••• Dr. Shirin Yekekar • CEO/Clinical Director, Mindful Oregon Clinic

name: Rachel Spooner (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My first priority in approaching our work together is to build a therapeutic relationship that feels safe for you to express your full self, feelings and experiences. This relationship is the foundation of our work together in exploring and reaching your goals. Wherever you are at, I will strive to meet you there and support you in building connection with yourself and others on your path to emotional wellbeing. When you are ready, we will work together to explore your thoughts, feelings, challenges, and goals. My therapeutic approach includes a combination of cognitive-behavioral therapy techniques, narrative therapy techniques and motivational interviewing. I have training in Exposure and Response Prevention, an evidence-based treatment for those with Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder, as well as Dialectical Behavioral Therapy, a skill-based treatment that equips individuals with a wide variety of tools to effectively regulate their emotions and increase mindfulness.
My Values as a Therapist
I am a White, cisgender female. I grew up in the Portland area and I am very fond of the Pacific Northwest. I received my Master's degree in Social Work in June of 2021 and have experience working with youth in various settings in the mental health care field. I also have a background in working with and advocating for survivors of domestic violence. I value a strengths-based perspective and creating therapeutic and supportive relationships that allow others to feel heard, accepted and validated. I am committed to pursuing social justice and work to maintain a systems-based perspective.

name: Living Well Counseling Center
approaches:
Our Background and Approach
Our founder, Emily Brems is a Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist who supervises a team of caring and compassionate interns at Living Well. We specialize in supporting couples, families, adolescents, and children, as well as assisting clients dealing with depression, anxiety, self-esteem, sexual abuse, and developing healthy sexuality. We are passionate about helping people restore peace in their relationships by building upon the foundations of love and trustworthiness.
Why We Started this Group Practice
We are centered on helping you build skills to love and live well. Living Well Counseling Center provides personalized care for each individual, couple, and family. Our work is focused on empowering you to achieve a high level of emotional regulation and mindfulness to produce meaningful and lasting change in your life and relationships.

name: Vicki Templeton (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Most people come to therapy because they want relief-- from pain, loneliness, anger, shame, etc. My training has taught me theories and techniques, and I've also learned just how hard it is to be a person sometimes! Sitting with you in your pain is one of the rare experiences that people get in the space of a therapy room. I value bringing you closer to your emotions. Clients have the ongoing decision of what to do with emotional pain. When you're ready, we'll give some attention to the pain, feel it, grieve it, and then heal from it. Therapy often involves letting go of the fantasy of creating a better past. Changing our relationship to the past is a staple of this work. There is no redo of what's happened to us, yet we can learn to transform the pain into something different -- something that can be carried better. With all clients, I serve as a gentle guide. I strive to lead with a balance of warmth, honesty, curiosity, humor, challenge and compassion.

name: Cedar Hills Outpatient Services
approaches:
Our Approach to Helping
We are not all in the same boat, but we are all in the same stormy ocean. Here, we recognize a need for versatility in treatment. We offer Intensive Outpatient (IOP) and Partial Hospitalization (PHP, aka, Day Treatment) levels of care. We have in-person and virtual options, as well as morning, afternoon, or evening groups. Programs are typically 6-8 weeks long. While enrolled, you will have access to the program's psychiatric provider for medication management. Group program options vary and currently include mental health focus co-occurring mental health and substance use disorders, health and wellness, DBT skills based, and women's group treatments. Our group therapists utilize skills in crisis intervention, safety planning, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Dialectical Behavioral Therapy, Motivational Interviewing, Narrative Therapy, Internal Family Systems, Acceptance & Commitment Therapy, and more. We specialize in treating depression, anxiety, bipolar, trauma, addictions, and more.
Our Personal Beliefs and Interests
All of our therapists have at least one thing in common: We are all human. We appreciate authenticity and accept you as you are right now. We believe in compassion, kindness, and strengths. We also believe that life is really hard sometimes, and we can support you through it! Our group program therapists have a wide range of professional backgrounds and experiences ranging from work in youth programs, shelters, crisis hotlines, psychiatric hospitals, domestic violence, addiction and recovery, medical social work, child welfare, individual therapy, schools, donation centers, the Peace Corps, and more. We also have a lot of fun and different interests. We're all a bit different, and we love it that way.

name: Dr. Chandra Mundon (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Many clients have described my clinical style as "real and authentic." I have extensive training and clinical experience in a number of different therapeutic modalities such as Time Limited Dynamic Psychotherapy, DBT/CBT/ACT, Narrative Therapy, and Emotionally Focused Therapy to name a few. Based on your needs and preferences, I offer short-term (accelerated) therapy , as well as longer-term "depth therapy."
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I enjoy working with clients on a wide variety of issues including, but not limited to: depression, anxiety, ADHD, perfectionism, self-esteem, family-of-origin, cultural identity, codependency, stress, etc. As an addiction specialist, I provide assessment and treatment for those struggling with alcohol and/or drugs, as well as those struggling with "process addictions" (eg: internet, gaming, gambling, etc.) I have specialized in the provision of gender affirming care for transgender and gender diverse individuals for over 10 years and am very passionate about this work. I provide consultation and supportive therapy for adolescents and adults who are in the early stages of exploring/questioning their gender identity, as well as assessment and letter-writing for clients pursuing hormones and/or surgery.

name: Osleidy Soria-Pons (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Building a strong therapeutic relationship based on authenticity, trust, respect, and genuine connection is essential to my approach. Through our work together, we will attend to your body and your mind – the whole you. We are a sum of all our parts and our healing should seek to integrate us and make us feel more whole. I use mindfulness, person-centered practices and other evidence-based approaches to offer support for a wide range of issues, including anxiety, grief, systemic racism, identity exploration, and intergenerational trauma. I hope to guide you as you unlock and magnify the resilience and wisdom within you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I became a therapist to create a space where Black and Brown individuals can find liberation and healing. Growing up as an Afro-Latina immigrant, I witnessed the deep impact of systemic racism, discrimination, and oppression on marginalized communities. These experiences shaped my desire to provide culturally-responsive therapy that honors and validates the unique struggles and resilience of BIPOC individuals. I believe in the power of therapy not just to cope but to dismantle oppressive narratives and systems. My practice is rooted in social justice, equity, and the belief that everyone deserves to be seen, valued, and supported in their healing journey.

name: Evan Stuart (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
A fellow therapist told me, “People want an experience, not an explanation”. The ideal client will have an experience of warmth when meeting me, a feeling of being listened to, feeling safe, empowered in authenticity, and building trust toward your healing in a therapeutic relationship. All these qualities are at the core of who I am as a therapist. We will celebrate your strengths and authenticity. I will listen to your experiences and then address where you want to change. I have over 1,500 hours of meditation teacher training to share tools in open awareness, stress and anxiety reduction, and help your growth toward releasing unhelpful thought patterns. I also specialize in relationships and family systems, and help you grow into a stronger, wiser self.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Individuals can move toward self-regulation when provided with safety and warmth. Both your strength and confidence grow through identifying where change is possible and recognizing where it is potentially necessary to let go. Our work together can help you to make that distinction. I have a deep commitment to mindfulness and work consistently to be of service in several different spiritual communities: some Buddhist, some secular. Many things that may have happened to you can be reframed moving forward with an alliance in your healing. May you find the right person to open your healing potential, and it will be an honor if you trust me with your story.

name: Darla Harrison (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Counseling can benefit anyone, but often it can be difficult to find a good counselor. My approach is respectful, non-judgmental and genuine. I support you and build upon your strengths to work toward positive change. I promote skill building and see the counseling relationship as a collaboration. Initiating change requires effort and my role is to support and encourage you in your efforts. I provide individual counseling. My specialties include anxiety, depression, self esteem, relationships, adjustment, trauma, family issues (parenting, divorce, conflict, relationships, etc.), women's issues (relationships, stress, trauma, adjustment, etc.). All of my counseling sessions are provided via teletherapy. Not only is it flexible, but also convenient, professional, and confidential. Call to see if I may be a good fit. I look forward to hearing from you!

name: Emily Palmgren (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Background
I hold a Master’s of Arts in Marriage, Couple, and Family Therapy with a specialization in Sex Therapy from Lewis & Clark Graduate School of Education and Counseling. I have clinical training in sexual and relational concerns like sexual difficulties, trauma, desire/arousal differences within partnerships, gender and sexuality expression, nonmonogamous and polyamorous relationships, and pain during sex. I completed a year-long internship with M.E.T.A. Counseling Clinic where I received extensive supervision, training in mindfulness, the treatment of trauma, anti-racism in counseling, supporting activists, and early childhood attachment. I worked with individuals and partnerships around a variety of concerns including anxiety, depression, communication skills, sex concerns, and trauma processing. At M.E.T.A., I co-facilitated a support group called PolyCurious focused on affirming and exploring the emotional attachment responses nonmonogamy can spur.
My Values as a Therapist
Working from a stance that promotes social justice is integral to my practice. In my mission to be a trauma-conscious provider, I prioritize client consent and safety. I am a kink/BDSM, polyam-, LGBTQIA+ affirming, body-positive, and continually learning anti-racist care provider. I am a white, Queer/Poly human working in Portland, OR (occupied land of the Clackamas and Cowlitz). My experiences in queer and multi-partnered relationships have been both challenging and profoundly healing. I am passionate about working with my community, and celebrate the full spectrum of relationships - from monogamy to relationship anarchy. I help people find resonant relationship practices for their lives centered in equity, consent, and compassion. I currently live in Corvallis (occupied land of the Kalapuya). When I’m not in session, I enjoy connecting with my loved ones, dancing, eating good food, and being in nature.

name: Amelia O'Neil (SHE/HER OR THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
If you’ve been impacted by painful experiences in childhood, know that you don’t have to face it alone. Together, we can work through the challenges, release the burdens of the past, and help you move forward with a sense of empowerment and hope. I believe that recovery is possible, and that every person has the strength to heal and rebuild with compassionate support. I work collaboratively with my clients to process painful memories, build self-compassion, and develop new tools for coping and resilience. Healing takes time, and I’m here to walk with you every step of the way.

name: Maryke Van Zyl
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I love helping growth-oriented adults address trauma, relationship difficulties, chronic stress, anxiety, and depression by building tangible tools and mindfulness-based skills. I use strategies informed by research to support healing and self-discovery, tailoring care to each client's needs. Therapy works best when it’s collaborative and supportive. Together, we’ll explore the root causes of your challenges and build practical skills to reach your goals. Between sessions, you’ll track patterns and apply tools we develop, fostering growth and resilience. My approach empowers you to create lasting, meaningful changes in your life.
My Values as a Therapist
As an immigrant, I appreciate the unique experiences of first- and second-generation immigrants and work with clients from diverse backgrounds with a culturally sensitive, respectful approach grounded in attachment research, neuroscience, and empirically supported modalities.

name: Kelsey Zettle
approaches:
Our Background and Approach
Our experienced and compassionate team is dedicated to supporting your mental well-being. We offer a range of evidence based therapy services tailored to your unique needs. Whether you're struggling with anxiety, depression, relationship issues, or life transitions, we are here to help. Take the first step towards a healthier mind and happier life. Contact us to schedule an appointment or to learn more about the services we offer or use the link below to request an appointment and one of our team members will reach out shortly to help match you to one of our practitioners.

name: Daniel Hofmann (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have a Masters degree in Couples and Family Counseling from Portland State University and experience working with children, teens, couples, families, and individuals in community mental health and private practice settings. My approach to counseling is trauma-informed and integrative, drawing on my knowledge of a variety of therapeutic models while being rooted in humanistic, psychodynamic, and emotionally-focused psychotherapies. I believe that we all naturally have many facets, or parts, of ourselves that help us navigate our complicated world. When these parts are balanced, we feel good and function well in relationships. Stress and trauma push our innate mechanisms for healing and coping into extremes. Many mental health symptoms can be viewed as counterproductive attempts at restoring our natural equilibrium. I will create a space in which we can safely explore the origins of your difficulties and help you find a new sense of balance in the present.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I work from a non-pathologizing and non-judgmental stance. I believe that all people have a core of good and wellness within them; even those who have it buried very deep down. When you view things from a systems perspective, concepts of fault and blame start to lose their meaning. I have yet to encounter a person whose feelings and actions didn't make sense after taking a holistic view of them. I will strive to understand and build your own awareness of who you are. I will be compassionate when challenging you to try something new. I will find and amplify the good in you, even when all you can feel is bad.

name: Dave Hires (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I graduated with a Master of Arts in Professional Mental Health Counseling—Specialization in Addictions from Lewis & Clark Graduate School of Education and Counseling in Portland in 2021, where I also lived for about twenty years. I am trained in EMDR as well as the Flash Technique. I am a Certified Alcohol and Drug Counselor (II), a Certified Clinical Trauma Professional and Advanced Grief Counseling Specialist.
My Approach to Helping
It depends on what’s feeling important! If you'd like, we could explore the role of trauma and wounds in your life and work together to gain a sense of safety and agency around these feelings. If you are then interested in deeper work, we could do some processing via EMDR or the Flash Technique, both evidence-based interventions that can heal wounds in a way that talk therapy alone can't do. Regardless - you set the pace. If your substance use is a concern for you, we could get curious about the needs you are meeting through your use, and whether you would like additional ways to get those needs met. We could work with your grief, working towards ways to remain connected to what you've lost, navigating feelings you're not sure if you're supposed to feel ("I feel sadness - and relief" or "I just feel angry"). Or maybe you're like most people - our grief, our trauma, our tendencies toward compulsive or addictive behaviors - are all interwoven. We can work on that too, at your pace.

name: Dean Sanchez
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In 2018, I made a career change after 15 years as a carpenter, returned to school and became a Registered Behavior Technician working with children diagnosed with Autism. I have since then completed a Bachelors in Psychology, and a Masters in Clinical Mental Health Counseling. Having first experienced therapy as a client myself and then dedicating my life to helping others including Clinical Mental Health Counseling with adults and Behavioral therapy with children, my main focus and interests have been geared to helping people effectively cope with major trauma, depression/anxiety, along with unexpected phobias and behaviors. As a person coming from very humble beginnings, I have geared my clinical focus to be involved with as many populations as possible throughout the United States. Having traveled and worked with a multitude of varying age groups, specialties, and populations in changing locations, I have become a very understanding, effective, and creative clinician.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
With advances in Telehealth style communication, knowledge of mental health needs in today's society, and understanding cultural diversity from one location to another and how it effects mental health needs, there are a multitude of reasons that each one of us can benefit from clinical mental health counseling. Whether there are ongoing and exhausting mental health symptoms, or just a check-in for mental hygiene and discussion about concerns happening in the world today, an effective clinical mental health counseling session can give individuals the advantage of increased self awareness, coping, and a reserved safe haven of support for life's challenges and goals. As a dedicated Clinical Mental Health Counselor, I will make every effort to make counseling sessions as effective and useful as possible for achievement of one's best self in all aspects of life.

name: Dani Dierking (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I firmly believe that all people are unique and all people are worthy of care. I feel honored everyday to create a space for others to move through the tender process of therapy. My aim is to help folks build new relationships with pain and suffering, at the same time that they invite in presence, connection, meaning, and creativity. Through the blend of art therapy and talk therapy, my practice might offer you the missing puzzle piece you need to access this important work for yourself. Read on and reach out if this feels right to you
My Approach to Helping
Within a couple of sessions, you will probably learn how open and engaged I am with what you are bringing into the room. I truly believe that you are the expert on how our work unfolds, even if you don’t feel that’s true. I will often offer choices like “Do we want to follow this thread around your work frustrations?” or “Do we want to return to this recent art in progress exploring your relationship with your partner?” When we start getting into the weeds of processing, I have favorite approaches to bring into the mix alongside art therapy, especially Gestalt therapy, Internal Family Systems (IFS), and Compassion Focused therapy. I love offering the nerdy science behind mental health, often pulling nuggets from Interpersonal Neurobiology, Attachment Theory, and Polyvagal Theory. I am continuously assessing, exploring, and re-adjusting to fine-tune your therapy experience so you leave each session feeling safe, seen, and supported.

name: Congruent Therapy: Alexandra Scott (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Whether you're an individual navigating your own path, a couple facing challenges, or part of a poly system exploring the depths of connection, I'm here to support you every step of the way. I understand that each person's journey is unique, which is why I take an individualized approach to therapy. Together, we'll explore your identity, life choices, and any societal norms that may be holding you back from living authentically. At the core of my practice are values like boundary-setting, communication, and collaborative problem-solving. I don't just talk the talk—I walk alongside you, modeling healthy behaviors and providing you with the tools you need to build fulfilling relationships and lead a genuine life. I'm all about tailoring therapy to fit you like a glove. Whether you're a couple, an individual, or part of a poly system, I recognize that your experiences and needs are as unique as you are. In my toolkit, you'll find a diverse range of evidence-based modalities.
More About the People We Treat
From Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) to Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Trauma-Focused Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (TF-CBT) to Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), `Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT), yoga, mindfullness, and more, I've got you covered. But it's not just about having a bunch of fancy techniques up my sleeve—it's about knowing when and how to use them to best serve you. That's where my expertise comes in. By integrating these modalities, I can effectively address a wide variety of concerns, from navigating tricky emotions to overcoming past traumas and everything in between. So, whether you're looking to untangle the knots in your relationship, conquer those inner demons, or simply find more joy and fulfillment in life, I'm here to help you.

name: Aurora Counseling (Group Practice) (SHE/HER,HE/HIM,THEY/THEM)
approaches:
Our Approach to Helping
At Aurora Counseling, our BIPOC clinicians meet you from a place of shared experience and understanding. We offer targeted trauma therapy, including EMDR, and Somatic Experiencing, to unpack childhood, relationship, and racial trauma, and help you show up in the life that you have now as a more grounded, self-assured version of yourself. You'll learn to feel more present, confident, and alive. We already believe that you deserve better, and we'll help you believe it too.

name: Irma E. Llanes (SHE/HER/ELLA)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
While I have adopted an approach that is collaborative, I approach you, my client from a strength- based perspective. You are the expert in your life, and with my guidance and support, I trust that you can discover your path to a healthier, more satisfying life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I bring over 20 years experience promoting wellness and the work of healing.  I specialize in working with women, children, adolescents, couples and families.  I have extensive experience in a multitude of issues including working with emotional trauma, emotional difficulties related to pregnancy, the postpartum period, personal relationship changes, marriage, parenting, behavioral problems, domestic violence, depression, anxiety, foster care, life transitions, acculturation, stress reduction and personal growth. My academic accomplishments include an MA degree in Counseling Psychology, with an emphasis in Child-Adolescent Psychology, from Lewis & Clark College, and a BA in Clinical Psychology with a minor in Child Development from San Jose State University. I am bilingual (English & Spanish speaker) and bicultural.

name: Brittany England
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
The integrative Mind-Body-Spirit approach I offer allows us to navigate the subconscious mind in order to help you have a different experience of your past and present so you can create a better future. I aim to assist clients with creating safety in their bodies, utilizing somatic based approaches. Our beliefs about ourselves, our self-worth, whether or not we are lovable and worthy, our relationships with money, success, love, partnership, family, health, body image, self-esteem, etc. are all programmed by a very young age, which means that we are operating from an outdated belief system inside of us. Because of this, it is crucial to gain access to the subconscious programs we carry to bring awareness and transformation to the roots of the challenges we face in one or more areas of our lives. Since roughly 95% of your thoughts and beliefs are dictated by the subconscious mind, it is important to access the information in there that is no longer serving you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My integrative Mind-Body-Spirit approach is influenced by spirituality, EFT (meridian tapping), mindfulness, polyvagal theory, NLP, neuroscience & attachment theory. I have worked with clients in varying capacities in the field of mental health since 1998. I specialize in working with anxiety, depression, stress, sobriety from addictions, codependency, chronic illness & pain, relationship issues, food & body image, sexuality, loss of purpose & fulfillment, life transitions, spiritual awakenings, and women's issues. I practice from a somatic, spiritual, depth-oriented, relational, trauma-informed lens. I have extensive experience working with other therapists, counselors, healers, and practitioners who are also on the journey of growth, transformation, and healing. My intention with folks who have chronic illness, pain, or any recurring patten in one's life, is to facilitate acceptance of one's experience and to explore potential root causes through a mind-body-spirit lens.

name: Robin Carlisle (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a result of seeking comes illumination. Both the bright and the shadow parts of your being come clearly into your awareness. But fear not, oh capable one, You will never be alone in this work. It is with my strong intuition, great admiration and total belief in your natural abilities, that you are shepherded with unwavering confidence and impeccable care through this powerful work. It is through kindness, compassion and an open curiosity that we able to heed the call of your heart and respond in a way that is in your highest benefit. It is my belief that you have the answers you need. It is my honor to help you find them!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
20 plus years in counseling. Certified Consulting Hypnotist, National Hypnotist Guild, 2020. Northwest Hypnosis Institute - Portland Oregon, 2019. MHAP Multidisciplinary Healing Arts  Practitioner. Year long Intensive Study under the instruction of Dr. Mindy Nettifee, Doctor of Depth Psychology, with an emphasis in Somatic studies - Individual instruction in the areas of: Eco-Psychology, Somatic Practices, Depth Psychology, Jungian Philosophy, Dream Analysis, Archetypal Studies, Shamanic Rites and Ritual, Extensive exploration in cultivating my personal healing gifts - Heart Medicine, 2018. CBTP, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy Practitioner Certification, Institute of Achology, 2016. HMC, HeartMath Facilitator Certification, HeartMath Institute, 2014. Adolescent Treatment Substance Abuse Counselor, 1999-2001. Alcohol, Tobacco, and Other Drug Health Educator, 1997-1999.

name: Ken Steinbacher (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I approach our work from a narrative therapy perspective. As its name implies, we will work on your story and how we define our lives through our learned history. Narrative therapy begins with the understanding that you are the expert on your life; my job is to help you look at that story. Life is hard and blame and shame are not a solution. I do not assign diagnoses unless a client finds it useful. This quote from Michael White, a founder of narrative therapy, encapsulates my stance: “The person is not the problem, the problem is the problem.” You have many skills, competencies, beliefs, values, commitments and abilities… let’s explore those! Let’s be curious together. In our work, healing is often found in a new perspective or new understanding of the story we create to define who we are. My hope is that we can build an awareness of the systems around us and how they affect our perception and the tools to a continued experience of your authentic self.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I’ve lived in Portland for 25 years and love being a part of this community. I am a graduate of Portland State University with a master’s degree in clinical mental health counseling and have completed internships at William Temple House and the PSU Community Counseling Clinic. I identify as a queer, nonbinary, white, able-bodied person and as such I know both privilege and marginalization and bring this to our work.. I have a background in fine arts and enjoy painting in my free time. I'm an avid hiker but think of myself as more the indoorsy type. I am a licensed professional counselor. My clinical focus includes gender and gender transition, sexuality, racial identity, anxiety, depression, trauma, and personal growth. I welcome people of all identities.

name: Helene Hanson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I use play, parent-child dyad modalities, and an attachment-focused, trauma informed approach to my work with children and families. I seek to create a space that is warm, respectful and safe for children to use the language they are most fluent (play!) to share their hard-to-share feelings. In play therapy sessions, parents are integral part of the process. We will develop tools that help your child manage their feelings, overcome fears, and improve relationships. I also offer parent coaching and parenting support groups for parents and caregivers of neurodiverse children and teens/young adults. Before I became a therapist I was a long-time early childhood educator and inclusion specialist. I have completed training in Circle of Security, Theraplay and Resources for Infant Educators. I am both an adoptee and an adoptive parent and I love working with adoptive families and supporting adoptees in navigating the murky waters of being adopted.

name: Clara Villalobos Andino (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I focus on trauma work, supporting new parents, addressing women’s issues, and providing couples and family therapy. I work with a diverse community of clients, always focusing on creating a safe, inclusive, and supportive space for healing and growth. I am direct and compassionate in my approach—I won’t provide the answers, but I will help you discover them. Whether working with individuals, couples, or families, I believe in the power of collaboration, empathy, and respect for each client’s unique lived experience. I approach therapy from a narrative and emotionally focused lens, guiding clients to explore and reframe their life stories while deepening their emotional connections and understanding of their experiences. I also offer walk-and-talk therapy, integrating the environment as a natural part of our sessions. In addition, I use Mentalization-Based Narrative Exposure Therapy (MBNET), a powerful method for processing trauma.
My Values as a Therapist
Originally from Argentina, I migrated to California as a teenager and have a rich background in agriculture, both as an educator and a small-scale farm owner. My experiences in farming deepened my appreciation for the interconnectedness of our humanity with the environments we inhabit and how these relationships shape us throughout our lives. This perspective informs my therapeutic practice and enriches the work I do with clients. I have a Masters in family, couples, and marriage therapy with a focus on ecopsychology.

name: Peter Addy (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
With a PhD in Clinical Psychology and over 15 years of experience of research and clinical work, I bring both academic expertise and personal insight to my work with chronic pain and health challenges. I'm a licensed counselor in OR and WA, with specialized training in ketamine-assisted psychotherapy and psilocybin facilitation. Living with fibromyalgia has given me a deep awareness of how chronic illness can affect every aspect of life. This informs my integrated approach, which combines evidence-informed practices with innovative healing modalities. I am dedicated to helping clients find new ways to live meaningfully. My practice is explicitly psychedelic-affirming, offering preparation and integration support for legal experiences through Oregon Psilocybin Services and ketamine therapy. I create safe spaces for transformation where clients can explore their relationship with pain, connect with their inner wisdom, and develop practical tools for living fully.
Social Justice Issues I Care About
I approach healthcare through a class-based analysis, recognizing that psychological distress and healthcare access are fundamentally shaped by capitalism and economic exploitation. As someone living with chronic illness, I understand how profit-driven healthcare creates and perpetuates suffering while limiting access to healing resources. My practice explicitly affirms all gender identities, sexual orientations, relationship structures, and body sizes. I work to create welcoming spaces that honor diverse experiences and challenge traditional medical models that have historically marginalized many communities. While maintaining cultural humility as a White, cisgender, educated practitioner, I recognize that effective anti-racist, feminist, and disability justice work must address the material conditions and class relations that underpin systemic oppression. I work toward collective liberation by combining direct support for clients with advocacy for revolutionary changes.

name: April Dvorak
approaches:
My Background
April Dvorak, PMHNP-BC, is a Board-Certified Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner and the founder of Head Well – Integrative Psychiatry, a mental health practice based in Eugene Oregon. She helped found and serves as the President of Projects on Purpose Inc (POPI), a nonprofit serving the seriously mentally ill. A native of Houston, Texas, April started her academic journey at Texas A&M University, graduating in 2009 with a Bachelor of Science in Biomedical Sciences. She continued her education at the University of Texas Medical Branch at Galveston, where she studied undergraduate nursing in 2011. She worked as a nurse in the Neonatal ICU and then various psychiatric settings before returning to obtain her Master of Science in Nursing and board certification from the ANCC as a Psychiatric and Mental Health Nurse Practitioner in 2017.
My Approach to Helping
After years of practicing in the public sector and clunky, bureaucratic institutions, April wanted to build a model based on efficiency and long-term, sustainable solutions. She believes in making sense of things and working through tough problems. April started Head Well – Integrative Psychiatry in hopes of doing both. Her clinical approach is integrative and functionally rooted. She is fascinated by how personality structure and emotional and behavior patterns account for much of how we view and interact with the world. People co-create their realities, and understanding oneself is the key to a cohesive, productive, and meaningful existence. April is passionate about mental health advocacy and maintaining humanity, rationality, and integrity in psychiatry and social services. She loves to learn and recently obtained certificate from Integrative Psychiatric Institute (IPI) as a Ketamine Medical Provider. She was recently awarded Eugene’s top 20 under 40.

name: Graham Borgman (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
My Background
I am a Licensed Professional Counselor and hold a Master's degree in counseling psychology with an emphasis in depth psychology from Pacifica Graduate Institute. Prior to my current independent practice, I worked as a therapist in a group practice, provided psychotherapy to adults in a community mental health center, and also have professional experience working as a counselor in a semi-residential psychiatric program for adults.
More About the People I Treat
I work well with individuals who are curious about their own minds and emotions. Individuals who can benefit from working with me are those who wish to understand and move through their suffering, as well as to enjoy the vitality that the healing process can bring. Reasons for seeking therapy which I work well with include: Anxiety & stress, depression, grief, relationship issues, existential concerns, difficulty with self-expression, feelings of guilt & shame, self criticism & self-esteem issues, anger issues, life transitions, many more: just ask!

name: Michael Vallejo, LCSW (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapeutic approach is non-judgmental and collaborative. I utilize strengths-based and goal-oriented strategies to help children and teens to better manage behavioral and mental health difficulties, as well as work with parents and families to improve family dynamics. I enjoy helping children and teens develop the skills necessary to effectively manage their emotions, maintain positive relationships, and develop themselves in all areas of their lives.

name: Robin Magdahlen
approaches:
How I Can Help You
Each person, couple and family are unique. I include a blend of conventional and alternative approaches, drawing upon a variety of styles and techniques to incorporate what would be most helpful for you. With sensitivity and compassion, I enable you to build upon your strengths and to identify and achieve your life goals. Some of these modalities include but are not exclusive to EFT Couples, Attachment Theory, EMDR, Interpersonal Nuerobiology.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have had a background in working with marginalized populations including incarcerated women, crisis intervention, abused and neglected animals, elders and special needs children. I believe strongly in Equity. My intention is to be an agent of healing and change one person, one couple, one family at a time, so that we move closer to being part of a greater human family. My Specialties Include: Telehealth - Tele-Therapy, Couples Counseling, Divorce Coaching, Families — Adoption/Divorce/Blended Families, Adult Adoptee/Birth Parent Reunification, Pre — Marital Counseling, Depression, Anxiety, Grief/Loss, EMDR, Crisis Intervention and Stabilization, Mediation, Substance Abuse, Animal Bereavment, Positive Aging, Senior Life Transitions, Mediation, Individual and Group Drama Therapy.

name: Nova (Stephenie) Knutson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My work uses mindfulness and a body oriented approach to foster palpable positive changes in the quality of the experiences you are having day to day. As your experience of life changes for the better, behavior, feelings and actions naturally change with it. My clients say that I am kind, warm and gentle but also willing to be direct. Therapy with me is effective and deep and also frequently joyful and filled with laughter.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a certified Hakomi Therapist and Teacher, I have more than a decade of experience and the skills to promote lasting change. I have extensive training and experience helping clients regain access to a sense of safety and possibility through somatic trauma resolution work. I also specialize in attachment oriented work including training and experience in both CIMBS and AEDP. Attachment therapy operates on the understanding that, because infants need their caregivers for survival, very young children learn at an implicit, unspoken level, which aspects of self will be welcomed, tolerated or rejected by our caregivers. We carry that unspoken "rulebook" forward into future relationships, often with painful results. These painful templates remain in play until we have experiences that help them change. Working directly with the attachment system, challenging the unspoken rules, can free us up to create fuller and more authentic relationships with others and within ourselves.

name: Becca Stock (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use EMDR and somatic-based techniques to process trauma and desensitize triggers that fuel substance use. Ego-state therapy helps you connect with and heal different parts of yourself. For anxiety, trauma, mood disorders, and addiction, I use evidence-based approaches to shift patterns and improve coping strategies. Sessions are collaborative, safe, and focused on building resilience and helping you live authentically.

name: Mimosa Constans (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My role as your therapist is to provide you space and support with attunement, compassion, and empathy so that you can put burdens down, and rest your weary heart. My goal is for you to feel safe to express your needs and vulnerabilities that you've buried for so long. In the process of uncovering the foundation you were built on, we can identify the spaces of your mind and heart that need healing. Highlighting your natural strengths and drawing from your inner compass to help us navigate our therapeutic journey together.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I'm a fully inclusive therapist and support all communities. I know how it feels to not quite “fit in”. I've lived in a world of many cultural influences and learned that we are all longing for love, acceptance, meaningful connection, and autonomy no matter where started or how we got here. Outside of therapy I am a wife, mother, and self proclaimed foodie. I love quality time with my husband, four amazing daughters and our pack of (4) dogs.

name: August Wagner (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
A core foundation of my approach is the belief that you are the expert on yourself. I will approach you with compassion, curiosity, and some expertise of my own, but healing and growth will ultimately come from within you and the relationships in your life. Counseling in my view is about coming to understand ourselves more wholly and with less judgment in order to live our lives with greater authenticity and love. I believe that every challenge is made more manageable when faced alongside someone else. It would be my honor to stand with you as you transform whatever is weighing you down into the steps leading to a better life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
It is my belief that human beings are both wonderfully unique and mercifully similar to one another. We are connected to each other in our individuality through the common strands of what it means to be a person trying to live our best lives in a world full of uncertainty, pain, beauty, and love. My approach to counseling reflects the uniqueness of each person’s individual life story, sociocultural context, trauma, and inner resources, while also tapping into the wisdom of our shared human condition and the immense power of relationships and community. I strive to strike a balance between the uniqueness of self and the commonality of humankind through an organic, authentic approach that draws on evidence-based practices and tools while bearing in mind that any tool or idea only really works if it works for you.

name: Veronica Marquez (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I aim to create safe and supportive spaces where we can explore difficult emotions and experiences together. My hope is to help you gain perspective on the stressors in your life and develop effective ways to navigate them. I value a strengths-based and collaborative approach, so that we can build on the abilities and resilience you already have. My work is informed by a variety of therapeutic techniques, including relational therapy, narrative therapy, mindfulness, and attachment theory. I also have training in skill-based interventions including dialectical behavior therapy (DBT) and cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT). Whether you are dealing with anxiety, depression, stress, life transitions, or other challenges, I’m here to support you.
My Values as a Therapist
As a Mexican-American woman, I have a particular focus on supporting BIPOC and LGBTQIA+ individuals, however, I am open to working with clients from all backgrounds. I earned my Master of Social Work degree from Portland State University in June 2021 and am committed to promoting social justice, advocating for equitable access to resources, and empowering those who have been historically marginalized. I have provided mental health services in outpatient and school settings and have experience working within the foster care and child welfare systems. This background has provided me with a systems-based lens to understand and address the complex needs of individuals and communities as a therapist today.

name: Katie Azarow (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am fairly eclectic in my orientation to therapy. I believe strongly in identifying what works for each client and tailoring therapy based upon such. With that being said, my personal goal in session is to ensure my clients are able to identify their progress as it is being made, and to ensure my clients are able to see value in their therapeutic work. I understand the value of my client's time and thus, work to always remain keenly aware of each clients personal time commitment to therapy; tailoring interventions and therapeutic approach to meet these needs.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a licensed clinical social worker in Oregon and Washington. I earned my Master of Social Work degree from University of Nebraska, Omaha, and have been involved in the helping profession since 2007. Over that time, I have worked to build a vast array of experiences ranging from survivors of domestic violence, to those struggling with addiction issues and trauma, to individuals making life transitions, as well as extensively working with adolescents facing anxiety, depression, adjustment issues, self-esteem and issues related to personal identification. I am most passionate about furthering my work with teens and young adults.

name: Emily Rose (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to healing is rooted in the knowing that you are already whole. This work isn’t about fixing you, it’s about creating space for what’s already within you to come forward: your clarity, your strength, your truth. What makes this work different is the depth. We don’t stop at surface-level coping. We go to the root; into the unconscious layers where real transformation happens. Where other approaches may focus on managing symptoms, this work helps you shift the patterns, beliefs, and internal narratives that have shaped your life. We’re not just talking about change; we’re rewiring it. Each session is designed specifically for you. I pull from a wide range of tools: hypnosis, somatic healing, parts integration, energy work, and NLP to meet you exactly where you are and guide you toward what’s next.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
Choosing someone to walk with you on your healing path is personal. You’re not just looking for credentials, you’re looking for someone who sees you clearly, honors where you’ve been, and knows how to hold space for where you’re going. I don’t take that lightly. You think you need something new—but what you really need is to remember something within you that was never lost, just buried. You’re not broken. You’ve been carrying too much for too long, and somewhere along the way, you disconnected from a part of yourself that’s still here… and ready to return. Healing begins when we create space for that remembering. If you’ve felt unseen or misunderstood in other healing spaces, know this: that won’t happen here. I work with the patterns underneath; ancestral wounds, relational trauma, and the unconscious beliefs shaping your life. Together, we meet what’s been waiting. And we begin again, with you at the center.

name: ariel reiner (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My work is informed by bioenergetics, spiritual psychology, and a relational, solutions-oriented approach. In my work with couples, I lean into the Hakomi principles of mindfulness, organicity, non-violence, body/mind holism, and unity. I am passionate about supporting people in becoming more fully embodied.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Many of us want the same things: to feel safe, to love and be loved, to be seen, heard and supported, and to be at home in our bodies. However, being a human is complicated, and when presented with more than is manageable, our bodies' innate intelligence takes over and holds onto tough experiences for us. This is where somatic psychotherapy can help.

name: Davonna Wilson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I will listen to your concerns with empathy and earn your trust so we can establish an effective individualized treatment plan. While medications can be prescribed if needed, I also utilize a therapeutic approach to alleviate symptoms. Sessions with me will involve a true connection, this is my favorite part of working in mental health. I will earn your trust and together, we will develop a treatment plan that propels you towards wellness.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I promise to be present, to reach you where you are and help you move towards lighter days. Judgment does not belong in mental health treatment.

name: Rachel Bagley (SHE/HER)
approaches:
I have deep respect and compassion for those embarking on the journey of healing and self-growth. I believe therapy should be collaborative and I offer a safe presence that supports the process of change. My practice is an experiential, somatic, and mindful process that allows clients to safely explore their experience. Based on clients' individual needs, my approach draws from Neurobiology, Attachment Theory, Hakomi, EMDR (Trauma), and Family Systems. Throughout the process of therapy, the client and I will work to transform wounds and unhelpful patterns into resourced and empowered ways of being.
I moved to Portland, Oregon in 1998. I attended Portland State University and graduated with a Masters Degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling. Early in my studies, I was fascinated with the ways in which the brain and body influence one another. This inspired my year-long internship at M.E.T.A., a mindful and somatic based clinic in Portland. I am trained in Attachment Therapy, Hakomi, ReCreation of the Self, and EMDR for healing trauma. My practice is also informed by neurobiology, which is the science behind how the brain and body coexist.

name: Mikki Jordan
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My approach is rooted in a relational, humanistic framework, which means I view therapy as a collaboration. I challenge the conventional, medical model which views therapists as experts and clients as patients with a disorder that needs to be “treated." I take a more holistic view because I want to understand you as a whole person. As an integrative practitioner I often incorporate expressive arts, yoga, mindfulness, and meditation as I believe that creativity, movement, breath, and focused attention have the potential to deepen our capacity for self-healing.
Why I Became a Therapist
I came into this work after recovering from my own eating disorder almost 20 years ago. Once I found myself on the other side, I realized I wanted to help others find their own path to freedom. I know first hand how difficult this process can be. If you're struggling, please know you're not alone. I'd love to support you in any way I can.

name: Simeon Roane (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Together, in a safe and supportive environment we will access the many therapeutic benefits found within Talk Therapy, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Restoration Therapy, and Mindfulness Based practices. So – regardless of your age, gender identity, sexuality, or spiritual affiliation – you are welcome here. And I look forward to working with you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Many times, people can handle life’s problems on their own but some problems, regardless of their perceived size, require a little help or even a ton of it. Asking for that help isn’t always easy. However, I can assure you that it is the first and perhaps most important step on your path to healing and recovery. As a practicing clinician with a lifetime of experience–some good, some bad and some ugly–I am trained to offer guidance and support to those who have come to a crossroads; those who are either willing or forced to make changes within themselves, within their lives, and within their relationships.

name: Stephanie Caballero (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I allow the therapeutic relationship to build naturally in a relaxed space where the client leads the session. We jointly set your goals and subsequently analyze how your past, present and future are causing negative emotions. We dive into your life narrative to help you find self acceptance and new meaning for your future. During this time I integrate various challenges to help you find answers that support self analyses as well as understanding of others. Sessions may include relaxation skills, such as breathing, visualization or meditation. I often utilize metaphors to increase self awareness and increase confidence in attempting interventions discussed in sessions. I find that therapeutic relationships that are rooted in genuine kindness and a welcoming presence strengthens the progress of therapy because it shows our humanity as therapists.
Why We Started this Group Practice
Mind Body Soul Mental Health was started by my husband, Dr. David Ixtlahuac PsyD, LMFT, and me. It is a long time dream to open up our practice that focused on our specialties and serves various populations in the city of Portland, including Latinx individuals. Through our experiences and training we’ve found success in treating the whole person: Mind, Body and Soul. We focus on body concerns or responses, neurodiverisity, development, cultural upbringing, identity concerns and welcome discussions about spirituality. I am a first generation Latina with parents born in El Salvador and Peru. I lived in Peru during my childhood and saw first hand the disparities between class differences in a third world country. Coupled with my education in Multicultural Studies, I learned that countries of origin, immigration waves and financial standing contributes to various diasporas in the United States which affect the children of Latino immigrants, faced with challenges growing up here.

name: Cassie Liu (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach can be described as warm, transparent, and insightful, and I strive to bring genuine care and humor to our interactions. Our work together will be collaborative and focused on your feelings of safety and trust in our relationship. Creating a safe space for you to authentically be yourself is at the heart of my practice. We’ll navigate things together at a pace that feels right for you, one step at a time.

name: Eric Goodwin (HE/THEY)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Clients often describe me as warm, patient, and curious—qualities I bring into every session. My approach centers on creating a safe, grounding space where you can explore your thoughts and feelings with mindful awareness and self-compassion. These tools help relieve anxiety, heal from past trauma, and gradually replace self-criticism with self-kindness. Life may be full of challenges, but your therapy and healing don’t have to be rigid and harsh. Together, we’ll work at a pace that feels right for you and collaborate on goals that help you develop resilience and a deeper connection with yourself. With over a decade of experience, including work in clinic and college settings, I’ve learned to be creative, flexible, and direct as we navigate your path forward. We may even share a laugh from time to time, because growth doesn’t have to always be serious. I’ll meet you where you are, walking alongside you each step of the way.
Why I Became a Therapist
When it comes to healing, I believe in the power of challenging the stories we’ve been told about who we’re “supposed” to be - stories shaped by prejudice, shaming, and expectations that don’t reflect our true selves. For many, these beliefs come from bigoted words or actions, especially toward the LGBTQ+ community. How often do you treat yourself with judgment, in ways you’d never treat a loved one? Together, we can explore where that inner critic came from and whether it’s time for a change. I also believe in the profound healing that comes from mindfulness and self-compassion. Beneath the layers of anxiety, shame, and pain is a clear, wise voice - your authentic self. In therapy, we can increase your connection to that voice, helping you develop a deeper sense of self-compassion for the everyday struggles you encounter. I’m passionate about supporting LGBTQ+ rights from an intersectional lens and advocating for mental health, especially when it comes to destigmatizing seeking help.

name: Seth Altshuler (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have built my practice around the belief that cultivating a trusting relationship is the best path to reaching your personal and emotional goals. I help people reconnect with their own ability to heal and become aware of obstacles or habitual patterns that get in the way or limit the potential for experience and connection. I have over 18 years experience in mental health, counseling, and alcohol and drug treatment. Adults and teens (16 and up) are welcome. My practice provides an affirming and safe space to process, heal, and grow.

name: Liz Denny (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
