I see people as unique and relational beings whose stories have been formed by the society they live in, the families they were raised by, and the stories they believe about themselves formed from their life experiences. I believe that the re-telling of these stories, and the language people use, form the realities people experience. My goal for each client is to gain confidence in each area of their lives and relationships. I emphasize relationships because they are an intricate part of every person’s life. I believe building strong and healthy relationships provides the support and community everyone needs. I draw from both narrative and emotionally focused therapies.

name: Carina Hartley (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that you are the expert on your experiences. I am passionate about meeting you where you are, and will show up in the therapy room as my authentic self. Our sessions will be filled with curiosity, humor, warmth and consent. As we work together, I hope that you will build more compassion and acceptance for yourself, while tapping into your innate resiliencies and strengths. I use an integrative approach that is based on the needs of each client. This often includes a blend of Internal Family Systems (IFS), somatic therapy, & narrative therapy. Above all, I am queer-affirming and embrace a multicultural lens. I strive for sessions to be a safe, non-judgemental and anti-oppressive space for everyone. I love supporting folks around LGBTQ identity development, body image concerns, anxiety & depression, and healing trauma.
Why I Became a Therapist
I am fascinated by the human experience; how we create meaning and become fulfilled in a world that often tells us we can’t or shouldn’t exist in our fullest expression. I hope that along my path, I can bear witness to yours with a deep sense of respect, empathy, and compassion. Outside the therapy room, I love making psychedelic rock music, creating imperfect pottery and reading sci-fi books. I spend my free time hanging out with my three cats and soaking up the sun whenever possible.

name: Jessica Kremm (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe in a holistic approach to therapy. I work from a trauma informed lens and use both attachment theory and family systems theory to help create a dynamic picture of who you are and who you want to be. I have experience working with those overcoming sexual abuse and those redefining who they are after experiencing loss, leaving a relationship, or leaving the church. I find great worth in the stories that create who we are and would be honored to hear yours and be able to walk alongside you in the growth and healing process. As I sit with each client, I see the unique individual that they are and counsel through a process of exploring how their past is interfering with their present.
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a therapist because I think humans are fascinating. I find great value in the stories that make us who we are and love being able to hear others. I also believe in the natural desire we all have to grow towards health but that sometimes we can get stuck and need a little help. I believe that human beings have an unlimited capacity to change and grow and would love to join you on your journey of becoming the person that you desire to be.

name: DON Gershberg
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
You are the agent of the change in your healing process. I respect that process and meet you where you are. Together we work to understand and work through the patterns ; issues and structures in your psyche and circumstance that have kept you from living a more fulfilled and embodied life. My theoretical orientation is grounded in psychodynamic and depth psychologies and is trauma informed. I am trained in EMDR (attachment focused; standard protocol; intergenerational trauma; EMDR 2.0), trauma resilience /somatic experiencing ; ifs ; mindfulness based practices; psychedelic assisted psychotherapy and other somatic modalities. I am also a long time student in the Ridhwan School/Diamond Approach. I work w issues of addiction/chemical dependency; anxiety; depression; gender; sexuality/orientaiton; spirituality; stage of life

name: Kristin Marshall (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapeutic approach is primarily relational, compassion-driven, and person-centered. I ground my work in attachment theory, hold a systemic orientation, and draw from Satir's Model of Therapy. I also specialize in Child-Centered Play Therapy for clients aged 3 to 10 years old who are experiencing social, emotional, behavioral, and relational challenges. I utilize other expressive mediums, like sandtray to aid in the recovery of my clients. I believe that you have resources and wisdom inside of you, and having a compassionate, empathetic person like me come alongside you on your journey can be just what you need to witness the challenges you have and currently are experiencing to walk towards the health and wholeness you are seeking. I work in a Christian Group Practice and serve clients of all faith backgrounds. This is something we'll address in our initial consult to make sure any questions or concerns you have are addressed.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I resonate deeply with the poem "Guest House" by Rumi, and find that therapy can be a safe place to welcome and entertain all the arrivals coming to your guest house at this time, and in welcoming them, you can find an internal peace and wholeness to steady yourself from. "This being human is a guest house. Every morning a new arrival. A joy, a depression, a meanness, some momentary awareness comes as an unexpected visitor. Welcome and entertain them all! Even if they’re a crowd of sorrows, who violently sweep your house empty of its furniture, still, treat each guest honorably. He may be clearing you out for some new delight. The dark thought, the shame, the malice, meet them at the door laughing, and invite them in. Be grateful for whoever comes, because each has been sent as a guide from beyond."

name: Ajay Dheer (HE/THEY)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I provide therapy that is grounded in social justice, queer and feminist theories, and cultural humility. My approach to therapy uses a blend of narrative, attachment-based, and body-focused techniques. In working with intimate relationships, I additionally draw from Emotion Focused and Gottman Method therapies. To put it simply, this means I place you at the center of your own story. I incorporate mind-body connection, emotional experiences, and relationships of all kinds into the therapy space. I truly believe that you deserve to live your best life, and I am here to offer whatever help I may toward that end. I completed my M.S. in marriage and family therapy with a focus on sex therapy at the University of Wisconsin-Stout.
My Values as a Therapist
As a child of immigrant parents, I have lived my life in multiple cultures, giving me a unique perspective to the ways in which race, ethnicity, and socioeconomic class may impact someone’s experiences. Due to my own upbringing and education, I work to include these same aspects of life into the therapy room. I don’t presume to know exactly what my clients have been through, but I do know what it is like to navigate the world in a body that has judgments thrust upon it. I want to use that knowledge to validate my clients’ identities and offer support or, possibly, new understandings of the ways these experiences influence the rest of their lives. Though social justice and anti-racism work inform much of my practice, my values rest on centering and working with all forms of marginalization (race, gender, sexuality, class, ability, etc.).

name: Karen Corona (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to healing trauma involves beginning to work with the parts of yourself that surfaced to protect the more vulnerable, wounded parts. This often shows up as distressing symptoms and emotions along with 'survival skills'. While these emerged to help you survive, these strategies are often what are keeping you stuck and blocked from creating the life you long for. The strategies are no longer serving you. We work to understand these protective parts and their roles in order to release the aspects that are creating problems for you in your life today. The outcome of this is more harmony in your inner system and space for emotional well-being and a newfound freedom to flourish. In this process I often integrate expressive arts therapy as a way to externalize the feelings and gain increased self-awareness as a catalyst for making these desired changes.
My Values as a Therapist
The combination of my own healing & recovery journey along with my clinical training equips me to effectively work with women to heal childhood trauma and restore emotional well-being. This is my passionate purpose and I'm honored to do this work with clients. In my approach to healing family-of-origin issues, I integrate Internal Family Systems (IFS) and Expressive Arts Therapy (EXA) for a unique and yet evidenced-based treatment for trauma healing. Additionally, the 12 Step program of ACA (Adult Children of Alcoholics & Dysfunctional Families) can be a powerful adjunct to healing trauma (especially the Loving Parent Guidebook which is a resource for Re-Parenting work of the wounded Inner Child). I know suffering up close and recognize that the darkness can block out the light of hope, but claiming a newfound freedom and awakening to a sense of wholeness is possible as you embark on a healing journey.

name: Andrea Love (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I’d describe my approach to counseling as passionately guiding clients through various life transitions, facilitating personal development, and offering support in processing experiences of anxiety, depression, and trauma. I value being compassionate, direct and having client-centered approach which I believe empowers individuals to overcome challenges and find healing on their path towards mental well-being. When I say I understand your sadness, grief, shame, pain and anger, I do. When I say that I understand how your trauma and experiences have affected your choices and all the relationships in your life, I do. I also know how to find the courage to overcome the unthinkable. My goal is to empower you to feel incredible and live a life you love. I will meet you where you are on your path and help guide you toward authenticity, freedom, and joy. Together, we will gain breakthroughs and clarity in your relationships, career, and health that you can carry with you each day of your life
Why I Became a Therapist
I know what it’s like to have your pain be so great, you’ll try anything to make it stop. Growing up, I experienced physical, emotional, and sexual abuse. I am a survivor of rape. I had an abortion at 19 and became a single mother at age 23. I have two DUIs and have also spent time in jail. I’ve navigated anorexia nervosa, addiction, chronic depression, a suicide attempt, and Graves Disease. And that’s just part of my highlight reel… I have lifetime experience with Cluster B Personality Group, and High Conflict Personality Disorder (HCP’s) deriving from my family of origin. I also have extensive professional experience with mental health disorders, as well as how your attachment style impacts your adult relationships. I only share these intimate details with you so you know where I come from and how far I’ve come in this lifetime. This is how I hold the lantern for you—with compassion, humility, honesty, grace and a deep understanding and awareness.

name: Refresh Therapy
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We feel that life always has potential and we want to help you discover the best version of yourself that you can possibly be today. We believe in a strengths-based approach while recognizing that you don’t live in a bubble — you live within a variety of systems including your family, your friends, relationships, career, past and everything else that makes you uniquely YOU. We know how important it is to understand YOU within YOUR context in order to customize your counseling experience through better understanding of your perspective.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Refresh Therapy was founded by Bryanna Goodman in 2010. A few years before that, she was working in community mental health services, dreaming about possibilities. Upon realizing her hopes and vision, she decided to take a chance and open her own business. It was thrilling and terrifying at the same time, but she knew there was a need for therapists in the community, so she opened the doors in August of 2010 in East Vancouver, WA. Since then, Bryanna has had three littles; Bryanna’s husband left his job in the financial services industry to come on board as her partner in 2015, making Refresh Therapy truly a family owned business!

name: Monica Marquard (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have hold an MA in Marriage, Couple and Family Counseling (2023) as well as an MA in Early Childhood Education and Child Life in Hospital Settings (2017). I have experience working with children and adolescents in various inpatient and outpatient hospital and clinic settings. I have several years of experience in working with clients and their families during eating disorder partial hospitalization and intensive outpatient treatment. I utilize a mix of Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Internal Family Systems, Strengths Based Therapy and HAES (Health at Every Size). I understand that each client's needs may differ from the next and I adapt my approach based on the client and family needs. I believe the most important aspect to the therapeutic process is the relationship between client and therapist.
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is joining you through a compassion focused lens. When working together, I will help you understand how your behaviors may have protected you in the past. Together, we can work to let go of harmful behaviors and integrate more adaptive coping mechanisms into your daily life. We will work together to integrate the parts of you that can get you to feeling like your more authentic self.

name: Brooke Lytz (Vaille) (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that everyone has the ability to heal and transform; my job is to help you tap into that. I'm not here to "fix" anyone — I'm more like your guide, holding up a mirror so you can see the strength and wisdom you already have. I am formally trained in Restoration Therapy which helps you get to the heart of emotional wounds and understand how past experiences, especially around attachment, impact how you connect with others today. I also mix in techniques from IFS (parts work), CBT, ACT and ERP. When it comes to couples work, I utilize Restoration Therapy and pull from Gottman Theory to help you build better understanding and connection with your partner. At the core of everything I do is a Person-Centered approach. I’m here to listen and provide a space where you can safely explore what’s going on, without any judgment. We’ll work together in a way that feels right for you.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I strive to create an empowering therapeutic alliance that encourages self-discovery, growth, acceptance, and healing. I am here to understand, support, and bear witness to your journey. I am here to accept you fully as you are while also helping you explore what causes you pain. Together, healing is possible.

name: Stephanie Avery (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My practice, Coastal Trails Holistic Counseling, provides integrative therapy to support you on your journey toward growth and wellbeing. To view treatment holistically is to take into account physical, emotional, mental, social, cultural, and spiritual dimensions. I use a combination of Internal Family Systems (IFS), Narrative Therapy, Emotion-Focused Therapy (EFT), and Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) to assist in exploration and integration of thoughts, body sensations, and emotions while also helping you move toward positive change. To learn more about these approaches, see the Techniques and Specialties section below.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in the restorative power of therapy, nature, spirituality, and creativity. I have lived experience with chronic health issues, grief and loss, and intercultural experiences and relationships which lends me a unique perspective as well as deep compassion and understanding toward the myriad life challenges that each of us face. My personal joys and interests include reading, writing, painting, cooking, and exploring our beautiful, natural world. I also have a sweet tuxedo cat that makes occasional appearances during telehealth sessions. I'd love to get to know you and speak with you further about your personal goals for therapy. Are you interested in navigating big life transitions or current challenges? Exploring new aspects of your identity? Discovering and committing to passions and pursuits? Healing childhood wounds, or changing current relationship dynamics for the better? Feel free to get in touch to schedule a free 20 minute consultation.

name: Paula Emerick (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
At your most vulnerable time, you need someone who will hold a safe space for you to approach your edges - the parts of us we tend to avoid or neglect. Addressing our 'issues' is intensely brave and can also be terrifying! So I commend you for embarking on this journey and invite you to call me if my words resonate with your needs.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have worked with folx in intimate relationships (poly/open and monog) and all kinds of friend/family combinations for over 15 years. I believe in co-creating safe therapeutic space to unravel and challenge deep rooted beliefs/dynamics/meanings/identities to foster new ways of understanding and connection. My specialties include attachment, relationship dynamics (couples, poly, open, kink), lgbtq identity, sex work, family of origin, mental health, recovery and addiction, and healing from trauma.

name: Jeffery Wise
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My clients typically describe me as approachable, compassionate, caring, intelligent and non-judgemental. I address immediate concerns but also tend to go deeper to address underlying thoughts, beliefs, structures and patterns that may be limiting and that prevent your true potential to come forth. I work with meditation, journaling, breath work, medication, visualization, cognitive behavioral therapy approaches, and deep listening. I have many years of meditative experience and am interested in a deeper energetic approach to blocks or resistances that could be impeding the fulfillment of goals and dreams. Sometimes our bodies may need extra support with medication. I see medication as a tool, not as an end. Together we identify those blocks that are preventing you from expansion. We address the fears that exist, for whatever reason, and gently address then, opening the way for freedom, authenticity, and robust joy to emerge.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My space is open and welcome for all types of people. I especially enjoy helping to turn difficult circumstances, past or present, into opportunities for growth. Whether that means a history of low self-esteem, religious dogmatism, sexual or gender identity, I am there to help. My clients who are open to addressing fears or resistance to moving into emotional spaces that are more conducive to who they really are tend to experience great joy and satisfaction with me. What is it that is not allowing you to be truly you? Identifying the root of dissatisfaction, apathy, and limited beliefs opens the way to spaciousness, increased self love, improved self esteem, more energy, motivation to make the changes that you know will result in joy and well-being. Courageously allowing yourself the focus and awareness to exist in the space is the greatest you can give to yourself.

name: Paul "River" Fagan, M.A. (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I view therapy as an unfolding process, a collaborative journey, and a microcosm in which the client has the opportunity to discover, explore, and transform deep-seated emotional patterns. As therapist, my role shifts with the needs of each client and each moment, while retaining a core of compassionate presence and clear reflection. I draw from feminist, psychodynamic, humanistic, and nature-based frameworks and tools, seeking to reconnect clients both with their own inner wisdom and with broader webs of support and vitality. These varying frameworks share a common thread of focusing on the experiential, i.e, on the power of present moment experiences to provide both insight and healing. I see mindfulness as a central tool for engaging with the present moment and deepening into the self. I am also drawn to the tools of creative writing, embodying metaphors, and co-created visualizations.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold a Master’s Degree in Professional Mental Health Counseling from Lewis and Clark College. There, I learned how to incorporate my feminist values and passion for social justice with a strong foundation of research-informed counseling tools. I supplemented my education through trainings at M.E.T.A. where I also completed a one-year internship. At M.E.T.A., I learned the power that mindful awareness can have in deepening our understandings of our patterns and experiences and in unlocking new perspectives and possibilities. More specifically, I learned about the Hakomi method, Recreation of the Self, and various strategies for facilitating insight, compassion, and transformation. Currently, I am completing a Certificate in Ecopsychology through Lewis and Clark. Ecopsychology explores the ways that ongoing ecological crises impact individual and familial wellness and also the resilience and healing power found through attending to our relationships with the natural world.

name: Jenn Delaune (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I’m in my final year of completing my Marriage and Family Therapy graduate degree at Capella University, a COMFTE-approved program. I am supervised by three Licensed Marriage and Family Therapists: Shari Davison, Liz Gregory, and Dr. Terri Mathias. From an integrative therapy approach, I’ll help you navigate life’s entanglements, transitions, stress, anxiety, depression, relationship issues, family conflict, work/life imbalances, grief, and trauma. I’ve been in a committed partnership for 18 years, experiencing the ebbs and flows of relationships. I also have 20+ years of corporate experience, understanding its complexities and politics to help you discover ways to prioritize yourself in that environment. During our initial sessions, we will explore the issues you are comfortable with and work together to develop a strategy for dealing with them in a way that will help you regain your life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that fresh air can shift your mood and perspective, music is a magical healing tool, you’re never too old to play, and taking a nap is never a waste of time. I believe trust is earned, not given. I believe therapy is one of the best investments you can make for yourself. Through my own therapy and having a trusted therapist by my side, I’ve learned to successfully manage when my past seeps into my present, personal insecurities, anxiety, depression, grief, loss, and trauma. I wish the same for you and would be honored to walk beside you on your journey to healing. I offer a nonjudgmental and supportive space for your personal exploration. I’m allied with the LGBTQ+ community and incorporate cultural awareness and sensitivity into my practice. Healing and transformation take work, and it will be uncomfortable, but it is absolutely worth it.

name: Lauren Hansen (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We cannot shame ourselves into behavior change, and that's why I take a compassion-focused approach to everything I do. Self-compassion might be brand new to you, which is totally cool! It's a skill that might not feel natural at first, but eventually can become the default way you respond to yourself (and hopefully others as well). I believe your experience in therapy should be as unique as you are. I integrate many different modalities to fit your personality, goals, and preferences. Everything we do in therapy will be collaborative, as my goal is to reduce the inherent power dynamic that the therapeutic relationship can create. I want you to feel in charge of your healing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received my Masters of Mental Health Counseling from Lewis and Clark College and also work as an Intuitive Eating Coach. I operate from a Health at Every Size framework in my practice and take a strong stance against diet culture. When working with clients, I acknowledge the different systems of oppression that intersect and impact lives in ways that absolutely affect mental (and physical) health. I value showing up authentically, and will always show up as my full human self in session. If you're looking for a more formal therapist, maybe we won't be a good fit, and that's ok! I am a bit of an astrology nerd, and am always hooked on a new hyperfixation hobby! Always feel free to ask me about it.

name: Julia Bennion (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I specialize in couples therapy and individual therapy with teens and adults for challenges such as trauma, depression/anxiety, intimacy issues, affair recovery, and ADHD. I utilize techniques like EMDR, the Gottman Method, Mindfulness, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Attachment Theory and Restoration Therapy to help you meet your goals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Portland native and in our free time, my husband and our family enjoy travelling, going to shows & playing board games.

name: Renee Melton (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
People have the capacity to learn and make meaningful change. This is one of my core beliefs. Likewise, it is part of the human experience to feel stuck, or to run into walls within ourselves that we are unlikely to overcome on our own. When facing hardships, unhealed wounds, life transitions or contending with behaviors that no longer serve us, it takes courage to reach out, open up and dig deep to process our pain and struggles. When we take that leap, we also have the opportunity to move towards insight, empowerment and change. My approach is person-centered, trauma-informed, depth-oriented and mindful. I assist clients with increasing awareness and insight by examining thoughts, emotions and behavior, the influences of the past and present and bringing the implicit or subconscious to the surface. Clients are also supported with identifying what they can do in the “here and now" to create the change they seek.
Why I Became a Therapist
My path to becoming a therapist came from a deep desire to understand the human experience. From a young age, I have wanted to understand why we suffer, how we can cope and how can we experience joy, satisfaction, connection and meaning with the time we have. I was also compelled by an ideal to contribute - to help ease suffering, promote dignity and respect for all people, and ultimately, to help others develop their knowledge, strengths and skill to navigate the profound beauty and sorrow of being. I have Master’s degree in Counseling Psychology: Art Therapy, and I am a Licensed Professional Counselor in Oregon and nationally Registered Art Therapist. The first decade of my career focused on providing intensive care in social services, community mental health, public schools and hospital-based programs, and since 2018, I have provided therapy in outpatient private practice.

name: Hala Samaan (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
Art therapy is a transformative modality where creativity becomes a catalyst for healing and self-discovery. With a foundation in person-centered, solution-focused, and existential-humanistic approaches, my practice is rooted in empathy, compassion, and a non-judgmental stance. Through art, I illuminate the intricate facets of healing, using the creative process as a conduit to delve into emotions, thoughts, and experiences that might otherwise remain unspoken. Art therapy transcends the confines of words, offering a dynamic means of expression that holds a unique power to bridge the gap between the conscious and the subconscious. It celebrates the freedom of artistic creation and encourages a focus on the journey rather than the end result. This approach fosters joy, curiosity, and self-acceptance, allowing exploration and introspection to flourish. My practice is inclusive and versatile, catering to individuals across various age groups, backgrounds, and walks of life.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I'm an ideal fit for you due to my holistic approach and empathetic foundation. My practice, influenced by person-centered, solution-focused, and existential-humanistic perspectives, offers a secure space for your unique journey. Utilizing art as a powerful tool, I'll guide you through exploration, healing, and growth. Together, we'll navigate complexities, foster resilience, and unveil your authentic path toward a more fulfilling life.

name: Danielle Morgan (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is gentle and aims to help clients reestablish self trust, feel more grounded and navigate life with more confidence and ease. When we take on this courageous work it can transform our lives and how we interact with the world around us. With a focus on neuroscience, I’m particularly interested in how painful memories are stored in our brains and bodies, triggering automatic responses to current life events. When unresolved, these wounds can cause depression, anxiety, relationship struggles, and even physical illness—what was once a protective response no longer serves us. Talk therapy and cognitive approaches are valuable for identifying stuck patterns, but sometimes deeper work is needed to rewire the nervous system. Medication management alongside the therapies I practice help to break these cycles, restoring trust, safety, and peace—a return home to your own healing energy.

name: Andrew Hughes
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is thoughtful, collaborative, and individualized. I work closely with patients to understand their goals and challenges in depth. I’m trained in psychoanalytic psychotherapy and often meet with patients weekly to integrate both therapy and medication management when appropriate. I take a neuroscience-informed, evidence-based approach to care, but also value lifestyle factors like sleep, nutrition, movement, and social connection. For those already working with a therapist, I welcome collaboration and provide medication support as needed. I believe in using medication carefully—sometimes less is more—and always in the context of a broader understanding of the whole person.

name: Sophie Blauer
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I work with teens, adults, and couples, and specialize in anxiety, ADHD, and relationship issues. I practice from a mindful, strengths-based approach, and I provide a safe, warm environment that is conducive toward you achieving your goals.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
Our mental health can impact all aspects of life. I look forward to starting this mental health journey together, and working collaboratively with you to increase your overall well-being.

name: Steven Balshem (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My collaborative approach to helping begins with establishing a therapeutic relationship rooted in trust, non-judgment, empathy, and validation. I recognize the challenges around sharing your thoughts, feelings, and life experiences with a complete stranger, so I am a big advocate of meeting you where you're at as we begin our work together. As our therapeutic relationship strengthens, we will begin to unpack the connection between your thoughts, feelings, and behavior (Cognitive Behavioral Therapy) to assist us in identify areas of growth that you feel will be most beneficial to you. Along with setting concrete therapeutic goals, we will also identify skills to assist you in increasing distress tolerance, emotional regulation, strengthening interpersonal effectiveness skills, and practicing staying in the present moment (Dialectical Behavioral Therapy). Lastly, we will often engage in effective problem solving (Collaborative Problem Solving) to address ongoing concerns in your life.
My Values as a Therapist
I am a Korean American, adopted, cisgendered individual who has worked in mental health since 2013. I've worked at varying levels of care after receiving my Bachelors Degree in Psychology at Oregon State University in 2012, and while completing graduate school at Portland State University, where I received my Masters in Social Work in 2017. Through my personal, educational, and professional experiences, I have completed trainings in Diversity Equity and Inclusion, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Collaborative Problem Solving, Dialectical Behavioral Therapy, along with running a social justice group for high schoolers and implementing a suicide prevention curriculum at a local high school. As an Asian American growing up and living in the Portland Metro area the majority of my life, I value addressing the impact of oppression and oppressive systems that often intersect with many aspects of identity and identity development for teens and young adults.

name: Lee Lynch (THEY/ THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My practice is grounded in my training as an Expressive Arts Therapist. This means that depending on your preferences and style of processing, we have the option to incorporate visual art, music, movement, writing and play into sessions to explore other modes of expression beyond talk therapy. My hope is that therapy can be a place where you feel comfortable being yourself, so that we can work through things together that might feel otherwise uncomfortable or heavy to process alone. To learn more specifics about my professional experience, see my CV: https://www.expressiveartstherapypdx.com/pagecv
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Personally, I learned about grief & loss, and “feeling different” at a young age. These experiences have led me to be able to look at situations from many angles and listen deeply and respectfully. As a neurodivergent queer & trans person, I naturally frame my practice through a lens that challenges oppressive systems, including recognizing my own role in systems of power (for example, as a white therapist). In our work, I hope to create a space where you can feel safe(r) to bring your intersectional identities. I dream of a world where there is equitable access to housing, healthy food, healthcare and rest. What gets me through hard times: tuning into seasonal cycles, plant propagation, herbalism and home-cooked meals.

name: Doug Chapman (HE/HIS)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I studied Transpersonal Psychology in graduate school and have a credential as a Spiritually Integrated Therapist. I am licensed as a professional counselor in both Oregon and Washington. I am a Master Addictions Counselor. I hold national and state credentials as an Approved Clinical Supervisor and has supervised and mentored many younger clinicians. I am a past secretary of the Oregon Mental Health Counselors Association. In 2009, I won an Award “for substantial contribution to the counseling profession." Regarding my own lived experience, I grew up in a fundamentalist sect. I was a minister in the sect in my 20s and 30s. I left the sect and experienced the power of healing via therapy. At this time in my life I identify as having multiple spiritual identities including Buddhist, Christian, Pagan, and Hindu. I enjoy helping clients who have experienced religious harm and also with clients who want to explore their spirituality.
More About the People I Treat
I have extensive training to provide services for the needs of African Americans, Hispanics, Older Adults and Sexual Minorities. I enjoy working with clients in life transitions including loss & grief, midlife crises, retirement, and other life changes. If you are feeling stuck in your life, experiencing a difficult life transition, or would simply like to experience more joy, fulfillment, and wholeness I invite you to email me about openings. Together we will help you to build a life truly worth living. Finally, if you are a registered therapist associate in Oregon or Washington I invite you to contact about my supervision services. Registered interns may be able to see clients remotely in Oregon AND Washington. Ask for details. Ind. $120. Group $60. 25+ years experience providing clinical supervision. Approved Clinical Supervisor in Oregon and a ACS through NBCC. Licensed in Oregon and Washington and have a CADC3 in Oregon. Contact me for a Supervisor Disclosure Statement.

name: Leanne Bergman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe healing happens within safe and supportive relationships. Whether supporting a child in play therapy or helping parents strengthen their connection with their child, I offer a space where clients feel seen, valued, and understood. My approach is warm, nonjudgmental, and rooted in connection, helping clients build resilience and deeper self-awareness.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
The foundation of my work is the belief that every person (child or adult) deserves to feel understood, accepted, and supported. I bring warmth, curiosity, and deep respect for each individual’s experience, creating a space where healing and growth can unfold. Check out our website to schedule a 15 minute consultation to see if we're the right fit.

name: Bex Mertulien (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My work is grounded in the wisdom of the body, and built around the evidence based techniques of Attachment, Mindfulness and Interpersonal Neurobiology. I draw much of my philosophy concerning gender and sexuality from queer theory which historically challenges normative society, established traditions, and binary or definitive constructs. Therefore, our discussions may touch upon how various forms of marginalization, oppressive systems and traditional social constructs may impact your healing journey.
My Values as a Therapist
I value personal autonomy, non hierarchical support, radical openness and radical honesty. I am a nonbinary queer person who is always changing and always unearthing new aspects of what it means to live in a black queer body within a white capitalist colonial framework. I believe in decolonizing oneself and one's wellness. I cannot and will not be your blank faced therapist. I value mindful ways of existing that allow us to center your health and well-being.

name: Terri Mishler, PsyD
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Because therapy works best when attended regularly, clients chose a weekly appointment time which is reserved for them. Clients choose which issues they wish to discuss or for which they need help. I endeavor to speak directly with compassion to enable my clients to make choices that will lead them to a greater experience of happiness in their lives.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a Bachelor's degree in Communications, and Masters and Doctoral degrees in Psychology. Additionally I completed a post-doctoral Masters degree in Clinical Psychopharmacology. I have been helping clients with depression, anxiety, trauma and relationship issues improve their lives and make better choices for over twenty years.

name: Jennifer Crawford (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
As a Licensed Professional Counselor and Leadership Coach with 20+ years of experience, I specialize in supporting adults to center themselves in their lives. My approach integrates Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), mindfulness, and somatic techniques to help clients navigate challenges such as perfectionism, perimenopause, work issues, and life transitions. With a collaborative and client-centered focus, I tailor our work to your unique needs, ensuring we use evidence-based strategies that promote lasting well-being. I believe in creating a supportive space where you feel heard, understood, and empowered to make meaningful changes. My approach engages your whole self by integrating an understanding of the body’s nervous system, brain science, mindfulness, psychotherapy, depth psychology, and movement. Together, we’ll explore practical solutions that align with your goals, helping you move through life’s transitions with greater ease and confidence.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe the world is a better place when we can fully be ourselves, and therapy is a powerful way to support that process. As a client-centered therapist, I bring a compassionate, down-to-earth, and strategic approach to my work, creating a non-judgmental and strengths-based space. With a social justice perspective, I help clients recognize the impact of oppression and related traumas on their well-being while guiding them toward healing and self-discovery. My journey of learning—through relationships, parenting, and embracing my strengths and vulnerabilities—deepens my understanding of this work. Outside work, I find joy in gardening, discovering new restaurants, and exploring nature. These passions help me stay grounded and continually inspired, much like the work I do in helping others find fulfillment and growth in their lives.

name: Martha Blake, MBA, NCPsyA
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Jungian analysis is a soulful method of working with life issues and emotions that is based on the teachings of the Swiss physician, Carl Gustav Jung, MD. Jungian analysis focuses on complexes, unconscious patterns, symptoms, relationships, and the development and maturation of the personality, a process called individuation. Clients tell their story, explore connections between past and present, and find meaning. Many clients report feelings of self-acceptance, transformation, authenticity, renewed energy for life, and enhanced relationships. Jungian psychotherapy, like Jungian analysis, is based on the teachings of Carl Jung, MD. Jungian psychotherapy is a problem- and solution-focused therapy. Jungian psychotherapy focuses on an experience that is troubling the client and the development of new skills with which to engage the world. Jungian psychotherapy is less philosophical and more practical than Jungian analysis.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I trained as a Jungian psychoanalyst at the C. G. Jung Institute in Zurich and earned a Swiss degree, a Diplom, in 2005. I was licensed in Oregon as a psychologist in 2008. I am certified as an NCPsyA, a Nationally Certified Psychoanalyst by NAAP. I have advanced training in Compassionate (Non-Violent) Communication, neurobiology, and trauma. As a member of the InterRegional Society of Jungian analysts, I participate in the training and development of future analysts and psychotherapists.

name: Jane Flanagan (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
My bread and butter is in melding somatics and creativity, which is why I call my therapy style "Embodied Expressive Arts". Between training in Hakomi (a mindful-experiential somatic approach), and the Life/Art Process at Tamalpa Institute (an expressive arts and embodied movement modality), I've found that holding deep healing spaces with a balance of the two has been the most supportive for others and natural for me. Sessions can look like making visual art in session, writing, voicing, moving the body, and developing practices for emotional regulation or embodied expression outside of session. I've worked with individuals in therapy for about 4 years and with groups sporadically for just as long, and hold a very strong value in collective healing. Particularly in communities of parents, queer people, and artists, there is such richness in the vulnerability, risk-taking, relief, and immense reward in showing up for others and being willing to be held.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
If you hold identities that are beyond 'traditional' and 'normie', you'll feel right at home here. I love working with those who want to embrace their full, authentic selves, in your intersections, histories, and complexities. That's why holding tending containers with and for queer people, parents, and creatives feels most in integrity with what I do an who I am-- particularly in this time rife with burnout, overwhelm, and widespread systemic oppression. I also invite those who are deep feelers, sensitive sensors, and caregivers to be nourished by this work. If you feel in textures/colors/images, rich visual/conceptual metaphor feels natural to you, or tend to gesture or draw as you talk (or in place of talking), I'd love to share how these organic ways of being can make integrating an identity or navigating a life transition so much more accessible. If the above is challenging for you but you're still reading and curious, please come my way and let's explore!

name: Benita Munson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have post graduate training in EFT Couples Therapy, which has allowed me to help couples create healthier, more satisfying relationships with themselves and others. EFT Couples Therapy is evidence based to be highIy effective in relationship satisfaction. I believe that quieting the inner critic, and increasing self compassion changes the way we see ourselves and the world, and I incorporate Kristen Neff's work around self compassion into my practice. I am experienced working with chronic illness and disability, and use EMDR in my practice to treat negative beliefs as well as previous trauma. I am passionate about helping clients recognize and set boundaries, and learn compassionate yet assertive communication tools. We will work collaboratively to identify areas in your life that are problematic, and work on necessary changes. For more information about me and my practice you can go to www.maplecounselingpdx.com
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I grew up in Canada and have been working in a helping capacity since my early 20's. I have a Bachelor's Degree in Criminology/Psychology and a Masters of Science in Rehabilitation Counseling. I am a Certified Alcohol and Drug Counselor, and have previously worked in inpatient drug and alcohol treatment. I am also a Certified Rehabilitation Counselor, which means I specifically support people with chronic illness and disability to achieve their maximum potential. I completed an externship in EFT Couples Counseling and continue to pursue training in Gottman, Terry Real and self compassion. I am passionate about my children, travel, animals, and equality for all people. In my spare time I enjoy connecting with friends, family and pets, finding new restaurants in PDX, nature walks, and playing pickleball. I enjoy helping clients make sense of their past, in order to appreciate the present, and look forward to the future. I truly love what I do.

name: Teresa Zahariades, LCSW (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My approach is rooted in acknowledging the acceptance and change process. Learning to be still and pause within our patterns supports the change process. Many people consider therapy after months or years of enduring emotional pain or wondering why things are as they are. Many have wished that sadness, grief or life stress would ‘just go away’ but don’t know how to transform pain into growth. Often people seek therapy when they feel they have worn out their natural supports such as family or friends. As a Psychotherapist and human I meet you without judgment. I work to challenge you while also helping you to support yourself differently to become less stuck. I specialize in working with people experiencing transition, whether relational, career, life or existential. If you are reading this, there is likely a part of you that wishes to be seen and to make important changes to grow and be more comfortable as you are. I hope to be a part of that change process with you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
-I've had extensive training in Buddhist Psychology, Accelerated Experiential Dynamic Psychotherapy (AEDP), Acceptance and Commitment therapy, Dialectical Behavior Therapy and presence-oriented clinical work. -I have practiced meditation on a daily basis since 1999 and believe it to be a pivotal factor in wellbeing, balance and change. -I have taught meditation since 2005, I had previously taught yoga from 2005-7 and see the importance of how we move trauma and emotions through the body which I incorporate into my therapy practice. -I personally heal through nature, being in the woods, exploring creative process via arts/ceramics/painting. My outlook is rooted in realistic understanding of the human experience. I specialize in trauma support, depression, anxiety and transforming losses/ transitions into growth/resilience.

name: Celina Paul
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Celina enjoys supporting people learning to navigate their emotional landscapes, as well as growing their capacity to connect with loved ones and create meaningful relationships. She works within a strengths-based, person-centered, trauma informed lens, and relies on art therapy, a systems framework, attachment theory, narrative therapy, as well as an awareness of intergenerational trauma, and holds a strong social-justice background. Celina works collaboratively with clients to assist their growth in sustainable and identity-affirming ways. She is EMDR trained and trauma informed.

name: Deah Partak (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I provide therapy through existential (value, strength, and big picture-focus) and humanistic (you first, authentic, relationships based) styles. In session we can explore the here-and-now, examine thought patterns, develop self care plan, or even use humor to embrace life and yourself. In session we will focus on using your strengths and values to tackle depression, anxiety, loss, life transition, work-life balance, or self-esteem.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As long as I can remember, I have always respected the power of stories. Not just literature, but the real stories about our lives. We share our experiences, hear about our ancestors, and define who we are in this world through stories. Everyone’s story is important. Everyone has the right to author their own story and live their authentic life. Often our lives do not feel like our own because of difficult turns and twists. I find value in re-discovering resiliency and strength that is developed throughout life. I firmly believe we all have the power and strength to tackle life's stressors, joys, and tragedies. Gaining awareness of our values and strength helps us cope, thrive, and skillfully live life. I have had the privilege of being witness to the resiliency of many people with many struggles and many successes. This privilege formed and carries my belief that we all have the power and strength to tackle life's stressors, joys, and tragedies.

name: Psychology & Wellness Group PDX LLC (Group Practice) (SHE/HER, HE/HIM, THEY/THEM)
approaches:
Our Background and Approach
I (Stephanie) am the owner of our group practice, supervisor, & a psychotherapist. I have years of experience treating different mental health issues but specialize in personality disorders and trauma / emotional manipulation. I am psychodynamically oriented. We have awesome therapists: Ames (Art, ADHD, Autism), Marissa (Music, Trauma), Sam (Teens), Aubry (Mental Illness), Sean (Relationships), Donna (Eating Disorders) Alex (SPMI), Jeremy (SPMI), Jessica (Teens). They have different interests and styles. Relational, kind, intelligent, curious, strength based, focused, mindful, invested, supportive, art based and allies of multiple communities. What we have in common is our passion for a genuine therapeutic relationship based on trust and collaboration. We want to get to the heart of the matter with you and help you alleviate your emotional suffering. We want to help you deal with the current problem and help change your long term patterns. We love what we do and we hope it shows!
Our Personal Beliefs and Interests
We believe in hope & inclusivity & equality. We all have dogs or cats that own us. We believe in the greater good. We take OHP & insurance; we want people to have access. The therapeutic alliance matters to us. Yes, we need to make a living; what is more important to us is to help others & enjoy our lives doing the thing that matters. Call for fee info. Stephanie - coffee drinking Buddhist, owns the practice. Savannah - practice manager, front line for our practice & dog lover. Becky - smart, funny, our insurance & billing wizard & admin. Kori - front desk and our friendly client engagement specialist. Therapists: 9 of them! All different humans who are interested in different areas of counseling and all work in person and via telehealth. We would rather see you in person in either our Tigard or Sellwood offices. Please contact us with questions. Our therapists have multiple interests and diverse personalities. We hope you become part of our community!

name: Maria Bonacci (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My goal as your therapist is to help you feel more like yourself and to achieve the goals you have for your life. I bring training in multiple philosophies and styles, and consider my practice style eclectic/integrative. My approach is strengths-based and person-centered. I completed initial training in Emotion Focused Therapy in Fall 2021. I use Narrative Exposure Therapy for trauma to treat PTSD. I hold a Master's of Science in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from Portland State University. I have 20 yrs. of experience in public health and mental health via work in syringe exchange, sexuality education, HIV testing/care, early psychosis intervention, interpersonal violence advocacy, trans competency in mental health care, and most recently in psychiatric emergency care. My previous work and my political beliefs are a strong influence on my work as a therapist.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a White, queer, genderqueer person from the east coast. For those who are curious: Libra, Libra, Capricorn; ENFP; Enneagram 8. I am committed to anti-racist work. I am thoughtfully assertive. I believe in approaches to healing that don't reproduce systemic harm, and in care networks that center transformative change. I want to live in a world without police and prisons, and I am realistic about the work it takes to get there. I aim to support people to live a life that is aligned with their values and which helps them achieve their goals. I do this while keeping in mind that the wellness of one person impacts the wellness of their broader social circles and society at large.

name: Rainer Rivenburgh (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I supervise with an integrated developmental, feminist, and trauma-informed model. My approach to supervision is to acknowledge and dismantle (to whatever extent possible) power differentials in the supervision relationship. This power issue is, again, mirrored in the client-counselor relationship. I prioritize psychological and physical safety for clients and for my supervisees. I help support supervisees in how to create a felt sense of safety in sessions. I help clients recognize when trauma response patterns are showing up in session--in themselves and in clients--and how to respond in ways that do not cause re-traumatization, and foster resolution of the trauma. I also help supervisees attend to vicarious trauma they are experiencing, repairing interpersonal/childhood shildhood survival patterns, so as to steward this trauma toward growth and autonomy rather than moral injury and shutdown.
Why I Became a Therapist
As a clinician, I hold LGBTQ+ community competences with a special focus on the needs of transgender/gender diverse people, including gender issues for children and youth, and WPATH Standards of Care. I have training in art therapy, Narrative Therapy, Relational-Cultural & Feminist Therapy, Internal Family Systems, and Somatic Experiencing© Therapy. I have training in mindfulness approaches. I work from a systemic, trauma-informed perspective, using a client-centered approach. I have worked in a broad range of settings including inpatient psychiatric, assisted living, day treatment, and outpatient settings. I am a Board-Certified Art Therapist, Art Therapy Credentialed Supervisor (ATCS, with the Art Therapy Credentials Board), a Licensed Art Therapist (LAT), Licensed Professional Counselor, and Approved Clinical Supervisor (including Disciplinary Supervision) for LPC and LMFT-track associates with the Oregon Board of Licensed Professional Counselors and Therapists.

name: Christina Sheehan (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I know that you are exhausted and feel there is nothing to be done about it. Too many responsibilities and people depending on you. You want to let go, but fear disaster if you are not in control. I know you feel pressure from friends/family/society to be ok, think happy thoughts, just do yoga, try this diet, stop being so sensitive, meditate 3 hours a day—and Oh the real reason I called you, can you do me a favor? I know that it feels like putting yourself first is selfish. I know that the over thinking, replaying conversations and worrying about making someone upset is exhausting, and I know there is more in life for you then to-do lists, and fulfilling others priorities.
My Approach to Helping
Counter-Culture. Rebellious. Empowered. Radical Counseling is about acknowledging that we do not grow up in a world free from influence and agendas and that social justice ills affect us all. We look critically at what society is offering- like the limited definitions of success, patriarchy, gender roles, developmental expectations, and we consider whether those things add value to our lives or not. We tune into our own guidance & sensitivity system- instead of only referencing the outer world-and use this wisdom to create the life that supports our inner wisdom.

name: Monica Farrar (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am passionate about supporting clients of all walks of life (straight, non-binary, LGBTQIA+) through a collaborative and empowering therapeutic relationship. My trauma-informed approach to counseling combines several therapeutic models including somatic therapy, mindfulness work, and Acceptance and Commitment therapy.

name: Colette Gordon (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
The heart of my work is providing politically informed relational talk therapy. I believe some of the most therapeutic work comes from talking about the things that are hardest to say while being deeply heard and supported. I prioritize building and deepening the connection between us and attending to your experiences of connection and disconnection in other relationships. I draw on anti-oppressive critiques of social, political and economic forces in order to deepen understanding of your struggles. * * * The more I do this work, the more I believe in exposure therapy. Exposure therapy isn’t throwing you into the deep end; it’s therapeutic work done with a lot of intention, negotiation, support, and consent. Narrative Exposure Therapy (NET) is a specific treatment I use to process trauma and other formative experiences that may be interfering with your life. It’s some of the most impactful work I do.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I might be a good fit if: You think it's time to talk about the hard stuff. You're open to getting vulnerable and letting yourself be seen. You find it helpful to say things out loud and want a counselor who will think out loud with you. You want to look at the politics surrounding your life, relationships and struggles. * * * There are a lot of ways to do therapy but what generally matters most is the quality of the relationship you have with your counselor. It doesn't have to be perfect but it should be pretty solid. So don’t settle for me just because I’m queer and trans and can bill your insurance. That matters but you need more. But if you feel like we might click, check out my website for more, get in touch, and let's talk about it! I really value your considering putting your trust in me as a counselor and will do my best to do right by you.

name: Bronwen Flores
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Bronwen Flores is a PMHNP-BC with over 12 years of extensive nursing experience, not including six years of dedicated service, and two deployments, as a combat medic in the United States Air Force. She specializes in integrative psychiatric care, seamlessly blending the strengths of both Western and Eastern medicine to promote holistic wellness for her clients. She is currently finalizing her Doctorate of Nursing Practice (DNP) at the University of South Alabama, where she obtained her Master of Science in Nursing (MSN) as a PMHNP. She also holds an ADHD Clinical Services Provider Certification and is in the process of completing her Integrative Medicine Certification at the IPI Integrative Psychiatry Institute. Bronwen also holds a certification: Mastering the Essentials: A Hands-On Introduction to Ketamine Administration, awarded by the American Society of Ketamine Physicians Psychotherapists & Practitioners (ASKP3).
Why I am a Good Fit for You
Her military tenure and bedside nursing career experience have profoundly shaped her approach to mental health, allowing her to understand the complexities of trauma and its long-lasting effects on individuals. Bronwen is deeply invested in fostering a patient-centered environment where collaboration and open communication are paramount. By partnering with clients, she aims to empower individuals on their journey to mental wellness, ensuring that each person feels valued, heard, and supported throughout their treatment.

name: Michelle Shelton (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe it is more important than ever to consider your mental health needs. I have over 10 years of experience working in mental health as a therapist, leader, advocate, educator, and researcher. I would love the opportunity to partner with you on your mental health and well-being needs. I am passionate about finding ways to work with individuals, families, teens, children, and adults in order to have their lives be more fulfilling, productive, motivated and healthy. I understand the challenges that are face in individuals, families, marriages, and as a parent. I am committed to working with my clients in establishing their goals and how to best work towards achieving them. My goal is to work with my clients in order to provide a safe, healthy, accepting and collaborative environment. In addition to my experience as a therapist I have worked for many years in higher education developing a deep understanding and commitment to the field of psychology.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
*Note: I am currently taking new clients! I have over 10 years experience working in mental health as a therapist, leader, advocate, educator, and researcher. I would love the opportunity to partner with you on your mental health and well-being needs. I am passionate about finding ways to work with individuals, families, teens, children, and adults in order to have their lives be more fulfilling, productive, motivated, and healthy. As well as having my master's degree in Clinical Psychology, I am also a certified suicide prevention and awareness instructor and feel passionately about speaking on this. My research and experience interests include: stress, burnout, developmental theory, mental health, positive psychology, family dynamics, and suicide prevention. As a wife and mother of three boys, I understand the challenges that are faced in families, marriages, and as a parent.

name: Allison Browne (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I use trauma-informed and evidence-based treatments with a heavy dose of humor and creativity. Clients often tell me that I am down-to-earth and appreciate the freedom to be irreverent. I will take your concerns seriously and meet you where you are. I am strongly influenced by research on attachment, cognitive psychology, emotion-focused therapy, mindfulness, and polyvagal theory. I emphasize attention to what’s happening in the here-and-now to help you create the change you want and access healing. I enjoy working with people from many different backgrounds. I began my anti-racist, anti-bias journey more than twenty years ago. I remain grateful for the patience and confrontation provided by my early teachers and mentors. I am willing to engage with openness around issues related to bias and oppression. I identify, in part, as a white, cis-gender, middle-age queer from the East Coast. I continue to do my own work around privilege and internalized biases.

name: Kate Keating (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I utilize a Person-Centered approach that often draws up Narrative Therapy, Internal Family Systems, Emotionally Focused Therapy and mindfulness interventions. Additionally, I'm trained in EMDR and can guide you through that experience. Most of the time, my therapeutic style looks like a natural conversation, but things will be moving and shifting and at times we will engage in specific interventions. Our work together will be collaborative and creative. I strive to provide a sanctuary for my clients away from the chaos of life, a space just for you. You will be challenged in this process, but I want our sessions to be something you look forward to each week.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My work as a counselor started in schools, where I've worked with hundreds of middle and high school students, supporting them (and their parents!) through the incredibly tricky time of pre-adolescence and adolescence. Since then, I've expanded my scope to include therapy with adults and families. I hold a Master's degree in counseling and have advanced training from the Portland Dialectical Behavioral Institute and Portland State University.

name: Isabel McCune (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that people are experts in their own lives and make the best decisions available to them with the resources that they have. I am interested in how we interact with and are affected by systems of power, and in how counseling can help decolonize mental wellbeing. My core commitment as a counselor is to affirm your autonomy and to be a witness as you grow and make meaning of your life. I approach therapy with curiosity, and invite you to explore how histories, structures, and intersections shape who you are and what you do. I use metaphors and thought experiments to offer new perspectives. I practice mind-body techniques to identify how emotions and experiences show up in the body as well as the mind. In our work together, I hope you find rich meaning in your experiences, build connection to your community, and make changes that are significant to you.

name: Maddie Andrews
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapeutic approach emphasizes emotional well-being, strengthening relationships, enhancing communication, and fostering resilience. I create a collaborative and empathetic space that acknowledges your unique needs and concerns within the broader context of societal, cultural, and systemic influences. I have extensive experience working across diverse communities, ensuring that our sessions are inclusive and respectful of your individual background and identity. Whether you face challenges in relationships, career transitions, or personal growth, my commitment is to support you to tap into your inner strength and resources to create meaningful impact for your life.

name: Anat LeBlanc (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
With deep compassion, a courageous heart and a warm and calming presence, I companion individuals and families through grief and loss, personal transformation, chronic illness and the mysteries of death and dying. Combining years as an interfaith hospital chaplain with potent personal experience, a deeply empathic heart and skills from neuroscience, mindfulness and meditative practice, I honor the holistic and somatic impact of loss, and the challenges of personal growth. Together we will hold space for your deeper story to unfold.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I graduated with a Master's degree from Sofia University in Transpersonal Psychology in 2010 and in 2013 completed a residency year of post-graduate training at Emanuel Medical Center (Clinical Pastoral Education) where I served on staff several years as an interfaith chaplain before joining OHSU's Bridges Pediatric Palliative Care team in 2017. I have deepened my practice with the M.E.T.A. Institute, studying Hakomi, a somatic and mindfulness based therapeutic modality and is a certified Compassionate Bereavement Care provider through the MISS Foundation. Working at a level 1 trauma center has given me extensive experience in companioning families and individuals through traumatic events, acute crisis and sudden loss, providing a warm and calming and presence for those facing significant life changes.

name: Kate Robinson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I utilize Cognitive Behavioral, Strengths-Based, and Mindfulness - Based treatment approaches. During the past 17 years, I have worked in Inpatient and outpatient treatment settings. In private practice my focus is on individual counseling for adults. My areas of specialization include working with individuals with Acquired Brain Injury (TBI, Concussion, Stroke, Tumor, encephalitis.) as well as helping people who are struggling with symptoms associated with Chronic Pain, Depression, Anxiety, and difficulty adjusting to life changes. My treatment begins with gathering some history and learning about what brings you to counseling. In the time frame that works best for you, I will help you identify what is not going well and what is keeping you from the life you want to lead. I will be an empathic listener and encourage you to practice new ways of thinking about what is troubling you and teach ways you can make subtle changes to start feeling your best.
Why I Became a Therapist
Life changed drastically for me when as a teenager I suffered a traumatic brain injury and spent several months in the hospital recovering and relearning everything. My recovery was aided by receiving individual counseling which helped me learn how to adapt to all of the emotional and physical changes as well as losses I experienced. Professional help put me on the right path and is one of the reasons I decided to become a counselor myself. Over the years, I gained confidence and focused my energy on helping others. I have been, and continue to be active in the brain injury community as a volunteer and public speaker. I was recently invited to be a guest speaker at a Symposium about Traumatic Brain Injury and PTSD in Warsaw, Poland. My interests include spending time with my family, gardening, meditation and traveling. I am fluent in French and my undergrad degree was in French studies. I love to learn and take advantage of any opportunities to expand my knowledge as a counselor.

name: Roger Tunks (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
He has 8 years of nursing experience working in emergency departments and ICUs. It was in those environments that he witnessed first-hand the effects of people struggling with mental illness who were unable to get the support before it became a crisis. Realizing the need for mental health services, he continued his education as a PMHNP. His hope is to help people understand what is causing their struggles and how to achieve a higher level of mental health before it comes a crisis. Roger sees psychiatry as a collaboration where the patient brings the knowledge of their lived experiences and unique symptoms while he brings his knowledge of mental health diagnoses and treatments. Together they are able to collaborate to create an individualized treatment plan.

name: Patrick Nagel (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I provide a safe and warm therapeutic space to explore your inner world, earn wisdom, and build stability. I specialize in trauma and PTSD using somatic/bottom-up approaches. I also specialize in working with LGBTQ+ folks, especially those exploring gender identity and gender transition. I practice Internal Family Systems (IFS), also known as “Parts work”. This process creates more self-leadership, more self-compassion, and deepens clarity. Another benefit to IFS is that it makes space of the ambivalence or hesitation of change. I have a very personable approach to the therapeutic process and believe it's important that you are treated and feel like a person in need of help, not seen as a diagnosis or label. My style incorporates Internal Family Systems (IFS), Attachment theory, and Brainspotting.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a therapist, I am in a unique position to see the impact and vulnerability of living life in our current world/society. You don't have to look very hard to find criticism, marginalization, and oppression in our daily lives. Through that understanding and earning my own wisdom I have adhered to certain beliefs and ideals. The frameworks I infuse into my work come from my identity in the LGBTQ+ community, being kink/queer positive, prioritizing social justice through a feminist lens, as well as practicing the HAES (Health At Every Size) model.

name: Emily Smith (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that how we exist in the world is shaped by our present and past experiences, triumphs and sorrows, relationships, and the stories we tell ourselves about it all. My path to becoming a therapist was born out of my own experience with therapy during a challenging season of life, and is grounded in the growth I have experienced myself. I strive to offer clients guidance towards true connection with themselves and to foster trust in the inner wisdom we each have. With deep respect and curiosity for each person’s unique experience, I am committed to meeting you exactly where you are. I incorporate Attachment & Emotionally Focused Therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, IFS, and mindfulness and storytelling techniques in my approach. I’ve been described as calm, warm, thoughtful, relatable, and compassionate. At my core, I deeply believe in the human capacity for growth, change, and healing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am dedicated to creating a welcoming and affirming space for people of all expressions and identities, providing culturally-sensitive, trauma-informed care, and supporting you in embracing and living your most authentic self. I believe every individual encounters struggles in life’s journey and deserves to feel respected, heard, and supported in their healing. I view the human experience as deeply connected to the natural world, often finding inspiration in the shifting seasons and natural life cycles as mirrors for personal growth and transformation. I have specific experience navigating relapse and recovery in couples relationships, and have served as both a leader and member within a community grief group of 21-45 year olds who have experienced significant loss.

name: Leslie Jones
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I draw on several important modalities in the work we do together. My foundational training of process oriented work helps enhance a deeper understanding of what may be causing repeated distress and challenging patterns of behavior. I utilize Emotionally-Focused Therapy to help deepen the process of talking about hard topics, while looking for and finding the root causes of distress. I compliment this, when wanted, with cognitive techniques that help reach resolution.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a licensed professional counselor (LPC), as well as a registered art therapist. I have a master's degree in counseling and art therapy from Marylhurst University. I have many years of professional experience working in community mental health agencies. My clientele included children, adolescents, adults and families. I have completed two levels of training in Emotionally-Focused Therapy, an approach that helps identify root causes and primary emotion. I offer talk therapy for adults focusing on depression, anxiety, shame, faulty communication patterns, wounds from family history, grief, emotional buttons that feel tender and overwhelming, and living in a complicated world.

name: Sarah Martin
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I take a strengths-based and client centered approach with a strong social justice framework. I utilize language of narrative therapy to externalize issues and develop skills to overcome barriers. I use creativity to engage with people and establish a meaningful connection before moving forward with the therapeutic process.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a licensed clinical social worker with 7 years of experience in community mental health settings prior to private practice. I specialize in working with adolescents who are navigating the transition to adulthood as well as members of the LGBTQI community.

name: Julianna Guenther (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe connection and safe relationships are essential to our well-being, however at times I know our deepest woundings can create barriers to the type of connection we crave. I strive to create an empathetic and empowering environment, where together we can explore authenticity, healing and self-acceptance.
My Values as a Therapist
My therapeutic approach is informed by Emotionally Focused Therapy, Narrative Therapy and Experiential Somatic (body) practices. I view therapy as a space to explore and discover new ways of being and relating to ourselves and others. I believe in the power of understanding how our deeper emotional processes impact the way we interact with ourselves and those closest to us. My training as a systemic therapist has given me insight into how our problems often occur in context and I work to deeply understand your unique experience. I challenge myself to stay culturally aware and curious. I practice (continuously learning) anti-racism inside and outside the therapy room, and professionally believe in the importance of engaging in trauma informed practices. I am committed to holding space for all identities, orientations, and relational configurations.

name: Tony Lash (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We all have inner strengths to draw on in our quest for growth. A trusting and supportive therapeutic relationship can be one of the most powerful tools to tap into these strengths and use them to work toward your personal goals. I integrate Object Relations, Internal Family Systems, and Emotionally-Focused Couple's Therapy in my work, with the goal of providing you with a safe, accepting, and nurturing space to work through unhelpful patterns and develop new ways of relating to yourself and others. I earned both a Bachelor of Arts in Psychology (2017), and Master of Arts in Clinical Mental Health Counseling (2020) from Portland State University. I am currently a Licensed Professional Counselor Intern, and am supervised by Sarah Williams, MA, LMFT, and Deanna Cor, PhD, LPC.
Why I Became a Therapist
