I have always had a passion for what connects us to one another. For over 30 years I expressed this through music, both as a music producer and performing musician. The most meaningful musical work I did always came from the more intimate spaces, helping to create moments where an artist's emotions could connect most directly with listeners. As I explored ideas for a new career, counseling felt like the most natural evolution of what I had always tried to achieve through music: helping people bring powerful emotions, whatever they may be, to the foreground. As a counselor, my greatest joy comes from providing the safety and trust needed for people to engage with and accept the parts of us we may find painful, neglected, or rejected so that we can carry them forward with us with compassion and validation.

name: Mitchell Passadore (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I was trained in Portland at Lewis & Clark’s graduate school of education and counseling. I have specific training in treatment for anxiety disorders, PTSD, addiction, depression, and trauma utilizing principles of Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Exposure Response Prevention (ERP), Motivational Interviewing (MI), Compassion Focused Therapy (CFT), and Mindfulness-Based Cognitive Therapy (MBCT) in both individual and group settings.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that anyone can benefit from counseling and that no matter where a person starts, they deserve respect and care. I am here to help people live better lives and uncover patterns in thoughts and behaviors that might be causing distress. For this process to be most helpful, it is important to find someone who fits well with who you already are. My goal is to help facilitate positive, lasting change and I believe that there is a counselor out there who can help make that happen.

name: Erin Bush (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use an eclectic approach with an emphasis on person-centered counseling and a multicultural framework. I believe that each person is unique and requires an individualized approach to therapy. I draw from various theories based on what is needed at the time including CBT, DBT, ACT, and mindfulness approaches. It is important to me to create an environment where every person feels respected, seen, and celebrated for all aspects of their identity. My style is supportive, flexible, and strengths-based.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that we are all doing the best we can with the tools we have at any given moment. What we see as problems within us are often ways we have learned to cope and survive our circumstances. My goal is to help you find the tools you need to grow, change, and heal.

name: Bonnie Lambert (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have over 17 years of experience working in mental health, first as a licensed mental health therapist and then as a psychiatric provider. I grew up in Oregon, attending Portland State University for a Bachelor of Science in Criminal Justice and Psychology (2002), then earning a Master of Arts in Counseling Psychology (2005). I achieved licensure as a therapist (LPC) in 2008 and then in 2013 began my nursing education, achieving my RN in 2014 and then board certification as an advanced practice Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner. I provide psychiatric evaluation, ongoing therapeutic medication management for adults and adolescents ages 15 and older. My work has led me through numerous levels of mental health care; outpatient (adult and children), inpatient medical as psychiatry liaison, inpatient psychiatry, emergency department, and correctional settings. Health and wellness are my focus that I'd like to translate into mental health. More info can be found on the website.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Holistic care involves the mind, body, and the environmental sphere in which we exist. Instead of only focusing on the medications, there will be a combination of psychotherapy and medication, either with an outside therapist or with capacity myself, where we can assess layers and apply appropriate interventions. Therapy is an essential component of mental health treatment. The combination of therapy and medication is often more effective than either alone. Therapy expands skills, often leading to a place where medications may not be necessary. As a therapist, then nurse practitioner, I often provide brief therapy with each session as part of the funneling down of medication effects matched with coping and self-care. More traditional therapy techniques can be learned in longer medication management sessions. I do not provide psychotherapy alone but can provide recommendations to those who do should you be interested.

name: Jooyoung Oh (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Explore long and short-term body symptoms, nighttime dreams as well as half dreams (flash of images or stories) to deepen an understanding of oneself Explore patterns in whom we fall in love with or we have a conflict with to understand what is being marginalized within oneself Explore childhood dreams and memories to understand our life myth I follow the code of ethics in therapy for practitioners in USA. It means… > Confidentiality. What you share stays with me. An exception of when there is a possibility of self-harming or harming others), equal power > Co-creation: I am not here to give you any advice as someone superior in any way. We are in this together.

name: Kent Yoder (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that each of us has a natural impulse toward growth, healing, and connection—with ourselves, others, and the world around us. However, life experiences, cultural backgrounds, and relationships shape the narratives we tell ourselves and the patterns we follow. While these can serve as strengths, they can also create barriers that make it difficult to navigate change or healing on our own. Therapy provides a space to slow down, reflect, and gain clarity. I strive to create a supportive and collaborative environment where clients feel safe to explore the emotions, experiences, and challenges that are shaping their lives. I take an integrative approach to therapy, drawing from modalities that honor the mind-body connection, personal narratives, and the ways we are shaped by relationships and environments. I am in the process of training in EMDR, an evidence-based approach that aids in the resolution of traumatic memories and deeply ingrained emotional patterns.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a member of the LGBTQIA+ community and a committed BIPOC ally. My work is deeply rooted in social justice, advocacy, and an understanding of the ways systemic oppression impacts mental health. I also have significant multicultural and international experience, which informs my perspective and ability to hold space for diverse experiences.

name: Rushini Jayawardena (SHE, HER)
approaches:
Our Background and Approach
I take a relational approach to therapy and strive to build unique and authentic relationships with my clients. My areas of focus with my adult-aged clients are: trauma, anxiety, life adjustments, inner child work, and navigating relational and cultural stressors. I identify as a POC and enjoy offering multicultural therapy services to other individuals in the community. I have the lived experience of being an immigrant and the stressors that come with navigating identity, values, and grief related to this process. I am also a certified trauma-conscious yoga instructor, certified complex trauma therapist and offer EMDR to clients who are interested.

name: ALISSA BURNHAM (SHE/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapeutic approach is collaborative, curious, relational and compassionate. I create a non judgmental space for you to explore challenging issues, strengthen inner resources, and learn new skills for moving forward. Through our discussions and evidence-based therapy methods, we will work together to understand your emotions/thoughts, make decisions aligned with your values, and live a more satisfying life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
A central part of my work is holding an anti-oppression framework and examining oppressive social structures including racism, classism, sexism, ableism, heterosexism. This could look like naming micro aggressions in the workplace, internalized capitalism and how it may cause you to label yourself in particular ways. Intersectionality is a core value personal and professional value, understanding how the world perceives and interacts with you and the impact this has.

name: Family Roots Therapy
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Our therapists are highly skilled in different therapy approaches, including Child-Parent Psychotherapy, art therapy, play therapy, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Gottman method, and postpartum/perinatal mental health. Because a mental health challenge in one family member affects all family members, we believe in supporting each person in the family holistically and meeting each person's needs. We believe that there is no one right way to be a family and that one family structure is not better than another. We want all families to feel welcome and included at Family Roots. That's why we have therapists who work with queer, non-binary, and trans individuals (children, teens, and parents), as well as polyamorous couples, blended families and divorced families.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Our counselors include Licensed Marriage and Family Therapists, Licensed Professional Counselors, and Licensed Art Therapists, as well as Professional Counselor Interns (who are working toward their hours for full independent licensure). Our counselors have a wide range of experiences, from working in community mental health to working in school, inpatient, or prison settings. Specialties include prenatal and perinatal mental health, parenting support using Collaborative Problem Solving and Positive Discipline, play therapy with young children, working with blended families and divorced families, and working with couples on the transition to parenthood.

name: Anne Morgyn (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I'm committed to the sacred process of finding and living your most authentic self in whatever stage of life you may be in. I believe in you, I believe you have a story that deserves to be heard and I believe you have a deep intelligence within you that can help guide you towards well-being. It is my passion to help you understand, accept (maybe even love!) and learn to trust yourself. Our work together centers on safety, collaboration and inclusion and I believe in meeting you where you're at without judgement or bias and keeping it compassionately reaI. I bring over a decade of experience as a LMT specializing in nervous system regulation. Like our bodies, therapy isn't "one size fits all" and I have many tools to draw on to help. I have additional training in treating eating disorders, CPTSD, I am a certified Somatic EMDR provider, am becoming a certified Body Trust provider and have numerous trainings in Internal Family Systems and Somatic Psychotherapy.
My Values as a Therapist
I'm a HAES aligned, identity-affirming therapist who welcomes all clients including LGBTQIA+, neurodivergent, disabled, BIPOC, and poly folks. You deserve care that can hold space for your whole self, where all parts of you are welcome. I also acknowledge the many current oppressive systems in power. Sometimes an important part of our work will be to investigate these systems and how they have infiltrated your sense of self, worth and safety. I also strive to keep any inherent power imbalances between us in check by practicing humility towards this process and keeping you centered as the expert of your experience. I'm a life long learner and committed to my own personal growth. I believe in walking the talk, I've seen the power that the right fit of therapy and therapist can do and I consider it a privilege to be able to pay that forward with others.

name: Heather Rensmith (SHE, HER, HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is one that values strengths and empowers people to pursue their ideal life situations. My goal is not to pathologize differences, but help people acknowledge their concerns, seek acceptance or accommodation and thier own versions of optimal functioning. I enjoy working with individual and couples who practice various relationship models and structures (monogamy, polyamory, non-monogamy, kink), who are part of the queer community, or who are still figuring out their community. I take an eclectic approach to therapy and draw upon common treatment approaches include cognitive behavioral therapy, Gottman Method Couple Therapy, Emotionally Focused Therapy, and narrative therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I most enjoy working with individuals and couples who are working on issues surrounding their sexuality and sexual health. Our work together could explore functioning (pain, erectile dysfunction, chronic or acute illness and its impact on sexual functioning), desire, arousal, and varying interests, communication, grief and loss, or trauma recovery. Sex therapy often involves education, validation, and normalization. I graduated from the University of Michigan School of Social Work in 2002 after obtaining by BA in Women's Studies from Michigan State University. I am licensed in the State of Oregon and Washington as a Clinical Social Worker. I have completed a Sexual Health Certificate from the University of Michigan School of Social Work in sex therapy and education. I am an AASECT certified sex therapist as well as certified by the Sexual Health Alliance in the treatment of Problem Sexual Behavior.

name: Claire Tuft (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
 My work is shaped by my belief in the power of personal narratives and the capacity to reclaim and reshape one's story, fostering new skills, resilience, and a greater quality of life. I view counseling as a collaborative process and am dedicated to supporting and empowering clients on their path towards healing and growth. When working with clients, I take a person-centered and strengths-based approach, drawing from evidence-based practices such as Trauma-Focused Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (T-CBT), Narrative Therapy, Internal Family Systems (IFS), and Attachment Theory. I like to incorporate mindfulness strategies and creative-based approaches, and while I am not yet certified, I am pursuing training and certification in EMDR.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Prior to pursuing my career as a counselor, I spent the last fifteen years working professionally in Theater and Film as producer, actor, and casting director. It was my own meaningful experiences over many years as as a client myself, commitment to repair and self actualization, and deep curiosity and compassion for the human experience that inspired my pivot to counseling. Creative expression and the arts are still a central part of my life, and I aim to continue integrating these passions into my therapeutic work in the future. Outside of my work as a counselor and my creative pursuits, I enjoy traveling, writing, running (slowly!), and spending time (especially in nature) with my partner and pup.

name: Alexandria Moore (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is intuitive, grounded, and warm. I will guide you through ways to physically calm your system, to be attuned to what signals the body is sending, and ultimately feel more connected to your self. If this work sounds right, let’s begin. It’s a long road, and there’s no knowing where we’ll be swept off to!
Why I am a Good Fit for You
My approach is intuitive, grounded, and warm, inviting moments that challenge assumptions with true care and respect. I allow the spoken and unspoken cues in the room to guide the process. From years of teaching yoga, I have a deep regard for the connection between mind and body. I will often ask you to consider, can you be curious and nonjudgmental towards your body to create a healing mindset? This will support our larger work, as we practice awareness that every thought and emotion has a physical, embodied component.

name: Alissa Holmes (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I consider myself an eclectic practitioner, meaning my approach is informed by a variety of evidence-based treatments. As a client-centered therapist, I tailor my approach to you and your needs. I believe that everyone has the capacity to heal, grow, and create positive change in their lives when given the right tools. My treatment approach is most strongly informed by Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, mindfulness-based techniques and motivational interviewing. My hope is that through our therapeutic relationship, my clients attain a sense of empowerment and resiliency, as well as the peace that comes with self-acceptance and embracing the here and now. While we can't always change our circumstances, we can always learn to respond to life's challenges in a way that correspond with our most deeply-held values.

name: Kristina Stuart, LPC, NCC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have been working with individuals in the mental health field since 2005, beginning my journey working with children and adolescents in a variety of settings. I completed my internship at Washington County Corrections working with addictions and recovery, facilitating individual and group therapy in Seeking Safety and Mindfulness Based Relapse Prevention models, and later provided adult outpatient therapy for a non-profit agency. I received a Bachelor of Science in Psychology, and hold a Master of Art in Counseling Psychology. I am a Certified EMDR provider, meaning I have completed all EMDR trainings and have completed certification tasks proving my efficacy. I also utilize Cognitive Behavioral Therapy , DBT, and Mindfulness to help make fundamental changes in perspective and to help shift the way you view your personal experience.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe that everyone has the capacity towards achieving personal fulfillment. Together we can identify barriers in your life that are making this difficult to achieve. As your therapist I will provide guidance and support on your journey. I will work with you to create self-directed goals and allow a safe place to explore feelings, experiment with new perspectives about yourself, and accept challenges, all as part of the process of change.

name: Kelly Sestero (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I graduated with B.A. in Psychology from Gonzaga University and an M.A. in Counseling from University of San Diego. I was originally trained in the CBT model, focusing on reducing suffering by understanding how our emotions, thoughts and behaviors work together. In recent years I have found my passion in mindfulness-based therapy using Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), Mindfulness Integrated Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (MiCBT) and Intuitive Eating. I work from a Buddhist Psychology orientation, integrating meditation, mindfulness and compassion to reduce distress from anxiety, depression, and chronic pain. I am a trained provider in MiCBT, an intensive 10-week program based on the work of S.N. Goenka and Insight Meditation retreats. This is a program that takes dedication and effort, but the outcome of peace and equanimity are well worth it. Invest in yourself!

name: Laurie Kress (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I’ve had the privilege of working with individuals, couples, families, and groups in my over 25 years of experience as an LCSW in the mental health field. I’ve worked in a wide range of practice settings including outpatient mental health clinics, community mental health, bereavement program, crisis intervention, and at-risk youth services. I draw from an eclectic mix of therapeutic frameworks and techniques including Acceptance Commitment Therapy, Dialectical Behavior Therapy, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, and Interpersonal Neurobiology, among others.
My Approach to Helping
My style is warm, collaborative, client-centered and trauma-informed. I strive to create a safe, respectful and supportive environment, honoring the intrinsic wisdom and strength of all my clients.

name: Janean VeDova (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I hold a Master’s Degree in Marriage, Couple and Family Therapy from Lewis and Clark Graduate School of Education and Counseling in Portland, OR. Major coursework included sex therapy, family and relationship life cycles with a strong emphasis on social justice in therapy and outside systems. I have extensive experience working with victims of sexual assault, families affected by HIV and other terminal illness, along with a background in family and civil mediation. I also hold a bachelor’s degree in Peace and Conflict and a minor degree in Philosophy. I work from a systemic framework, meaning I acknowledge the constraints and or strengths outside systems (i.e. school, work, court and religious communities) have on individuals, couples/partners and families. I also take into consideration biological, psychological, social and spiritual aspects of people’s lives. I am strength-based, I believe in focusing on the strengths, resiliency and successes of individuals and relationships.
My Approach to Helping
My approach is integrative, collaborative, humanistic and eclectic. Trust is at the core of my practice. My goal is to establish trust from the start so clients can feel safe and comfortable opening up about the most vulnerable parts of their lives and selves. I know a client's trust is precious and I am always honored to receive it so that I can help and support each person the best to my ability. Without trust in the therapeutic relationship, the work can get stuck. It can feel endless and hopeless. Clients would say I maintain a trusting relationship where they can not only be open and honest but I can as well. It is my job to help each individual and relationship identify what is important and provide them tools to achieve health and happiness in their lives. I will kindly be direct, I will gently push and I will guide us to address difficult areas or behaviors of your life and relationships that may be creating barriers to growth and happiness.

name: Sophie Bloch Miller (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
You are a unique embodiment of your life experiences, and as a therapist I bring genuine curiosity to know you and understand you, and help manifest your visions and dreams. My goal is to help you live a satisfying and fulfilling life, overcoming patterns that don't serve you. I offer both practical strategies and a supportive and safe environment for processing life and relational challenges. I am certified in Relational Life Therapy, developed by Terry Real, and completed certification in Healing Our Core Issues developmental trauma model. I draw from mindfulness, cognitive behavioral therapy, attachment theory, interpersonal neurobiology, nonviolent communication, and more. We will continually assess how things are going and make adjustments to ensure that your investment in treatment is providing you with meaningful outcomes. Clients tend to experience me as warm, accepting, creative, curious, insightful and encouraging. Expect to both laugh and cry during our time together.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I am passionate about helping clients cultivate meaningful and satisfying relationships. I believe in the healing power of relationships, which has inspired my certification in Relational Life Therapy (work of Terry Real) as well as in-depth training in The Developmental Model of Couples therapy, and Gottman Couples therapy. I participate in ongoing training and consultation in RLT and find it to be an incredibly effective approach for healing old wounds and "growing up" in a way that allows us to have healthy and deeply satisfying relationships. We will examine and heal early wounds that interfere in the here and now, liberating your adult self to thrive in relationships! The RLT way is honest and direct, including "care-frontation" of ineffective patterns and honing more relational skills. I am continually humbled by the transformation that is possible when people are committed and motivated to make positive changes, especially in the context of intimate relationships.

name: Sophie Toolanen (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is mostly insight oriented, but can also be solution focused, depending on your preference. In conjunction with classic "talk therapy", I like to utilize EMDR, DBT, and IFS strategies to resolve defenses, "stuckness", interpersonal difficulties, volatile emotions, and negative self-talk... much of which is often a result of unprocessed trauma from the past. I focus on identifying how your past influences your present and finding solutions for overcoming obstacles. I think of myself as the assistant detective on a case, where you are the lead, and your life is the case. * Please note that I am only working with adults at this time.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
- I will not judge you negatively by what you share with me, no matter how "ugly" (a judgment by the way) the truth sometimes is. You don't have to worry about my ability to handle hearing your story, the way you might with friends, family, or partners. - I have extensive DBT training and experience. DBT offers strategies for regulating mood, managing self-destructive impulses, and improving interpersonal effectiveness. - I am fully trained in EMDR (Eye-Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing). EMDR is an effective approach for shifting core negative beliefs you hold about yourself and freeing you from the negative effects of past trauma. - I had a multi-cultural and multi-family upbringing and I immigrated to the US as a child, which lends itself to me having a unique understanding of issues related to cultural acclimation, not fitting in, moving, and blended families.

name: DeShawn Williams
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My philosophy is there are no two individuals alike nor are two families alike. Therefore, my practice is more integrated utilizing multiple treatment orientation depending on the need of the client. I primarily work through a CBT, psychodynamic lens however; I do like to use other orientation ex. mindfulness, narrative therapy, motivational interviewing, etc. I believe that everyone can benefit from support and that is exactly what I provide. I am a proponent of counseling not because this is the work that I do, but those who attend counseling as a preventative save themselves time and money in the long-run. Simply put, counseling is for us all no matter what stage of life we reside. I employ the idea of us working together and taking a journey that may appear to be rough in the beginning, but the rough places will soon be made straight. As we journey together in agreement there lies hopeful, fulfilled, enlightened road ahead.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Portland, Oregon native. I have been working in this field with young people and families for 15 years. I obtain my Master Degree in Social Work, and my undergraduate in Administrative Justice. I am board certified Clinical Social Worker. I have experience in treating Anxiety Disorders, Depression, working with families and clients who have experienced their first episode of psychosis. I have worked extensively with multi-cultured, multi ethnic and disenfranchised families. I have spent many years working with families in the Salem area as an Intensive Services Therapist and in Multnomah County Schools offering individual and family therapy, group work, culturally specific services, trainings and consultations. I am trainer of ASIST (Applied Suicide Intervention Skills Training) Youth Mental Health First Aid, and I am certified to do SIPS (Structured Instrument for Prodromal Syndrome) screening. I enjoy seeing families and individuals find hope in the midst of chaos.

name: Heather Hemmeger (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I received a doctor of pharmacy degree with a minor in psychology from Purdue University before completing my master’s degree in nursing from Vanderbilt University . My experience in inpatient and outpatient settings as both a pharmacist and a board-certified psychiatric mental health nurse practitioner allows me to strategically blend elements of both approaches.

name: Kim Daglen (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I know that exploring emotions and discussing difficult or traumatic life experiences takes a great deal of courage and vulnerability. I welcome and honor you and your story. I draw a variety of experiential modalities such as mindfulness and body awareness techniques to help clients access greater calm, creativity, and joy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
With a foundation of trust, we can collaborate together on achieving your goals and unearthing a sense of peace and purpose. We can work together to increase self-awareness and presence of mind to create opportunities for growth and change in your life and relationships. I value providing a respectful, compassionate, and nonjudgmental space for all clients.

name: Joy Oelfke (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I enjoy supporting individuals with sleep disturbances and insomnia, primarily drawing from CBT for Insomnia, Interpersonal Neurobiology and DBT. My experience also includes working with trauma, anxiety and depression, in cross-cultural and community systems. I strive to be LGBTQ+ affirming and intentional about cultivating a perspective of cultural/racial humility and curiosity. I am a member of the Society for Behavioral Sleep Medicine. I have completed EMDRIA approved EMDR basic training Levels 1-3 through the Parnell Institute.

name: Colleen Van Zante (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I lead with genuine empathy and a deeply inquisitive spirit, engaging directly and authentically. I strive to understand my clients’ concerns and worldviews within the context of societal, cultural, and contextual factors. Whether you have been in therapy for years or this is your first time, I welcome the opportunity to walk alongside you on this journey. I am committed to fostering an open and supportive atmosphere.
Why I Became a Therapist
I am deeply committed to helping individuals navigate emotional challenges and strengthen their relationships. My interest in Emotionally Focused Therapy reflects my passion for understanding the role of emotions in healing and connection. I hope to foster emotional resilience, guiding clients toward healthier interactions and empowering them to create lasting, positive change in their lives.

name: Carl Jensen
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is humanistic and client-centered. I value each person as an individual. I will work collaboratively with you to nurture and support the healing and changes you envision for yourself. Our overall work together often will also support movement toward psychological wholeness and spiritual integrity.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
" ... Psychotherapy and analysis are as varied as human individuals. Treat every patient as individual as possible ... the solution of the problem is always an individual one," wrote CG Jung. I believe that you believe that there is in you the possibility of being another sort of person, the one you were meant to be. I want to help you with that work. I'm a student of Jung, dream work, psychodrama, and spiritual disciplines. My background also includes Redecision therapy, group therapy, Transactional analysis, Gestalt therapy, and Structural family therapy.

name: Sonia Holdaway (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Trained as a couples therapist, my focus has always been on how we feel in relationship with ourselves and others. It can be so frustrating to feel like you are not coping or relating to others in the ways that you know you want to be, especially in this uniquely hard time. In our work, I will help you explore your pain, as well as your strengths. I will help you hold the things that make you feel worst about yourself or your relationship, and also find ways towards compassion and feeling capable and worthy of the love and acceptance you deserve. I want to empower you to connect with yourself and others on a deeply satisfying level. I will bring a calm presence and an open heart. I offer no judgment, and I believe no issue is too small or unimportant to bring to counseling. I welcome all forms of relationships and identities, and particularly enjoy working with fellow members of the LGBTQ community. Learn more about my specific therapeutic approach by visiting my website below.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am very interpersonal in my approach to therapy, and strongly believe that satisfying relationships are at the heart of growth and happiness. My clients would tell you that the therapeutic relationship is one of my favorite tools for change, and I want to genuinely know you and connect with the person that you know yourself to be. I have been working in mental health since 2013, including in eating disorder treatment and family therapy, and have a passion for supporting positive body image and sexuality. I have specific training in Emotion Focused Therapy both for individuals and couples. These experiences have come together to affirm my belief that we all deserve to have our needs met and to feel loved and accepted, most of all by ourselves. I would love to help you feel better in how you relate to yourself and your loved ones. Contact me or visit my website below for a free phone consultation, to see if this feels like a good fit for you.

name: Gretchen Klipp (SHE/ER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapeutic approach is client-centered and collaborative, based on the fact that you are the expert of your own life, with your own unique lived experiences, relationships, strengths and needs. Because we are all unique, I tailor my approach to each individual client, utilizing evidence-based techniques and methodology.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Together, we will identify and celebrate the strengths, insights and tools that you already possess and determine how we can apply those to other areas of your life that may need some love and attention. I feel honored and privileged to have the opportunity to support you on your journey.

name: Jamie Hoover (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapeutic approach is rooted in a trauma-focused, person-centered framework that prioritizes the unique needs and experiences of each client. I create a safe, empathetic environment where clients feel heard and validated, fostering a strong therapeutic alliance. Specializing in trauma, anxiety, depression, and relationship/attachment issues, I integrate evidence-based practices to support healing and growth. I empower clients by respecting their autonomy and encouraging self-reflection, helping them uncover inner strengths and resilience. By tailoring interventions to individual needs, I guide clients on their journey of self-discovery and recovery, promoting lasting change and emotional well-being.
Why I Became a Therapist
Becoming a therapist was a deeply personal choice for me, shaped by my own experiences with trauma and the absence of support during my upbringing. I intimately understand the profound impact that compassionate guidance can have, and I chose this path to be the change I wished I had in my own life. My personal history fuels my passion for helping others navigate their struggles, offering the empathy and understanding that I once needed. By providing a safe space for healing, I aim to empower clients to overcome their challenges and find resilience within themselves. My journey from adversity to advocacy drives my commitment to making a positive difference in the lives of others.

name: Sarah Mustafa (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer individual, couple, and family therapy. My style is interactive, cultural, empathic, warm, direct, and intuitive. I share the premise that human behavior occurs within family, social, and cultural contexts. Additionally, I have a multicultural approach that bridges cultures of all kinds allowing for understanding and sensitivity to help clients feel more comfortable being understood and validated in their experience. Finally, I know the importance of understanding how race, gender, social class, sexual orientation, abilities, language, country of origin, and other social identities/locations intersect to maintain systems of power and privilege within couples, families, and communities. With that, I value a therapy that is non-pathologizing, non-blaming, and sees clients as experts in their own lives. So, as your therapist, my motto is to be your co-pilot to help support clients in increasing their self-compassion and self-confidence to achieve the life goals they desire.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have always been interested in relationships & what it takes to make them work. I come from a big family with many siblings and parents from different cultures. So I have grown up with an ongoing urge to learn how to navigate multiple relationships and be a part of two different cultures. Relationships of all kinds can be tricky & source exceptional joy & significant discomfort. Working with families and couples to connect well and find comfort with differences has been rewarding work. I support individuals to learn to have the courage to speak their truth with honesty and compassion. Feelings are complex. Creating understanding for people to create authentic conversations and emotionally healthy relationships can make room for connecting better to find a solution. As a multiracial person of color (Saudi Arabian & Black American), I cherish culture's uniqueness in people's lives. My cultural background has allowed me to be sensitive to cultural diversity & appreciate all cultures.

name: Emma Bridges (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker and I help those who seek to heal from trauma as a Certified Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR) therapist. EMDR is a highly researched technique that is uniquely effective at freeing people from traumatic experiences. I also delight in supporting those with ADHD. I enjoy acting, filmmaking, and comedy in my spare time. Seeking counseling is an act of bravery--I am here to travel alongside you and offer support. My goal is to put you at ease and offer exceptional support as you find your most grounded and healing path forward.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe Black lives matter and social justice is of core importance to therapy. I welcome and empower LGBTQIA+ clients. I inspire growth in my clients with gentle encouragement, evidenced based practices, and humor. I offer support to people with relational, career, creative, and personal challenges through a trauma-informed lens. I use CBT and mindfulness to help clients overcome anxiety, depression, or attachment challenges in order to help make life dreams come true. I see Oregon and California residents. My goal is to put you at ease and offer exceptional support as you find your most grounded and healing path forward. Email hello@emmabridgeslcsw.com for a free 20-minute consultation.

name: Aaron Kelsay (HE / HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My goal in working with a client is to help them develop the insight, skills, strengths, resources, and supports to work toward that state of being "healthy." I work with the best available, evidence-based modalities to support people on their journey towards health. This includes EMDR, trauma-based, and attachment-based modalities. What does all this mean? When you find yourself on your way towards healing, it will be because you have brought along with you many of the tools that we have developed and integrated in our sessions together. That may mean that the EMDR has re-wired your response to trauma memories. It may mean that you have developed new behavioral habits and routines, new boundaries, new ways of getting your needs met. As we help you on your journey, I will offer the best available skills, supports, resources for your healing, and my hope is that you will take away a few of the best fitting ones for you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
When I was younger, I was constantly anxious. I would read everything all at once for school, get all my HW done as fast as possible, worry about and plan for every possible scenario. Through my own counseling work, I've managed to slow down and better adapt to that way of thinking and being. It was also over the course of that healing experience that I noticed a shift in who I was and how I wanted to be in the world. I've shed and deconstructed much of my religious, spiritual, political values, just as I shed much of the pressure, self-criticism, and anxiety of my youth. What I kept from my upbringing was the "good stuff." I still value the ideals of love, hope, peace. The "love your neighbor" way has a more holistic meaning for me now in my chosen role as a counselor. Outside of the counseling office, my family and I choose to be open and affirming with the LGBT community, support BLM, and to work towards improving the material and spiritual conditions of all people.

name: Jenna Washburn (SHE / HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My counseling style is one based on empathy. Empathy is healing. It’s empowering. And it provides a space free from judgement where we can go beneath the surface of a problem and explore its depths. Weeding out the root of a problem is how to create real transformation and revolutionary change I have a double Masters degree in Counseling and also in the the field of Sociology. My education has given me a unique perspective to explore with both a micro and a macro lens. I seek to know my clients as novel individuals with their own unique experiences. And to also understand them within the context of their cultural and societal backgrounds. My theoretical orientation includes narrative therapy, depth psychology, attachment studies, and family systems. I tailor my approach based on each client’s needs and wants, incorporating different behavioral, cognitive, or emotional interventions as suits each individual person.
More About the People I Treat
As a fellow Empath and Highly-Sensitive Person, I understand the hardships us Empaths face. I understand the struggle to create healthy boundaries. The overactive caretaking of others to the point of fatigue. The chronic perfectionism and believing we are never doing enough. The endless chain of relationships with people exhibiting narcissistic behavior. That’s why I’ve created Focused Therapy to address the above issues and to empower you to heal, grow, and ultimately thrive.

name: Polly Gravely MS, LPC, MFT (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I’m trained in relational dynamics with a specialty in Emotion Focused Therapy and differentiation. These skills help me track how people get stuck in rigid ways of relating, either with themselves -- such as stuck in loops of anxiety or depression -- or in how they relate with someone else – perhaps reactive patterns of criticism and defensiveness. We are all constantly in relationship, either with people around us, or with ourselves. We learn our patterns from our first family and typically repeat variations of the same pattern. I help by increasing your awareness about these relational patterns – internal and the external, to notice where there’s constriction and limitation, and why. When we have awareness, we can have positive change. Common issues I treat: Individuals: a self-critical voice that creates defeat; a fearful inner voice that undermines confidence. Couples: codependency, disconnection, reactivity, unhealthy conflict or avoidance of it, discrepancy of sexual desire.
Why I Became a Therapist
Twenty something years ago I worked as a reporter in the US Senate in Washington D.C. Every day I had to find and report a story and it was typically related to conflict and clearly dysfunctional. I moved to Portland and retrained as a therapist. These days I have such a meaningful career helping people out of stuck places, and often sorting through conflict to resolution! There is a way through and it begins with understanding. I listen to what's underneath people's words, below the reactive emotions, and support growth into a more integrated, empowered life.

name: Melissa Perry (THEY/THEM/SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We often think of adventure as an outer process, AND there is an amazing inner world available to us, impacting our lives daily, a world that can be mysterious, disturbing, and exciting. I am here to support this inner exploration, while also making the link to the outer world. Together we will delve into aspects of your inner and outer world and see what they have to teach - finding out more about you are, and who you are becoming. With my supportive, loving presence and skill, we will adventure together to create more of the life you want. We will explore the places you come to over and over again that seem to stop you from growth and change, and you will learn how to use your biggest difficulties to see the gifts that lie beneath the false ideas of “broken” and “wrong.” I create a space that welcomes and values all your different parts, and I will approach your joys, desires and disturbances with curiosity, respect and heart.

name: Brandy Halprin (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My background includes work with children, older adults, those with life-limiting illness, and people struggling with grief, loss, and adjustment to life transitions. My approach is collaborative, conversational, and warm. I incorporate mindfulness and parts work to help you learn to manage every day struggles. I will continually work with you to set and re-evaluate your therapy goals, then tailor your sessions to work toward those goals.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe that the central task of therapy is finding the balance between acceptance and change - acceptance of what is and change to enable what can be. I believe that, like plants which put on new leaves every year of their existence, we all have the capacity to grow at every stage of our lives.

name: Jen Yerty (THEY/SHE)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
You can change your own life. I view my role as guiding that process, to help you identify and process things that might be getting in the way of you becoming the best version of yourself. My work is based on hope, and a strong belief in every person's ability to change, grow, and heal themselves. I tailor the therapeutic experience to the client, but generally find success with somatic interventions, mindfulness, narrative, experiential, behavioral, emotion-focused process work, and yoga, influenced by Zen philosophy of acceptance, non-attachment, self-kindness, and non-judgment, and I feel strongly that therapeutic collaboration with clients is crucial to treatment. I'm originally from the Midwest but have lived on the East and West Coasts, so I talk fast, am direct but empathic, and swear a lot with clients who are comfortable with that. I've been in Portland for over a decade now, and I share a space with 3 (loud) cats and a dachshund.
More About the People I Treat
I hold a CADC-I, and am trained in Somatic Experiencing and Brainspotting to facilitate trauma processing, and have a 200-RYT certification in yoga. Social justice is an important part of my therapeutic orientation and philosophy, and I prioritize working with marginalized populations, including LGBTQ and people of color. I have experience working with clients with complex trauma, dissociation, attachment challenges, mood disorders, psychosis, personality disorders, and marginalized populations, including BIPOC, LGBTQ+, and houseless populations. I received my Masters in Counseling Psychology at Santa Clara University (Santa Clara, CA) a Masters in Adult Education and Training, and my Bachelors in English Language & Literature at the University of Michigan (Ann Arbor).

name: Regina Tricamo (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker and have been working in Portland since 2015. Sessions are collaborative, focusing first on your strengths and what is going well. Then, as we look at the difficulties you’re facing, we build on those abilities. I encourage listening to your own voice and building confidence as you experience transitions and challenges. You can expect sessions that are supportive, compassionate and sometimes challenging, with a good bit of humor. Using a Family Systems framework we identify how you are shaped by your family of origin and the impact that those patterns and roles play in your current relationships and sense of self. We work on the ability to make connections, create community, strengthen your voice and to expand your passions and interests. Additionally, we can draw upon Sensorimotor Psychotherapy, cognitive behavioral therapy and mindfulness to help support understanding and change.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have lived in Portland since 1997 and attended graduate school at PSU for my Masters in Social Work. I grew up in San Francisco and lived for years in Brooklyn, NY and carry a bit of the sensibility of each of those cities with me.

name: Vedalia Zellers (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In therapy, my aim is to help you heal more deeply (from any loss, trauma, or pain that feels "stuck") by gradually traveling to places within yourself that you might be afraid to go. I hope that my accompaniment will make this task feel possible and that the rewards will be rich. In the process, you might discover a part of yourself – perhaps your capacity to play, rest, dream, grieve, be intimate, or receive care, for instance – that you buried long ago to survive. Recovering and reclaiming these parts, I believe, can help us feel more alive, expanding our sense of what is possible.
My Values as a Therapist
I’m a white, queer, disabled woman with a lifelong visual processing disorder. Having a body and brain that differ from the so-called “norm” has led me to work, in concert with others, toward a more just and inclusive world. I bring an anti-oppressive lens to my work, meaning I recognize systems of oppression impact the mental health of individuals. Although we can’t “solve” wider issues of injustice within individual therapy, we can explore how those broader realities have affected you, and we can unpack “internalized” oppression (i.e., harmful beliefs you may have unintentionally absorbed). I'm influenced by disability justice: a framework of values developed by BIPOC, queer, disabled activists in the Bay Area which promotes the collective liberation of disabled people. Before becoming a therapist, I spent four years in rehabilitative healthcare working with survivors of Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI) and eight years doing non-profit work including union and community organizing.

name: Coleen Moreno (SHE/ HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Coleen Moreno has years of 15+ experience helping adults grow into the people they want to be. She would like to offer to walk with you to encourage greater psychological flexibility and emotional safety. Maybe you are worn thin by old patterns but don’t know how to get to the next good place? Perhaps you have been listening to the voice of an Inner Critic and have judged yourself too harshly, or even unfairly? Coleen appreciates where you are and invites you to explore what you want for yourself right now. She offers sessions for individuals age 21+ and is open to address important relationship concerns, but does not offer couples work. Coleen is licensed in Oregon (L5249) and Washington (LW 60681599)
My Values as a Therapist
Counseling can be an act of healing and redefining purpose. Clients notice they have more freedom to unearth questions about themselves and often find a revised sense of meaning. I enjoy the process of watching people find their way to feeling better. Some of the skills I offer in session can help. EMDR and other trauma-informed methods can greatly reduce anxiety symptoms. Looking at the source of a client’s depression and how it has functioned can be freeing. In session, the use of action methods, such as inner dialogue and role reversal, can bring understanding and healing to relationships. Tools such as NVC (Marshall Rosenberg's work) often helps clients communicate with less emotional strain and guilt. Few of us are taught how to understand and manage emotions and unhelpful thoughts. To be able to walk with another and explore how it feels to be living this human life is such a valuable moment and is worth the time and effort.

name: Sabrina Sheehy (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I primarily view treatment through an attachment lens, however I also have training in various modalities, including Cognitive-Behavioral, Solution-Focused, Humanistic, Relational, and Trauma-Informed therapy. Having a background in clinical social work means I see each client in a holistic way, with the understanding that ones' community and family systems impact their overall well-being. I have worked with adults, children, and families in both schools and in community mental health settings. I have a gained specialty training and practice in working in perinatal mental health and have enjoyed supporting birthing individuals and their partners during pregnancy and the post-partum period. Experienced in serving the LGBTQIA+ community, I enjoy helping individuals navigating life transitions, whether it be more fully expressing one's gender or coming to terms with one's sexual orientation later in life. I also work with providers in the medical and mental health community.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a proud and passionate member of and advocate for the LGTBQIA+ community and welcome clients of all sexual orientations and/or gender identities. I also strive to live and practice with an anti-racist framework, and actively look for opportunities to become a more knowledgeable and competent advocate for racial justice and disability justice.

name: Lindsay Howson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
The fact that you are visiting this page implies that you have been through and are going through some very difficult, confusing, and painful life experiences. I understand how difficult it is to share these intimate experiences with someone you do not know and I am honored to be the person who is able to partner with you as you strive to heal. If you are experiencing depression, anxiety, outbursts of anger, relationship problems, and suspect that past experiences may be impacting your life, it would be my honor to help you find insight, healing, and joy as you heal.
My expertise is in working with survivors of sexual abuse and survivors of domestic abuse. I have experience with the leading treatments to help survivors heal and I will work with you to determine what will be the most helpful treatment for you.

name: Sarah Ellenwood (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I wholeheartedly believe in this process and love the deep dive of therapy—the engagement with stories, emotions, patterns, and change. It’s a joy when clients really "bring it” to our work together. I also recognize that everyone’s path to healing is unique, which is why I believe in attachment-based therapy. This allows clients to verbalize unspoken pieces of their story—things they’ve sometimes never had the courage to admit to themselves. Bearing witness makes this work life-affirming, and can truly change your life. I use a relational and nonjudgmental style to build relationships with clients. I incorporate Emotionally Focused Therapy, a trauma informed lens, practical skill building, and humor to help clients grow. I also offer walking sessions when it's a good fit.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
IT BEGINS WITH THE CREATIVE PROCESS I’ve always viewed therapy as a creative, one-of-a-kind, collaborative process between therapist and client(s), and believe all individuals inherently know how to heal themselves, but need guidance and support when they have lost their way. I avoid pathologizing people and their thoughts, feelings, and actions. OPEN AND AFFIRMING I also know how oppressive our cis/het-centric culture can be, and am open and affirming to the many ways that people create and maintain relationships, including: LGBTQI+, BDSM, kink, non-monogamous or polyamorous relationships, and sex positive attitudes and choices. My goal is always to create a safe space and I’m celebratory in my approach to sexual and gender minorities. A note to LGBTQ Clients: I am not only a LGBTQ-affirming therapist; I am LGBTQ informed. If this is a component of treatment for you, or part of who you are, get yourself qualified help.

name: Brandon Johnson (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach starts with respecting each person’s story and perspective and builds toward goals from there. I value engaging people holistically in the process of healing, pursuing goals, and seeking resolution. I work with couples, families, adults, and teens in a variety of approaches, based on the needs of each person or family. Every person and family are different and are worth responding to in a way that fits them uniquely. At the same time, certain underlying aspects of being human are shared by us all, and I try to connect through those pieces of shared humanity to bridge across differences and build understanding and real, shared experience in our time together. Some of the ideas I draw on to do that work is from Emotionally Focused Therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, Interpersonal Neurobiology, and other sources.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe all of us are worth something, even when we struggle to see it in ourselves or each other. Learning how to make sense of ourselves, each other, our history, and our present helps to put things in order and allows us to feel at peace within ourselves, even when life around us may still be unpredictable or out of control. I hold onto hope for more joy and peace, even and especially when my clients can't do that for themselves, with the goal of sharing it together. My role is to join you regardless of anything in your past or present so that we can calm the storm, find the break in the clouds, and rise into the sunshine together.

name: Anya Hicks (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that every person is born with intrinsic value while utterly dependent on caregivers who may do a good job or fail. But they do the best they could with what they had at the time. As you mature and adjust to others in order to fit in, your life becomes fragmented depending on the values, roles, and supports in your life. You may be vigilant and work hard to prevent disapproval, but this limits intimacy and trust. Do you control how close others are allowed into your world? You also may be a peacemaker and give to others more than they give back. Do you feel resentful or unappreciated? Do you bury anger and fear only to have them show up unexpectedly and hurt others? I do not judge people or behaviors. I believe in body-mind connection and the healing power of greater integration. There are several effective techniques, but my favorite is Brainspotting which allows you to calibrate your own processing, release stowed away trauma and rewrite your story.
Why I Became a Therapist
My career path to therapy was not linear, but every stage of life fits in now like a glass tile into a beautiful mosaic. Journalism taught me how to interview people with ease and it became handy for case management at Aging and Disability services. Stay-at-home parenting gave me appreciation for playfulness and humility to learn from younger generations. As a linguist, I worked inside court rooms and jail, assisted patients during acutely painful moments and at the end of life. But most of my involvement had to do with "side effects" of clients' circumstances. I desired to become and trained to be a healing presence in this world providing hope and tools for the clients to live better. I knew therapy was a good career because some gifted therapists helped me deal with similar struggles I help my clients. Some of the adversities in my life include loss of a parent at a young age and diagnosis of ADHD.

name: Erica Ochsenreither (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I am a mental health counselor specializing in art therapy. I practice from an integrated model that combines expressive arts, somatic, trauma-informed, Internal Family Systems, Polyvagal, and attachment approaches. I draw upon these modalities and theories to craft an individualized and holistic approach. Additionally, I am trained in EMDR. What this can look/feel like in session is focusing on exploring and holding space for all parts of you, guiding you to become curious about what is happening in your body, offering resources to establish more safety, safely approaching what feels overwhelming, and reclaiming your wholeness.
My Approach to Helping
I am passionate about providing a warm, collaborative, empathetic space with opportunity for embodied healing. I will support you in weaving together thoughts, feelings, sensations, and beliefs in a safe and supportive relationship, deepening your awareness and, at times, challenging you toward your potential. Come as you are, to a soft place to land, where you can feel wholeheartedly human. I am always honored when folks resonate enough with my profile to want to speak with me. If you would like to learn more about what therapy might look like or how I can support you, please contact me via phone or email. I look forward to connecting with you and honor the opportunity to walk alongside you through this journey.

name: Lesley Burke (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is very client-centered and I tend to be more non-directive although I do provide feedback and reflections, and will introduce skills and practices when appropriate. I believe everyone deserves a chance to heal and that therapy can help facilitate a connection to a client's inner healing intelligence. Some of the ways I approach therapy include art therapy, somatic therapy, parts work, and transpersonal therapy. I specialize in psychedelic integration and transpersonal art therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Art making is a personal path for me that is about much more than the finished work. From processing emotions and experiences, to moving energy or simply enjoying the flow state and process of creation, the creative process is the best healer. My counseling and psychology training was very structured, clinical and scientific. Always fascinated by nature and science, I can be a quiet observer, logical thinker and problem solver but I am first and foremost an artist and mystic at heart. In my free time when I am not making art I like to go hiking, work out, spend time with my pets, and take care of my many houseplants and herb garden. My personal spiritual beliefs and practices include nature-based spirituality, yoga philosophy, hermeticism, and I identify as a witch and recovering Catholic.

name: Kimberly Crow (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work from an integrative perspective, combining aspects of person-centered therapy, Motivational Interviewing, Mindful Self-Compassion, and psychodynamic perspectives. My goal is to collaborate with you to create a clear direction in the therapy process. I want both of us to know what issues we are navigating in order to use our time together well. You will have a strong voice in your treatment plan. I support harm reduction practices for many individuals. Group therapy is a passion for me. I lead groups for early recovery support, family members of opiate addicts, and creative blocks. I seek to provide a respectful and safe space to work in together.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My practice is built around an idea I learned as an actor: "Really talk, really listen." This means I work to be present and connected so our dialogue is honest and authentic. I focus on treating co-occurring mental health and substance use problems and previously spent 5 years working in residential substance use treatment. I graduated from Lewis & Clark College with an MA in Professional Mental Health Counseling - Addictions. I completed further training in Trauma Informed Care at Portland State University.

name: Heather Phillips (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
My work as a therapist has been focused on the goal of helping children and families thrive. For the last 5 years I have worked in a medical clinic with Nurse Midwives, OBGYNs, and other providers as a therapist. In this role I provided mental health therapy to treat mood disorders, and provided support with various issues related to parenting including adjustment, navigating high risk pregnancies and parenting children with medical issues, grief and loss related to pregnancy loss and/or infertility, and healing from birth related trauma. I am licensed as a Perinatal Mental Health Clinician (PMH-C) through Postpartum Support Int'l and I started my career working for over ten years as Mental Health Therapist in school-based health clinics. That experience was foundational to my work supporting folks in all stages of their journeys to either become parents, parent children in their care, or move through traumatic grief and loss.
My Values as a Therapist
I value and respect the diversity of individuals, and my practice is rooted anti-oppressive and anti-racist frameworks. I am committed to lifelong learning and cultural humility, and make this a priority in my professional and personal education and growth. I welcome individuals from all cultures, races, abilities, religions, sexual orientations, and gender identities to work with me.

name: Jon Fox
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I draw from gentle and highly effective mindfulness-based, mind-body approaches to counseling. These approaches help clients make profound changes and rapid progress towards their goals. I received a Masters degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from Portland State University in June 2012. I completed my internship at the M.E.T.A.(Mindful Experiential Therapy Approaches) Counseling Clinic in Portland, Oregon, where I counseled individuals, couples, and groups. In June 2013, I graduated M.E.T.A.’s Two-Year Comprehensive Training in Hakomi, RC-S, attachment interventions, and trauma release methods.
My Values as a Therapist
Like you, I’ve struggled with old emotional wounds and hard to break habits that have inspired me to embark on the path of increasing my self-awareness, self-acceptance, and sense of connection with others. I believe in your innate wholeness and value your unique situation, perspective, and path. I know the importance of creating a safe, secure, and open relationship with you that can guide and support your growth and healing and allow you to explore your creativity, passions, and connection to your inner guide.

name: Sarai Engel (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
The therapeutic relationship is always at the core of my work with clients, and I aim to establish a sense of trust and safety with each individual. It is important to make therapy a place that feels positive and supportive… and even fun! I work primarily from a psychodynamic and attachment-based perspective, while also drawing from trauma-informed care and interpersonal neurobiology. I am rather eclectic and flexible in my approach, and I collaborate with my clients adjusting to each individual’s preference and therapeutic need. Most children and teens (and even adults!) do not want to talk about their problems. This doesn’t mean they are unable to or unwilling to process their feelings, but it does mean they may need a more creative approach than just sitting in a room and talking to an adult. I work hard to try to find the best means of expression for each client. This can include creating visual art, sharing music, movement, or imaginative play and storytelling.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that most people are doing the best they can, despite the many challenges, behaviors, emotional roller coasters, and transitions that can come up in life. My mission, as a therapist, is to come alongside others in a way that can support and draw out their best selves. I have found that this often comes through creativity, playfulness, and humor.

name: Audra Carmine (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I earned my Masters degree in Clinical Psychology at Antioch University, and am certified in Somatic EMDR from the Embody Lab as well as Applied Polyvagal Theory in Yoga levels 1-3—both trainings led by Dr. Arielle Schwartz. I completed an 8 month training with Thomas Hubel on Ancestral Healing and Trauma, and a 16 week Breathwork course with Breathworker and author Jennifer Patterson. I also am a certified meditation teacher through Jack Kornfield and Tara Brach’s two year intensive training program.
My Values as a Therapist
My practice is rooted in an ethos of love, connection, kindness, & compassion. It is often our disconnection from care that leads us toward dis ease, anxiety, & that unbearable feeling of wanting to crawl out of our own skin. It is our reconnection with loving kindness that heals us. Our stories of anxiety and trauma are suddenly less alone. Therapy is, at its most essential, two individuals in a caring relationship. I have learned through years of therapy myself that I am deserving of love simply because I exist--and so are you. We get hurt by people, so we must heal with people. Therapy is a brilliant container in which to grow and remember who we really are. Without it, I would not be here. I would still be exhausting myself trying to get the love I so desperately wanted. Therapy is not the only way to grow and heal; however, it is a really clear, accessible, and effective path toward self-actualization, a more interesting life, strong boundaries, & love.

name: Shay Larken
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work with complex trauma and grief and the ways it manifests in our mind and body, coping strategies, and relationships. While relationships can cause harm, relationships can also heal. My work draws from multiple mind-body therapies and theory, including: polyvagal therapy, somatic therapies, Internal Family Systems (IFS) parts work, AEDP, Interpersonal Neurobiology, and mindfulness. I incorporate a bottom-up (somatic) and a top-down (cognitive) approach, where you learn to move into the felt experience of deep emotions and sensations, but also engage your ability to challenge trauma shaped beliefs about yourself and others. I offer a warm and authentic presence, combined with a commitment to staying current on leading research and practices in complex trauma therapy, providing you with a supportive path toward healing, growth, integration, and connection.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received my Masters in Professional Mental Health Counseling from Lewis & Clark College. I am a Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC), and a National Certified Counselor (NCC). During my prior corporate career, I felt a calling into a healing profession, but this required a radical life shift, one where I moved into a place of stillness, turned inward, called upon profound courage, and relied on my intuition to choose a new path. I know what it looks like to appear high functioning, but feel tormented and confused by the dark remnants of trauma within the body, beliefs, and relational patterns. My move into this work is rooted in the awareness that we cannot heal alone, and recovering from trauma and how it shows up in our life must tend to all of you - your mind, body, emotions, and soul. I believe we heal when we are seen, heard, and known. Seeking help is brave and your inquiry will be met with compassionate attention.

name: Laura Birchard (SHE/ HER)
approaches:
My Background
Laura Birchard, LPC is a depth-oriented psychotherapist in private practice, serving individuals in Portland, Oregon. She specializes in therapy for teens and adults, offering an insight-driven and transformative process. Depth psychotherapy creates space to engage with the living mystery—the edge between what is known and unknown. Through dreams, images, creative expression, and relationship dynamics, Laura helps clients reconnect with their inner depths and move toward greater self-integration. Laura has extensive clinical experience in outpatient, residential, and emergency psychiatric settings, working with individuals and families. She has specialized training in depth-oriented, psychodynamic, Sandplay, and experiential therapy. Her work is guided by the language of the unconscious—symptoms, symbols, and creative processes—inviting clients into a deeper dialogue with themselves.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
One of the paradoxes of therapy is that it is both deeply personal and highly professional. My practice is grounded in depth and analytical psychology (Jungian), trauma-informed care—including somatic therapies—feminist theory, and psychodynamic approaches. I also have additional training in assessment and DBT. I am committed to being an ally and anti-racist. Jungian analyst Marion Woodman wrote, “mystery is the depth of the sacred.” The sacred is not bound to any specific religion—it is woven into the very fabric of our being, connecting us to the earth, the stars, and one another. At times, this connection may feel fractured or lost, leaving us unmoored. Therapy offers a space to rediscover hope and deepen self-awareness. Beyond my practice, I find inspiration wandering forest trails, volunteering in my community, and immersing myself in yoga, meditation, and creative practices. Reading and creative expression are integral to my own ongoing inner work.

name: Erin Webb
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I provide individual and family therapy for youth (age 12 and up). I offer a free video consultation, so we can discuss how I can help. During sessions your teen will learn and practice calming skills and mindful self-compassion to increase feelings of connection, happiness and relaxation. We will also work on effective communication, exploring values and finding meaning in life to improve motivation. I love supporting and guiding youth as they learn to manage emotions and stress better and become more comfortable being themselves. I will listen closely to your teen and offer support, validation and humor. I will also encourage them to consider other perspectives, take responsibility, set goals and envision their future. Please visit my website for more information about my private practice. After-school and evening appointments are available.
More About the People I Treat
I also specialize in individual therapy for highly sensitive people and women who have experienced childhood loss, abandonment and trauma. I have found that combining the following approaches supports healing and change on a much deeper level: Polyvagal Theory, Internal Family Systems, Art Therapy, EFT tapping, Mindfulness and Emotion Code. However, services are individualized to fit your needs.

name: Dave Davis
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a therapist, I am committed to finding a balance between what I see as the depth oriented psychological traditions and the more practical evidence based treatments. I think it is important to develop insight into the structures and meanings of your life, and to understand your own values and aspirations. This insight and clarity is healing in its own right, but often in order to make lasting change, we need to develop skills and habits of mind that really move us in our intended direction. This is where the more practical therapies come in. We need to practice being who we want to be, not merely hope for change. I see my role as helping you get a birds-eye perspective on your life and aspirations, and then to help you develop ground-level practices and skills that align with and move you toward the vision of who you want to be in the world.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In 2010 I earned a master’s degree from Seattle University in existential-phenomenological psychology. This program emphasizes a way of seeing and being-with another person that focuses on their lived-experience of being human, with all its complexity and ambiguity, joys and tragedies. I consider this to be the grounding of my practice as a therapist, but I am also a pragmatic person, and my main purpose in being a therapist is to help. With this in mind, I rely on existential psychology to help people see their lives clearly, and define their goals for treatment. Then I draw on evidence-based practices, such as cognitive behavioral therapy and mindfulness, to bring about change in a future-oriented and timely manner.

name: Zara Roth (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I hold a graduate degree in mental health counseling from Northwestern University, as well as a certificate in child and adolescent psychology from The Chicago School of Professional Psychology. My therapeutic approach is eclectic and well-rounded, and I have a background in psychodynamic psychotherapy which allows me to dive into the unconscious, create self-awareness, and formulate practical solutions for clients dealing with trauma, personality disorders, depression, and anxiety. I previously worked at a DBT clinic specializing in borderline personality disorder and high-acuity patients; as well as at a rehab clinic where I led the mental health IOP group therapy program for those with PTSD and severe anxiety/depression. I aim to create an environment of validation, authenticity, unconditional positive regard, acceptance, and inclusion in my practice.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I take an individualized approach to each client and tailor treatment plans based on their unique and specific needs. I use established therapeutic modalities and somatic, sports, music, art, and laugh therapy. I believe that many existential questions can be answered using the wisdom of philosophers throughout history. I have a particular interest in working with marginalized communities and advocating for multicultural and feminist issues. I believe a main factor in mental health issues is the natural human reaction to a society which makes us sick. My focus includes the psychology of capitalism, complex trauma, ADHD, BPD, eco-anxiety, non-dogmatic spirituality, and psychedelic therapy.

name: Kristina Barnett (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I'm a systems thinker with a background in sociology and marriage and family therapy. I always consider your context including cultural, familial, and relational factors. I use a variety of models primarily centered around EFT and IFS, which are both models that focus on attachment, relational wounds, and creating secure bonds through a combination of mindfulness and reparative experiences in therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I bring an intersectional feminist, anti-racist, LGBTQIA2+-affirming, sex-positive perspective to the therapy process. I believe pleasure and ease are human birthrights. I believe the closer we can get to living out an authentic expression of our full, unique selves, the better we all are.
