I will co-journey with you to explore the depths and peaks of your experiences, and to provide support and reminders of your greatness along the way.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I embrace evaluating the “light” and “dark” within you, as well as the many roles and identities you simultaneously juggle as an individual. I want to take the time to learn about your path thus far, and to collaborate with you about potential obstacles that prevent you from living a life more aligned with your own values and sense of inner and outer peace.

name: Kimberly Dudley (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
*Therapy should be accessible for everyone. Let me know if you need a sliding scale, someone on my team might be able to help.* I utilize an integrative approach that focuses relational neuroscience (understanding the intimate connection of your mind and body), attachment science and story informed trauma recovery. I work from the perspective that people make decisions based on the information available to them, and I seek to empower all of my clients through education. I'll guide you through the process of truly knowing your story in uncovering what's worked for you and what has hindered you from feeling balanced and successful in your life. We'll work together to discover who you are beneath all the stress and chaos you've endured.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Leading people through the process of recovering from their stories of trauma is my life's work. I know the amount of courage it takes to face the hardest parts of our stories not knowing whether it's going to be worth the effort and discomfort it takes to work through to the other side. I've walked this path with many people and I'll trust the process for you until you can trust it within yourself. I have trained extensively to understand the long term effects of childhood adversities and traumas including domestic abuse, neglect, sexual traumas, and betrayal traumas such as infidelities. There is hope for life to feel different and I'm here to help you find it. As a doctoral candidate, I'm committed to training in trauma recovery both for my clients and for myself. It's my passion to train interns how to join the field as trauma helpers and we work diligently to be protective and insightful for our clients.

name: Avery Lewis (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy integrates relational-cultural, psychodynamic, and somato-emotional perspectives. These perspectives hold that we are shaped by our histories, both personal and collective, and that we carry patterns of thinking, feeling, and relating in our bodies throughout our lives, and across generations. By exploring our histories, we can often gain insight into our current ways of being, find greater understanding and compassion for ourselves and others, and pave the way for agency, choice, and deep connection in our lives.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a therapist, I aim to provide a collaborative, liberatory, and trauma-informed environment for you to explore your own depths, and attune to your own body and wisdom. I hold awareness of how dynamics of power and oppression may impact your health and wellbeing, and I strive to embody curiosity, respect, and cultural humility in my work. I believe that all people have the innate capacity to heal, and that healing happens when we center dignity, safety, and belonging.

name: Justin Panneck (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I specialize in life coaching, spiritual development, personal growth, getting unstuck, life transitions, career success, self-esteem and self-image, confidence, men’s issues, post-divorce for men. One of my most passionate interests and endeavors is utilizing a unique balance of humor, creativity, and psychological-cultural science. Over the last decade, I have combined my nature-based, psychedelic, and clinical experience toward a focus on optimal living and radical flexibility and achievement, employing peak performance components, which includes such concepts as flow states, “bullet-proof mind” modalities, authenticity, motivational or transformational coaching and behavioral health support (nutrition, movement, etc.).
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
When we get hit by a major life disruption, we often freeze with indecision and fear. The life transition is how we get out unfrozen and move into a period of reinvention and renewal. These transitions may involve confronting our emotions and turning to rituals to put our past behind us; unveiling our new selves and updating our personal stories and mythologies; shedding certain habits, whether mindsets, routines, delusions, or dreams; creating new things, such as new attitudes, aptitudes, skills, talents. These transitional periods can give us superpowers or bog us down. Sometimes the best ideas come from the worst times in our lives. I have spent the last 10 years researching and in some cases facilitating plant medicines and have witnessed some of the most powerful and remarkable healing transformations

name: Jacqueline Lydston, MSW, LSWAIC (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In your therapy, I will work carefully to provide a calm, warm, safe space in which to explore your unique self, and what happiness, satisfaction, and fulfillment looks like for you. I will accompany you as you discover meaning and purpose in the face of difficult feelings, experiences, and thoughts. As therapy progresses, we will talk about everything that is important to you; therapy is about you and your life, not just the therapeutic “tricks and tips.” Your body and mind constitute an original and resilient Self, and we will base your therapy on its resources. The point of your therapy is to help you make your life better as you define it, and I will do my utmost to tailor my approach to your needs. As we work together, it is my deep hope that you come to feel that I understand, respect, and honor who you are and what you value. I work from a relational, client centered approach, informed by IFS, CBT, DBT, and psychodynamic perspectives.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that we are all unique individuals, worthy of and deserving of respect, care, ease, and comfort. I believe that healing is not only possible, it is imperative. If you have had traumatic experiences, you deserve to heal. If you are overwhelmed, you deserve to have a place in which to rest and learn new coping skills. If you are confused, you deserve to have a place in which to find clarity. And if you simply want to know yourself better and learn to value and love the unique person that you are, you deserve a place in which you can discover your uniqueness and celebrate it. I am here to help you with any or all of the above; it is my privilege to work with you toward whatever goals you wish. Therapy is about you and your goals as you define them. I pledge to work with you on that journey.

name: Lindsay Day (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I earned a Bachelor's degree in Psychology (2015) and I hold a Master's degree in Marriage, Couple, and Family counseling (2020), both from Portland State University. I have completed trainings that include risk assessment and mandated reporting, implicit bias, a 40-hour Domestic Violence training, and an Internal Family Systems (IFS) Circle Course training (2023). I have a passion for body positivity and body neutrality to combat the negative and detrimental affects of diet culture. I have knowledge , passion, and experience working with survivors of domestic violence and understand the complex nuances to that experience. As a neurodivergent clinician, I have knowledge and personal experience of ADHD and work as an ADHD teen coach as well.
My Values as a Therapist
I personally strive to be a lifelong learner and continue to grow my cultural competence as a counselor so that I may be better informed of the diverse clientele and their experiences that I hope to see and work with. As someone who holds several privileged identities, I will continue to be aware of and acknowledge the privileges that come with having those identities and how it will impact my work and future clients moving forward. I also believe strongly that mental health services and counseling should be accessible to all. I will use my privilege and position to advocate for accessibility for all in the need of mental health services and support. I also believe the relationship between client and counselor is the greatest change agent, so I focus and work to build trust and foster comfort for my client throughout the process. I strive to create a safe and comfortable place for my clients and my intentional use of humor adds to that.

name: Joy Young (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have an eclectic approach with an emphasis on psychodynamic therapy. I am down to earth, practical, supportive yet challenging in the therapy I offer. I enjoy working with clients who are proactive, engaged in improving their emotional well being, and receptive to the therapeutic process.
My Values as a Therapist
I value psychotherapy as a method to understand your emotional and mental world. Psychotherapy can help you make sense out of emotional chaos, improve your emotional well being, and support you in taking healthy actions that can change your life. I have devoted my adult life to the study, practice, and sharing of psychotherapy. I understand that talk therapy may not be right for everyone, but for those of us who have benefited from it and those who may, I offer my service to you.

name: Shannon Dooley (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Shannon has a BA in Social Science and a Masters of Social Work degree from Portland State University. Shannon has experience working as a Massage Therapist which informs her interest and approach to working with trauma. She has experience working in community mental health with people experiencing severe and persistent mental illness and housing insecurity. Shannon is trained in EMDR, and is also trained in Cognitive Processing Therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In her free time, Shannon loves comedy, little dogs, going on adventures, and being out in nature.

name: Rebeca Rocha (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I approach my work with compassion, full presence, and a nonjudgmental stance, focusing on individuals' strengths. Witnessing the transformative journey of self-acceptance is a profound passion of mine. It would be an honor to earn your trust and accompany you on this special path. Taking into account each person's unique wishes and needs, I have effectively utilized therapeutic interventions such as Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR), Somatic Experiencing (SE), Motivational Interviewing (MI), and Mindfulness. My experience includes working with diverse populations, including domestic violence survivors, unaccompanied immigrant and refugee youth, and individuals addressing trauma, grief, depression, substance use problems, psychosis, and anxiety. While recognizing the impact of oppression, ableism, racism, misogyny, heterosexism, and trauma on well-being, I commit time to understanding the resources, qualities, values, and resilience of those under my care.
My Values as a Therapist
I hold the belief that humans are inherently social beings, continually influencing and being influenced by their environment. Furthermore, I acknowledge the profound connection between the body and mind. Following traumatic experiences, our bodies may retain the impact of those events as we strive to endure and move forward with our lives. Lacking sufficient support, these traumas can become overwhelming, manifesting in diverse forms like anxiety, depression, Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD), substance abuse, and a range of other physical and mental health challenges.

name: Kiersten Lindahl (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I approach each client with the understanding that their story and experience is unique to them. I seek to join with clients in a respectful way that acknowledges their own wisdom through practical, supportive, and empathetic connection. I recognize the array of challenges a person will come into my office facing and I seek to integrate emotions, thoughts, experiences and actions to hopefully move towards a happier more fulfilled life.My work is approached from a collaborative blend of different theories including Attachment, Family Systems, Emotion-Focused, and Internal Family Systems. Some of my goals with using these theories are to increase awareness and insight around your patterns in relationships and where you are getting stuck. We may do this by using your emotions in times of distress as information rather as something to avoid or be afraid of. We may look at some parts of us that are confusing and frustrating and try to approach them with curiosity as opposed to criticism.

name: Amelia Mackle MSEd LPC RYT
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I embrace the interplay between psychology, energy, mind-body medicine and the science of the brain to approach counseling more holistically. In sessions with me, you may enjoy breath work, movement, meditation to shift neural connections toward healing and self care. Paired with traditional evidence based practices, this prepares all aspects of the self to move toward your personal goals and away from old patterns that no longer serve you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have been licensed in the State of Oregon since I moved here over a decade ago from the Mid-west. I use a variety of traditional counseling theories to provide individualized care for my clients, drawing from Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Dialectical Behavioral Therapy, EMDR and Trauma Focused Care. I am also a Registered Yoga Teacher- 200hr through Yoga Alliance and this provides me with a clearer understanding of how our mind/body connection relates to all aspects of our functioning.

name: Sarah Blaszczak, M.A, LMFT (SHE, HER, HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Between and within humans, multiple perspectives exist, and what is discovered depends on how observing happens. This understanding has led to my intense compassion for all kinds of human suffering, and delight in the unique paths forged. It is possible to ultimately drop attachments contributing to suffering, in order to experience the innately loving conscious beings we are. Here the focus is less on some idea of "right and wrong" and more on the wise discernment of what ACTUALLY contributes to suffering based on experience. My therapy practice is driven by a belief that if anyone had been through what you had been through, they would be exactly where you are. I also believe in equifinality; the idea that there are multiple paths to the same destination.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have been working in the field for about 20 years. My license has afforded me the opportunity to work with a variety of clients in an array of settings. I have worked with couples, families and individuals in private practice settings, as a therapist at a drug treatment center for youth, as the lead clinician in an inpatient program for people who identified as female struggling with dual dianoses, as the Clinical Director of a transitional housing program for women leaving prostitution and escaping sex trafficking. My current Therapy and Coaching Practice is my love and joy. I support people in seeing through psychological conditioning and becoming more stable. We can then look into the true roots of suffering. I am particularly enjoying working with people who are approaching or have had an initial non-dual or "no-self" realization, in order to integrate their new awareness and do deeper shadow work.

name: Melissa Hartley (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I draw from the integrated theoretical frameworks of Person-Centered Therapy, Feminist Theory and Internal Family Systems (IFS). IFS is a non-pathologizing, evidence based conceptual tool that sees every human being as a system of protective and wounded inner parts lead by a core Self. Just like members of a family, inner parts are forced from their preferred states into extreme roles within us. This can happen several ways, including trauma and attachment injuries. Part of therapy utilizing the IFS perspective is helping people first access their Self and, from that core, come to understand and heal their parts.
My Values as a Therapist
I am a LGBTQ2IA+ friendly/affirming, and a sex-positive therapist. I support folx in exploring and defining how they want to be in relationships (or not!) and I believe sex work is real work. These values show up in the trainings I attend, how I show up in the counseling room and how I carry myself in my personal/political actions. It's also important to me to do what I can to make therapy accessible. I know it can be a huge expense when paying out of pocket, or even trying to cover a co-pay. Please feel free to reach out to inquire about reduced fee options.

name: Julia Chapman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I believe therapy has the power to transform people's lives and improve their relationships. My approach is collaborative and relational. I strive to help clients develop goals and strategies to increase self-knowledge and awareness of strengths. I assist clients in processing how past experiences may contribute to insecurities, unhelpful patterns in relationships, and professional dissatisfaction. Throughout the therapy process I help empower clients to change their life narratives by expressing and pursuing what they want in their lives.
Why I Became a Therapist
I believe strongly in the power of therapy to transform your life. My background is in public health and sex education, but I found that working directly with people is what I was most drawn to. I began my work in Los Angeles where I lived for many years and now am pleased to call Portland home.

name: beth rossi (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I enjoy working with motivated adults with a focus on the importance of awareness, interdependence and communication. My work with adult individuals is sometimes motivated by challenging social constructions. I use a solution focused approach with behavioral, narrative and mindfulness interventions to support client treatment goals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My husband and I (and my Pomeranian Nico) moved to Oregon from NYC in 2006. I immediately began working in social services. During my early career, I was fortunate enough to be able to work with local agencies serving marginalized populations. The goals were to support client safety and encourage general health and wellness. I continue to work from this model while collaborating on therapeutic goals with my outpatient clients. I served as an Addictions Counselor and Supervisor in residential treatment settings while attending Portland State University where I received my graduate degree in social work.

name: Angie Morgan (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Let's work together to discover your best self and to help you live the life you've always wanted. I offer help for concerns such as depression, anxiety, self-esteem, life changes, and trauma. I use a strengths-based, trauma-informed, and person centered approach to counseling. I most often use IFS (Internal Family Systems), CBT, and Person Centered Therapy. I seek to provide a calm, trustworthy, non-judgmental, and supportive environment that allows for honest, real, and life-changing conversations. I believe that the best results happen when treating the whole person, which includes mental, physical, spiritual, relational, and intellectual parts of self. Let's work together to find your hope and healing!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that every person desires to obtain optimal health and well-being. I hope to provide space for people to truly be themselves and discover their full potential. I value a nonjudgmental stance, equality, inclusion, and believe that diversity is what makes this world beautiful. I am a member of the LGBTQ community and come from a religious background. I have experience in how these two worlds collide and would like to help be a bridge between the two. I support racial justice and want to be an advocate for change. In my free time, I enjoy the outdoors, hiking, camping, SUP, music, reading, and spending time with family and friends.

name: Gia Buckberg
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My hope is to work with individuals in transition who want to see results, be inspired to go further distances and reclaim their internal joy. Many of my clients come to me feeling professionally stuck or too anxious to address and tease out what it is that they truly want more of in their lives. My approach is intuitive and insightful. I listen intently and fully and appreciate humor and candor in our sessions. My approach is essentially eclectic. I honor that we as humans are unique individuals that may benefit greatly from a myriad of therapeutic supports. I often incorporate elements of CBT, solution-focused therapy and a life coaching approach into my practice. I have found the outdoor beauty of the Pacific Northwest to be incredibly healing. This inspiration has led me to offer the opportunity to conduct our sessions in the outdoors or in the office.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a MSW from the University of Minnesota and am licensed to practice clinical social work in Oregon. I have worked in a myriad of treatment facilities including residential, inpatient and outpatient centers. As a practitioner in private practice, many of my clients are challenged by life transitions, changes in their family dynamic or those who are generally feeling stuck where they are currently at in their lives. I believe that the therapeutic process is a collaborative one and I find this to be one of the most fulfilling elements of our authentic work together.

name: Diana Oxley, Ph.D.
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use depth psychology as well as cognitive/behavioral techniques as clients’ needs and wishes dictate. I have a special interest in supporting clients' efforts to find and follow a meaningful life path. I help clients look at troublesome behavior and disappointing outcomes as outward expressions of what they are struggling with at a deeper level. I encourage clients to look to their own mind and body, their dreams, and life events as sources of knowledge about themselves that they may have learned to ignore. The benefits are gaining greater objectivity about what's going on in their life and what drives it and using that understanding to make more courageous and authentic moves.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Initially I trained as a community psychologist with an interest in primary prevention of psychological problems and pursued a 30-year career in secondary school reform which included teaching adolescent development and providing technical assistance to teachers and school administrators. Only later in life did I realize my lifelong interest in Jungian psychology with re-training in psychotherapy that began at Pacifica Graduate Institute and ended at the C. G. Jung Institute in Zurich.

name: Quinn Francis (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach as a counselor is warm and collaborative, focused on creating an authentic relationship where we can learn more about ourselves and practice ways we’d like to show up in the world. I aim for my work to be anti-racist and anti-colonial, affirming of diverse gender and sexual identities as well as relationship practices, sex positive, fat positive, and aligned with disability justice principles. As a justice-oriented therapist, I try to minimize unbalanced power dynamics by being transparent, seeking feedback from clients, and unlearning my own internalized oppressions. I utilize therapeutic approaches from person-centered and relational psychodynamic theories, nervous system regulation from somatic (body-based) therapy, and offer skills for emotion regulation and distress tolerance. I also bring attention to external power dynamics that may affect each client (familial, cultural, societal) as a way of identifying where we can have the most impact in our work together.
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a counselor because of the deeply healing relationships and transformation I’ve experienced in my own therapy. My respect for each client’s autonomy and intuition is also based on personal experience – I believe we all have an inherent ability to find balance and ease, and sometimes need a trusted guide to walk alongside us. Beyond our personal struggles, none of us are immune to the harmful impacts of oppression, capitalism, and climate change that we face today. We all benefit from having a dedicated space to turn toward ourselves and make new meaning of our experiences. As a queer and non-binary trans person, many of my counseling interests focus on clients with whom I share identities. My own experiences accessing care have shown me just how valuable it is to work with a counselor who has lived experience and deep integration within community.

name: Robert Sena (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Robert initially draws upon Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) and Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) to support clients in developing more effective thought patterns and behavioral skills. As clients establish a foundation of insight and stability, Robert incorporates Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) and Choice Theory to explore identity, purpose, and direction. His approach is rooted in constructivist theory—acknowledging ego development and early conditioning—while working collaboratively with clients to construct new beliefs, patterns, and possibilities. Throughout the process, Robert integrates Lifespan Integration therapy to maintain a macro perspective, helping clients identify and shift conditioned responses through gentle, structured awareness. When the therapeutic relationship has cultivated sufficient safety and interpersonal readiness, he incorporates Gestalt and Existential therapies to explore meaning, embodiment, and the human condition more deeply.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Prior to joining The Path Center, Robert worked in both outpatient and inpatient settings, including facilitating DBT and CBT groups and supporting individuals recovering from substance use. He has also worked in private practice and provides therapy to individuals aged 15 and older. In addition to his clinical work, Robert has studied traditional healing modalities. He trained under Dr. David Winston at The Center for Herbal Studies and with 7Song at the Northeast School of Botanical Medicine. He is also a certified yoga instructor and has taught yoga, bodywork, and herbal medicine in community settings. Robert was trained in Jin Shin Jyutsu and Actaline Therapy by the late Laurie Bechler—an energy-based modality drawing from Japanese meridian systems. In his personal life, Robert enjoys playing music, caring for animals, being in nature, and exploring unexplored trails.

name: Jackie Hamm (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that you are the expert of your own experience and that you are courageous for engaging in this part of your healing journey. The hardest part is often getting started and finding the support you need. With a collaborative approach to therapy, I hope to create a space where you feel understood, respected, validated, and strengthened to explore deeper layers of your experience while being open to self-compassion and growth. Through an eclectic therapeutic approach, we will explore and better understand your identities, your culture, and your overall lived experience to help empower you in the context of your life.
My Values as a Therapist
I value social justice, human rights, equity, personal autonomy and community. I try to live my life in a way that best reflects my values, while also advocating for others when possible.

name: Brittany Neighbours (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
There are many ways to create growth, vibrancy, and peace in your life. I integrate several orientations including Reichian somatic therapy, cognitive-behavioral therapy, Gestalt therapy, trauma-informed care, and humanistic methods. I emphasize attention to the present moment so that you can really connect with yourself and make adjustments as needed. My style is thoughtful, humorous, and down-to-earth, and we'll explore your experiences gently. You have an innate drive towards wholeness and well-being, and a therapeutic relationship that is accepting, creative, and curious can support this process. I take a collaborative approach towards counseling, blending techniques to best serve each person and welcoming feedback to increase trust and safety.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that every person has the capacity for growth and to make real contact with life and others. Sometimes the things we do to protect ourselves get in the way of true contact. I have spent many years learning to be truly present and accepting of myself and my experiences, and I hope to support you in a similar process. I also believe that each person is the expert in their own life, and I want to help others understand and honor their own needs and wants. Each person strives for some sort of authentic expression, it looks different for everyone, and space is needed for that expression. In my free time, I enjoy urban hikes, hanging with my two cats, volunteering at a farm sanctuary, live music, and karaoke. I really strive to reduce the amount of harm caused to other beings and the planet as much as possible. Prior to starting my journey in mental health care, I worked in a lot of retail settings, as a sociopolitical campaign advocate, and at a movie theater.

name: MOHAMED BAH
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I possess a strong clinical skill that enhanced my natural ability to build rapport with patients and families. I follow evidence-based guidelines blended with string clinical experience, personal observation, and intuition. As a Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner(PMHNP-BC), my role is multifaceted including, patient educator, mental health provider, therapist, and scientist. As a PMHNP-BC, I bring empathy, an easygoing communication style, and a sweet sense of humor, making it easy to talk to patients and family. I specialize in depression, anxiety, schizophrenia, bipolar, trauma, and acute psychosis just to name a few. Although I am able to prescribe medication as an option, I am more successful in with holistic approach of listening and educating my patient before resorting to medications, and many times it is a combination of all three.
Our Values as Mental Health Practitioners
Life is not often filled with puppies, rainbows, and unicorns but can result in trauma, stress, and the unexpected. I know you are not looking for a provider because things are going well in your life and that is my purpose for being here. I became a nurse because I wanted to help others and give back to the world; and then, became a nurse practitioner because I felt there was more I could do to help ease suffering.

name: Naomi Berg (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is relational, collaborative, liberatory, compassionate, trauma-informed, and grounded in a social justice orientation. I view people as shaped by a multitude of factors including: ancestry, biology, family, lived experience, and social, cultural and ecological contexts. I hold an awareness of how dynamics of power and intersectional identities may impact a person's health and well-being, as well as how they show up within the therapeutic relationship. I strive to embody a respectful curiosity and cultural humility, providing space for participants to define and explore their identities, lived experiences, sources of suffering, and desires for growth and change. I believe everyone possesses an innate capacity for healing, and that this innate part thrives within the context of meaningful relationship. I lean on the therapeutic relationship as a vital resource for healing, challenge, unconditional support, and growth.

name: Hello Me Therapy Group Practice
approaches:
Our Approach to Helping
At HelloMe Therapy, we believe therapy is a journey of self-discovery and healing. Our approach is personalized, combining empathy with effective tools to help you navigate life’s challenges. We specialize in Emotional Transformation Therapy (ETT), a powerful modality that helps release emotional blocks and transform how you process difficult emotions. ETT is designed to bring rapid, lasting change. We also offer Therapy Intensives—focused, immersive sessions that allow you to make significant progress on specific issues in a condensed time frame. Whether you’re dealing with anxiety, trauma, or relationship struggles, intensives can help you gain clarity and move forward faster. Please note, we are currently unable to accept insurance for therapy services. We offer flexible payment options to make therapy accessible to those ready to begin their healing journey. At HelloMe Therapy, we’re committed to supporting you in finding balance, healing, and transformation.

name: Ryn Rising (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer a tailored approach to fit your unique needs and framework. Sessions typically involve Socratic questioning, mindfulness exercises, and creative interventions. I tend to encourage clients to bring attention to their bodies and explore how the body is responding to what we are talking about, as I believe the mind/body connection is imperative for regulation and growth. One of many ways I provide a affirming space is encouraging clients to bring their comfort items, stimming equipment, and to move their bodies in session. I attempt to minimize unbalanced power dynamics inherent in the therapeutic relationship by seeking feedback from clients on an ongoing basis. So far, clients have told me that my approach is warm, authentic, and direct.
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a counselor because of my own lived experience as a client. Overall, I thought the field lacked authenticity, empathy, and understanding that the systems are not built for most people. What was most helpful for me and what encouraged me to join the field was a therapeutic relationship that was truly transformational. I became a counselor because I want to support people through their own transformations, no matter what that may look like. I became a counselor because I wanted to change the systems within the system.

name: Lev Schneidman (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I believe in creating supportive and empowering spaces where individuals can explore, notice, and redefine intimacy and pleasure as it relates to oneself, others, and the world. My approach is first and foremost to help build, grow, and reinforce one’s relationship to oneself. I received my master's in social work at San Francisco State University in 2017. I have worked both in direct client care and in macro social work in the Oregon State Public Health Department because I believe that change must be made both on individual, community and institutional levels. My background is working with LGBTQIA2S+ communities.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In my therapeutic approach, I practice mindfulness and somatic approaches, always moving at a pace set by those I work with. This means the client leads the sessions, and I'm here to guide and support them. I believe in a person-centered and relationship-centered approach, which is also deeply rooted in trauma-informed care. Sex and pleasure positivity is an integral part of my practice. I am also polyamory and kink affirming. Together, we can explore and understand the messages your body sends – the "yes," "no," and "maybe” messages that allows one to move towards the life they want. I deeply value the courage and vulnerability it takes to begin therapy and, as a practitioner, I'm committed to fostering trust within the therapeutic relationship. I firmly believe in supporting my clients to access their internal strength and wisdom to navigate life's challenges with some support.

name: Louise Davis (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
I am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker, and received my MSW from George Mason University and have extensive experience working with adults, families and youth in the Washington D.C. area, Arizona and Oregon. In recent years, I have focused on adults experiencing life changes such as empty nesting, divorce, career changes, retirement, caring for a family member, loss/grief, and supporting professionals in the social work profession. I provide supervision to those seeking licensure and I am a RYT 200-hour yoga teacher.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My approach is to provide a safe space for individuals to process their feelings, own what is theirs, and release limiting beliefs about themselves. It is my belief that everyone has the answers within themselves and the role of therapy is to support individuals on their own unique journey. It is equally as important for a therapist to have a strong commitment to their own growth in order to show up and be present for others.

name: Kat T. Kosmos (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am committed to co-creating a compassionate, affirming, and non-judgmental space for individuals from all walks of life. I believe in building a collaborative space where your unique experiences, struggles, and celebrations are honored. I understand that life can feel overwhelming at times. I aim to help build a supportive space where you can express yourself freely, identify and express your feelings, as you work towards growth, healing and radical self-love. My therapeutic style is grounded in evidence-based practices, including Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT), and mindfulness techniques. Together, we will create a personalized plan that honors your individuality and helps you build resilience, clarity, and self-awareness.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Being human means we're always learning about ourselves in the contexts that we find ourselves in. Sometimes, that can be really overwhelming when we attach parts of ourselves to the learning process. We can find ourselves saying, "It's too late for me to learn this," or, "I should have known this [XXX] years ago." I hold the firm belief that this ability to learn, and unlearn, is something to be celebrated. It is through learning that we nurture our growth edges to becoming our authentic selves. We all have growth edges that need self-compassion and nurturing! Together, it is my goal that we unlearn some of the ways we have interacted with these areas of growth and help you move towards authenticity and healing.

name: Nora Harrington (SHE / HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My practice is called Awake Together because I help my clients connect the the open, flexible nature of their true selves in the safety of secure relationships. I work from the principle that in relationship we need to know we're safe, and we need to know we can be ourselves. I believe that we are meant to bond in the all the places we'd rather hide. My sex therapy practice is guided by the belief that we all have a libido creature, driven only by it's own pleasure, desire and joy. It belongs to us and owes nothing to others. Once we can restore this creature to it's autonomous and authentic expression, our sex lives become an adventure in play and connection. I believe that sex the best proving ground for our ability to be truly close to another human, in all the messiness of the present moment.
Why I Became a Therapist
I’m a relationship therapist because I was the worst ever at relationships. I learned all that I teach my clients through the urgency of my own pain. I believe that there was a deep injury to the humanity of my white ancestors long ago. I continue to untwist my thinking and being from this injury, both for my own liberation and in the hope that I can stop perpetuating the harm. My work in this is deliberate and ongoing. The guiding therapeutic models of my work are Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT), and Internal Family Systems (IFS). Most of the work I do is centered in attachment theory. I have a deep love for the innovation and irreverence of the Marriage and Family field. I hold the Antioch University certificates in Sex Therapy and Sex Education. I identify as a witch, folk herbalist, and ceremonialist. These practices inform me from the background as I do my attachment-based work.

name: Garrison Cox (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
All beings have their own pathways toward transformation and integration. Sometimes, the result of transformation can feel like a broken bone calcified, or a lopped-off branch scarred over. Mentally and emotionally, these wounds and raw spots revisit us, make us feel vulnerable, anxious, angry, unable to connect or belong. I draw on somatic, systemic, experiential, and existential traditions of therapy and philosophy to support clients in exploring their personal obstacles and wounds in order to clear the way for self-agency and self-resonance. In session, this looks like slowing down and examining all the information available in the present - thoughts, memories, images, emotions, bodily sensations. I believe it is not enough to just gain mental insight in order to effect change; we also have to experience life and all its joys and tribulations differently in order to recruit our entire selves into the change process.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe every person is deserving of dignity. As an existential-humanistic therapist, I also believe that addressing the symptoms of our mental health struggles is not always enough to reconnect us with ourselves. Rather than isolate and eliminate, our work together (in therapy and in life as a whole) is to connect and embrace. Common wisdom and statistical study both show that life is about the quality of our relationships - and we're all in luck; the practice of therapy happens through relationship! Through the work of therapy, we can create opportunities to experience life differently. I identify as a gay man, and enjoy working with members of the queer community. I am passionate about helping people reconnect with their emotional experience in a world that prioritizes rationalization.

name: Carrie Sturrock (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
My name is Carrie and I'm so glad you're here. I'm a Professional Counselor Associate practicing in Portland, Oregon. I graduated from Portland State University with a M.S. in Marriage, Couple and Family Counseling. I am formally trained in Internal Family Systems, which holds that we're made up of many parts, ones we may embrace and ones we sometimes try to avoid. I love this IFS phrase: all parts welcome. I enjoy helping people engage in deep emotional work to better understand themselves and how they relate to experiences and the people around them. I am interested in your story and what's important to you. Some things about me: I find the natural world a healing place to be and spend a lot of time outdoors. Previously, I had careers in journalism and public relations.
My Approach to Helping
As a therapist, I help people connect to their inner wisdom to meet life's challenges with creativity and compassion for the self and others using a mindfulness-based approach. When we're connected to ourselves and aware of our emotions and how they show up somatically in our bodies, it's easier to be connected to others — which is the foundation of healthy living. I work to foster an authentic, trusting relationship with you and a space where you can safely explore difficult issues. I have a M.S. in counseling couples and families from Portland State University. I also have specialized training in Internal Family Systems (IFS), a psychodynamic, trauma-informed approach that considers each of us made up of different parts, which can be in harmony or polarized much as members of a family can be. My couples' work is informed by Emotionally Focused Therapy and its emphasis on our felt sense of security and safety in relationships as well as reactive patterns we can get caught in.

name: Deborah Nichols LPC, NCC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Clients come to me looking for a therapist who is in it, because therapy should be a place where you feel seen and understood. Sessions with me don't always look the same. We tailor each session to your needs so that together, we nurture and empower you to move towards being the mom you have always known you could be.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a certified anxiety treatment professional and mother of two, I believe that your anxiety is telling you something. Your body is telling you something. Something must change. You don’t have to continue to suffer with the constant worry, and you don’t have to do it alone. Contact me today for a free 30-minute consultation. I can’t wait to hear from you.

name: Stephanie Podasca (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Together we will explore the many dynamic parts of your life and how they have influenced or been impacted by trauma, addiction, anxiety, relationships, and other challenges. My goal is to create a compassionate, collaborative, and trauma-informed therapeutic container that is free from judgment and pathology. I want to help you reconnect to your body, mind, and spirit and to find more acceptance and meaning in every part of life’s journey. My work does not look like regular talk therapy - I invite a lot of somatic exercises, movement, mindfulness pauses, and play to shake up those patterned responses that have been holding you back. I believe that therapy is an active process and that YOU do most of the work. The time between sessions is just as important as the time we work face-to-face. I highly value client feedback, and it’s so important that you feel you can share when things both are and aren't working for you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I bring a lot of my authentic self into therapy with clients, meaning that my work is not only informed by specific techniques and interventions or theories. My work is also informed by my worldview and personal experiences in therapy, addiction, and recovery. I wouldn't be a therapist today if it weren't for my own healing and recovery process, taking a look at the hard stuff with the help and support of a therapist and community. I believe that humor and self-compassion can heal many deep wounds, that plant medicines are an incredible tool to access alongside therapy, and that mindfulness really is a superpower we're all born with. I also have a background in art, technology, and Buddhist meditation and I try to incorporate these skill sets into everything that I do. In the past, I have volunteered for local PDX nonprofits including: Children's Healing Arts Project, Lines for Life Suicide Prevention, and Recovery Dharma.

name: Laura Riley (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work from a somatic-based, relational and holistic approach supporting individuals and groups. At the center of the therapeutic relationship is our present moment connection. Everything revolves and evolves from the place of mindful connection. The orientations that I work from are Sensorimotor Psychotherapy, EMDR, expressive arts, Ketamine-Assisted Psychotherapy, experiential and process groups, NeuroAffective Touch.

name: Sarah King (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My clinical framework is deeply rooted in attachment theory, emphasizing the intricate and vital mother-child relationship. It's striking how easily society trivializes the journey of conceiving, birthing, and nurturing a child, assuming that a healthy baby equates to a perfectly aligned mother, regardless of her physical and emotional challenges. The reality is that motherhood is a complex journey, often portrayed simplistically by society, leaving women feeling forgotten or disconnected in the postpartum period, almost like an afterthought. As a Licensed Clinical Social Worker, I merge my professional expertise with my personal journey through motherhood and the postpartum period, offering empathetic support as you navigate this profound transformation. My aim is to help you find acceptance and validation for your experiences, fostering a sense of safety as you navigate the diverse facets of motherhood.
Why We are a Good Fit for You
At Yaga Healing Place, we are a group of clinicians united in our vision to provide inclusive and specialized therapeutic services dedicated to improving the lives of women, parents and families. Our providers work together under the same empowerment goal; holding a safe and nourishing space for the healing and rewilding of your feminine power. Currently, Yaga Healing Place offers trauma-informed, mental health services to all women, AFAB folks, and their families. Through individual, group, and intensive therapies, you will be provided specialized and holistic mental health care as you navigate through your healing. Our goal is to expand to other whole-body-focused disciplines to best nourish your mind, body, and spiritual needs during your journey through all shades of motherhood.

name: Sophia Kukladyer (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I am a Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist, specializing in couples and relationships. I am committed to serving a diverse clientele, including LGBTQ+ individuals, couples, and poly systems. I have completed Level One Gottman Method Couples Therapy and an Externship in Emotionally Focused Couples Therapy. I have 13 years of experience working in multiple settings including private practice, out-patient substance abuse clinics, schools, and various centers. I believe that every individual has the internal strength and resources to live a more fulfilling life and support them in cultivating these strengths to reach their goals.

name: Fox Convey (SHE, THEY)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I customize therapy to suit each client's unique presentation, needs, and goals. That being said, the primary therapeutic tool is the relationship we will create together. While I am interested in working with diverse clientele, I am particularly interested in working with gender identity and sexuality exploration, career/community role expansion, emotional literacy, boundaries, self-esteem, identity development, and folx experiencing intersectional marginalization. I have a background in social justice, anti-racism, herbalism, yoga, expressive arts, children and families, and the service industry. I have taught yoga for 10 years, so yogic concepts and body healing certainly have a place in the services I offer. I am invested in my client's overall well-being - the physical, mental, emotional, spiritual, relational, and metaphysical aspects of being-ness intertwined.
Social Justice Issues We Care About
I care about how systems of oppression have harmed and continue to harm certain groups especially, including but not limited to People of Color & BIPOC, women/womxn, LGBTQQIP2SAA+, low-income individuals, prisoners, the disabled, and senior citizens. No one is free until we all are free. I am an active member of the queer community and the Black Lives Matter community. I volunteer for The Council for Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion (CEDI), an activist group I cofounded to help fight racism in higher education. To learn more about our work, check out our website: www.thecedicircle.com. In the past, I volunteered through community herbalism with Herbs for Activists.

name: Bella Box, DC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How We Can Help You
I became a licensed LMT in 1982. My undergraduate degree was in clinical psychology, which I completed in 1978. I earned my chiropractic license in 1985. I became an accredited t'ai chi chih teacher in 2000. Finally, I became a yoga teacher in 2023. My psychology, chiropractic and massage therapy has informed my t'ai chi and yoga teaching style. I teach with a holistic lens, listening to my students to identify the learning sweet spot. I specialize in trauma informed sensitive brain approach with my teaching style. I flourish with students who struggle with depression, anxiety, Complex PTSD, Bipolar I and II.
My Approach to Helping
My philosophy is to help my students heal through moving their bodies, developing their mindfulness, learning to chant, and by providing a healing presence. Volunteer Service: Tommy, our 7-year old schipperke and I are a therapy team for Hospice. This work has been a calling for me. Assisting a client move from active living to active dying is a profound privilege. The Divine is easily recognized during the dying process. Additional Volunteer Service: I teach a group yoga class at Bybee Lakes Hope Center. This is an amazing program that teaches life skills to folks who have previously been houseless and having substance abuse. I love being an ancillary member to facilitate deep healing through yoga.

name: Aimee Frazier (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer tools for managing anxiety, trauma, depression, grief, and feeling more in-control of your life. I offer a therapeutic relationship where you are known and respected, and encouraged to support your mental well-being. I specialize in walk and talk therapy, and also offer telehealth and office sessions as well.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe we all have the capacity to heal from the results of past traumas and difficult experiences, if we are willing to do the work and try new ways of being and relating. In the therapeutic relationship I value building trust, respect for each person's way of making meaning, and facilitating the development of personal empowerment. I want the people that I support to live authentically within their identity and relationships, have clear direction, and in alignment with their values.

name: Shane Ladd (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a counselor, I will provide a safe environment, a comfortable place to land while we explore and examine how your trauma impacts you daily. In the therapeutic space, I will provide support, psychoeducation, tools and skills that are appropriate to your treatment goals. Through this collaborative process you will expand awareness, grow, heal and as a result cultivate a more adaptive life narrative that will empower and strengthen you. Our fit as client and therapist is essential to your healing process. I hope collectively we can bring curiosity and compassion in the therapeutic space. I am a trauma-informed therapist, my clinical work is integrative in nature. I’m grounded in a Person-Centered Approach that is enhanced by a number of supplemental theoretical orientations such as: Narrative Therapy, Compassion-Focused Therapy, Somatic Therapy, CBT, and Parts work (IFS) to name a few.
My Approach to Helping
In addition to being a Trauma informed therapist, I also work with bereaved parents. I am TFMR Mom that had a stillbirth. Through this experience, I have become incredibly passionate about serving mothers and parents who have experienced TFMR (Termination for Medical Reasons), Stillbirths and Child Loss at any point. The grief that accompanies this loss is tremendous and isolating. It would be an honor to walk beside you and support you through this heartbreaking loss and assist you in navigating through your grief. My counseling style is genuine, personable, and creative. My personality is warm, inviting and direct. It is my focus that you feel heard, seen and respected. I look forward to accompanying you on your journey of inner-healing.

name: Nicole Corbett (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
So much of what drives us as humans comes from deeply held pain, trauma, and beliefs that live in the subconscious mind. That is the place where lasting change is possible. Brainspotting and hypnosis work at the deepest levels that the conscious mind simply can't find on its own. That is where the magic happens!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Certified Hypnotherapist Brainspotting Practitioner Shamanic Practitioner/Guide (15+ years experience) Certified in Quantum Healing Hypnosis Technique Certified in Hypnosis for Pain Management Member of National Guild of Hypnotists Member of Hypnosis Motivation Institute

name: Chelsea Casey (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I've spent a good portion of my career working in Eating Disorder treatment higher levels of care and came to private practice in 2019 where I continue to support clients through recovery, difficult patterns in relationships with food or exercise, and long standing body image concerns. I'm also certified in Perinatal Mental Health, and have found so much. meaning in working with prospective and current parents through all it means to bring a child into a family. I can offer support through journeys of infertility, loss and miscarriage, family planning decisions, navigating pregnancy, choosing life without children, or the entrance into parenthood. A good portion of my clients are also folks between 16 & 35 looking to better understand themselves, their histories, and cultivating a life congruent with their values. I often work with people in the LGBTQIA+ communities, and offer gender affirming support for teens and adults.
My Approach to Helping
I see therapy as a healing relationship, not a quick fix. Because of this, I focus on trust and connection as a tool for helping you make practical changes, at your pace, and in a way that feels right to you. With me, therapy is a co-created, safe space. I offer warm, direct, and compassionate dialogue. I can offer support in areas of anxiety, depression, grief, family and relationship challenges, identity formation, sexuality, gender identity, and personal growth. I take a Client-Centered approach to therapy, while drawing upon insights from Narrative Therapy, Internal Family Systems, Attachment Theory, broader Family Systems, and Mindfulness. I aim to create an environment supportive to people with all body sizes/shapes, abilities, genders, sexual orientations, relationship statuses, races and ethnicities, and religious affiliations. I am committed to anti-racist work in my life and the therapy room. I'm also learning how to continuously do better here, and I'm open to your feedback!

name: Pam Wood (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I see kids, teens and adults, and lead two groups for women: Art Therapy and Mindfulness, and Therapeutic Yoga for Stress-Relief and Empowerment. I have 30 years of experience working in schools, day treatment centers and community mental health clinics. I am a dedicated student in the neurobiology of stress and trauma, and enjoy supporting adoptive kids and parents. I integrate art therapy, EMDR, Lifespan Integration, mindfulness, attachment theory, and DBT into my work with clients.

name: Jennifer Messina (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
She's deeply passionate about the social work vocation, with a wholehearted belief in creating a nurturing, safe environment for any navigating unique struggles: gender dysphoria, LGBTQIA, racial discrimination, ageism, or socioeconomic disadvantage. Her commitment to clients shows in her use of evidence-based techniques: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Motivational Interviewing (MI), & Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT). She's now pursuing specialized training in hypnosis therapy through the American Institute of Healthcare Professionals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In her free time, she enjoys an active lifestyle that includes listening to music and participating in barre and yoga classes and spending time with her family. She’d love to meet you in a free phone consult! Schedule one via email, call/text 503-694-3381, or click Schedule Now on our website. You can also register for our new 8 week group therapy program for insomnia using CBT! Up to 8 slots available. Call 503-694-3381, email us, or visit our website at https://skycloudhealth.com/

name: Morgan Vanderpool (THEY/HE)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I facilitate restorative, body-based experiences in which clients practice: creating balance within their nervous systems, while designing a relationship with self, others and their environment that fosters opportunities to opt out of trauma-based patterns embedded in systems of oppression, and into a liberated relationship with their body, mind, & relationships.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
If you’ve been seeking a brave and compassionate space to explore your relationship with gender, neurodivergence, trauma survivorship, sex-positivity, kink, interracial relationships, polyamory, community/collective living, trauma sensitive (re)parenting, and more, I would love to connect with you and collaborate in supporting you in your restorative and regenerative journey.

name: Danielle LaBruzzo (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
With over 15 years of experience, I specialize in working with individuals navigating trauma, addiction, attachment challenges, and relationship dynamics. I provide affirming care for LGBTQIA+ and ENM communities and support friends and family of individuals struggling with personality disorders or addiction. My background includes advanced training in cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT) and dialectical behavior therapy (DBT), along with a trauma-informed and attachment-focused approach. I draw on evidence-based practices to help clients develop skills to regulate emotions, break unhealthy patterns, and create meaningful change. My therapeutic style is compassionate, understanding, and efficient. I focus on improving your quality of life by aligning your actions with your values, cultivating self-acceptance, and addressing unprocessed shame. Together, we work to build a secure sense of self and foster authentic, fulfilling relationships.

name: Jonah Ross (HE/HIS/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I try to create a space where discovery can happen. We will work towards your goals, emotional growth, and full potential. I hope to provide the trust and safety necessary for these acts of courage, and, ultimately... love. Something that distinguishes me from most therapists is that I have a PhD from UC Berkeley in Humanities, in addition to an MA in Psychotherapy. Both programs included Psychodynamic, Psychoanalytic, Humanistic, and Existential theories, but Berkeley also offered Cultural Studies, Gender Theory, and Media Studies, which enrich my approach to therapy. I offer a free 15 minute initial call. I look forward to meeting you!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a Licensed Professional Counselor Associate, I am under Supervision, of two Supervisors: Dr. Sarah A. Baird, Psy.D. and Kelly Hirstein, LPC 3C5026.

name: Aaron Good (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I provide mental health and career counseling to individuals and couples dealing with life's challenges. Do you want to understand more about yourself, about your identity, about the way the patterns you've learned in the past might no longer be serving you? Do you deal with the challenges of depression, anxiety, guilt, past trauma, and grief or loss? Come on in and we can explore all of it in a supportive environment. Looking for help finding a new career? Trying to apply to new jobs but feeling like your skills are out-of-date? Do you want to stay at your current job but want to focus on your strengths? A professional career counselor might help - let's collaborate on figuring out what might work for you. Are you looking to deepen intimacy in your relationship, overcome conflict, or simply communicate better? We can work as a team to find what's working in your relationship and build on that while overcoming the barriers to satisfaction.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold a Master’s Degree in Counseling from Portland State University. Major coursework included: human growth and development, counseling theory, career counseling, ethics, disability, and understanding human relationships. I have taken workshops on topics such as group facilitation and overcoming oppression. I am also a Certified Rehabilitation Counselor, a specialization that focuses on vocational rehabilitation and adjustment. I have additional coursework in ecopsychology and ecotherapy - work that recognizes the role that nature plays in our lives and weaves it into therapy itself.

name: Veronica Vargas (SHE/HER/ELLA)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Veronica journeys alongside folks in their path towards healing, harmony, and contentment. She finds beauty in witnessing the unfolding of human potential, and cultivation of meaning. Her formal training is in counseling psychology with an emphasis in depth-psychotherapy. Depth-psychotherapy can be defined as a holistic approach that examines all aspects of a person's life, including dreams, art, fantasies, philosophies, and conscious thoughts. She was heavily influenced by the work of Swiss psychiatrist, Carl Gustav Jung. In her work as a licensed psychotherapist she assesses the whole person or family system by looking at relational and cultural systems; spirituality; personal and intergenerational trauma; systems of oppression; personal values and how they are or aren't reflected authentically in the person's life. She has a private practice in Portland, Oregon where she offers psychotherapy, dream work, and psychedelic integration.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a passion for personal and systemic health and harmony. I believe that we are all equipped with the potential to heal ourselves, but often can use support with this process. I believe that life circumstances can present challenges at unlocking that potential which then throw our systems and relationships out of balance.

name: Amy Galaviz (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My focus of therapy stems from a Person-Centered approach which means I put the client needs in mind first. My goal is to create an atmosphere where you feel safe and comfortable while working to build a positive, open, trusting, therapeutic relationship. It is through this therapeutic relationship that I see you as the expert in your own life, while I strive to understand your personal perspective of the world around you. Throughout therapy, I aim to keep your needs in mind, without judgment while seeing you in a positive light as you move through challenges and grow. Together, with a focus on personal strengths and growth enhancement, we take a holistic look at your life, by examining each part of your world that has an influence on your well-being whether it be personal relationships with family and friends, work, or health. I believe that people have the power to create changes in their lives no matter how small and am there to support you along the way at any stage in life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that we all have the ability to make changes in our lives, given the right support, guidance and faith that things can get better. Whether you are a new or single mother looking for support & guidance, a mother of three looking for validation & understanding, or a women who is uncertain about parenting, I believe all women have the right to the same care and support regardless of your situation, values or personal beliefs. Therefore, I offer holistic care practices that take a look at each client as a whole person, an individual, and their own expert in what works for them. I hold space for you, taking the time to help you identify your goals for therapy and the things you want in life in order to create the life you love. (*Extensive training in Perinatal Mental Health & PMH-C certified). For more info visit: www.mindbodymamapdx.com

name: Kayla Davidson
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that each individual deserves the right to tell their story. To feel seen, heard, and deeply understood. At the core, most of us desire the same things in life; to experience love, joy, health, connection and longevity. But we live in a world full of constant distractions keeping us from looking within. My approach is centered around deep holistic healing. I may use spirituality to connect deeper to those with similar views. I view each individual as a complete being that has shifted, changed and evolved during their unique experience on Earth. While I choose to view each client as who they are today, I believe much of the healing work happens when we reflect and look at the past with fresh, new eyes. I focus on acknowledging and healing trauma, and prioritize the idea of building the strongest relationship with yourself.
Why I Became a Therapist
Being the youngest of four with a long family history of drug addiction (specifically alcohol), I was extremely conditioned and had a lot to unlearn. I have spent majority of my 20's on a deep healing journey. I removed alcohol from my life, began seeing a therapist, and build my own self-care toolbox that has helped me learn who I am at the deepest level. I have found that answers come when you stop looking for them. Mediation, journaling, yoga/intuitive movement, and simply being present has allowed my heart to heal. I deeply enjoy connecting with other vulnerable souls and shining a light on their magic, the way all of my healers have helped me.

name: Nate Wilson-Traisman
approaches:
My Background
I am a Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist with the Oregon Board of Licensed Professional Counselors and Therapists. I hold a Master's Degree in Couples and Family Therapy from the University of Oregon. During my Master's program, I worked as a therapist at PeaceHealth Counseling, providing therapy to children, adolescents, and families at an outpatient pediatric clinic. I also provided individual, couples, and family therapy at the University of Oregon's Center for Family Therapy. My previous professional experience includes coaching high school and college baseball, working in college admissions, and working as an academic support specialist for student-athletes at the University of Oregon. Prior attending graduate school, I had a brief career as a freelance journalist. As an undergraduate student, I majored in English literature, and was the captain of my college baseball team.
My Approach to Helping
Deciding to see a therapist can be an intimidating process, and can be filled with all sorts of questions. My goal is to make therapy feel accessible and welcoming. My strengths-based approach draws on your strengths, resources, past successes, and hopes for the future. I believe the strength of the relationship between therapist and client is the single most important aspect of therapy. Consequently, my approach is founded on developing a strong working relationship with you. My therapeutic approach utilizes models of family systems therapy, cognitive-behavioral therapy, and mindfulness-based approaches.

name: Kimberlee Dennery (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My goal is to help you reconnect with your internal resources and feel more grounded in yourself, especially during times of stress or uncertainty. Therapy isn’t about fixing you—it’s about supporting you as you find ways to make sense of what’s happening and move forward with greater clarity. Clients have shared that they feel comfortable in sessions with me, often appreciating my warmth, authenticity, and sense of humor. I believe in taking an individualized approach, drawing from therapeutic frameworks like mindfulness, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT), narrative therapy, and family systems theory. Together, we’ll find what works best for you.
Why I Became a Therapist
Even as a high school student, I was drawn to psychology and sociology—I knew early on that I wanted to be in a helping profession. Over the years, I’ve worked in a variety of settings, including hospitals and outpatient clinics, supporting adolescents, adults, and older adults. These experiences have deepened my belief that mental health care should be compassionate, accessible, and affirming for everyone. In addition to individual therapy, I’ve provided support for families and couples, helping them navigate changes, improve communication, and better understand each other. My clinical training includes advanced coursework through Therapy Training Boston in family and couples counseling, which continues to inform my work with clients today.

name: Kerry Headley (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Consider what you believe about yourself—negative, limiting things you still tell yourself even though part of you knows it's not true. Ideas you’ve learned within the group, church, etc. How accurate are these narratives? Do you feel like you can’t do something or be something because within you is the voice of a leader, pastor, group member, or even the former you who was in the group? What would it be like to choose a different story—one based on your beliefs and what you care about? You may have forgotten about your strengths and abilities. Therapy offers space for you to tell your stories as well as rewrite them. Part of healing from religious trauma and/or coercive control is taking the role of the main character in your life story--a story that makes you feel whole. I can help you set realistic, inspiring goals and provide support as you work toward meeting them.
My Values as a Therapist
Collaboration: I am a person-centered therapist, which means you are the boss of you. I won't give you homework if you hate that (but I'm happy to provide therapeutic tasks that can help you continue the work between sessions if you like). I don’t make people meditate (but we can discuss mindfulness-based stress reduction techniques if you are curious). Creativity: You are a unique person. We get to know each other. We figure out together what’s most likely to help you meet your goals. Humor: Laughter makes the difficulties more bearable. Compassion: Change is often hard, and growth can feel like struggle. Being seen and understood helps while we are learning to remember who we are.

name: Mariah Beltran (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a Chicana feminist psychologist, my specialty is working with clients on their relationships with their body, themselves, & others as they navigate life changes. My multicultural feminist training includes anti-racism/anti-oppressive work & LGBTQ+ empowerment. I have worked with all ages, though I specialize in young adulthood/adulthood. I aim to guide clients as they work toward their own definition of health & finding their empowerment. I am dedicated to working collaboratively & aim to infuse every session with empathy, curiosity, compassion, collaboration, and a little bit of humor. If you're in a space ready to grow & heal, please schedule a free consultation.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As I've said, client's are the experts on their own lives and get to determine what their ideal health looks and feels like. As I've also mentioned, as a Chicana feminist, I recognize we contain multitudes and exist on this Earth with all it's influences (both great and no so great, or down right bad). With this in mind, I believe we are all humans doing our best with what we've got. My personal beliefs beyond that is that every human has a right to bodily autonomy, to love and be loved by whomever, and that the only way we get better is through a safe community and mutual aid.

name: Peggy Fulda
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am no good to you, your family, or your community if I cannot provide some level of ease and safety in our work together. I value relationship above all. My extensive experience working with immigrants, refugees, wilderness programs and international students have shown me that talk is not the only route to resolve distress. In a world that often feels foreign I use my background in Sensorimotor therapy approaches to help you understand and navigate the terrain within and around you. You don't need to be fixed; you are not broken. You are living in a space which attempts to distance each of us from ourselves, our heritage, each other. Mental Health is a western term to describe universal issues, regardless of socioeconomic status or geography. I offer a rest stop that lets you understand the beauty in how far you have come and the tools you need for your next journey. There is important work to be done even in the dark places and there is no need to do it alone.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My practice is body-centered, culturally engaged, and justice driven. My lived experiences as a partner, sister, daughter, aunt, granddaughter of Holocaust migrants, and product of a multiracial family all enrich and deepen my work supporting others. My studies of sociology, anthropology, and public health form the foundation of of clinical care. My professional work with refugees, immigrants, indigenous youth, exchange students, and other folk from elsewhere ground me in social justice frameworks and a critical engagement with power and privilege in the therapy room.

name: L. Simone D'Amore (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My clients describe me as down to earth and relatable. I use an eclectic approach of therapy models to meet the needs of my clients, including CBT, family systems therapy, and trauma-informed attachment theory. I also enjoy working with individuals and families who are adjusting to a pregnancy or a new addition to the family. I strive to help my clients understand their protective responses to adversity, help make positive changes, and hold hope when you might feel hopeless. I received my MS in Counseling Psychology from Alaska Pacific University in 2005. I have worked in a variety of settings, including schools, residential treatment, mobile crisis intervention, and outpatient mental health therapy. I participated in certification training from Postpartum Support International on maternal mental health and perinatal mood disorders. I have also completed certification in family therapy through DHS and PSU for foster and adoptive families.

name: Nick Hebert
approaches:
How I Can Help You
Over time, we all develop stories—once protective, now sometimes limiting—about ourselves, others, and the world. These narratives, often shaped by experiences of marginalization, can leave us feeling unworthy or overwhelmed. In our work together, I honor your unique journey by exploring these stories and uncovering the strengths you already possess. I specialize in supporting those grappling with anxiety, shame, low self-worth, relationship challenges, intimacy and sexual concerns, identity questions, and trauma responses. As a queer therapist with extensive experience serving LGBTQIA+ communities and individuals in poly and kink-positive relationships, I bring both professional expertise and personal understanding to our work. I believe that genuine healing happens in a collaborative, evolving relationship—one where we embrace both laughter and tears as we navigate the path to growth.
More About the People I Treat
I’m dedicated to supporting individuals who grapple with challenging inner narratives—those feelings of anxiety, shame, and low self-worth that may have originated from past hurts or marginalization. I work with clients facing relationship and intimacy struggles, sexual concerns, and identity questions, helping them uncover the strengths they already possess.
