My practice is especially affirming for LGBTQIA+ and queer communities, including those exploring polyamorous or kink-positive relationships. Every client brings a unique story and inherent resilience, and together, we work to transform those narratives into pathways for growth and authentic living.

name: Allison Spector (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Therapy is deeply vulnerable work, but by building trust in the therapeutic relationship we can explore and identify automatic patterns and habits that may be keeping you from feeling your best and work together to practice new ways of living. In my work, I pull from a variety of theoretical approaches including Brainspotting, EMDR, CBT, Family Systems Theory, Internal Family Systems, Attachment Theory, Narrative Therapy, Polyvagal Theory, and Mindfulness Based practices.

name: Carewell Portland Carewell Portland/Julie Berman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
Our Background and Approach
We support people to make changes. We realize that when you reach out to Carewell you are looking for support and a fresh, new approach to extremely difficult obstacles in your life and your family. We strive to help you and your kids if you have them, understand things a bit better, become more aware, so your relationships improve. Life doesn't have to be so hard and exhausting.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
https://carewellportland.com/ Licensed clinicians with many years experience, offering telehealth appointments with no wait.

name: Katy Hartnett Boruff (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My mission is to provide quality mental health services to emerging adults ages 18-40 through treating anxiety, depression, trauma, sexual identity concerns, and relational difficulties. We will work together to alleviate symptoms, help you learn to let go of outdated coping strategies (substance use, numbing or disordered eating) to help you meet your goals. I practice Gestalt therapy in a warm and supportive environment, where I will help you gain insight into past patterns of behavior that are no longer useful, help you uncover barriers that are holding you back, develop new ways of coping, and build on existing strengths.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
What sets me apart from other practitioners is that I specialize in the unique challenges of emerging adults and I am introducing Gestalt therapy to a younger population. Most people seek me out for my experience in working with young adults and because they seek a counselor who they feel can understand them and their individual concerns. I provide a new, different approach that you may have never tried before. I have experience working with all gender identities and racial backgrounds. I strive to honor your unique experience and I work to hold space to help you understand how your specific set of life experiences has informed your way of being in the world.

name: Andrea Woodward (SHE/HER/ELLA)
approaches:
My Background
To support clients’ transformation, Andrea employs Compassionate Communication, Solution-Focused Brief Therapy and the Gottman approach for couples. In addition, she applies a collaborative problem solving process (CPS) developed while working with Fortune 500 company innovators. Most recently, she has used CPS to help at-risk youth and families learn to communicate more effectively.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Andrea adores her adult kids and her ancient, 10-pound dog. A resident of Bend, Oregon, Andrea enjoys hot springs, mountain bike riding, standup paddling and snowboarding.

name: WellPath Counseling & Associates
approaches:
Our Approach to Helping
Therapy is a unique process that succeeds on the power of the relationship between the therapist and clients. Whether you are seeking individual counseling or relationship counseling, our goal at WellPath Counseling, is to build a partnership in which you feel safe and supported enough to make sense of challenging events or issues. Utilizing an interdisciplinary approach that is rooted in attachment and emotionally-focused modalities, we seek to create an authentic relationship that supports dialogue and discovery where you feel you are an active participant in your own growth and the growth of your relationships.
Why We Started this Group Practice
We are committed to providing exceptional client care that prioritizes the well-being and growth of those we serve. We believe that quality therapy is rooted in a supportive and nurturing environment, not only for our clients but also for our dedicated team of professionals. To foster this environment, we have established a unique business incubator program designed to empower our employees in their professional development. This initiative provides the resources, mentorship, and guidance necessary for our counselors to cultivate their skills and eventually launch their own private practices. By investing in our team, we enhance the quality of care we provide, ensuring that our clients receive the best possible support on their journeys toward healing and growth.

name: Samantha Adams (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer trauma-informed, culturally grounded, and relational therapy rooted in liberatory and anti-colonial perspectives. My training in attachment work and interpersonal neurobiology emphasizes the importance of healthy therapeutic bonds and attunement. My goal is to create a safe container in sessions that empowers clients to do challenging but transformative work. My collaborative and eclectic approach integrates somatic practices and adapts to your needs—whether you prefer a more solution-focused style, existential exploration, or a blend of both. I believe in client led work, meaning that I trust you as the expert of your own story and that you are in control of our time together. I may provide guidance and suggestions, but I will always encourage clients to trust their own inner knowing and sense of timing in this work.
My Values as a Therapist
My practice is welcoming and safe for everyone. I am a fat-positive/body-positive provider, as well as LGBTQIA+, BIPOC, and poly/kink affirming, aware, and inclusive. As a student of Collapse Psychology, I do not believe therapy alone can be a cure-all for the psychological pain we experience. Our healing is rooted in the context of relationships and community, both with humans and the more-than-human. I see therapy as a powerful tool to support ourselves in an increasingly challenging world, but acknowledge that the needs of my clients extend beyond therapy. I am always striving to grow my own skills and knowledge, engage in systemic advocacy work, and support my clients in building a holistic network of support. Whether you’ve had many counseling experiences, or this is your very first, I look forward to learning about who you are and where you want to go on your path towards healing. Reach out to schedule a free consultation to learn more about working together.

name: Joe Gianotti (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
During my clinical experience, I have encountered working with issues around anxiety, depression, trauma, men’s issues, and social anxiety. I emphasize the importance of rapport and trust building, and believe that counseling can be effective and beneficial if a strong therapeutic relationship is formed.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Life can be full of stress, grief, and suffering, and as human beings, it can be challenging for us to navigate through all of the negative thoughts, emotions, and experiences that we encounter. Coming from a Humanistic and Acceptance and Commitment Therapy background, my approach to psychotherapy focuses on getting to know you and your unique experience so that we as a team can find better ways for you to live the life that you want to live. I believe that you are the expert of your own experience and I hope to create a space where you can authentically express yourself in a safe environment that is free of judgment.

name: Nicole Quarles (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a systemic therapist you can expect me to be interested in your relationships, your family, your culture, your physical body, and your spiritual practices. I will pay special attention to things like your dreams and might ask you to work creatively or use mindfulness techniques during our sessions. In individual therapy we will work together to uncover your inner wisdom, explore aspects of your identity, process challenging experiences, and gain insight into the events or patterns that bring you to therapy. In relational therapy you and your therapy partner will learn new skills to self-regulate, communicate effectively and express your needs, and gain deeper understanding of each other’s inner worlds. We will work from an Emotion Focused Therapy (EFT) framework, an attachment based model, by relating current and past events, while centering you and your therapy partner’s emotional experiences.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that we are all entitled to love, respect, self-confidence, and the ability to live freely. Unfortunately, our society does not afford those privileges to everyone in equal measure. You can expect me to name the way society impacts us and to disclose parts of my own experience and identity that might influence our therapeutic relationship. My goal is to help you reconnect with your intuition and the ability to confidently express who you are with confidence and joy. Paying attention to the mind/body connection, as well as cultivating practices of deep rest(oration) has provided insight and healing in my own life and it is something you can expect me to bring into our work together. Clients typically describe me as gentle, authentic, and direct. -

name: Laura La Rosa (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Life circumstances can feel overwhelming and bigger than us. As parents and adults we can feel stuck and unable to access our wiser selves to accomplish what we want for our children, ourselves and in relationship to others. As teens, we can get bogged down with expectations and feel like we are sinking. Navigating with-in our comfort zone can sometimes hinder our emotional and relational growth. Your growing awareness of these conditions that hold you back will inform our work together. I can assist you accessing your authentic self which may invite you to challenge old habitual patterns and ways of thinking. Our work together may also give you the insight and tools to skillfully navigate the circumstances in your life that you want to improve. Your relationship to yourself and others may deepen and you may start to create a positive life narrative. My goal is to co-create a space with you that is nurturing and informative so you can begin your exploration with curiosity and ease.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a licensed clinical social worker (LCSW) graduating with an MSW from Portland State University in 2012. I am seasoned therapist with over a decade of experience providing mental health services to children, adolescents, adults and families in urban and rural outpatient mental health clinics, community and residential settings. I am particularly drawn to working with adults and families impacted by depression, anxiety, grief, loss, trauma related conditions and everyday “circumstances” that get in our way. I am passionate about empowering clients to heal from historical and current conditions that hold them from the life they want. I utilize a variety of trauma informed attachment / narrative based interventions. I draw from a personal mindfulness practice with a wealth of lived and professional experience that deeply informs my relational approach to understanding and working with my client’s challenges. IF I DO NOT RETURN YOUR CALL WITH IN 3 DAYS, MY PRACTICE IS FULL.

name: Han Tran (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My approach focuses on helping you cultivate self-compassion, clarify your needs and values, and develop healthier patterns of behaviors. I also provide couples counseling, helping partners break negative cycles, deepen emotional connection, and build a more secure, loving relationship. I integrate evidence-based therapies, including Emotion-Focused Therapy(EFT) for individuals and couples, compassion-focused interventions, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), and Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT). My goal is to foster a space where you feel seen and supported. Together, we can work toward greater joy, connection, and purpose in your life and relationships. With over 10 years of clinical experience, I have had the privilege of working with individuals and couples from diverse backgrounds in a variety of settings, including Veterans Affairs Medical Centers, Psychological Services Center at the University of Memphis, community mental health clinics, and group practice settings.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in honoring your wisdom and lived experience in our work together, blending these with evidence-based interventions. As a 1.5 generation immigrant who grew up in a low-income immigrant household, I bring a personal understanding of how systems and identities impact emotional well-being. I employ a multicultural, holistic lens to therapy, understanding each client’s experiences within the context of their unique history, culture, and community. I’m passionate about serving marginalized communities, including BIPOC individuals, immigrants, and those impacted by systemic oppression.

name: Julio Iñiguez (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer individual and relationship therapy focused on long term addiction recovery and behavioral addictions, healing trauma, and improving relationships. My approach is somatic, client-centered, compassionate, and collaborative. I draw from several modalities including: ACT, Sensorimotor Psychotherapy, Internal Family Systems, Language of Emotion, Emotion Focused Couples Therapy, and Motivational Interviewing. I focus on both bottom-up and top-down approaches through the use of somatic, emotionally focused, relational, and mindfulness based therapies. The relationship between a client and their therapist is the most important part of the therapeutic process. No amount of theory or frameworks can substitute an attuned and collaborative relationship between client and therapist. I invite you to connect with me to see if we might be a good fit for this work together. I offer a no cost video call consultation to get a good sense of what working together may be like. Schedule today!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I’m a bilingual (Spanish/English), bi-cultural, licensed marriage, couple and family therapist and have been in the mental health and addictions field since 2013. Before working in this field I was an aspiring teacher who took a detour into my own struggles with addiction. I am grateful to have found my process of recovery in 2008. After completing my Masters in Marriage and Family Therapy in 2014, I have held multiple positions in the field including direct service counseling in agencies, program development and management, as well as clinical supervision of direct counseling staff. I bring a systemic, culturally-informed and trauma-informed perspective to my work with clients. I have completed the Sensorimotor Psychotherapy Level 1 training and bring knowledge of treating trauma through working with the body to the services I provide. I have worked in the Problem Gambling field since 2013 and have a passion for supporting people with process addictions and relationship issues.

name: Liz Cloud
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I'm glad you're participating in the important and sometimes challenging process of finding a therapist. Here's a bit about my therapy approach to help you learn if we might be a good fit: my goal is to support you to move through pain and struggle in a way you feel good about. My therapeutic style incorporates warmth, humor, and honest feedback. I prioritize creating a space for you to safely experiment with new ways of being and communicating. I strive to help you live in a way that inspires deeper fulfillment by harnessing your natural ability to cope with stress. Sometimes when the strategies that used to work for us no longer serve their purpose, we start to struggle and suffer. When we identify these patterns and have support in trying something new, there is relief from "stuckness."
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Licensed Professional Counselor and received my education from Pacific University's School of Professional Psychology. My patients are adolescents and adults, many of whom struggle with anxiety, difficult transitions, and loss. I feel privileged to have served the Portland community since 2009.

name: Lauren Hoffman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My values as a therapist are to provide non-judgmental, empathic, and culturally informed care, while emphasizing an environment founded on personal growth, openness, and honesty. I use a multi-faceted approach to therapy to best fit the unique needs of each client because I recognize one size does not fit all. I often work from a psychodynamic-relational stance, using the client-therapist relationship as a tool to increase my client’s awareness of interactions between self, others, and the world to improve decision-making and enhance life satisfaction. I also incorporate mindfulness-based, trauma informed techniques to promote insight with practical, here-and-now interventions. I have experience diagnosing and treating a variety of presenting issues, including anxiety, depression, mood disorders, trauma, adjustment, and ADHD.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
For ten years I have been providing individual therapy to a diverse population of adults and adolescents in a variety of settings, including inpatient clinics, a community mental health center, university counseling settings, and now private practice. I would be honored to assist you on your journey towards healing.

name: Zoe Presley (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
During the COVID-19 outbreak, I am offering Telehealth (video or phone counseling). For the past decade, I have specialized in treating depression, anxiety, relationship challenges, and stress. My approach to your wellbeing includes practical tools, mindfulness, and gentle compassion.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
For well over a decade, I have guided individuals and groups in cultivating wellbeing. I am trained in Transcendental Meditation, and as a Depth Psychotherapist and Forest Therapy Guide. My approach is supportive, interactive and experiential, including mindfulness, somatic (body-based), and depth psychotherapy, as well as practical approaches to support you in feeling grounded in yourself and at peace with your life.

name: Deborah High
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
The act of doing therapy is a bit like tending to a garden; it takes diligence and care to achieve growth! It sounds challenging I know, but the outcomes and successes of working on yourself and your relationships far outweigh the negatives. We all have gardens that need occasional weeding and replanting, and when we are not taking care of ourselves, we can experience feelings of depression, anxiety, restlessness and frustration. In choosing to work with me, I will guide and encourage you by providing the necessary tools and resources that allow you to create a healthy and bountiful garden of your own.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In choosing to work with me, I will guide and encourage you by providing the necessary tools and resources that allow you to create a healthy and bountiful garden of your own. Counseling is a joint effort which cannot be successful without both of our commitments, hard work and courage. I also believe therapy provides a place to explore thoughts and feelings about relationships in your life as well as your relationship with yourself. This work allows you the opportunity to practice new ways of interacting with others in a safe and trusting atmosphere where you're not judged or criticized.

name: Sarah Hardin
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Above all else, I believe in the healing power of relationship. During therapy, we co-create a space that provides you with the safety to explore your feelings, challenge old assumptions and stories, and do the brave work of transformation. My work involves us noticing and responding together to whatever unfolds, and using the therapeutic relationship to unearth compassion and create change. I believe we all have the innate capacity to do this, but sometimes we need a little boost from an ally to remember what we're capable of. Come as you are. Expect both laughter and tears.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I'm a marriage and family therapist with a strong training background in family systems -- thinking about ourselves in relationship to others, rather than as isolated individuals. I am trained in Art Therapy. I have extensive experience treating trauma survivors, LGBTQ folks, non-traditional and blended families, and teens. My work tends to be emotionally-focused with a strong emphasis on relationship. My master's degree is from Antioch University Seattle and I hold a bachelor's degree from Harvard University in Visual Art.

name: Reyah Travis
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I practice emotionally focused therapy. We focus on cultivating emotional intimacy, not on deciding who is right or wrong or “fixing” someone or a challenge. Basically we run communication drills in session to rewire how we connect with the people that we love that leads to eveyrone feeling more connected and satisfied in their relationships.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I’m a mom, democratic socialist, animal lover, and believer in political engagement to improve our world. I offer emotionally focused couples and family therapy. I have training and specialization in themes related to indigenous culture, houselessness, suicide prevention, DID and the plural community, racial equity, and abuse prevention.

name: Rachel Kendall (SHE/ HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am heavily trained in Family Systems Theory, where I like to utilize a genogram to help individuals and families visually understand the origins of their behavior. Also being trauma-informed with anti-racist alignment, my clinical orientation is rooted in the mission to serve vulnerable and marginalized populations because when everyone's needs are met, we become healthier and thrive as a society. ~Please Note: I am only offering Telehealth and No In-Person sessions at this time~ ***Oregon Health Plan Clients please schedule here: https://restorativefamilybalancellc.clientsecure.me/
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold an MS in Marriage, Couple and Family Counseling from Walden University. I am a Licensed Professional Counselor with the Oregon Board of Licensed Professional Counselors and Therapists. My clinical experience has involved serving vulnerable populations, including traditionally marginalized identities, neurodivergent individuals, those in recovery from addiction and/ or struggling to have their basic needs met. I chose to specialize in EMDR because it is so broad in helping clients heal from experiences and establish a greater sense of safety and promote generational healing. ~Please Note: I am only offering Telehealth and No In-Person sessions at this time~

name: Karen Neri (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a relational therapist with an orientation towards social justice and I am drawn to working with clients from a culturally responsive, collaborative, and systemic lens. I take a holistic and strength-based approach. I view my clients in the context of their family, social and cultural environment. I work with emotions to strengthen the bonds in relationships and facilitate trauma healing using somatics, mindfulness, neuroscience and compassion. I am trained in Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT) and Eye Movement Desensitization Reprocessing (EMDR). I am especially interested in supporting interracial or multicultural/multiethnic couples in their partner relationships. I presently have some space to take on new partners and individuals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that people are resilient social beings. We each have the capacity to bounce back from challenges, maintain positive experiences, and grow from our experiences despite life stressors. At our core is our need for security and connection; both of which are crucial to our well-being. The manner we relate to ourselves, to others, and to the world around us can affirm or disaffirm our feelings of safety, acceptance and belonging. To this end, I see that we primarily interact with the world through our emotions as experienced by our senses, processed by our thoughts and reconciled by our language. It is from the place of social interdependence that we create a meaningful reality and experience an ultimate sense of well-being.

name: Olga Ward (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use the NeurOptimal®system – one of the most highly evolved forms of neurofeedback, safe and effective for all ages.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Certified Advanced NeurOptimal® Trainer (Zengar Institute) Access Bars® Practitioner (Access Consciousness) Certified Brain Health Coach/Neurofeedback provider (Amen Clinics)

name: Nicole Mayeda (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have worked throughout my career with anxiety, depression, and chemical / behavioral addictions. I researched binge eating and body-focused, repetitive behaviors (BFRBs) such as compulsive hair pulling and skin picking for my doctoral dissertation, and continued jobs in the field of impulse control and OCD behaviors thereafter. In my community work, I commonly treated individuals with substance use disorders; eating and body image disorders; gambling, porn, and shoplifting addictions; codependence and people-pleasing; perfectionism, formal OCD and OCD-related conditions, and general anxiety and depression. My private practice has a special focus on anxiety, OCD, and BFRBs (@bfrbtherapist on Instagram) currently, supporting teens, adults, couples, and families affected by these behaviors. Lastly, I run groups, consult, and teach, drawing from psychodynamic, ACT, ERP, CBT, DBT, IFS, and somatic approaches.
My Values as a Therapist
I make my role about liberating people, and incorporate liberatory values (e.g. abolition, body positivity, restorative justice, anticolonialism, racial equity) into my psychotherapy work. I believe in whole-person healing. I don't want to teach people to simply block behaviors while all of their underlying pain, anxiety, or frustration persists unaddressed. I want people's stuck patterns to lift organically - because they are happier, lighter, and don't need them anymore. I am passionate about unpacking the complexities in situations, such as the social and cultural underpinnings that make your mental health about more than your individual commitments. Together, we can create a healing journey through values, early origins, healthy dependence, intrinsic worth, self-determination, intuitive listening, and radical repair.

name: Dove Garvan (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Whether I'm treating individuals or couples, I work with all clients through a relational lens because healthy relationships are foundational to human thriving. I am a technically-focused clinician with masters' degrees in Conflict & Dispute Resolution and Couples & Family Therapy. I will help you view your interactional patterns in '3-G' (the good, the great and the gnarly!). From there, we'll guide them to full functionality using your individual and partnership strengths as fuel for the journey. Alongside exploring your attachment histories and patterns, I will help you build expertise in giving your partner(s) feedback about what you need in ways that keep negotiation moving along effectively. My job is to help restore your communication to its finest purpose: a technology for connection, security and satisfaction. To do this work in an informed and respectful way, I counsel from an inclusive and socially just lens.
More About the People I Treat
Love comes in many shapes and forms. I support clients of all racial, sexual and religious backgrounds with curiosity, humility, and the common sense to provide referrals if it's evident that I'm out of my depth. Hablo español, y me encanta apoyar a la comunidad hispanohablante en Portland. I am a poly & kink-affirming, sex-positive provider. If you've often wished for an ally to help with resolving 'messy human stuff,' I can't wait to join your team as a cheerleader and educator of effective communication and conflict resolution.

name: Lauren Hisada (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Depending on your unique circumstances and needs, sessions may be offered through an online, in-office, walk-and-talk, or hybrid format. For clients who are interested in supplementing their treatment with horticultural therapy, we can work with plants in-office, virtually, or at local garden sites. Please inquire about seasonal children's horticultural therapy groups, which focus on strengthening emotional awareness, emotional regulation, self-trust, self-worth, and collaborative skills via therapeutic horticulture activities. Why Nature? Research shows that connecting with nature, both indoors and outdoors, can help reduce cognitive overload, improve mood, and foster a sense of balance and well-being. Engaging with our natural environment has been found to minimize stress, increase emotional resilience, and create a deeper sense of connection to oneself, others, and the broader living world.
My Values as a Therapist
I approach therapy with a commitment to social justice and a deep respect for your lived experiences. My practice is an affirming and inclusive space where we can explore how factors like sexuality, gender identity, race, culture, ability, and systemic inequities shape your story and impact your life. Together, we can work to unpack the ways that external systems and internalized messages influence your well-being, while empowering you to draw on your resilience and address the growth you desire.

name: Kristen Cole (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As most people today are touched by mental health struggles, whether it be their own or their loved ones, we can use those experiences to learn to ground spiritually and mentally. I strive to understand the person in their present state and identify the root cause of distress in their life.
Our Values as Mental Health Practitioners
I believe that everyone in the healing profession should undergo their own journey of self-exploration, to better understand the people they’re treating. I am here to facilitate that process of healing.

name: Adabel Lee (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I offer individualized therapy using Acceptance & Commitment Therapy (ACT), Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), SPACE, trauma-informed approaches, and mindfulness practices. I work with teens and adults on an individual basis, and I also provide parenting guidance for children and teens. My ideal clients are those feeling overwhelmed, stuck, or challenged by life transitions, academic pressures, social dynamics, or relationship difficulties. I create a compassionate, non-judgmental space that honors your unique cultural background. Through evidence-based strategies and a gentle yet direct approach, we work together to process emotions, set meaningful goals, and foster positive change.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am personally familiar with the tensions that can arise from differences in cultural and generational values. I have worked with clients from a wide range of ethnic and socio-economic backgrounds. I welcome bringing your multiple identities into our work to understand how they may have influenced, and been influenced by, your relationships and life experiences, and to support you in choosing how your identities might guide your path.

name: Jess Firestone (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that growth and healing happen when you are seen, supported, and cared for no matter how you feel or what is happening in your life. I love listening to you and helping you hear your own wisdom about the challenges you’re facing. Sometimes it’s helpful to hear me reframe the way you’re thinking that may come from being too hard on yourself. I can help you develop confidence in your ability to ride the waves of emotion that can be overwhelming. It is beautiful and painful to be a human and I want you to know you’re not alone. I use a strength-based approach, helping you re-write stories about yourself and your relationships. While we work together, we can do art, go for walks outside, or play with toys. That goes for kids and adults!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Before becoming a therapist, I worked in schools for almost 20 years. All that time in school communities prepared me to help you navigate issues you may be having as a current student, as an educator, or as an adult processing things that happened when you were younger. I am deeply familiar with the ways school systems can cause harm or fail to offer the support you need, especially for BIPOC, queer, and neurodiverse students. I am passionate about helping clients identify and advocate for what they need.

name: Julianna Vermeys
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I provide individuals, families, and teens the opportunity to safely and confidently explore your life experiences and help you feel whole and well. My therapy practice offers an opportunity to become accepting and empowered about your life experience, your behavior and choices. I offer thoughtful, collaborative, compassionate, embodied, client-centered therapy. Part of my practice includes calling attention to how the body is responding to narrative, discussion, emotion and using trauma-informed, somatic-based skills to support the body in the therapy process. By being in relationship with the body, one can learn to have a comprehensive understanding of one self so that change is holistic and intrinsic. I aim to create connection and belonging that supports the practice and process of awareness, acceptance and healing.
My Values as a Therapist
I am dedicated to offering an ethical and compassionate trauma-informed, somatic-integrated, honest, thoughtful and unconditional counseling experience. A mindful approach to any therapeutic technique or theory can offer healing and empowering results; improve your mood, health, ambition and relationships. In addition to my counseling credentials, I completed 500-hr Yoga training and have taught yoga and meditation since 2003. I offer private, couples and small group yoga when therapeutically appropriate. Yoga is one of several types of mindfulness-based tools I offer in my practice. I acknowledge as a white woman in the US my cultural appropriation of practices outside of my culture and heritage. I provide Clinical Supervision to counselor interns pursuing their LPC license in the state of Oregon. My intention as a supervisor is to offer a space for one to consider their role as healer, mental health counselor, and role in the greater community of health and wellness for all.

name: Jeremy Whiston
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My strength as a therapist is that I'm a regular person, and I don't pretend to have any fancy magic powers. What I do have is a high capacity for understanding, for listening, as well as a few techniques that can help folks understand themselves better, and get some relief. The approach I take is based on the needs of my client on that particular day. Sometimes we just need to be heard, sometimes we need someone to tell us that we're not crazy, sometimes we need tangible tools to help overcome an obstacle or unhelpful behavior. I work with my client to create a plan that they feel good about.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I know from personal experience how difficult it is to find a therapist that you connect with (and who is available), and I will do everything I can to help. I believe we can make meaningful changes to ourselves and our lives if we really want to. I believe the most important thing I can do as a therapist is create a safe, non-judgemental space. My clients can share anything with me without fear that it will make me think differently about them.

name: Jane Ward, PhD (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe in the importance of personal accountability, integrity, and building on individual strengths. My treatment enables people to think more clearly, better manage their emotions, and make more mindful choices about their behavior. My goal is to help each of my clients live a good life with greater meaning and purpose.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a licensed clinical psychologist. My doctorate is from Seattle Pacific University. I have been working in the field of sexual adjustment since 2007. I am certified as a sex offender treatment provider in the state of Oregon and have advanced training in the area of sexual addiction.

name: Amy Manion (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I utilize compassion, humor, and respect in my work with the goal of assisting my clients as they move towards positive changes and living a more authentic and meaningful life. Together, we can make connections, gain insight and question some of the underlying, unconscious, and outdated patterns that may be keeping you stuck or holding you back.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I have additional or certificate level trainings in sex therapy, perinatal mental health, ADHD, CBT, Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT), Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT), Internal Family Systems Therapy (IFS) and also completed Level 2 training in Gottman Method Couples Therapy. I am a Registered Associate, supervised by Torey Andrus, LMFT.

name: Kaijah Bjorklund (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I draw from the relevant neuroscientific research that is emerging within the field of psychology that provides a deeper understanding of behaviors and experiences. As a Somatic (body oriented) counselor I consider the importance of your felt experiences and how they impact you. I engage the power of mindfulness, or the practice of self-awareness, which builds bridges in different parts of the brain, and allows for new possibilities in emotional patterns and access to new choices. I don’t have a one-size fits all approach to what I do. I offer support and acceptance for what each person brings to counseling and what their needs are. I consider humans not to be pieces and parts but an interconnected whole. I have a variety of tools and strategies to assist in understanding what is happening and then taking action.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that every person faces challenges and has areas in their life where they may suffer. Everyone can feel alone and isolated at times and counseling support can help reduce the feelings of isolation and provide tools for managing symptoms. I believe that individuals in marginalized communities may not only hold individual trauma but collective and historical trauma that is necessary to address.

name: April Oliver (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I believe the therapeutic relationship between the client and a counselor is a collaborative one. I believe you are the expert of your life, and I am a facilitator of change and processing. I am an engaged therapist. I will ask questions to invoke self-inquiry and discovery. I offer resources, handouts, apps, and YouTube videos to support techniques we learn within our sessions. I utilize Solution-Focused Brief Therapy (SFBT), which is a client-centered approach with a focus to empower you to take action to meet your goals and expectations, which are set and controlled by you, little steps at a time. Aspects of Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT), Internal Family Systems (IFS), and Somatic Therapy as relied on as needed.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I am an interactive mental health counselor. I ask questions, and inquire about past tools that have worked, as well as discover together tools that will support you specifically. I offer homework and techniques to practice via YouTube, apps, or with handouts. I am very supportive and encouraging. I believe in you. I am affirming to your beliefs and identity. My practice is focused on trauma, which means looking at where your trauma is currently affecting your life. Within sessions, time and energy is spent processing your current thoughts, behaviors, and emotions that are influenced by past trauma. Utilizing techniques that can help you understand, make peace with, and make other choices outside of repeating unwanted thoughts and experiences. If you're ready to put in the work to find progress, I'm a good fit for you.

name: Joan Laguzza, LCSW Joie PDX Counseling
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My approach is adaptable to accommodate your needs, whether you are hoping to focus on a specific issue, or wanting ongoing support for a chronic or complex problem. My perspective is trauma informed, and I rely on techniques derived from multiple therapeutic disciplines, including cognitive behavioral therapy, motivational interviewing, narrative therapy, and dialectical behavioral therapy. I work with adults, whether you are looking for short term support or are wishing to deepen your understanding of yourself. My practice is inclusive of all cultures, ethnicities, sexual orientations, and abilities. I specialize in helping older adults, their families, and caregivers with the myriad issues associated with aging. These may include memory loss, medical challenges, changes in family dynamics, depression, anxiety, loss of relationships, and the complexities of long term care and end of life planning. I offer consulting services in addition to traditional therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a clinical social worker licensed in the State of Oregon by the Board of Licensed Social Workers. I hold bachelor's and master's degrees in social work from Radford University in Virginia. I have practiced in a wide variety of environments, including psychiatric and medical hospitals, nonprofit community mental health agencies, and an outpatient medical specialty clinic. I am an experienced advocate, creative problem solver, and empathic listener. I have lived in large cities and rural areas, across the United States and in Europe. I welcome diversity in my practice, and invite the challenges of adapting techniques to individual needs. I am passionate about helping others and want to guide people to discover their true selves, feel personally empowered, and make progress on their life's journey.

name: Rainee Arevalo (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Therapy is a journey, a commitment to whole person healing for lasting change. I believe that clients have the power to reclaim their stories as the experts of their own lives. My therapy approach provides a personalized, collaborative, and empowering approach incorporating insights from a diverse strength-based theoretical framework, such as trauma-focused cognitive behavioral therapy, narrative therapy, internal family systems therapy, and attachment. I am EMDR certified.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In my free time, I love nature, gardening, music and watching movies.

name: Katie Trageser (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
The following is a snapshot of my education and certifications: -Bachelor of Science in Psychology: University of Oregon -Masters of Arts in Professional Mental Health Counseling-Addictions: Lewis & Clark Graduate School of Counseling Education -National Certified Counselor (NCC) -Certified Clinical Anxiety Treatment Professional (CCATP) 265977 -Licensed Professional Counselor in Oregon #C7773

name: Alex Lazar (HE/HIM)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
What you and I identify as your goals for therapy will guide me in how I approach therapy with you. I am a person centered therapist at heart who also uses motivational interviewing, cognitive behavioral therapy, narrative therapy, and existential therapy. Learning what works for you and what doesn't is very important to me in your treatment. If you have tried previous therapies you did not find helpful, I am not going to force that back onto you. Healing does not look the same for everyone. Some folks want to be able to get out of bed in the morning and make it to work on time without forgetting their lunch. Other folks want to be able to go to a grocery store without having a panic attack. And some people just want to live to see tomorrow. How you heal and what that process it looks like will not look the same for everyone.
My Approach to Helping
Couples Therapy: I help couples strengthen their connection using the evidence-based Gottman Method, with a focus on improving communication, deepening emotional intimacy, and fostering greater hope, acceptance, and appreciation for one another. I believe couples come to therapy because they long for something to shift—and that meaningful change is absolutely possible. My approach is collaborative, warm, supportive and direct, creating a grounded space where both partners can feel valued and understood. I provide gentle yet direct challenges when appropriate and aligned with your established goals. I offer structure, focus, and direction throughout the couples therapy experience, helping you stay grounded and purposeful in your growth together. I aim to help you build and strengthen your relationship awareness—like a muscle that grows with intention, reflection, and practice. I believe in the power of relationships to heal, grow, and transform. It would be a privilege to support you.

name: Erin Davis (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I’ve spent nearly a decade in mental health and social work, starting in child and family services before shifting to adult outpatient therapy. My approach is rooted in Internal Family Systems and I am able to offer EMDR for trauma and DBT skills and existentialism in our time together, but my greatest strength is seeing you—fully and without judgment. I meet you where you are and help you find a way forward that feels both achievable and meaningful. Therapy with me is a balance of directness, empathy, and collaboration, creating a space where real change can happen while you feel deeply supported. My lived and professional experience shape my work, allowing me to offer insight, practical tools, and a therapeutic relationship built on trust. Together, we’ll navigate what’s holding you back and build a path toward healing that actually works for you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a queer identified, non-binary therapist. Many of the clients I serve hold identities in the LGBTQ+ rainbow. I use a trauma-informed lens to approach the work I do and have extensive clinical experience working with individuals at any point in their journey. My background as a social worker and therapist also includes working with individuals who experience suicidal thoughts, self-harm and who have complex trauma histories. I believe that healing and change are possible for all people. I am sex-positive therapist as well and believe that finding pleasure (not just sexual) in all areas of life is possible and deeply important for lasting change and healing to take place. The lenses I use most frequently in therapy include: integration, trauma-informed care, existentialism, cognitive behavioral therapy, attachment theory, and positive psychology.

name: Milo Bissett (HE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I draw from Humanistic, Queer, and Systems theories. My background is in social justice and social work, I emphasize the impact of intersecting identities and cultural experiences on each client's journey. Through a combination of trauma-informed practices and techniques drawing from a variety of interventions, I provide a supportive space for clients to build autonomy, advocate for themselves, and navigate life’s challenges.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in building trust, authenticity, and genuine care with my clients. I center my client’s values, personal goals, and story to direct our focus, and work to adjust my approach to meet my clients however they show up. I am passionate about working with clients who feel burnout from systematic oppression, capitalism, and traditional expectations of what success and fulfillment means.

name: Kelsey Marple (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach towards therapy is relational and trauma-informed. Often, I find that people are aware of the hurdles keeping them from living life to the fullest, but can benefit from professional support in actively working through them instead of staying stuck in a cycle. I view counseling as a collaborative effort and help my clients to recognize their strengths, identify needs, understand conflicts, discover new options, and make informed choices in a warm and non-judgmental counseling space through empathy and curiosity.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
We create habits and patterns of how we exist in the world, sometimes without our consent. I believe you can create a new way of moving through life, and being in the world. I hope to empower you to ditch what isn't serving you any longer, and to make decisions and habits based out of your true values and needs.

name: Beth Ann AKA BA (bee-ay) Short LCAT, ATR-BC (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In my work with clients, my theoretical lens aligns with a postmodernist approach, drawing from intersectionality, social constructionism, feminism, queer theory, antiableism and antiracism. I believe human beings will always be growing and the therapeutic relationship relies on empathy, language, storytelling, and art. Sometimes this may mean there are multiple truths and realities to explore, understand, and witness. We all experience transitions in life and many social locations (some seen and unseen). Sometimes these transitions are welcome and exciting, others may be confusing, tragic, or containing grief. My goal in the work I do is to support individuals honoring their creative process, personal strengths, and intersections while providing tangible opportunities for healing and growth.
My Values as a Therapist
Locations of self: I identify as a mixed race, gender queer/nonbinary person. I come from a diasporic Maliseet/Wolastiqiyik family who left Woodstock, New Brunswick, Canada. I am also Irish, German and English. I experience multiple invisible disabilities. I grew up around the Great Lakes region, living in both Michigan and Ontario throughout my youth. Experiences and aspects of my own identity and social locations are always interacting within me and the work I do as an art therapist and a credentialed art therapy supervisor (ATCS). I am committed to provide support that affirms all the social locations of those whom I serve.

name: Al Newman (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that, although the human experience can be challenging, it holds the potential for deep meaning. My goal is to work with you to help you re-write your stories in order to create the meaning and connection you wish to see in your life. I take a collaborative approach and while I may pull techniques from various evidence-based practices, I believe that safety in therapeutic relationships takes time to foster. Thus, I primarily use narrative, existential-humanistic, and attachment-based approaches to help you untangle the many possibilities for relating to yourself and the world around you.
My Values as a Therapist
As a queer therapist, I use systemic approaches to explore how oppressive systems affect LGBTQIA+ individuals and other marginalized groups. Issues like capitalism, white supremacy, and heteronormative standards impact all of us. In therapy, we can work together to unpack and challenge the internalized messages and negativity these systems create.

name: Julia Hilton
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have over a decade of experience working with adolescents and adult clients with diverse concerns, including anxiety, OCD, and depression. My approach to practice includes person-centered and trauma-informed treatment focused on assisting clients with clarifying goals and developing strategies to heal and move forward into their ideal lives.

name: Sarah Craycraft
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have a Master of Arts in Mental Health Counseling from Denver Seminary, and am a Licensed Professional Counselor. My approach to counseling includes elements from Person-Centered, Cognitive Behavioral, Existential, and Narrative therapies. Most importantly, I believe that your growth and healing happens best when you have a trusting relationship with your therapist, so I value building that relationship.
My Values as a Therapist
I desire to provide a warm and safe environment to help you work through difficult times. Whether you’re struggling with a relationship, going through a transition in life, or just need someone to lend a compassionate ear, I am here to help.

name: Amanda Melbostad (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a relational therapist, I work from a nueroqueer anti-racist framework and lean on existential, queer, narrative and mindful theories while also utilizing strategic and evidence based practices. I believe its important to balance evidence based tools with some of the magic and mystery of ancient techniques we haven't quite figured out how to measure yet. As a certified yoga instructor I use movement, mindfulness and art to gently guide people into connection with their true self which is where healing can begin. My focuses include Somatics, Trauma, toxic work environments, Queer 2SLGBTQIA+ experiences, and grief.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that healing is a basic human right, and we all hold the potential to heal. The struggles of daily life and oppressive systems can cause us to lose connection to our soul and purpose, leading to our suffering. Our bodies not only hold the stories of our lives but that of our ancestors. There is no one magic tool or way to heal, everyone's journey is unique and I believe a good therapist guides you to find the answers and tools that are right for you.I have been a long time change agent and active participant in enacting change within the oppressive systems I've been a part of; from leading a corporate employee resource group fighting for gender equity, volunteering with the Black Resilience Fund and p;ear for over a year. I've been a part of large fundraising efforts for Leukemia and Lymphoma society and Undestructable (DV & IPV organization).

name: Nancy Pounds
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Imagine feeling confident, empowered and more in touch with your Authentic Self. Picture yourself free from the struggles of your past. Together we can bring about change in your life by tailoring your therapy sessions to meet your unique needs. Along with talk therapy, I offer EMDR and Hypnotherapy, which can aid in (and often hasten) the healing process. Take back control of your life. Become your best self. Therapy can help. Contact me for a free 20-minute phone consultation where we can discuss what’s happening and what you’re wanting to change about your life. I would be honored to walk with yiu o your healing journey.
My Approach to Helping
I use a psychodynamic approach to therapy, which means I believe our past directly affects our present. However, I don’t believe in spending a lot of time digging up the past, but instead, examining it to see what type of clues we can find to help you heal from present issues.

name: Hussein Idow (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use evidence-based approaches, including Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT), and Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), to support clients in overcoming challenges. With over a decade of experience, I’ve worked extensively with individuals facing anxiety, depression, trauma, and relationship struggles. My goal is to provide a safe, nonjudgmental environment where you feel empowered to share your story and explore the root causes of your struggles. Together, we’ll develop practical coping skills and strategies for lasting change. My approach is collaborative, compassionate, and tailored to your needs, ensuring every step of your journey is aligned with your goals for growth and healing.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I believe that everyone deserves a life filled with purpose, connection, and joy. Therapy is not just about addressing challenges—it’s about discovering your strengths, embracing your unique qualities, and unlocking your potential. My interests include exploring mindfulness practices, promoting emotional resilience, and creating tools for personal empowerment. I’m passionate about providing culturally sensitive care and ensuring every client feels seen, heard, and valued. What sets me apart is my genuine commitment to understanding your experiences and guiding you toward meaningful progress. Whether you’re seeking relief from emotional pain, navigating difficult relationships, or striving for personal growth, I’m here to help you succeed. Together, we’ll work to create a life that feels truly fulfilling and aligned with your values.

name: Daniel McClure (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I aim to deeply understand your experience and tailor tools and treatment techniques to your specific needs. My experiences in education, mentorship, client interaction, as well as my own personal mental health journey have equipped me with a diverse array of resources to offer you
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
The human experience is a profound journey filled with both joys and significant challenges. If you're here, it may be because you've sensed an inner calling that something in your life needs attention. Whatever unique challenges you face, we can collaborate to foster change, gain new insights, and make meaningful progress toward your therapeutic goals. I deeply honor the vulnerability of my clients and hold immense respect for the transformative work that occurs in therapeutic spaces. I've been fortunate to learn from exceptional mentors, supervisors, clients, and my own therapists. Together, we can embark on a journey toward renewed beginnings and the pursuit of a more meaningful and healthy life.

name: Kylie Dieringer (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My goal is to provide you with tools for your toolbox so that when life pulls a fast one, you have the tools to help regulate, process and get through it. I am direct, challenging, and interactive but also provide warmth and empathy to create the space that you need. I work from an eclectic style pulling from modalities such as Cognitive Behavioral, DBT and other mindfulness-based practices.
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a counselor after trying to process and come to terms with the trauma and loss that I had previously experienced. It took me many years to be able to talk about what I went through and that's when I realized that sometimes, you just need someone to listen. Someone specifically who is a neutral party, someone who can look at what you're going through and empathize without judgement.

name: Francis Aurora Otto (SHE | HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer a gentle and collaborative approach to help heal the deep hurts that hold us back. Each one of us has adapted to our life circumstances as best we can. The patterns we learn are rooted in our early relationships and in our body. These patterns are deep and precede words. Together, I help you attune to, unwind, and rewire these patterns. In each session, I offer simple and effective homework practices to support your healing process. My goal is to help you cultivate more resilience, ease, and wholeness in your relationships and in your life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a 4th generation Portlander who's experienced the city go through a lot of change. I have a Master's Degree from Naropa University in Somatic (Body) Psychology and Movement Therapy. For the past 20 years, I have worked as a counselor and therapist with experience in community-based addictions and mental health treatment. I have also been a performer and child play specialist. Over the past 18 years, I have played with over 20,000 children. This unique perspective and experience has taught me how to heal adults. Along with this, I bring extensive training in Interpersonal Neurobiology (the brain science of relational healing), Body-Mind Centering® (the embodiment of developmental change), Biodynamic Cranial Therapy (a gentle approach to unwinding trauma), and Biomechanics (the science of healthy alignment and movement). My goal is to help you have more nourishing relationships in your life.

name: Meghan Rich (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My counseling style is grounded in a person-centered approach through a humanistic lens. It is trauma informed, integrative, holistic, intuitive, sprinkled with humor, and infused with authenticity. I specialize in addressing self-esteem, managing symptoms of bipolar disorder, healing acute and complex trauma, and recovering from narcissistic abuse. I am trained in EMDR therapy, which is an incredibly effective approach to relieve acute and complex trauma and so much more. It's always an honor to bear witness to the profound healing that transpires through this remarkable, and relatively rapid, method of treatment. Additional issues: anxiety, attachment issues, bipolar disorder, boundaries, career changes, codependency, communication, depression, divorce/separation, domestic/relationship abuse, existential concerns, gender expression and identity, gender transition, infidelity, life changes, relationships, self-discovery, self-esteem, sexuality, spirituality, and more.
Why I Became a Therapist
After 20 years in the marketing design and fine art fields; I realized I had been drawn toward the mental health field throughout my life. In my mid 40's, I pursued a Masters Degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from Regis University in Denver, Colorado. After graduating in 2020, I opened a private practice and contracted with several counseling groups in the Denver metro area for four years. In 2024, I became dual licensed (LPC) to serve clients in Colorado and Oregon. I've relocated to the Portland Metro area—where I was born and raised—to live close to my family again. It's wonderful to be back home!

name: Aaron Finbloom (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
To do parts work is to investigate our inner system of parts to more deeply understand them and thereby understand ourself. It is to find parts that are marginalized and help them feel accepted. It is to be a self-therapist for our inner family of parts and help them get along. It is to develop and strengthen parts that are missing or neglected. It is to gain greater awareness of our parts as they interface with the world. It is to be less controlled by our parts. It is to give "problematic" parts love, care, nourishment and acceptance so that they too can transform. My sessions integrate techniques of Psychodrama, Parts Mapping, Visioning, Process-oriented Psychology, Buddhist Meditation and Circling in an emergent relational process guided by the trauma-informed lens of Internal Family Systems (IFS). To read more about these practices, please go to my Parts Work website listed below. At this time I am only seeing clients virtually.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
For as long as I can remember I've been in love with questions. I adore the play of investigation – the curious back and forth that leads deeply and joyfully into the unknown. But this investigation gets even more fruitful when it looks into who and what we are, and when it leans into the emotions of this journey and the connection and intimacy between us curious humans. My foundational ethic is to foster curiosity, care and play towards our darknesses, and I believe this is what brings growth, transformation, healing and goodness in our world. Investigating my own inner parts, and learning how to hold them with tenderness and care, has had a tremendous impact on my own life, and I'm so very drawn to help others along this path. I hold a PhD in relational practice from Concordia University's Interdisciplinary Humanities & Fine Arts Program. I have received trainings in Circling, Psychodrama and Process Work. My sessions are deeply inspired by the trauma-informed practice of IFS.

name: Tara Roland (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer a safe, non-judgemental, and compassionate environment to explore, reflect and process. I'm trained on interventions such as EMDR (eye-movement desentization reprocessing), parts work, psychodynamic, grief-informed and trauma-informed, psychological astrology, and energy work & somatic therapies. Everyone is unique and my approach to therapy honors this by using an eclectic style. I pull from a lot of modalities to support my client's. My goal is to learn from you and help to facilitate desired healing and growth. You are the expert of your life and I am here as a guide. This physical reality can bring experiences of suffering. Know that you are not alone; and there is transformational power being witnessed in your truth. Come as you are. I welcome all parts of you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe we all hold gifts in this world and often times they are the ones that have been passed down throughout our lineage; at first disguising as trauma-responses. As we begin to heal, we get the opportunity to peel back the layers and reclaim ourselves as whole, worthy, and complete through the alchemical process of being open to transformation. My learning in the field of therapy has not only been in the classroom and gaining hours to complete my licensures. I too have been through the process of knowing what it is like on the other side of the seat in session work. To me, this is vitally important when working with clients as I know the vulnerability it takes to entrust someone with the deepest core of ourselves. Remember it is a privilege to hear your story! I'm fascinated with honoring creative aspects within the field that can be just as powerful as traditional modalities. I'm often learning new philosophical, theological, and mystical teachings and tools to incorporate.

name: Leslie Rosenfield (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I am grounded in a trauma informed person centered model. I believe each client is the expert in their own lives and are the drivers of the therapeutic process. Healing can be held and processed through a strong, trusting relationship, empathy, self-compassion, and unconditional positive regard for each client. I integrate internal family systems, expressive arts therapy, somatic therapy, and mindfulness based techniques into my practice. I believe that integrating breath-work into the counseling practice is a key component to regulating the nervous system. I support many clients that are moving through anxiety, depression, relational stress, grief, life transitions and also teachers! I am a former teacher so I now how taxing the education space can be for many.
Why I Became a Therapist
I decided to become a therapist while I was a middle school teacher. I had so many of my students coming to me with their struggles related to anxiety, family life, social issues, relationship issues and everyday life. I felt a strong pull to be able to support and help my students. However, my training was limited to teaching, I knew I wanted to do more. From there I earned a masters in science in counseling so I could learn how to best support those in need of mental health support.

name: Kate O'Brien (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have over 12 years working with children, youth, families, and adults. Starting out on my journey I spent 6 years as a behavioral therapist working with neurodivergent children and youth providing various types of family support. After graduating from PSU with my Masters in Social Work, I spent years as a therapist and social worker in schools. I became certified as an Adoptive and Foster Family therapist as well as a Somatic certified therapist. I am also a board approved licensing supervisor. I use an attachment lens, systems theory, racial theory, queer theory, a fat positive lens, a strengths-based lens, a whole person approach, and anti-racist trauma informed framework. I believe that our relationship and shared experiences can be foundations to work through your challenges. With an integrative and relational process I believe that in this context, you will be able to heal old wounds and live a more full and satisfying life.
My Approach to Helping
I believe that you are the writer of your own story and you know yourself better than anyone. I truly feel honored to be any part of someone’s journey. I believe anyone can heal and anyone can change. I am a person who has experienced trauma in childhood, struggled with attachment challenges, suffered great loss, and walked my own complex gender/queer journey. I believe all of these experiences inform my practice and how I can show up for you. As a white provider, I am committed to anti-oppressive and anti-racist practices. I attend regular trainings and engage in educational opportunities on anti-oppressive practice regularly. This will be ongoing work for me that I am deeply committed to. Over the past years I have honed in my practice to primarily serve the 2SLGBTQIA+ population and spent time supervising a team of therapists working at an 2SLGBTQIA+ healthcare clinic as well. I am deeply passionate about serving our community.

name: Annie Vail (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am both a Certified Hakomi Therapist and a Licensed Professional Counselor. My approach is ultimately holistic and somatic. I draw from variety of mindfulness, somatic, and attachment-based therapeutic modalities, including: -Hakomi Therapy -Internal Family Systems (IFS) and Gestalt -PACT Couple Therapy -Recreation-of-the-Self (RCS) -Somatic trauma release -Compassionate communication/ Non-violent communication (NVC). This combination of approaches fosters healing, empowerment, self-awareness, inter-relational awareness, transformation, authenticity, and creativity. My therapeutic style is a blend of compassion, warmth, and support, as well as being relational, grounded and direct. I aim to create a safe and empathetic environment for clients to explore and address patterns that hinder them from achieving their desires and goals. My true passion lies in supporting my clients to know themselves better, heal from the past, and step further into their own authenticity.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that beyond the struggle and pain of life is an innate wisdom that guides each person to their wholeness. As my client, I will see you in this wholeness, not your wounding. Your unique strengths, wisdom, and resources become allies in the therapeutic process. I will celebrate and embrace you exactly as you are now, while supporting your movement towards greater well-being. I will help you to compassionately see and understand the impact of core patterns and beliefs that give rise to your current circumstances. I am deeply committed to holding a systemic lens for all clients, but especially those with marginalized identities. Throughout my adult life, I have prioritized ongoing education and awareness about privilege, power dynamics, and oppression. I believe it is essential to consider how systems of power and privilege relate to each person's experience and I welcome these considerations and inquiries in my office.

name: Ajna Rae (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe we all have the ability to heal and grow from our sources of wounding. We can attend to issues of depression, anxiety, addiction, anger, codependency, and more that can arise from unresolved past experiences and current struggles. My collaborative therapeutic approach is rooted in mindful awareness and acceptance, informed by relational and attachment models, and guided by an appreciation of each person’s unique existential search for meaning. I aim to foster a space where you can engage in your present experience, show up as you are, and find awareness, choice, and support in the life that awaits you. You are a complex system of your experiences. We will work together for you to become more aware of yourself and discover possibilities for choosing a more fulfilling and satisfying relationship with yourself and others. Change can happen!
My Values as a Therapist
At the core of my values is a true understanding that you know yourself better than anyone else. It is sad to me that even in a society that values independence as much as ours, there is such a lack of promoting self-efficacy. As a therapist I believe that my job is not to fix problems or make quick judgments, but rather that the purpose for my presence in the therapeutic relationship is to offer unconditional respect of your uniquely complex human Self. It is extremely important to me that each person I sit with comes to understand their inherent strengths as they work towards their individual values. I know that we all have the capacity to exist in authenticity.

name: Brittany Schreurs (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work with clients who may be struggling with self confidence, self esteem and self worth that can cause anxiety and depression. These often have an impact on our relationships and daily life. Together we can look at how to best use your areas of strength to overcome these obstacles. I will be honest, transparent, understanding, empathetic, but I may also question, confront, and summarize. We are only human, so I may make mistakes. This isn't a one way street, it is a partnership. I will expect you to give feedback, question, and confront, but ultimately feel safe in doing hard work. I am a part of your journey. Only you know what will ultimately work for you in your life. (Who knows you better than you! Am I right?!) We will work together to determine how and why things aren't going as planned and how to get it all back on track so that you can start feeling and living your authentic life.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I am a retired educator and born and raised Texan who has traveled and lived all over the world, I have connected with people of different backgrounds, religions, cultures, languages, and values. I have experience working with neurodiverse and marginalized populations and am a passionate ally! And, I would be thrilled to work with you so that you can take steps to live the life of your dreams.

name: Terra Anderson (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a somatic sex therapist, I help you tap into what your body needs to relax, unfurl, and connect to pleasure. We will work with both body and mind to build awareness, skills, and confidence in navigating sexual communication, consent, and connection. What does this mean, you ask? Whether talking about sex comes easy to you or not, sexual satisfaction isn't just about lip service ;) its a full-body experience. My approach to therapy explores how movement, sensations, and breath are gateways to understanding your needs, desires, clear communication, and most importantly, consent.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My passion lies at the intersection of identity, pleasure and embodiment. Everything I love in this world has to do with one of those three things. My approach to therapy is no different. I'm on a mission to create more accessibility to pleasure in the LGBTQIA+ community. That means deeper erotic exploration, healing internalized shame, developing epic communication skills, claiming reverence for our bodies, and trailblazing new ways to structure relationships that truly meet your needs and leave heteronormativity to shrivel and rot on the vine. I'm interested in changing the world for us. I want you and I to have all the ecstasy and self-love society never taught us we could have. That's what I'm passionate about.

name: Maddy Engelfried (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have a background in art therapy as well as counseling, and I take a warm, compassionate approach to therapy. I’m genuinely fascinated by people, and I like to lead with my curiosity, both in getting to know clients and in helping to understand what they’re going through. Research has shown that what makes therapy work is the therapeutic relationship, so I focus on cultivating that with every client I work with. I think every behavior has a reason behind it. We don’t make ourselves miserable because we want to! However, the coping mechanisms that fit one circumstance don’t always transfer well to the rest of our lives. By figuring out why we do the things we do and what we can do instead, we can start to shift our way of being in the world. A large part of my work is helping clients learn how to process difficult feelings about these changes. Therapy is hard work, but with creativity and humor, we can make real, lasting changes to our lives and relationships.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As an existential therapist, I think everyone has both the right and the responsibility to figure out what brings meaning to their life and do it on purpose. I strongly believe in the power of personal narrative. I've noticed that many people who come to therapy need help rewriting the stories they tell themselves about who they are and where their lives can go. Fortunately, we all have creative potential that we can harness to revise our personal stories and attack our problems instead of ourselves. As a nerd, I appreciate that it’s possible to think very hard about our feelings without actually feeling them. Unfortunately, the feeling part isn’t really optional - if you decide not to feel your feelings, your body will eventually feel them for you. Our society doesn't usually give us the time or tools to do this, but therapy can! When I'm not therapizing, I spend my time playing TTRPGs, drawing comics, and enjoying Portland's beautiful outdoors.

name: Bethany Ingram (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I utilize various techniques to help you cope with (and feel) difficult emotions, connect to your mind and body, and understand the impact of your past and present. I believe that a safe, supportive therapeutic relationship is vital to the healing process. I will provide empathy, curiosity, humor, and connection as we build trust and find a rhythm that works for us. I specialize in treating grief and loss, PTSD, including Complex PTSD and early childhood trauma, as well as anxiety and depression. My focus is in healing your relationships - with yourself, with others, and how you relate to the world around you, including the natural world. Depending on your needs, I might use short-term solutions focused interventions to find current relief and/or psychodynamic interventions that focus more on your childhood wounds and internalized belief systems. I rely heavily on the relational process and getting to know your strengths, needs, and relationships as an individual.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
