I approach my work with the belief that we are all connected - and our connections to ourselves, to others, and to the earth is where we find healing. I see my clients as the expert on their own lives and myself as a guide to support them to listen to themselves and trust themselves in their own healing and growth. I come to this work as someone who has experienced healing from trauma and grief within a therapeutic relationship, and it is my honor to provide the safety and connection to others as I have experienced in my personal life. This world is full of pain and trauma, systemic oppression, and humans hurting each other, yet I see an abundance of goodness and beauty in humanity, especially in my clients. I love shining this light of goodness and beauty within my clients when they find it difficult to see it in themselves. It is why I do what I do.

name: Jennifer Yamin
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I focus on contextual and intergenerational factors that have shaped your relationship with yourself and others. This includes uncovering attachment injuries that may have occurred both in your history and in current relationships. These events from our past often cause us to reenact unhealthy scenarios, in hopes of a different outcome. Using a variety of techniques, including Narrative, Solution- Focused and EMDR, I will help you reframe your story, allowing you to make choices that better serve you. I believe you are asking me to take you somewhere you haven’t yet been. As your guide and companion, we will walk this journey together, but you will get there by your own resiliency and courage.

name: Stephanie Luz Hernandez (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My clinical work is rooted in gender-affirming care and extensive experience supporting LGBTQIA+ communities. I take a relational, trauma-informed, and liberation-centered approach to therapy. Together, we co-create a space of safety, empathy, and curiosity, allowing you to explore your story, heal wounds, and reconnect with your inner resilience. My focus is not on “fixing” you but on helping you access the internal tools you already hold. We work collaboratively to unpack anxiety, shame, gender dysphoria, early attachment wounds, or body disconnection, and to cultivate meaningful, lasting changes. I am a WPATH GEI SOC8 Certified Member.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Therapy, for me, is both personal and political. As a queer and trans therapist, I am committed to dismantling systems of oppression that harm our bodies, relationships, and sense of self. My practice is grounded in values of equity, bodily autonomy, and collective care. I support movements for trans liberation, racial justice, disability justice, and decolonial healing. Outside the therapy room, I’m a proud cat-lover and a synth music explorer, and creative practices that feed my spirit and remind me of the nonlinear beauty of healing. I believe everyone deserves access to affirming care, and it’s a privilege to walk alongside clients as they reclaim their voices and imagine new possibilities for themselves.

name: Allison Amo MA, LPC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I provide a collaborative and holistic mind-body approach to psychotherapy. I believe that every individual has an innate capacity to heal and deepen their connection to self/others. I offer practical tools to help you achieve a greater sense of growth and well-being and find relief from the unhealthy patterns that keep you stuck. It is my goal to create a safe and compassionate environment in which your unique strengths and resources can be accessed, and the negative experiences from the past that continue to affect the present can be uncovered and resolved. I specialize in working with adolescents and adults who experience relationship issues, depression, anxiety, trauma, substance abuse, and eating disorders. Within these modalities, I integrate EMDR, attachment theory, IFS, sensorimotor psychotherapy, and somatic based approaches to provide the most comprehensive and holistic care.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold a master's degree in counseling psychology from Lewis & Clark College with an emphasis in Eating Disorders and Addiction. My training includes clinical experience in both partial hospitalization and outpatient levels of care with children, adolescents, and adults. I offer four years of experience in a partial hospitalization and intensive outpatient level of care as the Primary Therapist at the Eating Disorder Center of Portland and Program Director at Center for Discovery Portland Outpatient, which allowed me to work with an array of eating disorder symptoms and co-occurring conditions. I specialize in working with adolescents and adults who experience relationship issues, depression, anxiety, trauma, substance abuse, and eating disorders. NOT ACCEPTING NEW CLIENTS AT THIS TIME

name: Gayle Waitches
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My study of medicine and counseling continue to awaken my awe and sensitivity to the challenges and wonders of being human. I offer a holistic, inclusive, openhearted approach to therapy which welcomes all of who you are and rests on the belief that you hold unquenchable and trustworthy impulses for health, wholeness and Self-love. Our work together will be a safe and collaborative journey through which you lead the way through your life’s digestion process. A crucible for transformation, deep intimacy with oneself or another requires courage and authenticity. Tuning in and honoring your inherent wisdom allow you to carve a more congruent life which feels “true”, empowering and deeply satisfying. Potential vehicles to guide your therapeutic journey include Mindfulness, Internal Family Systems, AEDP, Hakomi, Attachment, Emotion Focused Therapy, Interpersonal Neurobiology, Polyvagal, Relational Life Therapy, Intimacy from the Inside Out and Multicultural Humility.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
A lifelong learner and practitioner, I am both a physician and a marriage, couple and family therapist intern. I obtained a MA from Portland State University while also immersing in a year-long internship at the M.E.T.A. Institute (Mindfulness Experiential Therapeutic Approaches) where I studied Hakomi and other mindfulness-based approaches. I also hold a Doctor of Osteopathic Medicine degree. While I continue to practice radiologic medicine, a yearning for deeper human connection and soulful exploration created a shift in my practice toward the study of mental and emotional well-being. Radiology and counseling share similarities as both require exploration and insight beyond the surface. Therapy offers a loving, safe, consistent and collaborative relational space through which to reacquaint more deeply with oneself. My training in the holistic world of Osteopathy braided with my conviction in the resilient human spirit are foundational for my counseling approach.

name: Katherine Noonan (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use an evidence based, integrative approach, and my goal is to identify your unique needs and aspirations. You’ll find I am warm , empathetic, kind and accepting, and that in our work together, I create a space where you can feel comfortable and free to share all parts of yourself.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
When I am not at Aspire Psychology, you’ll find me on a bleacher at a high school ball game or walking the golf course. I enjoy reading, traveling and most importantly, spending as much time as I can with family and friends.

name: Miranda Nelson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I believe that most pathology stems from scarcity, and that our scarcity is not always obvious to us. We feel good when we have enough time, space, energy, money, mobility, information, and community. The first step is identifying what kind of scarcity you're experiencing. I believe that navigating scarcity makes us more creative (finding ways to make the most of the resources available) and compassionate (deep familiarity with one's own scarcity makes the scarcity of others more visible.) I believe that community is an especially important resource. Communities pool resources and give access to more time, space, energy, money, mobility, and information than we could ever secure on our own. Because of this, I believe in the healing power of relationships. Relational therapy involves investigating what might be interrupting your ability to experience a secure sense of community. It's working towards finding more belonging and feeling more at home.
My Approach to Helping
Therapy is not life, it's a dress rehearsal for life. My therapy style focuses on helping you connect deeply with others, manage conflict, maintain meaningful relationships, and put language to the parts of your experience you fear nobody can understand. The therapy space is a practice arena where you learn to cultivate belonging and feel a bit less alone. Therapy should be a space for you to explore your unique identity. I'm an LGBTQIA2+ advocate and ally, practice from a poly/ENM/kink-aware perspective, use a feminist/multicultural lens, and believe it is imperative to recognize systems of oppression in clinical work. I believe in the power of consistent, dependable, unfiltered relationships and prioritize rapport when working with clients. I believe in using evidence-based interventions to provide meaningful care.

name: Jason Jones (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My style is pragmatic, conversational, genuine, and sometimes I interject my humor too! My ultimate goal is to create a compassionate and collaborative space where you feel comfortable and able to share your true self.

name: Jordan Knight (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapeutic style is mindfulness-based, acceptance-based, and attachment-based. My approach to counseling explores the impact of family and other important relationships on your mental health and wellbeing. I tend to work well with individuals who want to get in touch with their emotions, explore patterns and histories, and who are open to trying new strategies.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
You might be struggling with anxiety, depression, trauma, body image, or relationship stress. Or maybe you’re feeling overwhelmed by managing ADHD or OCD in a neurotypical world. I hope to meet your pain with compassion and uplift your strengths. I hope we can work together to empower you to create a meaningful path forward. Please reach out if you think we could be a good fit!

name: Cora Guren (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
You have taken the courageous and often difficult step of seeking help. It might help to know that I approach each new therapeutic relationship with warmth and curiosity. We are each unique in our life experiences, beliefs, joys, and struggles, and the most important thing I can do is to really listen. There are so many tools and approaches we can use, and there is no one “right” way to approach an issue. I believe in the importance of holistic approaches including exercise, mindfulness, psychotherapy, and medication. My practice style is diverse and is informed by cognitive therapeutic and humanistic approaches and an interest in Nonviolent Communication. I look forward to supporting you on this journey.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
The field of psychology is a long-term passion. As long as I can remember, I have spent my time trying to understand the human experience. I enjoyed studying in my PMHNP MSN program at Gonzaga University as well as my previous degrees, a bachelor of nursing degree at Linfield University and a BA in psychology from Carleton College. Prior to becoming a PMNP, I worked as a registered nurse for 17 years in a variety of settings across the lifespan including residential psychiatric treatment for children and adolescents as well as inpatient geriatric psychiatry. My education and experience leave me with a strong background to draw upon in my practice.

name: Melissa Masini, LPC, LMFT (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I draw from Systemic, Emotionally-Focussed, Cognitive Behavioral and Narrative theories in my work. I begin therapy with creating a warm relationship as I get to know your current concern and past relationships. I investigate context and offer alternatives to unsatisfying patterns as we use current life examples and plan for long-term goals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I graduated from the Marriage, Couples and Family Therapy Masters Program at Lewis and Clark College and have worked with individuals, couples and families in a variety of settings from residential treatment to research. My ongoing studies have included courses in sex therapy, Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR), and mindfulness. In my personal life, I have been participating in an Anti-White Supremacy book and work group and have been learning as much as I can about Climate Change and my place within that work.

name: Carl Glissmeyer (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I seek to create an opportunity for real change through both reflection and action. My belief is that each person has a unique story that deserves attention, and I am committed to listening. Approaching discussions in a relaxed and practical way, I have seen many positive shifts in people. Let's make decisions together – I want you to feel well-informed and actively engaged in your care.

name: Mindful Therapy Pacific NW, LLC
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We believe that mental health involves the whole person: not only the mind, but our bodies, spirit, unique personal history, occupation, environment and personal relationships. Our providers range from veteran therapists to highly-trained graduate student interns. All of our dedicated practitioners have specialized training in mindfulness and the somatic experience to address complex life challenges such as trauma and attachment injuries. Each of our practitioners has their own specialization and training, but they all emphasize the practice of mindfulness, which is a particular state of mind or vulnerability. This allows us to have reactions and responses to life events without judgment or self-criticism so that we may study ourselves and understand why we do the things we do. Once we can understand ourselves at this deeper level, we have options and choices of how we can respond rather than unhelpful and dysfunctional automatic and habitual responses.
Why We Started this Group Practice
Mindful Therapy Pacific NW, LLC’s philosophy is to promote holistic wellness, build resilience and initiate transformation. We believe and assume that each individual is inherently already whole and through therapeutic support and utilizing nonviolence, mindfulness, organicity, unity and body-mind awareness that wholeness can be rediscovered and engaged with once again. The primary intentions of Mindful Therapy Pacific NW, LLC are to provide trauma-informed, high-quality and low-cost counseling services to underserved populations in Oregon and Washington. Mindful Therapy Pacific NW, LLC also aims to offer a rich and supportive learning experience for graduate students in counseling programs and Registered Associates working towards licensure.

name: Lesley Smith (THEY/SHE)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW) in the state of Oregon and I hold a Master’s of Arts in Social Work from the University of Chicago. The primary type of therapy I use is Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), with a trauma-informed, harm reduction, and anti-oppressive practice lens. I aim to be creative in my approach, meaning that I want to adapt to whatever approach best fits your needs. I am neurodiversity-affirming and love to find ways to make your life easier. I am currently being trained in Somatic Experiencing (SE) and am in the Advanced Year of training. SE is a body-centered trauma-healing modality. I also offer Safe and Sound Protocol (SSP) to assist in nervous system regulation. I specialize in working with people with anxiety, difficulty regulating emotions, recent life transitions, and communication issues. I am here to witness your emotions, help you find harmony, and discover where you want to go.
My Values as a Therapist
My main value is safety - I want you to feel safe during our time together. This is why I also say that I use anti-oppressive practice. To me, that means that I am constantly learning and growing along with you. It means that I am open to understanding the identities that you hold, beyond the labels, with the hope that you aren't needing to educate me. This also means that I will label racism, classism, ableism, homophobia, transphobia, and other oppressive systems when they show up in our time together. I do this because these systems of oppression impact our experience of the world and our relationships. I value curiosity and want to learn more about you and your relationships with the labels you hold. I take a non-judgmental stance and want to help you figure out what works best for you. also value creativity in the therapy space and want to make sure I am adjusting my approach to fit your needs. I look forward to exploring your needs and supporting you on your journey.

name: Joyce Korschgen (MS)
approaches:
What We Specialize In
Our mission is to provide services that improve personal well-being through facilitated contact and relationship development with nature. The Center at Heron Hill offers services for children, youth and adults. Our approach includes work with individuals, families, and groups. We are a group that varies greatly in training approaches. We operate off of a trauma-informed lens with a goal-focused practice. We utilize experiential therapy approaches such as hiking, archery, animal tracking, survival skills training, art and play, mindfulness, and animal-assisted therapies.

name: Kimberlee Harrison
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I take an integrated approach to therapy that is tailored to each individual's needs. Collaboration is imperative for therapy to be effective, as I believe that every person is the expert of their life. Together we can work on empowering you to make the changes you want for your life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold a Master of Science (MS) in Clinical Mental Health Counseling, am a Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC), a Certified Grief Informed Professiona (CGP), and a Level 2 Certified Clinical Trauma Professional (CCTP-II). I published a book in 2017 titled "Empowerment Through Mythology: A Clinician's Guide to Narrative Group Therapy for Survivors of Trauma and Abuse." I also have a YouTube Channel called "Mental Health Minute with Kim."

name: Genevieve Cleverley (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
By working together we can address your concerns and explore creative ways to make change while acknowledging your inherent strengths and resilience. My approach is eclectic and trauma-informed. I incorporate interventions from modalities including narrative therapy, Internal Family Systems, and ACT in treatment. As a person-centered therapist, I believe it is necessary to be collaborative and transparent in planning treatment. I enjoy helping clients find unconventional ways to incorporate art, music, and other forms of creative expression into their healing journey.

name: Beau Henderson (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We'll work together to create a counseling process that meets your needs and provides safe, relationally engaging and reparative experiences. You are the expert on your life and we will utilize your strengths as well as the collaborative relationship between client and counselor to get you where you want to go. I work from a person-centered and experiential approach with special training in Child Centered Play Therapy and integrate techniques from attachment science, interpersonal neurobiology and sandtray therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As people are provided an environment and therapeutic relationship characterized by safety, acceptance, and therefore freedom to explore self, they are empowered to build a healthy self-concept, personal responsibility, self-direction, self-acceptance, decision-making skills, sense of control, self-reliance, coping awareness and skills, internal source of evaluation, trust of self and resiliency.

name: Mikaela Rodriguez (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer collaborative, empathetic, and evidence-based medication management and psychotherapy for anxiety, depression, ADHD, cannabis & alcohol-related issues, insomnia and sleep problems, phobias, hair-pulling/skin-picking behaviors, and more. I also offer comprehensive diagnostic ADHD evaluations (2 hours+). ADHD Therapy: I specialize in helping individuals develop skills to overcome ADHD-related challenges (e.g., time management, organization, planning, motivation, procrastination, and more) while also addressing the shame and feelings of inadequacy that often accompany ADHD.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I offer appointments online via secure video Monday's-Thursday's 9 am - 4 pm. Established clients can schedule and cancel their appointments online through their secure patient portal. Due to my psychiatry practice being virtual-only I do not currently prescribe controlled substances (stimulants, benzodiazepines). I can collaborate with your PCP, sharing detailed recs for how/why to start a stimulant, if needed. I am in-network with Pacific Source, Moda, and Blue Cross/Blue Shield commercial plans only (not OHP, Medicare). To increase my availability to individuals with limited financial means, I am not accepting new cash-pay or OOP clients. am not currently offering formal CBT-Insomnia only sessions. To get started click **SCHEDULE NOW** above and complete a PATIENT INQUIRY FORM (a response is not guaranteed).

name: Susan Lehman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
With over 40 years of experience working with families and children and a licensed minister, I have a wealth of practical knowledge and education to support you in unconditional love and without judgement. Willing to do Phone/Facetime/Zoom sessions at clients' request. Office in Northeast near Alberta Arts district.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have 40+ years of experience/training in Family Services, Certified Positive Discipline Training (C.P.D.T), Parent Education, Holistic and Rapid Transformation (H.A.R.T), Creative Therapy, Evolved Parenting and Co-Active Coaching models, Metaphysics, Infant/Child "whispering", crystal communication & spiritual counseling. I also incorporate shamanic practices and crystal healing as needed. I am a member of the LGBQ community. INCOME BASED REDUCED RATES.

name: Caty Buckley (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
So, for part of my life, I work at an area high school. Another part is working in private practice with young people and finally, I have my own teenagers who are navigating issues just like you. I am knee-deep in teenagers/young adults and I wouldn't have it any other way! I think my clients like working with me because I am easy going and playful in sessions. I'm relatable and truly bring no judgment to sessions. Additionally, there is no topic that is off limits to talk about. I bring my quirky, off-beat sense of humor into sessions and feel like it's important to balance hard work with fun. You drive the bus and my job is to be a tour guide to provide you with ideas and questions about where we might need to go to reach the goals you set for yourself. It's a collaborative approach and I depend on you to communicate with me about what works for you and what might not. I truly believe that the magic happens when the client and therapist really "vibe" with one another.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I actually don't believe that every therapist is a good fit for every client. Sometimes a therapist's style is really not the best match for a client's particular personality traits or preferences. According to some of the clients I work with, they appreciate me because I don't just tell them what they want to hear. I like to think of therapy as just having a conversation with a caring, unbiased person about things that can sometimes be hard to talk about. My role in that conversation is to be like a mirror to reflect back to you both what you are describing and also what I am observing. I believe that we don't get results if we stay in our comfort zone and my job is to (gently) support you as we, together, push that comfort bubble to expand a bit r over time. It is a true joy in my life to see young people do things they never thought they could do, to address pain and challenges that have long been buried, or to just feel liberated by telling their story.

name: Lucius Wheeler (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Integral therapy begins with a basic attitude that all people have a tremendous capacity for change, and that we all contain within us a natural impulse towards healing. Therapy is a collaborative relationship, I will invite you to “lead the way” as we explore what is present for you. It is important to me that you feel seen, heard, and empowered in the therapy process. Together, we will work towards the creation of an atmosphere of safety and trust that supports your healing process. My work is experiential, using dialogue and awareness to help those I work with to understand themselves and regain a sense of agency in their lives.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Licensed Professional Counselor in the state of Oregon. I received my master's degree in Integral Counseling Psychology from the California Institute of Integral Studies in San Francisco, CA. My graduate and post-graduate experience includes specializing in psychedelic integration services, working as an outpatient drug and alcohol counselor, a school-based mental health therapist, and an intensive outpatient mental health therapist. Previously, I worked in the field of personal growth and natural healing for over a decade. My work is informed by my own process of growth and learning, which has helped me to understand the challenges and opportunities in every life change. I receive ongoing training and supervision in Gestalt therapy from Gestalt Therapy Training Associates Northwest, Gestalt Associates Training Seattle, and other professional trainers.

name: Cayley Mesterheide (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My passion for psychiatry began before I started PA school while working as a nursing assistant in the psychiatric unit of a hospital. While working at the hospital, I achieved my Bachelor of Science in BioHealth Sciences degree at Oregon State University. I then went on to pursue my Master’s in PA Studies at Lipscomb University in Nashville, Tennessee. My approach to treating mental health is holistic and includes a focus on medication management while incorporating supportive therapy and lifestyle modifications. I strive to connect with each and every patient and make it my goal to ensure that they feel heard and understood. Helping patients to find the right therapeutic regimen that allows them to function best in life brings me fulfillment in my career. I look forward to meeting and collaborating with you to help you achieve your mental health goals.

name: Nicole Craig (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My work is strongly founded in establishing a safe and secure therapeutic relationship. I provide non-judgmental support for a variety of issues including anxiety, depression, life transitions, identity concerns, PTSD/trauma, mood disorders, psychotic disorders, personality disorders and chronic illnesses. I tailor my approach to your needs. Some ways we may work together include art therapy or mindfulness-based approaches. I see every client as a unique individual to be treated with respect, an open mind, objectivity, and acceptance. I provide a safe environment where you can speak both openly and freely.
Your well-being, happiness, and progress are at the center of your therapy. I'm a strong advocate, and I work with adolescents and adults of all ages. I am an LGBTQ affirmative provider, and am experienced working with other non-traditional lifestyles. I love working with people invested in personal growth and healing. I will work to help you find new outcomes to problems that may keep repeating. I have several years experience working with addictions and am a certified alcohol and drug counselor (CADC). Prior to becoming a therapist I worked as a first responder in responding to psychiatric, medical , and traumatic emergencies, and I enjoy working with other current and former first responders. Variable hours, including evening and weekend appointments.

name: Shayla Pellerin (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to helping is grounded in empathy and collaboration. I believe in empowering individuals to discover their own strengths and solutions, rather than providing prescriptive answers. I prioritize creating a safe, non-judgmental space where clients feel heard and understood. Utilizing a variety of therapeutic techniques, tailored to individual needs, I aim to facilitate self-awareness, promote healing, and foster personal growth. I am committed to culturally competent care, respecting diverse backgrounds and experiences. My goal is to guide clients on their unique journeys towards a more fulfilling and authentic life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I deeply value authenticity, compassion, and social justice. I believe in the inherent worth and dignity of every individual, and I am committed to creating a safe and inclusive space for all. I strive to be an ally to marginalized communities, and I actively support the LGBTQ+ community, recognizing the unique challenges faced by individuals within this population. I believe in the power of empathy and understanding to bridge divides and promote healing. My dedication to cultural competency stems from a belief in the importance of acknowledging and respecting diverse backgrounds and experiences. I am committed to ongoing self-reflection and education to ensure that my practice is aligned with these values. My goal is to empower individuals to live authentically and thrive, free from judgment and discrimination. My drive to become a counselor was born from a desire to create a safe space for people to find their own strength, and to help them build a life where they feel understood.

name: Asha Jetmalani (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer psychiatric medication management, psychotherapy and comprehensive psychiatric evaluations using a HIPAA secure virtual platform to patients in Oregon. My psychotherapeutic style is eclectic, drawing from a range of effective modalities including Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT), Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), and motivational interviewing, alongside principles of Nonviolent Communication. I believe deeply in the power of holistic approaches to support mental well-being. Exercise, nourishing food, and connection with community are essential components of healing. If you are seeking ongoing psychiatric care, we will meet for 1-3 initial consultation visits to assess if we are a good fit for one another. If we mutually decide to move forward with ongoing care, we will collaborate to find an appointment schedule that meets your needs.
Social Justice Issues We Care About
I am dedicated to providing compassionate, inclusive, and trauma-informed care. My practice is rooted in principles of anti-racism and actively challenges systems of oppression, including anti-LGBTQIA+ discrimination and all other forms of marginalization. I believe that mental health care should empower individuals by validating their lived experiences and understanding how these intersect with larger systemic issues. I am committed to creating a safe, supportive environment where people from all backgrounds can feel seen and heard.

name: Tyler Walsh (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background
Greetings! I am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW) and Certified Alcohol and Drug Counselor, practicing at Rose City Therapists and at Rainfall Medicine as a Ketamine-Assisted Therapist in Portland. I hold a Master of Social Work (MSW) from the University of New Hampshire and a B.A. in Environmental Studies from the University of Colorado. I am also a veteran of the Iraq and Afghanistan wars. My clinical experience includes inpatient behavioral health, intensive addiction treatment, individual counseling, and community mental health. In addition to traditional therapy, I am a 400-hour certified breathwork facilitator and a 200-hour certified Tantra Yoga teacher. My approach integrates Transpersonal Psychology, Jungian Psychology, Internal Family Systems (IFS), Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), and Depth Psychology. I offer a grounded and compassionate presence, honoring each person’s innate wisdom and unique path toward healing.

name: Leslie Magana (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
By approaching every client with radical compassion and honoring their unique lived experiences, I can begin to gently confront harmful behavioral patterns. This process opens the door to tackle difficult emotions and the defense mechanisms that keep a person stuck. I believe that change is facilitated through deepened awareness of the beliefs, emotions, and cultural influences that shape us. This informed self-awareness combined with compassionate accountability, provides the foundation for growth. Relationships can be deeply fulfilling – and also incredibly challenging. My expertise lies in helping individuals and couples break harmful cycles, improve communication, and strengthen connections. Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT) and Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), with an awareness of power dynamics and systems.

name: Emily Trum (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I often use neuroscience based art therapy directives to support nervous system regulation and positive brain function. I am a person centered, attachment focused art therapist who often uses the Stages of Change and the Hero's Journey to support identification of self and goals in therapy to increase positive life experiences, make changes and increase personal identity.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that art is for everyone. Art making can support an integration of aspects of self for wholeness and healing. Art holds value in the process of creating, the resulting product or any range in between. Art has defined who I am and helped me process my own life experiences and integrate them into myself in a healthy manner. I personally make art to process my own stuff and continue to reflect on how I can interact with this world as a person that I like and am proud to be. It is my goal to help others engage in the world comfortable in their own skin with hope and direction.

name: Natascha Yogachandra (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe our work together is collaborative, and that you are the expert of your own experience. I view your coping mechanisms as wise strategies that your body has relied on over the course of your life. Together, we can examine these strategies, explore if there’s anything that might work differently/better, and introduce more choice into your internal landscape. I often rely on somatic practices, and look to your body as a powerful messenger. Even if your connection to your body feels severed or damaged, we will work to repair that line of connection at your own pace. I also draw guidance from Internal Family Systems, which views our inner system as a collection of parts that protect our core Self. I am firmly grounded in a decolonized approach to therapy, and will often address the external and oppressive systems that impact your well-being.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Brown, biracial, queer, woman living with chronic illness and disability. I grew up in both the US and in Asia. I experience the world in a marginalized body, while also holding many privileges (cis-gender, formally educated, US citizenship, midsized.) I do not believe in the “blank slate” therapist, and will always welcome conversations about how our identities (whether they overlap or differ) impact our work together. I have my own personal relationship with various forms of trauma, and have intimate awareness of the messy work it takes to experience our own path of recovery. I deeply believe in harm reduction and mutual aid. I am particularly interested in our relationships with our own bodies, and the way in which these relationships are affected by chronic illness, anti-fat bias, white supremacy, and capitalism. I recognize I am still learning and growing—and always will be—and I am committed to further grounding myself in anti-oppressive practices.

name: Justice Arledge LPC Associate, MS (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Life can be a very difficult road to haul, but nobody said you have to do it alone. In the world, we have doctors, teachers, all kinds of people that help us along the way it only makes sense to have somebody to help you process through difficulties in life. Hello, my name is Justice Arledge I have been working in this field since 2009 I also have my Masters degree in mental health counseling. I have more than 10 years of experience helping people work through the many unpleasantries that we find in our everyday life. I have helped people work through trauma, depression, and PTSD. I have worked both with adolescents and adults. My goal as a counselor is to put you back in the driver seat get you to a point in your life where you can make decisions and trust yourself. Help you to make and achieve long-term goals and to walk through life proudly and loving who you are. We all struggle with something, but my goal is to help you find positive voices that can defeat the negative voices.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Seeking help is hard, but it is also one of the best things you can do for your life’s journey and the people that are apart of it. I Think of it this way, if you had a broken leg you would not run a marathon before healing with the help of a doctor. Every day you are running the marathon of your life by trying to get along day today. You could be struggling with anxiety, depression or any other issues with mental health. Seeking a therapist or counselor for your mental health is no different than seeing a doctor if you had that broken leg. My Approach to Counselling and Therapy This is a question that is difficult to answer because there are so many wonderful tools out there. Below are some of the theories that I do lean towards. I consider myself a cognitive-behavioral therapist. I use a lot of elements from other theories, One of my favorite techniques is reality testing and exposure therapy.

name: Marisol G. Westberg, Ph.D.
approaches: I am sex-positive and am inclusive of alternative relationships and erotic minorities.I am an Assistant Professor in the Marriage, Couple and Family Therapy Program at Lewis and Clark College.  I coordinate the Sex Therapy Track for the program where I teach sex therapy classes and supervise students in their clinical training.  I am the author of the book:  "Pleasure: The Secret Ingredient in Happiness."

name: Magdalene Kidd Vasconez (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
When working with patients, I strive to create a collaborative, safe, and nonjudgmental space where your experiences and goals are valued and prioritized. I believe that meeting you where you are while gently encouraging forward movement, leads to successful growth and change. My approach is rooted in balance—helping you navigate where you are now and where you want to be. I look forward to supporting you every step of the way on your journey to healing and personal transformation.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
My approach to therapy combines evidence-based practices like Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT), and Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR). I also draw from Solution-Focused Therapy (SFT), Internal Family Systems (IFS), and Gestalt techniques to tailor the process to your unique needs.

name: Casandra Perks (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
When we work together, you can expect a warm, down-to-earth presence that offers a compassionate, non-judgmental space for you to deeply reflect and explore your thoughts, feelings, and stories. I value humor, both in life and in work, so you can expect a little of that as well. I use a trauma-informed, person-centered approach, believing that with the right support, everyone can grow into their best self. I don't have the answers to how you should live your life, you ultimately know what will be best for you. Life can be busy and distracting, making it feel rare to have dedicated time to focus on our needs, which can leave us feeling overwhelmed, unclear, or stuck. Sometimes it just takes slowing down and doing a bit of digging and reflection to clarify or make sense of things. Whatever it is that may have brought you here today, I would be honored to have the opportunity to understand your experience so you feel seen and supported along the way.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
Much of our work will focus on your strengths, highlighting the skills and resiliency you’ve naturally developed over the years, all while supporting you in developing new ways of coping if old patterns are no longer working. Together we will build tools to help you move toward the healing and growth you want to see, even if that simply means growing more comfortable sitting with the uncertainty life holds. Other methods I incorporate into my practice include psychodynamic theory, somatic noticing, and mindfulness. I believe what we have experienced in the past affects how we live in the present. If it feels safe and comfortable for you, we can use somatic noticing to tune in to your body, exploring your experiences on a deeper level. Becoming a therapist has been deeply driven by my own life experiences of loss and yearning for self growth. I really love this work.

name: Jennifer Violette (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Therapy is more than overcoming struggles or navigating life’s challenges—it’s about connecting with yourself and what truly matters to you. In our work together, I offer a compassionate and collaborative space where you feel heard and empowered. At the same time, we will take a direct approach, aimed at challenging patterns, reframing perspectives, and exploring new ways of being. I use a trauma-informed, person-centered, and holistic approach to this work, acknowledging the deep connection between the mind, body, and spirit. I often use emotional experiencing, thought defusion, acceptance, and mindfulness exercises to support your growth and healing. Inclusion and cultural humility are core values of my practice, where people of all identities and backgrounds are welcomed, can feel safe, and are valued.
My Values as a Therapist
I value an environment grounded in authentic connection, empathy, acceptance, and learning. My practice is rooted in balance, recognizing the importance of addressing both the challenges and strengths that clients bring to the therapeutic process. In therapy, clients are supported to explore their values, navigate life's complexities, and create meaningful change in their lives. I believe in challenging societal norms and promoting body positivity and acceptance for all body types. I am passionate about supporting fat liberation and actively working to deconstruct bias toward fat individuals. My goal is to empower clients to embrace their bodies and cultivate a sense of self-worth that transcends societal expectations.

name: Avery Bill (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I specialize in talk therapy both online and in-person. Our relationship and compatibility is the foundation for productive therapy. The therapy process can yield positive growth and help you to gain insight, reduce your suffering, and enjoy life and relationships more by moving through discomfort rather than avoiding it. I am here for you and we can figure this out together. I am a trauma-informed provider and I believe that we can learn how to better regulate our emotions and our stressed out nervous systems, be less reactive, and experience lasting change. I am a flexible, caring, and non-judgmental practitioner who is present and attuned with you. I am educated in a variety of strategies to help you to access and integrate past trauma and make sense of things now. I am passionate about integrating tools that really work for you, including process/talk therapy, yoga, meditation, and sound therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a clinical social worker who strives to support and advocate for my clients. As a parent myself I have a strong awareness of the difficulties of parenting, especially with all of the stressors in our lives recently. I believe that many of us are stuck in a chronic state of flight or flight/nervous system activation due to the level of stimulation and demands of our modern lives. Together we can assess your stressors and come up with a plan and tools to help bring you into a more peaceful and balanced state of mindfulness and understanding. In my personal life I am a practitioner of Buddhism, yoga, and a Reiki master. I enjoy cats, camping, and my VW bus.

name: Sarah Tedrick (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
There are many modalities that I am trained in, but it is my commitment and priority to collaborate to find a best fit approach, ensuring we develop a cohesive relational space in which to work together toward your goals. I believe relationship has the power to create positive change and I welcome the opportunity to walk alongside you in that endeavor. Reaching out for support can be challenging, but I encourage you to take that first step. I look forward to working together to achieve the outcomes you desire for yourself. You got this, and I believe in you. In the meantime, take care of yourself and those around you; take a moment to notice the spark inside you leading you forward. Until then, -Sarah-

name: Bryce Cohan
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I am drawn to relational therapy because I believe healthy partnerships are fundamental to people's quality of life, yet this area often receives insufficient direct support. I am particularly passionate about providing support to clients who struggle to find help due to their sexual and gender identity or their relationship structure. As a queer and non-monogamous therapist, I offer clients a level of familiarity with these areas that remains rare, even in Portland. I will do my absolute best to help everyone in the room feel seen and understood and work from that base to figure out what comes next. My therapy style is warm and present and straight forward; we’ll talk about goals, and timelines, and how to stabilize quickly so that getting from one week to the next feels less challenging. I offer free 15 minute phone consultations to get a better idea of whether I’d be a good fit for you. I look forward to talking.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I grew up right here in Portland, and while I’ve spent time living in other states and countries, Portland has always felt like home. I love the city and I love the beautiful environment around us, but Portland has always been home because of my relationships with friends, family, partners, and community. When I am not at work, I’m dragging my friends and partners to secret swimming holes, fiddling around with endless projects--often involving woodworking or computers--and sharing good food and quality time with loved ones.

name: Lee Scher (HE/HIM, THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My approach is deeply informed by Buddhism and Jungian psychology. As I listen to clients I am listening for the core feelings they are expressing and the images that are arising, whether in their dreams or waking life. By reflecting back the feelings and images I hear, I can support them in understanding the journey they're on, what's at stake for them, and how they can act on their own behalf and advocate for themselves in relationships.
Why I Became a Therapist
As a neurodivergent (autistic) person myself, I understand how challenging it can be to navigate this world as a highly-sensitive, open-hearted person who may experience things differently from the people around you. My central inspiration as a therapist is to help people listen more deeply to and trust their own experiences. We are not all the same, and generic advice cannot help you for your own way forward. You need to trust your own organism. That's where you'll find the most trustworthy guidance. My job as a therapist is to help you do that.

name: Sally Rudolph (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
As an art therapist, I offer creative opportunities for growth that are informed by IFS (Internal Family Systems) somatic approaches, journaling, and regular talk therapy. I offer a supportive, hands-on way to process and transform life challenges. Art therapy is based on the belief that we all have creativity within us that can help us heal, grow and integrate our experiences. I have worked in community mental health, day treatment, hospice and the hospital settings. In private practice I have experience working with trauma, anxiety, depression, health crisis, and grief. I have a particular passion for life transitions that may cause grief, such as loss of health, the death of an important person, relationship changes, or existential grief. I provide workshops, groups and individual counseling. I am a licensed-certified art therapist in Oregon (LCAT) and board-certified art therapist (ATR-BC). I have a BS in Human Services, a BFA in Art Education and MA in Art Therapy Counseling.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
These are the things I believe in for myself and for any other human being: Art is able to express crucial things we don't have words for. We all have untapped wisdom within us. Everyone deserves to be seen and heard, just as they are. We all need more meaningful connection. Our bodies send us messages that are important to listen to. Everyone has creativity within them. Nature is medicinal. Making things feels really good! We should all strive to find and be our authentic selves. We are whole just as we are (even with imperfections.)

name: Ryan Varian (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
With a background in community mental health and primary care behavioral health, I offer an integrative, culturally responsive, and values-based approach to therapy. I believe therapy is most effective when it goes beyond symptoms—when we explore your values, goals, and passions together. Through this lens, we co-create a foundation rooted in curiosity, connection, and emotional safety, opening space for healing and growth.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I’ve had the privilege of expanding gender-affirming care at a community health system in Seattle and helped develop a behavioral health department embedded within a community center—where I facilitated programming focused on emotional wellness and connection. These experiences have shaped my commitment to making therapy accessible, affirming, and grounded in real-world community values.

name: Stacey Berry (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I prioritize connection and bringing myself into the room in order to facilitate comfort and rapport with my clients. I adapt how I work with clients based upon their age/developmental level and personality. I primarily utilize cognitive behavior therapy (CBT), behavior therapy (BT), and motivational interviewing (MI) techniques in session. It is a joy to help youth understand the way that the mind and the body are connected and to learn skills that will assist in their overall wellness.

name: Karenna Bozicevich (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use an eclectic approach to counseling, integrating elements of narrative therapy, Dialectical Behavioral Therapy, Acceptance and Commitment therapy, and existential therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I work from a client-centered and strengths-based perspective, which views counseling as a collaborative process and the client as the expert in their own life. I work with each client to foster self-exploration, strengthen their relationships with themselves and others, and to find meaning and purpose in their lives.

name: Jennifer Wohl, MA, LPC
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In addition to providing deep, empathic listening, I offer clients the opportunity to work with dreams, expressive arts, Hakomi, and EMDR (Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing, an evidence-based treatment for healing trauma). In all of my work, I strive toward increasing clients’ compassion toward themselves and others. I work mostly with individual adults in one-on-one psychotherapy. I also offer bereavement groups for anyone grieving the loss of a loved one.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received my masters degree in Counseling Psychology from Pacifica Graduate Institute in Santa Barbara, CA. in 2005. I started seeing clients in 2004, and I have been licensed since 2008. I have training and experience in EMDR, grief and loss, dreamwork, sandtray work, and expressive arts. By far, the best training I have had was my own 5-year course of personal psychotherapy with Andrew Feldmar in Vancouver, BC.

name: Bethany Haug (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is relational and experiential. Being relational means that I'm not a "blank slate" type of therapist, keeping myself at a distance for the sake of remaining objective. I bring my own experience of our process into the session so that we can join in working together and stand on equal footing. Being experiential means that I work from the assumption that if we're only talking about issues, we aren't really engaging the full mind. In contrast, I invite present-moment awareness of what you are experiencing in your body, mind, and in our session to encourage new experience and learning. This involves bringing compassion and curiosity to protective patterns as they emerge in our work. We'll integrate your experience of old and new ways of being, so that new meaning, choice, and flexibility can emerge. My clients often describe this way of working as surprising, rewarding, and quite different from previous therapy experience!
My Values as a Therapist
I am a queer therapist and my work is guided by the belief that our intersecting identities play a significant role in our lives and the therapy process. I believe that you deserve to have a therapeutic relationship where you can bring your whole self, and I look forward to welcoming all parts of your life experience. I embrace nonviolence as a guiding value and strive to provide care that aligns with greater freedom and harmony, within yourself and within the world.

name: G. Ravyn (Ravyn) Stanfield (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I believe we need empathy, acceptance, support, and accountability in order to heal our old pain and create positive changes. I know a lot of tricks and tools that can help change stories, habits, perspectives, patterns, relationships, and eventually, lives. Often, we come to therapy to change a behavior that was originally developed as a survival strategy. As we learn and grow, we evolve past our own strategic defenses. Too bad our habits and patterns don’t always get the memo! We can learn to shift stubborn patterns of protection, in order to find joy and connection. Sometimes, we make change one breath at a time. Other times, there are body based exercises we can use to release stress and tension. And still other times, we have to challenge systems and narratives that don’t support our values. Clients generally experience improvement in the first month, and find growth in goal areas throughout the course of therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that all people are worthy of dignity and respect, no matter what their circumstances. In the past, I have worked as a social worker, an advocate for accessibility, an environmental activist, a theatre artist, and a writer. I currently work as a therapist, an acupuncturist and a Trauma Informed Care trainer. I play multiple hand drums, including djembe, tar, doumbek, and am learning to play the Irish bodhran. Much of my stress relief comes from hanging out with the natural world, including playing with my dogs, and hikes in the PNW. I'm a world traveler, and have been to Nepal, Israel, Palestine, Bali, Germany, Iceland, Scotland, Greece, Australia, and Italy, to name a few. I have family in Eastern Europe and plan to travel to Poland and Russia at some point.

name: Allison Riegel (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I create a safe therapeutic container which is nourishing, welcoming, and of the moment. I invite curiosity, play, and spaciousness, so that clients can engage in their own inner processes of self-understanding. People have described my therapeutic style as: nurturing, heart-centered, intuitive, curious, and graceful. I bring a grounded, sturdy, non-judgmental presence into therapy and strive to meet my clients just where they are at: in celebration of their unique ways of being. I come to this work not only with a sensitivity to the way clients describe their experiences—the words, culture, and body language they use in expressing their personal narratives—but also an acute awareness of the energy present in the therapeutic space. The more subtle fluctuation of connection and realization as it unfolds. We'll dig into old patterning, honor unmet needs, identify your strengths and desires, and support you in living a more connective, creative, and expansive personal truth!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in the mystery of the universe, and I witness and honor this divine energy as we engage in the work of transformation in psychotherapy. Dreams, Synchronicities, and our Relationship to Spirit are powerful allies as we build awareness and traverse the landscapes of our lives. Paying attention to our inner worlds opens doors into new realms of selfhood and individual flourishing. Engaging with the unconscious through dreamwork, the body, expressive arts, we uncover significant messages from the deep that have unparalleled potential power in guiding us in our growth and toward personal alignment. I'm also aware of the ongoing oppression that exists in our culture via colonialism, capitalism, patriarchy, and institutional racism which most deeply effects BIPOC and LGBTQ+ communities. As a cisgender, white woman, I recognize the horrendous effects of white supremacy and consciously work to unwind the ways in which it shows up in my life.

name: Emily Wagner (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I'm known to give unconditional empathy and warmth, while providing direct and honest feedback. I view you as a whole person, and I care for all the parts that make you who you are. Building a close and trusted relationship is very important to me. I help you understand and regulate your emotions while using a trauma-informed therapy structure safe for your brain. I help you find solutions based on what you're naturally good at. Sometimes, we do go into the past to understand the now. We may question your thoughts and beliefs about yourself, and other times we will look to the body as a source of wisdom. These techniques are also called: Emotionally-focused (EFT); Trauma Informed; Strength-based; Psychodynamic; Cognitive-behavioral (CBT); Somatic; Internal Family Systems (IFS); Ketamine-Assisted Psychotherapy (KAP)

name: Greta Reitinger (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
Following in the footsteps of wise analysts and therapists who have illuminated the field, I subscribe to the notion that a new psychotherapy must be created for each new person. To this end I am trained in a variety of therapeutic methods, including existential talk therapy, somatic psychotherapy (Hakomi), EMDR, Lifespan Integration, Relational Psychodynamic Therapy, and IFS-informed parts work.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe therapy should be nothing short of life-changing. If your psychotherapy doesn't challenge and enliven (and sometimes maybe even upset) you, and if you don't feel it's making an important difference in your life, get the hell out of there, and find another therapist! Sometimes this has nothing to do with the competence or training of the psychotherapist, but more to do with the strange alchemy that occurs between any two human beings. My philosophy is imbued with the egalitarian principles of racial and economic justice, cultural humility and nonviolence. It acknowledges the paradoxes inherent in life: How do we go all-in and commit to this life in the face of its unpredictability and our own inevitable death? I welcome adult clients from all walks of life to meet with me and see if we make a good team for your psychotherapy work.

name: Sae Gally (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My approach is client-centered, trauma-informed, and relational. I work with you to establish a sense of safety so that you can explore your edges and grow into the parts of yourself that you are ready to embody. We will help cultivate the wisest parts of yourself, uncovering a foundation that will support you in your daily life and work outside of our sessions. We will work towards body-centered sessions, allowing the non-verbal awareness of your body to guide your growth and integration. All of my work is rooted in an awareness of systemic oppression. I recognize that many of our struggles and coping mechanisms are healthy adaptations to an unjust world. I believe that our individual healing is a part of the work of our collective liberation. While we work together with a focus on your well-being, we support the whole community's well-being, in turn.

name: Erin Needham (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I specialize in complex developmental trauma, attachment wounds, anxiety, and depression. I assist you in exploring and transforming your internal reality to give birth to a freer and more authentic version of yourself. I listen for the undercurrents at the root of your suffering to elicit change at a core level. My approach is one of deep attunement. I specialize in weaving Ketamine Assisted Psychotherapy with Depth Psychotherapy, Transpersonal Therapy, and Dynamic Trauma Modalities. Theories and modalities that inform my work include Relational Psychotherapy, Psychodynamic Therapy, Mindfulness-Based Therapy, EMDR, Attachment Theory, Emotional Freedom Techniques, Neurobiology, Existential Therapy. I often utilize parts work and am trained in Internal Family Systems (IFS). The parts work model that I utilize is the Developmental Needs Meeting Strategy (DNMS). I find that if offers internalization of Self energy much more quickly than IFS.
My Approach to Helping
The seeds of transformation are often found in the places that feel most difficult and overwhelming. These symptoms are not just disturbances; they are messages from the psyche, guiding you toward greater wholeness. By listening to these messages, we can uncover the wisdom and potential for healing within you. There is gold hiding in the shadowy aspects of ourselves. Our fears, shame, anger, and grief can become the fuel for expanding consciousness. By facing these internal barriers with compassion, we can untangle life energy from stuck places, allowing our true capacities to unfold. You are not defined by your thoughts, emotions, body sensations, or experiences. You are the awareness that observes these changing phenomena. As we cultivate a more spacious sense of awareness, it becomes easier to understand that we are not separate from the rest of existence. By releasing outdated identifications and limiting beliefs, we can connect more deeply with our inherent wholeness.

name: Zoë Schwartz (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Using a trauma informed lens to helps my clients explore the familial, relational, and systemic factors that have shaped their experiences. By teaching mindfulness techniques and pulling from different modalities such as, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy and Dialectical Behavioral Therapy, I aim to help you develop awareness of the interactions between your thoughts, feelings, and behaviors and a greater sense of agency in your personal and emotional life. It is an honor to have the opportunity to collaborate with you on your path to healing and pursuing what living a truly fulfilling life means for you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that change is possible and that growth is an inevitable and often painful part of life. Together we will develop the insight and tools that can improve the quality of your relationships and allow you live in a more authentic and intentional way. People are the experts of their own experiences and I see it as a fundamental part of my role to remain non-judgmental in our work together. Using a strength-based therapeutic style centered around your knowledge and experience allows us to find strategies that will best support you in managing challenges such as negative self-image, anxiety, and depression. I value the fundamental importance that trust, humor, and transparency play in this process. My past experience includes working with children, adolescents, and families, survivors of intimate partner violence, those experiencing homelessness, and students with disabilities. I am passionate about social justice and motivated by the vision of liberation for all oppressed peoples.

name: Cherrie Mae Padua (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe finding the right therapist is a key part of having a successful and positive therapeutic experience. As a therapist, my approach is warm, honest, and encouraging. I utilize various evidence-based techniques but often draw from Narrative Therapy, CBT, and mindfulness techniques. I tailor my approach to meet your specific needs and work collaboratively to overcome roadblocks in order to move towards growth.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe everyone has the ability to reach their full potential. Through therapy we can gain a better understanding of our lived experiences, discover new parts of ourselves, and gain skills to promote conscious change to reach our goals. We all struggle, but we don’t have to do it alone.

name: Brad Poser, MS, CRC
approaches:
We all get bogged down with what we ‘should do’ or worry about how we are ‘supposed to’ feel. Through no fault of our own, these thoughts can become overwhelming. We lose track of what makes us who we are, what gives us our sense of self worth. When these thoughts won’t go away, sometimes everyone needs a helping hand. Other times, we just need a break from the world and a safe place. Emotions aren't something we can easily just think our way out of. They are real. Just like any pain, they can require real attention. There is no therapeutic formula that 'fixes people.' I believe in meeting people where they are, in listening to their stories and finding their strengths.
I hold a Masters of Science in counseling and was an RSA Scholar. I am a member of the American Counseling Association, a certified rehabilitation counselor (CRC) and an LPC intern with the Oregon board of licensed therapists. I have years of experience working with the Poly and LGBTQ community. I was also a ADA and social services support professional for ten years, both in Portland and elsewhere. I have advanced training or certification is Depression/anxiety, Mindfulness, Motivational Interviewing, Trauma recovery, PTSD, Autism, TBI, Disability rights and case management.

name: Deanna Allmaras (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
We’re all doing the best we can with the relationship tools we currently have. Some of those tools may not be helpful; our parents weren’t good role models, or our perceptions and judgments are flawed, or our beliefs and points of reference don’t serve our interests. This can lead to feeling overwhelmed and discouraged. What you want is relief from suffering, and strength to overcome challenges to gain a better understanding of you and your partner. I am here to provide a safe environment for you to say the hard things and hear your needs. People grow, change, and heal, within the context of relationships. I am dedicated to providing you exceptional care by implementing effective evidence-based treatment that helps you accomplish your goals and live a happier, healthier life. I use an integrative approach that melds Gottman Method, Emotion Focused Therapy, and CBT.

name: Donald Marks (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
I am currently accepting new clients through tele-therapy. During these uncertain times many of us are struggling in ways that we might not have in the past, or with things that were going well and are now struggling with. I am here to support you during these challenging times and have immediate availability. I come from a Humanistic and strengths-based approach in session, and believe that collaboration and trust are the foundations in a therapeutic relationship. I also pull from Cognitive Behavior Therapy (CBT), Dialectal Behavior Therapy (DBT), as well as Radically Open DBT (RO-DBT), so that collaboratively we can identify specific skills and techniques that work for you. I work with adolescents and adults offering support and expertise with treatment of eating disorders, anxiety, compulsive exercise, depression, self-esteem/self-worth, trauma, life transitions, and personal growth. I also offer parent coaching and meal time support for clients and their families.
I hold a Master's Degree in Professional Mental Health Counseling, from Lewis and Clark College. I had the privilege of working at Monte Nido's residential eating disorder facility for adolescent females, Clementine Portland. There I spent over 3 years as an individual, family, and group therapist supporting teens and their families as they moved through the recovery process. I was able to work with a wide variety of diagnosis and eating disorder symptoms and develop proficiency and knowledge in the assessment and treatment of eating disorders. I also gained experience and knowledge in skills building and parent coaching. I frequently attend professional trainings stay up to date on best practices and enhance the services I am able to offer to my clients. I am passionate about creating a safe place for all people regardless of their gender identity, sexual orientation, ethnicity, race, age, size, religion or abilities.

name: Selin Strait (THEY/THEM, SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Life can be hard in so many ways. We live in oppressive systems giving or denying power, access and affirmation based on racial, gendered, class, sexual, ablist constructions of humanity. Our experiences, generational, familial, interpersonal, societal, cultural impact our everyday reality as well as teach us about who we are. They are held in our brains and bodies in unconscious and subconscious patterns throughout multiple systems. When we mindfully bring our attention and acceptance to our experience in the moment, these patterns can open and reveal themselves and allow for awareness, connection to self and truth and support greater choice.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a 52 year old, white, queer, gender fluid human with invisible disabilities. I understand webs of trauma result from colonialism and settler colonialism, capitalism, cis-hetero patriarchy, from generations of stolen land and chattel slavery; i know that ongoing genocide and violent anti-Blackness constantly retraumatize and reharm ...as a white therapist, I recognize that I embody white violence and can/may/do perpetuate it. I am practiced at identifying, uncovering and confronting white supremacy in myself and the world around me. I work to understand and see clients and their unique experiences within context, including the generational violence of settler colonialism, capitalism, cis-heteropatriarchy and white supremacy and value their knowledge of their experience, acts of resistance, survival and resilience.

name: Sarah Block (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Therapy looks different for everyone. Everyone learns and grows in their own unique ways. My approach with each person takes into account what works best for them. What you can count on is that I’ll take a holistic approach to healing and emotional growth through body-centered modalities, trauma-informed practices, and culturally sensitive interventions that help empower you to set your own goals and work towards the life you want to live. I'll help you identify and explore your own positive beliefs and values that support you in finding more joy and meaning in your daily life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My pronouns are she/her. I live with my family in the Pacific Northwest, just outside of Portland, Oregon where I love getting outside as much as possible. Science, nature, and art have always been my favorite subjects and I’m always ready to dive in and explore life’s mysteries with curiosity. I can be serious but I can also be silly. I can be scientific but I can also be woo-woo. At the end of the day, I find it important to show up as my authentic self with my clients, and find the humor together where we can.

name: Kacee Markarian (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
With over 10 years of experience and a Master's degree from a world-renowned Institute in San Francisco that blends Western and Eastern perspectives (CIIS), I incorporate traditional and alternative views and methods to facilitate your personal exploration and discovery of new tools and strengths. My clinical orientation is psychodynamic, existential-humanistic and trauma-informed, while also incorporating good old-fashioned humor and creativity. We're all sparkling gems in our own ways. I will help you sand down your rough edges by patiently attending to your pain, add to your shine through loving celebration of your unique gifts, and grow in appreciation and acceptance of your dark "flaws." I also use art therapy, psychosynthesis and Gestalt perspectives and tools.

name: Mary Kevin Holdeman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
With compassion and respect, I create a non-judgmental space for you to improve your relationship with yourself and others. I work beside you as you discover the strengths you already have, helping you to dig deep and allow your authentic self to emerge where you are in your healing journey. I help you focus as you navigate new beginnings, finding what has been holding you back and getting in your way. I'm with you as you create a blueprint to get you there. After spending over a decade working at a world renowned substance use treatment center, I feel confident in helping you address addiction and the challenges that go along with it, such as grief, unresolved trauma, anxiety, and feelings of being overwhelmed by day to day life. I help families of loved ones struggling with substance use, and the related issues challenging each member.
My Values as a Therapist
In addition to my training to become a licensed professional counselor in the state of Oregon, I am passionate about the issues of homelessness and social justice in our local area. My ongoing trainings have focused on ethics, multicultural issues, mindfulness, family dynamics, trauma, and compulsive behaviors. Continuing education and life experience inform the way I visualize human struggles and the way I approach care.

name: Lorraine Storm
approaches:
In times of distress, it is natural to seek a guide, teacher, or healer, and a therapist can be all of these things. More than just someone to listen to what you’re going through, a counselor provides a sounding board and opportunity for reflection, feedback and increased self awareness. I look at the individual as both a unique entity, and as part in a number of a number of larger systems. I integrate various therapy techniques and incorporate a global perspective- integrating health and lifestyle changes that can improve your mental health. Regardless of the modality, the most important factor predicting success in counseling or coaching is our personal connection – it doesn’t matter how good someone’s advice is, it’s not going to help you unless the person who’s giving it is someone you respect and trust.
I hold a Master's degree in Marriage and Family Therapy from Portland State University. My undergraduate degrees are in Psychology and Studio Art from the University of Redlands. I am also a Certified Integrative Health Coach. I have worked with children, adolescents, and families providing in-home and community-based therapy and crisis response. I have also worked in school-based settings providing individual and family therapy to students from a variety of backgrounds. I have extensive training in child development and trauma-informed therapy, as well as Strategic Family Therapy and Family Systems counseling. I am passionate about helping couples strengthen communication and deepen connection.

name: Lacy Lowry (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use creative methods to explore emotions and thought patterns that present in your life. Though verbal inquiry and art-making, together we use non-judgmental curiosity to better understanding your story and your needs. My work is trauma-informed and mindfulness-based. I believe that mental health is intimately tied to relationships, and that therapy can provide a safe setting to practice new ways of engaging with others and oneself. No art experience is necessary, but an openness to trying new things is always helpful.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that the relationship we develop with ourselves is the most important one but can not happen in a vacuum. Therapy is an important step toward living with intention and improving our connection with ourselves. I have personally experienced how art-making and mindfulness practices can facilitate this process. I believe every human is innately creative and curious, but sometimes negative messages, stress, and trauma block that natural flow.  Our work together can help unblock it. Besides my training and experience as an art therapist, I have extensive training in Interpersonal Neurobiology.  This framework guides my approach, utilizing attachment science, neuroscience, and poly-vagal theory to understand the body-mind-relationship connection.  I have practiced yoga for over 20 years and maintain a personal art practice to stay connected to my own body and mind. I also love traveling and playing outdoors with my two teens and my dog Sunny.

name: Sara Carmona (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a compassionate, collaborative and dynamic therapist specializing in the treatment of pregnancy and postpartum mood and anxiety disorders, fertility concerns, miscarriage, abortion, difficult birth experiences, and perinatal loss. My role is to create a safe and nurturing environment so you may explore difficult issues, strengthen your inner resources and build a repertoire of skills to use moving forward.
