I use conversation, practical strategies, and mind/body interventions to help you understand your emotions, make decisions that fit your values and help you live a more satisfying life.
Why We are a Good Fit for You
I am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker in the state of Oregon and hold a Masters Degree in Social Work from Portland State University. As a Certified Perinatal Mental Health Therapist, I pursue advanced training in the diagnosis and treatment of reproductive mental health concerns. My professional affiliations include the National Association of Social Workers, Postpartum Support International and an allied member of the Perinatal Mental Health Alliance for People of Color.

name: Bri Dittlinger (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My theoretical training and interests include Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Family Systems Theory, Narrative Therapy, Mindfulness Based Interventions, Polyvagal Theory, and Trauma Informed Interventions. I believe in a holistic, systems based approach to healing, and offer telehealth only services on Tuesdays and Fridays.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a social worker, advocate, therapist, and trauma survivor. I have been in the field for a decade, and my favorite part of this work is witnessing the strength and resilience of the people that I get to work with! I believe in an open and honest approach to therapy, using storytelling, psychoeducation, analogies, somatic healing, and plenty of real talk. This process can be heavy and difficult, yet also rewarding and powerful. It takes a strong person to confront their trauma narratives and embrace their inner alpha, congratulations on starting your journey to conquer that challenge!

name: Sarah King (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My clinical approach is based in attachment theory, meaning I work within the relationship to help clients feel safe enough to grow in the uncomfortable. I also utilize cognitive-behavioral, dialectical behavioral, and schema therapies as a way for my clients to practice new skills to make effective, forward-moving changes. Each individual's therapeutic process is unique and personal, as we are dynamic and complicated beings. Therefore, it is important to have someone you trust walk with you as you take your next steps.
Why I Became a Therapist
I believe that we all deserve compassion and understanding for the pieces of us that make us fallible and human yet so often we hold these secrets in isolation, out of fear of exposing our rawness to the world. Therefore, I found myself specializing working with clients who hold the highest level of shame and secrecy in hopes to demonstrate that everyone is deserving of an nonjudgmental and safe place to feel at ease in sharing their realness in order to uncover internal motivations for making positive change. I welcome all people, regardless of race, sex or gender. I hope that you find my casual, structured and compassionate approach to be a good place to start your healing journey.

name: Jen Masotja-Wright (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Jen takes an evidence-based, person-centered, and strengths-based approach when working with clients with theoretical frameworks such as Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Narrative Therapy, Attachment Theory, Family Systems Theory and EMDR. When preferred by clients, Jen utilizes grounding techniques and breathing exercises to prepare clients for sessions ensuring there is a healing, open and safe space for sharing and growth. Jen is a parent to two incredible children who are biracial/bicultural (African and North American) and although she can never know what it means to walk through the world as a person of color, she does have experience advocating for, providing support to and being ally for communities of color.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
With a strong focus and commitment to a work/life balance, Jen loves spending time enjoying all the beauty and joys of the Pacific NW with her wife, kiddos and 2 dogs. She embraces time in the kitchen exploring the tastes of many cuisines and listening to music from her favorite artists.

name: Kimberly Reilly (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I value a collaborative approach to therapy, that allows for clients' strengths and wisdom to unfold, and be heard. We'll start with what's current in your life, and find ways you can improve your sense of control around your thoughts, emotions, and body. I draw upon many different evidenced-based therapies such as Internal Family Systems (IFS), CBT, DBT, Cognitive Processing Therapy (CPT), and Mindful-based philosophies that address the root cause of your pain or struggles, vs. just treating the symptoms. I am solution and compassion focused, and approach treatment from a non-judgmental awareness of how we got to where are. I engage in, and appreciate ongoing professional development training regularly, in order to constantly improve my skills set, and support system.
Why I Became a Therapist
Becoming a Therapist is something I was meant to do from an early age. Because my own multi-generational family system was often challenging and painful, it gave me the opportunity to learn first hand, what many of my clients experience also. Without these early challenges, I could not be as complete, compassionate, self-aware, self-forgiving, self-loving, self-expansive, or as self-evolving as a therapist, or person, as my healing journey has allowed. I love what I do, and I value the opportunities that the difficult experiences in life, provide us. The human condition is not easy, no matter what the grass may look like on the other side. Growth, wisdom, and inner healing can only arise out of the ashes, and from these ashes come the seeds of rebirth, and life itself. The field of psychology and therapy have come such a long way, and it is a very exciting time to be a therapist. Never before has treatment been so cutting edge, effective, and had so much research to back it up.

name: Kimberly Noall (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
The world is changing quickly and we are definitely feeling the impact. Feelings of being stuck, anxious, frustrated and depressed can be difficult to navigate. Finding time to process your feelings and repairing ruptures in your life and relationships may feel more stressful now. I want to help. I am curious about your world, how you experience it, how you are connected to it, and your desire to make positive change. We will work together to develop and refine goals that meet your needs and values. We will collaborate to identify and connect to your strengths and support systems, and give you tools to cope and change barriers in achieving peace of mind. My experience includes working with adults struggling with anxiety, negative communication patterns, relational and intimacy issues, domestic violence, divorce and parenting issues. I draw from a variety of evidence-based therapeutic models. My desire is to help you process the anxiety and depression that accompanies feeling stuck and
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I earned my Master’s degree in Couples, Marriage, and Family Counseling from Portland State University. I employ my training in Emotion Focused Therapy for Couples, Family Systems, and the Gottman Method in my approach to the counseling process, as well as other person-centered and existential therapeutic interventions. In addition to couples work, I have a background in working with individuals and families adjusting to divorce, trauma, and co-­‐parenting issues. I also have ten years experience of crisis counseling with victims of domestic violence and four years of supervising counseling interns in a learning clinic.

name: Katie Kotsovos (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is warm, collaborative and transparent. I’m happy to share why we do what we’re doing in therapy and explore what I can offer from my skillset that resonates with you. I draw from a variety of therapeutic approaches to help folks embrace the present moment, connect to what’s going on in their bodies and work with their emotions. I also help clients explore the impact of trauma (big or small) on their current functioning and work towards feeling more regulated and self-compassionate. I’m trained in Eye-Movement Desensitization Reprocessing (EMDR), which can help alter one’s relationship to negative beliefs and distressful memories. I’m also a Health at Every Size (HAES)-aligned therapist. HAES is a weight-neutral approach to care that recognizes the harm of the thin-centric model.
My Values as a Therapist
My utmost goal is to create a safe space for your healing. If you've been harmed by the dominant culture we live in or by your family of origin (even inadvertently), I believe your growth requires a reparative experience of feeling that your emotions are valid and who you are matters. I strive to do that always by meeting you with empathy and respect. While I am not a member of the LQBTQIA2S+ or BIPOC communities, I am dedicated to cultural competency education and understanding the dynamics of privilege and oppression in all their forms and the impact that has on one’s mental health.

name: Elaine Kovacs (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
With over 12 years of professional experience in a variety of outpatient, crisis care, and hospital settings, I am experienced in navigating the challenges of the various healthcare systems. My hope is to provide a safe and welcoming space amid a difficult period in life, while providing concrete tools and positive, supportive feedback. While I utilize a variety of modalities depending on your needs, I am particularly experienced in the following: Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Narrative Therapy, Existential Therapy, Mindful Self-Compassion, and Solution Focused Brief Therapy (SFBT). I also am deepening my skills in trauma-informed approaches such as Internal Family Systems (IFS) and Written Exposure Therapy. I graduated from the Master's in Social Work program at the University of Texas at Austin in 2013, and before that I obtained a BA from the University of California, Berkeley in 2005.
Why I Became a Therapist
While I did not initially study to become a therapist, I found my way to this profession in my 30s after personal loss and grief. It has been my mission since entering the social work field, to provide support and empathy to caregivers, grievers, and those feeling lost. I have a special interest in the needs of adults and older adults as they are navigating grief, aging, and a shift in identity. But I am also open to exploring how other major life transitions--from a death, a break-up, a career change, an illness, or traumatic event--can also shake the very foundations of one's sense of values and purpose. My goal is to provide a space where I walk alongside you, honor your inherent worth and value, and share in the despair, anger, and anxieties of life's struggle. If I have provided a space where you can feel 'seen,' I feel joy in making this my profession.

name: Rhen Miles (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My core approaches currently include increasing awareness of parts of self (Internal Family Systems), understanding how our current experiences of ourselves and others have been influenced by relationships throughout life (attachment theory), increasing emotional regulation and grounding skills through an understanding of nervous system states and somatic-emotional-cognitive connections (polyvagal theory). I infuse a trauma-informed and anti-oppressive lens throughout these frameworks. My approach is influenced by the following theories and frameworks: Attachment theory Critical Race and Intersectional Theories Internal Family Systems Liberation Health Model Non-violent communication Polyvagal Sensorimotor Psychotherapy Safe and Sound Protocol Trauma-sensitive and responsive
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
There is deep value in the healing capacity of relationships. This requires the growth of a sense of safety through being seen, heard, and valued for all parts of self that one brings into therapeutic settings. There is not one avenue of healing and I aim to facilitate a collaborative space where your internal expertise can be nourished. To foster a collaborative dialogue, transparency around identity, privilege, power, and oppression can be integrated into discussions. I believe it's important to situation our individual experiences withing broader systems of power.

name: Jessie Weisstein (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have been working with people who have experienced trauma for a over a decade. I use techniques that have brought relief to people suffering from PTSD and other trauma related issues. The most important piece of how I work is establishing safety in the relationship and never pushing clients to talk about issues unless they are ready. I use a variety of techniques including learning about the way your body holds trauma and increasing curiosity and compassion towards the ways you have learned to protect yourself from pain. I often use a technique called EMDR to help my clients reprocess traumatic events and reduce negative thoughts, feelings and sensations.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe strongly in the power of relationships to both shape who we are to heal. I believe that all people have the capacity to shift their lives in ways that align with their values. I was drawn to this work because of the profound impact that therapy can have on individuals and communities. I also believe strongly that all people have a right to healing and I use anti-oppressive practices in my work with clients. I am allied with the LGBTQ community and love supporting folks who are exploring their gender identity

name: Liv Siulagi (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I base my practice on Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, which means that during our sessions we will examine how your thoughts, emotions, and behaviors impact you. I ground this more "micro" with Liberation Psychology, basing my work on the belief that humans naturally strive for health and connection, but that we live in an oppressive society that limits our ability to heal from trauma, build lasting communities, and forge full, meaningful lives. Through compassion, open-mindedness, humility, accountability, authenticity, and my critical background in intersectional feminist theory I work hard to treat my therapeutic participants as equals: helping them develop insight, heal, and tune into their natural creativity.
My Values as a Therapist
As a mixed race (Samoan, filipina, and white), cis-woman raised in the primarily white city of Portland my lived experience of oppression and the privileged aspects of my identity have inspired me to examine my own role as an ally and advocate. In my final year at Lewis & Clark this interest led to a year-long research project focused on improving white allyship and avoiding performative activism in the field of art therapy. This work paired with my art advocacy, volunteer work with marginalized populations, and undergraduate research experience on sexuality has helped me dedicate myself to a reflective practice based in antiracism, liberation, and cultural humility.

name: Taylor Kravitz (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I’ll help you your partner understand why you’re not aligning sexually, learn how to communicate about sex in a more connecting way, and incorporate exercises that will help you access more pleasure together. If you're here to feel more at ease with your own sexuality, I'll help you better understand what barriers are getting in the way of sexual empowerment and pleasure & will give you tangible tools to build a more fulfilling relationship with your sexuality. If you're here to get support with your relationship, I'll help you and your partner navigate whatever challenges are coming up in your relationship so you can feel more connected, understood by one another, and happier together.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I know finding a therapist who is a good fit can be a vulnerable and challenging process. You may have read a lot of these bios and just want to finally find the right person to support you. I’ve studied sexuality & relationships for over 9 years and I bring that professional expertise and my own experience with how hard it can be to be a human/partner/sexual being into the therapy room. I’m gentle and compassionate, but will still hold you accountable to the changes you want to make. I truly love doing this work and it’s such an honor to help. I’d love to hear from you and offer a free phone consultation to ensure we feel like a good fit.

name: Caitlin Reidy (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I decided that I wanted to become a therapist in response to my own navigation of relationships and sexuality. Sexuality has always fascinated me but I grew up in an environment where repression was the norm. I always held the belief that sex is a normal part of life, and began looking more critically at the messages that I had grown up with. As a result, one of my primary goals as a sex therapist is to deconstruct narratives of shame or guilt that may have been internalized, and develop a safe, fulfilling relationship with clients.
My Approach to Helping
I adopt an integrationist approach and practice therapy from a systemic, trauma-informed lens, applying frameworks such as EFT, IFS, and narrative therapy. I incorporate social justice and equity into my approach to therapy and believe that it is critical to creating a safe relationship free of judgment. I am excited to walk alongside you on your path.

name: Raquel Harmon (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I believe that we all move towards wholeness when we have the safety and understanding we need to show up fully for ourselves and in relationship. My style is warm, non-judgmental, present, and hopeful. I want to offer you a space where you can feel seen and un-alone as you navigate life’s complexities, explore more intimately the systems (relational, societal, cultural, political, environmental, religious, etc.) that impact you, interrupt cyclical patterns you may feel stuck in, and find new movement towards a deeper sense of connection with and compassion for yourself and the humans and non-humans in your life and community.
My Values as a Therapist
My approach is present and human; I believe that reparative relational experiences are an intrinsic part of how we heal past wounds and repair relationships. I draw from Somatic Mindfulness, Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT), and biophilic theories. My work is also influenced by interpersonal neurobiology and how trauma shows up in our body from day to day. I view care work as advocacy and strive to meet each person’s lived experience and wisdom with cultural humility and curiosity. I am continually prioritizing my own education and integration of anti-racist, environmental justice, and disability justice frameworks into my practice.

name: Ashley Wilkins (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I am here to provide support and accompany you on your path toward healing. Healing is about more than just surviving, it’s about finding better ways to cope, acceptance and growth; and it can take time and extra support to get there. My therapeutic style relies on authenticity, empathy, and trust to provide a supportive environment for growth and change. I approach therapy as a collaboration and offer a space that is trauma-informed and free of judgement, recognizing the value and uniqueness of your life experience. I use clinical approaches like EMDR, psychodynamic theory, attachment-based therapy, mindfulness, and cognitive-behavioral interventions. I work primarily with adults but have extensive experience working with children, adolescents, and families; and have provided therapy in a variety of settings including schools, hospitals, and community mental health centers. I currently work exclusively through telehealth for clients based in Oregon or Illinois.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I'm passionate about what I do because of my own personal experiences with grief and coping with mental health challenges. Through my work I’ve been witness to the incredible resiliency of those who’ve experienced trauma; and a person’s capacity for growth and change when facing challenges. I rely on a systems lens that focuses on treating the whole person within their environment; because the truth is, problems don't exist in a vacuum. We are interconnected, shaped by our experiences: how we grew up, how we are treated, what access or barriers we’ve experienced, and whether we’re able to get our needs met. I consider all of this in determining how to treat the issues you bring to therapy. You are the expert on your life, and you get to set the pace and depth to which we work. Together we’ll identify and leverage your own inherent strengths to help you thrive.

name: Jillian Brown (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a depth psychologist and infinitely fascinated by the subconscious mind and the vast wisdom and potential we possess, but don't often access. I tend to use modalities that bring forward subconscious material and support creating new neuropathways. Our brains and bodies are in full support of us being safe, happy and healthy in this world, we just need to find ways to work in collaboration with ourselves rather than living in conflict with different parts of ourselves.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a Masters in Psychology with a Counseling focus from Saybrook University. I have been working as a Hypnotherapist since 2011. I have a certificate in Process Oriented Psychology from the Process Work Institute. My favorite modality lately is Family Constellations. It is a powerful tool for creating big changes and also healing ancestral trauma. I have studied shamanic journeying, energy work, NVC, pre and perinatal psychology, and attachment theory is one of my main lenses.

name: Kelley O'Gorman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I will always start with your strengths so you have a foundation to build from. I use a Narrative approach, then look at Family Dynamics and then Evidence Based Skills that we tailor to you. I am all about building your confidence and resiliency in coping with your life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I was a business gal once, I worked in advertising & at Nike building Niketowns. After the birth of my first child I became more interested in the ever changing brain. So I went back to graduate school. My focus was Marriage and Family Therapy as well as Addictions. Over the years it has become relationship therapy with specialties in: Depression, Anxiety, Relationships, Self Confidence, Skills & Goals.

name: Amelia Righi (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I use an integrative, psychodynamic approach incorporating Narrative Therapy, Narrative Exposure Therapy, EMDR (Eye Movement, Desensitization, and Reprocessing Therapy), and attachment-based perspectives. I’ve always been drawn to people and questions of justice, healing, and how to make sense of ourselves, our experiences, and our place in the world.
My Values as a Therapist
I am anti-racist and equity focused in my work. This means I am sex, kink, body, and fat-positive, incorporate the tenets of disability justice into my practice, and aim to provide polyamory / non-monogamy and neurodivergent affirming care. Our lives are political, and our mental health and well-being is always tied to the systems and structures we live within.

name: Shelley Sauer (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer a variety of therapeutic techniques, and collaborate with you about what feels most aligned with your goals. My intent is to meet you where you are at, and to reach an understand where the problems, or blocks, really are, and to work towards getting them unstuck. My overall goal is to help you to move towards being your genuine self.

name: Elizabeth Ellisan (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
It is my goal to provide a safe, compassionate, and open space for you to navigate through life changes, identity changes, anxiety, panic, and past trauma. I can help with finding your voice and identity, coping with the impending sense of doom that comes with life changes, and identifying skills to move through anxiety and panic. It takes courage to begin therapy, even at the best of times. When you find the space, I hope you reach out and we can work together to find and maintain your personal sense of peace.
My Approach to Helping
I work from a mindful, humanistic foundation. This means that you can expect my nonjudgmental attention to the present moment and a deep respect for your personal experience. I believe that you are the expert on your own life, and I believe you have the power to change the things that are no longer serving you, even when that change feels out of reach. I often use mindfulness techniques to help you remain in the present moment so that you can get the most out of your session. I incorporate creative interventions into therapy including pens, pencils, paintbrushes, games, and movement.

name: Ronald L Johnson
approaches:
Clients engage in several traditional counseling approaches to reconnect with a deeper sense of self, including talk therapy, mindfulness meditation on the breath and inner emotions, and scanning and awakening of the energy field of the inner body. These create healing and meaning in one's life and promote a positive outlook. Gradually, less constrictive thought processes replace these conditioned reactions and released energies are poured into new areas of growth. Clients may receive support for physical regimens at local gyms or yoga studios, as well as reassessment of educational goals, occupational, and relational potentials to provide for holistic, long term health.
I received a bachelor's degree in psychology and religious studies from University of California, Santa Cruz, where I really enjoyed studying. I started learning yoga and martial arts there, and was introduced to Zen master Kobun Chino Sensei. I studied meditation with him at Zendo Santa Cruz and practiced there independently. Back home in Oregon, I trained in alcohol counseling at OHSU and earned a master's in psychology and creative writing at UO Eugene. More recently I obtained additional educational requirements for my LPC studying mindfulness through Walden University online. I have published papers on poetics of mindful awareness and the various counseling modalities related to consciousness development and personal growth. Besides counseling I teach yoga classes around Portland that include harmonium and chant practice.

name: Amanda Ichihashi Jagerman (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Background
Amanda Ichihashi Jagerman (she/her) is servant & helper of humanity at this particular time. She is a licensed clinical social worker (LCSW) in the state of Oregon & an honors graduate of San Francisco State University & Portland State University. She is a San Francisco Bay Area native, mixed race Asian/Jewish person, daughter of an immigrant, LGTBQIA ally, & visual artist. With 15+ years experience in non-profits working with underserved communities, she is also highly intuitive, empathic, & holds strong interests in mysticism & healing of all kinds. She has additional certified training in EMDR & is a non-denominational Reverend with an honorary Doctorate of Divinity degree from the Universal Life Church. As a therapist who has been in a lot of therapy herself, Amanda understands the need for a safe space with a safe person as well as warmth, client-centeredness, & humor in the theraputic relationship. Alchemy Mental Wellness (AMW) was established in January 2021.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
For 20 years, I lived with misdiagnosed/undiagnosed PTSD. I have used my personal journey of recovery, & academic & professional experience to support 300+ clients in this sacred soul work. My guiding values are creativity, diversity, healing, social justice, & storytelling. I am aided in my purpose by my ancestors, particularly my Great-Grandmothers & Grandmothers. Alchemy Mental Wellness acknowledges that Portland, Oregon is build on occupied land of the Multnomah, Wasco, Cowlitz, Clackamas, Chinook, Tualatin, Kalapuya, Molalla, & many other tribes along the Columbia River. AMW is committed to anti-racism, anti-opporessive practice, and de-colonizing mental health to support women, POC, & Queer folks. By speaking & acting in ways such as (when appropriate) listening, educating, disrupting, sitting with discomfort, sharing, donating, providing mutual aid, volunteering & supporting businesses, this practice supports & stands in solidarity with the BIPOC community.

name: Miles Joyce (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background
I hold a master’s degree in Marriage, Couple and Family Therapy from Lewis & Clark College and a bachelor’s degree in psychology and behavioral neuroscience from the University of Portland. This path was set in motion in 2012 when I first began working in the unhoused population of East Los Angeles. This laid the groundwork for years spent working with similar populations in Portland via p:ear and New Avenues for Youth, overseeing culinary, music, and transitional housing programming. I additionally spent several years working in wilderness therapy as both a guide and manager at a program that specialized in leveraging the healing potential of the land to treat complex developmental trauma via an individualized approach. Together, these experiences have established an ethic of nuance in my practice, working to integrate the sometimes conflicting confluence of interpersonal, environmental, and political demands.
Why I Became a Therapist
My passion lies in what I’ve come to call “re-villaging efforts,” meaning that I see therapy as an effective catalyst for the culture-work necessary to heal the harm associated with being a human in increasingly troubled times. I have humbly apprenticed myself to the lineage of Bill Plotkin, Francis Weller, the School of Lost Borders, and others who have prioritized finding the therapeutic intersection of initiatory practices, contemporary rites of passage, and soul-centric practice. I have been trained in EMDR by the EMDR Institute founded by Dr. Francine Shapiro and utilize this approach to assist clients in reclaiming a sense of wholeness after the fracturing of traumatic experiences. I have special interests and additional training in men’s work, grief and loss, navigating transitions in identity, parenting, boundary issues or violations, sexuality, and religious trauma. Right now, I primarily work on telehealth and have limited availability for in-person walk and talk sessions.

name: Catherine Gobel (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I want therapy to be a safe space for you to feel vulnerable touching into and understanding your emotions and what they're trying to teach you in a way you haven't before. I like to use mindfulness and somatic tools to help you regulate your nervous system in order to make positive change. Once we build a safe, trusting relationship, hopefully we can make space for parts of you that you haven't had access to before to tell us what their needs are. A lot people have a very critical voice in their heads. My goal is to help us understand why that voice feels the need to criticize you and rewire your brain to be able to hold you accountable to achieve the goals you want to achieve while holding compassion for all the parts of you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am often told that I have a very non-judgmental grounding presence. I've been through some hard things in my own life and I'm proud of the ways I've learned to understand myself and navigate my own difficulties. I feel the experiences I've had provide me with a unique opportunity to understand deeply what you're going through and learn how we can address whatever you're wanting to work on.

name: Kombai Nonnarath (SHE/HER)
approaches:
Our Background and Approach
I’m deeply committed to working with multicultural communities and honoring the complexity that comes with layered identities. As a relational therapist, I draw from a range of modalities guided by intuition and what feels most supportive for you. I integrate holistic practices with evidence-based approaches like Internal Family Systems (IFS) and Brainspotting. In our work together, I will invite you to explore deeply, ask tough questions, and gently bring awareness to the patterns that may be holding you back. My goal is to walk alongside you as you reconnect with your inner wisdom and strength. I invite you to approach this journey with openness and curiosity, as we reflect on the experiences that have brought you here. Together, we’ll co-create a space where you are empowered to heal, grow, and thrive.
Why I Became a Therapist
Growing up in Oakland as a 1st generation immigrant has deeply informed my perspective on identity, resilience, and healing. With roots in Animism and Buddhism deeply woven into the cultural and spiritual practices of my upbringing, I am inspired to carry forward the healing legacies of my family lineage.

name: Beth Bogdon (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I deeply believe in our innate capacity for wholeness and the wisdom that emerges when we create space to listen deeply to ourselves. My approach is shaped by Jung's understanding that the psyche speaks through symbols and images, and that our ongoing development continues throughout life. At the heart of my practice is a reverence for the mystery of becoming. While our culture often pushes for quick fixes and logical explanations, I've found that real transformation emerges through patience and curiosity. Like the natural world, human development has its own timing and wisdom. I'm particularly drawn to working with the imagination as a bridge to the unconscious. Through my training in Embodied Imagination, I've witnessed how this gentle approach allows hidden aspects of ourselves to emerge and be integrated, leading to a deeper sense of wholeness. I see myself as a companion on the journey, helping to create conditions where healing and growth can occur.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I deeply believe in our innate capacity for wholeness and the wisdom that emerges when we create space to listen deeply to ourselves. My approach is shaped by Jung's understanding that the psyche speaks through symbols and images, and that our ongoing development continues throughout life. At the heart of my practice is a reverence for the mystery of becoming. While our culture often pushes for quick fixes and logical explanations, I've found that real transformation emerges through patience and curiosity. Like the natural world, human development has its own timing and wisdom. I'm particularly drawn to working with the imagination as a bridge to the unconscious. Through my training in Embodied Imagination, I've witnessed how this gentle approach allows hidden aspects of ourselves to emerge and be integrated, leading to a deeper sense of wholeness. I see myself as a companion on the journey, helping to create conditions where natural healing and growth can occur.

name: Anastasia Zankowsky (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that self awareness and connection can heal. My practice is a place where we can build safety and vulnerability, and work together to help you better understand yourself and your distress. I can help you find clarity, maintain healthy relationships, & live more intentionally, whether you are ready to dive into the deep end, or you are just treading water in your day-to-day. My practice has at its foundation an awareness of systemic oppression; I understand that we do not exist in a vacuum, and healing ourselves and coping with the challenges in our lives requires acknowledging and working against the systems of oppression that contribute to them. I work through a framework that is relational and strengths-based, and is LGBQA, trans, poly, and kink affirmative. Therapy is not always easy, but here you can regain a sense of control and create momentum. Let's connect to discuss what you need and how I can help you.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I am a white, queer, jewish, currently non-disabled therapist who is committed to anti-oppression work in and outside of the therapy room. I am a palliative care trained clinician (meaning I work with people who have serious illness, and/or their families, from diagnosis onward to cope with the trauma and existential crises related to illness), have worked in hospital systems and cancer centers, and have trained in couples and family therapy at the Ackerman Institute in New York City. I work through a framework that is relational and strengths-based, and is LGBQA, trans, poly, and kink affirmative. I help those are who are managing: Grief & loss Anxiety & Depression Trauma & PTSD LGBTQIA sexuality or gender identity (incl. trans, non-binary, & GNC identities) Poly, kink, or sex work dynamics Relationship, couple, & family conflict Healthcare burnout

name: Wendy Jensen (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a licensed therapist here to guide you in a process towards goals that you decide in therapy.  I use curiosity, self-disclosure (where helpful/relevant), and challenging questions to help you uncover the hidden baggage that is keeping you from being your best self.. The way in which we cope in the world is greatly influenced by how we see the world.  Dealing with anxiety, depression or grief can be difficult but I believe by using "baby steps" in a safe space, growth and healing happen quickly. My goal is to facilitate an awakening within you and deep healing of long-held problematic patterns and destructive core beliefs that are keeping you from living life to its fullest and finding joy and peace of mind in your daily life.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
As an experienced therapist, I work with people who have experienced trauma in their life,  no matter how big or small, whether it be in their family of origin, their relationships, their work place or their communities. My clients often feel overwhelmed and confused by their past experiences, and how those have impacted them emotionally, physically, socially and psychically.  Navigating through life's transitions is hard enough, but it can be downright daunting when we have "baggage" hanging on from our past.   Lets work together to explore those wounds, and old belief systems to heal from past traumas,  develop self-acceptance and understanding, gain more self-compassion and confidence and move toward a healthy, happier life. I use a variety of approaches including Energy Psychology/Body Centered Techniques for releasing trauma quickly and permanently, Shamanic healing methods including soul retrieval,  EMDR,  Emotionally Focused Therapy, Past Life Regression and Hypnotherapy.

name: Haley Bosco Doyle (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I love to move towards present-moment emotional experience when I’m with clients. That often looks like me inviting a client to slow down and notice sensations in their body. We can follow body tension to uncover what may be held underneath the surface and then build more self-compassion, understanding, and awareness. I will always be transparent with you about the ins and outs of the therapeutic process because I believe that collaboration and consent are vital to creating safety and trust in our time together. I bring genuine love and care into my relationship with my clients. Many clients I work with have had trouble receiving love because they’ve felt unworthy or unlovable; I view therapy as a potential cozy space for corrective, reparative experiences that can start to soften clients’ inner experiences. That’s the whole idea behind the name “Soft Spot”: melting away the hardness that our society or trauma forces unto us and, in place, deepening into more of the love.

name: Jdeanna Winn (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
If you need help with deep inner work and recovery from abuse, or just life transitions and stress, I am committed to creating the opportunity for you to have non-judgmental, trauma informed, affirming safe space to process whatever life has brought you.
More About the People We Treat
Are you confused about a relationship that has ended? Are you struggling to understand what has happened? I specialize in abuse recovery work, and have specialized training in abuse intervention. I can offer a unique understanding and support for abuse recovery work. Are you artistic? or do you want to integrate creating art into your healing journey? I have a background in Art, and can mesh your use of art and expressing yourself with your healing journey. I am a member of the LGBTQ community. I offer understanding and support for all.

name: Jules Joannini (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
As an L.M.F.T. I have been working with adults, adolescents and families over the past 13 years, in both private practice and community mental health settings. I utilize Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, Internal Family Systems, Dialectical Behavioral Therapy, as well as Mindfulness based practices in my work with clients.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I believe it is important for every person to have a relationship where they can challenge their beliefs and assumptions about themselves and the world. I value a relationship that encourages self exploration, insight and positive change. I work collaboratively with clients to provide a safe and supportive environment, one that allows reflection in order to develop new ways of handling life's challenges. My style is warm, nurturing and engaged, while at times honest and directive.

name: Jillyan Medina (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe problems occur in context and are influenced by our societal systems. I work together with my clients to replace old narratives, with a story that is more authentic and allows folks to engage with themselves more positively. I will always be transparent with my clients about the therapeutic process because I believe that collaboration and consent are vital to creating safety and trust. I bring genuine care into my relationship with my clients.

name: Bennett Gibney (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
It does not take much to see we live in a world built for white, hetero, cisgender people. Coming from a background in social work, my approach emphasizes the impact that marginalization and oppression can have on the mental health and well-being of individuals. Our culture, communities, and external worlds can be hinder our ability to manage stress and the emotional waves of life; and this is particularly true for those who feel “othered” or alone. As a queer nonbinary human, I’ve done my fair share of work to unpack my own internalized shame. I value the vulnerability that clients of all ages bring to the therapeutic space, whether that is exploring the pain of childhood wounds, examining relationships and significant events, or delving into the thoughts and feelings that are keeping folks stuck and from living the life they want.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a therapist, I am a firm believer that healing happens within relational settings. I will partner with you to better understand your unique experiences, needs, and emotions so you can create the meaningful change you seek.

name: Ellie Mental Health
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We’ve created a comfy, judgment-free zone where you can be authentic, get real about where you’re at in your mental health, and receive the compassionate care you deserve. We strive to break down treatment barriers and provide you with customized counseling services that meet your therapy needs. We offer: EMDR Individual therapy, Family therapy, and Group therapy Therapeutic Life Story Work DBT CBT Motivational Interviewing Person centered, strengths based, compassionate work with all of the individuals who walk in to our space!
Our Values as Mental Health Practitioners
We value equity, inclusion, and safety for all people.

name: Maren Corbly (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
~Grief Groups: Using evidence based curriculum developed with Judi's House in Denver, CO (similar to Dougy Center) ~Eye Movement Desensitization & Reprocessing (EMDR) for use with trauma, grief, phobias, depression, anxiety (learn more about EMDR in this 10-minute video at https://www.emdria.org/about-emdr-therapy/ or listen to my interview on this podcast: https://www.buzzsprout.com/1822210/episodes/12008329) ~Mindfulness and Meditation from global perspectives ~Pathfinders individual and/or group to process grief (Curriculum for all ages developed by Judi’s House in Denver, Colorado) ~Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) techniques to reducing stress, supporting improved impulse control, and finding inner stability ~Attachment focus including family of origin, friendships, and romantic relationships ~Communication skills such as Nonviolent Communication and Love & Logic ~Art therapy related techniques for expression and exploration of emotions and intuitive knowing
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Born and raised in Portland, after a decade spent on my own little adventure in Colorado and some international travel, it makes my heart sing to be back again amongst the trees, close to the ocean, and able to appreciate the great food we have to offer. As a person with my own death losses and chronic illness in my life, I draw from both my professional and personal experiences to inform my empathy with my clients. That being said, I know that each experience and each individual person is different and I have done my work so I know how to hold safe boundaries between our experiences. I live my life with care and intention so I can bring integrity, stability, and compassion to our work together.

name: Forrest Pandit-Kerr
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
The relationship between client and counselor has been shown to be the most powerful force of change in the counseling process. It is the foundation for all the we will do together, and I lay that foundation by being transparent, genuine, non-presumptuous, warm, and curious. My curiosity will help me come to understand the complexities of your experience and engage in dialogue without judgement. I recognize that the people I serve have widely varying values and I strive to engage in counseling work through your value system and worldview, not my own. While I have seen how foundational the therapeutic alliance is to creating meaningful change, I believe it is important to build on that foundation with counseling theories and techniques. I am flexible about which theories I use with each person, and I will regularly be checking in to see if the approach we are trying together is working for you. I recognize the power a diagnosis has and engage in collaborative diagnosing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Counseling is an inherently collaborative process and I believe that the client is the expert in their own lives, whether they are aware of it or not. My role is to help foster that self-awareness and provide a guiding voice in developing skills for applying that self-awareness in healthy ways. I recognize that many of the systems and tools developed for counseling are inherently biased and I make sure to adjust my approach accordingly for each person I serve. The winding pathways of life have given me a unique perspective on the world and the role I play in it. My time spent exploring nature and other cultures, making and listening to live music, and with my pets (Rhodesian Ridgebacks) is intricately intwined with who I am. Outside of my work I volunteer providing behavioral health crisis support at events and festivals. From years of working in high stress situations I have developed a strong, and often dry, sense of humor. I only bring that into session if or when appropriate.

name: Lucia Kilday (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I believe in the power of collaboration and empowerment in mental health treatment. I work closely with my patients to develop personalized treatment plans that integrate medication management, therapy, lifestyle modifications, and complementary therapies. My goal is to not only alleviate symptoms but also to promote long-term mental health and well-being. I am dedicated to staying current with the latest research and best practices in psychiatric care to provide my patients with the highest quality of care. I look forward to partnering with you on your journey to mental wellness.
My Approach to Helping
I am dedicated to staying current with the latest research and best practices in psychiatric care to provide my patients with the highest quality of care. I look forward to partnering with you on your journey to mental wellness.

name: Harini Sukumaran (SHE/ HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I strongly believe in a collaborative, holistic, strength based approach to therapy. I draw out an individual’s unique inner strength and foster optimum physical, emotional and mental health. I listen and respond to each person in a non -judgmental, compassionate way, so that each person feels safe, develops trust and be heard. With children, i routinely uses child centered play therapy, where a child’s play is used as a natural medium for expression of feelings, learn social skills, and learn effective problem solving skills to cope with their difficulties. With children i enhance the play therapy, by weaving in Body Awareness ( Yoga), Mindfulness Meditation , Art Therapy, Cognitive Behavioral therapy and PCIT. With Women & Families, I implement different psychotherapeutic treatment modalities including but not limited to Mindfulness based stress reduction, CBT, Attachment therapy, Internal Family systems, Acceptance Therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My unique qualification in Counseling, Yoga, Ayurveda and Energy healing medicine helps me to treat clients holistically, addressing the physical, emotional and spiritual needs. I have over 10+ years of experience treating women and children in both inpatient and outpatient settings; my specialization includes, treatment of children with behavioral/emotional struggles, women suffering from trauma, depression, anxiety and Pregnancy/Postpartum mood disorders.

name: Taffy Spencer (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Therapy can help you reconnect with hope, purpose, and your true self. Finding a therapist who can create a safe, non-judgmental, and constructive environment built on mutual trust and respect is critical to the success of the therapeutic process. We will work in collaboration to set attainable and meaningful goals for therapy, and I’ll tailor my practice to work with your style and needs. I am trained in Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), somatic therapy, and cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT), and I integrate these tools with elements of narrative and solution-focused therapy modalities. I also incorporate the science of neurobiology into my work with clients because many people find it helpful to better understand how the brain works.
More About the People I Treat
My point of view as a therapist is strengths-based, trauma-informed, and social justice-oriented. I warmly welcome clients from all walks of life and particularly enjoy working with people with diverse life experiences, including the LGBTQ+ community, people living with ADHD or autism, and people of color. My role is to be your helper, ally, and guide in your travels as you work through the past and move toward a healthier, brighter future.

name: Lizza Chenven (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am particularly interested in couples work and continue to train in IFIO, which is the couples version of Internal Family Systems (IFS). I believe that each person has the capacity to experience emotional safety and connection in their life and relationships. As a therapist, I work with you to establish a trusting relationship and a welcoming space for your self-exploration.

name: myra bazell
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Myra Bazell is a Licensed Clinical Social Worker and received her Masters in Social Work from the Bryn Mawr Graduate School of Social Work & Social Research. Myra comes with an extensive background in dance that has provided her with an understanding of how our histories live in our bodies and provide a great resource for understanding our lived experience. Myra is passionate about escorting clients on a journey of rediscovery; the revealing of one’s natural strength and deep-rooted source of power is for her a road to managing stress and embracing daily challenges.
My Approach to Helping
Myra employes a wealth of client centered therapeutic modalities including; Cognitive Behavioral Therapies ( CBT), Narrative Therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), Attachment-Based Therapy, Mindfulness, Breath work, Compassion-Focused Therapy (CFT), and when appropriate Trauma Informed Authentic Movement. Give yourself permission to do you. Overcome stress and enjoy your daily challenges. Explore early attachment and its impact on your current self-view. Improve your relationships. Rewrite self-script. Plan your next move while learning to be in the moment. Discover reasons to be holistic. Transform negative energy into creative ability. Align with your true self.

name: Catherine Caruso (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Our work will help you identify and focus on your strengths. It will help you bring attention to and interrupt the behaviors you wish to change. Part of my role in your journey towards wellness includes offering emotional regulation skills for the here and now to help with the longer journey. We will work together to reduce distress, allowing you to work on deeper/bigger issues should that be your intention. I offer DBT, CBT, and Interpersonal Neurobiology along with a myriad of modalities to best suit individual needs.
Social Justice Issues I Care About
I work firmly in harm reduction. This applies to all areas of my work, but in substance use care and mental health. We can reduce harm by adding safety, supports, and knowledge.

name: Lewis & Clark Community Counseling Center
approaches:
Our Approach to Helping
The Lewis & Clark Community Counseling Center provides low-cost counseling and therapy services that are culturally sensitive and designed to help with challenges including, but not limited to: depression, sexual intimacy, stress, marriage and family problems, domestic violence, experiences of oppression or injustice, substance abuse, problem gambling, eating disorders, school or career concerns, and trauma. All therapeutic services are strictly confidential and are available in English; limited services are available in Spanish. We provide individual, couple, family, and group therapy options.
More About the People We Treat
We share the space with the Lewis & Clark Problem Gambling Services (PGS), who aim to relieve the suffering associated with problem gambling by providing high quality treatment to individuals, couples, and families. All PGS services are free and confidential. We are dedicated to supporting social equity by providing easily accessible and culturally sensitive treatment. As a graduate training and research center, our interdisciplinary team is charged with developing innovative and effective approaches to treatment, sharing what we learn with others, and training the next generation of problem gambling treatment providers. We also offer Sex Therapy services, which addresses wide-ranging issues concerning sexuality and relationships. Sex Therapy at the Lewis and Clark Clinic aims to increase awareness and action in regards to sexual oppression.

name: Jordana Krueger-Toscher (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
By using Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) with a focus on mindfulness and your unique strengths, I can help you understand and change unhelpful thoughts, feelings and behaviors. Through our work you'll develop a better awareness of who you are, what you want and how to get it. I'll help you discover and feel confident in yourself, and figure out how to live the life you want. Together we'll identify problem areas or places where you feel stuck and find new ways of responding that will afford you more enjoyable, fulfilling outcomes. I'm dedicated to creating an environment of respect and appreciation for all clients' racial and ethnic membership, gender and sexual identity, social class, religious affiliation, nationality, body size, and disability status. Throughout our work I'll promote an atmosphere of openness, trust, and safety where all attitudes, beliefs, values, and behaviors can be freely explored and discussed.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
My experience and training make me especially qualified to help individuals manage a broad variety of concerns. I earned my bachelor's degree in psychology from the State University of NY at Stony Brook in 2003, and my master's degree in social work (MSW) from the University of Pennsylvania School of Social Policy and Practice in 2005. I then completed three years of intensive clinical practice and study, earning my LCSW (licensed clinical social work) credential in 2011. I have extensive training in evidence-based practices including Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) and Mindfulness Based Stress Reduction (MBSR).

name: Cassandra Walsh (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Healing from trauma is a journey, and every step you take is a powerful act of self-care and resilience. No matter where you are in your process, I believe in your capacity for growth, change, and healing. Together, we can work toward a future where you feel empowered, whole, and connected to your authentic self. It’s never too late to reclaim your story and build the life you deserve.
Social Justice Issues I Care About
I am deeply committed to practicing cultural humility, recognizing and respecting the unique identities and backgrounds of each client. My approach is sensitive to identity(s), including ethnicity, race, ability, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, immigration status, body size, socioeconomic position, and religion. Conversations about identity and culture are always welcome and encouraged in our work together. My commitment to social justice extends beyond the therapy room. I work to advocate for non-discriminatory practices, human rights, equity, and safety for all. It is my hope that these efforts contribute to a safe and therapeutic atmosphere where you can express your authentic self.

name: Kim Spencer (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use relationship building as the foundation of my therapy practice. I believe feeling seen, held, and safe are vital components of being able to grow. My hope is to create an atmosphere where the people I meet with feel met and known which will allow us to walk together through vulnerability. With all my clients I take a Neurobiological approach, which is a fancy way to say I think a lot about nervous systems and how to regulate them. Our nervous systems need co-regulation before they can learn to self-regulate. Together we will explore what feels soothing to your/your child’s particular system and build strategies from there. I use Child Led Play Therapy strategies in my work. I also pull from Attachment Theory and child development to support young children and their parents. For older clients I use more talk therapy, informed by Internal Family Systems (IFS) or parts work and Narrative Therapy techniques. My style is client centered, collaborative, affirming, and trauma informed.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a White, cisgender female. I have a deep connection with nature and feel the most restored in the forest or by the river. I grew up in the Midwest and completed my Bachelors degree in Social Work at Central Michigan University. I relocated to Portland 10 years ago and completed my Masters of Social Work at Portland State University. I interned and then worked at Morrison Child and Family Services for a total of 5 years. During my time at Morrison I became certified in Parent Child Interaction Therapy (PCIT) and completed my licensure. In my professional life I have been a daycare provider, camp counselor and director, paraprofessional, preschool teacher, and worked in a therapeutic school before becoming a therapist. I have over 18 years of experience working with children of all ages. In my personal time you can find me at the river with my dog, Nova, hiking in the forest (and hugging a tree or two), having coffee with friends, reading, or reflecting over tea.

name: Sarabeth Casey, LPC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Change is hard. Talking about feelings can be hard. It is important to me that the therapy environment feels supportive and comfortable, and that clients feel heard and understood. My therapeutic approach is relational and collaborative. I tend to utilize cognitive behavioral therapy, mindfulness, and strengths-based approaches, along with psychoeducation for parents/natural supports. I may guide you/your kiddo to integrate other resources in the community to balance social-emotional-physical health.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I graduated from Lewis and Clark College in 2006 with my Master's degree and an emphasis in working with children, adolescents, and families. I became licensed in 2009. As a therapist, I've worked with youth in residential treatment centers, outpatient clinics, in the community providing crisis intervention, schools, and in a partial hospital day treatment program specializing in treatment of eating disorders. I have completed a certificate program through PSU for therapy with adoptive and foster families. I use a trauma-informed framework to gently gather information and help a client gain insight about how past experiences impact current feelings and behaviors. In my spare time, I volunteer with Guide Dogs for the Blind and adopted a "career change" pup, Cookie, now a certified therapy dog. She occasionally makes an appearance in sessions! I also enjoy running, reading, soccer, and art.

name: Rebekah Springs (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am trained in Child-Centered Play Therapy (CCPT) and in DIR/Floortime therapy, a holistic, interdisciplinary approach to working with children, especially those diagnosed with Autism Spectrum Disorder and other sensory processing differences. The work that I do with individuals, families, and parent-child relationships is influenced by interpersonal neurobiology and current research on attachment theory and mind-body integration. I love supporting parents and families as they confront new diagnoses or work through new challenges that come with with each stage of development.

name: Tiffany Del Pozo (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
My specialities include working with clients who are experiencing anxiety, Obsessive compulsive disorder, panic, depression and or trauma. I’m also very skilled in helping clients navigate changes they want to make in their lives whether that’s a career, relocating, leaving a relationship and or divorce, etc.

name: Kaden Winterkorn (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Everyone’s path of healing and growth is unique; I work together with my clients to find an approach that feels right for them, honoring their wisdom, strengths, and preferences. My style is integrative– drawing from narrative therapy, IFS, mindfulness, and self-compassion practices. I meet my clients with deep respect for their process and approach our work together with creativity, curiosity, warmth, and an open heart. At the core of my practice is developing strong and trusting relationships with clients to guide the work we do together.
More About the People I Treat
I believe we all have intrinsic wisdom about ourselves and our life paths; counseling can be a way to come home to that wisdom. In addition to individual counseling, I offer support groups for the trans & non-binary community. These groups are spaces for folks to build community, share wisdom and support, and find affirmation and sense of belonging with each other. If you would like to learn more, I’d love to hear from you.

name: Myles Rizvi (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have dedicated myself to the treatment of anxiety and OCD-spectrum disorders. Passionate about providing early intervention for anxiety, I obtained my Psy.D. at Pacific University, where I focused my training in the Child and Adolescent Track. I completed a postdoctoral fellowship at Texas Children’s Hospital’s Obsessive-Compulsive and Anxiety Disorders Program to become proficient in treating a variety of anxiety and OCD-spectrum disorders. I have treated anxiety and OCD-spectrum disorders across the lifespan as a licensed clinical psychologist since 2019. My treatment approach is strongly informed by cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT) and exposure and response prevention (ERP). I also incorporate treatment principles and interventions from acceptance and commitment therapy (ACT), dialectical behavior therapy (DBT), and metacognitive therapy (MCT). I provide individual and family-based treatment, the latter of which is often the best practice for treating pediatric anxiety.
Why We are a Good Fit for You
Treating OCD and anxiety requires a collaborative, supportive, and team-based approach. You and I will work together to treat your symptoms by learning how to change the way you think and (perhaps most importantly) change how you behave in response to fear, anxiety, and panic. I will help you learn that you are stronger and more capable than your anxiety may suggest. It is difficult to move towards what scares us, and you will always have the choice of pace in your therapy journey. Each individual always chooses how hard they are willing to push themselves in order to face challenges and create change. My clients usually struggle with at least one of the following: Generalized Anxiety Disorder, Panic Disorder, Social Anxiety Disorder, Specific Phobias, Agoraphobia, Separation Anxiety Disorder, Hoarding Disorder, Body Dysmorphic Disorder, Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder, hair-pulling disorder (trichotillomania), and skin-picking disorder (excoriation disorder).

name: Lily Robertson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I help women and couples deepen self-awareness, build self-compassion, and create meaningful change. Our sessions are collaborative—I show up authentically, offering support and gentle challenge. Together, we’ll explore how past experiences live in the body and find a path toward healing, growth, and deeper connection. Here, you can show up as your raw, authentic self—without judgment. Over time, you'll gain the confidence to bring that true self into the world beyond therapy. You are the expert in your life; my role is to guide, support, and help you explore with curiosity. Together, we’ll create meaningful change and growth. Reach out today for a free consult.

name: Dana Fears (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use a creative, holistic and deeply attuned approach— with the following approaches: Person-Centered,Trauma-Informed, Strengths-Based, Solution-Focused, Motivational Enhancement, Informal Parts Work, Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), Narrative Therapy (NT), Relational and Reality therapy, Attachment Theory, Family Systems, Psychodynamic and Interpersonal therapy, Emotion Focused Therapy (EFT), Embodiment Therapy, and BrainSpotting. There is sacred power I have tapped into with these approaches and I am confident that we can surpass your current narrative together reaching a state of true and intentional empowerment.

name: Kristin Tebow (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Therapeutic approaches are determined based on needs, and may include: cognitive-behavioral therapy, attachment therapy, EMDR (Eye Movement Desensitization Reprocessing), relationship therapy, and supportive therapy.
Our Values as Mental Health Practitioners
My mission is to serve individuals experiencing mood disorders, attachment difficulties, addiction, difficult life experiences, anxiety, grief and loss, women's issues, gender issues, confusion, relationship difficulties, feeling stuck in life, and those looking for growth and development. Whatever you are experiencing, you are not alone, and you can change.

name: Shelby Hurd (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I see both individuals and couples in my practice. I offer goal directed treatment using Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT), Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT), and Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT).
Why I am a Good Fit for You
It is an honor for me to walk alongside my clients through their journeys of healing and self exploration. I bring a lively, open, and compassionate presence to my practice, and have room for humor in the work that we do together. I look forward to connecting and engaging in the work with you.

name: Kelly Sullivan (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My goal is to encourage people to reflect about themselves (their thoughts and their bodies), their experiences and their relationships using compassion, curiosity, and humor. My approach employs family systems, mindfulness, and interpersonal neurobiology. I am interested in building on people's strengths, and supporting them as they transform their reflections into different ways to navigate the world and their relationships.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker, with a Masters in Social Work from Portland State University. I have worked with people in private practice, agency, hospitals, and shelter environments. Over the years, I have gained the most experience in issues related to pregnancy, post-partum, adoption, infertility, bereavement and cancer diagnosis and survivorship. However, I have also worked with individuals and couples experiencing life transitions, overwhelming workplace stress, trauma, and relationship issues.

name: Alexandra Harrington (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In therapy, I use Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT) as my primary approach because it helps clients deeply connect with their emotions and understand the root of their struggles, whether they relate to relationships, personal transitions, or past wounds. EFT, combined with other therapeutic tools, provides a space where we can safely explore your emotional experience, helping you to heal, find clarity, and cultivate stronger connections with yourself and others. Whether you're healing from a relationship, managing stress, or trying to navigate significant life changes, we’ll work together to find strategies that are unique to your needs and values, empowering you to feel more present and connected in your life.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I may be a good fit for you if you’re looking for a therapist who brings warmth, curiosity, and a nonjudgmental presence to the work. I support individuals, couples, and families who are navigating relationship challenges, life transitions, or feeling overwhelmed by stress and emotion. My style is collaborative and compassionate—I’ll meet you where you are and walk alongside you as we explore what’s getting in the way of feeling more grounded, connected, and at ease. If you’re seeking a space where you can be yourself, feel understood, and move at your own pace, I’d be honored to support you on your journey.

name: Wes Harris (HE, HIS, HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I incorporate a variety of approaches into my way of working with people and believe that it is important to collaborate to find an approach that fits the client's needs. That being said, I often utilize Acceptance and Commitment Therapy and other mindfulness based approaches. Many people benefit greatly from having the opportunity to talk through the challenges they are facing in life. Additional benefit can be had from focusing on a particular difficulty and developing a focused plan to bring about change. Counseling is not a one-size-fits-all endeavor.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I hold a masters degree in professional mental health counseling from Lewis & Clark College. I am a Licensed Professional Counselor in the state of Oregon. My professional experience has brought me into contact with a wide variety of people. I specialize in supporting people struggling with difficult mood states, and who are seeking to remove obstacles to their wellbeing. I am also a Certified Alcohol and Drug Counselor.

name: Michelle Anderson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe a safe therapeutic relationship can be a foundation for harnessing community, connectivity, interdependence, and joy. I aim to build trust with authenticity, non-judgment, and humor. I believe in working through emotions and sensations, not around them--that rather than offering band-aid approaches, "the poison can be the medicine." This means leaning into stuck feelings in order to release them, often with a blend of mind-body approaches, including inner child and parts work. I'm here to help you find your true, core self, not fit you into a box.

name: Emily Radcliffe (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am an Emotion-Focused therapist, which means I believe that emotions are a powerful piece of the puzzle of our internal landscape. To complete the puzzle, I also draw on Attachment Theory, Intergenerational Family Therapy, IFS, DBT, CBT, Mindfulness, Spirituality and earth/nature-based healing. I believe that our pasts, our surroundings, and our relationships heavily influence our current experience. Understanding these influences can be powerful, liberating, challenging, and even scary. I'm here to remind you, you're worth it. I bring my full and authentic self to therapy and I believe that a positive, trusting, open and safe therapeutic relationship (our relationship) is essential to successful outcomes in therapy. My first goal will always be to connect with you authentically and create safety in our relationship. Safety is not an objective state, and my job is to ensure the environment we create together feels safe to you. This allows us to explore and dive deep.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in the interconnection of life. As humans we form our world view based on our experiences, stories, and relationships. Let's be real... The world can feel like a really difficult and scary place to live right now, especially for those who have marginalized identities. I stand firm in my values of anti-racism, LGBTQ affirmation, body positivity and beauty at any size, anti-hate, anti-ableism, and valuing/affirming neurodiversity. I strive to confront and dismantle systems and societal narratives that make and keep humanity sick and keep my clients stuck. I've spent significant time working with individuals, couples, and families to help them break through barriers and step out of boxes and cycles that make them feel trapped and hopeless. I have done this both as a therapist and in community-based non-profits where I provided support and mentorship. I am committed to community engagement and consider community and connection to be essential to human wellness.

name: Katie Playfair (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I provide therapy in-person and online across Oregon and Washington and education services worldwide. Technically, my approach draws from cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT), acceptance and commitment therapy (ACT), and more recently, Radically Open DBT (learning & training in process). More generally, I want to be your partner in discovering a better relationship with your mental health and/or work life. I want to empower you by providing education and transparency about how I see your experiences and what the evidence says about how we can help you live better. My hope is that you walk away from our meetings together knowing what works for you and what doesn't, in terms of mental health care, so it's knowledge you can use way beyond our therapy relationship. Also, therapy can be really fun and I hope to make it that way for you!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Beyond my clinical work, I really enjoy helping other clinicians integrate evidence-based therapies into their own personal style. Evidence-based practice doesn't have to be dry and mechanical. In fact, I think that the difficult work of therapy is much more tolerable for clients when we bring our unique selves, our senses of humor, and our humanity into the consulting room. I work with other clinicians via formal trainings, consultation with licensed clinicians, and supervision of associate clinicians. In my personal life, I am a mom of three kids and two dogs, living with ADHD, and navigating the challenges of middle-age and perimenopause. Firmly in the sandwich generation, I live with my spouse, kids, and my dad. Multigenerational living is rich in meaningfulness and challenges. Coming from a family of physicians and nurses, I apply that knowledge to help ensure that my clients are seeking high quality consultation from appropriate medical professionals while in counseling.

name: Jasmine Quistorf (SHE, THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As someone who is personally neurodivergent and trauma recovering, I have a deep connection to this work. I love supporting others in their journeys of peace finding, and I have a lot of gratitude for being able to learn from the folks I support. Therapy can be really scary for a lot of people, especially those of us who have been judged, unwelcome, or harmed by others. This is why the therapy space I aim to create with you prioritizes acceptance, openness, and comfortability. Whether we're sitting attentively in chairs working on deep processing or hanging out on the floor with some snacks and coloring books, the therapy we do together adapts to what your system needs in order to feel secure. My style is direct, honest, engaged, warm, and empathetic.
My Values as a Therapist
Three goals I aim for as a therapist are to: 1. Honor your unique experience. Your lived experience matters. I work with you to identify your unique needs, preferences, and goals. Whether you're dealing with anxiety, social challenges, reactivity to triggers, or just navigating the world as a neurodiverse individual, my goal is to create a tailored approach that works for you. 2. Focus on your strengths. Neurodivergence is often framed as something that needs to be "fixed" or "cured." I take a strengths-based approach, highlighting the ways your brain works in its own beautiful, creative, and insightful ways. We will explore how you can use your strengths to navigate the world around you. 3. Provide a safe, non-judgmental space. As a neurodivergent therapist, I understand the importance of a space where you feel truly seen and heard. You won’t have to explain yourself here or worry about me making assumptions about how you should think, feel, or behave.

name: Sara Rosenau (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a therapist, I begin with the understanding that you are the expert of your own life and you have inner knowledge and capacity for healing.
