My intention is to support your growth by offering a non-judgemental and supportive environment for you to be exactly where you are right now. I will hold space for you to be with your pain, tell the truth about what’s wrong, explore limiting beliefs, find new insight, and make courageous shifts towards healing and deepening purpose. Through therapy, I hope you can begin to express your “full and free human-beingness.” My approach is relational and centered in your strengths and resilience. I tailor my approach to each individual. I specifically work with the mind-body connection and am trained to introduce mindfulness into sessions in a way that gently guides you to pay attention to the body’s signals to deepen your understanding of your struggles. Just having a willingness to be curious in self-exploration is a good place to begin.
My Values as a Therapist
I am a member of the LGBTIQ community. I am kink/poly aware and sex positive. I actively examine my privilege and seek to align my practice to support the thriving of all individuals, especially those who are marginalized by the dominant culture in areas of race, gender expression, and disability. I have deep values in social justice and volunteered my skills for Black Lives Matter and other social movements for liberation.

name: Sara Amundson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
It's important to me to partner with others based on who they are and to make space for their unique needs. I am happy to adjust my approach as needed to co-create a therapeutic relationship. I am passionate about care that serves the whole individual and am especially interested in skills-based work. I utilize the following models most often in the mental health services that I provide: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Dialectical Behavioral Therapy, and Rational Emotive Behavior Therapy. I have a strong passion for serving queer individuals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a behaviorist. I believe that we may not always be able to change our automatic thoughts or our perception of the world but we can change how we behave. In learning to navigate conflict, adversity, and pain in ways that do not make situations worse we often reduce our shame, improve our relationships, and ultimately reduce our negative thinking patterns that we thought would never change. I believe that stigma is hurting our communities more than we realize. There is no diagnosis I am afraid to offer a client if it is appropriate nor a diagnosis I will refuse to work with. Everyone is welcome here as long as I am trained in how to best support you. I also believe that community care is essential. I will always work to connect you to informal supports and peer supports in your community as we learn best from and maintain our skills for health with others and not in isolation.

name: Samantha Rott (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy primarily follows Integrated Therapy utilizing Psychodynamic, Attachment, and Family Systems theories. My pronouns are she, her, hers and I look forward to learning yours. I find behavior to be an important opening to explore the underlying feelings an individual is experiencing in response to others. I view therapy as a collaborative process in which you are the expert of your own life and I am an empathetic and supportive guide helping create a brave, accepting space. With you willing to explore the past experiences that are relevant to seeking therapy today, I provide reflections to offer alternative perspectives for possible change. This is a process of discovery within your systems of influence, such as your family—however you may define this.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I enjoy spending time with my partner and children, other family, traveling, and anything outdoors. I have additional training in Child-Parent Relationship/Filial Therapy, Child-Centered Play Therapy, Sandtray Therapy, Family Therapy, Couples Therapy, as well as training in Trauma Response Services, specifically Risk Assessment and Suicidality Crisis Intervention Techniques, Traumatology and Trauma-Informed Care, Interpersonal Neurobiology of Trauma, Race and Trauma, and Grief and Loss Across Lifespan.

name: Nicole Kammerlocher (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Struggling with issues around sex, relationships, gender, and sexuality can be isolating because they seem private - you don't talk about them much, and it can seem like no one else is struggling the same way you are. You aren't alone in your struggle, and my top priority is to make sure you feel welcomed and accepted in our sessions. I use many different therapeutic techniques, but work primarily from Feminist, Cognitive Behavioral, and Emotionally Focused frameworks. I specialize in working with clients exploring their gender and sexual orientation, low sexual desire, and experiencing sexual disconnection. In our sessions, we will work together to explore your current relationship to sex and make a plan to build the relationship you want to have with sex through practical skills, tools, and deeper discussions about what sex means for you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I wanted to become a sex therapist because growing up as a queer kid in Oklahoma, it seemed like all of the messages about sexuality focused on why it's bad and how we should feel ashamed of sex. I saw how this hurts our relationships with ourselves and partners. I understand the challenges created when we feel like it’s not okay to talk about sex openly and honestly. I obtained my BA in Psychology and MS in Mental Health Counseling from Oklahoma State University and am currently training to become a Certified Sex Therapist through the California Institute of Integral Studies. For me, the most rewarding part of sex therapy is helping people build relationships with themselves and their partners that celebrate their authentic sexuality, and I would be honored to help you create deeper connections and explore your fullest sexual life.

name: Madilyn Long (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that all people hold within them an inherent ability to heal, connect, and thrive. With challenges that arise over the course of a life, that ability may become diminished or more difficult to access. I believe that a therapeutic relationship based on honest and mutual connection, trust and respect can be the catalyst for life-altering healing and change. I strive to offer an empathetic and affirming approach to all of my clients as they express themselves through their many identities, experiences, strengths, and challenges. I see my role as someone that will walk beside you, as a companion, as you find the solutions that work best for you. I value the right dose of empathy, compassion, and humor to help facilitate a safe and comforting environment in which you can engage and find your way to a more joyful and purposeful life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I chose to pursue a career in social work because I knew that through my education and experience working as a professional I would be exposed to people, thoughts, and experiences that were different from what I was familiar with. I have always been deeply passionate about helping others but often felt powerless when faced with the weight of all that needs "fixing" in our world. Through my work as a clinical social worker, I have found ways in which I can be most effective as an agent for social change. I continue to recognize, deconstruct, and acknowledge the ways in which I am privileged in an effort to leverage that privilege for good. I am a vocal and passionate advocate for education, harm-reduction and radical societal change in regards to the most vulnerable members of our society.

name: Maggie Riley (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Through compassion, openness, and warmth, my hope is to provide a safe, healing, empowering space for individuals, adolescents, couples, and families to navigate life's challenges. I am here to walk alongside you through growth, healing, and self-discovery. I believe therapy can be a powerful space to uncover and explore aspects of the human experience. I graduated from Gonzaga University with a Master's in Marriage and Family Counseling. I am a Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist, as well as Licensed Professional Counselor in the State of Oregon. I have worked in a school-based mental health program working with adolescents and their families. I also have extensive experience working with clients with eating disorders, including adolescents and their families, and adults, in residential, partial hospitalization, and intensive outpatient programs. My cash fees are $100 - $150 per visit and I will be accepting several insurance plans.
My Values as a Therapist
I am passionate about creating a safe, inclusive space for adolescents, individuals, families, and couples to take steps toward healing. I want to guide you in creating an authentic relationship with yourself as well as authentic connections with others. We are relational beings and I work from a systems lens. I believe every individual is unique and I want to honor the therapy process that works best for you. Healing is a unique journey and my role is to support you in both self-discovery and exploring your relationships with others. I believe in a holistic approach to healing, and finding peace both within yourself, and your personal connections. All are welcome!

name: Betsy Malolepsy (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Betsy is a graduate student in clinical mental health counseling whose theoretical framework is primarily person-centered, enriched by elements of existential, feminist, queer, and family systems approaches. This integrative model reflects her belief in clients as experts of their own experience while acknowledging the impact of systemic oppression, identity formation, and the search for meaning. While these theories align with her values and worldview, she maintains flexibility in their application, recognizing that effective therapy must prioritize client needs over theoretical preferences.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Betsy's journey to mental health counseling has been shaped by diverse life experiences that inform her culturally humble approach to client care. In her early twenties, she worked and traveled extensively, developing a deep appreciation for different cultures and backgrounds. These experiences taught her the universal human need for authentic connection and the importance of creating safe spaces where people can be their true selves. As a parent of three children, including twins — one of whom is transgender — Betsy has firsthand experience supporting LGBTQIA+ youth and understanding the crucial role of acceptance and affirmation in mental health. Her experience navigating the loss of her mother to Alzheimer's has also given her a deep, personal understanding of grief and loss, enhancing her capacity for empathy and compassionate care. It can be hard to take the first step toward therapy. If you'd like to learn more about working together, please reach out for a conversation.

name: Gillian Chachere
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that change takes place within the framework of strong, healthy relationships. I strive to be a partner in healing, building a bond of trust and connection in which clients feel safe unpacking difficult emotions. I also use cognitive-behavioral tools to help clients problem behaviors and life patterns. I have a strong grounding in feminism and social justice advocacy, and provide a safe space for all human experience and identity.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I provide depth-oriented trauma therapy to children, adolescents and adults, using both relational interventions and a knowledge of the effects of trauma on the brain. I also have a focus on the difficult and painful issues that come with infertility and pregnancy loss. In addition to these specialties, I work with issues around relationships, family dynamics, children's and adolescents' behavioral issues, parenting tools, self esteem, body image, anxiety, depression, and many other areas of mental health.

name: Ashley Sullivan (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I hold a Masters Degree in Social Work (2011), a B.S. in Child Development/Family Relations and a B.S. in Psychology (2009). Following my education, I have worked with adults, children, and family-systems within clinic, home-based, and hospital settings. Clients describe me as warm, compassionate, and relatable with a willingness to incorporate humor as part of the healing process. While I am kind and supportive, I also challenge stuck-points to create movement and directly address client distress and mental health symptoms. I utilize an eclectic approach to therapy as every client benefits from a tailored plan that matches their unique needs. My therapeutic lens is heavily influenced by attachment theory, systems theory, human development, self-compassion, and trauma-informed care. I believe in a collaborative therapeutic relationship in which we can work together in treating your symptoms and helping you participate more fully in your life.

name: Natasha Laumei (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work from a client-centered and collaborative foundation, supporting individuals looking to process and heal from trauma, develop further personal insight, improve relationships with self and others, and explore opportunities for growth, empowerment, and deepened quality of life. As a therapist I bring empathy, intuition, and humor into session and integrate trauma-informed, attachment-focused, anti-oppressive, neurodiverse-affirming, and feminist frameworks into my clinical approach. I utilize EMDR, parts-work, interpersonal neurobiology, somatic, narrative, creative expression, ecotherapy, and mindfulness-based approaches in my work with clients. All of my work is rooted in the belief that health and wellbeing are a right and not a privilege and I am so grateful to join each of my clients along their journeys of growth and healing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My decision to become a therapist was informed by many things: studying human stories through the lens of creative writing and narrative structure, working with refugee families as an AmeriCorps volunteer, supporting teens at a therapeutic boarding school in the Sapphire Mountains of Montana, and my own lifelong journey of trauma recovery, growth, and healing. I am a white, cis, queer, neurodivergent woman, and in the rest of my life I love spending time in nature, being with my sweet dogs and sassy cat, traveling, and diving into my new love of Brazilian jiu-jitsu.

name: Erin Winn Oliva (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My therapy style is collaborative & flexible, tailored to the preferences of you as the client. I approach all individuals through a trauma and attachment lens, & find it deeply beneficial to explore how our pasts shape the way we navigate the present. I offer reflection & alternative perspectives to help you approach challenges in new ways. I invite you to include your loved ones in our sessions, as I believe healing happens as a family/community. I have been working in the mental health field since 2016, and running my own business since 2021. My clinical history includes integrated behavioral health care in a midwife/OBGYN clinic, trauma-focused therapy, individual and group therapy with immigrants and survivors of domestic violence, therapy and case management with houseless individuals, and extensive experiencing performing psychological evaluations for immigration cases.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My practice is rooted in my social justice philosophies– all human beings deserve access to quality mental health care; all human beings deserve a safe space to be vulnerable and honest; all human beings deserve time dedicated to healing from the inherent challenges/trauma of everyday life. I believe that societal healing depends first on the healing of the individual. My practice welcomes individuals of all races, genders, sexual orientations, and abilities.

name: Melissa Owens LPC
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I can help you get unstuck; strengthen your communication, connections, relationships and boundaries; free yourself from addiction, addictive relationships, and compulsive behaviors; heal emotional reactivity and stop hurting yourself and those you love. I am skilled in DBT, Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR); Somatic Experiencing; Internal Family Systems (IFS) and other Ego-State therapies involving mind/body awareness using breath, movement and imagery, as well as Jungian Active Imagination and Kundalini Yoga therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have been providing therapy for over 20 years. Prior to establishing a private practice, I served as Clinical Supervisor and Addictions and Mental Health Counselor at DePaul Treatment Center, an adult dual diagnosis residential program. I worked on both the men's and the women's floors and became familiar with the interaction between trauma, relationship difficulties, and addictive/compulsive behavior.. I have been an ESL Teacher and Bilingual Education Coordinator and worked at a Women’s Resource Center as Skills Trainer. I earned an MA in Counseling Psychology with Addictions Specialty at Lewis & Clark College. Prior to that I held a Teacher Certification from the University of Alaska. I earned a BA in literature from the University of California, San Diego.

name: Emily Yang (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My priority is to create a space that feels safe and comfortable for you to bring your full self to the counseling room. This means holding space for your wins, losses, joy, and pain. I take a collaborative approach and enjoy utilizing a relational and emotionally focused approach. I may also utilize behavioral approaches when helpful. Healing and hope are possible, and we can find it together.
Why I Became a Therapist
My desire to become a therapist began when I walked through my own spiritual and cultural deconstruction, which led to first hand experience of my own depression and anxiety. Part of my healing has been creating space to advocate for my intersectional identities as a second generation Taiwanese American woman and mother, and process the grief that comes from institutional religious and emotional trauma. Now, I believe uncertainty can feel clear, our stories are nuanced, and healing is collective.

name: Hollywood Mental Health
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We believe in treating the whole person, and see people of every socioeconomic and ethnic background. We support individuals who identify with the LGBTQ community. We believe in helping our clients find a healthy balance and meaning in their lives. In regards to medications, we believe in a careful informed consent process. This involves sharing potential risks and benefits in some detail without jargon. We believe in evidence-based medicine.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a group, we have the ability to provide therapy and medication management for most issues. We work with all ages and provide support related to anxiety, depression, life transitions, relationship issues, bipolar, family systems, adoption and foster care, perinatal mental health, issues related to transgender and queer identity and ADHD to name a few. We use some of the following modalities: CBT, DBT, EMDR, Narrative Therapy, Humanistic and Family Systems work and Art Therapy. Learn more about our team at https://www.hmhpdx.com/providersandstaff/ If you are interested in making an appointment please complete a new client inquiry form at: https://www.hmhpdx.com/new-client-inquiries-1/

name: Amber Rose Dullea (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Amber Rose Dullea is a Licensed Clinical Social Worker in Oregon and graduated from University of Southern California in 2022 with a Masters of Social Work in Adult Mental Health and Wellness. Amber Rose brings a diverse background including whole systems design, massage, interfaith spirituality, coaching, and LGBTQ+ perspectives. As a coach they worked with a diversity of college students from state schools, fine arts, HBCUs, adult learners to graduate education. In social work, they have worked in community mental health, hospice, bereavement, and private practice.

name: Sunya Grantham
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
When our relationship has become (or has been) the cause of distress we often create a negative story of our partner that is difficult to re-write. Once our mind views our partner as a source of hurt and pain, it becomes difficult to see them as anything but potential danger. This further entrenches the negative story of our partner and our relationship; it seems unfixable. I help clients identify the thoughts and feelings causing them to behave in ways that prevent them from getting what they want out of their relationship. I provide my clients with education and practical tools to disrupt unhelpful communication patterns to help them share what they need from their partner in more productive ways. You can begin to interact in ways that leave you open to a renewed sense of connection and more positive story. What is consistent in my approach is: collaboration, an empathetic attitude, and laughter. Much like you would expect in your significant relationships.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am committed to providing affordable therapy to persons within marginalized groups. I reserve sliding scale slots for BIPOC and LGBTQ identifying clients. I completed a M.A. in Marriage & Family Therapy at Pacific Lutheran University. I have experience working with couples and families experiencing high conflict, infidelity, and people living with a person impacted by mental illness. I offer a poly affirming space which acknowledges the challenges members of this community face. In addition, I have specialized training in Medical Family Therapy, allowing me to respond to the impact of chronic illness and disability on relationships.

name: Danette Gillespie-Otto
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am an intuitive, attachment-based therapist who specializes in working with Womxn and Femmes of Color. Together we unravel the knots that bind you from the inside out. I offer compassion, connection, fresh perspective and practical tools to aid in your healing. I use cultural sensitivity, interpersonal neurobiology, sand tray therapy and cognitive behavioral therapy in sessions.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received my Master's of Social Work from the George Warren Brown School of Social Work at Washington University in St. Louis, Missouri in 2000. I have been a licensed clinical social worker since 2007. I have worked in community mental health, quality improvement, administration, local government, mentoring, research and private practice. I specialize in working with African-American, biracial and multi-racial clients of African descent, though I work with clients of all racial and ethnic backgrounds. I provide a holistic assessment and support that is individualized to your unique needs. I practice in a way that forms bridge between biology, psychology and spirituality--balancing these aspects of the whole. Give yourself the gift of deep alignment and healing. You deserve it!

name: Jonah Stabinski
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
So often, we treat our pain like an enemy to be conquered. But pain is not the problem—it’s a signal, a call. It speaks in the language of longing: for connection, for safety, for meaning. In our work together, we won’t rush to silence it. We’ll listen. We’ll turn gently toward what hurts—not to fix it, but to understand what it’s asking of you. I’ll invite you to meet yourself with tenderness and patience, to grow your willingness to stay with what’s hard, and to take steps—however small—toward a life that feels rich and true. Change isn’t about erasing the hard parts. It’s about learning how to carry them with care, while making room for what brings you joy. As a Social Worker, I understand how mental health problems can be a product of an oppressive society. For this reason, I value anti-racism, queer and trans liberation, climate justice, compassionate immigration policy, and a strong social safety net.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Alongside my traditional therapy training, I have received certification in Psychedelic-Assisted Therapy from the Integrative Psychiatry Institute. My interest in this work comes from a desire to bridge the worlds of psychedelic healing and evidence-based mental health care. I believe that psychedelics, when held within legal, safe, and research-informed contexts, can offer powerful opportunities for growth. I also provide integration support for those reflecting on psychedelic experiences, with a focus on safety, support, and long-term change. Please note that I do not facilitate access to psychedelic substances or experiences outside of legalized settings in any way.

name: Julia Perretta (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
I am a Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist. I specialize in couples therapy with an eclectic approach rooted in EFT, attachment, neuroscience, somatic experiencing, and IFS. I also work with individuals, utilizing my background in DBT, IFS, solution focused, and somatic/mindfulness based modalities. I am a certified yoga and mindfulness instructor, which provides a lovely backbone for the other therapeutic approaches I utilize. I really enjoy working with couples experiencing conflict, distress, and infidelity, as well as individuals struggling with anxiety, stress, time management/organization, depression, life transitions, addiction, and eating disorders.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I believe that change is always possible. I tailor my therapeutic approaches to meet the needs of each individual client, couple, or family who I work with. I am trained in EFT, DBT, and solution focused methodologies. I operate from a trauma informed, body positive, feminist-identifying framework. When I first meet with a couple I gather information about attachment and past relationships which will guide us in tracking patterns of interaction and inform our work, in later sessions. Throughout the therapeutic process, we will explore emotions, patterns, parts of self that arise as you make your way through these patterns, and increase vulnerability and connection through emotion-focused interventions. In individual sessions I will gather information about your goals, and develop a personalized treatment plan to help you accomplish these goals. I support LGBTQI activism and the BLM movement by staying engaged and promoting social justice in and outside of the therapy room.

name: Justin Rock (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I utilize existential-humanistic therapy to reach through the difficult and towards the beauty of life. I work collaboratively with my clients to create a safe and supportive relationship for them to explore being with themselves and being with others in the world. Too often we want to look “out there” into the world to find an answer to our struggles. Instead, I believe we are capable of creating our own answers. For this reason I work with diverse clients, among them clients challenged by death anxiety, identity death/grief, depression, anxiety, lack of fulfillment, life transitions, relationship issues, personal growth, LGBTQ issues, and polyamory & consensual non-monogamy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I became a therapist over a decade ago because of the pathologizing marginalization that occurred to many in the Poly and ENM community in Portland. This requires honoring the clients experience and knowledge whoever they are and for whoever they are becoming. This is true for, in my opinion, for every human. Therapeutic work is possible when the humanity is seen in each other, not the pathology. I am a founding member of Existential-Humanistic Northwest, a professional non-profit which is here to impact the world through our commitment to and passion for existential-humanistic values of authenticity, integrity, responsibility, inter-connectedness, inclusion, and awe. I am also an Adjunct Professor at Lewis & Clark College in the Marriage and Family Therapy program and the Art Therapy program, teaching classes such as: Introduction to Therapy Theories, Addictions in Family Therapy, Polyamory and Ethical Non-Monogamy, and Family Development.

name: Emily Haygeman (THEY/THEM/SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
With over 15 years of experience working directly with families and children, I offer a compassionate and integrated approach through a variety of evidence-based modalities. My quality psychological evaluations result in a comprehensive psychological report, and I aim to "tell the story" of an individual rather than simply assign a "label." All evaluations yield individualized recommendations for home, school, and therapeutic environments. Following evaluation, I provide a client-centered, in-depth feedback session aimed at empowerment. I closely follow the work of autistic self-advocates and leaders in the area of neurodivergence such as Dr. Devon Price and Dr. Nick Walker. I am constantly improving my work through self-reflection, learning, reading, and advocacy. For adult autism evaluations, I utilize measures that are appropriate for adults rather than relying on measures best suited for children. I recognize populations that have been left out of our diagnostic samples.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that psychological evaluation should be a therapeutic process. I am an advocate for my clients, and see the evaluation process as an opportunity to empower an individual to know their strengths and challenges. I don't believe in simply assigning a diagnosis. All of my evaluations offer more than simply diagnostic clarity. I am a member of the LGBTQIA2S+ community and take an affirming approach in regard to my clients' identities. My services are trauma-informed. I also understand that as a white psychologist, I have been afforded much privilege in my life. The field of psychology has a varied past in that direct harm has been done, particularly to BIPOC and indigenous populations. I am committed to understanding these issues in an ongoing capacity in order to mitigate the impact of my biases and privilege on my clients' experience.

name: Rebecca Lundberg (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapeutic approach is integrative and collaborative, emphasizing connection and compassion. I draw from various modalities, including: *Attachment Theory: examining how interpersonal relationships affect emotional well-being. *Mindfulness: focusing on present-moment awareness. *Relational-Cultural Theory: highlighting the significance of connection in relationships. *Internal Family Systems: aiding individuals in understanding and harmonizing different aspects of themselves. *Art and Play Therapies: fostering expression and healing through creativity. I view counseling as a way to explore the complex ways our bodies and minds adapt to this wild world. Together, we will tune into your experiences and support you as we delve into the core beliefs, values, and narratives that shape your life. Recognizing that there is no one-size-fits-all approach, I am committed to tailoring our process to meet your individual needs and goals on your path to exploration, growth, and healing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My career journey has taken me through diverse experiences, including landscape design, working with at-risk youth, adults facing memory loss, and individuals undergoing physical rehabilitation. Additionally, my background arts education has shaped my perspective on healing and connection. As a native Oregonian, I have come to appreciate the beauty of soggy hikes and frosty beach walks—because who needs sunshine when you have character, right? I deeply believe in the inherent value of our relationships—with ourselves, our loved ones, and our communities. My intention is to create an authentic, affirming space where you feel seen and valued. I strive to highlight themes of intersectionality and the impact of context and culture, acknowledging how these elements shape our lives. Whether you are facing challenges or seeking personal growth, I am here to walk alongside you.

name: Allie Corcoran (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is authentic, non-judgmental, trauma-informed and inclusive. I use a combination of behavioral, emotional, and mindfulness-based approaches to meet you where you are. Technically the therapy approaches I use are CBT, DBT, ACT and EFT. It's a lot of letters, I know. I'm happy to chat with you about what might fit best for you! Therapy should be made-to-order. From me you can expect acceptance, validation, affirmation, kind and honest feedback, and insights/tools. I value inclusivity and welcome diversity in gender, sexuality, race, body size, and ability in my work.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Though pain is inevitable it doesn’t have to prevent us from living a fulfilling and meaningful life. I'm big on self-compassion and acceptance and use a fair amount of those ideas in my work with clients. I believe in embracing who we are and bring my true self to sessions, so with me you can expect some good-natured sass and playfulness and a lot of honesty. If it sounds like I may be a fit for you, let’s talk.

name: Shelley Gillespie (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is collaborative and holistic. I draw on eight years of community-based therapeutic practice in which I’ve served people across the ages, from children to the oldest-old, and from different racial and class backgrounds, among other identities. I aim to co-create a therapy space that is welcoming, supportive and a source of liberatory health. Our sessions are based on your stated goals and our collaboration to reach the well-being you are seeking from therapy. As appropriate, I incorporate elements of Internal Family Systems, Cognitive Behavior Therapy, Narrative Therapy, expressive arts, mindfulness, and self-compassion. I recognize the role oppression plays on individual and collective well-being and tend to those dynamics, as appropriate. I’m committed to reflecting on my own biases as an integral part of being a social justice-oriented practitioner. I believe in the vast possibilities inside of us to tap into inner resilience and relational strength.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
“You are the sky. Everything else – it’s just weather.” -Pema Chodron. In our times of grief, and struggle for a healthy planet, for racial and social justice, and inner well-being and outer connection, how do we hold intense emotions and experiences and keep on? Life in these times often doesn’t feel like just weather. How do we know and feel that we are the sky-that inner sense of okayness as we are? How do we draw on the strengths of our inner selves and our supportive relationships to create a sense of wholeness? These are the kinds of questions I’m interested in answering in times of struggle, and in answering them, setting in motion practices that help nourish healing, fortitude, and even un-imagined growth. In seeking answers to questions that will be unique to you, I offer my compassionate witnessing, attuned curiosity, and collaboration to your healing and wellness. Please reach out to our practice and let us know what you would like therapy to support you with!

name: Brandy Tidwell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe we each have a fundamental need to be seen, valued, and accepted for exactly who we are. My primary goal as a therapist is to provide a safe and affirming relationship where we can collaboratively explore who you are, how you came to be, and who you wish to become as we work together toward your goals. I practice therapy from a holistic and systemic perspective, taking into account the multitude of ways your physical, psychological, and relational health interact and inform one another. In session this might look like processing emotions through mindfulness and somatic exercises, practicing skills for regulating your nervous system, getting curious about your attachment style, learning to communicate your needs and boundaries, examining your belief systems, learning to relate with yourself more compassionately, and exploring ways to live in alignment with your values.
My Values as a Therapist
I’m a white, queer, cisgender woman. I grew up in a highly conservative and homogenous culture where I struggled to find a sense of belonging, which taught me the importance of diversity and inclusion, influencing my path toward becoming a psychologist and passion for working with marginalized communities. It is among my highest values to honor and celebrate diversity in all forms. I hold anti-capitalist, anti-imperialist, and anti-racist values. I work to educate myself about various cultural backgrounds while also meeting each client with openness and humility to learn about their unique experience and expression of their identities. I strive to be aware of my privilege and remain open to feedback about my impact in the world. I’m committed to the lifelong process of deconstructing systems of oppression within and around me and hope to contribute toward a more liberated, inclusive, and just world for all beings everywhere.

name: Preston Turner (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Our time together starts by exploring your values and learning how we can make the therapy process align best with you, ironing out the wrinkles standing in your way. While our initial sessions together are more structured, later sessions will reflect a structure that best suits you. During our initial consultation, we discuss what you'd like from treatment and see if we're a good fit. Afterwards, during our 50-minute intake session we learn more about each other and establish a treatment plan allowing us to stay mindful of your goals, how we reach them, and how they change. I often encourage my clients to practice certain exercises outside of session to nurture relevant skills and experiences to best serve their treatment goals. Additionally, I reserve multiple reduced-fee slots in my caseload for those needing assistance.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Openness and acceptance of uncertainty is key to how I grow and learn. It allows me to act and learn from experience rather than worry, and find new insights. Whether this leads me to gain unexpected skills and perspective from an eclectic source of hobbies like bouldering, tufting rugs, roasting coffee, or building instruments, to incorporating an eclectic approach to treatment, branching insights and concepts from across disciplines. Among the various things I am interested to learn more about, one example is the intersection of behavioral strategies with music therapy.

name: Emily Stasko (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My overarching goal as a therapist is to help you do more of the things you want to in your life, despite the pain and distress that you may experience. Regardless of the type of work we are doing, the context of your life and your values are key to helping you meet your goals. For individuals processing past trauma, this means focusing on what you would like to be doing if the trauma took up less space in your life. For those who are struggling with chronic or new health diagnoses, this means looking at how you understand your health conditions and how to live a meaningful life despite them. The annoying truth is that we often can’t change the source of your distress- we cannot change the past, erase past traumas, or undo reality. We can however help you change your perspective, build skills to cope with your realities, and invest in your version of a meaningful life. You are the expert on your life. For therapy to be effective, it is essential you feel seen and understood.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
We are all shaped by the worlds we live in- by our communities, our experiences, and our identities. As someone who has moved frequently (first as a military brat and later by choice), I learned early on that my experiences were not always the same as those around me. This has given me both awareness and a particular interest in understanding how people navigate the changes involved in starting over- whether that is in a new place, a new job, or new relationships. As someone who has also managed chronic health conditions since I was young, I have first-hand knowledge of what it can be like to navigate life with visible and at times restrictive health issues. My experiences have shaped me as a therapist and helped me deepen my connection with clients. I strive to be authentic and self-aware and pay keen attention to how my life experiences may be impacting my understanding of you and our relationship. I approach each client with compassion and without judgement.

name: Beverley Duke-Young (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
If you have never been to talk with a counselor, I assure you that I will do my best to help you feel safe and relaxed. As a seasoned therapist there is not much new that I haven't heard or helped people find ways to deal with and resolve in a more satisfactory manner. I value your trust in feeling you can share your burden and leave feeling better than when you came in. I don't think most people need endless sessions of therapy unless there has been serious traumas. Often just talking about things with someone who is not involved with you is very helpful. I work with you to support you in making the decisions in what you are seeking to manifest.
More About the People I Treat
I have dual degree: B.S. in Psychology and Sociology and a Master of Science in Counseling, Education & Psychology. I am licensed as a Marriage Counselor with many years of continuing educations in specialties in the field. I am also trained in the field of addictions treatment and work with codependency issues.

name: Madeline Ball (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
I was raised in a Roman Catholic family where my sexual and gender identity was not only not dismissed but downright restricted. By the time I entered into a queer friendly and sex positive community the freedom I felt was empowering. I discovered the desire to encourage my community to heal from the collective wounds we face. I recognize how hard it is to confront these attachment wounds and choose differently, however I believe there is always a chance to grow more meaningful and impactful relationships. I am excited to use my knowledge and experience to connect and work with you towards a more fulfilling relationship with yourself and others. I hope to promote healthy relationships and help others increased sexual pleasure and dive further into alignment.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I value depth, longevity, and presence. These three values are woven through sessions as we dive deep into what you believe and who you aspire to become, encourage insight into the present moment, and use that insight to create lasting change in your life. I might be a good fit for you if any of the following apply: -You are ready to dive into a deeper understanding of yourself -You are stuck and struggling to figure out the next step in your relationship/life -You want more fulfilling sexual pleasure [from a variety of sexual disorders, KINK/bdsm, or otherwise] -You and your partner are considering marriage or want to revamp a relationship you have been in for years -You are LGBTQIA+ -You are interested in opening your relationship or are struggling in an current ENM relationship -You are eager for more in life!

name: Kelly Hubert (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapeutic style integrates psychodynamic understanding with relationship-based techniques, supported by evidence-based practices like Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT). I believe that exploring how past experiences shape current patterns can create meaningful insights that lead to lasting change. In our sessions, you'll find a nonjudgmental space where you can safely navigate difficult emotions while discovering pathways to healing and growth. I take pleasure in helping clients develop deeper self-compassion and practical strategies for creating a life aligned with their core values and goals.

name: Graham Sterling (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
It is my philosophy, that each of us are on a path that encompasses understanding who we are in relation to our surroundings. Answers are found through interpersonal connections and within ourselves. It is my job to offer a safe, supportive space and the skilled assistance that allows you to discover your strengths and the tools necessary to overcome life's hurdles.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have an MA in psychology and am a Licensed Professional Counselor.

name: Robin Friedman, LCSW (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
I draw from many disciplines and personal life experience. My formal education includes a BFA from Colorado State University and teacher education from University of California at San Diego. I earned my MSW from the University of Utah in 2004. I completed a certificate program in Person Centered Expressive Arts Therapy and level 1 and 2 training in EMDR Therapy through EMDRIA. Since 2000, I have attended many professional trainings and workshops including Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), trauma therapy, mindfulness and somatic based psychotherapy, and sexual assault crisis intervention training. I regularly attend conferences, professional trainings, seminars and ongoing peer consultation. Clinical Experience: Therapist since 2003, Private Practice since 2006, Supervisor for MSW interns and CSWA's since 2008, Senior Therapist at a Rape Recovery Center 4.5 years, Therapist at an outpatient adult substance abuse treatment center. As well as varied and wide life experience.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Therapy is a process and can help calm the storm, allowing room for self discovery, and positive change. Whether we are working long or short-term, we will allow each session to move in the direction needed at that time. My style is to be honest, accepting, respectful, spontaneous, intelligent, creative, and compassionate. You will have full permission to be and express all of who you are. I work from a client-centered perspective and a social justice lens. I believe each person has within them the ability to heal, and is ultimately the expert on their own lives. My role is to collaborate with you on the journey of healing, self expression, and identifying and honoring your unique strengths. As your witness, I will offer my presence, knowledge, and deep listening, as well as provide you with experiences and skills aimed at assisting you in healing, growth, and living in alignment with your self-identified values. In addition, I use the creative process whenever possible.

name: Christa Brennan (SHE/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Using elements of art therapy, drama therapy, IFS, EMDR, and the latest research on how to regulate the nervous system I approach your situation with compassion to help you confront unhelpful patterns of thoughts and behaviors, break free from guilt/shame, rekindle inner-joy and cultivate resiliency for an integrated sense of self.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in your innate ability to heal, to make meaning from suffering and bounce back from adversity. I am an LGBTQ+ ally, cisgender woman with over a decade of experience working in the field. Specific areas of focus include grief, loss, addiction, domestic violence, sexual abuse, anxiety, depression, ADHD, and relationship issues. In 2012, I developed an art therapy workshop called Broken Bowl based on the Japanese philosophy of Wabi Sabi and Kintsugi. Wabi Sabi embraces the flawed or imperfect aspects of life and Kintsugi is the practice of repairing broken pottery with gold. I guide individuals through a process of confronting change, accepting impermanence, and deciding how to move forward with what is left. If you are interested in participating in this workshop please reach out for upcoming dates.

name: Abby Hall (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Background
I graduated with my Masters Degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from The George Washington University in Washington DC in 2019. Since then I have worked primarily in group practices working with wonderful clients all across the life spectrum. I worked with children and teenagers during my internships and early career, moving to adults from all walks of life later in my practice. While I have loved and learned so much from my clients throughout the years, I have become passionate about specializing my practice to working with queer adults, specifically those who are exploring their asexual and aromantic identities, as well as those who have histories with high demand religions or religious trauma.
My Approach to Helping
I provide queer affirming sex therapy for adult individuals and couples who are wanting to explore their identity, sexuality or address sexual functioning concerns. I pull from attachment and trauma-based techniques in our sessions with the purpose of creating a better relationship with your self and body, and exploring your experiences with intimacy.

name: Brian Pollock (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Today, my approach blends modalities and is always built on a collaborative foundation. This includes integrating Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) and Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) with medication management to help patients on their way to achieving balance and growth.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Outside of work you’ll likely find me in one of a few places. I value the time I can spend with the people I care about the most. To that end, I spend most of my free time at home with my wife, daughter, dogs, and cats. I also enjoy being active and love the ritual of going to the gym. Otherwise, you might find me preoccupied with brewing the perfect cup of coffee.

name: Sprout Therapy PDX
approaches:
Our Background and Approach
We know how hard it is to finding someone who gets it. Who’s relatable and real. Who’s going to be there to weather your storms and get down and dirty with the weird stuff that makes us all human. Who you can laugh with as well as cry. We just want you to know- we are here to help.
More About the People We Treat
We work with young adults, kids, teens, families, and couples of any kind. Our team works with trauma, anxiety, depression, and many other treatment issues. We are an LGBTQIA+ allied practice and identify as progressive and anti-racist. We are now accepting Oregon Health Plans (OHP) clients with authorization. Contact us for more information on your specific coordinated care organization.

name: Katherine Alexander (SHE / HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I like people. Teacher, coach, cross-cultural worker, and ministry leader are all career hats I have worn over the years. I grew up in a large family and understand the challenges that families face daily. For a decade I lived and worked outside the U.S. in different parts of Asia and Africa. I have learned to think globally and to appreciate ways of thinking, living, and worshiping beyond my own. Some of my clients include: -Those who want to change the trajectory and legacy of their families. -Couples who want to better understand themselves and their partners. -Those who are preparing for and adjusting to a new cultural context. -Helping professionals such as teachers, nurses, ministry leaders, etc. -Those who are curious about personal growth and healing.
Why I Became a Therapist
A little bit of support can go along way. I consider working with families, couples, and individuals to be sacred. Providing a safe and non judgemental space for others to explore their past, present and future is a privilege. When I am not with clients I enjoy cooking, hiking, gardening, family time, and adventures.

name: Desiree Guzman
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I am solution focused. One of my previous places of employment involved training new staff. This was invaluable experience for me because it taught me the need to adapt my training style to the needs of the people that I worked with. Each person that I work with is unique and requires a slightly different approach. Some individuals prefer assigned homework and other individuals may prefer to work in session on suggested techniques. For this reason, I routinely check in with people that I work with to see what is and is not working in our sessions.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Most of my training and experience has been in working with individuals who have experienced trauma. I previously worked at a homeless shelter where I worked with individuals who struggled with a broad range of challenges that included substance use, mental health issues, job loss, or were fleeing a domestic violence situation. I have hospice and home health experience as well, and have worked at agencies where I primarily worked with couples and provided life coaching as well.

name: Heidi Scanlan
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Heidi specializes in the use of Eye movement Desensitization Reprocessing (EMDR), Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), and solution focused treatment. Heidi also utilizes mindfulness practices and EFT tapping with her clients. Heidi has been noted to have a very warm energy that is a joy to be around. She brings her quick wit and questioning spirit to every session. I specialize in working with adults who are navigating past trauma and distressing events. I provide a direct, results-oriented approach to help you achieve your goals more rapidly. Additionally, I offer specialized support for individuals in the postpartum phase, guiding them through the unique challenges that arise during this critical time.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Graduating from University of Washington in 2012 with her Masters in Social Work Heidi Scanlan is a Licensed Clinical Social Worker, therapist, clinical supervisor, and coach. Heidi has worked as a specialist in medical social work, mental health, and crisis services. Heidi is fascinated with the mind and body connection and each person’s ability to transform and become better versions of themselves. Heidi is also training as a pilates instructor and working towards certification as she believes so strongly in the healing harmony of mind and body. Heidi loves being a therapist because she enjoys seeing clients light up with insights about themselves and their situations that forever change them. In her off time Heidi values traveling to sunny places, spending time with her large extended family, trying new cuisine, learning about different cultures, and being immersed in nature.

name: Clea Partridge (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that all humans have the right and ability to reach their fullest potential and embrace wellbeing. To remove blocks to wellbeing, I practice an integrated approach to counseling. I incorporate polyvagal and somatic therapies, interpersonal neurobiology, and existential therapy from a humanistic base. Practicing from a humanistic base maintains a client-centered focus, allowing clients to determine their own goals and be experts on their own lives. My philosophy also incorporates themes of liberation, feminism, and client wholeness. Clients are encouraged to explore their relationship with these themes on an interpersonal and intrapersonal level. I graduated from PSU with a Masters in Counseling in 2023 where I engaged in independent study in ecotherapy, including a focus on healing from climate change-related mental health issues.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I came to counseling after many years of farming and managing restaurants in and around Portland. Eventually, I wanted my skills and passion for connecting with others to make a deeper impact. When I learned about ecotherapy, I realized I could combine my interest in helping people with my love of nature by tapping into the power of the human-nature connection. I have spent many years doing my own therapeutic work and believe strongly that this work can help us improve our relationship with ourselves and others. I use she/her pronouns and identify as a white, straight, cis-woman. I am currently able-bodied. I am a Pacific Northwest local and love this region for its trees, water, birds, and swimming season. I enjoy biking, hiking, camping and backpacking, birding, drinking coffee in the sun, reading novels, and cuddling/harassing my cat.

name: A Healing Intention LLC
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Here at A Healing Intention, LLC, therapy is about owning all aspects of who we are in an accepting and empowering fashion. Through the therapeutic process, individuals are encouraged to discover the joy of being authentic and self-accepting through a process of becoming self-aware.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
One of the most important relationships that we will have in our lives is actually with ourselves. Therapy allows individuals the ability to discover ways to realize who they are and then become congruent in who they are both on the outside as well as the inside.

name: Kelsey Huber (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach incorporates being your advocate while working to challenge you to bring about change and growth. I endorse acceptance of the self by digging into the why's of behavior. I am trained in EMDR and have done extensive research regarding somatic and behavioral therapies. I conduct trauma informed care and social justice oriented care in order to bring about regulation to the body. I bring in aspects of motivational interviewing, somatic therapy, person-centered, relational, psychodynamic theory, narrative therapy, and existential therapy to customize a therapeutic approach that would be most beneficial to you as an individual. I am always open to feedback from my clients in order to shift what might not be working for them so we can make this the most effective use of your time.
My Values as a Therapist
Working with clients in their healing journey is the highest honor. It's my personal belief that we all have the strength to change, grow, and heal. We will work together to design and implement a plan to heal and help you live life to your fullest potential. I would love for you to send a message sharing a bit about what you want support in as well as times that work to connect. Looking forward to meeting and working with you! I spend my off time as Treasurer and board member of The Equi Institute, a nonprofit working to enrich the health of the Trans, Queer, Gender Diverse and Intersex communities through trauma-informed and culturally affirming care and social justice advocacy. I am originally from LA, but have found my home in the Pacific Northwest. I love baseball, all things horror, painting, traveling, weightlifting, and listening to the rain while enveloped in a good book.

name: Bri Puetz (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
I have nearly 10 years of experience working as a therapist in both community mental health and private practice settings. I hold a Master of Science in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from Western Washington University. I'm licensed at the state level, hold designation of National Certified Counselor from the National Board for Certified Counselors, and am also a Level II EMDR trained clinician. Specialized post-degree formal training has included Eye-Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR) training and supervision through the EMDRIA approved Institute for Creative Mindfulness, and Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR). In addition to my clinical work, I have served as an adjunct instructor for the Psychology Department at Columbus State Community College.
My Approach to Helping
A compassionate therapeutic relationship is the foundation of my practice. This space is client-centered, collaborative, and fosters curiosity and non-judgmental challenging. I aim to address barriers and underlying factors contributing to clients’ current concerns, and equip them with the tools and insight to confidently navigate life moving forward. My approach blends evidence-based therapies, including cognitive-behavioral based therapies, mindfulness-based therapies, and EMDR. These therapies focus on increasing awareness of thoughts, emotions, and behaviors while developing practical strategies to respond to them effectively, building insight around unhelpful patterns and practicing alternatives, fostering self-compassion while unlearning self-limiting beliefs, identifying and communicating underlying needs, processing past experiences impacting the present, and aligning actions with values to live more intentionally and to create meaningful change.

name: Chantal Miniet (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I view counseling as a journey of facilitated self-exploration to increase the level of self-awareness and understanding that helps the individual better navigate the challenges they are facing. I work from a client centered perspective, adapting to each individual's needs. I believe every person is different, and therefore, my modalities should adapt to the needs of the individual. I act as a facilitator for the individual to discover their own strengths, modes of coping, spiritual belief systems, and creativity. Ultimately, it is my job to help individuals identify, develop, and mobilize their resources to successfully find ways to meet their own needs. I also aim to help my clients cope with and process past trauma.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I strive to create a safe and compassionate environment for my clients. I believe that if we gain insight into our behaviors and change the way that we talk to ourselves, we can change our lives. I believe everyone has unique strengths that when realized, can create healing and lasting change. Life is hard and our experiences over time create ways of coping that helped us survive when we needed it but now now no longer serve us. I'm a therapist who has done (and continue to do) my own therapy to overcome past challenges. I am oriented towards a social justice lens in counseling, and utilize intersectional feminist theories.

name: Donnella Wood (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I understand that having a relationship in one’s life that is free of judgement and infused with compassion is essential in a healing process. When we feel truly seen, heard, and received, parts of us that have gotten injured or shut down have an opportunity to arise, be tended to with kindness and transform. These parts no longer need to be annexed and a more integrated way of being can emerge. The heart of my work involves helping you listen deeply to yourself and trust your own experience. I meet you where you are and work collaboratively to attend to your individual needs. Together, we’ll invite curiosity and the body as guides for our work. I believe that the body can be a central guide in healing. When you attend to the body and have the courage to establish a trusting relationship with your body, the potential for a more sustaining and integrative healing can happen.
My Approach to Helping
My broad-based education has helped me engage innovative body-based and mindfulness practices that support well-being, body integration, and resiliency. I hold a Master’s degree in professional mental health counseling from Lewis and Clark College, am a Registered Somatic Movement Therapist, a long-time meditator, and have advanced training in Somatic Experiencing Trauma Healing. For over twenty years I have supported people to listen more deeply to themselves, cultivate body awareness, and develop the inner resources and tools needed to trust their experience and live with more ease and authenticity. I offer counseling, movement education & therapy, and coaching services. My practice is open and inclusive. I embrace the Health at Every Size model and have a weight-neutral orientation. I would welcome the opportunity to talk with you about how I might be of service. Please visit my website or don't hesitate to contact me for a free consult.

name: Tina Lilly, MS LPC
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to counseling is based on the belief that we are here to wake up to the fullest expression of ourselves. What we may feel instead is a poignant dissatisfaction with our career, partner, body, or self. I draw from insight-oriented spiritual traditions and contemporary body-mind practices to support you in your quest for meaning, purpose, authenticity, and connection.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My training includes a Master's degree in counseling (PSU), EMDR, post-graduate training in EFT, sex therapy, hypnosis, and other modalities. I am also a writer, book author, and meditation teacher.

name: Slade Wolf (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Firstmost, I use science to guide my practice so that all the work I do is backed by decades of strong research, indicating that it makes couples and individuals healthier. Specifically, when working with couples I use a behavioral skill-building approach. We will learn and practice effective communication skills so that you can resolve your conflicts respectfully and with increasing amounts of emotional intimacy. I use cognitive-behavioral therapy to help individuals conquer their depression and anxiety and learn the skills necessary to keep those symptoms at bay. I also specifically help men to improve their emotional wisdom and well-being, overcome anger and/or depression, and be happier and healthier humans, for themselves and their loved ones.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I earned my master of social work degree at University of Washington in 2000. I have worked in a variety of settings as a clinical social worker: -Doing drug and alcohol counseling at methadone clinics -Providing psychotherapy at a community based mental health clinic -Program lead at Clackamas County Mental Health in a program for young adults who had developed schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, borderline personality disorder or other severe and persistent mental illnesses and who were also suffering with drug dependency -Coordinator for a program at Oregon City High School for students with social and emotional needs -Owner of Oregon City Counseling and co-owner of Men’s Resource Center, both of which focused on providing Domestic Violence Intervention groups for men -Currently the owner of Oregon City Counseling where I continue my work with men and have expanded to doing work with couples and helping individuals with anxiety and depression

name: Michael Caylor (HE / HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that everyone is the expert of their own experience, and as your counseling intern and student, I am here to help you explore and achieve your goals in a way that makes sense to you. Our experiences shape us. They impact our view of self and our values, which can lead us to feel disconnected from self and others. I would love to sit down with you to get to know you and understand your life experiences in a way that values where you come from, and how you see the world. I view counseling as a collaborative process in which we will work together to address discomforts and focus on areas you want to grow in which can include your goals and aspirations. I want to help you address stressors and pain, while collaboratively developing solutions. It is my goal to provide you with a safe, comfortable, and non-judgmental environment as well as a genuine, positive, and empathetic relationship to work through these things. I am here for you and believe in your ability to grow.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
Finding a counselor that works for you can be difficult! As a counseling intern and student, I will come to the meeting a blank slate with your best interest in mind. It is my goal to get to know you as best I can to help you identify and understand your emotions and experiences on a deeper level. This deepening of knowledge can help shift your perspective on your emotions, behaviors, and yourself. Then, together, we can determine if action is appropriate. If this sounds helpful or you would like to know more, please feel free to reach out for a free consultation. I look forward to seeing you!

name: Spencer Uemura (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In session, I draw from narrative, compassion-focused, embodied, and anti-oppressive approaches as well as my own lived experience of marginalization. If your cultural experience has been left out of therapy in the past, I can be a good fit. I have received training in approaches such as psychoanalysis, CBT, DBT, Narrative Therapy, and somatic experiencing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
We are all essentially good, loving, creative, and courageous, but oppression hinders our ability to see that and act from that deep truth. With unconditional support, we can release the distress, nourish our resilience, and live out our values. I am a lifelong learner and engage in my own emotional healing work as a foundation of providing good therapy to others. Contact me through our group practice, Second Growth Counseling, and our team will support you through the set-up process. I look forward to working with you!

name: Ashlee Adams (THEY/SHE)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
With training in various therapy modalities, including TF-CBT, IFS, and NET, I tailor my approach to each client’s unique needs, focusing on what works best for their system. This is where we collaborate to discover what feels right for you. Additionally, I offer pre-surgical assessments for individuals seeking gender-affirming surgery.

name: Anne Thompson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
My therapy style is warm and interactive. I am compassionate, curious, trauma-informed, and social-justice oriented. I believe that counseling support helps people feel less isolated and alone when facing lifes difficulties. My approach combines emotionally focused approaches, cognitive-behavioral methods and a depth and attachment perspective to help you achieve self-awareness and healing. Together we will define goals and objectives to meet your unique and specific needs. I am committed to helping my clients experience change outside the therapy room. I look forward to working with you!

name: Anna Schaum
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Maybe you've tried coaching or therapy before, but were frustrated by a lack of action, assertiveness, empathy, or engagement by the practitioner. Your family history isn't necessarily short on trauma, nevertheless you survived and even thrived. You sense that if you could understand it all better life would improve, personally and professionally. I get it. I was a world class professional symphonic musician before becoming a personal leadership coach and therapist, and understand how to help you successfully leverage the complementary powers of attuned listening and deliberate practice. My ideal clients are high achievers searching for meaning, direction, community, support through loss, and/or renewed purpose.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I use a unique blend of self awareness tools to help you tune in to your own creative flow. We'll reduce your stress, clarify your intentions, define what success means now, and lay the groundwork for your next right steps. Equally comfortable with teams and individuals, I've been called a "team whisper" more than once. Feel free to contact me to discuss how I can help you and/or your team thrive.

name: Lori Eckel (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have trust in your ability to find healing and connection to your own sense of possibility, power, and choice whatever your circumstances might be. We can work together to discover meaning in living and dying, and to connected to what is most important to you. I bring a trauma-informed, anti-oppressive, anti-racist approach in my work and aim to be responsive and to affirm people of all races, genders, ages, abilities, and identities. There are many forces that contribute to health disparities and inequity. I bring mindful awareness to the way these experiences create vulnerability.

name: The Hope Connection Counseling Services
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Our therapists and interns are trained in numerous theories and can work with all of life's challenges. We have a mind-body holistic approach to our treatment facility. You can read each person's bio on our website or you can reach out by email or phone and our intake coordinator will assist you with finding the right therapist for you. We have offices in SW Portland and in Newberg.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Specialties include: PTSD, Eating Disorders, Men's and Woman's Issues, Anxiety, Depression, Loss and Grief, Play Therapy, Child-Parent Relationship Therapy , Prepare/Enrich Assessment, Sex Trafficking, Illness and End of life stages

name: Osakpolo Uwoghiren
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Osakpolo Uwoghiren is a board-certified Psychiatric-Mental Health Nurse Practitioner trained to provide a wide range of mental health services to patients and families. She has worked with a diverse population of clients throughout her years of clinical experience in inpatient psychiatric settings, outpatient psychiatric settings, correctional settings, and psychiatric emergency rooms. She enjoyed her role and take the initial interest in client. Her optimal goal is to provide the highest level of functioning. She strongly believes in an integrated comprehensive approach to psychiatric care and my core belief is focused on patient centered philosophy. We worked together to meet your specific goals and needs. We are here for your needs and value you as individual. Contact me today.

name: Sarah Hart
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is dynamic, collaborative, and Humanistic. I work from a culturally-informed & inclusive model & incorporate all aspects of an individual’s identity & experiences into treatment. My specialties include anxiety, depression, identity, Autism & relationships. I am uniquely suited to work with entrepreneurs and people in the Service Industry.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Training I hold a master’s degree in Counseling Psychology from Saybrook University. Coursework included counseling theories, individual, group and couple’s therapy, career and lifestyle planning, assessment, diagnosis, using research to inform the practice of counseling, human development and professional ethics. I also hold a Master’s degree in English from the University of Oregon. I currently train with Gestalt Associates Training Los Angeles (GATLA) in Seattle and have trained in the GATLA European Summer Residential Couples workshop.

name: Bohemian Counseling, LLC (KARI HAYS)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
As a therapist, I followed a non-traditional route to this field. I spent two years in the Peace Corps in Panamá , where I worked with youth and young adults with disabilities. Upon returning to the United States, I continued my work with individuals with disabilities in the classroom, vocational rehabilitation and mental health fields. I hold a Master of Rehabilitation Counseling (M.R.C.) from Texas Tech University and a Master of Arts in Special Education (M.A.-SpEd) from New Mexico Highlands University. I am currently a PhD student in a Counselor Education and Supervision program. I hold certificates in Brainspotting (Phase 1), Mindfulness-Based Cognitive Therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, and Animal-Assisted Therapy. I am a Licensed Professional Counselor (C6460) and I am committed to offering professional and affordable counseling to fellow Oregonians.

name: Rebecca Garetz (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is rooted in evidence-based practices, including Exposure Therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), and Somatic Therapy. Together, we will gently and effectively work through challenges, helping you build resilience and confidence. Exposure Therapy can support you in confronting fears in a safe and structured way, ACT encourages embracing life’s uncertainties with openness and values-driven action, and Somatic Therapy connects the mind and body for deeper healing. Throughout our work, I bring a spirit of gentle compassion and humor, recognizing that healing takes a lot of courage and vulnerability.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I became a counselor because of my interest in how we can feel safe in a world that has a lot of uncertainty.Growth and healing often require stepping into the unknown, facing discomfort, and embracing change with courage. The brain has an incredible ability to adapt and rewire itself—a phenomenon known as neuroplasticity. Exposure Therapy is a powerful tool in this process, helping retrain the brain to experience safety in situations that once felt threatening. When we avoid feared situations, our brain receives the message that they are dangerous, reinforcing anxiety, and pain. Over time, avoidance strengthens the belief that we are not capable of facing our fears. Through gradual, guided exposure, we work together to challenge these patterns, allowing your brain to recognize that you can tolerate discomfort and that many fears are not as threatening as they seem. This process helps break the cycle of avoidance and fosters a sense of empowerment, resilience, and confidence.

name: Stacy Sheffler (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am gender queer and focus on therapeutic work with the LGBTQ community and am transgender affirming and aware. I have focused my work with the LGBTQ community, but also very comfortable and competent with any other identifying person or couple. I have had a private practice in St John's since 2011. I ascribe to somatic, trauma focused treatment and practice EMDR. I also ascribe to cognitive based modalities as well and focus on understanding attachment styles and how they influence our relationships with others.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I earned my bachelor's in psychology and criminal justice at Indiana University and my Masters in counseling at Eastern Michigan University. I spent several years working with adolescent girls struggling with substance use and 8 years working with adolescent boys at a residential treatment center focusing on sex abuse and trauma. In my private practice since 2011 I have worked with individuals, couples and families and focus on having a queer, trans, non binary affirming practice.

name: HEATHER MATHEWSON (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Background
I am a former middle school counselor in the Portland area, and due to my desire to help this age group more in depth, I have switched to counseling full time. As a school counselor, I led Anti Racism lessons, and cultivated an LGBTQIA+ inclusive space. I helped students navigate: Anxiety Depression ADHD Self-esteem/confidence building School/life balance Social challenges Life and school transitions Time management I am also well versed in helping families connect with school counselors for additional support in school as needed.
My Approach to Helping
