In my practice I use a person-focused approach. This means I trust my client to know themselves best, and my job is to help guide them in their own self discovery. I also use techniques from reality therapy: to help set achievable goals, and to provide a framework to reset when things go awry.

name: Aleks Taylor (THEY/HE)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My practice is a combination of transpersonal and humanistic-relational therapy through an intersectional and harm-reduction lens. You can find out more about these models in the "specialties" section. My belief that you are the expert of your own life extends into the therapy room. I will work with you to foster an environment of safety and stability, where you feel comfortable working through whatever has brought you to therapy at this time. My hope is that you leave the therapy room with the feeling that you can navigate the world with confidence and authenticity, despite the challenges it throws at you. I also provide psychedelic integration. I approach integration from a holistic lens that works with all facets of your experience. All feelings about your journey are welcome here. I use theory and practice that is queer/trans affirming, neurodivergent-affirming, antiracist, sex and kink-positive, monogamous and non-monogamous relationship affirming, and person-centered.
My Values as a Therapist
As a therapist, I view my clients and their situations through an intersectional, social justice lens. This means considering all the societal factors that apply when addressing mental health concerns. Socioeconomic status, race, gender, age, sexual orientation, ethnicity, education, ability, and many more factors should all be considered in therapy, and when they are left out, those who are most vulnerable can slip through the cracks or get misdiagnosed. Together, we can work to address some of the root causes of suffering from external sources as we also work to build and maintain a healthier version of you, internally. When I'm not providing therapy, I spend time working with a racial equity group in the jam band scene as part of the BIPOC advisory committee and working as a volunteer trainer, supervisor, and integration coach for a psychedelic support line.

name: Caitlynn Hill (THEY/SHE)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I approach therapy from a Cognitive Behavioral and Humanistic perspective. Sessions with me are relational, focused on deep understanding, and recognize that you are the expert of your own life! As your therapist, I see myself as walking along the path with you. You lead the way but I am there to help clear the weeds and find the best path forward.
My Values as a Therapist
I consider my personal and professional values to be one in the same. They include: - To accountably dismantle racism and other oppressions in myself and the mental health profession. - The inherent worth and dignity of every being, community, and relationship. - The right to free and responsible search for truth and meaning for every being and community. - Respect for every being and community as my liberation is tied to theirs.

name: Colin Wolf (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My Master's degree in Integral Counseling Psychology is enhanced by training in Brainspotting, Narrative Exposure Therapy, Pain Reprocessing Therapy and polyvagal approaches. My clinical approach is trauma-informed and integrates psychodynamic, existential-humanistic, CBT/ACT and mindfulness-based perspectives and strategies that are customized to your preferences and needs. My therapeutic style is fundamentally relational. I'm your confidante and ally. We'll explore your thoughts and emotions, and how you relate with yourself and others, to increase your awareness of the patterns and stories of your life and transform limitations into resources. Discover new perspectives, life strategies and ways to move through anxiety, depression or numbness to feel more connected and grounded, with increased ability to flexibly respond to life stressors. I'll help you know (and love!) yourself more, experience deeper connections in your relationships, and feel more ease and joy.

name: Majken Elek, MA
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I utilize a creative integrative approach, centered in mindfulness, somactics and attachment, to promote increased consciousness of thoughts, values, needs, and mind/body behavior patterns. Observing our emotional self awareness increases our ability to cope with stress, relationships, and life challenges. I will collaborate with you to move toward balance, wholeness, and a more integrated self. Free 30 min phone consultation and sliding scale fees. I am located within the Portland Insight Meditation Center. I do not accept insurance at this time, however, I can offer out of network billing and we can work together to find a session pricing that is realistic for you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold a Masters in Counseling Psychology from Pacific University. I hold a BA from the University of Oregon. I have training in Vipassana Meditation, completing a 10 day silent meditation course in 2013. Additional training: CBT, DBT, MI, & CRM. I have clinical experience with addictions, and refugee populations and group experience in: mindfulness relapse prevention, grief, therapeutic journal writing, & mental health cultural adjustment. My credentials are also informed by my life experience. I have offered various support services while working in the education, corporate and non-profit fields for the past 15 years. This included serving a wide variety of ages, cultures. With these experiences I have gleaned valuable and applicable insight and wisdom which inform my practice giving me a holistic, open worldview. I’ve been told I have a warm, calm, stable presence. Hobbies: gardening & astrology. Places lived: Cannon Beach, Denmark, Boston, NY.

name: Katharine Frissora (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Having personally navigated my own mental health journey, I understand how overwhelming it can feel. Those experiences have shaped my compassionate, personalized approach to care. Mental health isn’t one-size-fits-all, and I honor each person’s unique needs. Whether through mindfulness, lifestyle shifts, or creative outlets like art therapy, I help clients build treatment plans that feel truly aligned with who they are. I strive to create a safe, judgment-free space where individuals can explore emotions, build resilience, and develop practical tools to manage daily stress. While medication can be a helpful part of treatment, I also emphasize holistic approaches—nutrition, movement, and mind-body practices—to support whole-person healing. My goal is to not only address symptoms but also uncover root causes, helping clients find balance, strength, and lasting well-being. Healing is a journey, and I’m here to walk alongside my clients every step of the way.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that each of us, as individuals, is doing the best we can in any given moment, shaped by our unique experiences and personal circumstances. We are the best experts of our own bodies and lives, and it’s important to honor that truth. Compassion—both for ourselves and for one another—is a powerful healing force, something the world could always benefit from a little more of.

name: James Reling (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I specialize in working with "late-diagnosed" Autistic and ADHD adults, offering support in your exploration of neurodivergence. Being neurodivergent myself, I can empathize with the challenges you might face, and that is why I started offering Autism/ADHD assessments in the first place. My program includes: Thorough Assessment: We delve into your experiences and traits, guided by your perspective. Neurodiversity-Affirming Testing: An in-depth evaluation to confirm your neurodiversity. Personalized Guidance: Tailoring support to address how your neurotype impacts various aspects of your life. Future Roadmap: Providing recommendations and resources to guide your next steps. I'm committed to creating a stigma-free and safe space for your neurodivergent self-discovery. You don't have to go through this alone. Feel free to reach out to initiate your assessment process by going to my website and scheduling a consultation.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I'm an autistic individual myself, which gives me a unique perspective. I've faced denial and struggled with my own misconceptions and stereotypes about autism. I also understand the societal stigma around neurodivergence, making it challenging to discuss with family and friends. I've experienced a lifetime of wanting to connect with others but often feeling misunderstood, leading to strained relationships. Most of my clients come to me when they are looking for answers to better understand themselves so they can lead more fulfilling lives. As a therapist, I prioritize your goals and won't push specific interventions like social skills training unless you express a desire for them. I'm here to listen, help you process, and choose what you want in your life. I'm open to exploring your special interests, understanding their importance, and using them to aid in emotional expression.

name: Jeff Guenther (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Many clients believe it is the therapist’s job to make them feel better. While feeling better may be the outcome of counseling, it is not always the case in every situation. The therapist’s main role is to help the client feel the emotion more clearly and to perhaps provide insight as to why it is surfacing so intensely. Individual therapy is a safe place to experience emotions, no matter what they are or how they manifest. It may seem counterintuitive to think of therapy as not making you feel better, but if one can fully allow and accept an emotion without duress (which can prompt unhealthy or destructive reactions), then one can learn to handle stronger emotions in the future. If a client develops the capacity to endure intense negative emotions, then a process of normalizing and desensitizing occurs, thereby reducing the tendency to react and/or take action against uncomfortable feelings.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a very strong background in family and couples work. I received my master’s degree in marriage and family therapy from the University of Southern California, and my bachelor’s degree in child and family development from San Diego State University. Prior to going into private practice, I worked in the public school system providing individual, group, and family therapy to high-risk students. I also taught parenting classes on a regular basis. My therapeutic career started out at a crisis line here in Portland, where I mainly worked with people who were suffering from suicidal thoughts and severe anxiety. I have lived in Portland since 2005 and the bulk of my work focuses on seeing couples and individuals in my private practice.

name: Leticia Meza (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a bilingual therapist offering therapy in English and Spanish (and Spanglish works too!). As a Latina woman, I have a deep connection to and understanding of the Latino/x/e community. I am passionate about providing culturally specific care that honors and integrates each client's unique background and experiences into our therapeutic work.
My Values as a Therapist
I want to acknowledge the courage it takes to prioritize your mental health, whether you are continuing your healing journey or starting therapy for the first time. I understand that seeking support can be challenging and requires a lot of strength, so I am glad you’re here! I view the therapeutic relationship as a collaborative partnership where we work together to support your growth and healing. In that I also honor that we are all the experts in our own life and that my role as a therapist is simply to walk alongside you and create a safe and supportive space that can help you meet your goals and needs.

name: The Center for Couples & Sex Therapy
approaches:
Our Approach to Helping
We understand relationships. Whether you’re struggling with communication, sexual connection, or infidelity, our expert therapists know how to help you find your way back to each other. It’ll require some hard work, but we’re here to help you do the heavy lifting. Individual Therapy is an incredible way to invest in the relationship you have with yourself and with others. Whether you’re partnered or single, young or old, sex-savvy or sex-scared, we would be honored to partner with you on your journey to creating more connection and pleasure in your life.

name: Jane Mayer (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I tailor my approach to the needs of each individual client. My training is in psychodynamic psychotherapy, which helps me to grasp the impact of your life's experiences and their impact on your way of being in the world, your relationship to yourself and others. My additional training includes work with grief work, attachment theory and somatic-based therapies which help me frame for you how you interact with your feelings, beliefs, assumptions and choices. My interpersonal style is active, engaged, honest and humorous. Most of my clients report feeling deeply supported and challenged in their work with me.
More About the People I Treat
My specialties include grief and loss work for people facing loss of loved ones to death or life changes, illness in themselves or recovering from life in dysfunctional/alcoholic families. I specialize in working with adults who are grieving the death of their life partner/spouse. I became a psychotherapist because I love the life journey, despite how harrowing, joyous and confusing it feels at times. A lifelong seeker of health and resilience, I first pursued my own growth work (and continue to) and then entered social work to develop reliable skills to help others find health, choice and possibility. I have been licensed and in practice since 2000. My additional training has been in grief and loss work, sensorimotor/somatic psychotherapy and in parenting education.

name: Sasha Rosenfels, MA, LMFT (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My specialty is relationship-related issues, which most often include your relationships with your partner, your children, as well as your own parents. We'll explore the ways these relationships impact you, expand your awareness of unhealthy patterns of interaction, increase connection, and learn to thrive instead of just survive.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Our life experiences hold the all the extremes; immense joy and profound love as well as overwhelming frustration and emotional struggle. I am incredibly grateful to be able to offer support to individuals as they navigate the huge ups and downs of this complex journey.

name: Lili Micaela
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I specialize in Women's Issues across the lifespan. I walk beside you as you discover and amplify your growing edges. And I offer support as you seek solace and mending for experiences where society blunts female potential and breaks our hearts. Being witnessed in compassion can bring relief. My hope is that our work together opens the way for new choices, self-knowlege, and sovereignty. My formal educational background includes Psychology, Botany, Counseling, and Expressive Arts. I have provided therapy in community clinics, Employee Assistance Program, and private practice. Prioritizing collaboration and empowerment, I individualize sessions to match each client’s unique needs. My commitment is to provide a safe space to assist you in identifying and exploring your concerns, and then, with respect and compassion, accompany you to resolution.
My Values as a Therapist
My psychotherapy practice is called ‘Mended & Stronger'. This name is a reference to kintsugi, the ancient art of mending broken pottery using material containing precious metals. Practiced in Japan, Korea, and China, a vessel repaired in this way was highly valued. Philosophically, I see kintsugi as an honoring of the sundered, the incomplete, the subdued that lives within ourselves and within our world. Our faults, our mistakes, our regrets, our heartache, can be mended, and they need not be so hidden. They can even be treasured. Think of this for yourself: Our wounds and our healing make us stronger, just as the golden joinery on pottery. We can alchemize the gains from past experiences into the beauty that is Mended & Stronger. I identify as an Elder, a Lesbian, a Survivor, a woman, raised working class. I am a white comrade who longs for liberation for all, currently investigating more about my Jewish and my Irish heritages.

name: Patience McGinnis (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapy approach is tailored to each individual’s needs with an emphasis on honoring every person’s unique cultural identity.

name: Skylar Windham
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am not one who finds utility in elaborate hypotheses filled with flowery psychobabble. I work primarily within frameworks of Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, as well as Existential Therapy, and I consider psychodynamics to be a crucial component of the therapeutic process.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I'm Skylar first, therapist second. This means that while I take professionalism and the American Counseling Association's Code of Ethics very seriously, I bring my authentic self to each session. The relationships I form with clients are real, and necessarily so, because it is through genuine human connection that clients find their way. I am LGBTQ+ affirming and welcome clients from all walks of life, experiences and identities. I grew up on the southern Oregon coast and I am a member of an indigenous community, the Tolowa Dee-Ni Nation. I have taught in Oregon public schools, traveled throughout various countries as an ESL teacher, worked in port administration as well as local politics. I enjoy spending time in nature, dancing Argentine tango, sailing and frolicking with my corgi Scuttle.

name: Stuart Malkin (HE/HIM/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am now seeing clients in person! One of the most important factors that predicts successful therapy is the relationship between us. Our working alliance must allow you to trust, feel safe, and feel open. I strive to attune and collaborate with you to help build a safe, authentic connection that will serve your healing and growth. I incorporate somatic and 'bottom-up' based approaches of Hakomi, Lifespan Integration, Recreation of Self (RC-S), attachment work, trauma resourcing. In couple's work, I use a combination of PACT (Psychobiological Approach to Couples Therapy), Gottman, experiential and mindfulness, and compassionate communication/NVC.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I've been in practice since 2015 and have been specializing in mindfulness-based depth work , trauma resourcing, and attachment. Most recently I completed Level 2 of Lifespan Integration, which is helpful in processing childhood wounding. I completed a Masters Degree in Counseling in Clinical Mental Health at Portland State University. In addition, I completed a year-long internship practicing mindfulness-based, somatic therapy approaches with the M.E.T.A Counseling Clinic. Previous to my internship, I had completed the two-year M.E.T.A. Comprehensive Training program. I also assisted in teaching the M.E.T.A Comprehensive year one program in 2015 to deepen my training.

name: Emma Stern (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a therapist, I am warm, real, relatable, and come with a sense of humor. I am committed to being honest and while I will hold you accountable in our work, I will always honor your discernment and expect you to be the expert on yourself. I specialize in work with complex trauma; anxiety; grief; codependency; premenstrual dysphoric disorder (PMDD); recovering from perfectionism and people pleasing; and recovery from burnout during late-stage capitalism and global pandemic. I work from a psychodynamic and somatic lens that combines traditional talk therapy with mind-body inquiry and education about the brain, body, and nervous system. “Feelings” are literally physical manifestations of the brain and nervous system’s response to emotional stimuli. Through developing a cognitive understanding of the mind-body connection, we find manageable ways to face challenges and cultivate deeper compassion for ourselves and others in times of distress.
My Approach to Helping
I work from a trauma-informed lens. As the body can be a source of pain and oppression, I hold somatic work with care. I also understand that the medical industrial complex can be harmful and am committed to normalizing and advocating for mental health care. Coming from a blended family that is White, Black, Latinx, and Jewish, I am committed to questioning identity dynamics, power, privilege, and positionality. I welcome the opportunity to work with clients from different backgrounds and seek to maintain humility and sensitivity in work with clients who hold different identities. I am dedicated to social justice and will support you in using your personal healing journey to work towards collective liberation and social change. I am EMDR trained. I am a yoga practitioner and instructor (E-RYT 500, YACEP) and upon interest will support you in bringing yoga philosophy and asana into your healing journey. Working alongside you is an honor and privilege I will treat with deep respect.

name: Adrian Larsen Sanchez
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I approach therapy as a collaboration, rooted in understanding each person as an individual in the context of their family, history, culture and daily life. I particularly value the vital role of deepening insight into emotional issues, developing satisfying relationships and realistic self-esteem for healing, growth and sustainable symptom relief. I primarily practice psychoanalytic psychotherapy, an evidence based treatment, though I am also able to integrate techniques from other therapy models (e.g. cognitive and dialectical behavioral therapy). [Read more about psychoanalytic psychotherapy here: http://www.apsa.org/content/psychoanalytic-psychotherapy.] As a part of my practice, I also offer collaborative/therapeutic psychological assessment (testing). In collaborative/therapeutic assessment, patients are engaged to develop questions about themselves to be addressed through the assessment. Validated psychological tests are used to help illuminate underlying difficulties.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I earned my doctorate in clinical psychology at Pacific University and completed my clinical internship at Jacobi Medical Center/Albert Einstein College of Medicine in NYC. I completed my postdoctoral fellowship at Kaiser Permanente, NW Division and a fellowship in psychoanalysis at the Institute for Psychoanalytic Education/NYU School of Medicine. I am a graduate of the 4 year psychoanalytic training program at the Institute of Contemporary Psychoanalysis in Los Angeles.

name: THRIVE Clinic
approaches:
All THRIVE clinicians practice cognitive, behavioral, and mindfulness-based interventions that have significant research to support their effectiveness. In general, the relationship between a THRIVE clinician and client is not unlike that between a coach and player. THRIVE clinicians help you identify your goals, define a skills-based approach for attaining your goals, and practice, practice, practice until you no longer need a coach! We focus on the right balance of active engagement (i.e. skills building and practice) and compassionate encouragement (i.e. validation and cheerleading).
Our clinicians are graduates of well-established medical institutions, rigorous clinical psychology departments, and respected social work and counseling programs. Our clinical staff is comprised of LPCs, MAs, MSWs, PhDs, and PsyDs. We also have a psychiatric mental health nurse practitioner (for med management) and a registered dietitian. All of our staff is concurrently practicing at the Portland DBT Institute and is comprehensively trained in DBT.

name: Kimberly Clifford (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
Kim is originally from Colorado, she earned her undergraduate degree from the University of Northern Colorado before attending PA school at Heritage University in Toppenish, Washington. Kim spent seven years in emergency medicine in Portland, Oregon, gaining valuable experience in acute care. She is passionate about volunteering, she works with nonprofits to provide direct medical care to those experiencing houselessness. She believes in always meeting her patients where they are at regardless of their current situation. Prior to attending PA school Kim worked as a massage therapist, working alongside other integrative medicine practitioners. She has the perspective of working in both the eastern and western medical world. While working in the hospital system she grew frustrated with the large healthcare machine and the lack of holistic interventions. She has now transitioned back to outpatient medicine where she can focus on integrative care for adult ADHD
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
We view ADHD not just as a diagnosis, but as a unique way of being where people experience the world in a non-ordinary way. People with this diagnosis have gifts that inspire new approaches to life's challenges. We understand the difficulties of navigating the modern world with ADHD both personally and professionally. We are dedicated to fostering a supportive environment where people in our community impacted by ADHD can thrive and achieve their full potential.

name: Chell Oak (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is gentle, collaborative, and engaged. I value building trusting relationships with my clients so that they can feel fully held and cared for in their vulnerability. I use a combination of interpersonal process, parts work (IFS), EMDR, and mindfulness-based approaches to inform our work together. I have spent the past seven years gaining expertise in serving the LGBTQ+ community, learning about trauma-informed care, and reckoning with oppressive, colonial systems and their impact on our individual and collective wellbeing. I also have respect and appreciation for psychedelics and have experience supporting individuals integrating their psychedelic experiences.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that mindful self-compassion is a necessary foundation for recovery and growth. By treating our emotions with curiosity, respect, and care rather than burdens to be fixed, we can learn to hold ourselves in our suffering and become a trusted ally to all parts of us. Building self-compassion and self-trust strengthens our resiliency and capacity to move through grief and trauma. I aim to provide nonjudgmental support to help you face your pain, feel your feelings, and reconnect with yourself.

name: Rachel Ward (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to healing encompasses a holistic view of your well-being. It's about more than just medications; it's about addressing the whole person. When appropriate, medication management is integrated into your treatment plan, tailored to your specific needs. But beyond that, we explore the power of exercise, diet, and supplementation to support your mental health. It's about empowering you with tools to thrive in all aspects of your life. We focus on learning new coping strategies that suit your individual challenges, helping you navigate through life's ups and downs with resilience. Additionally, we delve into healing relationship patterns, recognizing the profound impact they have on your overall wellness. By embracing this comprehensive approach, we're dedicated to guiding you towards a balanced and fulfilling life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I grew up in a home surrounded by untreated struggles with mental illness. Today, those same family members are thriving because of proper care. As I saw my family members bloom with the right medications and support, after so many years of improper diagnosis and missed treatment, I realized how great the need was for early detection, treatment, and support of persons struggling with mental illness. I have strong ties to the Christian community, and in the spirit of that faith I aim to support all persons and work every day to be a compassionate ally to all persons regardless of gender/race/sexuality. In my spare time, I love to garden, bake for my family, and play with my service dog, Molly, who you'll get to meet if we have an in person visit.

name: Adam Ellis
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have been offering medication management since 2015 for diverse patients who have a broad range of mental health needs. While serving 20 years as a naval officer, I have developed a strong active listening skills and a high level of consideration and respect for others. I aim to use my experiences to help you optimize wellness, and provide psychiatric care with a trauma-informed and LGBTQIA-informed perspective in mind. I currently treat ages 13+.
More About the People We Treat
Skycloud Mental Health is a clinical placement site for Yale School of Nursing students. We are accepting new patients for medication management via telehealth in OR, WA, UT, AZ, MN, and NM as soon as this week. In-office appointments are available near Portland, OR, with offices in Seattle, WA and Salt Lake City, UT coming soon! Go to our website, skycloudhealth.com, and click "Schedule Now" to register and schedule online. Alternatively, you can call our office at (503) 694-3381, extension 2, to register and schedule over the phone. There is a therapy waitlist available on our website. We are available for single-case agreements with Kaiser. Contact our practice for more info.

name: Sara Penner (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe therapy is a collaboration between therapist and client and there are a number of ways to understand a problem and find solutions. My therapeutic approach is grounded in solution-focused treatment, parts work, cognitive behavioral therapy and mindfulness practice, within the context of a non-judgmental relationship. My strengths are in using curiosity, warmth and humor (when appropriate) to create a safe space for our work together. You are the expert on your own life and I am here to help you explore and process the hard stuff, build upon your own inner resources and introduce new skills to help you navigate your emotions going forward.
More About the People I Treat
I have a long established career supporting kids and teens and with that, their families. I continue to have room in my practice for young people who are available to meet during school hours. However, through this work and my own journey as a parent, I have also developed a passion to support parents. Parenting is hard work! One of the best gifts we can give our children is the gift of taking care of our own mental health. If you feel stuck or like your own stuff is getting in the way of you being the best version of yourself, or that you lost yourself somewhere along the way, we can work to help you recenter and find your true north again. If you find yourself reacting instead of responding or can't seem to ever relax, we can work together to get you back to living a life that is more grounded and centered. I truly love this work and would be happy to talk more if you have any questions.

name: Liz Mungovan (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
You are worthy of the fulfilling and life-affirming relationships that you seek. But it’s possible that your ability to co-create these types of stable connections is impeded by unprocessed past relationship wounds, painful early childhood memories, or struggles relating to your attachment style. Let’s get into it, and journey into the heart of who you were, who you are, and who you’ve yet to become. Together, we’ll gently meet those fragments — I call them “parts” — of yourself that are shut down from past trauma, or still sweaty-palmed from unhealed anxieties or outdated embarrassments. Informed by evidence-based modalities such as Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy and Internal Family Systems, we’ll cultivate a gutsy self-compassion that fuels your resilience, opens you up to fresh possibilities, and empowers you to find — and hold onto — the relationships that you’ve been wishing for.
More About the People I Treat
If you're a young adult (20-35) navigating the aftermath of toxic relationships—romantic, familial, or platonic—let's collaborate to reclaim your power and create a future where you thrive. I enjoy and have experience working with BIPOC and LGBTQ+ communities and see the knowledge and resistance of oppressive systems as an essential part of my work as a licensed therapist. Additionally, if you're navigating complex trauma, regardless of your background, and resonate with my approach, please reach out. I would love to hear from you.

name: Cat Ward (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
During my social work career I have worked providing social and clinical services to children and adolescents in residential facilities, community mental health agencies, intimate partner violence shelters, schools, in the home, and outpatient private practice. I have specialized training in working with neurodivergent children and children who have gone through traumatic experiences such as neglect, abandonment, attachment disruptions, bullying, changes in primary caregivers, physical and/or sexual abuse, and social rejection.
My Approach to Helping
I believe that every child and family has the potential for positive change. My practice is centered on the principle that positive change develops when children and teens believe they are accepted, understood and capable. My approach to helping individuals in therapy is accepting, empathetic, validating and encouraging. I use creative, expressive, playful and engaging techniques based on each client's interests, personal preferences and individual capacities. I welcome each client where they are developmentally and join in each child's world so that together we work towards growth and healing.

name: Kathy Cox (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
With warmth and open curiosity, I guide and support who you are and the concerns and intentions you bring. We make space to hear from the varied influences affecting your experience of life and take time to encourage and tap into the guidance of your inner knowing; mind, body & heart. In taking time to grow awareness of your inner and outer life, room is made for increased connections and understanding, paving the way for new choices and desired shifts. This bringing of focused, caring attention to what surfaces in session helps to improve the ability to notice and make sense of patterns, tend to feelings and sensations, address limiting beliefs, find clarity, discover resources, build trust, and deepen resilience. The work we do unfolds as we attune to and address what matters to you.
My Values as a Therapist
My training includes an M.S. in clinical mental health with a somatic emphasis and 2+years of study in the Hakomi method through M.E.T.A (Mindful Experiential Therapy Approaches). Ongoing studies I engage in include deepening my learning about the relationship between chronic pain/conditions, the mind-body connection, and the role of regulation as we encounter our inner and outer world. I value drawing from approaches that facilitate growth in awareness, self-compassion, self-acceptance, regulation, agency, and resilience. Informed by what arises in our work together, I may incorporate elements from mindfulness-based, body-centered, emotion-focused, relational, psychodynamic, or existential therapies. For the time being, I offer telehealth video sessions only.

name: Kudzai Chifamba (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
She uses CBT to explore the beliefs and thought patterns that may contribute to harmful or unhelpful emotions, behaviors, and symptoms. Kudzai uses person-centered and solution-focused approaches to work collaboratively with clients, helping them uncover their needs and find the best ways to address their concerns so they can live as their most authentic selves. She recognizes that mental health impacts every aspect of a person’s life and works with clients to improve their wellness across different domains so they can feel their best.
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a therapist because of my own struggles with anxiety and trauma. Through therapy, I was able to identify the source of my suffering and begin my healing journey. I hope to do the same for others. I view it as a privilege to be able to work with you in uncovering the root of your own suffering and work together to find a path towards healing. I also came into this field in hopes of providing a safe space for BIPOC folks and people from communities that are often overlooked and/or underserved.

name: Richard Nalbandian (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Dr. Nalbandian completed his internship and post-doctoral residency in Boston, Massachusetts as a fellow at the Danielsen Institute at Boston University. He has focused training in and provides psychotherapy for individuals, couples, and families. Additionally, he completed two years of post-graduate psychoanalytic training at the William Alanson White Institute in NYC for the treatment of Eating Disorders, Compulsions, and Addictions (EDCAS). Dr. Nalbandian's therapeutic style is relational and collaborative.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I approach therapy as a collaboration. I value the cultural context and story of each individual, and am particularly interested in areas of creativity, psychological trauma, immigration/acculturation, group dynamics, grief, and relationship issues. I have in-depth experience working with individuals in the helping profession (i.e., therapists, clergy, and medical professionals), artists, and survivors of trauma. I have experience and interest in exploring vocational pursuits/conflicts and intersectional identity development including neurodiveristy.

name: Kathleen Thompson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have a very process, relational style. I am trained in many different methods to help people understand why we do what we do & to learn betters ways of coping & relating. How we learned to connect to others in our early years impacts us everyday. Let's work on new ways of connecting & being in the world.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have training in Career Counseling, Addiction recovery & relapse prevention; couples therapy & attachment styles; Healing from trauma including EMDR & Lifespan Integration. I also work with adults who are neuro-diverse (Autism) including testing/assessment. Let's work together to build strong relationships or find meaningful work.

name: Alex Harrison (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is to help you explore the unconscious forces contributing to pattern repetition, emotional distress, and disconnection from self and others. I strongly believe we all possess an inner healing intelligence that speaks to us in a variety of ways, and my intention is to facilitate this process by listening deeply and providing space for you to express those parts of you that have been suppressed or discouraged out of fear or out of necessity. I am also trained in somatic therapy and often incorporate techniques like meditation, art, and parts work to help access and integrate difficult life experiences, amplify your intuitive capacity, and remove the blocks keeping you from sharing the gift of your authentic self with the world.

name: Kyle Butler
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Therapy takes courage and honesty. My goal is to facilitate and help navigate the specific and unique obstacles you are presented with. I want to share the right tools and skills that will help you navigate future dilemmas. I am here to help how I can, and have specific focus on depression, anxiety, and self-esteem problems. I believe that healing is a holistic process that involves the mind, body, and spirit. My goal is to create a safe, non-judgmental space where you can explore your thoughts and feelings, uncover the root causes of your struggles, and develop practical strategies for overcoming them.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
While not helping others in therapy, I enjoy reading, talking with friends and family, 3d printing, trying new foods, cooking with my wife, and keeping up with my children’s zest for life. I love trying new things and bettering my relationship with failure. I am more than the skills and usefulness I provide to my relationships and I'm allowed to grow and learn because the only thing where you start on top is digging a hole.

name: Richard Halpern (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My counseling approach is practical and down to earth. My focus is on positive parenting and couple strengthening strategies, which are easy to apply right away. I also work with couples to resolve conflicts and establish ground rules so that you feel seen, heard, and understood. To help ease your parenting anxiety, we develop a structured plan forward, while emphasizing your strengths and parenting style. You deserve a life feeling balanced, energized, and fun! For separated or divorced parents, we focus on co-parenting as a team, even after a difficult breakup. For sole parents or dealing with a relationship ending, we focus on self care and community resources. My practice is affirming to ALL parents and couples from diverse relationship orientations, LGBTQ+ friendly, and empathetic to the trauma of tough break ups. It starts with a deep breath and a free consultation. Sliding fee scale. In keeping with social distance during this pandemic, all sessions are via video.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My career has traversed working with parents in a huge variety of settings. I coordinated a public School Counseling program K-12 for 16 years and am a licensed School Counselor in Oregon. I worked with parents on the many issues that impact kids at school. I also coordinated an alternative program for children age 11-17 arrested for minor crimes. We worked with parents to teach empathy to their child. I currently teach the class: Helping Children Cope With Family Change for Multnomah Family Court. This is exclusively for separating & divorcing parents. My Masters Degree is in Counseling from the University of Montana; my B. A. in Psychology is from the University of Tennessee. I'm certified as a Trauma Informed Coach, a Certified Positive Discipline Parent Educator, and a Master Trainer with the Oregon Registry (workshops for child care professionals). As a divorced father, I positively co-parented raising 2 wonderful kids with their mom.

name: Brian Smith (HE/HIM)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
The decision to participate in therapy should be about addressing your needs, desires, and goals, so the decision to partner with someone in treatment is essential. I have held many high-level professional positions; but I also know what it like to grow up impoverished and use government safety nets. People want skills and practical counsel to make their own best-informed decisions. These abilities are part of what I bring to the proverbial table in helping you. My assistance to you is based on practical applications and not just theories. Beyond my therapeutic counseling skills and finalizing a graduate degree in clinical mental health counseling, I hold a graduate degree in Biblical Studies from Moody Theological Seminary. Earning this degree helps inform my empathy, compassion, and respect in therapy for ALL cultures, beliefs, and lifestyles in your therapy experience. I also speak some limited Russian when needed, as I hold a Russian Language and Eastern European Studies degree.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In many ways, counseling is like going to your physician or mechanic. Just because you visit them does not always mean something is wrong, but more often, you are being good to yourself to ensure that things function at their optimum. My approach to therapy is eclectic and not a one size fits all. I am versed in many therapy systems, including CBT, Adlerian, Psychodynamic, and Attachment therapy; the therapy style is based on your unique needs, values, and goals, not mine. Therapy with me is a collaborative effort. I am passionate about helping families, couples, and young adults reach their full potential. Working with multicultural, multiethnic, Black, Indigenous, and People of Color (BIPOC) communities is among my goals, given the limited availability of minority therapists within the region.

name: Tessa Rose (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Using methods of Process Oriented Facilitation, I integrate Process Work into my practice in a variety of ways: through dreamwork, exploring body symptoms, and slowing the process down. My approach to therapy is unfolding what happens in the present moment. By connecting to what's happening inside ourselves we can access our power, combat our inner critic, get in touch with sources of anger, rebuild trust, and find a sense of safety within ourselves. I aim to work with my clients on self love and reconnecting to the feeling of what it means to be at home and at peace. I don’t push my clients to dive too deep before they are ready, as true transformation is a process that takes time. As long as you’re open and willing to explore, we can work together at your own pace towards positive growth.
My Approach to Helping
I believe that everyone is capable of making positive changes in their lives. My mission is to help clients reach their fullest potential by providing a safe and supportive space. Through my counseling services, I help my clients to learn to empower themselves, conquer their inner critic, and learn to live in love. I specialize in a holistic approach to counseling, which means that I take into consideration the mental, emotional, physical, and spiritual aspects of wellbeing. My goal is to help my clients gain personal insight, discover their unique strengths, and ultimately create a life filled with joy and fulfillment.

name: Allison Medford (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I currently offer individual counseling and am not accepting new couples or family clients at this time. My approach is rooted in Social Justice, Feminist, and Humanistic perspectives, allowing me to tailor our work to your unique needs. I provide trauma-informed care to help you build resilience and harness your strengths. Whether you're navigating trauma, anxiety, or simply need a supportive space, we’ll work together to find your path forward. I am especially passionate about supporting queer and BIPOC communities and remain dedicated to ongoing learning to provide the most inclusive and affirming care possible. My practice is anti-oppressive and culturally humble, ensuring you feel seen, heard, and respected.
My Values as a Therapist
As your therapist, I honor the uniqueness of your journey. My approach is authentic and engaged, creating a welcoming, non-judgmental space where you feel truly seen and heard. Therapy is a collaborative process, and your insights shape our work together. Whether you're facing anxiety, depression, trauma, or relationship challenges, I’m here to support you. Let’s explore what works best for you and prioritize your well-being.

name: Kanyimbe Ker (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe in a person/family centred approach with collaboration in treatment between the counselor and client/family. The goal of counseling overall is wellness and/or reconciliation with one’s self, family and the community. I am an integrationist in my counseling theory, which means that I believe in incorporating various theories into my practice to suit the needs of whom I am serving. I operate from a Person-Centered and Existential framework but I also draw in a wide variety of evidence based practices depending on your needs.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
There have been several key points in my life where I have been overwhelmed with depression, chronic pain, loss, relationship challenges, or stress. In those times I needed to become vulnerable and rely on others to support me through the process. I became a counselor because I want to be there for others who need some extra support so they can turn difficult periods of life into times of deepening maturity and healing. My fundamental attitude as a counselor is curiosity. No matter who you are or what you are done, I will accept you, learn from you, and partner with you in overcoming the challenges you face. I also approach my work with hope - positive change is possible for each person, no matter how broken they may feel.

name: Emily Kittelson
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a counselor my goal is to help you process your experiences and gain a deeper understanding of yourself and the world we live in. I believe that the stories we tell ourselves are deeply influenced by our past and our cultural experiences, and these stories continue to shape our experience of the world around us. I use an eclectic trauma-informed approach of Mindfulness-based Cognitive Behavioral Therapy and Narrative Therapy. My aim is to help increase flexibility of thought so that we can re-write your narrative, lessen your distress, and shift your emotional reactions. I am curious and supportive in my approach of gently challenging you to consider new perspectives to your familiar patterns. My aim is to help you find a new way of relating to your past so that you can feel empowered to make the best choices for yourself. Let's work together to form and strengthen new neural pathways through exploring your old patterns and creating new narratives.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My career in mental health began working in residential foster care with children and adolescents. During my graduate internship I engaged youth and young adults in substance use and dual-diagnosis counseling. Since obtaining my degree I have worked in multiple mental health crisis settings with adults and children alike. I have a Masters degree in Mental Health Counseling from the University of Vermont and am currently a Licensed Professional Counselor in Oregon.

name: PAULINE ROBERT (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a Mental Health Therapist and licensed Clinical Social Worker in Oregon, I engage with compassion, respect, humor, and directness. Whether we are working to increase emotional stability, heal trauma, or find our personal meaning, my aim is to guide you in your goals toward empowered well-being. Building upon insight, personal strengths, and self-compassion, we can all develop a greater connection to ourselves and those around us. I engage with clients using a trauma-informed, culturally affirming approach. I use a variety of evidence based interventions, including Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Eye Movement Desensitization Reprocessing (EMDR), Life Span Integration and Mindfulness Based Stress Reduction. I look forward to meeting and working with you.

name: Johanna Courtleigh
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Together we explore the beliefs and patterns that hinder you from living fully, that keep you ‘safe’ and separate from self and others, and from fully experiencing, expressing and enjoying your unique self. As you become aware of and reclaim the parts of yourself that you have repressed, numbed or disowned, you are able to move toward a deeper sense of self-love, awareness, personal empowerment, ease and integrity--internally, and in your relationships with others. This allows for a deeper sense of emotional, mental, physical and spiritual security and integration; the experience of Coming Home to your Self.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Licensed Professional Counselor (#C1612) and Hypnotherapist. I have been in private practice since 1991, and working in the helping professions for over thirty-five years. I have had a broad and varied background working with: • Depression • Low Self-esteem • Relationship Problems • Adult Children of Alcoholics • Eating Disorders • Co-dependence • Fertility • Stress management • Transitions • Grief and Loss • Separation and Divorce • Spiritual issues • General Life Confusion Beyond my formal therapeutic training, I have also trained in the Regression work of Dr. Brian Weiss, which is now the primary focus of my practice. This work is deep, powerful, fascinating and transformative.

name: Rochelle Schwartz
approaches:
I believe we all have the inner resources to heal ourselves, and that sometimes we need help accessing those resources; I guide you in the discovery and application of those resources. Approaching therapy from a deeply compassionate, genuine, and sometimes humorous angle, I offer a client-centered approach. We talk about your goals, identify the obstacles getting in the way of your success, and develop tailored-to-you skills to help you through these barriers. When feels right to both of us, we assess progress made and discuss maintenance of changes so that you may sustain your progress without therapy. I approach each individual utilizing techniques from evidence based cognitive behavioral therapy and mindfulness-based stress reduction. Along the way, clients often find more peace, self-acceptance, understanding and life direction. Together, we work toward increasing your satisfaction with yourself and life. I find deep meaning in helping others, and am honored to do this work.
I have a background in multiple therapies such as cognitive-behavioral, mindfulness, existentialism, humanistic, dialectical behavioral therapy, ecotherapy, and collaborative problem solving. I have a Masters in Counseling, over six years in private practice, and 11 years of experience in a variety of mental health settings. Though I don't accept insurance, I'm able to provide you with all necessary information for you to do so yourself if paying out of pocket is not an option. I offer telemental health and phone sessions to help reduce travel time, traffic frustrations, cost of gas, as well as to serve those in rural areas and those who find it difficult to leave the house - please inquire if desired!

name: Megan Maxwell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapy work comes from a relational perspective that encourages growth through insight, self-acceptance, and choice. At its heart, all of my work is client-centered and built on a supportive therapeutic relationship. My approach is integrative and developmentally responsive to the needs of the client. I engage children in Child Centered Play Therapy. With relationships and adults, I go into deeper work using a Contemporary Gestalt approach. I believe that all people have the innate ability to self-regulate and meet our own needs in relationship with others. We adapt creatively to do the best we can with the tools we have and the circumstances we encounter. However, over the course of our lives we learn that some needs should not be expressed, or will not be met. My focus in helping is to bring awareness to unmet needs, insight into what gets in the way of us meeting them, and the ability to make a choice for the future.
Why I Became a Therapist
Over fifteen years as a classroom teacher, I developed a strong understanding of the unique struggles and pressures faced by children and parents today. I found a passion for supporting children and parents with emotional and behavioral challenges with a strengths based approach. In becoming a counselor I have discovered that the challenges we face and lessons we learn in childhood follow us into our adult relationships and lives. These can be lessons we learn from our families of origin, from our community, or from society at large in relation to our identities and the ways that we move in the world. I believe in working with children and parents to build strong foundations for the future, as well as working with adult relationships and individuals to address the issues that arise from our past in order to move forward.

name: Veronica Melo (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Veronica specializes in work with tweens and teens and is able to clearly support families in need. She is knowledgeable in the barriers the Latinx population face and is allied with LGBTQIA+ communities and other intersectionality. Please contact her today to get started! It is hard to reach out for help but the benefits can be very rewarding.

name: Janet Sandell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work in partnership with people to facilitate positive change. My approach emphasizes acceptance, finding balance, exploring values, and developing new skills to gain freedom from limiting patterns. Each person I work with is unique and therapy is specially tailored to meet your individual needs and concerns. I believe people have a great capacity to grow and heal and make their lives satisfying and fulfilling.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have worked in a variety of different fields in my early work life, and was drawn to counseling because of the profound and lasting positive changes that the counseling process can offer to people. I received my master’s degree in Counseling Psychology from Lewis and Clark College, and have bachelor of science degrees in Chemistry and Computer Science. I am a Licensed Professional Counselor in Oregon. I have experience and training using Emotionally Focused Therapy, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, and EMDR Therapy. I am a certified EMDR provider and am also a Certified Gambling Addiction Therapist in Oregon. I work from a resilience/trauma informed perspective bringing mindfulness, interpersonal neurobiology, and neuroscience into my practice. I use an integrated approach to therapy, tailored to meet the unique needs of every client. I enjoy working with clients from many different backgrounds and have worked in managed care, community mental health, and private practice.

name: Shari Davison, MA, LMFT
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In addition to my diverse experience working with clients, I have been married for 37 years and am the mother of 3. These life experiences are helpful in bringing a level of understanding and insight to my clients. My approach is client centered and strengths based.

name: Tess Krakoff (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I provide culturally competent therapy and utilize an integrative, holistic, collaborative approach. By blending elements from different approaches, treatment is tailored according to each individual client’s needs. I am rooted in humanistic therapy, which is a client-centered approach that emphasizes unconditional acceptance. Sometimes it’s painful to face things about ourselves or our situations that we would rather ignore. It's worth it to face them because it gives us the opportunity to look at things from a different perspective, to ask different questions, to challenge ourselves, and ultimately to get more of what we want out of life.
My Approach to Helping
I listen with respect and curiosity. Psychotherapy can help you in becoming conscious of all parts of the self by improving self-awareness and self-expression, as well as sharing dreams, daydreams, metaphors, narrative stories, bodily sensations, intuitions etc. My pronouns are she/her/hers. I am an active member in the LGBTQ+ community. I am originally from New Jersey and moved to Oregon in 2015 to explore life on the West coast and to broaden my experiences. I believe that interpersonal relationships are important both in and outside of my work. When not at work, I enjoy spending time with family and friends, especially while travelling, camping, or on weekend trips to the woods or the coast. I also love to cook and try out new restaurants. Professional Interests: LGBTQIA2S+, social justice, human relationships, multicultural therapy, crisis intervention, trauma-informed care, IFS

name: Nateesh Noakes (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
At the heart of my work is the belief that every client brings unique strengths and qualities to the therapeutic process. I focus on these strengths to help individuals navigate challenges such as anxious feelings, depressive thoughts, and traumatic experiences. I take a strength-based, client-centered approach and see clients as active participants in their personal development. I honor experiences and work collaboratively with clients to identify the therapeutic methods that best meet their needs.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
As a therapist, I believe in creating a compassionate, inclusive space where you feel heard, empowered, and capable of meaningful change. My approach is collaborative and tailored to your unique needs, drawing on evidence-based practices like ACT, MI, CBT and Solution-Focused Therapy. I also incorporate elements of Internal Family Systems to help you explore and understand different parts of yourself with curiosity and compassion. My focus on empowerment, advocacy, and humor helps to create a supportive environment for personal growth. If you're ready to show up, invest in yourself, and engage in the process, I’m here to support you every step of the way.

name: Heidi Colligan (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Using a Narrative Therapy approach, I help clients explore the stories they tell about themselves, identify patterns that no longer serve them, and begin to shape new, empowering narratives.I also focus on the mind-body connection, recognizing how emotions are experienced physically and how stress, anxiety, and trauma can impact both body and mind. Together, we’ll work to understand these connections, develop emotional awareness, and integrate mindfulness and grounding techniques to foster healing and balance. Therapy can be a powerful space for gaining clarity, strengthening resilience, and moving forward with a deeper sense of purpose. Let’s work together to help you reconnect with your authentic self and create meaningful change.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
“Owning our story can be hard, but not nearly as difficult as spending our lives running from it.” Brene Brown

name: Vinnie Gwozdz (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I employ a person-centered humanistic modality which empowers you to be the author of your story, to be the captain of your ship, and to be the person you want to be. Our sessions will be directed by what is important to you and we will use fun and insightful tools such as sand-trays and genograms to help with this process. I believe everyone benefits from therapy as it carves out regular and structured time and space to process all that life throws at us!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I earned my graduate degree in Clinical Rehabilitation Counseling in 2016 and have been a counselor/therapist since that time. I am a Sate of Oregon Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC) and am a Certified Rehabilitation Counselor (CRC).

name: Lisa Kaiser
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My focus is on trauma, grief & loss, and low self-esteem. My toolbox includes Restoration Therapy, Play Therapy, Art Therapy, ACT, CBT, DBT, SFT, EFIT, and family systems. I don’t believe there is a “one size fits all” approach and that all individuals are wonderfully unique with varying life journeys.

name: Portland Counseling & Anxiety Specialists
approaches:
What We Specialize In
We are passionate about helping others. Each of us has experienced challenging times in our own lives, and we know how valuable good counseling can be. We are here to help you work through whatever you are struggling with and help you find satisfaction in your life. If you struggle with depression, PTSD, or anxiety, you are not alone. These disorders represent some of the most common mental health issues that impact Americans today. At one extreme, you may feel held back or triggered by life events in a way that makes you resigned to a less than fulfilling, yet still functional, life. On the other extreme, you may find that your anxiety, PTSD, or depression significantly impacts your quality of life and defines your sense of self. Whatever you’re struggling with does not define you, nor does it need to be a part of your future. Your life can change for the better. We can help.
More About the People We Treat
Our therapists are trained in one or more of the following: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Emotionally Focused Therapy, the Gottman Method, Internal Family Systems, Interpersonal Neurobiology, EMDR and Motivational Interviewing. Our counselors are qualified to treat anxiety, including generalized anxiety disorder, specific phobias, OCD, social anxiety, panic attacks, and trauma. Our counselors are also trained to help with abandonment issues, grief, couples counseling, shame, co-dependency, low self esteem, identity issues, relationship problems, depression and more.

name: Grace Bezek (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is grounded in two core beliefs: that we are all hardwired for connection and that we each already have the tools for personal and relational healing within ourselves. Yet, systemic obstacles, societal expectations, and trauma can leave us feeling frozen, unseen, and without a safe space to connect and be truly witnessed. I work with clients to recover from these experiences, develop new relationships with themselves and others, and commit to a process of uncovering their strengths and internal resources. I believe that therapy looks different for everybody. I operate from a place of curiosity and non-judgment, aiming to honor what is meaningful to you. My primary modalities are narrative, emotionally-focused, and solution-focused therapies. Together we will cultivate your unique path toward more authentic, fulfilling, and sustainable approaches to navigating life and relationships.

name: Madeline Jacobs (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We primarily offer medication management services and are not currently accepting new patients for psychotherapy alone. Personally, I take a trauma-informed perspective on mental health care. For example, I am trained in EMDR (eye movement desensitization and reprocessing), an evidence-based trauma therapy. I also employ ADHD coaching, cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT), internal family systems (IFS), and mindfulness-based (MBCT) techniques. Intake available every week with our valued providers. Skycloud Mental Health is a clinical placement site for Yale School of Nursing students.
More About the People I Treat
I'm a Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner with a Master of Science in Nursing from Oregon Health & Science University, and have been working in the psychiatric field since 2015. I've worked at the Los Angeles LGBT Center, the largest LGBT Center in the US, and would love the opportunity to broaden the reach of LGBTQ+ affirming mental health care through telepsychiatry. I take a trauma-informed perspective on mental health care and am trained in EMDR (eye movement desensitization and reprocessing), an evidence-based trauma therapy. I specialize in ADHD, anxiety, depression, and transgender-related mental health care. I treat patients aged 18+ and am licensed in Minnesota, Washington, Oregon, Utah, and Arizona. I speak English and Spanish. I may not be accepting new patients with all insurances in all of these states. Visit https://skycloudhealth.com/ and click on Get Started to be matched with a provider who fits this same philosophy and suits your needs.

name: Naomi Wright (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use evidence-based strategies to truly understand your unique challenges and strengths. Together, we set clear, actionable goals and develop a practical toolkit tailored to your needs. For young children, I work directly with parents to reshape daily interactions and foster positive behavior, while with older youth, I balance individual sessions with family time. We start with small, manageable steps—practicing new skills between sessions—to build momentum toward meaningful change. Many see progress in as few as 10-15 sessions, though ongoing support is always available. My focus is on creating a collaborative, empowering process that helps you break free from old patterns and embrace a healthier, more connected life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe therapy is a collaborative journey, where you are the expert in your life and I bring expertise in evidence-based treatments. My background includes working with diverse families in county hospitals, shelters, and community clinics—experiences that echo my own humble beginnings in rural Oregon. These experiences taught me that many challenges are beyond our control, yet change is possible. Grounded in scientific research on trauma and interpersonal dynamics, my work is also enriched by a creative, theater-inspired approach. I tailor interventions to fit your family’s unique context, blending serious, structured work with moments of levity to foster resilience, growth, and connection.

name: Mary Hanel (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
After over 30 years working with a wide variety of adolescents, families and adults; I have wisdom to share. I will partner with clients to reach their mental health and social connection goals. We will look at the challenges through a Poly Vagal lens and move forward to Safety and Connection.
My Values as a Therapist
I am a woman, a mother and a Social Worker; I believe in the goodness of all people and acknowledge that fear keeps many from establishing and keeping safe and healthy connections with others. I am an Oregonian, daughter of a Forest Service Worker, wife of a carpenter and was a Portland Public Schools employee for over 20 years. I became a therapist/Social Worker because people are amazing and I want to help folks realize their own gifts and how they can use these strengths to improve their lives and the lives of others. I want to help diminish the hopelessness that often leads to behaviors that can be hurtful.

name: Sitting by the Well Psychotherapy, LLC Ardiana Halilaj
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I am a bicultural person who has lived most of her life in a country that is different from where I was born and raised. This gives me a unique perspective and approach to therapy. I relate to and deeply value the 'in between' spaces that we all live in throughout most of our life. This is the space where we meet the exiled parts of us that yearn to reunite with the whole. Holding the tension of these opposite realities in our bodies and minds, can be unbearably difficult, feeling stuck, torn and suffering deeply. As a result, we develop harmful ways of coping that perpetuate a behavioral pattern that can be wounding to our self and others. Often these patterns are encouraged by the culture and/or family we grew up in, and sometimes they were even helpful to us. And yet, if we can find other ways to be with our bodies and our inner suffering tremendous healing may occur. We may learn effective ways to harness wisdom in a way that propels us towards living more fully.
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is person and relationship centered, collaborative and to deeply attune to bring your strength and inner wisdom to utilize in our therapeutic process and your growth. It's my belief that depression, anxiety, addiction and other symptoms show up to initiate us on this journey of healing. My depth oriented psychological approach with a focus on embodied feeling will enable us to take a deeper dive into the issues that bring you to therapy, by looking beneath the surface of symptoms, and toward the root of suffering. Together we will explore and try practical approaches to closely pay attention to your body's sensations, feelings, beliefs, images, dreams, actions and other aspects of your inner world. The more you learn about yourself in a non-judgmental way, you may develop more agency over some life choices and relate to yourself with increased compassion. This deeper connection with yourself can lead to relating to others in also, a deeper and more effective way.

name: Jams Markovics (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe mental illness or mental health challenges are due to both intrinsic and extrinsic factors, or nature AND nurture. The intrinsic factors are related to genetics and the function of the nervous system, while the extrinsic factors are related to the impact of environment (including family-of-origin and society at large). Therefore, I believe that treating mental health challenges requires treating both of these factors. Since everything we do affects the brain, there are many different ways to treat the brain, including top-down processes such as talk therapy using aspects of narrative, feminist, and acceptance and commitment therapy [ACT], mindfulness and/or spiritual practices, physical exercise, and positive social interactions, as well as bottom-up processes such as self-calming strategies including self-havening, tapping/EFT, and breathing exercises, as well as mindful eating of a personalized diet, typically anti-inflammatory.
Social Justice Issues I Care About
Since I can remember, but particularly since middle school, I have wanted to fight for the underdog. I fought to bring awareness to climate change back in the 1990s which has now become a climate emergency; I remember playing John Lennon's Imagine in a piano recital in 1991 when Bush senior decided to invade Kuwait in project Desert Storm (or the first Iraq war). Later in high school I came out as a lesbian, (and now more recently I have transitioned to become a trans-masculine, non-binary individual), and, ever since, I have been very active in the LGBTQIA+ community and activism. In more recent years, I was involved in the "No on 8" campaign in California, then I was active in the federal push for marriage equality and I have also participated in Black Lives Matter and anti-ICE protests. I carry these values into my work as a counselor, as well. I believe that societal discrimination based on who you are or what you believe is inherently traumatizing.

name: Amy Hughes (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
I earned my bachelor's and master's degrees from New York University. In my graduate program, I interned at a medical hospital and in the acute unit of a children's in-patient psychiatric center. In 1992, I began practicing as a therapist at an out-patient clinic in New York, where I lived for eleven years. Since that time, I have lived in San Francisco and Portland, Oregon. I have worked in other mental health settings and have taken hiatuses to work in other fields and raise a family.

name: Hailee Kendrick (THEY/THEM/THEIRS)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I specialize in the treatment of trauma and employ Love Ethic as my practice framework. Love Ethic, developed by bell hooks, utilizes the dimensions of loving through respect, responsibility, knowledge, commitment, care, and trust. I integrate several modalities like, DBT, CBT, and mindfulness as tools to assist in the therapeutic process. We can examine the elements of your artwork and life together to uncover unconscious patterns that may impact you in meeting your goals. My goal is to nurture a safe environment for you to explore coping skills and yourself. My sessions are grounded in compassion, creativity, and collaboration.
More About the People I Treat
I can provide mental health assessments for gender affirming surgery referral letters. Certified through the National LGBTQIA+ Health Education Center in 2025. I offer sliding scale for eligible clients.

name: Diane Ornelas (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am an ADHD specialist also treating co-ocurring disorders such as anxiety, depression, self-esteem, and relationship issues. I serve clients across the gender spectrum, with a sex-positive approach & am an Altsex/Kink/Poly-Friendly clinician. I am also LGBTQ+ affirming & vegan affirming, provide support to social activists, sex workers, veterans, minorities, & am Spanish speaking. I work with clients on adjusting to new responsibilities & roles, personal growth, & determining the root cause of your distress. I will also teach you to silence your loud inner critic. I am both directive and non-directive; easy to talk to, but will call you out if you're being inauthentic, self-deprecating or self-sabotaging. I use various therapeutic & coaching techniques, & provide education on your symptoms. My techniques are accompanied by humor & rapport building to ensure our relationship is a trusting one, so you can be honest without feeling judged. Video sessions offered!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Bilingual (English/Spanish) Licensed Professional Counselor in the State of Oregon, as well as Licensed Mental Health Counselor in the State of Washington. I hold a Doctoral degree in Clinical Psychology completed at Albizu University in my native state of Florida, and am currently pursuing licensure as a Clinical Psychologist in the state of Oregon.

name: Kristen Genzano (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
There is so much noise around us – particularly as women – it is often difficult to discern our own truth. I believe when we are given the space to slow down, reflect, and connect with our inner selves, we find the answers we are searching for. If you are feeling stuck, unsure of how to move forward, drained from listening to the expectations of those around you, therapy could be helpful. In our sessions together we will explore thoughts and feelings that may be interfering with your sense of peace, joy, and contentment. We will work collaboratively to create an environment where you can hear your own inner wisdom. In our work together, I will be honest and direct, gentle and challenging. I will do my best to understand your experience so that, together, we can discern a path forward that leads to your personal fulfillment.
Why I Became a Therapist
Before I was a therapist, I worked in the corporate world. During this time, I witnessed many of the complicated challenges women face as professionals, as business owners, as mothers, as daughters, as partners, and as friends to one another. It was this experience that led me to become a Therapist. I attended Northwestern University in Evanston, Illinois. During graduate school I completed two years of advanced training in Mindfulness & Behavior Therapies and specialized in working with survivors of childhood sexual trauma. Since graduating I received additional training in Emotionally Focused Therapy for couples, Experiential Therapy, and Relational Psychotherapy. I created my practice to offer women a compassionate and nonjudgmental place to talk through the many unique challenges they face. To learn more about my practice please visit my website - www.kristengenzanotherapy.com

name: Drew Smith (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Incorporating art and creativity in the context of therapy is not about creating a masterpiece or finished product, but about engaging in the process as a tool for insight, self-awareness, and catharsis. Worrying about how "good" the work looks can actually be counterproductive to the goals of therapy. Most of our lives are not perfect. The art doesn't have to be either.
