Sometimes, talking about painful topics can be overwhelming and disorienting, but externalizing thoughts and feelings through a creative medium allows you to process challenges with the cognitive, emotional, and sensorimotor areas of your brain, all at the same time. In short, things often seem more clear, connected, and contained.   My work is firmly rooted in the understanding that individual health is deeply influenced by systems of power, oppression, culture, and heritage, which demand our active attention in all spaces, and especially in the context of therapy. 
Why I am a Good Fit for You
You don't need to be an artist or identify as creative for us to work well together, although creative process does allow for us to jointly examine aspects of your life in a new way. If you haven't felt that talking about things has been the most helpful, then working with creative elements may provide a fresh and inspiring perspective. If you have a creative hobby, profession, or identity (in any art form or medium) you may also feel that my approach feels especially supportive and helpful relative to traditional talk therapy approaches. If you are steeped in creative ways of thinking, that can make approaching your own emotions, challenges, and areas of growth with this lens extremely powerful. If you are struggling professionally with burnout or other challenges, then reconnecting with your creativity can help you access personal and professional clarity, calm, and meaning in your life.

name: Brittany Miller (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
The beginning of our work together will be centered around understanding your unique experiences and building a safe, trusting relationship. Whether you’re dealing with stress, anxiety, depression, or simply seeking personal growth, my goal is to provide a compassionate and nonjudgmental space where you can explore your feelings and learn to embrace vulnerability. As we move forward in the therapeutic relationship, we will work to help you break free from any rigid thought patterns or overwhelming emotions that keep you stuck. This will allow you to pursue a more enriching and satisfying life. I specialize in assisting individuals to navigate this process by using evidence-based practices, particularly Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) and Mindfulness Based Stress Reduction (MBSR).
More About the People I Treat
Inherent to all these treatments is scientifically validated skill-building. These skills can include mindfulness, self-compassion exercises, and values and goals clarification. Research shows that these practical tools can make a significant difference in your everyday life. I’ll ask you to incorporate these skills outside the therapy room and we’ll work together to find the ones that work for you. I believe that therapy is a collaborative process, and I am committed to partnering with you to overcome barriers and make meaningful changes.

name: Jenny Painter (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is rooted in empathy, active listening, and collaboration with you, the client, who is the expert of your own story. My goal is to help you build a deeper understanding of yourself, develop healthy coping mechanisms, and cultivate lasting positive changes in your life. I incorporate various therapeutic methods including Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Dialectical Behavior Therapy, Internal Family Systems, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, and mindfulness practices.
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a therapist because I’ve always been drawn to understanding people on a deeper level. Life is filled with challenges, and being able to provide support, guidance, and hope for someone navigating those struggles feels incredibly fulfilling to me. As a therapist, I’m privileged to witness my clients’ resilience and growth, and to play a role in their journey toward healing and self-discovery. It's also profoundly rewarding to be able to help people feel heard, understood, and empowered as they work toward meaningful change in their lives. I truly believe in the power of human connection and the strength that comes from collaboration, which is why this profession feels like the perfect fit for me.

name: Jason Wilkinson (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Finding the right therapist can be challenging. It almost always comes down to relationship - whether this person “gets me.” I create a warm and welcoming atmosphere that allows for the you to be seen and known. You need to be able to share and explore what is causing pain or circumstances that brings you into counseling and given the space to discover the truth of your strength. It is my belief that the world benefits from people who are able to function with a great sense of contentment, wholeness, and self-efficacy - and that happy and healthy relationships with self and the significant persons in our lives help us reach that state of being. I help people feel empowered to tackle their concerns of relational and marital conflict, anxiety, depression, and low self-esteem by helping them develop the skills needed for emotional self-regulation.
Why I Became a Therapist
The increase of people struggling with anxiety and depression motivated me to become a counselor. Feeling trapped by the feelings of sadness or fear is not the type of life people want. I desire to walk with others to a place of freedom from those emotions. With over 18 years of experience working with teenagers, young adults, and parents, I have honed an ability to have positive interactions with individuals and couples in need of assistance. With an education in understanding how individuals, couples, and families are impacted by systems, I use an integrative approach that includes Restoration Therapy, Emotionally-Focused Therapy, and mindfulness. My process provides tools that empower clients to change painful systems or cycles that they are participating in. Having earned an MDiv degree has also prepared me to partici Having earned an MDiv degree has also prepared me to participate in conversations regarding spirituality if that is a part of your story you would like to explore.

name: Stephanie Bauer (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use an eclectic approach to therapy including motivational interviewing, CBT, trauma based therapy, solution focused therapy, emotion focused therapy, and codependency individual and group therapy. I have a commitment to create a safe environment that fosters the growth of well-being of others in session. I enjoy working with clients to help cultivate the understanding that progress takes place in and out of session and is not always linear.
More About the People I Treat
Therapy for women: Specialities include codependency, depression, anxiety, grief, trauma, stress management, relationship and communication issues, spirituality issues, past trauma, sexuality, social justice, and family issues. I enjoy working with women on hormonal issues, endometriosis/PCOS support, fertility issues, postpartum, body image, aging, postpartum, boundaries,, sex and intimacy, finding outside providers/testing to help meet your needs. Therapy for therapists: It is very important that we take care of ourselves as mental health providers. Whether you are a student completing a graduate program or an established therapist, it is never too early or late to have additional support, grow, or meet goals. Therapy for young adults: I specialize in therapy for young adults (over 21). We can work together on transitioning into adulthood, life goals, college transitions, jobs, sex and relationships, impacts of social media, living with others.

name: Ali Archer (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Ecotherapy is the practice of welcoming nature into the session, and the ways we can do so are endless. Ecotherapists are grounded in the fact that people are inherently inseparable from the natural world, and seek to facilitate and uplift the many ways nature nurtures our bodies and minds. This can look like: processing climate grief in my office, managing anxiety through developing your relationship with your garden, improving symptoms of stress/burnout through nature-based mindfulness, or taking a walk together outside. Together, we can decide what ecotherapy means in your healing. In addition to ecotherapy, I practice as a Level I Certified AEDP counselor with adults and teens, with a focus on improving relationships and healing relational and attachment wounding. My practice with children is highly informed by Child-Centered Play Therapy and interpersonal neurobiology. I often utilize somatic, mindfulness, and other experiential methods with all ages.

name: Christina Gonçalves (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How We Can Help You
If you’re looking for some help & support in your journey, don’t hesitate to reach out & schedule a consult with me. You’ll be met with compassion, someone with lived experience, & a person to collaborate with. I have several years experience supporting people of all different backgrounds navigate mental health issues. I work a lot with trauma, anxiety, depression, psychosis, and multicultural issues.
My Values as a Therapist
A few facts about me before deciding to work with me, is that I’m warm, non-judgmental, & down to earth. I’m also LGBTQIA2S+ affirming, femme, biracial Latina/White/Brazialian Born (receive light skin privilege), Fat Affirming (HAES), Sex Positive, Single Parent, & understand what poverty feels like. I have experience working with a lot of different folks. I am glad to hold space for folks of all backgrounds and intersectionalities. I work create a safe space to land, be heard, and heal at a pace that feels good for you.

name: Christopher Marquardt (HE/HIS/THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer a unique counseling experience: I blend compassion with a dash of directiveness topped off with a heaping dose of encouraged autonomy or suggested self-advocacy.
Why I Became a Therapist
I worked in Education for a long time and wound up being a teacher for students with severe behavioral disorders. Much of my work, in that position, was around helping those kids make better decisions. It was during that time (and my own time seeing a therapist), that I decided to pursue this field as a career. I realize that not all therapists are the same and I really hope to bring significant, life-changing change to/with my clients. I am a Returned Peace Corps Volunteer and value people from all different backgrounds, races, religions, ethnicities, genders, sexual preferences, ability levels, etc. I consider myself working towards the tenets of anti-racism and have taken the anti-racist small business pledge. I have experience with meditation and try and incorporate morality into all of my actions. I love the Timbers.

name: Mary Hacopian (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe in providing a safe and interactive environment for my clients to delve into their thoughts and emotions. Through collaboration, I assist them in overcoming obstacles and uncovering their inner strength. I offer support and guidance to help clients reach their goals and start living the life they desire.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Throughout my career, I have been committed to promoting mental health and wellness for all individuals, regardless of their background or circumstances. I believe in the power of therapy to facilitate healing, growth, and empowerment, and I am dedicated to helping my clients achieve their full potential.

name: Ryoka Kim (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Additionally, I specialize in addressing end-of-life concerns, including caregiver stress, family communication, and grief. Facing death brings various challenges, but it can also lead to a powerful experience of appreciation and meaning. Whether it’s about one’s own mortality or a loved one’s, I assist my clients to feel educated and prepared to deal with the transition.

name: Mia Koenig (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
My approach to therapy comes from a collection of viewpoints of connection and relationships with friends, family, and the self. I focus on repair between relationships and individuals wanting to cultivate a meaningful identity of self. Our collaborative work together will be fruitful with purpose and intention. I have a passion for working with adolescents and their families to navigate understanding and to promote growth as a system. My experience thus far includes a great emphasis on eating disorders and embodiment. I offer an integrative, client-centered approach using evidence based practices of narrative therapy, cognitive behavioral therapy, solution focused, positive psychology and mindfulness.

name: Ashley Parkinson (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
As a Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW) and Certified Alcohol and Drug Counselor (CADC-I), I practice therapy from a strengths-based perspective, meaning that I work with my clients to understand what strong, natural skills and talents they possess that can be used as launching points to tackle issues causing hardship in their lives. I personalize therapy to meet the needs of my clients, have deep respect for clients’ rights, and work to build a strong therapeutic alliance. I strive to be a genuine, warm, non-judgmental therapist, committed to creating a safe space for people to share their concerns.
My Values as a Therapist
I am a parent, daughter, sister, spouse, nature lover, and therapist. I use she/her/hers pronouns. I am a white, cisgender, heterosexual, middle-aged, body positive woman. I am a lifelong learner, read novels and therapy books for fun, love hiking, skiing and birdwatching, and believe in doing the same personal "work" that I support my clients in doing for themselves. I came to the field of social work 20 years ago and since that time have been privileged to work with people across the lifespan and from a wide variety of walks of life. While so many of our challenges are universal, I recognize that systems of oppression affect us each differently, creating privilege for some while making healthcare harder to access for others. I welcome people of all cultures, races, sizes, abilities, religions, sexual orientations, and gender identities in my counseling practice.

name: Jennell Roden (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My aim in therapy is to increase my client's toolbox for coping strategies, while also providing a corrective relational experience to address attachment wounds and trauma. I know that reaching out for help can feel scary and overwhelming. Know that when you contact me, I will be respectful, nonjudgmental, and compassionate. I am currently a Graduate Student Intern, enrolled in the Masters of Arts in Counseling Program at the Multnomah Campus of William Jessup University. My coursework includes a trauma focus.

name: Matt Joslin (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work from a client-centered approach that places significance on the therapeutic relationship. I work to get to know you as an individual, to know your stories, experiences, values, pain, strengths, and desires. I enjoy helping clients build upon their already existing strengths, utilizing them to build confidence and trust within yourself. I incorporate aspects of narrative therapy (the stories we tell ourselves), attachment theory (how we relate to ourselves and others), mindfulness (being present in our experiences), and somatic therapy (focusing on the impact of trauma on our bodies). I believe healing happens when we extend compassion, empathy, love, and care to ourselves and others, recognizing harmful patterns and working to change them. I believe that in order to heal our pain, we are required to feel and experience the depths of that pain. I value sitting with clients as they feel their pain, providing care, kindness, empathy, compassion, and humor in the midst of it.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I firmly believe in the human capacity and desire for growth, change, and connection. Humans are relational, social creatures that require healthy attachment to ourselves and one another in order to truly live. I also believe that the social systems in which we live create barriers for us to connect with ourselves and others, and that our individual pain reflects the societal pain of our environment. Working from a systems perspective, I am dedicated to providing inclusive, culturally-sensitive, affirming, anti-racist, anti-colonial care for all people, of all identities and expressions. As a white male, I recognize the need to educate myself on the experiences of others, especially marginalized people. In my spare time, I enjoy walking my dog, connecting with nature, reading, spending time with friends and family, and visiting wonderful coffee shops in Portland.

name: Angie Martin (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work to create a space that is welcoming, compassionate and affirming. I will approach you with a warm, non-judgmental stance and utilize the best therapeutic intervention that meets your needs. I am active in the process and provide modalities such as psychodynamic, narrative therapy and CBT. I have learned the value of practicing compassion while challenging clients to look internally and lead a life that nurtures well-being. Also, I feel a little humor goes a long way.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe mental health is a key component to healthcare. Though I have practiced therapy for over a decade, the client is the arbiter and authority of their well-being. I am happy to be a part of this journey with all clients. People don’t need to have a mental health disorder to seek therapy. Sometimes we just need someone to listen and offer extra support through life’s transitions and challenges.

name: Meghan O'Connor (THEY/THEM/SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
Codependency is a pattern of chronic appeasement and dislocation from Self. It's a strategy of relating to others that is typically the result of developmental and socialized trauma. We get spun up in attempting to master things we can't control. It feels automatic. Life, and all its creative potential, atrophies. This pattern creates symptoms - resentment, dissatisfaction, deadness, depression, overwhelm, bitterness, anxiety, and even self-harm. With the right help, focusing on both nervous system and the root events that birthed these strategies of relational survival, we can create relief from the preoccupation and obsession with others, and find ourselves again, perhaps, for the first time.
Our Personal Beliefs and Interests
I'm queer and my roots are working class and East Coast. I grew up white-bodied in a multi-ethnic, multi-racial environment. My healing practice includes a life-long study on the impacts of colonization and civilization trauma, resolution of post traumatic stress, and I am personally invested in movements that seek reparations, justice, and healing for the historically marginalized. I believe the queer mind body is innately wild and precious, and has specific gifts to offer to the world. I am in personal relationship with the body of the earth, working as best I can as a conduit for collective grief around environmental and ecosystem collapse. I encourage you to browse my website to learn more about me and my work - https://www.meghanoconnortherapy.com

name: Anna Claybaugh (SHE/HER/THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Utilizing a nonjudgmental approach that is person-centered, I will meet you where you are at to work on the concerns most important to you. I believe you are the expert in your experience and with a little guidance, you have the ability to heal yourself. Our work will be bespoke to your needs and may include eye movement therapy, internal family systems work, attachment healing, nature-based healing and collaborative problem solving. Most of us are stuck living in an avatar we’ve created to maintain safety, connection and feel belonging with our world. You are not alone in your experience. I will support you in identifying strengths, barriers and supports that interact with your ability to reach your goals. You are worthy of taking a step forward for you. Together, we can figure out what’s holding you back from securing happiness and abundance for yourself.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I deeply value working towards collective liberation and decolonial ways to being in relationship with the world and the beings in it. I am passionate about working with other LGBTQIA2S+ people and supporting them in living an authentic life. I am interested in the inner energetics of things and have been working with tea as a practice for this, in addition to other tools. Tea has been used for centuries as both a daily drinking tonic and as a medicinal beverage. I am trained in pouring Gongfu style tea as a tool to support you in understanding your energetics & healing from within. I believe that nature and being in right relation with earth can support our deep healing. If you want to learn more about how I can help, shoot me a message!

name: Linnea Stenhouse (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
You know change is constant, and yet, it can knock the wind out of you. It is often disorienting, unsettling, and can call into question basic assumptions you have about yourself, your identity, your relationships. Even when you’re moving in your desired direction, adjusting to change brings up a lot of shit. To complicate matters, you may have unknowingly created complex internal systems to bypass or avoid painful aspects of your life throughout your development. Over time you grow, adapt, change, but old worn out ways stubbornly stick. Therapy can be a space to shed through old skins that no longer fit, discover new ways of being, and explore and process yourself, your relationships, community and context. My approach is relational, collaborative, liberatory, and trauma-informed. I practice with transparency and believe in a non-hierarchal approach to healing. To learn more about my approach, please check out my website!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I pay attention to and am curious about, how power and oppression show up in and outside of the therapy room. I believe we are wounded not just by individual life experiences, but by collective and historical forces, lineages of power, oppression and privilege, and present day context. My commitment to this radical practice includes ongoing evaluation of how my whiteness, femmeness, queerness, and the myriad of other identities and experiences I’ve had, show up in my role as therapist and beyond. My lens of considering layers of context includes existential perspectives and the mystery of our broadest context of living (and dying) as human beings. I love to zoom into the minutia of our nervous system and its response to daily life and also zoom way way out to the absurdity of our existence as strange beings on a floating ball. Considering our many layers of living can help cultivate mindfulness of the present moment and place us in the room in a more meaningful and alive way.

name: Shannon Glade, LMFT (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy involves a focus on mindfulness as well as somatic experiencing, while incorporating a variety of other, evidence-based techniques to best suit individual needs. In sessions this often means learning acceptance and other mindfulness skills, learning to connect the mind and body, processing emotions & past events, improving coping & communication skills, and increasing self-compassion. I provide a safe space for LGBTIQA identifying individuals and couples.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a Master of Arts degree in Marriage, Couple, and Family Therapy from Lewis & Clark College. I graduated summa cum laude with a Bachelor of Arts degree in Psychology from Portland State University. I worked in community mental health with individuals and families before starting a private practice. I have experience facilitating groups with both adults and children concerning a variety of mental health topics. I continue to educate myself on issues relevant to mental health and therapy through continuing education courses, readings, supervision and consultation.

name: Amanda Lowrey (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I am passionate about working with emerging to middle-aged adults with a wide range of issues, including anxiety, depression, recovery from trauma, self-worth and people-pleasing behaviors, stress management, boundary work, career and identity exploration. In therapy, I often incorporate parts of self work, somatic and mindfulness techniques to help clients foster the connection between their mind and body. I also work with couples and adult families with a focus on multi-generational patterns and narratives, conflict resolution, enhancing intimacy and communication dynamics!
Why I am a Good Fit for You
Recognizing that you are the expert of your own life, I prioritize meeting my clients where they are at and tailoring therapy to meet their unique needs. As a trauma-informed, inclusive, and LGBTQIA2S+ affirming therapist, I value diversity and strive to create a safe and supporting environment where my clients feel hear and understood, I aim to provide a warm, curious, collaborative, person-centered and strengths-based approach with a sprinkle of humor!

name: Ayala Parker (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe humans have an innate propensity for growth, and have the strength and ability to overcome challenges. I am a holder of hope, and offer my clients deep compassion and care, along with steady and direct guidance. Drawing from attachment based, and experiential modalities, I begin therapy by fostering the therapeutic relationship. I support my clients in exploring their inner worlds, and facilitate new ways to engage with themselves and others, and to allow the openness and freedom they seek. I believe that each person’s blueprint is unique, and therefore, I am committed to understanding your particular experience of what you bring into the room as well as your unique strengths and resources. I strive to provide a non-judgmental, compassionate, supportive, and positive therapeutic environment that encourages growth and healing. I believe clients know themselves best and support organic unfolding and discovering of internal wisdom.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a background in social justice work as well as working with trauma in family and social systems. I believe problems occur in context and bring to my approach an understanding of social justice and socio-cultural considerations. I work to bring equity into the relationship(s) in the room, and the social systems in which they exist. My practice is trauma informed, (continuously learning) anti-racist, LGBTQIA+ affirming, sex positive, kink-aware, body/fat positive, polyam friendly, and pro-disability justice.

name: Ryan Grassmann, M.A., LPC
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
While my therapeutic approach is grounded in the cognitive-behavioral practices, I integrate numerous other useful modalities, like Animal Assisted Psychotherapy, Existential-Humanistic Psychotherapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, Emotion Focused Therapy, Gottman Method (relationship therapy), Relational Life Therapy (relationship therapy), and more. My relatable style allows for our work to progress along a seamless path that's curated for each individual. No two clients are the same, and no two courses of therapy are the same. The process evolves along with us.
Why I Became a Therapist
Like many in the helping professions, my motivation to pursue this work arose from my lived experience. Like so many of us, I've endured pain and suffering. As a result, I noticed an innate response to help; people, animals, even the environment. I use my entire life experience in harmony with my education and training. I don't know of any other way that would be more congruent with my core personal and professional values. A bit about my journey into the art and science of my training- I earned a Master’s Degree in Counseling Psychology from Pacific University School of Professional Psychology where I learned the ethical practice of evidence-based integrative healthcare. I also completed a concentration in organizational behavior or "work psychology." I earned a Bachelor’s of Science in Psychology from Portland State University with a strong focus on neuroscience, cognition, and philosophy of medicine. Earlier, in my pursuit of justice, I earned a Paralegal Science degree from PCC.

name: Tamara Iliff
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I use play, expressive arts, and talk therapy in my practice with children and adults. My work is grounded in a person-centered approach, meaning that you can expect our work together to be relationally focused, collaborative and guided by your individual needs. I believe that understanding, self-compassion, and genuineness are foundational to healing and growth. My background includes extensive training in Child Centered Play Therapy, which is a developmentally responsive and relationally-based counseling intervention for young children who experiencing social, emotional, or behavioral challenges. I also enjoy supporting caregivers in their relationships with their children using Child Parent Relationship Therapy. I have a master's degree in Counseling and Development from Texas Woman's University and a doctorate degree in Counseling from the University of North Texas, where I specialized in play therapy and assessment of children.

name: Kathryn Woicicki (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I partner with you to dismantle the grip of painful thoughts and deep-seated disconnection. Using Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) as our core approach, we’ll clarify your personal values and develop concrete, practical skills to move toward what truly matters—despite the noise of overwhelming emotions. I also draw from Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) to offer evidence-based tools that help you manage intense feelings and reduce self-criticism. Together, we’ll work step-by-step to create space for joy, genuine connection, and self-compassion, helping you discover that healing isn’t about “fixing” something broken, but about reclaiming the parts of you that already work.
More About the People I Treat
I treat individuals and families who feel overwhelmed by depression, anxiety, trauma—including postpartum challenges—and the everyday stresses of parenting. Many of my clients come to therapy feeling isolated, burdened by self-criticism, or trapped in patterns that rob them of joy and connection. Drawing on my personal experience as a parent who has navigated postpartum depression, I offer a uniquely empathetic perspective alongside my professional expertise. I’m dedicated to providing an anti‐oppressive, LGBTQIA+ affirming, and decolonized approach that respects your lived experiences and values, paving the way for lasting, meaningful change.

name: Gina Adorno (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I work with a variety of issues using equally varied tools, depending on the client's needs, preferences, and proclivities. I also offer comprehensive neurodivergence-affirming autism and ADHD evaluations for adults. I draw from several research-backed approaches such as parts work, CBT and DBT, and motivational interviewing (a respectful, supportive, autonomy-forward approach for addictions). I specialize in working with people facing multiple diagnoses concurrently such as addiction and depression or trauma, or autistic folks who need to address mental health needs with a provider who understands how their neurotype may impact them.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Prior to becoming a therapist, I trained as a violinist and opera singer. My development as a musician informs my treatment approach as I see providing therapy as similar to developing facility on an instrument - a development of awareness, skill, self-concept, and self-confidence under whatever the given circumstances. It's a process of strengthening what supports you and weeding out tensions that may be holding you back from giving your fullest natural sound. I take an artistic stance to treatment and enjoy working with other artists frequently. My social locations: I am a white heterosexual cisgender female, LGBTQIA+ and BIPOC ally. Sex positive, adamantly gender affirming, and kink & poly friendly. I offer a free 15-minute consultation so we can connect and have a chance to ask a few questions. Currently accepting all listed insurances, however I can only take OHP CareOregon/HealthShare for evaluations or short-term treatment for now.

name: Catherine Cooney (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My psychotherapy approach is a blend of my training and experience in Humanistic, Psychodynamic and Cognitive-Behavioral therapies. In addition, I have training in Trauma-informed, Attachment-based, Experiential, Psychodynamic, Cognitive Behavioral and Addictive Disease approaches. What this means in practice is that I meet you with a broad range of methods and 25+ years of experience enabling me to tailor our work to best meet your needs. It' s my honor to create a space where you feel fully seen and heard, and to enter into a journey with you toward your healing and wholeness. Some people prefer more talk therapy, some prefer more focus on feelings and physical experiences. I am interested in what works best for you and my commitment is to provide a safe, confidential, supportive environment for your healing and personal growth.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am Licensed Professional Counselor with a Master’s degree in Clinical Psychology. Since 1992, I have treated individuals, couples and families experiencing a broad range of mental health and addiction problems in both outpatient and residential settings. I have had specialized training in treating depression, anxiety, trauma, sexual behavior problems, and relationship issues including those experienced by sexual and gender minorities. I hold the Certified Sexual Addiction Therapist (CSAT) credential, and am a provider of Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing. In recent years, my concentration has been in treating individuals and couples experiencing sex and love addiction, intimacy disorders, betrayal, trauma, depression, and relationship problems. A member of the queer community, I also specialize in treating LGBTQ individuals. I work from a sex and kink-positive framework respecting every person's expression of authentic self.

name: Zachariah Meints (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a therapist, I’m inspired by being a part of the growth and healing process. Personally and professionally, I value bringing more kindness, vulnerability, self-compassion and creativity to the world. I enjoy working with a large range of clients and I specialize in helping clients heal from depression and anxiety, improve their interpersonal skills and overcome unwanted habits. I focus on creating a therapeutic atmosphere where people can truly feel safe, grounded, and open to exploration. Working with me means we will explore evidence based approaches and techniques, in a flexible way, with attention to your needs. I draw from fourteen years working in mental health, including a background in social work, which has offered me the opportunity to better understand intersectional oppressions and the process of liberation. We engage in change everyday, but working with a therapist can help us along when we get stuck.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
People can make positive changes in their life in many ways, including: Receiving attuned understanding, compassionate engagement with our internal dialogue, connecting with our emotions and bodies, and creating new behavioral patterns. I practice the evidence based treatment of Cognitive Behavioral Therapy. Certified in TEAM CBT. Trained in Motivational Interviewing. Informed in Emotion Focused Therapy, Internal Family Systems, and Somatic Experiencing. If my approach sounds like a good match for you, please reach out.

name: Therese Long (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My style is direct yet gentle and I speak from an honest heart. I pull from various models but most often utilize Narrative Therapy, CBT, and mindfulness based techniques. Through a combination of these modalities, I work to help clients re-write unhealthy narratives and adjust patterns or ways of thinking that reinforce these inaccurate self-views.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold a masters in Marriage and Family Therapy through Lewis and Clark and am a licensed therapist in the state of Oregon. My work is done through a systemic lens with the understanding that we are always influencing, and being influenced, by the systems around us. I have experience with children ages 5 and up, couples, families, and individuals. I believe strongly in incorporating holistic perspectives into therapy and working to bring mind body aspects into the room to fully address concerns. I also provide supervision to LMFT Interns and LPC Interns working towards licensure in Oregon. Please inquire about cost through email.

name: Carmen Ryan (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
I've served families, students and staff as a school counselor for the past 11 years in primary and middle school settings developing inclusive practices where all students attend their home school and learn in general education classrooms, collaborating with internal and external specialists to ensure every student fully accesses their education in order to unleash their genius. I'm pursuing my professional counselor license to deepen my work with families, helping them find more peace and balance at home, as well as navigating the school setting to ensure they have the resources and support they need for their student(s) to thrive. I've earned many certificates and highlights include Play, Nature and Art Therapy; Trauma-Informed Practices; Crisis Prevention and Care; YogaCalm; and Collaborative Problem Solving. My dream is to create a healing focused rescue farm for families that have experienced trauma.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Humans are designed with a space within us that yearns to connect with our creator, and this need is filled in different ways - something I honor with my clients. Faith and community are my greatest strengths, and I’ve explored and grown them in many ways through the years. I love nature and how we are interconnected, as we can't be whole without this understanding and interaction. I have a heart and passion for rescue animals, as helping them heal is such a special process that has shown me the power of love, understanding and patience.

name: Hunter Vaughn (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I approach therapy as a collaborative process, recognizing that you and I both contribute valuable knowledge and expertise to our work. My experience is that when we combine our efforts, we are able to develop creative, personalized, and powerful ways for you to be more effective, engaged, and satisfied in life. I draw from a variety of psychological theories and techniques to tailor therapy to your particular strengths, goals, needs, and personality. For instance, we may work at increasing our understanding of your emotional experiences & why they occur; challenging & changing ways of being or thinking that no longer work for you; processing or reconceptualizing experiences & relationships; etc. Occasional “experiments” between sessions help to reinforce or inform our work; whether the experiment “succeeds” or “fails” we have new data. The overarching goal is to help you get unstuck, increase your understanding of yourself, & enhance your ability to adapt to and enjoy your life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I earned my doctorate in clinical psychology from Pacific University in 2012 and have been working as a mental health therapist since 2007. I've worked in a variety of settings including community mental health and college counseling centers, gaining experience and training spanning all ages, many cultures, and the full range of socioeconomic statuses. I'm trained in a variety of theoretical and technical approaches to therapy, which allows me to tailor therapy to a client's particular needs, strengths, and situation. I continue to seek training, knowledge, and experience in working with all types of clients, but especially those identifying as sexual, gender, and ethnic/cultural minorities.

name: Brian Thompson (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I am a licensed psychologist and Director of the Portland Psychotherapy Anxiety Clinic, specializing in evidence‐based treatments for anxiety, stress, and worry. My clinical work focuses on conditions such as OCD (including Pure “O” and relationship OCD), repetitive hair pulling, skin picking, trauma/PTSD, panic, and health anxiety. I primarily draw from Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) for its flexibility and scientific rigor, frequently incorporating exposure therapy to help clients approach challenges. I tailor each session to your unique goals, teaching practical skills that foster meaningful change. Typically, treatment lasts between 10 and 30 sessions—enough time to see tangible results while ensuring you feel that every session is a valuable investment in your well-being.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that therapy works best when it’s a collaborative, structured process that respects your time and your unique journey. I’m committed to helping you build a life that aligns with your values by focusing on skill-building and actionable change. I value a transparent, goal-oriented approach where you are an active participant in your healing. Outside the therapy room, I engage in ongoing research, clinical training, and professional collaboration to continually refine my methods. My passion lies in empowering clients to transform barriers into stepping stones for a more meaningful, connected, and vibrant life.

name: Natalie Thomas (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that we all have innate strengths that we can build upon in our journey to better mental health. Rather than fixating on what is outside of our sphere of control, I guide my clients through a journey toward peace that comes from finding the ability to let go of external pressures, and use what is with in us to guide us through life. I use an ACT approach, as well as exposure therapy, equine facilitated therapy, CBT, strengths based, and EMDR to assist my clients in letting go of what they can’t control, and working to become their best selves, no longer trapped by anxiety and trauma triggers. I received my Graduate Degree in Counseling Psychology from Lewis and Clark College in 2019. I earned a Bachelor of Science in Communication from Portland State University in 2011. I have worked with those experiencing severe and persistent mental illness, trauma, anxiety, and depression since graduating with my Master’s.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that we are all capable of finding self love, and confidence. That we all have the ability to live a unique, and whole experience by addressing our fears, anxieties and triggers head on, and accepting what is outside of our control. The path to mental health does not have to be paved with re-occurring traumas, and tearful sessions, but empowering, educational, and even fun, outside the box therapeutic approaches. I offer equine therapy at a facility in Wilsonville, Oregon where clients learn about horsemanship, learn to ride, and learn to trust, build confidence, learn to regulate their emotions, and to form trusting bonds, while overcoming fears.

name: Brooke Hammond (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I specialize in treating the symptoms of trauma (anxiety, fears, phobias, nightmares, flashbacks) using Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR). EMDR is a remarkably effective tool for resolving traumatic or painful events from the past that continue to affect you today. EMDR can bring relatively quick relief from troubling feelings and memories. It does not erase the painful memories, but rather, takes away their physical and emotional impact. EMDR might be useful for you if: you have been feeling “stuck” and unable to “get over” certain events or feelings; you find yourself repeating patterns throughout your life that are damaging to you or the people you care about; you have experienced a significant traumatic event or a series of hurtful incidents that continue to influence your feelings and behaviors.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have over 20 years of experience working with children, adolescents, and families in a variety of settings, including community-based mental health agencies, prisons and immigration detention centers, families’ homes, schools, international social service agencies, and psychiatric hospitals. I work with survivors of domestic violence, abuse, trauma, and torture; immigrants and refugees; LGBTQ; people experiencing depression and anxiety; and people struggling with transitions, grief, and loss.

name: Amanda Fitzpatrick (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Our work will be collaborative and deeply informed by social justice and equity. Many problems where we assign personal blame are the result of social systems that value some identities over others and generally perpetuate hierarchy and judgment that translates to individual pain and disconnection. Together we will lead you back to a strongly attached and empowered connection. I believe that you have everything you need for the healing and recovery work we will do. My job is to help you heal from old wounds, reconnect both of you to the wisdom of your emotions and your intuitive knowing, and grow into relational empowerment and mastery. Together we will help shine a spotlight on negative patterns that may have led your partnership to where it is now and help you navigate the challenges that change can bring. My personal experience is that growth comes through hardship and that it is through our deepest pain that we find our greatest gifts. I am honored to begin this journey with you
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Marriage, Couple and Family Therapist licensed with the State of Oregon. I received both my undergraduate and graduate degrees from Lewis & Clark College in Portland, Oregon. I have extensive additional training in Terry Real's Relational Life Therapy, Internal Family Systems, affirming care for neurodivergent folx, Body Trust, and the affair recovery work of Esther Perel.

name: Guy Burstein (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
(I am currently only accepting new clients for Men's Group Therapy) In my experience, life is better and easier if you have someone that understands you, sees it from your point of view, and helps you navigate through it. A good therapist is like a fellow traveler who journeys with us into awareness, facilitating the insight necessary to heal and grow. I am a passionate facilitator of this process, helping my clients make sense of their lives and relationships, heal what's kept them stuck, explore new ways of being and live richly. The therapy we do is deep and has long-lasting effects, and the changes become your second nature, even after therapy ends.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Before becoming a therapist, I worked for six years as a welder, four years as a homesteader, and ten years as a political organizer and activist. At age 40, I spent four years finishing my undergraduate degree and earning my Masters degree in Social Work. I've worked for the past 26 years in Portland, first at community mental health clinics and then for the past 22 years in private practice. I completed two years in post-graduate studies in Contemporary Relational Psychoanalysis, and advanced training in marriage and couples therapy. I have a 30+ years meditation practice and have taught mindfulness meditation to personality disordered clients in outpatient settings.

name: Karel Chan
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Mindfulness, at its core, is the practice of observing the mind’s activities, including all of its deeply ingrained negative thoughts and feelings, to realize that you are more than the mind. From the wisdom I’ve gained through my own mindfulness practice, healing, and transformation, I work with you to overcome the negative thoughts, painful emotions, and unhealthy patterns that prevent you from living the life you want — and deserve — to live. Through mindfulness, cognitive behavioral therapy, insight and inner child work, and a compassionate humanistic approach, I will guide you in understanding how and why the mind works the way it does, then learning how to train — yes, train! — the mind to respond to pain and life stressors in an empowered, confident, and healthy way.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Being a therapist has been a heartening, enlivening, confusing, humbling, and defining experience for me. To be in relationship with so many people over the years, to open space for their truest selves with my own heart, has awakened much of my own truest self and also invited me to investigate and heal parts of me that have been blocked and hidden away because of my own pain. I believe that being a therapist not only invites, but demands this, so that I am genuinely with you in intimate fellowship, rather than teaching or advising from a higher authority. I am deeply committed to fully embodying my human presence — flaws and imperfections included — and continually cultivating pure, limitless compassion. Studying to become a therapist is only half of it; what I have found to be the true marker of wisdom and growth is the heartfelt humility and connection that arises when I sit in vulnerability and love with you in sacred presence.

name: Kerrins Conroy III (HE/HI/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I approach counseling from an Existential-Humanistic approach, which allows you to discover new aspects of your life, and how to live a more intentionally fulfilling life. You will be met with empathy, and the understanding that your lived experience is more important than any preconceived notion as to how someone should be living their life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that when people are given support, validation, and empathy everyone has the capacity and potential to live a fulfilling life. Grief, anxiety, depression, and personal growth are all manageable with the right support. As a counselor, part of my job is to provide that support you may have not gotten before. Through exploration of different life themes, we will work together to create the life that you have always envisioned.

name: Jessica Janovicz
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
At Soulset Counseling, I specialize in Cognitive Behavioral Therapy and a Strengths-Based Approach, empowering clients to overcome obstacles and achieve personal growth. In our work together, I use evidence based strategies and interventions for emotional coping and wellbeing related to anxiety, depression, grief, life transitions, and other specialty areas.

name: Sofia Jasani (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I believe each client has the capacity for healing and self-actualization. I bring genuine empathy, acceptance, curiosity, and hope into my relationship with clients so they may feel understood, valued, and empowered to move towards growth at every point in the lifespan. I celebrate my clients’ unique culture, wisdom, strengths, and identities. I collaborate with clients to explore how systems of power and oppression impact their well-being. I support clients in making meaning in their lives and relationships. I provide individual counseling, play therapy, and child-parent relationship therapy. I also provide group therapy for women of color. My approach is informed by Existential-Humanism, feminism, and multiculturalism. I hold a master’s degree in Clinical Rehabilitation Counseling from Portland State University with a focus in chronic illness and disability.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a passionate advocate and community leader. I currently hold a Governor-appointed, Senate-confirmed position on the Oregon Board of Psychology. I am the Past President of the Oregon Association for Multicultural Counseling and Development, served as the Secretary of the Oregon Counseling Association, and served on the American Counseling Association Anti-Racism Taskforce in 2020. I am the proud daughter of Muslim immigrants and am a first-generation college student. I have been teaching in PSU's Counselor Education Department since 2020. I am currently offering both in-person therapy and secure telehealth video sessions for clients in Oregon during the COVID-19 pandemic. Schedule a free 20-minute video consultation so we can get to know one another and find out if we are a good fit! I am currently accepting new private pay clients and do not accept insurance at this time. I have a waitlist for sliding-scale clients.

name: Jonathan Green (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Through the modalities of CBT and ACT, a multitude of areas can be addressed and I can help individuals understand their past in a context that will breathe growth in the present and future. Through my experience in victims advocacy and crisis intervention, I have a strong base for continuous growth in supporting individuals with anxiety, depression and grief. The work I do is always in consideration of cultural contexts. Although I seek to be competent in my approach to each individuals life experience, humility is at the forefront to learn clients feelings and experiences through their eyes. In the therapeutic relationship, I seek to join the client in their experience and add dimensional understanding to how we can be effective in the work we do together.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I value the idea that the client is the expert on themselves. Although as a therapist I have (and continue to) study methods in which to be an effective advocate and resource for growth, it is the client who can only understand what is truly best for them. I am an active member of the LGBTQIAA+ community and seek to promote a world in which we can thrive and be treated with decency and respect. With this desire, I have worked with The Trevor Project as a crisis counselor for at-risk LGBTQIAA+ youth and continue the mission of advocacy through educating and learning in my free time. Core to who I am as a person is the belief that everyone deserves to be heard, and it is my job to listen, learn, and ensure is congruent to their intersectional experience.

name: Ghassan Ammar (THEY/THEM, HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I utilize mindfulness as a tool to assist you in exploring your present experience. Even when dealing with past events or future concerns, I find that paying attention to our current state yields a lot of insight. Paying attention to yourself includes all the different areas that make up who you are! This includes not just your thoughts and feelings but also your body. By becoming more embodied, we can begin to feel more into who and where we are in relationship to ourselves, others, and the world. I am also interested in the role of interpersonal neurobiology and the impact of stress and trauma on the attachment system. As such, I place a high value on cultivating a safe, warm, and loving therapeutic relationship.
Social Justice Issues I Care About
I am a deeply committed activist and organizer for socialism, transformative justice, and non-hierarchical systems. I do volunteer work around conflict resolution processes to help cultivate healthier organizing cultures within social justice movements.

name: Kellie Collins, MS, LPC
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a mental health therapist, and clinic director, I work from a client centered, integrative approach. I utilize cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT), Dialectic behavioral therapy (DBT), and solution focused therapy. I also believe that we need to work together, as a team, to help you achieve your goals. The body and mind are greatly connected and intertwined, which is why I believe in a holistic approach to mental health care. I am happy and honored to provide you with a non-judgmental, safe space for you to tell your story, set your goals, and become the person you were meant to be. I have a very warm approach and want, above anything else, to meet you where you are at so we can work together towards your goals!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have been in the mental health field for the past 12 years and have done much of my work in non-profit, community mental health and medical field, which I believe has been incredibly important in being able to work with all kinds of clients. I am an advocate and ally for the LBGTQ+ community and providing inclusive therapy is very important to me. I also identify as a feminist. I have dealt with anxiety and panic myself, since the age of 14, and this actually led me to this field, wanting to help others who went through the same kind of struggles that I did. I have found with the right balance of counseling, self-care, and improvement of self-esteem, you really can have the kind of life you have always wanted. I would love to help you reach your goals!

name: Sally Van Meter (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I understand that our world has chaos and many challenges. With more than 35 years of practice, I know personally and professionally that change and healing are possible and I also understand that can be hard to trust. I invite you to gently step in and find comfort at a pace that works for you. I serve as a support and guide in helping you in healing your challenges with anxiety, depression, trauma and other mood and relational disorders. My approach is supportive, client centered, culturally aware, and strength based with a mind/body/spiritual approach. I work with you on what is most important to you and how you want to live your life. I employ treatment modalities matching your needs; accepted for a Level I IFS training, am IFS Informed, CBT, Trauma informed, 6 week Psychedelic Therapy and Integration Certificate, enrolled in Advanced Training, and approach healing with helping you experience the parts of you that are clouding your unique and whole Self.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I was an unhappy teen, as my father's Post WWII PTSD erupted untreated, and although creative and playful, I knew something was terrible unsettled in me. My family was a hotbed of conflict, drama, secrets, substance abuse and violence. Through mentors, education and personal therapy the SELF in me that I believe we are all born with and that it is whole and intact underneath our traumas and symptoms, began to shine and I found a career that was all about focusing on what is authentic and true for each individual and frequently gets squashed early in life. I have deep intuition and sense when others are not ok with what is going on for them and maintained that intuition and desire to find out what could be done about this, for myself and my clients and after some 4 decades, this is still true. I have personal and professional experience that I bring to my clients that yes, you too can change and heal and ultimately, that is inside of you.

name: Daniel Swenson (HE/THEY)
approaches:
My Background
My path to becoming a therapist was circuitous. I graduated college in 2000 and worked as a musician, arts policy researcher, and career counselor, before getting a Master of Social Work degree in 2014. I have always been a sensitive person and for most of my 20s relied on alcohol to avoid emotional discomfort. All of this lived experience informs my clinical work. As a person in recovery, I help people to skillfully approach, experience, and express their emotions, without falling into problematic coping strategies. As a career counselor, I coach people in working toward long term goals. As a policy researcher, I prioritize evidence-based treatments. And as a musician, I listen and respond mindfully and without judgment.
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is behavioral and relational. I believe that changing ineffective behaviors, and recognizing thoughts as just thoughts (not facts), can free us to pursue the relationships, passions, and community that make life worth living. I also take a systemic perspective, and view capitalism as a driver of human suffering. Progress includes learning to reorient toward connection and mutual support over striving and consumption. I am intensively trained in Dialectical Behavior Therapy, and before opening my private practice, worked as a DBT therapist for seven years. I have also been trained in Exposure and Response Prevention, Behavioral Activation, and the DBT Prolonged Exposure Protocol. I am comfortable treating anxiety, depression, trauma, OCD, and substance use disorders, among other concerns. I have a keen interest in plant medicines, and enjoy helping people to integrate and consolidate the wisdom gained through psychedelic experiences into lasting behavioral change.

name: Meaghan Frost (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I am a Licensed Independent Clinical Social Worker in the states of Washington and Oregon and hold a Master’s degree from Portland State University. I have experience working with a wide range of clinical issues and specialize in anxiety, complex traumas, and navigating stressful life events. I use an eclectic approach, integrating modalities such as EMDR, attachment theory, parts work, and somatic therapy. I believe in the transformative power of human relationships to reveal our best selves, understand our desired changes, and choose paths to healing and growth.
More About the People I Treat
I primarily work with adult women and college students, addressing issues related to trauma, anxiety related to a wide range of topics, and life stress. I also support clients navigating major life transitions and other challenges. Starting therapy can feel daunting, but I'm here to help make the process as smooth as possible. Feel free to reach out with any questions to see if we might be a good fit. I aim to respond promptly to phone calls and emails. Please contact me via email or phone to schedule a free 15-minute consultation. I look forward to meeting you!

name: Zoe Wade
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
1. Exploring childhood traumas, feelings of abandonment and wounds from previous relationships that may be impacting you in your relationships today 2. Creating new narratives that make vulnerability and obtaining closeness in relationships feel more safe 3. Healing unresolved feelings from any past relationships that did not work out
Why I am a Good Fit for You
In our work together, we will go beyond a surface approach of therapy, and dig deep to discover ways to get the security you need out of relationships. We will heal the wounds of breakups and unpack the baggage you don't want to bring into your future.

name: Jen Dobie (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Through a somatic informed, body-centered approach, I don’t just listen to your story (and the many stories that reinforce your beliefs about yourself and the world). We’re also paying attention to the sensations you feel in your body and all the ways you move and hold yourself. This is how we begin to notice the stuff showing up in your life automatically and unconsciously. We’re looking for limiting patterns - the ones that keep you stuck, while also understanding these patterns likely formed as an attempt to cope with pain and discomfort. I believe healing happens in relationship - to parts of self, others and in community - and that how we feel in our bodies, our hearts, our minds and our spirit intersects with systems of power and oppression, culture, where we come from and how we identify and express ourselves within our lived experience.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
My work as a therapist is grounded in humanistic psychology, meaning I won’t see you as a problem to be solved, instead I tend to look at personal difficulties in light of new experiences that want to happen, just haven’t yet. I see the work as supporting you so the right kind of internal information becomes available, where together we experiment, look for limiting patterns and consider new insights and resources. I’ve been practicing clinical social work in hospitals, residential treatment centers, community mental health and private practice for over 10 years. I’ve worked in rural and remote Canadian territories, urban indigenous communities and here in Portland since 2017. I'm committed to integrity and a values-driven practice.

name: Pearl Waldorf (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Our human physiology is a brilliant system which can guide you to life you wish to live. I work experientially and collaboratively to build connections to your inner wisdom. Through a combination of psychoeducation, somatic and relational approaches, I offer an empowered excursion into the world of your self. My work is deeply grounded in an attachment orientation, which supports a deep sorting process: As a client you'll begin to feel, choose and act from a sense of who you truly are vs. from historical models that no longer serve you. Offered in both individual and group formats, our work creates a space that activates knowing what is *right* for you, as well as clarity about where your lines are. This is not about cutting people off. I'll help you grow the solidity that comes from moving through your life connected to what you think, feel and want. I'm looking forward to meeting you to learn more about how I might be of help.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My key values Number 1: Dignity... every human is distinct. You bring a unique gift to the collective. "Wellness," is NOT an interior job. It's thoroughly relational. Culture, the systems we are swimming in, your family's location within those systems, your access to community influence your experience of worthiness. THIS is healing work. Number 2: Nourishment... To live well is to be nourished here & now. We live in a culture that disrupts our access to simple life goodness. Most of us have work to do to recognize flow, sweetness, vitality & yet the searching itself can create the road blocks. It's complex discernment I'm here to help with. Number 3: Process... Life is an opportunity in every unfolding moment. We skip these moments in a thousand ways if we are not paying attention. There is the content of life. That jerk at work, the dream of a home etc. Looking at the process... the how, boundaries, interior patterns in play enlivens new perspectives. Let's take a look together.

name: Brian Hannah (HE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use an integrative person-centered, systems-informed approach to therapy by blending somatic, humanistic, internal family systems, narrative, and acceptance-commitment therapy, all with the goal of providing an individually tailored approach for each client and situation. Trained in multiple psychedelic-assisted therapy models, I incorporate these methods to address complex issues such as existential anxiety, chronic health concerns, challenging psychedelic experiences, shame, grief, complex trauma, hopelessness, and spiritual distress. With a firm grasp on neuroscience, mindfulness, and transpersonal experiences, I help clients navigate both day-to-day concerns and larger macro (lifespan) themes. When it resonates with a client's values or interests, I also incorporate ritual practices and take-home work. My clients report finding me warm, intuitive, reflective, interactive, and skilled at providing a holistic and empathetic therapeutic experience.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe in the capacity for growth and change in all people. Just like the body heals a wound with the right support, our minds and spirits can heal with a warm, accepting presence. Being truly seen and witnessed is essential for healing, and I am honored to provide such a space for my clients. As a queer and non-monogamous person, I support those exploring their queer identity and ethical non-monogamy, recognizing the stigma and challenges faced by those outside normative culture. In my practice, all parts of my clients are welcome, especially the most challenging, dark, and stormy parts. These often ignored or disregarded parts (the "shadow") need examination, understanding, and integration; this is core to my practice. I am dedicated to creating a space where all aspects of my clients can be explored and understood, fostering healing and growth.

name: Kaysey Crump (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
When I became a parent I learned first-hand how life-altering this transition can be. How anxious or depressed thoughts can make it difficult or impossible to feel present and joyful with your children. I’ve experienced and seen how even the most seemingly aligned and committed relationships can be rocked by the transition into co-parenthood. My experience as a child and family therapist, supporting foster and adoptive parents allowed me to witness the beautiful ways that people parent. I specialize in supporting people with: Parenting, Pregnancy & Postpartum, Queer Family Formation, Adoption & Foster Care
Why I Became a Therapist
I came to therapy after years of working as a full-time nanny and childcare provider. Being so closely involved in the lives of different parents and children gave me a deep understanding that there is no one perfect way to raise kids. After graduate school I worked as a Child & Family Therapist in school and outpatient settings. I spent several years working with adolescents in the foster care system, and supporting foster parents. I spent the last two years providing therapy to adults in a medical setting. I use Trauma-Focused Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Collaborative Problem Solving, Mindfulness Based Stress Reduction, and Dialectical Behavioral Therapy in my work. My style is relational, trauma-informed and focused, and humorous whenever possible. I work from an intersectional and anti-oppressive lens. I grew up in southwestern Colorado and lived in Nashville, Washington, DC, New York City, and Denver before settling in Portland where I have lived for just over a decade.

name: Lynne Coon
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I'm direct and down to earth. I use Cognitive Behavioral Therapy to help you make lasting changes. I’m a problem solver who loves mysteries so I enjoy the challenge of solving the mystery of how your brain works for, and against you. You may feel depressed, or struggle with self esteem and find it difficult to love yourself, perhaps because you’re a perfectionist, or hold yourself back because you're afraid you'll fail or believe that if you do or say "bad" things it means you're bad. I help you let go of being perfect and accept who you are, tolerate failure so you can take risks and get unstuck and love who you are by accepting all of who you are. For those who feel anxious I help you tolerate uncertainty, worry less about the “what if’s” in life and trust that no matter what comes your way you have the ability to handle it. I also specialize in Obsessive Compulsive Disorder and use Exposure and Response Prevention therapy to reduce or eliminate thoughts that cause you distress.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Since I was a child I've been curious about human emotions. I've always wondered what makes people tick. I was drawn to counseling for that reason and the desire to help those in emotional pain. I realized how much we create our own suffering through our beliefs about the world. We can't know what we don't know and so take our internal beliefs as the truth. We can be afraid to tell others how we think and feel for fear of being judged. Counseling is a safe place to share your inner most fears and get a new perspective. I feel privileged when clients seek me out and trust me enough to share the things that cause them emotional pain. I received my masters in counseling at Portland State and have worked with clients from many walks of life, sexual orientations and cultures since 2004.

name: Catherine Palmer (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am skilled at helping individuals who are experiencing relationship problems, anxiety, grief, depression, low self esteem, lack of motivation, past trauma, life transitions, and family of origin issues. Whatever your concerns, we'll work together to improve your life. I am warm and nonjudgmental, yet interactive and engaged. Your time with me will provide a safe space to explore your personal goals, gain insight on what is most meaningful to you, and work around any difficulties that may be holding you back or disempowering you. In couples therapy, I can help you break dysfunctional cycles and replace old, worn-out ways of interacting with more intentional and compassionate ones. In addition to teaching new communication and life skills, I can help you expand and reorganize your emotional responses in a way that helps increase empathy and understanding, engenders self-growth, and brings greater intimacy and joy. www.marriagecounselinginportland.com
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I earned my MS degree in counseling from PSU, with a specialty in Marital, Couple and Family Therapy. I've been certified by the NBCC and I have additional, long-term training in Emotion Focused Therapy for Couples, Emotion Focused Therapy for Individuals, EMDR and Internal Family Systems. I've been able to help many individuals, families, and couples create healthier, more satisfying relationships with themselves and others. It's a profound experience to witness the ways in which people are able to overcome their struggles and empower themselves. It brings me great satisfaction to be able to help others make permanent positive changes in their lives and relationships. I also have a Ph.D. in Science and have discovered that my knowledge of both psychology and biology provides me with powerful tools as a counselor. I believe in the interpersonal neurobiology concept of 'inspire to rewire' the brain via a nurturing therapeutic relationship.

name: Sara Fuller (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I use a non-judgemental, collaborative approach that includes mindfulness, strengths-based skills, brain science and CBT. I work side by side with clients to determine their therapeutic goals and the steps needed to reach those goals. We explore various techniques and tools, many of which can be used right away, to help manage anxiety, build emotional strength and stability, and nurture positive relationships. My goal is to help you to find kindness for the parts of you that are struggling, create understanding around your experience, and practice strategies to work with your emotions so you stay in control of them instead of feeling like they control you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Through my own practice and research I have found that by understanding our existing coping mechanisms and their role in either supporting or holding us back, we can consciously take what isn't working and create new strategies to put us back in control. With awareness and intention we can change the brain's neural pathways and create healthy, compassionate ways of managing anxiety. The goal isn't to eliminate anxiety but to change our relationship to it and decrease it's power over us. The body is continually sending messages to the brain about how we are feeling about our circumstances and our environment. I believe that by growing the connection between the mind and body we are strengthening our awareness and understanding of our feelings and how they impact our thoughts and overall well-being.

name: Monica Byers
approaches:
My Background and Approach
She holds a Master of Science - Nursing (MSN) Adult Health degree from Florida Atlantic University where she received the prestigious Talon Leadership Award, served as Treasurer of the Student Government Association, and graduated summa cum laude. She has a Master of Science –Healthcare Administration (MHA) degree from the University of West Florida, again graduating with top honors from a rigorous program. She achieved a Prost-Graduate Certificate (PGC) from Frontier Nursing University in Psychiatry and Mental Health care which further added to her extensive knowledge and skills. With over 25 years of experience in various care settings, Monica is known for her compassionate care and commitment to helping individuals achieve their optimal wellness.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
This accomplished clinician honed her clinical skills and deepened her understanding of the medical and mental health care needs of our diverse population by achieving certification as a Transgender Care Therapist and completing extensive post-graduate coursework in LGBTQIA+ and BIPOC care needs. Uniquely, Monica also provides services to individuals and couples within the kink community, focusing on issues such as identity exploration, relationship dynamics, communication skills, and emotional well-being. Always striving to provide safe, inclusive, and compassionate spaces for individuals to achieve their healthcare goals, Monica is accepting new patients at our office in Eugene and via telehealth. All are welcome!

name: Kara Kazemba (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I'm a former CPS social worker, which lends me to really appreciate how early childhood experiences tend to shape how we show up in the world today. With that understanding, I use a variety of modalities including attachment, DBT, EMDR, and somatic experiencing- tailoring your therapy to fit you and how your brain operates. After the free consultation, we would do a "life story" session, where I come to understand the circumstances bringing you to therapy. At the end of that appointment we discuss your hopes and goals- AKA how will you know if you get to "fire" me? Then we come up with a "treatment plan" together- talking about how we will get there in our sessions together. As I mentioned before, I am blunt. My approach does include taking a look at how you contribute to patterns and situations. Then, the plan is to provide you with tools and supports to break those cycles.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I became a social worker because of the person-in-environment perspective that the profession takes. As a counselor, I need to appreciate that you can't heal in the same environment that made you sick. That means being mindful of not only direct interpersonal trauma, but trauma at the macro level as well. I try as hard as possible to be anti-racist and LGBTQ+ affirming, and where I falter or fall short I will 100 percent own that. I am active in the LGBTQ+ community and sit on my local chamber's diversity council.

name: Jill Lees (SHE/HER)
approaches:
Our Background
Over the past 5 years, I have gained experience in diverse settings, including pediatric oncology and transitional housing for single parents. In my current practice, I specialize in working with women. I decided to become a mental health provider because I genuinely enjoy learning about others.
