I believe that people have the answers to their own life challenges within themselves, and I find fulfillment in facilitating their journey to uncover those solutions. I have also been a Reiki healer for over a year, and I find joy in providing support to those who may not find it in their everyday lives. I take pride in being a trustworthy, empathetic, and non-judgmental person. As a woman, I can relate to many of the struggles that women face in the mental health realm. My goal is to help women empower their voices and find the strength to be their authentic selves.
My Values as a Therapist
I am a lifelong learner always engaged in different trainings. I've completed trainings for for Internal Family Systems (IFS) and Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR). I believe in a holistic approach and recognize that there is no "one-size-fits-all" solution for every client. For me, it's crucial to have a diverse array of tools in my therapeutic toolkit to support each client's unique journey to healing. To maintain my clinical license, I am required to complete 40 hours of professional education every two years. I continuously seek out educational opportunities that will further develop and strengthen my skills, particularly in working with women dealing with anxiety, life transitions, and grief and loss.

name: Jordan Karr (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I strive to create a comfortable and safe atmosphere for each of my clients. I have a warm and compassionate personality, as well as a strong sense of humor that will help us connect along the way. My clinical approach draws on Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) and Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT). Together, we'll explore what behaviors and thoughts support or hinder your efforts to move closer to your values. Along the way, you will learn to navigate difficult emotions and painful thoughts with greater mindfulness, acceptance, and self-compassion.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe change and healing is best inspired by connecting with what matters most to you, whether that be friendship, family, safety, nature, or whatever else! It's our values that drive our best actions and help us face life with resilience. I care deeply about human rights, diversity, nature, and compassion so these values show up in my work with clients. Together, we may examine how societal factors drive many of the mental health issues of today, how to connect with nature in a way that enhances mindfulness and activates awe, and how to maximize compassion in our relationships and self-talk.

name: Laurel Barnes (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
I've been a social worker since 2008, but before that I gained experience working in public health, and LGBT family advocacy. Since getting my MSW, I've worked in Child Welfare, and as a Medical Social Worker specializing in pediatric oncology. As a clinician, I work with clients around issues related to grief and loss, life transitions, adoption, neurodivergence, identity, family of origin, relationships, and chronic or life-limiting illness.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a person who carries a lot of privilege. I recognize that I am responsible for my own learning about the role I play in perpetuating oppression. I became a social worker specifically because I was interested in being an advocate for LGBT parents within the child welfare system. At that time, marriage equality was a big focus and I raised my voice for that purpose. As I've grown, and our culture has become more polarized, talking about the issues that matter to me and also affect all of our mental health, like misogyny, racism, ableism, weight-bias, classism, have come to play a bigger part of my practice.

name: PK Foss (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use Brainspotting as a foundational part of my work with clients. We use it to reprocess and move feelings, triggers, and reflex actions that keep people stuck. Most people when they’re not overwhelmed or triggered can figure out what it is they need to do and how to live by their values. Most couples when they’re in a good place can communicate well together. Brainspotting is one tool I use to help get people to the place of being able to access their best self and respond rather than react. Then if needed, we do some work around whatever skills are needed to move forward. This can include communication skills, boundary setting, stress harnessing, neurodivergent brain harnessing/management, etc. I also utilize some Gottman methods when working with couples and parents.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a queer, anti-racist, feminist, mama who lives in Portland. If I could step outside of capitalism entirely and live my dream life, I would contribute by growing food, cooking things, fermenting things, feeding people, and doing Brainspotting for people who want or need it. I’d also spend a lot of time adventuring with people I love. Especially, surfing, white water rafting, and open water swimming. As a therapist, I love my job. I figure if we’re not laughing a bit, we’re doing therapy wrong. I love seeing people make changes and feel proud of themselves. I got into this job because I needed to learn a lot and do my own work. I’m here because I know how good it feels to do it and see change. I want to help other people do theirs with empathy and a belief that we're all doing the best we can in the moment and sometimes we need help to do better.

name: Ann Staple, MA, LPC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work from a nonjudgmental, strengths based perspective. I love helping people tap their unique abilities and resources - often forgotten during difficult times. Our work will be rooted in your goals - what you want to achieve. I will provide tools to help you manage difficult emotions and collaborate with you to generate new possibilities and implement change. I offer a complimentary 15 minute consultation.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received my master's degree in Counseling Psychology in 2004 from Lewis and Clark College. I have been practicing as a therapist for 19 years. I spent 11 years of those years working as an Employee Assistance Counselor helping adults experiencing major life events. I have also worked in community agencies and for a state health department.

name: Alicia Turgesen (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I've found every client to be different in what they need to heal. Some need to talk and feel heard while others just want to feel validated in who they are. With that, my approach to you will be just as unique as you are. We will collaborate to find what works for you! My work is influenced by many different models but is deeply rooted in the workings of Internal Family Systems, Expressive Arts Therapy (including SoulCollage®), Emotionally Focused Therapy, and Experiential Therapy. With these perspectives in mind, I create a space for you to explore you inner and outer world, to create meaning through art, and to find your voice in a space the is validating, affirming, and empowering. 
Why I am a Good Fit for You
If you're ready to feel confident, to find your voice, to explore and understand more about who you are, then I may be the right fit for you! My passion for therapy is driven by the people I call my clients. They each have a story to tell, and when I'm lucky enough to witness their healing and transformation, there's truly nothing more gratifying. I am committed to supporting each and every client - when a client walks in, ready to heal, there's no limit to what they'll do and how they'll grow! I'm a cis-woman, mother, partner, friend, sister, therapist. I've gone through challenges, much as we all have, and through my own experience in therapy and healing, rediscovered who I am. To have felt disempowered, to then regain that power for myself, is what fuels my work with people. I earned a Masters of Arts in Marriage and Family Therapy and a Certificate in Ecopsychology from Lewis & Clark College. Recently, I earned my credential in SoulCollage®.

name: Jennie Hayes, LPC
approaches:
I work from an existential humanistic perspective, using emotion focused, narrative, internal family systems and reality therapy approaches. Before becoming a therapist I worked in advertising as a writer, helping brands find and make meaning. I bring that creative perspective to my work.
I'm a Nationally Certified Counselor and Licensed Professional Counselor Intern with a Clinical Mental Health counseling degree from Portland State University. I work with couples, individuals and teens ages 13 and up who are facing challenges like: job stress, creative blocks, relationship struggles, self esteem, depression, anger, anxiety, gender identity, spirituality, sexuality, major life transitions, and grief and loss.

name: Eric Nielson
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Overall, my style is what would be called humanistic and holistic, which means I see you as a whole person, with your own inner wisdom and innate health, who is in need of some guidance and assistance at this time in your life. It also means that I see the relationship between us as an important part of what will contribute to your growth. As such, I strive to create an open, safe and trusting relationship and setting for you to explore your life and experiences in a way that is authentic, healing and inspiring. A basic guiding principle in my work is that, when we are able to really see and accept where we are, and who we are, we begin to heal and grow. My way of working is informed by a number of therapeutic approaches, the primary ones being Gestalt Therapy and attachment-based therapies including Emotionally Focused Therapy, or "EFT" (for couples and families). I integrate trauma-based therapeutic approaches when this is helpful for my clients.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I graduated from Vassar College and then worked and studied at Esalen Institute in Big Sur, California, where I studied Gestalt Therapy and Gestalt Awareness Practice. I did my graduate training at the Institute of Transpersonal Psychology in Palo Alto, California before settling with my family in the Portland area. I have over 15 years experience doing individual, couple and family counseling. I started my career working with children and teens in a residential psychiatric setting. I have worked in a foster care agency providing counseling for foster children and foster families, and worked in community mental health providing intensive in-home therapy to support families going through crisis. As I transitioned to private practice work, I also focused more on working with couples and individuals, which have now become the main focuses of my practice.

name: Susie Barrios (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My belief is that each person has the ability to find their answers and to grow their capacity to navigate life’s challenges with courage and lead a fulfilling, connected life. My work is grounded in trauma-informed approaches, mindfulness, and creating a safe, compassionate relationship supporting personal growth and transformation. My approach honors that each of us is unique, and that no one therapy approach is right for everyone. I work with clients to discover what they need and how to best support them in healing and thriving in their lives.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe in the human capacity to change, grow and cope with the painful challenges that life brings. We all have needs and strengths. Mental health issues arise when our attempts to meet our needs bring more suffering. I believe that we can deepen our understanding of how to best meet our needs, further our strengths, and grow our ability to live meaningful lives.

name: Candice Guertin-Carey (SHE/HER)
approaches:
More About the People We Treat
Candice's approach is grounded in an integrative perspective, which means she draws from multiple modalities and techniques to tailor her approach to each individuals needs. One aspect of this approach involves teaching clients tools to regulate their nervous system, such as deep breathing exercises, progressive muscle relaxation, and mindfulness practices. By learning to regulate their nervous system, individuals can experience a sense of calm and better manage their emotional responses. Over time, individuals will discover their own inner balance and develop lifelong resiliency. I help children: Regulate their nervous system Cooperate with rules and limits Be adaptable and flexible Feel confident to work through challenges Form positive relationships with peers Function well in group settings (i.e., childcare or school)

name: Mitchell Passadore (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I was trained in Portland at Lewis & Clark’s graduate school of education and counseling. I have specific training in treatment for anxiety disorders, PTSD, addiction, depression, and trauma utilizing principles of Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Exposure Response Prevention (ERP), Motivational Interviewing (MI), Compassion Focused Therapy (CFT), and Mindfulness-Based Cognitive Therapy (MBCT) in both individual and group settings.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that anyone can benefit from counseling and that no matter where a person starts, they deserve respect and care. I am here to help people live better lives and uncover patterns in thoughts and behaviors that might be causing distress. For this process to be most helpful, it is important to find someone who fits well with who you already are. My goal is to help facilitate positive, lasting change and I believe that there is a counselor out there who can help make that happen.

name: Dixie Hairston (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is to hold space for you during sessions so you can heal and explore what you want for your life. We will work to reconnect your mind and body in a way that builds inner trust. I have experience working with individuals that are working through anxiety, depression, grief and loss, religious/spiritual trauma, body image and self-esteem issues, caregiver stress, individuals in recovery from eating disorders and disordered eating, and individuals that have experienced childhood trauma including physical, sexual, and/or emotional abuse.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
You are the expert on your life and we will work together to identify the ways you would like to grow.

name: Kelly Richardson (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My work is an anti-oppressive, trauma-informed and strengths-based practice. I draw from the Liberation Health model and I integrate Somatic EMDR, Brainspotting, Polyvagal theory, Internal Family Systems (IFS), Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Narrative therapy, Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT), attachment theory and more.

name: Sylas Veit (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My work is collaborative and client-centered. I want us to define goals based on what you would like to see in your own life. I use existential, metacognitive and narrative therapy orientations with concepts from acceptance and commitment therapy (ACT). I am interested in the ways that you define yourself, your relationships and the world. Using these perspectives can shed light on the patterns you find yourself in, and help find a way of being that more closely aligns with your values. Even the traits about yourself that you like the least are trying to serve a purpose! Sometimes we grow past a certain way of coping, and benefit from exploring new options. When we are no longer operating from a place of avoidance, opportunities begin to multiply, and our true self leads. You already have all of the tools you need within yourself, and you know yourself best. Let's find out which paths are serving you, and which ones you may let go.
My Approach to Helping
Gender and sexuality are complex concepts that mean something different to everyone. I am not here to tell you how to transition, who to have relationships with, or to judge your decisions. I want to help you find the most authentic understanding of yourself in a way that moves away from fear and denial and toward loving acceptance of yourself and the important people in your life. As a gender therapist, it is important to me to incorporate my client's values in our identity work. I have specific training in gender assessments and writing referral letters for gender affirming care, including top and bottom surgery. I am inclusive of polyamorous, non-monogamous and kinky clients, as well as those who engage in BDSM. I am happy to discuss sexual concerns and desires, for clients who are in relationship or not.

name: Jane Sproul (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am easy-going, solution-focused and mindful of evidence-based practice. I'm an east-coaster who has lived in Portland for years. I work well with folks new to counseling, newly diagnosed or who have trouble paying attention or remembering what they talked about in a counseling session.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a master's degree in Counseling from Portland State University. I have completed professional development in bipolar disorder, intercultural communication and ADHD. I have worked in a healthcare setting for many years.

name: Beth Keeney
approaches:
My Background
Beth dedicated the first twelve years of her career to women’s health services as a labor and delivery nurse, then decided to pursue her family nurse practitioner degree at the University of Texas Medical Branch in Galveston. She then began working in the Student Health Center at Texas State University where she broadened her skills as a mental health practitioner and worked closely with the LGBTQIA+ community as a member of the Gender Affirming Hormone Therapy team. This experience sparked Beth’s interest to explore non-traditional treatments for mental health, such as ketamine therapy, which she provided for patients at a clinic in Austin, Texas. Her passion for mental healthcare then led her to pursue her psychiatric mental health NP certification from New Mexico State University.
My Approach to Helping
Beth incorporates a holistic approach into her practice, helping others to explore and connect with their truest selves. She enjoys teaching patients about cultivating wellness in their lives and providing them with means toward self-empowerment. She is experienced in treating various mental health diagnoses through both traditional and non-traditional methods. Beth is excited to be a part of the team at Head Well and is proud to provide her services to the community.

name: Liz Powers Wells
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My work is rooted in a deep, unwavering belief in every person's potential for growth. I am guided by curiosity, empathy, and a commitment to being fully present with you. I draw from my EMDR training, Internal Family Systems (IFS)-informed parts work, and attachment-based, trauma-informed approaches to help clients recognize patterns, soothe their nervous systems, and reconnect with their inner strength and self-trust. For younger clients, I provide a safe, non-judgmental space through child-centered play therapy to facilitate authentic expression and self-discovery, often using accessible digital tools like Minecraft. I also help parents understand and strategize around behaviors that may seem challenging but are often the child’s way of expressing a deep need for connection and support.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I’m an avid reader of sci-fi and fantasy, especially works that help me imagine a different world rooted in community care and harmony with the earth. This interest aligns with my work as a therapist, where I challenge capitalist systems that often leave neurodivergent and otherwise disabled people behind. I’m fascinated by how the autistic brain offers fresh perspectives on disrupting broken systems. I’m driven by a vision of creating a better world, which is why I’m drawn to working with kids and families to help build stronger, more connected futures.

name: Wild Wish Counseling (SHE/HER)
approaches:
Our Background and Approach
Wild Wish Counseling is a clinical group practice that offers support to individuals across all ages and identity. Through various services and treatment options, we offer an eclectic style of therapies. This requires our clinicians to be highly skilled and knowledgeable in the most recent research and evidence-based treatment for mental health concerns. As a community we face many challenges of depression, anxiety, trauma, PTSD, grief/loss, gambling, addiction, sexual identity, attachment or behavior issues and relationships which means that specialized care and a commitment to education and training is imperative to providing ethical and knowledgeable care to all individuals.
Our Personal Beliefs and Interests
Wild Wish Counseling clinicians advance their expertise by focusing on advanced training and holistic theories which requires passion and commitment to the field of emotional health. All graduate interns participate in ongoing supervision and consultation. Graduate interns attend accredited online or local programs because we believe in providing the best care with a pledge of quality in providing services to the community. We believe that interest in your life, identity, and narrative are most important to the therapeutic process of healing.

name: Elinor Tejeda (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In therapy, I take an integrative approach, drawing from evidence-based modalities such as Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Gottman Method Couples Therapy, Motivational Interviewing (MI) and Ecosystemic Structural Family Therapy (ESFT). During sessions, I am an active participant, providing guidance, feedback, and interventions to help clients achieve their goals. My aim is to empower clients with the skills and insights they need to navigate life's challenges. I believe in creating a collaborative, non-judgmental space where clients can feel supported and heard as they work towards positive change.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a biracial, and bilingual (Spanish/English) therapist, I value diversity and strive to provide culturally sensitive care. I offer telehealth services only, and new clients can schedule a free consultation via my website. For individual therapy I am able to bill Regence/BCBS Commercial, PacificSource Commercial (no OHP) and the employee assisted program Lyra Health. I can also provide a super bill. **For couples work I am unable to bill insurance, and am out of pocket, but can also bill EAP Lyra Health. My current hours are Monday's 10:00am-6:00pm and Tuesday's 10:00am-2:00pm

name: Katie Emard (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe every human being has the inherent potential for positive change and self-actualization. At times we can feel overwhelmed by the search for life meaning and the daily struggles that are a part of life. We do not have to bear this burden alone. Support and guidance in a safe and comfortable, therapeutic setting can provide the space needed to explore pain, trauma, behaviors that no longer serve us, our strengths, our multiple identities, our own potential and the internal and external
My Values as a Therapist
I see therapy as a collaborative process where we can access your own inner gold and uncover your truth and deep knowing. As a practitioner of feminist and Critical Race Theory modalities I understand that systems of oppression, such as: racism, trans-phobia, homophobia, sexism, and poverty contribute to mental health and impact the way we move through the world. We are all immersed in a culture that uplifts us, or steps down on us based on our identities. In therapy we can explore these aspects as well as your interpersonal and intrapersonal dynamics in order to find healing and growth. I am aware of the identities that I bring into the room as a counselor, and am deeply committed to addressing my own biases as a white passing person and around any and all other areas of privilege that I hold. I am committed to a process that is egalitarian and collaborative. You are the expert of your lived experience, it is my role to support and guide you through your personal exploration an

name: Kelly Yan (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My treatment philosophy is grounded in evidence-based practice and patient-centered care. I believe in using the gentlest interventions that will achieve your specific goals. I believe in holistic care and collaboration with primary care providers, therapists, and community partners. I do not perform ADHD assessments, but I can provide ongoing ADHD treatment. I do not prescribe benzodiazepines long-term.

name: Kelsey Weise (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I believe that the healing process starts with deepening insight into how we connect with other beings and with ourselves, honoring all the emotions that move us—grief, sadness, anger, joy—as valuable signposts toward how we make meaning in the world. In therapy, we can work together to untangle complex thoughts and feelings with compassion and curiosity. I primarily draw from Narrative and Internal Family Systems (parts work) therapies, with sprinkles of Somatic work to attend to the mind-body connection. I also offer Ecotherapy, which involves more focused attention on (re)connecting with the natural world as a key facet of healing. These sessions take place primarily outdoors; either in our on-site garden, around the firepit, walking through the neighborhood, or even in nearby parks and forest trails. Interested? Let's consult and see if my Ecotherapy offerings are a good fit for you :)
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe our stories are shaped by the systems we are a part of: our families, social circles, political spheres, and ecosystems. Knowing that these systems interact with each of us differently, socio-cultural sensitivity is fundamental to my practice. I aim to hold an affirming and uplifting space for those with LGBTQIIA+, BIPOC, neurodivergent, and other socially marginalized identities. I especially gravitate toward work with my fellow neurodivergent and queer folx. I believe that internalized stories related to these identities can form unique complex traumas. I aim to unravel these norms and nurture a more liberated and affirmed sense of self. Connecting with the body and with nature are also key facets of my personal approach to healing & to my work as a therapist. But I know these connections don't come easily to everyone! We'll work with your current level of comfort at all levels. Above all, I believe you are the expert in your own healing.

name: Jasmine Ziter
approaches:
My Values as a Therapist
As a therapist, I am passionate about guiding individuals on their journey to reclaim mental wellness and navigate life's challenges through genuine, wholesome, and playful connections. I embrace a holistic approach, weaving in the natural rhythms of everyday life and the healing power of the environment. My practice is enriched by the wisdom of Buddhism, Taoism, and other nature-based spiritual traditions, highlighting the profound connection between body, mind, and spirit. I believe in the transformative power of physical movement to harmonize our energies and nurture our well-being. Dedicated to addressing burnout from the pressures of capitalism and the weight of ongoing oppression, I aim to create a space of empowerment and balance.

name: Tamara Rasmussen (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How We Can Help You
Skycloud Mental Health is a clinical placement site for Yale School of Nursing students. New patients welcome weekly for telehealth med management in OR, WA, UT, AZ, MN, NM, & NV. Sign up for group therapy for insomnia! In-person visits available in Clackamas, OR; 1175 E 3200 N Lehi, UT 84043; 1133 N. Main St, Suite 100, Layton UT 84041. Seattle visits soon! Click our site/call 503-694-3381 ext. 2 to schedule an appt, same week! See us in-person at 13568 SE 97th Ave Suite 202 Clackamas, OR 97015.

name: Michael Pollet (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Our work begins with building a strong therapeutic relationship. Together, we’ll uncover the unconscious conflicts and patterns maintaining your struggles and explore the underlying causes of your challenges. This process promotes the insight needed to interact more effectively with the world and develop strategies to manage the forces shaping your choices.
My Values as a Therapist
My goal is to create a space where you are safe to explore the uncomfortable. I welcome humor in therapy and offer a warm, judgment-free environment, where we can work together to navigate challenges, uncover your strengths, and make meaningful changes.

name: Maia Bellavia (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer personalized therapy sessions to help develop deep self-worth and confidence to overcome any challenges and live a more wholehearted and fulfilling life. Striving to provide supportive psychotherapy during each appointment. My practice involves using an eclectic approach based on your needs, including Solution-Focused Treatment, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Dialectical Behavioral Therapy, Mindfulness, Somatic Therapy, and Positive Psychology therapeutic approach. Valuing a holistic, integrative treatment approach, which includes behavioral strategies, to equip each client with skills and strategies to improve their quality of life.

name: Alli Santa (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use different approaches to help each client with their individual difficulty. Therapy is a very personal experience and can sometimes be a little scary and/or overwhelming. I facilitate a judgement free and safe environment where I encourage clients to express their frustrations as well as help them gain knowledge and insight to inspire healthy beliefs and attitudes towards the difficult situations life throws their way. I've also been known to incorporate a little humor throughout the healing process. "Sometimes the smallest step in the right direction ends up being the biggest step of your life."- unknown
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My clinical education and training begain in 2003. I have a B.S in Speech Language Pathology and an M.A in Counseling with an emphasis in clinical mental health. I'm a registered associate through the state of Oregon and I'm a certified Clinical Trauma professional. I'm also a member of the American Counseling Association.

name: David Singer (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
CERTIFIED MINDFULLNESS-BASED STRESS REDUCTION COUNSELOR TRAINED IN ANIMAL ASSISTED INTERVENTIONS - EMOTIONAL SUPPORT ANIMAL ASSESSMENTS APPROVED TO PROVIDE CLINICAL SUPERVISION TOWARDS LCSW IN OREGON

name: Evan Egener (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach leverages mindfulness, compassion, and behavioral change. The interventions I use are outcome-oriented and evidence-based. These include Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), and the Gottman Method. As you seek healthcare, I believe you deserve tangible progress that makes contact with your full and complex human experience. My essential aim is to help you live meaningfully, experience connection, develop resilience, and feel present as you do what matters most. Seeking help is daunting and overwhelming under the weight of painful challenges. Finding the right fit can be both exhausting and worth it. Thank you for considering me.

name: Linda Schartman
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
With two master's degrees (the first in sociology and the second in clinical mental health counseling), I utilize evidence-based practices to provide respectful services that celebrate human agency and diversity in all of its expressions. I honor your courage in reaching out for help as well as your perseverence in finding good help--it's not easy and I know many of you may have had bad experiences opening up in the past. You matter and the world needs you, even though it not feel that way right now. I will do everything within my power to support you.
My Approach to Helping
I utilize evidence-based practices to provide respectful services that celebrate human agency and diversity in all of its expressions. My techniques are mostly aligned with narrative therapy with blended applications of humor, DBT, ACT, Somatic Reprocessing, Expressive Therapy, and Exposure and Response Prevention.

name: Jenny Labrousse
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Utilizing evidence-based Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), I will work closely with you to identify thoughts, emotions, and behaviors contributing to your distress. I also integrate Restoration Therapy, mindfulness, contemporary psychodynamic therapy, Exposure and Response Therapy, and other empirically supported treatments as needed.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My focus is in treating Anxiety and Depression. However, I work with a wide range of concerns. I am a level 1 Restoration Therapist. Please reach out for a free 15 minute phone consultation. I look forward to your call or email!

name: Stephanie Thompson-McCollough (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Therapy Approaches 1 Person Centered Therapy is a collaboration. I am a guide to help facilitate reflection, growth, and opportunity for change. You are the leader of the therapeutic process. 2 Motivational Interviewing Change is uncomfortable. Together we can explore what is keeping you feeling stuck and preventing you from living your most authentic life. 3 Mindful Only when we are fully present are we able to truly experience our lives. Therapy can help each of us tap into being present and aware of each current moment. 4 Unconditional Positive Regard I believe each person is doing the absolute best they can within their circumstances. I provide therapy from this deeply empathetic place.
My Approach to Helping
Life unfolds in complicated, beautiful, chaotic, and at times, immensely painful ways. As we make our way through, we often need support navigating the terrain of our inner world so that we can show up more fully to all that is happening in our outer world. If you’ve been feeling overwhelmed, disconnected, stuck or alone, then I’m glad you found your way here. I know from my own life experience that finding the right person to support you in your healing is both a vulnerable and important task. I feel honored to do this work and I am in awe of our ability as humans to accompany and support each other as we heal and grow. I have a background working in a variety of settings including PH/IOP, outpatient, emergency department, inpatient, and community based care. I also have experience working with a variety of different types of challenges including: depression, anxiety, grief & loss, family conflict, infertility, attachment, life transitions, parenting, self esteem, SI, eating disorder

name: Ofelio Resendiz (HE/HIM/ÉL)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have experience working with Adolescents, Adults, Families, and Couples experiencing anxiety, depression, trauma, and identity issues. I approach therapy with the understanding that we are all shaped by experiences throughout our lives in context with our family of origin and cultural background. These experiences inform how we make meaning in our lives. I believe the therapeutic relationship is the most important element of therapy within which we collaborate to discover a meaningful change. I primarily practice Contemporary Gestalt Therapy, an evidence-based practice that seeks to build awareness in the present moment and understand how you make meaning of your environment. From this perspective symptoms of mental illness are our attempt to cope with inner and environmental distress. Our work in therapy will be supporting these symptoms and becoming curious about their intended function which paradoxically leads to their resolution.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I earned my Masters in Applied Psychological Science, with an emphasis in Latino/a Psychology from Pacific University. I have experience working with culturally diverse populations which include Asian, Native American, and Latino/a/x, Adolescents, and adults. I utilize EFT for relationship work. I also conduct sessions in Spanish for patients needing a Spanish-speaking clinician.

name: Cornelia Taubmann (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Background
As a hypnotherapist with over 600 hours of training and Somatic Experiencing ® Practitioner (SEP), I understand the importance of a multimodal approach to treatment. While traditional talk therapy can be helpful, I believe that true and lasting change often requires engaging the body and subconscious mind.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe unhelpful patterns are a result of a survival/adaptive response that has become obsolete. Reconnecting with our body and small gradual changes steps are the keys to changing our nervous system's response.

name: Bronwyn Santaella (THEY/SHE)
approaches:
My Background
I hold a BA in Feminist Theory from UC Santa Cruz, where I developed a deep understanding of the complex intersections between identity, culture, and the body—foundational to the work I do today. Over the past five years, I’ve worked extensively in all levels of eating disorder care, including residential treatment, partial hospitalization programs, and intensive outpatient programs. This experience has given me a comprehensive, trauma-informed perspective on the recovery process and the many ways disordered eating can manifest and evolve. I’ve supported clients through the full spectrum of eating disorders, including anorexia, bulimia, binge eating disorder, and ARFID. My approach is relational, intersectional, and rooted in social justice. I strive to create a space where clients feel seen and supported as whole people—not just diagnoses. I’m especially passionate about working with adolescents and young adults who are ready to begin or deepen their healing journey.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I come to this work with a deep commitment to fostering empowerment, radical self-love, and liberation for my clients. My approach is grounded in the belief that healing from an eating disorder isn’t just about food, it’s about reclaiming your body, your voice, and your right to exist unapologetically. My politics and background in feminist theory shape the way I see and support my clients. I recognize the systems of patriarchy, capitalism, and white supremacy that fuel disordered relationships with food and body image. I believe recovery is a political act, and I work to create a space where clients feel safe to explore not only their personal narratives, but also the societal messages they’ve internalized. I'm passionate about working with adolescents and young adults because I believe in their power to disrupt harmful norms and build something more authentic. Together, we’ll work to unlearn shame and rebuild a foundation of compassion, strength, and self-defined worth.

name: Alisha Phillips (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I will spend time getting to know you- your concerns, your strengths, your values, and your hopes for the future. Together we will work toward understanding your story and practice building on your strengths, identifying resources, and learning new skills. I work with each client in a unique and different way, which may include Dialectical Behavioral Therapy, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, and mindfulness and relaxation techniques. I have extra training in treating Complex PTSD. My background is in early intervention and parent coaching, building strong bonds and healthy attachment. Though I no longer work with children as clients, I still emphasize the importance of understanding the early experiences of my adult clients and their relationship with their younger selves and family of origin. I have a Master’s degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from Bushnell University, and a Bachelor’s degree in Family and Human Services from University of Oregon.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I moved from my home state of Alaska to Oregon as a college student and have lived here ever since, now settled down with my husband, three young children, and sprawling garden. I strive for values of simplicity, balance, acceptance, and kindness. I am especially passionate about caring for women and mothers and for helping people see their true selves through layers of systemic oppression, shame, and silencing. I am fascinated with the integration of Eastern philosophy and spiritual practices with Western psychology and academic pursuits. Clients of mine know I talk about the most recent discoveries in neuroscience in the same conversation as a Buddhist parable. I am a student and fellow traveler in many ways, rather than an expert. I chose to become a counselor because it is the most rewarding and enlightening path for me.

name: Brettyn Gibson
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am trained as child play therapist, but am currently focused on helping parents learn these same skills to offer therapeutic interactions to their children in their own homes. I’m currently offering virtual parenting support services in Oregon and Washington.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a child & family therapist who specializes in family & child play therapy. I especially value working with families and on helping children and families grown and heal following traumatic experiences. I am a Licensed Marriage & Family Therapist (LMFT), registered with the Oregon Board of Licensed Professional Counselors and Therapists (OBLPCT) and the Washington State Department of Health (DOH). I am supervised by Dr. Kimberly Jayne, PhD, LPC, RPT-S, CCPT-S. I am happy to answer questions about the supervision process.

name: Mikaylee Dato
approaches:
How I Can Help You
Life can be a wild ride, but you don’t have to do it alone! I utilize a client centered, strengths-based, trauma informed approach to therapy. I bring insight and dedication to helping clients feel seen and empowered to grow from life’s challenges and develop resilience, making therapy a collaborative journey toward greater well-being. I offer a safe and collaborative process to guide you to evaluate, understand, and process thoughts and experiences that will allow for significant healing. I will meet you where you are at and draw upon your strengths to empower you as you process all life has thrown at you, as well as challenge thoughts, beliefs, and behaviors that might be keeping you stuck. With my holistic approach, I will integrate all facets of your life into our work. I tailor my theory and interventions to meet each client's individual needs and abilities and therefore, have understanding and practice in various evidence-based modalities. I am also trained in EMDR.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In my time as a therapist, I have worked in both inpatient and outpatient settings providing individual, group, and family counseling. I have also enjoyed providing therapy services to multiple private and public elementary and high schools to make access to care easier for students and families. As an Oregon native, I’ve always loved the outdoors—whether it’s hiking, camping, wake surfing, snowboarding, or snowshoeing. I enjoy exploring new places, immersing myself in different cultures, and connecting through food. When I’m not out adventuring, you’ll find me with a great cup of coffee or tea, binging a good show, and soaking up quality time with my favorite people.

name: Ashlee Garau-Brittingham (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
It is so common to have internal voices encouraging avoidance, judgment & shame toward ourselves. Unfortunately, these voices don’t invite curiosity, awareness, empathy, or collaboration, which is where so much of therapy & healing live! I can help you understand why unwanted emotions arise, how to build new intentional skills to avoid repeating unhelpful patterns or reactions & give you a sense of being in control. I will provide tools that you can take everywhere to help slow down racing thoughts, clarify your intentions, & support your body to become regulated & centered. Therapeutic approaches I draw from surround narrative therapy, mindfulness, attachment, CBT, motivational interviewing, family systems therapy, & sensory-motor arousal regulation treatment (SMART). Safe space is so important! I create this environment by being curious, compassionate, & relational focused. It's also not uncommon for there to be laughter & humor in the moments we are both being relational humans.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I really believe every person holds innate strength & capabilities to pursue self-acceptance, growth, change, healing, & empowerment! I also take a position that firmly trusts all feelings are neither good or bad, right or wrong. Feelings are truth tellers & help provide context to what we are experiencing. Many clients describe trying to manage an unwanted feeling through avoidance & resistance because they lack the skills or have a belief that the strong or unwanted feeling is “bad/wrong”. I have witnessed when people are taught the skills to feel the honesty & vulnerability of an unwanted feeling, they find new perspectives, self-trust & opportunities to navigate toward a different outcome of that is more aligned of their values and needs.

name: Grace Provost (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
If some of these resonate with you, please reach out for a free consultation to see if we are a good fit. I specialize in treating trauma, chronic pain/illness, codependency, anxiety, depression and neurodivergence. I work with adults of all ages, children ages 11+ on a case-by-case basis, and offer parent coaching. My role is to serve as a guide, sounding board, and support you as you continue to discover who you are and who you want to be in this life. I will teach you to be present with yourself in a way that helps you get to know yourself better. This makes it possible to examine what you may be holding on to from the past that impedes where you are going. I combine knowledge in neurobiology, somatic work, IFS and EMDR. I draw from many modalities and enjoy collaborating with individuals to find a treatment plan that works best for their needs.
My Values as a Therapist
I value social justice, radical love for all, self compassion, self attunement, somatic work, trauma work, evidence based modalities, intuition, expansiveness, connection with nature, slowing down, being strong and vulnerable and brave and scared all at the same time. I value all parts of everyone- all of our anxieties and fears and sadnesses, I value our inner children, our hopes and dreams, I value the little you that said "when I grow up I want to be a ___!"

name: Elizabeth Olsen (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
Have you noticed behavior changes in your teen? Is your teenager struggling with identity issues, bullying, body image concerns, or feeling like they don’t fit in? You’re not alone, and help is here. If your teen is dealing with these challenges, they may feel isolated, anxious, or unsure of themselves. You might notice things such as withdrawal from family activities, declining grades, or mood swings. These issues can impact their self-esteem, mental health, and overall well-being, making it hard for them to navigate daily life and school. The pressure to fit in and find their place can feel overwhelming and exhausting.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
With specialized training in teen mental health, I am dedicated to helping teenagers overcome these challenges and build a brighter future. Picture your teen feeling more confident, connected, and capable of handling life's challenges. Take the first step towards supporting your teen by scheduling a free 15-minute consultation today.

name: Sasha Strong (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My view of therapy is grounded in the perspective of brilliant sanity: all beings have an innate wakefulness that underlies any suffering and confusion. I use Hakomi, IFS, DBT, humanistic, and trauma-informed approaches to help you find a new relationship with your basic goodness. I like to co-create an individualized approach to therapy by drawing on what I have studied and practiced and adapting that to your preferences and interests. Basically, I think that what we should do in therapy is what works best for you! I work from a neuro-affirming, anti-oppression, and non-pathologizing stance. While I am open to providing you with a diagnosis if you wish (and we may need to do so in order to bill insurance), I operate in a strength-based model that celebrates your resources, resilience, and wisdom.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I'm interested in building queer community and helping queer and trans folks overcome stigma, erasure, and internalized oppression. I try to use my academic status and professional position to advance equity and access for queer, trans, and multiply marginalized folks. I think providing access to care is much more important that gatekeeping around gender. I'm also interested in psychedelic assisted therapy, harm reduction, and cognitive liberty. I view these medicines as powerful tools for healing, and I'm interested in helping people integrate these psychospiritual experiences. I'm a late-identified Autistic person with low support needs and I enjoy helping people unmask / learn to mask strategically and on purpose. I believe in and enjoy the emancipatory power of pleasure. This includes touch, sexuality, and the many fun things we get to do as human animals. I enjoy cycling, camping, hiking, cooking, and reading. I speak French, and I'm learning Spanish.

name: Tiffany McCleary, Psy.D. (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I integrate current research and traditional approaches to help clients reduce distress, improve daily functioning, and find fulfillment. Many clients I work with benefit from learning specific skills for stress management and interpersonal effectiveness. Others explore broader issues related to clarifying values and personal growth. I provide a safe, non-judgmental environment that allows us to build a collaborative relationship as we work toward symptom relief, self-understanding, positive change, and personal growth. My orientation and training is an integrated approach of cognitive-behavioral therapy, interpersonal process therapy, and family systems therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received my Doctorate in Clinical Psychology from Pacific University in 2008, Pre-doctoral Internship at Northwestern University Counseling Center 2007-2008, and have been a licensed Psychologist since 2009. I have worked in community mental health, counseling centers, out-patient clinics, and private practice. I have experience working with individuals, couples, and families (children ages 4 - 17). I specialize in helping people who struggle with anxiety, depression, trauma recovery, low self-esteem, relationship difficulties, LGBTQ issues, and parenting challenges.

name: Mahalia Lind-Diamond (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is collaborative, warm, gently challenging and validating. I believe that we all have the innate ability to heal, grow, and break patterns, but sometimes we need support in gathering more tools to be able to do so. We often carry both our darkest moments, and experiences of excitement and joy alone. It is my belief that we heal through relationship. This is where we are able to reveal our truest selves and heal the wounds that we have both inherited and developed throughout our lives. I utilize tools from psychodynamic, emotion focused, somatic and attachment based lenses. My approach to counseling is relational, meaning I will ask you to both notice how you relate to me in our sessions, and how you relate to your self and body. We will practice noticing physical sensations that arise in your body and make connections between our therapeutic relationship and how it might mirror other dynamics in your life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
While therapy will not solve systemic oppression, it is a place that we can examine our role in those systems and how they impact our lives. I am committed to continually exploring my place in oppressive systems and how my identities interact with those of my clients. I try my best to make therapy accessible to everyone through offering sliding scale options for those paying out of pocket. If you would like to share your concerns about OHA taking away pre-licensed counselors ability to accept OHP HealthShare insurance starting July 2025, please use this link to do so: https://fastdemocracy.com/campaigns/tICYT3fdX/Protect-Access-to-Your-Mental-Health-Provider/

name: Kris Cavanaugh LPC, CADCI
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
People seek therapy for different reasons but most often because they have been unable to 'fix' something that feels off in their life. I believe every individual and family operates within its own unique culture and system. My goal in working with clients is to maximize existing strengths, facilitate insight into patterns of behavior and foster the development of new skills, while honoring the uniqueness of each individual or system
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Licensed Professional Counselor and Certified Alcohol and Drug Counselor. I have experience working with kids and adults managing stress from divorce. I am trained as a mediator and as a parent coordinator and work extensively with high conflict families as they maneuver changes in their family system.

name: Danielle Davis (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapeutic approach is organized by a belief that our bodies hold the secrets of our wounds as well as the intrinsic wisdom to heal them. At this site of embodiment, profound transformation naturally occurs: of wounding into self-knowing, of constriction into aliveness, of pain into resource. I practice trauma-informed, mindfulness-based and attachment-focused somatic therapy which invites you to slow way down & bring curious attention to the fabric of your inner world. Practicing non-judgmental attention to your moment-to-moment experience within the context of a safe and supportive relationship allows us to collaboratively study how your particular system organizes experience & to become aware of the patterns in operation & their origins. Throughout this map-making process, your system will organically show us what it needs in order to process and repair places of pain and stuckness. With kiddos, I use sand tray and expressive arts therapies, + family sessions.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I work from a systems perspective that understands our individual self as being situated within & impacted by a nest of wider systems - generational, familial, societal, & global. These contexts - many of which have taken on oppressive qualities via the vehicles of settler colonialism, racism, cis-hetero patriarchy, & late-capitalism - impact us and the families we are embedded within profoundly; they inform how we experience ourselves in the world, imprinting our brains and our bodies, shaping us in often deeply unconscious ways. When we slow down and bring mindful, non-judgemental awareness to our here-and-now experience, subconscious patterns begin to reveal themselves, giving us the possibility to cultivate understanding, nourishment, resilience, and ultimately, transformation.

name: Mara McLoughlin
approaches:
What We Specialize In
My team specializes in a community-based, proactive approach to mental health. We work with autistic and other socially struggling teens, young adults, and their parents. Our lens is neurodiversity-affirming and informed by the social model of disability and the latest autism research by actually autistic researchers. We teach in a group setting, entirely via Zoom. Class materials, handouts, videos, class roster is all provided to families for the 16-week masterclass. We provide a safe, sheltered small group context in which participants are seen and heard, validated and acknowledged. These are not soft skills — these are hard skills. We've got your back.
Our Personal Beliefs and Interests
Being effective is doing what works. We teach interpersonal — and intrapersonal — skills to anyone who is motivated to learn more about themselves and others, and how to use discernment to navigate the social world. It's complicated! And even those who have great social skills don't necessarily know how to teach them. Ours is an experiential approach that also trains the environment — in this case, the family context — to look at behavior through an empathy lens. We teach about self-regulation and co-regulation, which is the strongest safety cue. We believe that humans heal in community. We are a multilingual, multicultural team of speech therapists, occupational therapists, direct support workers, and licensed professional counselors — a crackerjack group to which your family will have full access throughout the 16-week course.

name: Cassondra Arroyo Asla (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Cassie combines humor, a direct approach when needed, and her easygoing personality to create a relaxed, welcoming space where clients feel comfortable, supported, and ready to dive into the work. Cassie utilizes an integrative, evidence- based approach to therapy, drawing from Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT), Transformational Systemic Therapy, Attachment Theory, and Gottman Couples Therapy. By combining these frameworks, she helps clients foster lasting change, strengthen emotional resilience, and deepen meaningful connections in their relationships.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Before joining The Path Center, Cassie worked at a child- and family-focused clinic in Southeast Portland, where she provided support to marginalized youth and their families. She also co-led groups at The Portland DBT Institute, where she helped young adults, ages 18-25, develop crucial skills for understanding and managing emotions while navigating the challenges of chronic health conditions and mood disorders. Prior to her mental health work, Cassie spent years at a child development center, where she provided developmental play therapy to children and families, particularly those with children ages 6 months to 5. Additionally, Cassie spent 16 years as a stay-at-home parent to her four children and has had the privilege of welcoming a friend of her daughters into the family as an additional child since 2021, navigating the role of caregiver and parent.

name: Cayla Panitz (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I collaborate with people to help them live a more authentic, connected life. I approach counseling with a deep empathy and belief in the power of your strengths and encourage insight, empowerment and self-compassion. I rely on the creation of a strong therapeutic relationship and targeted interventions to do so. I also aim to point out the ways that our experiences are shaped by the systems we live within and are impacted by. My counseling style is sincere, collaborative, validating and often filled with nerdy metaphors, a good laugh and pet stories.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I’ve got PNW roots. I find happiness at Portland Thorns games, playing with the dog, getting outside and reading Samantha Irby books. I work within a relational, feminist, anti-oppression framework. I adhere to the Health At Every Size® paradigm and work to dismantle weight stigma and fat phobia. I am queer affirming and make every effort to create an anti-racist, inclusive environment for people of all walks of life including, but not limited to: body size/shape, gender, race, country of origin, sexual orientation, ethnicity, religion and disability status. I am continually learning and growing in my own anti-oppression work. I received my Masters of Counseling Psychology from Lewis and Clark College. My experience involves individual, group and family work in a variety of settings and programs. In addition, I have specialized training in the treatment of eating disorders & the application of Intuitive Eating, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, and Emotion Focused Family Therapy.

name: Monika Sammon (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that healing begins in a safe and supportive environment where you feel seen, heard, and valued. My approach to therapy is collaborative, client-centered, and grounded in a systemic lens, honoring your unique story and strengths. I integrate Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT) with an attachment theory focus, Internal Family Systems (IFS), Narrative Therapy, and Child-Centered Play Therapy to best fit your therapeutic goals.
My Values as a Therapist
My experience as a therapist has been shaped by my work in community-based mental health, where I have supported individuals, children, adolescents, couples, and families through a variety of challenges. Working with diverse populations, including marginalized communities and those affected by trauma, has deepened my appreciation for the unique stories each client brings into therapy and reinforced my commitment to providing trauma-informed, culturally responsive care through a systemic lens. I believe in meeting clients where they are at without judgment, offering a compassionate and collaborative space for healing, self-discovery, and growth. My approach is grounded in empathy, respect, and an understanding of the complex systems that impact mental health, with a dedication to supporting clients in building meaningful relationships, embracing their strengths, and achieving their personal and therapeutic goals.

name: Emily Aumann (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I am committed to cultivating collaborative therapeutic relationships rooted in honesty and shared humanity. My goal is to meet you where you are and walk with you towards the change you want in your life. I strive to listen deeply and weave pragmatic tools, information, and reflection. Together I believe we can tend a space of care and curiosity to integrate difficult experiences. My trauma-aware theoretical lens is influenced by somatic parts work, polyvagal theory, relational, attachment, and pain recovery models. I combine a liberation health framework with what I know about human nervous systems to practice healing as a relational art.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My approach to therapy rests on the understanding that mental, emotional, physical, and spiritual health are not separate, and individual and collective wellbeing are deeply intertwined. Personal experience unraveling chronic pain combined with an awe of the human body and brain stokes my fire to share this work with others. I am interested in what possibilities arise when we center the body in our healing, particularly related to chronic pain/symptoms and trauma. I recognize social, cultural, economic, and political contexts as layers of the complex being that is you. Alongside this work, I enjoy tending the garden, creating in the kitchen, making/listening to music, befriending animals, and adventuring outside. I find deep healing through connection to land and seasonal cycles.

name: Ally Simone (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe every individual is the expert of their lived experience and is equipped with the resources they need to grow, change, and thrive, and I also believe we all need a little help to get there sometimes. I am here to walk alongside you in your journey, offering empathy, challenging harmful beliefs, and providing tools for personal growth. I view relationships through an attachment lens and explore emotions as a way to better understand ourselves and others. As a trauma survivor, I have a real understanding of how past experiences can shape our current realities, and the transformative power of processing old wounds. We will work together to create a safe and brave space where you can practice noticing and disrupting patterns that aren't serving you, identifying your needs, setting boundaries, and improving communication. While most of our work is done in session, I also like to offer reading and practices on topics like mindfulness, self-compassion, and nervous system regulation.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Systems theory is foundational to my work as a therapist, and I am always interested in exploring the factors outside of ourselves (e.g. family, culture, and politics) that contribute to who we are, what we believe, and how we feel. I welcome all parts of your identity into our work together and aim to co-create a space where we can explore these external influences as well as our own internal narratives. I am anti-racist, LGBTQIA+ and gender affirming, aligned with Health at Every Size principles, sex and kink positive, and deeply enjoy working with relationships in all types of configurations, including consensual non-monogamy, whether you identify as polyamorous, swingers, monogamish, or otherwise.

name: Ariel Anderson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe in helping empower you to solve your own problems through cultivating confidence, positive regard, and gaining power over thought distortions. I believe that experiential learning through the art making process and the therapeutic relationship can help one increase their ability for creative problem solving, self soothing, thought exploration, emotional expression, somatic processing, healthy communication, and reflective distance. I believe in dealing with the complexity of this world through utilizing chaos theory– the more we fill up the metaphorical toolbox of coping skills, the more options we have to use whenever life brings us troubles or something unexpected, and the more we have an open mind to potential paths forward when we face troubles, the more likely we will choose a path that is in line with our values and helps us in achieving our desired goals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I was born and raised in SE Portland, attended Linn Benton Community College, Oregon State University, and graduated from Marylhurst University. I identify as a pansexual female. In my free time I enjoy: making multimedia artwork, reading fantasy and sci-fi novels, listening to audiobook lectures about astrophysics and the universe, creative writing, cuddling with my Great Dane and binge watching TV shows, baking and cooking, hanging out with friends, and DIY projects. I am passionate about working with individuals struggling with addiction because I detest the stigma that exists for addicts and think addiction is intimately connected with one's other mental health struggles and treating them together is one of the best ways to create a holistic and effective relapse prevention plan. I am also passionate about helping folks stay in recovery because I know it is a life long journey, not a box that can be checked by going to treatment.

name: Luke Colbourn (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer therapy that draws from the wisdom of the buddhist tradition and evidence based practices (such as ACT & CBT). It is both gentle and direct. Together we build a relationship of trust and respect as we explore your feelings and circumstances. The insights we uncover together guide us to the interventions that can help you. Throughout therapy you will be cultivating self-awareness and developing a tailored set of skills for handling life's stresses with greater ease.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe part of my role as a therapist is to help my clients uncover their inherent wisdom. Another part is to help alleviate suffering here and now. In both cases, I bring active and deep listening to build an honest and engaged relationship. Insight and awareness practices from the buddhist tradition are an important part of my worldview as a therapist: I am influenced by two decades of meditation practice, participation in retreats, and active study with lineage holders. These experiences have taught me that we are all co-creators of our shared reality, each of us being influenced and influencing others. I value being able to see from my clients' perspective to deeply understand their experience. I also value my years of teaching and outdoor leadership experience, which help me remain pragmatic and solution focused when needed.

name: Emily LaMarca (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I believe the way we think often informs how we feel and behave. Together we can work on confronting harmful and distorted thought patterns in order to develop healthier and more hopeful views of life and the future. This can involve acknowledging past hurts and trauma, learning new ways to cope with anxiety or depression, practicing self-acceptance, developing and maintaining self-care habits, learning mindfulness exercises, and so much more. The counselor/client relationship is also an important piece of healing. I would like to partner with you in your journey and healing process. Wherever you are in that journey, I look forward to meeting you.
Why I Became a Therapist
I have always been drawn to "helping" fields of work but a big part of why I have chosen to pursue a career in counseling has been my own experience as a client. I learned skills that have helped to change some of my perspectives and enhance my life. The personal growth and healing I experienced through the therapeutic relationship are things I will always carry with me. I hope that I can help others in a similar manner.

name: James Whalen (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Our time in therapy will focus on teaching you effective skills to change the way that you relate to yourself, your emotions, and your thoughts, while also exploring and validating the reasons that you developed your current patterns in the first place. I pride myself in my deep sense of empathy, and have been told often that I have a calming, nonjudgmental presence that helps my clients feel safe and heard. I have nine years of experience in the mental health field, often treating clients at their most difficult moments, and I try to learn from and use that experience to help my clients move through challenging times safely.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I believe strongly in the power of counseling to change lives: I'm in this field because I've experienced it firsthand. I also know that reaching out for help can be challenging, especially when you're struggling. You're already making progress towards change by starting this search and being on here! I would love to help you continue that journey.

name: Kelly Aldinger (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
**TO START SERVICES, COMPLETE THE NEW PATIENT REQUEST FORM at www.upwellpsych.com/contact** Aloha! I'm a doctor of nursing practice who is passionate about helping people access their own strengths. I am committed to walking beside you as you gain insight, learn new skills, and grow the self-belief to create the life you want to live. I firmly believe that one path does not fit all, and my life is evidence of that. I worked as a therapist prior to becoming a PMHNP, and bring a diverse set of professional, educational and life experience to each session. Using empathy and authenticity, my goal is to help you find and live your own truth.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I've grew up in Hawai'i, and that spirit of aloha pervades my ethos in life. After growing up on a small island, I spread my wings and have lived on 5 continents and speak a bit of Spanish, French, and Woolof. You can often find me listening to the latest psychiatry/psychology podcast, reading books in the hammock, or suiting up to surf Oregon's cold coastal waters.

name: Chidozie Urom (HE/HIM)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
Oftentimes, we lack awareness of how our thinking patterns affect our behavior, so I use cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) to locate and change faulty assumptions that keep us from growth. CBT is an evidence-based treatment plan that is backed by science and research data. In addition, I believe that you are more than just an individual, but an integral part of society and the universe. I therefore makes use of Adlerian psychodynamic therapy. This treatment strategy is holistic and addresses all dimensions of the self. This includes social, occupational, physical, emotional and also spiritual. A strength-based approach which is mindful towards ethnic and cultural issues and concerns.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My mantra is that as a strong ally and observer of your life, it is my duty to guide you through your struggles towards resolution. While the journey may not be easy, we can learn to find joy in the climb. I immigrated from Nigeria at a young age. Young enough to know my culture and language and fresh enough to grasp the culture of the United States. I understand and relate to the struggles of minorities and immigrants. My experiences as an immigrant and minority has led to a calling in Psychology. I believe spirituality is valuable for those who seek it in all its form. I am a Christian and welcome all religious practices.

name: David Haddow
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a person-centered counselor and I work with clients around increasing awareness of their experience of reality through present-moment centered phenomena such as meditation, yoga, flow states, et al. I lean heavily on wisdom that has evolved from Eastern philosophies and Transpersonal psychological study. I work closely with clients to create a safe environment for deep exploration and the facilitation of personal growth towards desired areas of change. I have a focus on depression, anxiety, trauma, substance use disorder, and coping with societal or existential stressors. I also provide psychedelic experience preparation and post-integration under the supervision of Dr. Peter Addy for psychedelic-curious adults.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in change and I promote sustainable personal growth through self awareness and the development of insight. I support increasing client driven personal wellness goals and have a personal spiritual path of providing compassion and support for all beings. I work hard to increase access to therapy for clients through pro-bono and reduced-fee services as well as advocacy for the needs of people in marginalized positions in society.

name: Scott Fletcher (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a believer that people possess incredible strengths and resources and that it is my job as a therapist to help folks creatively explore and access these natural talents. I assume that each person is the expert when it comes to their experience and my role is to help them gain insight into their own thoughts, feelings, desires, behaviors and goals. I take the role of experienced guide, pointing out things that might be helpful in achieving your goals, instead of the directive expert teacher who tells you what you ought to be doing. I have experience guiding people to make meaningful changes to increase their satisfaction with life. People I work with often report increased ability to recognize and respond more skillfully to life’s stresses. A sense of confidence, contentment, hope, fulfillment, safety and stronger connections with your important people is what I work to achieve with you.
More About the People I Treat
Some of the areas that I have experience in helping: - Anxiety, fears, phobias, and panic attacks - Depression, unhappiness and feeling stuck in life - Emotional awareness and expressive communication to improve quality of relationships - Various trauma including physical, sexual, emotional, relational and family of origin abuse - Young adults or teens looking to build independence and confidence in self-identity - Psychedelic integration or processing of recent profound psychedelic experiences - ADHD or challenges with feeling as productive and focused as you’d like to be - I am an LGBT+ ally and advocate for the marginalized -Men wanting to improve their relationships - Anger and frustration in relationships - Navigating Life transitions - Grief, loss and mourning

name: Sarah Lange (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My goal as a therapist is to help my clients experience transformative change in their lives. I am interested in going deep to find out what our subconscious is creating in our lives and how self-understanding can help shift our unwanted patterns.
My Values as a Therapist
I use a holistic approach to therapy drawing on Family Systems theory, Existential, Attachment theory and Psychodynamic therapy lenses. I also use tools like mindfulness, and somatic practices. I believe in “inner healing intelligence,” the intrinsic knowledge that each of us possesses about how to heal ourselves.

name: Ellie Ritson
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I utilize evidence-based therapeutic approaches. I am trained in problem-solving therapy to find coping skills you actually like, cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) to identify thought processes that can help you feel good, dialectical behavioral therapy (DBT) to explore , internal family systems (IFS) to connect with the pieces that make you you, feedback-informed treatment (FIT) to make sure you're getting what you need from our sessions together, and polyvagal theory to help you connect to your body. Most of the people I work with experience trust and emotional relief within the first month, followed by additional sessions to begin working on long-term healing and growth.
My Values as a Therapist
I value meeting you wherever you are in life, supporting you without judgement, finding tools together that really work for you, and practicing therapy within my own life and development so I really know what I'm talking about. I'm not going to tell you to exercise or give you breathing exercises that I've never done before of if you don't want to. Long gone are the days where a therapist is aloof, high, and mighty. Today I'm here to be your wellness partner and find what works best for you. Together we can tackle things at your speed and in a way that makes you feel empowered and at ease.

name: Diana Russo
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I strive to provide a calm, safe and warm space for clients to show-up as their athentic self where they feel heard. I believe counseling is a collaboration between the client and counselor. My holistic approach combines Person-Centered Humanistic Therapy and Solution-Focused Components to foster growth and fulfillment. I firmly believe that unconditional positive regard and empathetic understanding are crucial in nurturing a person's desire for personal development. With a respectful, non-judgmental, multicultural perspective, I see supporting the client's goals as a partnership. Together, I aim to facilitate and develope a deeper understanding and self-knowledge and acceptance as well as your potiential for change and growth.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
After working in schools both in the United States and abroad Diana brings this unique perspective to the therapy setting where she focuses on life transitions, belonging and multicultural counseling to enrich and infor her lens for attachment, belonging and wellness.

name: Angela Keeney, LCSW (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My style of therapy is warm, collaborative and open to wherever the work may lead us, though I'll also help us to stay focused on your goals. I often use humor and creativity in my work.
