I'll check in along the way to make sure we're heading in a direction that is helpful and at a pace that feels right for you. The therapeutic relationship is one of the most important aspects of effective therapy, so it's important that this feels like a good fit. I truly love what I do and am endlessly inspired by the human capacity for growth and healing. My hope is to provide support, tools, and encouragement to help you find a greater sense of balance, authenticity, strength and overall well-being.
I'm a Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW), registered in the state of Oregon. I earned my Master's degree from Portland State University and completed my undergraduate work in psychology at Southern Oregon University. I have nearly 15 years of experience in the mental health field, including five years working in a residential treatment home for adolescent girls, and five years as a Child & Family Therapist in community mental health, prior to opening my private practice.

name: Colleen Burke-Sivers, LPC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is straightforward and genuine. For those who like therapy terms, I am a Humanistic practitioner. I build a secure and trusting connection with each person and help them to foster these kinds of connections with the people in their lives. I have a unique ability to put people at ease in the therapy sessions and to work to uncover the stumbling blocks that are keeping them from living full and satisfying lives.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have 25 years of post-graduate experience, working in agencies, schools, hospitals and private practice. Some specialities include depression, anxiety, and other mood disorders. Some unique populations I like to work with include new parents, young adults, and people making major life transitions. However, I find I enjoy a variety of people and have worked with various populations and have experience with a wide range of issues.

name: Jessica Powers
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My approach to helping is cognitive-behavioral, meaning that I am listening for negative thoughts (cognitive) that we can challenge and reframe more positively, as well as things that you can do (behavioral) to feel less anxious and more hopeful, and to take good care of yourself mentally, emotionally, and physically. I may also suggest many ideas drawn from dialectical-behavioral therapy (DBT), which is a kind of cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT) that focuses on mindfulness, emotion regulation, distress tolerance, and interpersonal effectiveness. I began practicing in 2015 at an intensive outpatient center, and completed my Master of Science in Clinical Mental Health Counseling in 2016. In the state of Oregon, I am a Licensed Professional Counselor, and Approved Supervisor Candidate, and a Certified Alcohol and Drug Counselor. I am currently in-network with some Providence, PacificSource, Kaiser, and Regence/BCBS of Oregon health plans.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that regardless of what we have done or been through in the past, we can all live the life we want, both now and in the future. I can help you learn and grow from your experiences, and provide the safety and comfort in our sessions together that give you the confidence to follow through on the changes you want to make. In my free time, I love coloring, creative journaling, and reading books. I have lived in Oregon since 2000, and I currently live with my husband and our two cats.

name: Hudson McCarthy (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I take an existential-humanistic approach with an anti-racist, social justice and holistic lens which means I see the client being the expert on their own life and I am there alongside them in support of their healing journey. I incorporate an eclectic variety of modalities that is unique to each individual as we work collaboratively in session. Some of these modalities include but not limited to: DBT skills, somatic therapy, harm reduction, creativity and trauma informed. I graduated from Lewis and Clark College in 2024 with a Masters of Art in Professional Mental Health Counseling focusing on Addictions and certifying in Eco-Therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a counselor I strive for a calm, compassionate, empathetic and authentic energy. Life is hard, messy and sometimes even chaotic. I strongly believe that everyone has the capacity within themselves to see hope again when it was once lost. As someone who holds various marginalized identities, I know first hand how challenging it can be to have self compassion, patience with yourself in times of healing and growth, and I also know that it's very possible with persistence, resilience and just continuing to work on personal growth. My interests of focus reside in harm reduction approaches to substance use, incorporating nature into therapy and creative strategies to navigating hard feelings and working with folx who are just transitioning into adulthood to later adulthood. I enjoy leaning into just having raw, authentic dialogue in sessions and feel that with time a strong therapeutic relationship will be made that feels safe.

name: Carli Barnum (SHE/HER/ELLA)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I came to the counseling profession in an unconventional way. I worked as Peace Corps Volunteer in a rural town along the Dominican/Haitian border where I worked with school counselors to enhance their sex education curricula. In addition to my training as a clinical mental health counselor, I am also a trained school counselor. Because of this, my approach emphasizes skill sharing and understanding the why behind the how. As an experiential therapist, I facilitate transformative experiences that lead to those "AHA" moments and encourage clients to discover new ways of interacting with their environment. My goal is to create meaningful experiences that model and guide clients in forming fresh, fulfilling connections with themselves and the world around them.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that each individual is the expert of their own life. Because of this my approach is client lead and collaborative in nature. I look for progress over perfection, and I think having a strong connection to personal values serves as a compass for how to make life decisions. In my free time I plan art summer camps for low income students. I press flowers, do mosaics, and participate in a book club. I feel most alive when I am tending an altar or working in my garden.

name: Renata Austin (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In our counseling relationship I provide a safe place to be fully yourself, focus on challenges you are experiencing, and consider making changes that might be helpful to you. I help you look at things differently by making connections you may not be aware of and shining a light on things that are in the background of your experience. I practice counseling with a non-judgmental, validating, empathic and strengths-based approach focused on building trust so that you feel safe in sharing what you need to. I work collaboratively with you to discover unmet needs, underlying feelings, important values, and practical solutions in collaboration with you. I believe that individual issues don’t exist in a vacuum and have to be understood within the context of their relationships, family, and community. I seek to understand you through the intersection of your many identities (race, gender, sexuality, ability, class) and broach conversations about systems of oppression in my work.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I identify as a biracial 3rd gen filipina/white, able-bodied, neurodivergent cisgender woman. As a person who exists in the in-between, I have first hand experience navigating the challenges of not “making sense” to others and of feeling like I don’t belong, and bring this understanding into my work with clients. I’m also familiar with the mental health stigma present in AAPI communities, in particular the Filipino/a/x community. I grew up in Berkeley, CA and moved to Portland, OR in 2011. I completed my M.S in Marriage Couple and Family Counseling from Portland State University in 2020 and spent two years working as a counselor serving refugees, immigrants and other low income individuals at a local community agency. Before I became a counselor I engaged in supporting individuals and families around reproductive health, pregnancy options, early childhood, and parent engagement. Outside of my work I enjoy spending quality time with friends, gardening, camping, and playing music.

name: Dr. Nicholas Hong (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background
I am Dr. Nick Hong, a licensed psychologist who provides individual therapy for adults and teenagers. I received my PhD in Clinical Psychology at the University of Oregon, advancing and honing my clinical skills at Stanford University and at the University of New Mexico medical centers after. I have worked in a number of settings over the last two decades, from outpatient therapy clinic to inpatient hospital; spoken at nationally and internationally conferences; facilitated regular group therapy sessions and led workshops for families, parents, and couples; and directed clinical, research, and psychology internship programs.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I believe that we are born with the inherent desire to love and feel loved. We want to feel safe and seen. You haven't had all the answers; that's okay. We'll work together to "see" you -- listening to what hurt or has helped. I will counsel you on the basis of what has been shown to work in the science of applied psychology and equally important, on the basis of what works for you. While I am collaborative in my approach, I am also not shy about suggesting direction and providing pragmatic strategies to empower and evolve you to a place of greater contentment. In the context of a safe relationship, you can heal through the weekly pauses in therapy that is tailored to your life experiences and in which you feel seen.

name: Heather Asaadi (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am passionate about building meaningful therapeutic relationships - it is within relationships that healing and growth occur. Within the safety of therapy, you can more fully explore your patterns, come to understand factors that contributed to your development, and find a balance between accepting yourself and changing patterns that are not serving you anymore. I work to be attuned to the systems that impact us, such as, the social and cultural effects on our lives, as well as, exploring how your identities intersect and interact with your environment. My approach is eclectic and trauma-informed. I gravitate towards relational/interpersonal therapies which emphasize exploring patterns of relational disconnection and enhancing connection. My practice is focused on collaborating using a dialectical framework, which is about looking through a both/and lens to appreciate complexity, find more balance, and increase compassion towards ourselves and others.

name: Andie Whittaker (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer trauma-informed, person-centered, somatic, cultural relational, and attachment-based therapy. I believe therapy can be a place to deepen our understanding of ourselves, which can in return offer more choice to day-to-day life moments. I remain curious, compassionate, and non-judgemental to complex emotional experiences.
More About the People I Treat
I work with many different populations. From my lived experience of being a neurodivergent + genderqueer person, I strive to offer a creative, individualized, and affirming space. I value your lived experience and intuition. Some modalities I lean on are AEDP, attachment lenses, somatics, and relational approaches.

name: Amanda Frankel
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a student of mindfulness and transpersonal studies, the modalities I offer support you to become more aware of thoughts and behaviors through such practices as mindful attunement, breathwork, and meditation techniques. I welcome spirituality of all kinds. As a former environmental educator, I also deeply believe in the restorative powers of nature. My background, experience, and values weave together into a therapeutic approach that is embodied, curious, and creative. If this resonates with you, please reach out to set up a consultation.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In my spare time, you can find me exploring Oregon’s backcountry with my dog Sitka, reading a book by the creek, seeing live music, or slowly drinking a good cup of coffee, preferably in the mountains.

name: Amanda Smith
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Specialties: Eating disorders, body image, ADHD, Anxiety, Depression, Parenting Support, Career & Life Transitions Issues: Stress, Burnout, Emotional Regulation, Low Self-Esteem, School/Academic Issues, Family Conflict, Trauma (Complex and Developmental) Approaches / Modalities: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Spiritual/Christain counseling, Trauma-Informed Therapy, Mindfulness-Based Techniques, Self-Compassion Practices, Person-Centered Therapy
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a therapist because I believe in the power of being truly seen, heard, and supported—especially during life’s most difficult moments. From a young age, I was drawn to understanding people’s emotions and stories. Over time, I saw how meaningful change could happen when someone had a safe space to explore their struggles without judgment. Becoming a therapist allowed me to combine that passion with evidence-based tools to help others heal, grow, and reconnect with themselves. It’s an honor to walk beside people on their journey toward clarity, confidence, and self-compassion.

name: Kat Schoolland (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
With skill-building through Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) and Exposure Response Prevention (ERP), we help kids and parents develop a toolbox of evidenced based strategies to face their fears and courageously take back their lives from Anxiety and OCD. The therapy we provide focuses on teaching children to be aware of their own emotions, to notice and critically evaluate their thoughts and to employ adaptive behavioral strategies, mindfulness, and other self-soothing skills to help themselves remain grounded in difficult situations rather than getting swept away by worry or big emotions. For over a decade we have successfully helped children build skills to manage stress, improve mood, increase self-regulation, and optimize potential. My playful, energetic, and collaborative style connects with kids and I prioritize finding fun, developmentally-appropriate ways to individualize treatment. If you're feeling stuck give me a call, I can help.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I understand that you want the best for your child. You're proactive about their emotional well-being and ready to support them through the challenges of growing up in today’s world. You recognize the importance of resilience and emotional intelligence, and you're looking for expert guidance to foster these qualities in your child. I believe in a collaborative approach, where therapy isn't just a service, but a true partnership. I believe strongly in the power of mindfulness & self-compassion. I integrate these principles into my own life and in my practice, creating a rich toolkit of techniques and exercises for those I works with. These skills are designed to guide my young clients on a path of self-care and acceptance, helping them build a lifelong habit research shows can profoundly impact their well-being. Through this approach, I offer a meaningful way for kids to connect with themselves and the world around them in a positive, impactful manner.

name: Jennie Hagen (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Change is a scary thing. Usually, people are ready to make changes when they’ve come to a couple different conclusions. You may have found that the ways you’ve tried to cope with an issue aren’t working and you’re feeling stuck. Or you may know that something in your current situation isn’t working but you’re not sure what to do about it. When people come to counseling, it’s often because they’ve recognized that the discomfort of things staying the same has become worse than the fear of the unknown. During our work together, I may ask you to try out some new ways of thinking or new behaviors. But first we will establish a foundation of trust. This helps create a safe space where you will be heard and understood; where you know you can open up without judgment. Moving at your own pace, we begin to edge gradually out of your comfort zone. By doing this mindfully we can make small changes that feel safe and attainable, while keeping a close eye on emotions that come up during the process.
My Values as a Therapist
We as therapists don't have all the answers. You're the one driving the car down the path we navigate together, and I'm helping read the map (if only there was a GPS for mental health!) I'm constantly reading, learning, and absorbing to learn more about the world, my place in it, and how to better support the people I work with. I have the most training and experience in supporting the LGBTQ+ community and am building knowledge around racial justice topics. Black Lives Matter. I'm an intersectional feminist and can help even if you're not exactly sure what that means (I'd love to explain!). I also enjoy working with veterans and volunteer with the Returning Veterans Project. I'm a mom of two young children, and if you're a parent reading this - you're doing an amazing job. I see you, and know how hard it is. Everyone is doing the best they can with the information they have. Processing our traumas and building community help us to do better for ourselves & each other.

name: Tracy Bryce Farmer (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use an interactive approach where you will no longer feel alone in looking at your life. I combine work on feelings with concrete skills that will help you begin to make changes from the first visit. I enjoy working with individuals, couples, families and groups on personal, relational and professional growth.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Talking with me is different. It's lively and emotional and practical. I have a Master's in Fine Arts degree and I bring creativity to the change process. My Master's in Social Work degree will empower you to deal with oppressive systems. I became a Certified Alcohol and Drug counselor because addiction affects so many of us. I started my training in New York City more than 20 years ago and I update my education continually. I can support you to cope with your past in a safer way, in order to make the present work better for you in the here and now.

name: Ashley Davis (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am an art therapist in training, specializing in grief and trauma. By integrating the creative process and making art in the therapeutic practice, I hope to provide clients with unique and powerful tools for self-expression, exploration, and healing. Through a combination of methods, art therapy can be effective in helping clients achieve harmony between their emotional, intellectual, spiritual and physical aspects of life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As an adoptee and single parent, she brings a deep personal understanding of caregiving, personal growth, and the unique challenges of adoption. In her free time, Ashley enjoys anything to do with the outdoors like kayaking, fishing, birding, and whale watching. She also likes reading, running, playing guitar and creating art! She is consistently pursuing additional education and training to expand her existing knowledge and skills for the communities she serves and has plans to become a certified EMDR therapist.

name: Elena Logvinenko (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Please see my website for more info about me, my practice, and my general vibe :)

name: Ember Von Letkemann
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Through EMDR and other body based methods, I hope to support you in feeling at home in your body. At times life comes with challenges that can prevent us from feeling authentic and whole. I aim to help folks to regain and strengthen their sense of contentedness, while acknowledging the challenges in their environment. I have experience working with healthcare professionals, including those who offer crisis services. Through EMDR and other body based approaches, I like to help people to find relief from anxiety, depression and post traumatic stress. I like to work with clients who are looking to regain or strengthen their sense of self, and sense of meaning. Finding a good fit for therapy is important. Please feel free to contact me for a consult or with any questions.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in each person's innate ability to heal. This can happen in a variety of ways, one being through the counseling relationship. I have a special interest in nature, it's cycles and the lessons that it can teach us about life and healing. I have worked directly in mental health crisis services for many years and find that personal and/or professional crisis can be an opportunity for profound, positive change. Through EMDR, expressive arts and other body based approaches, I hope to help folks to feel more at home in their bodies. Helping folks to find or regain their authenticity and sense of meaning is my hope.

name: Stefan Bulatovic (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I hold a Master of Science in Nursing (MSN) degree from the University of San Diego, completed in 2021, and have a robust background as a Registered Nurse in the mental health field, where I gained six years of experience prior to pursuing my advanced degree. My approach combines this extensive experience with a commitment to individualized care. I take the time to explore the various factors influencing your mental health and work to create a treatment plan that is both effective and collaboratively developed. My focus is on creating a supportive environment where you feel heard and empowered in your treatment journey.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that every individual deserves a compassionate, empathetic approach to mental health care, and I am deeply committed to fostering a therapeutic relationship built on trust and mutual respect. Outside of my professional life, I am passionate about spending time in the nature, fishing, and socializing. These interests help me stay balanced and bring a holistic perspective to my practice. I value continuous learning and strive to incorporate new insights and approaches into my work to better support my clients.

name: Anna Brown (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
Deepening our capacity for self-compassion decreases the internalized shame we come into contact within our selves and others. My approach is to lead with curiosity to distill habitual judgment; by expanding your own self-understanding, you are creating internal space to process old hurts and heal.
My Values as a Therapist
Autonomy, collaboration, and humor are my guiding lights as a therapist. This is your therapy and it can look however you need it to. I love collaborating with clients to determine what they want more or less of in their life and tailoring therapeutic goals to reflect unique needs. Humor reminds us that we are two people in a room *or a zoom* learning together and that therapy is not just a sad and scary space to process pain - it is also a tool that can help you find more joy, love, and connection in your life and relationships.

name: Cathy Wenberg (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
With over 20 years of experience supporting individuals navigating the complexities of life, I deeply understand the emotional and psychological impacts that accompany periods of change and transition. I utilize a strengths-based and person-centered approach to highlight each person's unique lived experience and capabilities. Through Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) and psychodynamic interventions, I aim to help you build healthier thought patterns, cope with emotions, and address deeper-rooted issues that contribute to your overall distress. Life’s transitions, whether expected or unexpected, can be uncomfortable. However, with the right support, these moments can become opportunities for profound transformation. I look forward to supporting you as we explore the strengths and solutions that can help you lead a more fulfilling life, even in the face of life’s challenges.

name: Kelly Washam (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I promise to be supportive in every step of your journey. My goal is to help you grow from your struggles, heal from your pain, and move forward to where you want to be in your life. I achieve this by providing a neutral safe space, listening to your concerns, and customizing a treatment plan. I specialize in helping people struggling with relationships, life transitions, depression, anxiety, and addiction/substance use (your own addiction or helping you deal with a family members'). For the past 20+ years I have been providing confidential counseling enabling people to learn new ways of coping with life's problems. I believe change is possible.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that each person lives most fully when they are connected to their deepest self, unimpeded by the barriers and burdens we all accumulate during our lives. My work is to help people free themselves so they can live from a place of honesty, compassion and love. I believe we all have the capability to create the life we want to live. I will help you discover what blocks your life's development.

name: Emaline Friedman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My education is in humanistic, existential, and psychoanalytic approaches to personality maturation. My PhD research focused on Internet Addiction and the way that digital life changes our experiences of ourselves, relationships, and ways we express ourselves. Some notable symptoms of digital culture - that psychotherapy addresses - are alienation, loneliness, increased social comparison, impulsivity, a sense of meaninglessness, and lowered tolerance for frustration and disappointment. As a therapist, I offer a protected space for working through fantasies, dreams, intense affects, and relational and emotional difficulties. Therapy is an opportunity to develop a relationship that supports construction of your individual history and thinking about personal and relationship issues that get pushed to the margins of awareness.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am constantly in awe of the tenderness, and tenacity, of the human spirit - especially under difficult social and environmental conditions. While therapy addresses self- and relationally-generated obstacles to living fully, external obstacles are ever-present in the clinic. This is why I practice therapy in addition to more overt activism and political participation around universal basic goods, digital and privacy rights, climate justice, and resisting the encroachment of financialization and digital capitalism. I also train and facilitate non-violent communication, in support of our right to education about how to stand for ourselves. I am also a lover of music, dogs, pickleball, and crossword puzzles.

name: Khrystal Johnson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Life can be tough. That is why I developed an ENTIRE practice devoted to talking about it. I'll use humor and compassion so we can talk about what it means to be human. I identify as Black and Native and am an enrolled member of the Umatilla Indian Reservation here in Oregon. I have experience working with Native populations through traditional cultural practices living on the reservation as well as providing therapy at the Native American Rehabilitation Association (NARA). Before obtaining my master's in Counseling Psychology, I gained over 10 years of experience working within the realms of social justice, student leadership, and diversity development. After graduating, I gained additional training in DBT (couples and individual), interpersonal neurobiology, trauma-informed practices, relationship therapy, and therapy targeting BIPOC. I welcome alternative relationship structures, BIPOC, and QPOC.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Finding the right therapist can sometimes feel overwhelming. It’s no secret that how well you connect with your counselor is essential to flourishing in therapy, and you have a right to choose what works for you. Therefore, I offer the first session free, so that you can decide if my approach best suits your needs. In session, I will create a safe atmosphere where you can learn skills to deal with life's obstacles, explore your concerns so you can begin to resolve them, and gain a deeper understanding of yourself and where you are at in life. My therapeutic style involves walking alongside you on your brave and adventurous path toward rediscovery. I hope to facilitate your process through open, supportive, and genuine dialogue.

name: Audrianna J. Gurr (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is described as active and supportive. I work with clients to address current issues and long-standing challenges. Applying sensitivity, empathy and compassion, I help individuals bring resolution to a wide range of life challenges and clinical psychological issues. I work with each client to find the appropriate match for your needs regarding the challenges you are facing. Our work will be active, I ask a lot of questions and we will discuss your answers to help you best help yourself. From time to time I use handouts, online resources and to supplement our work. I utilize an approach of applied empathy to help you feel safe and in time, confident to discuss your concerns. I'm trained in shame resilience, do a lot of assertiveness, cognitive reframing work as well as unpacking life experiences around maternal and menopause journeys as well as grief and loss along the life span, not just losing people, but lives changes and those effects on you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I specialize in maternal mental health challenges (miscarriage, infant loss, new parenting, difficult births), grief and loss, trauma, shame resilience, assertiveness training and addictions (both substances and behaviors) as well as perimenopause work. With an integration various methods, drawing from a selection of styles and techniques to apply what is most helpful for each individual. Together with each client I work to build on strengths and achieve life goals.

name: Molz Wirtz-Wold (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My mission is to support my clients with a compassionate and creative approach, attachment-centered using research based methods. I want to help people discover ways to heal and move forward in their lives, and to feel more free to be their authentic selves. Transgender/non binary affirming and identifying: they/them Trauma-informed practices Trained in Emotionally Focused Couples Counseling (EFT) and Gottman for Couples In training for PACT Level One (Psychobiological Approach to Couples Counseling) Sex therapist, AASECT member Polyamorous friendly/knowledgeable Anti-racist, anti-oppression, committed to doing my own work Kink friendly/knowledgeable Queer Enjoy supporting other therapists, helping professionals Experienced/trained in supporting people with Bipolar Disorder Experienced, trained in supporting Autistic people, people with ADHD and other kinds of neurodivergence Health at Every Size supportive, fat liberation
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I enjoy helping people explore their gender (or lack of gender) in a safe environment. I come to this work from an existential perspective and love helping people explore concepts such as identity, personal meaning, freedom and change. In relationship work/couples counseling, I want to help people develop healthy connections and attachments to the people they love and to themselves. I believe couples are the experts on each other, and my job is to help them make more accurate guesses about each of their their feelings, wants and needs. I am fat, queer and trans. I benefit from white privilege and work to educate myself so I can minimize the harm done via white supremacy culture in the therapy room and beyond. In my free time, I love to garden, play analog and modular synthesizers, hike to hot springs, and eat all the delicious foods. I play a lot of video games (recent fave is Valheim), go see live shows, and updating our 1906 foursquare home with my wife.

name: Joyce Yuan
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My goal in providing psychotherapy is to help people alleviate their suffering, improve their relationships, and understand themselves better. I believe all people have positive potential that can sometimes be stifled and find it rewarding to help others work through their obstacles. I have been trained in a wide range of theoretical approaches (including psychodynamic and cognitive-behavioral). I integrate insight-oriented and problem-solving techniques to best serve my clients' individual needs. I believe psychotherapy is a collaborative process by which thoughts and experiences are explored in a safe setting with a trained therapist. In order to help you resolve your problems and achieve your goals, it may be helpful to examine past life events that have brought you to this point, in addition to developing new coping skills and perspectives.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In addition to my private practice, I have worked at VA hospitals, where I provided psychotherapy to military veterans of all eras. I completed my undergraduate studies at Amherst College and my graduate training at the University of California, Berkeley. I have trained and worked at well-known medical institutions in Portland, New York City, and the San Francisco Bay Area. I also have a background in psychological research, studying emotional functioning in various clinical disorders.

name: Kestrel Fae Tippie (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I can help you: Heal stuck trauma that keeps you feeling distressed, shut down, anxious, isolated, and fragmented. Use therapy as a laboratory to re-pattern, experiment with, and integrate new ways of being. Experience your body as a safe refuge and wise communicator. Connect to Nature as a supportive refuge that cares for you. Increase creative access to joy, play, and passion. Find belonging with your people and build community. Nurture healthy relationships where you feel safe, cherished, held, and seen. Live your gifts and soul essence to bring what is needed into the world. Relish contentment, fulfillment, satisfaction, ease, and calm. Be available and present to enjoy the juice and goodness of life.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I have journeyed myself many times down this path, and supported many others. I will guide and accompany you with a compass to navigate by, a map of the territory we’re wading through, and a toolbox of skills and resources. We will create a transformative container in which you feel connected, supported, anchored, seen, and encouraged to practice and embody new ways of being. I know and trust completely that the life you long for is also longing for you and reaching back towards you. You can absolutely move through trauma and overwhelm to thriving. Let's get you rooted in deep belonging with life and connection with your people, relishing contentment and fulfillment, acting from your values of what matters most, and embodying your gifts for this world. That is so possible for you. If you can't access that right now, I'll hold it until you can see and trust it for yourself. Welcome. Your presence is a blessing and you are worthy of enjoying life and thriving. Let's journey together.

name: Amanda Feaver, MA, LPC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I love my work as a therapist. Before my graduate work in counseling and psychology I spent five years studying theology, developmental education and spiritual development. With a strong focus in mind, body, and spirit, I work with clients to find a deeper sense of congruence, wholeness, and presence. Counseling is a beautiful combination of compassionate presence and curiosity that leads to forward movement. Together, as we pay attention to your life, your loss, and your desires, you will become more aware of your own wisdom.
My Values as a Therapist
I’ve had the privilege of working with psychiatric patients, at community clinics, on crisis intervention teams, and in private practice. I’ve also spent time working internationally with people who have experienced extreme loss through the violence of war. Much of my work with clients here in Portland centers around grief from the loss of dreams, relationships, the death of loved ones, and our current ecological crisis. My clients come from a variety of communities and socio-economic backgrounds; through them I have learned so much about the strength of human beings. I thoroughly believe that positive and sustainable changes are possible for everyone and it is such an honor to witness the courage and resilience of the people and communities I work with as they seek growth, freedom and a new sense of well-being.

name: Emma Keefe (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer the kind of therapeutic relationship that does not seek to pathologize your behavior, but instead, helps you slow down with your thoughts, feelings and experiences, compassionately. Through the help of relational-cultural therapy, we examine the quality of your relationships, both past and present, and determine how these relationships may impact the way you orient to yourself, others, and the world. Polyvagal theory encourages us to explore what feels safe, and unsafe, to your body, and how your body communicates messages of ease and dis-ease on a moment-to-moment basis. Lastly, Buddhist psychology inspires us to bring moments of suffering to the surface and practice building the tolerance needed to examine them gently. We consider how attachment, aversion, impermanence, and many other Buddhist concepts might play a role in your suffering, and how you might be able to meet them with more equanimity today.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a mixed-race, white and Mexican Indigenous demi-woman. I am polyamorous, pansexual, and am dedicated to providing gender-affirming care. As a white-passing woman raised in the United States, I understand the responsibility I have to provide anti-racist care and remove all characteristics of white supremacy from my formal education and counseling work. I am also dedicated to providing affordable mental health counseling in my effort to combat our economic system's ability to monetize relational safety, compassion and mental health care.

name: Glynis 'Glee' Lumb (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I provide a wholistic, eclectic approach uniquely tailored to the individual or group pulling from 20 years of experience in human services, parenting, and clinical practice. I collaborate with clients to build positive experiences in a caring therapeutic relationship through talking (Person Centered, CBT), somatic approaches (Lifespan Integration, Trauma Informed, Art Therapy, Parts Work), mindfulness, playing (children), sand and art therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Trained and experienced in Art Therapy, Attachment theory, Trauma Informed Care, Solution Focused, Dialectical Behavior Therapy, Sand Tray, Lifespan Integration & Somatic Approaches, Flash Technique for Trauma Processing, Mindfulness and Spiritual Practice. I work through a lens of neurodiversity and intersectionality.

name: Olivia Pennelle (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
While my title is a therapist, I see my role as a co-collaborator, and it’s an honor. In my work I strive to provide support and problem solve in a trauma-informed and strengths-based way. Central to my approach is a focus on self-empowerment, autonomy, and self-direction. What that means is, I believe that we are all experts in our own lives and the therapeutic process should collaboratively strengthen and bring to the surface the skills and inner strength to harness our innate wisdom and strength. As a therapist associate, and fellow human, I am not an expert on what is best for you, but I can help you figure it out.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a person who lives in a larger body and navigates multiple disabilities and identities, I actively support HAES, disability justice, and making the world a more inclusive place for people like us to take up the space we deserve.

name: Lauren D'Agostino (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that the work of therapy is to come home to ourselves. Who we are is a blend of the ways we were raised, supported, marginalized and tended to. Our selfhood is our own and co-created by the people around us, and therapy is a gentle process of forming nourishing ties to others and learning to trust our own inner knowing. I work from a somatic and intergenerational framework, assisting clients in weaving together our bodies, minds, relationships and ways of knowing. In therapy, I will ask you challenging questions, validate your sorrow and rage and shine a light on your innate strengths. I will walk with you while we explore your histories, identities and dreams. I work in-person, virtually and ecotherapeutically (outdoors).

name: Taylor Alderson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a graduate of Pacifica Graduate Institute in Santa Barbara, California. I currently hold a Master of Clinical Counseling with an emphasis in Depth Psychology degree (MFT). I am also a certified practitioner in Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR). I am a trauma-informed clinician working through a structural family and ecological systems perspective. I use the science-informed Polyvagal Theory in helping my clients learn and understand their own nervous system survival responses increasing their ability to safely connect with others. Using evidence-based psycho education and exercises clients will learn tools to help manage mood states, relational difficulties, symptoms of anxiety and depression, and other manifestations of past relational and event traumas. I have completed MAPS-Certified MDMA-Assisted Therapy Practitioner training and Fluence's Psilocybin-Assisted Facilitator training programs. I am a licensed Psilocybin Facilitator.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My Philosophical approach to Psychotherapy includes a psychodynamic interpretation of mental and emotional processes rooted in psychoanalysis drawing from object relations, ego psychology and self psychology which aims to address the foundation and formulation of the psychological processes with the goal of reducing symptoms and improving people’s lives. My role as a therapist is to help people gain insight into their lives and current problems by evaluating and reviewing patterns that include emotions, thoughts, early life experience, and beliefs. Recognizing these patterns help people see how they avoid distress or develop defense mechanisms to cope. I believe these insights can allow for lasting change.

name: Vanessa Servellón (SHE/HER/ELLA)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My practice focuses on supporting individuals who are struggling with their relationship with food and/or their body’s and trauma. For this work, I use a Health At Every Size (HAES) and trauma informed lens. I believe it’s important to allow time to process experiences, learn coping skills and also how to find healing in a way that aligns with you and your values. We'll also explore how different parts of yourself can impact you in different life experiences. My practice is culturally sensitive, gender affirming and welcoming to all body sizes. I work through a holistic approach where I believe in including your whole self in therapy, which includes the mind-body connection, your background, values, beliefs, identity and any other important aspects that make up who you are. I believe that in order to experience the benefits of therapy, you have to connect and feel understood by your therapist. That’s why I value feedback through the therapy process. Every therapist has a different a
Why I Became a Therapist
Ever since I discovered the positive impact therapy can have, I became passionate about supporting others through their journey. Working on your mental health is a lifetime process. That’s because over time, you change and continuously go through different experiences. And some of these can even have long lasting effects on your emotional and mental health. Therapy can offer you a safe space to process these experiences, discover more about yourself and how you navigate the world, learn techniques & coping skills and dedicate time to your emotional wellbeing. It’s not easy to tell someone else about your experiences and emotions, it requires you to be vulnerable. I know what it can be like to step into the therapy world. It can be scary, but I

name: Cat Bice (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have been involved in mental health work since volunteering as a teen and my work has spanned a wide range of settings. My professional career started in the emergency department providing crisis intervention and spanned different roles in the hospital system before leaving to start my own practice. My history of in hospital work has given me insight into the particular stresses that medical professionals experience and sparked a commitment to working with physicians, nurses, and other health care providers.
My Approach to Helping
I know finding a therapist can be a daunting task, so it might be helpful to know a little about me. I have a warm, open style with room for humor. I work to help clients feel safe and heard so we can attend to the emotions that underlie the distress that brought you into therapy. With that goal in mind, I tend towards a straightforward approach and have been called direct, but my directness comes with compassion and deep respect for how hard it can be to show up to the therapeutic process. My work is grounded in relationships and connection, so I show up as me to my sessions. I am a mom, an animal lover, a gardener, and a cook. I believe in life-long professional growth and along with continuing to grow my skills as a therapist, I bring a commitment to an anti-oppressive lens and actively work to understand the roles of privilege and bias on our individual and collective wellbeing.

name: Skyler Johnston
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have undergone numerous trainings in trauma focused cognitive behavioral therapy (TF-CBT) and collaborative problem solving (CPS) and meet with a consultant weekly to ensure model fidelity. I’ve found these to be extremely helpful tools but of course everyone is different. For that reason, I work to adapt a unique approach depending on the client’s wants and needs. In general, I try to build a strong genuine relationship where people can feel safe and understood.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold a Master of Arts in Counseling Psychology from Lewis and Clark College. My undergraduate work was in psychology and creative writing and I have been passionate about personal development and mentorship for over twelve years. I have five years’ experience working with individuals across a wide spectrum of presenting concerns. My practice is further backed by extensive academic study of counseling theories, trauma informed care, psychopharmacology, couples counseling and substance abuse.

name: Kelly Hirstein
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My intention is to connect with you as a unique being, and to offer support and curiosity to the life experiences and challenges that bring you into therapy. Our work together may include the use of traditional talk therapy, interpersonal connection, present-moment experience, mindfulness, experiential and body-oriented approaches, studying thoughts and impulses, exploring dreams, spirituality and existential experience. My hope is to lead clients towards a greater sense of self-awareness, that can lead to more choices in the way you respond to or engage with life experiences. In addition to counseling, my background in Nutrition and Massage Therapy provide me with knowledge and awareness of physical health challenges, body symptoms, and lifestyle concerns.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I graduated from Lewis and Clark college in Professional Mental Health and Addictions with a focus in Eating Disorders and Somatic Psychology, and I have a certification in the Treatment of Eating Disorders. I am Certified Hakomi therapist, with additional training Attachment, and Re-creation of the Self through the M.E.T.A. Counseling Center (Mindful Experiential Therapy Approaches). This work is rooted in mindfulness and body-oriented approaches, and I completed a year long internship with this as an educational focus and approach to counseling. I have also studied with the Process Work Institute, which is based in Jungian dreaming process, body symptoms, shamanistic and spiritual exploration, and experiential approaches.

name: sandeep kumar
approaches:
I use Compassion Focused Therapy, which is similar to Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT). Both focus on the inner thoughts that drive behaviors. The process uncovers thoughts, examines what motivations and questions their relevance to the current situation. While CBT seeks to change thoughts, I ask my clients to accept thoughts that had been useful in the past. Negative self-talk develops at a young age and can be detrimental for healthy thinking and perspective. My style of therapy brings greater awareness to self-talk. It’s difficult to sustain this process without an equal amount of compassion. I emphasize self-kindness and awareness when working with self-talk. I believe in the benefits of positive self-talk and positive thinking, but it works best when authentic. I ask my clients, “What do you want to create for yourself in the world? What do you want for and from your life?” A person who believes that one can get what one seeks, stands a better chance of attaining it.
I've practiced meditation and martial arts for over 20 years, and after teaching children’s classes in the Japanese martial art, Aikido, I became interested in the intersection of mental health and the learning process. I saw that the mind and body were at an optimal learning receptivity when one was calm. As I worked with more students, I realized that many students lacked the confidence to accomplish what they wanted. I entered private practice, as a way to provide dedicated, ongoing healing for adults and children who seek a better way to experience their lives through improving self-esteem, and examining belief systems. We are often unconscious of core, ingrained beliefs, the narratives we hold about our own lives and how events fall into a constructed narration. Therapy allows a person to step back and see the narrative as something one has created, and can be recreated to follow a new journey where one is at greater choice.

name: Nancy Masters
approaches:
How We Can Help You
Skycloud Mental Health is a clinical placement site for Yale School of Nursing students. We have intake appointments available within a week! We are currently accepting new patients for medication management via telehealth in OR, WA, AZ, UT, MN, and NM, and have a therapy waitlist available on our website. In-person visits available near Portland, OR, with offices in Seattle, WA and Salt Lake City, UT coming soon! Go to skycloudhealth.com and click "Schedule Now" to register for an appointment. Alternatively, you can call (503) 694-3381, extension 2, to register and schedule over the phone. We are available for single-case agreements with Kaiser. Contact our practice for more info.

name: Lucia Kilday (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I believe in the power of collaboration and empowerment in mental health treatment. I work closely with my patients to develop personalized treatment plans that integrate medication management, therapy, lifestyle modifications, and complementary therapies. My goal is to not only alleviate symptoms but also to promote long-term mental health and well-being. I am dedicated to staying current with the latest research and best practices in psychiatric care to provide my patients with the highest quality of care. I look forward to partnering with you on your journey to mental wellness.
My Approach to Helping
I am dedicated to staying current with the latest research and best practices in psychiatric care to provide my patients with the highest quality of care. I look forward to partnering with you on your journey to mental wellness.

name: Sharad Yadav (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I view the counseling relationship as a kind of partnership where the client brings what they can of their authentic presence while the therapist brings the empathy, skilled observation and non-judgmental understanding needed to draw out the inner strength and wisdom of the client. Seeking help is an emotional risk. My desire is to honor your courage and create a safe environment for you, in all your uniqueness, to be seen and understood so that together we can face your problems and discover how life might become more manageable.
Social Justice Issues We Care About
As the child of immigrants, raised in a rural setting, I also maintain a passion for racial justice and identify with minority experiences. Having suffered with both depression and anxiety, weathering spiritually abusive environments and attending to the severe mental suffering of friends and family, I know both the pain and hope involved in healing from psychological and spiritual wounds. If you’re struggling, please reach out and book a free consultation or make an appointment. You’re not alone.

name: Amy Welsh (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am energetic, passionate, straightforward, and don’t skirt around issues or avoid addressing what’s needed. Most of my clients are skilled at talking about their problems but need help thinking about their problems differently. Years of crisis work have made me comfortable with holding you accountable to the work. I genuinely want you to feel better. We will find some humor and develop good metaphors along the way as the journey to healing is hard and painful work, but you don’t have to suffer getting there.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I use Cognitive Processing Therapy (CPT) to treat trauma disorders and anxiety related to traumatic events. CPT is a modern trauma treatment that evolved out of decades of research on CBT. CPT is a 15-18 week structured treatment approach that helps clarify the connection between our traumatic experiences, our views of ourselves, our views of others, and our active symptoms. Who is helped by CPT and how does it work? CPT works well with people who have active symptoms of Post Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD), anxiety, and/or depression that are related to a traumatic event and have an identifiable memory from that experience that they are able to describe or discuss.

name: Nicole Phelps (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is strengths-based. This means that I believe you are the expert of your life, unique and an individual. I am here to be a support to you, gently challenge you, and provide accountability. I am passionate about working with individuals who want to connect or re-connect to themselves by becoming intentional about what they need and want in their lives. I believe that we all have the answers within ourselves in order to thrive, but we need to be willing to reach out for support, surround ourselves with people who are going to foster this work, and give ourselves permission to be still and listen to what our bodies and minds are telling us. I commit to creating brave spaces for you, so you can nourish and grow the most important relationship you have, the relationship with yourself.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I earned my Master of Social Work from Portland State University and my Bachelors in Social Work from New Mexico Highlands University while living in Santa Fe, NM. I have had the privilege of working in various social services agencies in New Mexico and in Oregon whose focus is supporting individuals and families with children who have chronic medical needs, with survivors of domestic violence, and families who have suffered abuse and trauma. I also present at venues around the Portland metro area about trauma and how it impacts people’s lives. All of these experiences continue to inform and shape my work as a therapist. As part of my licensure, I participate in continuing education so that I can continue to learn and grow as a professional and better support my clients. When I am outside the therapy space, I practice self-care by taking in the beauty of Oregon, I enjoy reading, making delicious food to share with my family and friends, theater, storytelling and my many pets.

name: Allison Hoyman-Browe (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My primary theoretical orientation comes from Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), a mindfulness based practice which seeks to foster psychological flexibility, give you the tools you need to navigate hard emotions and live a values driven life. I tailor my sessions to the client before me, addressing their unique needs and goals by integrating ACT with trauma informed and harm reduction approaches. I am trained in Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR) a powerful tool for processing trauma. Our mental health is impacted by the world around us and I fundamentally believe that we all carry with us the impacts of our unique identities and positionalities, our families of origin as well as our found families, our experiences of joy as well as our experiences of trauma and oppression.

name: Dmitri Dosamantes (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is solution focused, relational and affirming. I strive to be culturally competent, and inclusive of all ways of being. I work from a compassionate and collaborative, strength-based approach, and capitalize on what works for you. I view the therapeutic process as an opportunity for us to work together to identify and address barriers in your life. Together we will find what best fits you.

name: Jami Howell
approaches:
My Background and Approach
If you are feeling stuck, overwhelmed, or dissatisfied, I can help you understand what is getting in your way and develop tools to help you move forward. My aim is to create an environment where you feel safe to explore your problems without being judged. Once we have established a working relationship built on mutual trust, honesty, and respect, my goals are to help you better understand your problems, identify obstacles that have inhibited your capacity for growth and support you in the process of making positive and often times difficult life changes. I've been training in Gestalt theory since 2008. I believe in the importance of understanding you as you are & attuning to your thoughts, feelings & actions to heighten your awareness. My approach is collaborative, client-centered & compassionate. Engaging in therapy is a courageous step. I would be glad to talk with you about how to start living the life you want. Set up a free phone consultation, and see my website.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In my practice, I work adults and adolescents which includes separate sessions with their parents. I treat people with wide range of mental health issues from various diverse backgrounds. My areas of expertise include grief counseling, body image issues, eating disorders, & relationship struggles. My approach is grounded in psychological theory and research.

name: Greg Campanile (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is psychodynamic. I have found the Internal Family Systems model to be helpful, and also use motivational interviewing, expressive arts interventions, and mindfulness training when appropriate. I have a background in arts and music, and creativity has an influence on the way I practice psychotherapy. Every client is unique, and I try to tailor my approach to meet your specific needs and to help you achieve your goals. I may be able to negotiate a sliding scale fee for clients paying out of pocket and experiencing financial hardship.

name: Lena-Liis Kiesel (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work from a Humanistic background and incorporate Attachment, Acceptance Commitment Therapy and Family Systems as well as other modalities including mindfulness as needed, individualized by client.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a specialty in substance abuse and am a CADCI. I have also worked extensively with couples and use the Gottman Method. With children I have experience with play therapy and sand tray. Brainspotting is a more recent addition to the modalities which I feel comfortable using.

name: Kendyl Watts (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I strive for a balance of therapeutic connection, support, reflection, and skill-building, but the amount of each is flexible depending on client needs and preferences. I draw heavily from a cognitive-behavioral framework with other evidence-based techniques woven in as appropriate. My approach is warm, responsive, and compassionate. I am a Certified Clinical Trauma Professional. My highest priority is helping you to reach the things that matter most to you, whatever they may be. For some, that may be a tangible goal or completed task; for others it's increased feelings of safety, clarity, or empowerment. I pride myself on being flexible and responsive to meet my client's unique needs.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I completed my Master of Social Work degree in 2004 with a concentration in Sexual Health Promotion and Advocacy. I obtained my clinical license in 2007 and continued the work I was already doing with a highly diverse client base in St. Louis, Missouri. I have extensive experience working with people living with or affected by HIV and AIDS. I also have many years of experience helping teens and adults in all stages of family-building including infertility, pregnancy, decisions around abortion and adoption, and early parenting. I consider it a privilege to provide letters of support for gender-affirming medical care and third-party reproductive technologies. I strive to create an affirming environment for ALL people regardless of body size and ability, racial identity, gender identity, spiritual beliefs, relationship status, and sexual behavior.

name: Tracey Hoffman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am passionate about helping my clients gain insight into recurring patterns, improve their coping skills, and address deeper issues on their journey to becoming a more whole, authentic, stable, and well human being with secure relationship capacities. I am interactive, reflective, and offer information and ideas etc. as needed to facilitate your needs and goals. I am interested in helping clients to understand themselves at a deeper level to access core issues and themes and effect change in neural networks through emotion focused and relational approaches. Therapy is individually tailored and focuses on your needs and goals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a licensed Psychologist (previously licensed at the Master's level) and have been in the field over 25 years. I specialize in individual therapy with adults and couples therapy. I have taught graduate classes and supervise students, interns, and psychology residents. I continue to seek opportunities for learning and growth in this work that I love so much.

name: Jennifer Jim, LPC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Imagine a bulls eye and at the center of that bulls eye is your best life. The life filled with calm, gratitude and self-compassion. My approach will help you name and identify your goals for treatment. We will then work together to reduce the intensity and frequency of symptoms you are experiencing so you can begin focusing on how to use gratitude and self-compassion to move forward. My approach is both didactic and focused on cognitive work. I also however come from a somatic approach through Accelerated Resolution Therapy (ART). ART is an eye movement therapy that can address trauma, phobias, ADHD, and many forms of anxiety.
My Approach to Helping
Due to the high demand for therapy services, I'm sure you've met with many clinicians who do not have availability for new clients. I'm included in that as I have a full roster. However, I wanted to offer something other than one-on-one therapy that can also help you. I have an M.S. in Education and am a former adjunct professor and clinical director. I used these skills and put together a self-help psychoeducational resource library that you can access for a monthly subscription. It isn't intended to replace therapy but be an added support while you wait or even in addition to treatment. The resource library currently has over 60 hours of content, and new material will be added each month. It includes the following: Stop Overthinking Podcast, Self-Talk Audit Workshop, Bold Goal Workbook, Burnout Prevention & Recovery Masterclass, and Stress-busting strategies training. If you're interested to know more, send me an email jennifer@renewtherapynw.com.

name: Shannon Champagne (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I worked for the VA Portland Healthcare System for three years, where I established skills in mindfulness, veteran-focused care, and substance use/trauma-informed care. I have extensive experience with person-centered and mindfulness-based therapies. My treatment approach is rooted in evidence-based practices that focus on addressing the whole person: mind, body, and soul. In our first session, we will explore what brought you to therapy and then decide the best ways to help you make progress. I also offer preparation and integration sessions pre and post medicine/psychedelic journeys. I have completed a MAPS and VA training for MDMA-Assisted Therapy. I am a board-certified Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW) from Portland, OR.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am passionate about healing, nature, spirituality, building community, and creating.

name: Melanie Sellers (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Change is a gradual and deeply personal process, and I believe your body holds the innate wisdom to guide you. My role is to support and empower you on your journey of self-discovery, creating a space where your autonomy leads the way. Together, we’ll move at a pace that honors your comfort and readiness, ensuring a personalized and self-directed exploration. Nature is central to my approach, offering a unique lens through which we can explore your experiences and deepen your healing. Reconnecting with the natural world can provide grounding, clarity, and insight into your relationship with yourself and others. Drawing on a variety of somatic and relational skills, I weave evidence-based practices from my CACREP-accredited master’s program at George Fox University with a holistic lens. This integration helps us connect the physical, emotional, and relational aspects of your life to foster growth, resilience, and a more embodied sense of well-being.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in the power of connection—to ourselves, to others, and Nature. My work is guided by values of inclusivity, authenticity, and holistic healing. As someone passionate about social justice, I aim to create a space where individuals of all identities feel seen, heard, and valued. I am deeply inspired by the interplay of nature and healing. I’ve always felt a strong bond with the earth’s rhythms, which I now integrate into my therapy practice. I also advocate for body sovereignty, sexual health education, and breaking the stigma around mental health and intimacy. Outside of my practice, I enjoy exploring nature, practicing mindfulness, and deepening my understanding of embodiment through ongoing training and research. My work is not just a profession—it’s a calling to help others uncover their innate capacity for healing and transformation.

name: Emily Lichtenberg (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe in the transformative power of the creative process. What I love about art therapy is that creative expression can look and feel different for each person, and treatment is highly customizable based on the needs and interests of my clients. When my clients are open to it, I incorporate eco-art therapy techniques into session to bring the more-than-human world into the conversation through metaphor and experiential offerings. I believe in a client-led approach to therapy, where we co-create the therapeutic journey together. I embody this in a clinical style that blends gentle structure with open-endedness to allow for insight to reveal itself in the ‘here-and-now.’ My professional philosophies have grown out of my foundation as an Intentional Peer Support specialist, which holds value in the belief that every person is the expert of their own story. My goal as a professional is to come alongside my clients on their journey, rather than to choose their paths for them.
My Values as a Therapist
As an art therapy student, I believe that my job is to hold a safe and supportive container for you to explore the depths of yourself and your healing journey. I value a client-led approach that supports your autonomy. I practice this by inviting my clients to co-create every step of our therapeutic journey. I believe you are the expert of your life and your needs, and my role is to offer possible directions, options, and insights along the way. I value an existential-humanist and relational approach to therapy. This means that I believe therapy looks different for each individual, and that the core of the process is in our relationships. I value the importance of all relationships, which expands beyond interpersonal into relationships with the more-than-human world, with artworks and art materials, and with the objects we hold sacred. I value the power of the ineffable and the mystical. This might be explored through eco-art or art therapy for psychedelic integration, or both.

name: Brooke Lavarias (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I offer a supportive, nonjudgmental space to help you navigate challenges like anxiety, depression, ADHD, and life transitions. Together, we’ll identify what’s keeping you stuck and develop tools to help you feel more confident, connected, and in control. Whether you’re overwhelmed by big emotions, struggling to stay focused, or adjusting to a new stage of life, I’ll meet you where you are and walk alongside you as you move toward growth and healing. My approach is collaborative and compassionate—I believe therapy works best when you feel heard, understood, and empowered to make meaningful changes. I welcome children, teens, and adults into my practice and tailor sessions to meet your unique needs. You don’t have to face it all alone. I’m here to help.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe everyone deserves to feel seen, heard, and valued. As a therapist, I’m passionate about creating inclusive, affirming spaces for all identities. I'm especially committed to supporting neurodivergent individuals and those navigating anxiety, depression, or major life changes. I'm trained in Parent-Child Interaction Therapy (PCIT) and have a strong foundation in evidence-based practices. I became a therapist because I know how powerful it can be to have someone truly listen and walk alongside you in hard times. My goal is to help you feel more grounded, empowered, and connected to yourself and others.

name: Michaela Rushing (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I utilize a person-centered, compassion-focused approach to help you learn how to support yourself and make the positive changes you want to make in your life.
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a therapist after going through therapy as a teenager for an anxiety disorder, and realizing how important finding the right therapist is for the therapy process. I also genuinely enjoying hearing peoples stories and helping them process their emotions.

name: Robin Bodhi (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Brain spotting is a technique I use to specifically address trauma. It has a strong somatic component in which the client guides their own process. I bring in an emphasis in somatic awareness and mindfulness to my counseling sessions. Because of my history as a body-worker I'm skilled in helping others connect with themselves through embodied approaches: such as mindfulness techniques, guided meditations, and somatic practices. My work is relational as I believe many issues start with a lack of proper and healthy early attachment. My focus is for my clients to feel safe and cultivate a more solid foundation of sense of self. Addressing unconscious patterns is a key component in creating change. I offer psychedelic integration for individuals who are needing support prior to and after a psychedelic (or medicine) journey. Sometimes journeys can be hard and having support to integrate the insights and/or lessons received is essential and just as important as the actual journey.
Why I Became a Therapist
Becoming a therapist was a natural next step in my career.. After working for many years as a body/energy worker my focus began to shift toward helping people with trauma so I furthered my education and received a MA in clinical mental health. During this time I became aware of how important it was to address my own personal trauma so dove deep into studying somatic trauma focused work as well as nervous system regulation. I now integrate these into my counseling work along with brain-spotting and other modalities. A large part of what calls me to do counseling is my desire to help others feel empowered and help them create the lives they want. I believe that people can have rich and meaningful lives and it is necessary to examine what gets in the way and actively address and change deeply en-grained patterns. Through learning new skills and with sufficient support I can help you create the life you desire.

name: Luc Boudreau (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
For the past 20 years, I've worked in various settings including inpatient, outpatient, community health, as a crisis counselor and as a college professor. As a therapist, i draw from several therapeutic frameworks in order to meet your unique needs. My approach integrates neuroscience with psychology to help maximize growth. I use an intuitive and integrated approach based on trauma-focused principles that restores the mind, the body, and spirit. I have specialized training in EMDR, IFS, CBT, ACT, Psychodynamic Theory, Attachment Theory, EFT & The Gottman Method. I offer services via telehealth for clients residing in Oregon & Washington, and in-person at my office in Hillsboro Oregon. I am also bilingual in English & French.
Why I Became a Therapist
My private practice was born from a deeply personal journey - one of struggle, resilience, & transformation. As a skinny, awkward kid, I knew what it was like to feel invisible, to battle social anxiety, & to carry the weight of rejection & bullying. The pain of those experiences was then compounded by years of trauma which left me feeling stuck, uncertain, & alone. Through my own healing, I discovered the power of self-worth, confidence, & resilience. Now as a therapist, I've made it my mission to guide others on their own path to self-empowerment. My calling is to help people break through self doubt, overcome past wounds, & step into the strong, confident lives they deserve. I help clients turn pain into power, fear into self confidence, & struggle into success. Now matter where you are in your journey, you don't have to do it alone. I'm here to help you show up for yourself, reclaim your confidence, and thrive - even in difficult moments.

name: Carrie R. Conklin
approaches:
How I Can Help You
Naming and identifying the feelings that cause us to react versus respond is hard work! Through hearing your story, we can begin to identify how you react from places of pain, and over time, identify ways that you can respond from places of peace. This will allow you to build a sense of self that aligns with your values, be more effective in your relationships, and address any "stuck points" that are impacting your life. The combination of the mind and body is a powerful healing tool. I am passionate about helping people access a calm and grounded state that creates a pathway to well-being, growth, love and safety. This is the beginning of hope and change. As a person who loves wholeheartedly, I welcome all people and am committed to supporting individuals, couples and families in their unique therapeutic journey.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In my free time I can be found reading a good book, listening to true-crime podcasts, traveling, eating delicious food with friends, and spending time with my family.

name: Kathleen Peters (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
As we navigate this journey, my desire is to bring the best of myself to your growth and healing. I am currently studying under grief expert Megan Devine to obtain a Grief Care Professional Certificate. I've also had the privilege of sharing my insights through professional speaking engagements and the creation of a self-help workbook. Due to my love of humans going through their teen years, I obtained my bachelor’s degree in Secondary Education. I have been married 30 years and am the mom to two delightful grown men in their 20’s.
My Approach to Helping
Sometimes we’re too close to our own challenges to see what is getting in our way. This is where I can be helpful. Drawing from over three decades of people helping experience and supporting individuals through trauma, grief, and faith challenges, I will bring compassion and care to our sessions. My aim is to come alongside you in collaboration, assisting you as you navigate your own unique journey – YOU are the expert in your own story.

name: Su Yim, MSW, LCSW (SHE/HER)
approaches:
When we meet, we will focus on your desires, worries, hopes, and fears. I will offer guidance, patience, and compassion as we focus on what makes sense for you. I am experienced in working with people going through grief and loss, relationship struggles, depression, anxiety, substance use, and trauma. Using evidence-based therapy tailored for you, you can learn to manage emotions, improve your loving relationships, make decisions that fit your values, and understand the roots of your actions. As a therapist experienced in brief and long-term therapy, I will help guide our work around what you need. Part of our time may be spent on acknowledging how the past influences you today, but I also believe the purpose of therapy is to move you forward. I balance compassion, comfort, and patience with a shared understanding that progress is important.
I am a licensed clinical social worker with a master's degree in social work from Portland State University and a bachelor's in journalism from Northwestern University.
