My experience includes work in community mental health clinics, local hospitals, crisis lines, and with university students. I have post-graduate training in EMDR, Dialectical Behavioral Therapy, Motivational Interviewing, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Internal Family Systems, and trauma-informed care. I also have completed the required hours to become a certified alcohol and drug counselor. In working with you, my goal will be to listen not just to your story but to the themes and patterns of your life. I do not believe that you are broken, or that I have "the fix." I work from a strengths-based perspective, which means I believe that we all have inherent value and capabilities. I embrace our partnership as an opportunity for us to understand what's keeping you stuck, and how to move toward changing it.

name: Lavinia Magliocco (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Our body is a gateway to authenticity, resolving trauma, integrating emotions. I use Trauma-informed somatic & mindfulness based interventions. When appropriate, we may combine movement to build awareness, help you regulate intense feelings, work with trauma, chronic pain, anxiety, or depression. You can expect that I will listen deeply & challenge you to expand beyond your perceived limitations. I use a psychodynamic orientation which means we use the therapeutic relationship itself to explore your patterns. I may include IFS (Internal Family Systems), ACT (Acceptance & Commitment Therapy), DBT (Dialectical Behavior/Cognitive Therapy), Gestalt, Somatic, and existentially oriented interventions. As a long time student of Eastern wisdom traditions, my work is informed by Buddhist psychology, Taoism, and Qigong - a Chinese mindful movement healing practice which supports health and wellbeing. I am poly, sex, and kink positive with a harm-reduction lens toward addictions.
My Values as a Therapist
As a queer, bicultural first-generation Italian-American with a chronic illness I've experienced bullying, othering, & invalidation for physical symptoms. I've had two bowel excisions and resections, a double mastectomy, and other musculoskeletal surgeries. I know pain. My experience confirms that each of us has innate wisdom longing to guide our lives towards greater freedom, connection, and well-being. Though not in control of many things, we can free ourselves from behaviors, thoughts, and emotions that repeat unfulfilling patterns. Strategies that kept us safe in childhood may get in our way as adults. Being able to live in your body and express who you are is especially essential when we are burdened by systemic injustice and oppression. I am poly, sex, and kink positive with a harm-reduction lens toward addictions.

name: Madison Meron (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
The foundation of my work as a therapist is forming a safe and trusting relationship with my clients. I view therapy as a collaborative process between therapist and client and take a strengths-based approach, believing you are not at a deficit and are not defined by a diagnosis or symptoms. I recognize that social justice issues are a major contributor to many mental health concerns and do not see my clients in isolation from the issues going on in our world. You can expect to move at a pace that is comfortable for you, learning to trust the wisdom of your body through engaging in play, artistic expression and embodiment practices. I believe that we already have resources within us, but that trauma (individual, family and on a systemic scale) has separated us from ourselves, each other and the earth. I have extensive training/experience using trauma-informed modalities, like IFS & EMDR, to help you feel more connected to yourself and your relationships.
More About the People I Treat
My areas of focus include: Family therapy & Parent support; Couples/Partner therapy; Empaths & Sensitive folks; Religious trauma recovery; and Foster care/Adoption support. I have had extensive experience in supporting trauma recovery for those with PTSD & other other post-traumatic diagnoses. I have worked in the community mental health setting supporting children, families and couples with relational & attachment traumas. I have led support groups for parents, LGBTQIA+ youth and in-school sexual assault recovery groups for high schoolers. Some specific traumas I have experience working with includes: Infant & early life trauma, sexual abuse/assault, foster care/adoption and religious trauma. I love helping people heal within the context of their important relationships and believe that lasting change comes when we address the entire system.

name: Andrew Drwenski
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I offer a wide range of therapeutic approaches to help you set goals and achieve them. I specialize in Structural Family Therapy and Compassion Focused Therapy in addition to regular use of CBT and client centered methods. With your lead, I like to take an active role in session allowing for space to process emotions and tools to use outside of sessions. I'm happy to meet you where you are. I'm excited that you've come this far in researching therapists and I hope to have the privilege to lend my expertise to our professional relationship. Paying for therapy can be difficult and I want to make it accessible to as many as possible.
My Values as a Therapist
I want our time together to be efficient without feeling pressured. I value and ask for feedback on sessions in a way that is meant to help you inform me on what work and what doesn't. I want the skills you learn in session to serve you throughout your life. It's very important to me that you walk away with a positive interaction with therapy services. Taking the steps to reach out for help is hard, I hope that providing a meaningful experience lowers the barrier to ask for help in the future.

name: Kevin Lang (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am an Emotion-Focused Therapy trained clinician who takes a humanistic approach to treatment. I blend skills from Emotion-Focused Therapy (EFT), Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy (CBT), and Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) to help clients achieve the goals we set out together. I am very humanistic and existential in my approach, meaning clients who will likely benefit the most are highly motivated and open to exploring openly and authentically. I view clients and their unique issues through an attachment-based lens. I recognize that almost all behaviors make sense in the context of attachment. I use this to help clients make sense of their own behaviors and emotional responses. Together, through experiencing a safe and accepting therapeutic alliance, frightening, alien, and unacceptable emotions can be safely explored.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
None of us come out of childhood without some cuts and bruises. Sometimes physical, but more oftentimes emotional. For many, these emotional wounds are still tender, open, lightly dressed with a bandage, forgotten, or even neglected. So when these emotions get touched we find ourselves reacting in ways that aren’t in line with the kind of person we want to be. Our work will include processing, identifying, and exploring emotions. Learning ways to respond to our thoughts and feelings in ways that allow us to act more in line with our values, which can in turn help you in living a richer and more meaningful life.

name: Matthew Lucas MSW, CSWA (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Working collaboratively with compassion as the foundation. We will have an open, honest, empathic, and nonjudgmental exchange of ideas. The goal is to create a safe space where individuals feel heard. If you're experiencing shifts in relationships (family/ partner), feelings of anxiousness, or depression, let's work together to find ways to manage the complexities of life and relationships.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I've seen firsthand how ethnicity, culture, and gender influence who receives therapy and how it's delivered. As a Black gay clinician with intersectional identities, my goal is to reduce barriers and normalize therapy. For anyone who has experienced marginalization due to their identity. Let's start today.

name: Miranda Bayard-Clark, LPC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have completed Level 2 training in Gottman Method Couples therapy, and see mostly couples in my practice. We will have a first session together to gather information, followed by two individual sessions, and finish with a feedback session, where I summarize my assessment and create a specific treatment plan for the couple.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Even the happiest of relationships can be strained with challenges over the years. From finances, inlaws, new baby, to lack of intimacy, couples are tested to the fullest, especially during major life changes. Many of my clients are couples who are working to maintain their relationship as a couple. Using client strengths, Emotion-Focused couples counseling, and Gottman techniques, I will support you in finding balance in your personal life, and your relationships. I hold a Masters degree in Counseling Psychology from Lewis & Clark, and am a current member of the International Center for Excellence in Emotion Focused Therapy.

name: Celine Redfield
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Working from an empathic-based perspective, I teach clients to nurture themselves by using the tools of creative arts therapy, cognitive restructuring, mindfulness, visualization, acupressure and energy medicine (EFT) to help them to find their strengths to improve their lives.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have worked with various different populations including: at-risk youth, LGTBQ folks, the geriatric population and those struggling with addiction. In my training I learned to mix art making with an understanding in Interpersonal neuroscience. I was trained in the art of self compassion mindfulness. Though our work together we can rewire the way that you think and behave through the use of art, mindfulness and EFT.

name: Emily Warner (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My approach to counseling is integrative in nature. I believe that every individual is unique, and I love to match my style to the client rather than vice versa. I am rooted in person-centered theory, believing that the client is the expert on their own lives and healing can occur through empathy, congruence, and unconditional positive regard. I also integrate internal family systems, interpersonal theory, and expressive arts practices into my practice. Though my services are virtual there are still so many ways to integrate creativity into our healing and mental health journey. I have experience working with folks through life transitions, schooling, and differentiating their identities in emerging adulthood as well - and can bring a solution focused and/or identity exploration lens to the table. I am here to meet you where you are at, and help you achieve your goals and recognize the power you hold.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am from the PNW, having lived in Vancouver, Seattle, and Portland. I received my Master’s in counseling from Portland State University. My areas of focus while training to become a counselor were adolescence and suicide prevention. I previously worked for the Seattle Crisis Clinic as a teen suicide prevention specialist. I worked at Aloha High School as a school counselor for 2 years, providing counseling to students and families. In my free time I am also a licensed yoga teacher, and value the connection between body and mind. I have worked with theater nonprofits and public schools for a majority of my life - and have experience with the LGBTQ community. I believe in lifelong learning, and putting in active work to be an ally to by queer and BIPOC friends and community members. It is so much more than just a statement, it is a commitment to help create change. I also have a cat and love talking about pets!

name: Douglas Counseling
approaches:
Our Background and Approach
Our therapists have extensive training working with couples and relationships. We use a variety of therapy models to help our clients including Relational Life Therapy (RLT), Emotionally Focused Couples Therapy (EFT), Gottman Method, and many others. Our therapists on staff have years of experience helping couples and individuals navigate challenges in their relationships and lives. Our practice also has sub-specialties in the areas of affair recovery, men's issues, and women's issues. We have licensed therapists including Licensed Professional Counselors, Licensed Marriage and Family Therapists, and Licensed Clinical Social Workers.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
At Douglas Counseling we believe that all clients are capable of making lasting changes to positively impact their lives. We believe that each person has the capacity to gain insights, change thoughts, manage feelings, gain skills, and create a happy and healthy life. Our values include approaching each client with acceptance and understanding. We strive to create a safe and non-judgmental environment for our clients to share their experiences and challenges.

name: Adam Benjamin
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My clients develop lasting relief from compelling feelings and self-beliefs that may find their roots in childhood or adverse life events. Living fully means seeing current events clearly, but also developing flexibility in the reactions and expectations we developed long ago. These "old files" are stored in our felt, bodily experience as well as our stories about ourselves. The work of therapy is getting curious together not only about your thoughts and feelings, but also about your sensations and impulses -- the subtle machinery of experience that constitutes (and often limits) your range of choices. I am certified in Hakomi psychotherapy, a kind of mindful & experiential therapy, and have advanced training in resolving attachment wounding. I have a particular interest in working with individuals who grew up with a dysfunctional, immature, or mentally ill parent.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Therapy is my calling, but it is not my first career. I also have a PhD in Communication Studies and taught college courses before turning toward psychotherapy. In my spare time I enjoy repairing and riding old motorcycles. I grew up in the New York area and have lived in Portland since 2001.

name: James Freeman (HE/ HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Pushing beyond traditional talk therapy, much of my therapeutic practice is inspired by my time working in Wilderness Therapy and witnessing the freeing power that nature, movement, and connection have on all of us. In somatic psychotherapy, we start by understanding our nervous system and the link between our physical body and our mental health experience. Then, we address the physical symptoms of trauma or anxiety by focusing on body awareness, psychoeducation, and emotional regulation. We take a look together at how our past may have shaped coping tools or emotional blueprints that dictate our current responses, and imagine a possibility where we can leave defenses that no longer serve us behind. This work paves the way for essential life skills like slowing down, forming meaningful connections, and learning effective communication. I currently offer sessions via telehealth or outdoor-based sessions at Mt. Tabor or other outdoor settings in Portland.
My Values as a Therapist
I am currently a therapist through Broken Top Counseling. I believe that relationships and connection are at the core of healing. Connection with our Self, our bodies, each other, and the natural world - all of these are separate ingredients that if missing, leave us feeling alone, aimless, and like strangers in our own skin. As both a psychotherapist and rock climbing guide, I’ve experienced firsthand how these relationships can lead to empowerment, confidence, and increased resilience. And it is this experience that I bring to my therapy sessions. By working with the body and the nervous system, my goal is to help you understand trauma and empower you to change your relationship with yourself, your body, and your past. I work from both a “top-down” and “bottom-up” approach, including cognitively and emotionally examining the impacts of your experiences to create change, alongside utilizing Brainspotting or somatic techniques like breathwork to bring relief to the nervous system.

name: Grace Willow (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I specialize in a holistic experiential embodied approach to trauma healing & transformation, attachment repair and inner child healing, self-worth, Archetypal and Shadow work, sexuality challenges, sex, pleasure and embodiment, and nervous system healing and resiliency. Modalities I utilize include Person-Centered, Somatic Trauma Resolution, Somatic Psychology, Ketamine-Assisted Psychotherapy (KAP), Hypnotherapy, Attachment, Polyvagal, IFS “parts work,” Feminist, Gestalt, Imaginal and Integral Psychology, Interpersonal Neurobiology, and Transpersonal Energy Psychology. I also draw upon over 27 years of professional experience as a Licensed Massage Therapist, Women’s Holistic Pelvic Care™ therapist, Certified Professional Coach, Energy Healer, teacher and mentor, as well as breathwork, sounding, journaling, Aroma Point Therapy, flower essences, & EFT Tapping.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe people heal when they are held in a safe space where all parts of them are welcome and accepted unconditionally. I feel deep and profound gratitude and honor for the gift of hearing each of your unique and beautiful life stories, sitting with you during times of curiosity, grief, pain, a-ha’s and illuminating insights, and supporting your profound transformation of whole-making. I look forward to supporting you in your unique, sacred journey. I offer a safe inclusive space that is welcoming and affirming of all people, including LGBTQIA+, Health at Every Size, Sex Positive. Outside of work, you can find me writing, spending time with my partner, hiking, backpacking, kayaking, dancing (Nia), listening to live music, cuddling with my sweet Russian Blue rescue cat, Rose, and leaning into my own personal growth and development as a fellow human.

name: Shanon Emerson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My philosophy, rooted in my training in both Somatic Experiencing and Depth Psychology, is that by noticing and allowing the feelings and sensations that arise within us, we can heal the patterns, behaviors, and defenses that limit our capacity for experience. This process fosters a deeper understanding of our authentic Self. As we come to know ourselves better, we begin to trust our inner guidance more than external influences like culture, family, and society, which often try to dictate our lives.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a queer artist and writer, mom of two adult kids, and curious explorer of what’s possible on this wild ride called life. I am trained in Somatic Experiencing (SE) and am a certified coach with the International Coach Federation (ICF).

name: Amy Altenberger (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a Licensed Professional Counselor trained in Art Therapy and a yoga instructor, I am able to offer experiential therapy, mindfulness, as well as breath and movement work to promote healing and connection. It is my hope that through our work together you will feel felt, heard and nurtured. Over the past 25 years, I have worked with children and adults who have experienced anxiety, depression, grief and trauma. I am deeply committed to working with clients who have experienced loss and help them with their journey.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe in a collaborative and nurturing approach to counseling. I feel that people are shaped by the culture in which they were raised, the experiences they have had, and the stories they create about themselves. Through our work together, I will help you tell your story; to find your voice, and create a new narrative. Through the use of mindfulness, breath and body work you will develop techniques to decrease anxiety, feel empowered and connected.

name: Sabrina LaGood-Robinson
approaches:
How We Can Help You
The first step can be the hardest but you’re here! I’d love to help support you on your journey.

name: Heather Lokteff
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I finished my Masters in Counseling Psychology in 2006 and I have 16+ years experience providing therapy to a variety of clients. Prior to starting my own practice in 2011 I worked at various non-profit agencies providing in-home and intensive community-based treatment for children and families in crisis and/or DHS involvement. I helped create safety and stabilization in families and worked with children and teens providing intensive mental health services. I continue to carry my knowledge and experience with children into my work with teen and adult clients and feel it gives me a unique perspective on people’s pasts, family of origin, and struggles that started in childhood. I’ve expanded my practice to work with couples as well as continuing with teens and adults. I utilize evidence-based therapies including Emotion-Focused Therapy (EFT), Acceptance & Commitment Therapy (ACT), and am a Level 1 trained clinician in Internal Family Systems and the Gottman Method.
My Values as a Therapist
I value a practical, collaborative and personalized approach to your therapy experience. I provide insight, encouragement, and a safe and non-judgmental space. I value honesty, humility, and self-compassion. I value my clients and their self-advocacy and resiliency. I value humor, engaging in life with a sense of adventure and gratitude, and being holistically healthy. I believe in identifying and building on personal strengths, and equipping you with practical life skills to get you to where you want to be. I believe in the transformative power of a safe, supportive, collaborative and confidential relationship that therapy provides. I value creating a compassionate, non-judgmental space to be heard and listened to. I value being a neutral support person and a knowledgeable sounding board. It’s important to have a therapist you feel safe with and encouraged by. This is how I aspire to operate in my practice.

name: Ravnoor Sahni (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My personal lived experiences, my background in Medicine and my professional training in family systems approach all inform my therapeutic style. My approach is conversational, client-centered, strength-based, trauma-informed, and culturally sensitive. I draw from SERT, EFT, PACT, IFS, Gottman, Narrative and Solution Focused Therapy. I also utilize mindfulness and somatic approaches. I tailor each session based on what works for you and your family, chosen or biological, as I believe that each individual, couple, and family is unique. I utilize a decolonizing, intersectional, body positive and an anti-oppressive lens in therapy and operate from a framework of relational equity and social justice. I am inclusive of all races/ethnicities, gender, sexual and relational orientations. I am passionate about centering and working with individuals, couples and families from BIPOC, LGBTQIA+ and other historically marginalized communities.
My Values as a Therapist
We all hold multiple intersecting identities. Some of these identities afford us privileges and power in society while others can be marginalized and oppressed. As a person owning multiple minoritized identities, I understand why some of us build walls around ourselves to feel safe from relational injuries and systemic traumas. It might feel unsafe to take down this wall but that is exactly what I will invite you to do, brick by brick during sessions, at a pace that feels safe yet challenging. I would understand if that protective wall comes back up after the session. Sometimes that wall is necessary for coping with past and present injuries, and we get to those vulnerable places, that have either never been explored, or have been sealed shut due to shame, sadness, fear or anger, I will be by your side as a collaborative guide and shine light on your inner strength and resilience. Feeling seen, heard and understood in a safe space is one of the best ways to heal, grow and connect.

name: Esther Berg (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe healing happens when we feel secure enough to turn inward and gently explore the layers of our experience, both the conscious and unconscious. My approach is warm, collaborative, and grounded in depth-oriented, somatic, narrative, and IFS-informed therapy. I meet you where you are, honoring your inner wisdom while offering gentle guidance to help you uncover patterns, heal emotional wounds, and reconnect with your authentic self. Together, we create space for new emotional experiences that promote self-trust, resilience, and deeper connection with others. I view therapy as a journey of remembering, reclaiming, and integrating the parts of yourself that have been left unseen or unheard.

name: Art Chaklader (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Experiencing stress, anxious thoughts, unhealthy habits, and feeling depressed can be a part of the journey to a better you. I can help in providing a lens to that journey, to help you be mindful of where you have been, where you are, and where you are going. I also help provide the tools, to recognize barriers, and overcome old patterns. I have devoted my life to understanding psychology, working in the helping profession, and finding the right resources when things are out of my scope of practice. My style is person centered, informed, and really deeply rooted in creating an environment and relationship in which a person can feel like they can truly be who they are, and express fully what they want. My fees vary depending on the service, and I take many insurance plans. Please do not let cost be a barrier. I provide a free consultations to see if I would be the right fit for your counseling needs.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Licensed Professional Counselor with the board of Oregon, and have worked in a number of settings including Drug and Alcohol, Community Mental Health, School, Crisis Work, and private practice. I have M.A. in Mental Health Counseling from Marywood University, Licensed in Oregon, and I am a Nationally Certified Counselor.

name: Miranda York
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My practice is anti oppression and trauma informed. I am trained in psychodynamic and psychoanalytic therapy techniques, as well as various somatic techniques for trauma. I am warm and interactive. The therapy relationship can serve as a safer space to discover your old patterns of relating and try out new ones. I listen to you deeply, trying to hear what you are saying between the lines and work to empathically understand your inner conflicts and elaborate on unconscious dynamics that may be driving difficult patterns in your life. We will work together to explore the root causes of your struggles, to release these patterns and help you move towards more freedom, liberation, and ease in life. I work with couples to understand attachment conflicts, and I can also work with attachment in open relationships and polyamorous arrangements. Sometimes old traumas can cause reactivity and repetition in relationships, and we will explore this to enable more connection.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold the following credentials: Master's degree in Somatic Psychology, California Institute of Integral Studies Certificate in Drug and Alcohol Studies, Bellevue Community College Sensorimotor Psychotherapy Training for the Treatment of Trauma- Level 1 AEDP (Accelerated Experiential Dynamic Psychotherapy)- Level 3 EMDR- Basic Training Parts 1 and 2

name: Rebecca Hyman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
One of my areas of expertise and interest is posttraumatic stress. Trauma haunts us—it sticks around in our thoughts, our bodies, and our dreams. It steals our safety and trust. Sometimes it erupts in our relationships as violence or abuse, and we are caught off guard. It can feel overwhelming to face parts of ourselves that we fear. Yet in grappling with loss we claim our power. My goal is to assist each person I work with to either find or develop their True Self: the person they’ve always wanted to be; the person deep inside who hasn’t spoken in a very long time; the person able to give a very particular gift to the world. I honor difference in all of the ways that it expresses itself. I see each of us as both empowered and constrained by dynamics of gender, power, sexuality, privilege and oppression and I bring this understanding to the work I do. I draw from a number of theoretical approaches and am happy to speak with you individually about how we might work together.
Being a therapist is a second career for me. Before I was a therapist I was a tenured English and Women’s Studies professor. More recently I taught in the School of Social Work and Family Studies Program at Portland State University. I bring a focus on language, interpretation and meaning to my work as a therapist, and in addition to my specialties in mental health, I have a particular interest in helping people succeed in academic and creative pursuits. I received my MSW from Portland State University. As a social worker I’ve worked with people coming out of the criminal justice system, people struggling with substance use and dependence, people navigating domestic violence relationships, veterans coming home from war, and people who are living with bipolar disorder, depression, anxiety, posttraumatic stress disorder and personality disorders.

name: Marissa Kendall (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapeutic approach is rooted in Emotionally Focused Therapy, Internal Family Systems, and Person-Centered principles. I focus on helping clients recognize and reshape emotional patterns, strengthening their connection to themselves and others. By fostering self-compassion and understanding, I support clients in creating more secure, fulfilling relationships—both internally and externally. I'm passionate about supporting individuals through identity shifts, struggles in their relationships, intimacy issues, depression, anxiety, and internalized shame.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that every part of us is shaped by the need for connection and protection. When we learn to approach our emotions with curiosity rather than judgment, we create space for healing and growth. My role is to guide you in deepening your relationship with yourself, helping you move from disconnection to a place of emotional security and wholeness. I use she/her pronouns. Outside of work, I enjoy any type of craft, photography, and spending time outdoors with my dog.

name: Anne Pippert (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe in highlighting the uniqueness of the individual, while understanding that we all function as part of a multitude of larger systems. The relationships we create with ourselves, with others, and with the world around us, can both transform and challenge us. In my work, I utilize treatment modalities including CBT, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, mindfulness-based practices, Emotion-Focused Family Therapy and The Gottman Method for couples. I have experience working with adults, adolescents, and families in residential, partial hospitalization and intensive outpatient eating disorder treatment facilities.

name: Leah Marinakis (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe therapy is most successful when we find the path that truly supports the growth you’re seeking. In our work together, we will find an approach that fits your unique needs, drawing from my experience with a range of therapeutic orientations, including trauma-informed therapy, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Mindfulness, and relational approaches.

name: Kati Lear (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I’m a licensed psychologist and active researcher specializing in time-limited psychotherapy. With a Ph.D. in Clinical Psychology and years of practical experience since 2014, I offer focused treatment—up to 20 sessions—designed to help you break free from unworkable patterns. My approach is anchored in modern cognitive-behavioral therapies, primarily Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) and Emotion-Focused Therapy. I also integrate techniques from Compassion-Focused Therapy, Radically Open Dialectical Behavior Therapy, mindfulness practices, and exposure-based strategies. Together, we’ll work to identify your core values, challenge your inner critic, and build a kinder, more authentic relationship with yourself, setting the stage for lasting change.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that healing flourishes in an environment of genuine connection and self-compassion. Growing up in a culture that often emphasizes flaws over strengths, I’ve learned that our inner critic isn’t an inherent defect but a call for deeper self-understanding. I continuously examine my own privileges and biases, striving to create a space where you feel truly seen, heard, and valued. Passionate about integrating creativity into therapy, I draw on my interests in art and music to enrich our work together. Ultimately, I’m committed to making mental health care accessible and affirming—especially for women, LGBTQIA+ individuals, and those from ethnically diverse backgrounds.

name: Amber Liechty (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I offer group therapy for teens in high school. Since group therapy out-performs individual therapy in research, I prioritize my schedule for teen therapy groups (some individual therapy on a case by case basis). For my fellow colleagues: I hold a masters in dual-diagnosis (mood/addiction/eating disorders) from Lewis & Clark College. I have since worked in co-occurring addiction and mental health social services treating eating disorders and addiction at the following institutions: Oregon Youth Authority, Community Services NW, Sound Mental Health, and Harborview Trauma Hospital in Seattle, WA. In addition to continuing education in ACT and Harm Reduction, I received extensive specialty training in Radically Open DBT from Abri RODBT (clinic in Portland, OR).
Why I am a Good Fit for You
If you are looking for a therapist that is a little rough around the edges in terms of demeanor and language while having high-level knowledge of the issues you struggle with, I'd be your therapist. I can be validating but am also one to challenge my clients with difficult questions - humor/sarcasm also plays a big role in my therapeutic approach. Check out my website for more info!

name: Jackie Turner (SHE, HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I'm an integrative therapist who incorporates aspects of attachment theory, Parts work/IFS, emotionally-focused therapy, and ecopsychology. While I offer insights, observations, and gentle challenges, my priority is to guide you toward building the skills you need to uncover and connect to your internal sources of strength, insight, intuition, and integrity. In couples and relationship therapy, I will compassionately challenge you to identify and grow from your well-worn patterns and do the brave work of stepping into more authentic connection with your partner. Healing comes when it is meant to, and sometimes we have to move through the muck and the messiness before we reach moments of breakthrough. I provide a safe and positive container for you to move through these difficult periods on the journey toward greater connection to one another.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I support every person's right to be honored for who they truly are, and I believe in educating myself as a therapist to support my clients in their identities. I work to attend to issues of oppression and marginalization as they arise, while also holding onto the fact that you are your own, complex, nuanced self. I am open to all spiritual and religious belief systems and respectful of how much or how little you want to include these beliefs/traditions in your therapy work. I believe strongly in every person's right and ability to define their unique values, and I emphasize building inner trust, integrity, and discernment in the process of living your values in the world. No matter who you are, I believe that you are worthy of dignity, respect, and care. I am firmly opposed to dehumanization and injustice in all its forms, and I believe in approaching everyone with curiosity, humility, and in good faith.

name: Tanuja Carlson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I draw from various modes of therapy, including Object Relations Theory, which explores the ways in which early relationships/experiences influence our current patterns of behavior. I also incorporate Cognitive Behavioral Therapy which identifies how your thoughts impact your behaviors. I will work to help you better understand yourself, to help promote feelings of self worth and deservedness. My hope is to enhance your self perception which can contribute to meaningful changes both internally and within your relationships. I have 16 years of experience working with children and adolescents, individual adults, couples and families. I’ve offered therapeutic support in Psychiatric Day Treatment, School Based and Outpatient settings. I am also a Certified Case Manager. Many of my clients report feeling seen and heard as a result of my calm, compassionate, insightful and collaborative approach. I love to listen to understand, and offer a safe space to talk, learn and grow.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As someone who identifies as a Millennial, I can whole heartedly say I understand the challenges that we have been faced with over the past 25 years. Amidst ongoing changes in technology, social media pressures and an ever changing and divisive political climate, I truly empathize and share in your experience; add a full time job, parenthood (also a full time job!), and the complexities of relationships in life, it is absolutely understandable that you might find yourself here, ready to ask for help. I am also a recent Breast Cancer survivor. This is a diagnosis that seems to be increasingly prevalent in society today. As a Mom of 3 young children, working full time, with no genetic history indicating the potential or possibility, please know I understand the emotional and physical rollercoaster of this illness. I empathize with anyone experiencing this challenge firsthand, as well as those supporting a loved one facing it. I would be happy to hold your hand through that experience.

name: Jodi Palensky (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Licensed in multiple states including Oregon, Nebraska, Alaska, Idaho, Illinois, Iowa, Maryland, Minnesota, New Mexico, North Dakota, Colorado, Michigan, Washington, Arizona, and Montana. I offer online psychology and counseling teletherapy services designed to connect your recovery plan to your home environment and help you heal, grow and re-engage with your life following life-changing events. I have worked with individuals and families recovering from tragedy for over 20 years. If you, your loved one, or your family is struggling to cope with and recover from significant life changes following an injury or illness, you are not alone. I am happy to support you during this challenging time to find ways to help you heal, grow, and re-engage with life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that we all have strengths, we all have the capacity for change and growth, and we all experience times of struggle. Acknowledging the struggle and reaching out for support and assistance requires courage. I honor that courage, and I feel lucky to be able to join people on their journey toward recovery from injury and illness, during times of personal struggle, and as they strive for their fullest potential.

name: Shannon Haan (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have received training in multiple approaches which I use in my practice including Motivational Interviewing, CBT, Internal Family Systems, and Mindfulness-based practices. I am currently part of a group studying Internal Family Systems and parts work to apply this lens and technique to my approach, as well as a supervised group to expand understanding my work through a racial justice framework. I also understand healing through a somatic lens, centering the experience of our bodies and the wisdom they hold. Through my experiences working in the mental health field for almost 10 years, I deeply value low barrier and client centered care and aim to orient my practice around these principles. My work is relational and I believe you are the expert of your story, it is my job to co-create a safe environment for you to become deeper in touch with your own internal wisdom. **We have an income-based sliding scale payment arrangement to offer for folks without insurance benefits.**
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a white person and a cis woman, I seek to educate myself regularly on anti-oppressive perspectives and approaches to inform how I work, and live. I am a member of the LGBTQ+ community and this as well as a feminist lens is a guide to my life, my connections, and my work. I am also someone who lives with a chronic health condition and I believe in and practice deeply in my life the understanding of the mind body connection, and how to honor and care for our bodies even within a less than supportive culture. My goal is to build a collaborative space with you to work together, and create a sense of safety and non-judgement for your healing. During my free time, I love to be cozy at home with a book and a cup of tea, spend time with friends, play board games, go dancing, swimming, traveling, and spend as much time in nature as possible.

name: Ivy Campbell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I am a licensed Social Worker in the state of Oregon and New York who specializes in supporting adolescents, young adults and their families confronting a range of issues. I earned my bachelor's degree in Sociology and Masters Degree from NYU. I am currently enrolled at the William Alanson White Institute's Child and Adolescent Program. My approach is personable and affirming, informed by a holistic approach to care that aids in the promotion of wholeness and healing in the mind and body. Through this lens, I create an empowering environment, integrating the humor, honesty, and critical consciousness that is so integral to my personality inside and outside of the therapy room. My therapeutic orientation is trauma-informed and psychodynamic, honoring the complexities of your personal history while integrating relevant interventions in service of your unique needs. The goal is to find compassion for your hardships and find useful, long-term solutions.
My Approach to Helping
Through a holistic lens, I create a relaxed and open-minded space, integrating the humor, honesty, and critical consciousness that is so integral to my personality inside and outside of the therapy room. Calling on my background in Social Work, I support client’s in building greater self compassion through understanding how the systems in which we exist aid on our harm, and our healing. I am formally trained in the use of Dialectical Behaviors Therapy (DBT) to manage some of those tougher challenges through the use of emotional regulation practices, with the goal of increasing self awareness and balance.

name: Wanda Burgi (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I approach therapy through a trauma-informed lens, with the understanding that emotions are a vital part of our experience. They can offer insight into our needs, desires, and the challenges we face. I believe that our bodies also hold important clues to our emotional and mental states, and by tuning into them, we can deepen our understanding of ourselves and heal in a more integrated way. I am committed to walking alongside you on your journey toward healing, while providing a safe, supportive, and empathetic space where you can explore and honor this season in your life.
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a therapist because I saw individuals and communities—across many different contexts—struggling, often without realizing they needed support. I noticed how people were acting and speaking from wounded places, sometimes causing harm to themselves and those around them. That moved me. I believe that spaces like places of worship, which are meant to offer refuge, often lack the mental health support needed for true healing and connection. My hope is to bridge that gap, helping individuals find the peace, self-awareness, and acceptance we all deserve. To me, therapy is about more than just coping—it’s about growing into a more compassionate and whole version of ourselves, for our own sake and for the sake of the communities we’re part of.

name: Tim Perez
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Mental Illness has a way of making us feel devalued. My focus is to help you rediscover, restore, and retain your sense of individual value or worth. I primarily offer medication management therapies coupled with talk therapy and an ongoing emphasis on education into my patients' conditions. I find that many who come see me may have a history of being treated for a mental health condition without achieving satisfactory relief. I will take my time with you, listen, and seek your buy-in before WE determine the direction of YOUR treatment. I may use medical, psychological, logical, religious and spiritual approaches to help with your concerns, which ever you feel helps you the most.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I feel strongly that all people have value and worth that is intrinsic and not defined by the whims, rants, and ideas of the multiple opinions around us. Our worth is like the sun, it is always shining bright and is extremely powerful. So are we. I believe that much of our mental illness issues come from our lack of realizing who we really are. Mental health and stability comes when we are enlightened to our own personal worth and value and that we must all accept ourselves first, before we truly have the capacity to help, love and serve others. Yes, offenses and anxieties will come; they are all around us. But realizing who we really are and treating ourselves with love and respect first, will give us the power to help those around us. Let's meet and see if we can explore together your absolute value! After all, your worth is as bright as the sun :)

name: Sean Robertson (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
From my experience, the first thing that will happen is we’ll try to see if we could work well together, or not. That typically is hinted at strongly and quickly, and it’s something we’ll navigate throughout our working relationship. I will frequently check-in to make sure the goals and style of your services with me match your hopes to the best of my ability. So that you know, the styles I was trained in and that I am most comfortable working within are existential-humanistic ways of relating, psychodynamic ways of exploring, and cognitive-behavioral ways of thinking or doing. I also have acquired additional specializations in treating psychological trauma. Some call this form of therapy an “integrative perspective.” Basically, it means I welcome your desire for healing that is special to you, and I will attempt to meet you with the various fields of expertise that I have gained in my short life.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe your life is a gift. But, I am betting that gift of your life has been hard to swallow, often. I am willing to bet further that others have not always seen your life as a gift. I know, for me, life doesn't always or even mostly feel like a gift because life circumstances seem to just be "thrown at me" without asking me permission or checking to see if I’m prepared to receive what life is "throwing". The trick, I think, is to look at that “thrownness” of life as an opportunity to exercise the freedom to choose—whether that is to openly weep about the loss of a loved one, “laugh in the face of danger,” or to set very clear boundaries (and protect those boundaries) to whomever might be trying to take the right to choose away. My hope is that all of us humans come to a place of accepting our life with honest gratitude and honoring our choices to respond to the gift of life with respect and openness. So, I welcome the gift of your life and how you have responded to your life.

name: Jacob Curtis (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am now offering teletherapy. Every person comes to counseling with a unique background and need. In some situations ways of dealing with things that were effective in the past may no longer work. Others may be seeking enhanced wellness or balance in their lives. Counseling begins with a safe and supportive relationship. Often the counseling process involves identifying one’s values, as well as one’s beliefs about oneself and the world. I use research-informed approaches to help my clients identify problem areas, create goals, and try new ways of doing things that work for them in their unique situation. My theoretical approach to counseling is integrative, but draws from Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), CBT-I (for insomnia), Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (a type of CBT), and Existential Theory.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold a Master’s Degree (MA) in Counseling Psychology from Lewis & Clark College, Graduate School of Education and Counseling and hold professional membership in the American Counseling Association. I am also a Certified Drug and Alcohol Counselor I (CADC I), a Nationally Certified Counselor (NCC), and a Professional Counselor (LPC) licensed by the Oregon Board of Licensed Professional Counselors and Therapists. My training includes: working with individuals and families; lifespan development; treating people experiencing anxiety and depression; cultural diversity; treating people who have experienced trauma and abuse; treating individuals with personality disorders; interpersonal effectiveness and communication strategies; diagnosis and assessment; Cognitive Processing Therapy for trauma; Cognitive Behavioral Therapy; Dialectical Behavior Therapy; Motivational Interviewing; treating people with addictions; and other treatments, modalities and interventions.

name: Maxfield Koontz (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is rooted in experiential modalities that bring awareness to all aspects of the human experience from a non-judgemental, compassionate, and curious place. I believe that when we allow ourselves to sit with our present felt experience, to be with discomfort and pain, possibilities of change come into reach. It is also through vulnerable connection with others that we heal both individually and relationally. Often, our inability to be with ourselves is what inhibits us from the relationships we crave. Connection to others is what makes life’s pain bearable, and is a means to find joy in times of hardship. I view our time together as a collaborative space to explore the complexities of your human journey. I work with individuals, those in relationships, and families.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a cisgender gay man I have lived experience in the queer community and am committed to practicing from an intersectional, anti-oppressive, trauma-informed lens. I strive to hold an inclusive and affirming space that honors the experiences of all marginalized identities. Whatever your background may be, I invite you to bring your authentic self into the room.

name: Rachel Starck, LPC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I utilize a cognitive behavioral, solution focused, and mindfulness based approach to facilitating change with my clients meeting in person or online! I draw on my training in Dialectic Behavioral Therapy as well. Initially we will discuss your history, identify goals, and obstacles. Then we will clarify what you have the power to change (Yes, I am a big fan of the Serenity Prayer) and develop step by step plan for getting there. Depending on your goals, we will identify concrete skills, exercises, and behaviors for you to apply between our sessions. Frequency of our meetings can vary. We may meet weekly, every other week, monthly, online or in person as needed depending on your situation and goals. On the business side, I use MindEase Billing Service to submit insurance claims. If we opt to work together, they will call you to get insurance information, then clarify your benefits and let us both know what your financial responsibility will be.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a licensed professional counselor in the state of OR. I graduated from Lewis & Clark College in 1997 with a MS in Counseling Psychology after obtaining a BA in Biology. I volunteered in domestic violence shelters, worked for Oregon Youth Authority at Hillcrest Youth Correctional Facility, and then in adolescent residential treatment facilities under Morrison Center and Trillium Family services as a therapist and clinical manager. During this time, I studied DBT which fits well with my therapeutic orientation and also became qualified to provide supervision to therapist interns. I entered private practice in 2003 and initially specialized in working with adolescents and their families. I currently focus on helping parents with stress management, relationship, and parenting skills since becoming a parent in 2005. My office is N Portland where I have cultivated a professional community of healing practitioners, whom I share space with at The North Portland Growth Place!

name: Amy Newman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My clients tell me I offer acceptance, insight, and a sense of humor. I believe we all deserve compassion and the chance to grow, heal, and change. It is my intention to listen deeply and attune to your unique needs. I want to explore what gives meaning to your life and learn how counseling can be helpful. I seek to create an environment where we can work toward growth that is in alignment with your values. Authenticity is a core principle of mine. The therapeutic relationship is vital and I work to create a warm, collaborative space. I can help you get to know yourself better, tune into your own experience, and find your inner wisdom. I am influenced primarily by Existential-Humanistic theories as well as Relational-Cultural Therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, and mind/body integration practices. With those ideas in mind, my primary objective is to help you build self-awareness because I believe YOU are the only expert on your life.
My Values as a Therapist
Prior to graduate school, I worked in the performing arts. That experience grounds my counseling work in mindfulness and creativity. I have studied yoga and meditation for over 20 years, strengthening my personal practice and building my somatic awareness. I will bring a curiosity to our collaboration that orients from a social justice and multicultural perspective, which means that we will consider all aspects of your lived experience. I have studied addiction formally and informally for decades. I am interested in how it impacts loved ones and how we can continue to work toward destigmatization. I am relational in style (a focus on relationships), which also includes the counselor/client relationship. I am committed to creating an environment that is safe and where you will feel heard, respected, and valued. Through our collaboration and from a holistic perspective, we will take time to explore how counseling can best serve you.

name: Magnolia Medlin (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In my practice, I use a blend of Somatic Experiencing, Voice Dialogue Parts Work, and mindfulness-based present moment inquiry to create a compassionate and attuned space of inner exploration and healing. Often, experiences of depression and anxiety are the result of an impulse getting “stuck” in the system and not being able to fully move, release, and complete its cycle. In working with me, you will learn how to track and nurture your nervous system and to support life energies to move more freely through your body. With parts work, we gently disentangle the knot of different “selves” or competing perspectives that can dominate our lives. Together, we make space for the critic, the pleaser, the know-it-all, the fearful child, etc etc., and get to know their deeper desires and needs. In our work together, you come to the embodied understanding that you contain these perspectives, but they are not who you are, fundamentally.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I first considered becoming a therapist after finishing a two year period of residency at a Buddhist monastery — I wanted to find a vocation where I could have authentic encounters with other humans, where we could open to the inherent capacities of the human body/mind to heal and to grow into greater ease and wellbeing. I am particularly drawn to working with girls, women, and female-identified folk of all ages to support the feminine to tune into the wisdom, power, and joy of the body to heal itself and to move from "stuckness" into flow. I am an expressive arts therapist, and I like to use a wide range of creative practices, including embodied parts work, dream exploration, writing exercises, sound/singing, and visual art/collage, to tap into the deep reservoirs of creative wisdom inside of you. Ultimately, I see therapy as a space where you can discover, when shame and fear are no longer dominating the system, what it means for you to live a joyful, creative, meaningful life

name: Louise Gordon (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am committed to following the guidelines to protect our health during the COVID-19 pandemic. I am able to do telehealth sessions. I use a variety of techniques depending on your needs. Feeling safe and secure are the most important indicators of positive therapy outcomes. Starting with that as our foundation, therapeutic techniques such as EMDR and Internal Family Systems, and solution focused therapy will provide added support to the therapeutic process. Many symptoms are caused by unresolved inner conflicts and inner polarizations. Resolving these, you are able to experience your own truth and relate to the external world in the way that is the most powerful for you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I studied Counseling Psychology at Lewis and Clark College and after that received training in Addictions Treatment, EMDR, trauma, Internal Family Systems. I also taught at Lewis and Clark College and became an LPC supervisor. I was the co-Clinical Director for A Home Within, a volunteer program for counselors to help kids in the foster care system. I have studied yoga and meditation for several decades. I believe we need to move beyond pathologizing ourselves and others. I always hold the space for each client as a complete and whole person, not a diagnosis or a problem to fix.

name: Arah Erickson (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Background
Welcome to my practice. I am so glad you are here! My name is Arah, and I am a Professional Counselor Associate based in Portland, Oregon. I received my Master’s degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from Portland State University. My training included work in community counseling and university counseling clinics, and I have served as an adult group facilitator supporting grieving families. I bring to this work a passion for providing a warm, accepting environment where I hope you will feel safe, seen and cared for. Some things you may be interested to know about me: I am an Oregon native who spent many years on the East Coast. I am a cancer survivor, and before I became a counselor, I had careers in international economic development and business & technology.
My Approach to Helping
My counseling style is compassionate and accepting, and I am curious about what it’s like to be you in the world. I strongly believe that you are the expert on you and that, once given a little time to breathe in a safe space, you will begin to connect to important parts of yourself that will enable healing and growth. I will be your partner in this process, helping you to gain fresh insight, feel self-compassion, and see your strengths. My counseling approach is rooted in person-centered, attachment-based therapy. I believe that an authentic, trusting relationship between counselor and client is the foundation for change and serves as a powerful launching pad for exploring specific therapeutic strategies and skills that best fit with your intentions, hopes and goals. I often incorporate emotion-focused therapy and Internal Family Systems therapy into my work. I find emotional awareness can be a powerful catalyst for change.

name: Will Fairchild (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My work is grounded in the belief of the inherent strength of each person, and I am dedicated to helping clients build resilience, self-compassion, and agency in their lives. My goal is to provide compassionate, trauma-informed care that empowers you to create meaningful change. Whether you're seeking CBT for anxiety, trauma therapy, or support navigating gender identity, I offer a collaborative, affirming, and client-centered approach to help you move forward.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
When I’m not working, you’ll likely find me running around the city or Forest Park training for the next half-marathon, at the coast or a hiking with my two wiener dogs, and most importantly spending time with my friends and loved ones.

name: Hannah Brassell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a therapist, I take a holistic and individualized approach to understanding each person's unique challenges, drawing on evidence-based modalities like CBT, ACT, and solution-focused therapy. I also value the mind-body connection and incorporate this exploration into our work when it aligns with an individual’s needs and goals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that a strong, collaborative, therapeutic relationship is essential for meaningful change. My approach centers on creating a safe, supportive space where you feel heard, understood, and empowered, with your goals guiding our work together. My aim is to help you build resilience and foster personal growth, and I am here every step of the way!

name: Jae Douglas
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I utilize cognitive-behavioral and strengths-based therapies to explore the experience and meaning, as well as the challenges and opportunities, present in major the changes that they are experiencing. In session, we focus on grounding tools to develop and utilize strategies to calm and ground yourself in the present, and to imagine and realize a future when you will feel stable, healed and whole.
My Values as a Therapist
I strive to create a warm, welcoming atmosphere that is thoughtful, active and engaging. I believe it important to create a safe space for clients to explore their history and life story with openness and curiosity. I focus on supporting and guiding clients in exploring how their life experience, skills, strengths and insights can be brought to bear on their current situation so as to find and move authentically and positively forward.

name: Cheyenne McDougall (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Stepping into the world of therapy can feel intimidating, but it’s also a space where transformation begins. My own journey with therapy started at a young age, with pauses along the way, yet I always found myself returning. Each time, I poured out the swirling thoughts and emotions running wild in my head. My hope is to offer the understanding and encouragement that you might need, as we navigate the raw, unfiltered, and messy parts of life. Drawing on years of experience with vulnerable populations and Trauma-Informed Care, I use a person-centered approach to therapy, while also including elements from Feminist Therapy. This means we focus on you—your emotions, strengths, and sense of belonging—so you can navigate this wild, unpredictable world with confidence and purpose.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe that we live in a world, in a society, that tries its hardest to make us feel small. I believe that we are constantly met with contradicting expectations of who and what we should be in this world. It is these kinds of expectations and norms that introduce pain and chaos into our lives. I value each individual for who they are and welcome diverse individuals, beliefs, and values into my space, with an effort to help you make sense of who you are in this world. Though therapy is hard work, I emphasize and encourage self-care in all forms with my clients (firm believer in receiving tattoos as a form of therapy) and am no stranger to recognizing that our society has not been built to encourage self care.

name: Christina Dunning (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Using our counseling relationship as a foundation from which to grow and heal, together we can explore patterns of interaction and ways of being, deepen understanding and acceptance of underlying emotions and needs, and help you gain freedom in choosing how to be in the world and with those you love. I work from an attachment and family systems perspective, exploring internal parts (Internal Family Systems) and patterns (both internal and relational). I see counseling as a place to collaboratively explore patterns and roles that were once helpful or necessary, but no longer serve us, and as a place to practice new ways of being and connecting in the present moment. I also see counseling as a place to grow understanding and acceptance of our authentic selves.

name: Raina Moore (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I specialize in adhd, depression, anxiety, & trauma. Theories I use are Mindfulness, Trauma Informed CBT, EMDR, Somatic, Strength Based, Art & Inner Child Work. I work within a social justice lens, sex-positive lens, & health at every size lens. I work to create a safe space for the queer community, neurodivergent community, & fat community.
Why I Became a Therapist
I grew up in foster care where I lived and moved into over twenty homes in the Midwest before I aged out of the system at the age of 21. I saw a lot of people suffer around me, including myself. I experienced different types of childhood abuse, trauma, and grief growing up. I also experienced systemic barriers from growing up in poverty, neurodivergent, existing as a plus-size (aka fat), and femme presenting person. I have learned a lot about suffering through my experience, my education, and my career. I know on a very deep level that it is possible to change your life, heal, and overcome. I would feel honored to help be apart of your process healing and growing.

name: Paige Cameron (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Pleased to offer EMDR services as a Certified EMDR Therapist. I strive to create a warm, safe, and accepting environment where you can truly come as you are. I find that sometimes the best medicine is a bit of laughter and understanding that we are all humans in progress. I believe the client is the expert and already has everything they need to heal move forward inside themselves. Sometimes, we all need a little help to get unstuck, to see things from a new perspective, and to take a breath and realize how far we have come. In our work together, we will use Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, Polyvagal Theory, and EMDR focusing on your strengths, bringing an awareness to how our body holds trauma, and utilize a variety of tools to create real change and bring healing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Therapy is deep, life changing work, and it is a privilege to be able to walk with someone on their journey towards healing. My passion is working with those experiencing anxiety, depression, trauma, survivors of sexual assault, and loss and grief. My primary focus is individuals ages 14 and older. I am able to accept OHP insurance and can provide Superbills for the client to then submit to their insurance company. I also offer a "at the time of session" cash pay rate. Please reach out to see if we would be a good fit. I look forward to hearing from you!

name: Kaci Schmitt (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a strengths-based, relational and compassion-focused therapist in Portland, Oregon. I love working with adolescents and adults with anxiety, depression, and difficulty adjusting to change and indecision, as well as helping clients process and integrate grief and loss into their lives. My approach to therapy is guided by my belief that through our work together, we can create a safe space where you can find peace, develop your own intuition and wisdom to make your own choices about what's right for your life, and create new meaning to live in a more balanced, free, and gentle way. Therapy is a safe place where we can process your life together. I believe that you are your greatest source of wisdom and that in our work together, we can uncover what is keeping you stuck and find the resources you need to move forward. I believe that compassion and warmth are essential to therapy, and I prioritize creating a strong therapeutic relationship with my clients.
More About the People I Treat
I help my clients explore their unique challenges and life situations so that they can uncover joy, contentment, and ease, as well as find solutions to unresolved issues and process and manage trauma and pain. I encourage my clients to look within themselves to find their own strengths, resources, and inner wisdom. And because being a human is not always easy, I encourage my clients to develop deep self-compassion and curiosity about themselves and their lives. This process is complex, and each person is unique, so I approach each client individually and with care and compassion. Many of the clients who find me (or whose parents find me) tend to be highly sensitive individuals who are empathic, attuned to their environments and other people (and often had to be beginning in childhood due to trauma), and want to live happy, meaningful lives but who are overwhelmed by their current challenges or find themselves “stuck” in certain emotional, relational, or life patterns.

name: Robyn Cullen (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Becoming a therapist was a second career for me. After 10 years in the fast-paced world of corporate marketing, I was laid off during the 2008 recession. The much-needed career change directed my inner compass to a master's program in marriage and family therapy that same year. I had no idea how becoming a therapist would completely unravel EVERYTHING I thought to be true about life, family, love, relationships, spirituality, and ME. Such a gift. This is a space to talk about any of the nuances folks experience relating to (but not limited to) race, sexuality, gender, neurodivergence, ability, unearned privilege and/or advantage and anything else that may be or have been “taboo” in other areas of life. I bring this work to life through individual therapy for those in California & Oregon, group community-building gatherings, Usui reiki energy work and holding spiritual spaces for those who gave up on organized religion but deeply miss and yearn for a spiritual community.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe therapy is political - meaning that therapy as a practice & institution is influenced by and contributes to the politics of society. I acknowledge the privileges & advantages (both earned & unearned) that I hold as a working class white American citizen with settler ancestry and post-secondary education who presents as neurotypical, straight, and able-bodied. While my position as a non-straight, neurodivergent person with mostly-stable mental health has allowed me to generate more insights and compassion into these lived experiences, I am committed to listening, learning, unlearning, decolonizing, and growing within my personal & professional worlds. I live & work through a lens of anti-oppressive & anti-racism. I am constantly working to create supportive & supported spaces for those who are marginalized, oppressed & silenced to be seen, heard & witnessed by hiring and learning from mentors of the global majority.

name: Chris Gorski (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I practice from a Person-Centered approach. I believe in the human Spirit’s potential for growth, change, and renewal to bring about transformation that creates a life worth living that you can be excited and passionate about. I utilize the Science of Yoga and Meditation and Mindfulness practices such as the power of the Present Moment and other Mindfulness practices that bring compassion and unconditional acceptance of self to help my clients achieve their treatment goals that we agree upon together. I use Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT) to help clients in working through and resolving personal relationship issues that they struggle with at their job, at home as well as with their children and spouses. Changing is hard I encourage you to have compassion for yourself during this process. I have been a Yoga and Meditation Teacher for 7 years and have practiced and been a recipient of the transforming power of the Science of Yoga and Meditation for around 20 years.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe the most important component in Counseling is first and foremost the Therapeutic Relationship. This will determine how effective and successful therapy will be for you. Do you feel heard? Do I see you for the unique individual that you are? Do you feel understood, do you feel safe? All research confirms the relationship between therapist and client needs to be supportive, nurturing and intact for you to feel safe enough to change and achieve your goals that we set together as a team. I have served in diverse types of nonprofit agencies over the years. Such as an emergency services counselor at a homeless shelter to battered women and children at a runaway youth shelter to working at a juvenile detention facility with felony convicted juveniles. I have also worked with elementary, junior, and senior high school students with different personal and academic issues with which they were struggling. I have worked with the elderly. I worked with impoverished kids from Mexico.

name: Tate Sprite (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
My main therapeutic modality is Emotionally Focused Therapy. This attachment-based approach is non-pathologizing: we trust that a person’s inner experience as well as their actions make sense in some way that is trying to help. What has healing power is when you have enough safety in a relationship (e.g. with a therapist) that you can recognize your patterns and befriend your own emotions with compassion. Once you have new, more connected emotional experiences in real time, you start to find a new sense of self. Your capacity to navigate human experience grows. Then you can safely begin to deepen into difficult experiences from the past, to now directly feel and see and integrate them in a new, better, fuller way. Gradually, these changes shape a new story of yourself and your life, and naturally lead to new actions. This approach is known to be helpful across a variety of diagnoses but especially with those facing depression, anxiety, and the effects of trauma.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I may be a great fit if: ~~ you want to do attachment work ~~ you practice mindfulness, or want to start ~~ you want to be more aware of your body or do somatic work ~~ you've exhausted your options with talk therapy ~~ you are right-brained, artistic, holistic, or intuitive ~~ you don't just want new tools and behavioral skills, you want to re-organize your basic operating system for doing life ~~ you're already committed to personal growth or have done a lot of work on yourself ****** My special affiliations: ~~ born and raised outside the US ~~ dysfunctional family ~~ spiritual seeker from when I was a kid ~~ lived through the violent fall of communism ~~ spent many years in a cult ~~ highly intelligent and highly sensitive ~~ am queer in a number of ways

name: Shannon LaDouce (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
My decade of nursing experience spans across every chapter of life, from labor and delivery to end of life and cancer care. -Outpatient mental-health facilities -Active-duty army registered nurse, variety of inpatient hospital settings all over the country -Nursing leadership -Nursing faculty and clinical instructing
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am also an International Sports Sciences Association Certified Fitness and Nutrition Trainer, and along with medication management, love to talk about ways to support your overall wellbeing.

name: Peter King (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I’m a Professional Counselor Associate with a Master’s degree in Professional Mental Health Counseling and a Certificate in Ecotherapy from Lewis & Clark Graduate School of Education and Counseling. I also hold a Bachelor of Arts in Psychology from Portland State University. My clinical work focuses on depression, anxiety, trauma (including PTSD and complex-PTSD), life transitions, career success, and men’s issues. I work with children (ages 10+), adolescents, and adults, tailoring my approach to each individual’s unique needs. I integrate Existential Therapy, Attachment Theory, and Ecotherapy in my practice, helping clients explore the connections between their emotional well-being and the world around them. This holistic, client-centered approach emphasizes healing through relationships and our connection to nature. I believe that understanding the interplay between personal struggles and external relationships is key to emotional growth and healing.

name: Corri Rekart (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I graduated from Rush Medical University School of Nursing and have worked in child, adolescent, and adult psychiatry. I am a Doctor of Nursing Practice who is board-certified in Psychiatry. My focus is collaboratively working to utilize individualized, evidence-based, integrated treatment strategies with clients. Many people face similar challenges, and together we can explore alternative solutions that resonate with you and your unique experiences. My goal is to create a safe and empathetic space where you can express your feelings and work towards meaningful change. I support alternative methods of treatment such as esketamine, TMS, lifestyle changes, and chronotherapy and provide education and referrals for those services.
My Approach to Helping
I can assist with medication management, mood disorders, and alternative treatments. A typical treatment plan includes personalized strategies, regular support, and consistent monitoring through a collaborative approach. You can expect to experience improved mental well-being, enhanced coping skills, and a more balanced life.

name: Lynn Emmons LCSW, LICSW, RN, CADCI (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use a holistic, compassionate and client-centered approach utilizing your strengths and learning style to individualize your therapy course; my toolbox is made up of techniques/ interventions from an array of models/theories and my approach is also informed by what we now know about the brain.
