For those with alcohol/drug use/addiction, I will work from an abstinence-based or harm-reduction stance depending on individual goals and challenges.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have almost 40 years experience as a psychiatric and addictions nurse and clinical social worker (LCSW) and addiction counselor (CADCI), in hospital, residential, and outpatient mental health and addictions settings. My experience is with children, adolescents, young adults, adults and families. I have a passion for working with people who struggle with addiction and I will provide support for facing the difficulties of early recovery or when life's hurdles challenge recovery along the way. I also have worked with health care professionals with mental health/addiction problems and can help support the unique struggles of working in healthcare and managing self-care. I also have experience and enjoy working with college students.

name: Andrew Conner (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
During therapy, I encourage my clients to reflect on how their past experiences with important people in their lives might be influencing how they go about their lives today. In addition to discussing things that have happened in the past, I ask my clients how they are feeling about me and relating to me in the moment. This can provide valuable insight into how they might relate to others outside of the therapy room. I welcome the discussion of dreams during therapy as a way to facilitate deep listening. I invite my clients to connect to their bodies and notice how emotions might be showing up there. I help my child client's express their feelings through the symbolic language of play.
My Values as a Therapist
I value inclusivity in my work. I welcome clients of all gender identities and sexual orientations, and those who are in a process of exploration. I welcome clients of all racial, ethnic, national, religious, and caste backgrounds.

name: Madeline Fox
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work with individuals to identify repetitive thoughts, ingrained expectations and outlooks that aren’t serving them and together we work to develop skills to address your unique challenges. We've structured modern life around the idea that one big relationship should be everything: the place where you get acceptance and intimacy, friendship and love, spontaneity and mischief. Everything all rolled into one. And for so many, and maybe you too, it’s just not working. Are you feeling deep sadness when you consider your life? Do you often struggle with feelings of hurt, disappointment, or anger? Do you feel like you just can’t ‘figure out’ how to make you life or relationships work? If you’re experiencing one (or many) of these, it’s time to shake things up. You don’t have to struggle through this alone, I’m committed to providing empowering guidance and insight to help you move forward toward the life you want to be living.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a Masters of Arts degree in Marriage, Couple, and Family Therapy with an emphasis in sex therapy from Lewis and Clark College. I experienced working with diverse clients as a therapist in multiple community mental health settings. In the spring of 2023, I participated in an in-depth couples therapy training with Terry Real. In November of 2017, I immersed myself in training with Esther Perel around rethinking infidelity and better understanding how to work with couples and individuals who are struggling to maintain desire and vitality within their relationships. In 2016, I worked with Esther Perel and Terry Real in their Relate training program to more comprehensively understand the intersection of love, sex, and power, in modern relationships. When I’m not helping clients build satisfying emotional and erotic lives my favorite pastimes are setting up picnics under big shaded trees, reading fiction novels, making pot pie from scratch, and catching up with friends.

name: Melinda Norman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
****My practice is in person with the option of telehealth in specialized circumstances**** As a practitioner, I believe in walking with my clients as they become responsible for the creation of their experience. This is not a simple task and takes significant effort as this path requires the mastery of your impulses, emotions, and body. You must have bravery to look into your own shadow. It can be painful and ungrounding. This path is not for the faint of heart. We will walk into the experience that you are having together; not bypass it while simultaneously assisting you in developing the skills necessary to reflect who you are choosing to become. I will support you as you learn to alchemize your pain and reorder your relationship with your experiences.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I operate on optimism with the ever important understanding of realistic expectations. My treatment focuses include *Generational trauma *Soul Retrieval/Power Retrieval *Regression therapy *Narcissistic abuse recovery *Abandonment and attachment *Somatic Trauma Therapy *Non religious spiritual development

name: Jessi Dey (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I received my master’s degree in counseling from Portland State University in 2008, and I opened my private practice later that same year. The focus in my master’s program was on couples, marriage and family counseling. I completed both a practicum and an internship through Portland, State University, where I counseled individuals, couples and families. Additionally, I provided counseling services at the YWCA counseling center in Portland, as an intern, and a volunteer and have maintained my busy private practice for over 15 years . I have had the opportunity to work with people from diverse backgrounds and people in nontraditional relationships of many kinds. My style is warm, open honest, straightforward, humorous, and non-judgemental. I find that counseling is often a process of unraveling early messages we may have received about ourselves and the world, figuring out where they originated, discarding those that are unhelpful and getting back in touch with who you really are.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Therapy is about having a caring, objective, involved listener who is invested in helping you to create meaningful and lasting change and difference in your life. I work to create an atmosphere of acceptance, safety, and hope in the counseling office. My goal is to create an environment in which people can trust me. I believe my philosophies and approach are inclusive, and can be effective with both individuals and couples, as well as with clients with backgrounds different from my own. I feel honored to be given the opportunity to hear my client’s stories, and witness as they make connections between the past and the present, and learn to listen to their inner voice, and accept themselves as they truly are. As this happens, people can begin living a more full and satisfying life.

name: Amanda Sawyer
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I balance empathy and compassion with a healthy dose of challenging you. I believe you have the power within you to change your unhealthy patterns in relationships and live a more fulfilling and embodied life. Therapy is not always a comfortable process. It requires us to turn toward ourselves in ways we likely have not done before. It is my intention to be right there with you in this process, so you know you are not alone. I am not the expert on your life and want to learn about you, from you. I am committed to providing you my honest feedback and building a rapport that helps you to share more and become open to the possibilities within you.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I use an eclectic approach to therapy, and operate from an ecotherapy lens. What is ecotherapy? It looks many different ways, depending on who the client is: some will opt for outdoor sessions and some will want to explore ways in between their sessions to increase the benefits of therapy by engaging in out-of-doors homework. I weave in knowledge about our connection to nature and how a disconnect from nature often adds to human suffering. Our modern, fast-paced lives were not designed with our well being in mind. Together, we will explore ways you might increase your connection with nature, of which we are all a part, and most of us are totally separate from.

name: Camillia de la Garza Thompson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I know that it is through relationship that we achieve deep and lasting change. The therapeutic relationship is unique in the way it supports personal growth so that we can set social constraints aside in order to explore how we truly feel. Therapy is serious business, but it can also be enjoyable. I bring joyful humor as well as compassion to the work. With a background in religious, spiritual, and cultural studies I go where you need to go, holding space for your truth to emerge. I believe our problems are sacred answers in disguise, which supports a non-pathologizing and collaborative approach to treatment. We are already whole, no matter what circumstances have led us to seek counseling, and the work of psychotherapy is a process of remembering this wholeness. Please note, at times the PTC messaging system does not deliver messages. If this happens, please reach me at therapywithcamillia@gmail.com
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received my Masters Degree in Counseling Psychology with an emphasis in Depth Psychology from Pacifica Graduate Institute. I am a Certified Movement Analyst through the Laban Institute of Movement Studies in New York City and have studied with many maestros. I bring a rich life history and a background of cultural psychology into my clinical work. My approach is rooted in extensive time abroad studying, living, and traveling through diverse regions of the globe. From these experiences I have come to know that we are cultural, spiritual and biological beings and bring this sensitivity and awareness to my approach. This allows me to appreciate and support the unique orientations my clients bring. Please note I do not bill insurance directly but can provide documents for out of network coverage.

name: Modi Soondarotok (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work best with community members experiencing trauma symptoms such as PTSD, anxiety, depression, ADHD, or grappling with the overlapping challenges of oppression and climate change distress. I also provide support for personal growth, career transitions, and navigating complex relationship dynamics, including those in monogamous and ethical non-monogamous partnerships. I am inspired to journey with teens, young adults, adults, and couples who value anti-oppressive frameworks and seek healing through lenses of social justice, intersectionality, and cultural humility. My practice integrates existential psychodynamic frameworks, Liberation Psychology, Queer Theory, Indigenous Theory, System Theory, Buddhist Psychology, and Interpersonal Neurobiology to co-create a path forward tailored to each individual's needs.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As someone deeply committed to equity, climate justice, and collective liberation, I strive to cultivate a therapeutic space that uplifts and honors each client’s unique lived experience. I offer gender-affirming care for LGBTQIA2S+ individuals and cultivate an inclusive, safe environment for BIPOC clients. Together, we will reimagine and decolonize the therapeutic process, nurturing growth and adaptability in ways that align with your values, identities, and aspirations. If you share a commitment to transformation, collective care, and healing that challenges oppressive systems, I welcome you to connect with me. Let’s envision and create what’s possible—together. Now accepting few new clients. Prioritizing BIPOC & Trans/Queer community members with OHP CareOregon/HealthShare. Affirming space for healing.

name: Victoria Gorman (SHE/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I utilize brainspotting, acceptance and commitment therapy and emotion focused therapy to help you feeling more calm and less overwhelmed in your day to day life. Brainspotting can you help you process emotions more deeply and get to the root of anxiety and depression. I also utilize skills like mindfulness and structured problem solving to help you improve your executive functioning.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that everyone has the inner resources and resiliency to change and grow into the person they have always wanted to be. I have ADHD myself and am very passionate about helping others navigate life in way that works with their brain rather than against it. I have always had a passion or advocating for social justice and decentering the patriarchy and bring this lens into sessions.

name: Bjanikka Ben (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I graduated from Lewis & Clark College with a degree in Professional Mental Health Counseling, and I approach my work through a social justice framework and a trauma-informed lens. I believe in challenging and transforming old narratives that may no longer serve you in your daily life. My goal is to help you identify and heal wounded parts of yourself, fostering resilience and growth. Additionally, I deeply value the bond between humans and animals, recognizing its healing potential. I have a golden retriever puppy named Wednesday who is a trained service animal and you may see her in our sessions.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
A Beginner's Mind Counseling approaches healing through curiosity, non-judgment, growth, and learning. It invites individuals on a journey of self-discovery, where all parts of themselves can be fully felt, seen, and heard. I believe it is essential to cultivate a capacity for change, which allows us to shift unhealthy narratives and better understand the root causes of maladaptive behaviors. By doing so, we can actively engage in the healing process—because time alone doesn’t heal; it only creates distance.

name: Susan Evans, PsyD (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work in a collaborative style, incorporating techniques and theories from psychodynamic, humanistic, cognitive behavioral theories with the research on development, attachment and the neurobiological impact of trauma. Please use the link to go to my website where there is more info. And please free to contact me for more info.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received my doctoral degree over 15 yeas ago from the Wright Institute in Berkeley, CA and I am a licensed clinical psychologist in Oregon. In my training and working years, was lucky enough to be able to learn from gifted clinicians and teachers. I had the good fortune to train at UCSF at the AIDS Health Project, UCSF Child Trauma Research Project, and at the San Francisco Psychotherapy Research Group. I've since received advanced training in Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT), EMDR, Buddhist Psychology, and PTSD treatments. When not working, I can be found walking my dogs, spending time with my family, digging in the dirt (both metaphorically and literally!) and dreaming of road trips.

name: Lisa Wittorff (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use an evidence-based yet intuitive approach to working with people. I have many years of experience, and that helps me to be able to quickly assess how to work with you in a way that works best and helps you to make progress. We laugh together, cry together and grow together. I am direct, which means I say it like it is, I don't sit back and just reflect what you've said. I help you think and feel through what's happening in your life and what you'd like to be different. I help you figure out how you can think and feel differently about your life, so you can change it.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker in Portland, Oregon. I have many years experience working with families and children with special needs. I especially love working with children who are not neurotypical, including diagnoses such as RAD, FASD, ASD. ADHD and other challenging behaviors that don't include a diagnosis . I am also the adoptive parent of 3 children from foster care who are now young adults. My children have been through almost every struggle and diagnosis possible, so I have a lot of “hands on” parenting knowledge. I have tried many kinds of parenting throughout my journey, and discovering Mindful Parenting and Collaborative Problem Solving finally made everything going on with my children and the families I work with make sense. I was able to use these tools to transform my relationship with my children, and help other parents change their relationships as well. To learn more, check out my website at wiseowlcoaching.org , or email me at wiseowlparenting@gmail.com.

name: Israa Al-Hasani (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
With sixteen years of experience working in diverse communities, my therapeutic approach is deeply rooted in multicultural awareness. As a survivor and member of the global south community, I bridge personal experience with professional expertise to create healing spaces grounded in authentic, culturally-affirming relationships that honor both individual and collective wisdom. My approach integrates relational and anti-oppressive frameworks, acknowledging that healing occurs within the context of our cultural, social, and political realities. Together, we'll explore your experiences with deep empathy and respect, drawing upon the enduring strength of our ancestors and the transformative power of creative expression.
Why I Became a Therapist
As an Arab woman, immigrant, survivor, and mother, my path to therapy emerged from profound lived experiences. Beginning as a peer support specialist, I witnessed the transformative power of authentic connection and shared understanding in healing. Navigating cultural transitions, motherhood, and survival has gifted me with deep insights into resilience and growth. These intersecting identities taught me that healing isn't linear – our struggles often contain seeds of wisdom waiting to flourish. My journey from peer support to professional therapy strengthens my commitment to breaking down hierarchical barriers. My approach weaves together clinical expertise with personal understanding of how culture, trauma, and resilience shape our healing paths. This unique perspective helps me create a space where your story can unfold authentically, your experiences are deeply validated, and your resilience is recognized as a wellspring of strength.

name: Natasha Yaganeh (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My goal is to attend to you or the relationship with consideration, curiosity, kindness, and empathy. This journey looks different for each person, couple(s), and relationship structures as we are all unique in our experiences and development. I will meet you where you are as we begin a journey toward your own personal evolution, whatever that may be. I draw from several theoretical modalities based upon my clients’ needs, primarily working from a humanistic and person-centered stance. My clinical approach continually draws from a Trauma Informed lens utilizing Emotion Focused, Attachment Models, Polyvagal Theory, Internal Family Systems, Accelerated Experiential Dynamic Psychotherapy (AEDP), and Interpersonal Neurobiology practices. In our sessions, we will explore your past and present experiences to inform the process of self-discovery and growth.

name: Elisabeth Duckwell
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I empower clients to utilize practical skills to cope and manage anxiety, depression, grief & relationship conflict. I provide a therapeutic space to facilitate open & honest dialogue. Using evidence based & experiential practices like Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Motivational Interviewing & Attachment theory. Let's identify support needs & create goals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
*Not accepting clients until mid Sept, not accepting adults clients. Let’s talk wellness, let's talk healing and let's talk baby steps. Many of us are exploring therapy for the first time and figuring out what we need. Feel free to reach out for consultation, support and insight. Along with clinical knowledge, I am passionate about advocacy to decrease mental health stigmas. I am licensed professional counselor in the state of Oregon.

name: Eileen Summit (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker(LCSW) with over 30yrs of experience. I worked as an elementary school counselor for 7 yrs, then as a Hospice and Palliative Care Social Worker since 2004. I started my private practice, Mountain Sage counseling, in 2020. I have had the opportunity to work with youngsters and their parents, and patients, family members and caregivers. I understand the health care systems benefits and problems, and am familiar with supporting elders and their families, as they consider transitioning to higher levels of care. I have an empathetic, client-centered approach, focused on helping you recognize and enhance your strengths, improve your coping skills, alter you negative thought patterns, and find meaning and joy in life, by living from your values. I use an insight-oriented approach and draw from many therapeutic models, including cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT) and internal family systems(IFS).
Social Justice Issues I Care About
I was raised in the Judeo-Christian tradition. I had opportunities to work as a volunteer at a 'soup kitchen' and with children with disabilities, from the age of 13. In HS, I was drawn to service activities, and to 'helping those less fortunate'. In college, I learned about racism, feminism, 'the military industrial complex', and the dangers of nuclear energy. I developed a justice lense, asking, "who gains by this action or policy?" and developing a 'preferential option for the poor' stance. I had the meaningful opportunity to be a 'Jesuit Volunteer', after college, working with emotionally abused children, and then moving to the NorthWest to be a supervisor for volunteers working in rural cities and on 'Indian Reservations'. I learned to love the NorthWest, and continued working with low income families until I went to graduate school. I have been fortunate to have a degree that allowed me to do the work I care about, while raising 2 daughters!

name: Anthony Marchant
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
The perspective I have on health and healing is deeply rooted in my training in Transpersonal/Buddhist Psychology. I use Gestalt, Systems, and Experiential therapy to help clients move deeply into their experience in the here-and-now to live life more fully. I use Somatic Experiencing (TM) to work with all trauma related issues. I use Nonviolent Communication to help clients explore their feelings and needs, improving their connection to their self and to others. I also use meditation and mindfulness practices to increase a client's awareness, helping clients to feel grounded in their self and in the world. I work with people’s strengths and challenges to help them find the unique tools that are just right for them in their journeys towards health and happiness. The path towards greater fulfillment in life begins the moment one feels this longing for something more and answering the call to one’s personal quest is one of the most rewarding gifts a person can give their self.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold a masters degree in Transpersonal Psychology from Naropa University and did my undergraduate work at the University of Oregon in psychology. In my early twenties I began to get involved in mentoring youth-at-risk and learning and practicing Nonviolent Communication. I also spent two years living abroad as a volunteer in the Peace Corps. I am a Licensed Professional Counselor in Oregon and have extensive experience working with couples, families, and adolescents. I also have experience working in a school setting and with domestic violence issues. Some of the areas I specialize in are: co-dependency issues, adolescent and parenting issues, communication problems, anger, mood and anxiety problems, men's work, spiritual/personal transitions, and trauma related issues. I have been practicing in Oregon since 2009.

name: Natalya Imboden (SHE HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I approach the body as a completely intelligent, conscious, and energetically attuned system. Your body knows exactly what it needs to heal and give you the feedback for innate health. I define body as Mind, Spirit, and Physiology. Each person is unique in their pace, nourishment needs, style of connection, and change pathways. We clarify your needs through simple body centered practices with sensation, breathing, perception, and orientation being our guides. We understand change comes through repetition through various neurological pathways so we create guided and self-directed resources of: breathwork, audio sound meditations, dance prompts, cognitive reframing, and nourishment styles. I also do hands on work with your energy system and your blocks that may need your attention. Once we feel your are resourced and their is relational safety and trust I offer a somatic session of selective inhibition with Medicine or non-medicine to access deeper biological charges.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a survivor of Complex Childhood PTSD and this gives me a nuanced lens through which I view the process of healing and transformation. I began my recovery from dissociative states and invisibility through the study of dance ethnology and dance movement therapy in the 90's. I lacked a sense of belonging and power which led me to studying with other cultures rituals and spirituality. Experiencing firsthand the power of shamanism, primal movements, emotional release, and the power of a witness I learned a non-colonial approach to healing. I found that most healing happens not by talking about it but by the nuance of experience, emotions, felt sense of attunement, rhythm (repetition), and human connection. I also was blessed to meet my Spiritual Guru at a young age who offered me a calm resting with clarity that was not conditional on outer phenomenon. These two orientations create a creative and open atmosphere of my coaching practice.

name: Erin Carney Moline (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I meet my clients where they are, and connect with what is important to them, in order to elicit the best possible experience both in and out of session. We all have stories we need to tell, skills we need to learn, and feelings we need to feel. My mission as a therapist is to help my clients to heal and be in deeper connection with themselves, their gifts, and our shared world.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have been studying meditation and spirituality my entire adult life, and these experiences and my feelings around the importance of love and connection in life deeply inform my practice as a therapist. Formally, I received my MA in Counseling Psychology from Lewis & Clark in 2007, and my LPC license in 2009.

name: Fatema Rashid (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am dedicated to helping clients accept and tolerate the discomfort and pains that are a normal part of the human experience. By working with me, you will gain deeper insight into your patterns and valuable tools to cultivate self-regulation. By increasing self-awareness, we create greater self-compassion and understanding, which ultimately results in a happier, healthier life. I use attachment focused therapies, Hakomi, somatic and mindfulness, and parts work to support clients through their healing. I strive to create a safe, non-judgmental, and inclusive space for all individuals to explore their emotions and experiences. I prioritize the needs and goals of my clients and am committed to their growth and healing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a deep appreciation for the human experience and I value diversity. I work with people of all races, spiritual faiths, and who identify as LGBTQ+. I hold a safe, supportive space where you can explore, integrate, and shed layers that may not be serving you anymore. Together, we will honor all of your experiences and work towards creating a life that feels meaningful, even in the midst of difficult times.

name: Carol Roberts MA LPC LMFT
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
For over 30 years, I've had the honor of assisting couples, families, and individuals of all ages, as they identify, clarify and find constructive coping strategies to deal with issues relating to anxiety, depression, grief, anger and relationships. As a Marriage & Family Therapist, I especially enjoy working on issues such as affair recovery, attachment disorders and phase-of-life challenges. I prefer to work with those who have clear goal goals, and who are solution-focused.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a dually-licensed (LPC and LMFT), multi-modality therapist with over 30 years of collective experiences of empowering individuals and families, my goal is to help you uncover your true potential and lead a life that is worth celebrating. While we can't change difficult situations of the past, we can work together to better understand and resolve challenges in your life. By applying complementary therapy approaches and techniques, we will unearth long-standing behavior patterns or negative perceptions that may be holding you back from experiencing a more fulfilling and meaningful life. If you're looking for extra support and guidance through a challenging situation or you're just ready to move in a new direction in your life, I look forward to working with you to achieve your goals.

name: Julie Jorgensen (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
My background includes play therapy, sand tray, expressive arts, and collaborative problem-solving.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I firmly believe that the strength of the therapeutic relationship is crucial to effective therapy. Finding a therapist with whom you feel a strong connection can make a significant difference in the therapeutic process. With 25 years of experience working with children and families, primarily in school settings, I bring a wealth of knowledge and a deep commitment to emotional awareness and self-regulation.

name: Ellie Antico (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I'm work primarily from the Internal Family Systems model, which engages both client and therapist's innate capacities for courage and compassion as tools for transformation. My work is also informed by several other approaches including EMDR , IPNB-informed boundary work, and others. We'll weave these into the combination that works best for you.
My Values as a Therapist
Struggle is universal. Through sharing our experiences with one another we’re able to feel connected, make new meaning, and conquer shame.

name: Kimberly Zeszutek, LPC (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Excited to relay! The Ecotherapy Geodesic Dome office is now open! FIRE Circle Neurodivergent teen group - now meeting in person at a local art collective! My style and practice align with adults, young adults, and adolescents; with a focus on Neurodivergent support. I provide a healthy balance of concrete skills with alternative informed approach. Sessions are co-created, meaning lead by your needs with therapist insight and challenges to help with growth. I am experienced in supporting complex emotions, transitions, relationships, suicidal ideation, self harm, depression, anxiety, and learning differences. Ecotherapy is introduced to liven the environment and integrate the healing powers of nature. My services are BIPOC, LGBTQIA+, and Psychedelic friendly.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My career in counseling began in 2007, when I graduated with a Bachelors in Psychology at the University of Minnesota, Minneapolis. Spending a few years in the Rocky Mountains, in 2014, I earned my Masters in Counseling at Regis University, Denver. I am heavily influenced by my internship which allowed exploration of ecopsychology. My background as a counselor includes ABA therapy for children diagnosed with Autism, residential counseling for adults with severe and persistent mental illness, domestic violence shelter advocacy, crisis response therapy, and DBT for teens and families. I have extensive training with a Full Fidelity DBT Model, however do not provide full Fidelity DBT in this practice. I have supported individuals and families in clinical center settings, home therapy, and outdoor adventure camps. My specialization has developed into supporting those who are struggling with suicidal thoughts, understanding neurodiversity, major life transitions, and/or behavior changes.

name: Heather Simpson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My work centers on providing intersectional, pleasure focused, trauma informed therapy for individuals, couples, and polyamorous /nonmonogamous folks to create a space for queer affirming, relationship counseling. My mission is to provide comprehensive and systemic relational therapy centered on providing spaces for self agency and trauma informed care. My philosophy and approach is centered on mindful, contextual, emotion focused and trans-generational practices.
Why We are a Good Fit for You
I am a speaker, advocate and mental health professional with a Masters degree in marriage couple and family therapy from Lewis and Clark College. I also holds bachelor degrees in psychology, philosophy, and women's and gender studies from the University of Michigan-Dearborn. As a queer Black woman, I am committed to providing trans-generational contextual and emotion-focused therapy to people of color, LGBTQIA+, non-monogamous, and kink communities.

name: Megan Samsel (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Whether we are exploring the relationship we have with ourselves or with others, I believe compassion and loving-presence are essential to understanding the parts of ourselves that feel tender and/or condemned. It isn’t uncommon for these tender parts to appear to be stuck in patterns that seem to further disconnect us from ourselves and others. I specialize in working with couples and individuals who are neurodivergent, and/or have complex trauma. I help clients create sustainable change through first understanding the "why" behind what they do. From there, we work on reducing polarization (I'm so bad/they're so bad) in order to create the spaciousness needed to initiate change how YOU see fit--not how I see fit. I use a blend of somatic, relational, and emotions-focused techniques to create an embodied sense of awareness of my client's patterns and support them in deciding what to do next with this new awareness.
My Approach to Helping
As a therapist, I value the reparative experiences we can find in relationship to others. Whether I am working with individuals or couples, I work using a relational and emotions-focused approach to deconstruct the "values" that an individualistic and toxic culture have instilled in all of us. Some of these values include notions such as: we must have more control over our partners, children, and even ourselves (I must be more positive, more productive, I must be self-sufficient, etc.). The antidote to many of our modern day problems is healthy interdependence and co-regulation, but we are taught from a young age that this is a threat. Let's use the therapeutic relationship to have a reparative experience and use it as a spring board to get you connected to others. Let's figure out what your values and goals are to create the life/relationship you want. Let's build the distress tolerance needed to accept imperfection in yourself, and in others.

name: Edward Quedado (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Psychotherapy is fundamentally an open space, an invitation. There is a freedom to it that takes courage and tolerance. It can involve a deconstructive or constructive momentum where you may not necessarily be told what to do or how to feel. I provide a space that is confidential, patient and curious to allow us to experience what comes to mind and indeed, what does not.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My personal belief is that all people should have access to long-term, deep psychotherapy. The origins of the field at one point lived up to this promise, and I believe that we should continue to honor this history by treating the psychotherapeutic relationship as a place where political, cultural and psychological strains can all converge and mix. Please feel free to get in touch about sliding scale options; it is something I am open to discussing.

name: Christine Finucane (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We will use both mindfulness and Somatic Experiencing, an approach developed by Peter Levine, Ph.D., to help you come into easeful alignment with your physiology. When we are alignment with our physiology we learn to trust ourselves and our own innate inner wisdom. We are no longer living in a state of fear, anxiety and worry. I am also trained in brainspotting through phase 2 which can be a very powerful modality to heal repressed trauma. I have also recently completed a training program in Somatic EMDR.
Social Justice Issues I Care About
I am very proactive in addressing all kinds of discrimination such as racial, ethnic, culture, gender, sexual orientation, ableism, etc. I am committed to addressing any systemic barriers that our patriarchal society is responsible for. I have been involved politically to address many of these issues. I work somatically with the nervous system to heal all forms of trauma.

name: Henry Cameron (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe in creating a safe, non judgmental, creative, and illuminating space to unpack and examine the different parts of ourselves and our environment.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
An Oregon native with 20 years of experience in the Portland area. I am an LPC and I greatly enjoy working hard with Individuals or couples to help you achieve the kind of life you want for yourself.

name: Michael Crockett, PsyD
approaches:
My Approach to Helping I utilize an integrative approach drawing from mindfulness, CBT, and humanistic therapy systems. The focus is on what is happening in your life TODAY and how you want it to look differently. I strive to provide a safe, non-judgmental space so that you don’t need to continue struggling on your own.

I specialize in treating anxiety, depression, relationship, and identity concerns. Depending on your needs, we can take a directive approach targeting specific symptoms (e.g. social anxiety, excessive worry, panic attacks) or a less structured approach where you can reflect upon your life and identify what you want to pursue.

Together we can uncover the patterns in your thoughts, behaviors, and relationships that are holding you back, and establish a happier and healthier way of being in the world. I utilize a collaborative client-centered approach to provide the balance between supporting and challenging you that is necessary for promoting growth and change. My Personal Beliefs and Interests I have studied psychology for a decade, completing a MA in General Psychology from NYU in 2007, and a PsyD in Clinical Psychology from Pacific University in 2014. My dissertation examined the influence of significant experiences (for example: a traumatic event or a spiritual emergency) on shaping one’s worldview and approach to living. For Internship I worked for Seattle University's Counseling and Psychological Services, providing individual and group therapy to students. Prior to operating a private practice I worked in a crisis line call center which I found to be challenging and rewarding work.

name: Danielle Ropp (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I place a strong emphasis on developing insight and empathy within a non-judgmental and compassionate environment. From a strengths-based perspective, I work to empower clients to gain the skills necessary to address and resolve problems, change behaviors, improve relationships and navigate and cope with stressors.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I earned my master’s degree in social work from Boise State University. I have over 14 years of experience practicing as a nurse and working within social services providing counseling and care coordination to adolescents, adults, and families. My therapeutic background is rooted in medical and health psychology, assisting individuals and families coping with normal or unwanted transitions, chronic illness, end of life issues, and those experiencing symptoms of anxiety and depression. I am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker through the Oregon Board of Licensed Social Workers and I am an active member of the National Association of Social Workers (NASW).

name: Martha Carlson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We will work together to determine which style and type of therapy will work best for you. There are so many to choose from and your buy-in will increase the chance that therapy will reap the benefits you are seeking. I deeply believe in harnessing your strengths in our work together.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe growth and exploration is a useful tool at any age or stage in a person's life. I value compassion, candor and humor.

name: Erica Damazo (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
As a compassionate and collaborative therapist, I believe that healing happens through connection, and I am here to witness your process with authenticity and humility. I approach therapy from a person-centered lens, recognizing that each individual who walks into my office is the true expert on their own life. One of the bravest things a person can do is ask for help, and I am honored to be a part of your journey toward healing and self-discovery. I earned my Master of Arts in Professional Mental Health Counseling from Lewis & Clark College, where I also completed my practicum in an outpatient setting at their community counseling center. I gained valuable experience during my internship at Kaiser Outpatient Addiction Medicine, where I focused on addiction recovery and mental health support. Prior to this, I earned my Bachelor of Arts in Social and Behavioral Studies from George Fox University.
My Values as a Therapist
I value the opportunity to understand your life from your unique perspective while considering the larger systems you are connected to. Together, we will explore the areas of your life that need attention and establish goals that will guide our work together. Whether you're navigating anxiety, depression, addiction, or life transitions, I aim to create a safe, non-judgmental space where you can heal and grow at your own pace.

name: Molly Sterling (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I graduated from The Evergreen State College in 2005, with a BA in Fine Arts. I then completed a Masters in Art Therapy Counseling from Marylhurst University in 2011. I have experience in a variety of clinical settings, including residential/subacute care, inpatient psychiatric hospitals, community mental health, and outpatient therapy. I have been a Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC) in the state of Oregon since 2015. Along the way, I have discovered a passion for working with trauma, including complex/developmental trauma and how early attachment patterns continue to show up in our relationships as adults. I would describe my therapeutic style as relational and eclectic, meaning I don’t adhere to one modality exclusively. I am trained in Somatic and Attachment Focused (SAFE) EMDR and also utilize modalities such as IFS/Parts Work, Mindfulness, Compassion-Focused Therapy, and expressive arts in my work with clients.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in the power of connection and curiosity, and I strive to meet clients with compassion, wherever they are on their healing journeys. I am committed to providing therapy that is collaborative, responsive, and affirming. Though I do not identify as part of the LGBTQIA+ community, I am an ally and am committed to understanding dynamics of privilege and oppression. I am neurodivergent affirming and Health at Every Size informed. Outside of work, I enjoy spending time with friends and family, including my partner, son, and our two cats, Ollie and Vesper. I am also involved in my community, helping organize neighborhood events and volunteering at my son’s school. I am Oregon born and raised, and am continuously grateful to call this beautiful place my home!

name: Donna Prinzmetal (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am warm, respectful and attentive, with a good sense of humor. I am a poet and believe in the healing power of creative expression. I use an eclectic approach and sometimes include writing and other creative outlets in my practice to further healing. My goal is for clients to feel heard and empowered.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I earned my Masters in Psychology at Antioch University in Los Angeles in 1983. I became licensed in California in 1985 as a Marriage and Family Therapist. I moved to Oregon in 1994 and got licensed here. For over thirty years in private practice, I have worked with adults, adolescents, couples and families to provide a safe place to explore the possibilities for healing. I treat a wide range of issues and have a number of tools in my therapeutic toolbox. As a writer and a teacher, I understand that healing can come in many forms. Sometimes I incorporate creative visualization, writing and other imaginative processes to facilitate awareness and transformation. I have felt privileged to witness my clients make positive and dramatic changes in careers, relationships, sense of self and personal empowerment as they have emerged from some very dark places. Huge changes can begin with one small step. I believe that growth and healthy connections are possible for everyone.

name: Natalie (Nat) Day (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Nat Day, LCSW and graduated from Boise State University with a Masters in Social Work in 2018. Over the last decade she has worked in a variety of settings: residential treatment, on-campus college counseling, substance abuse treatment, inpatient psychiatric care, and community mental health. Nat has earned a certification in treating trauma (Certified Clinical Trauma Professional Level II), 20+ hours of training from the Be Body Positive Institute, sex and couples counseling, and training in polyvagal theory and somatic approaches. She is currently enrolled in a certification program through the Center for Positive Sexuality. Specializing in the treatment of trauma, addictions, and sexuality (with special focus on kink/BDSM/sex work), her approach to therapy is eclectic, drawing on a variety of evidenced based practices, including DBT, CBT, attachment therapy, relationship trauma, existential therapy, harm reduction, and body image issues.

name: Malia Graves
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a systemic therapist, I believe our relationships, connections, and community are integral to our emotional health. When life is difficult, our relationships support and help pull us through hard times. I want to support your healing and guide you towards sustainable goals, allowing you to live to your full potential. If you’ve arrived to my page, then you’re in the right place so let me help you take the first steps towards growth!
Why I Became a Therapist
Becoming a therapist has been a fulfilling path, and like many others it began with a deep curiosity to explore our human experience. After changing my major multiple times, my education shifted towards music therapy - and while music is still a big part of my life, I found myself wanting to spend more time connecting with my clients and hearing their stories, eventually leading me into psychology and talk therapy. I continued my education at Antioch University in Seattle, a school with over 170 years of social-justice education and advocacy where I earned a masters in couple and family therapy. Pulling from both my education and personal experiences I strive to bring elements of humanity, egality, and authenticity to my practice. I approach treatment with a systemic perspective giving a holistic view of who we are, our cultures, attachments, values, relationships, and experiences.

name: Don Marr (HE/HIM)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I help adults navigate anxiety, depression, trauma, grief, and self-doubt using Accelerated Resolution Therapy (ART)—a gentle, powerful method that doesn’t require reliving painful memories in detail. I also support highly sensitive people, deep feelers, and those stuck in perfectionism, people-pleasing, or rumination. Many of my clients are managing life transitions, strained family ties, or long-standing emotional wounds. I bring warmth, creativity, and structure to sessions, helping clients reconnect with themselves and move forward. I am in network with Moda Health, Aetna, Cigna, Regence BlueCross BlueShield of Oregon, Carelon Behavioral Health, and Anthem EAP (Bank of America).
My Values as a Therapist
As a therapist, I value authenticity, creativity, and compassion. I believe healing happens when we feel truly seen and supported without judgment. My work is grounded in deep respect for each person’s inner wisdom and capacity for change. I aim to create a space that’s safe, collaborative, and gently challenging—where growth can happen at your pace. I also believe in the power of imagination and somatic awareness, especially when words fall short. Whether we’re using structured approaches like ART or simply exploring what’s coming up, I stay attuned to your rhythm and needs. I see therapy as a kind of improvisation—where we listen, adjust, and create together. Above all, I want you to walk away with greater self-trust, clarity, and freedom to be who you truly are.

name: Margot Standeven (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Being resilient allows you to recover and adapt to difficult experiences. Instead of being overwhelmed, the resilient person is able to manage their strong feelings and impulses and communicate their concerns and needs. Resilience is not a trait that you either have or do not have. It involves behaviors, thoughts, and emotions that can be learned and developed in all of us. Couples who are resilient are open, attuned, and responsive to each other. They are able to recognize their deepest needs and longings, and risk reaching out to their partner to communicate and connect. Most often, couples who develop an enhanced emotional connection also experience greater intimacy and sexual satisfaction.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received a B.Ed from McGill University in Montreal, Canada and earned my Master’s degree in Marriage, Couple, and Family Counseling at George Fox University in Portland, Oregon. After graduation, I spent five years volunteering at William Temple House before launching my private practice. I have obtained advanced training in Emotionally Focused Therapy ad have worked primarily with couples over the past decade.

name: LaShaun Garrett-Fears (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In the safety of our therapeutic space, I offer a warm, direct, and collaborative approach to help you navigate life's challenges. Specializing in aiding individuals through significant life changes, stress, anxiety, and depression. I address communication issues and guide you through the identification and expression of emotions in ways that foster positive growth and fulfillment. Together, we will actively define your goals, leveraging your strengths and insights to release self-limiting beliefs, resolve internal conflicts, and discard unhelpful behavioral patterns. This transformative journey empowers you to confidently embrace your truth. I'm a firm advocate for inclusivity, viewing it as a fundamental right. Join me in this holistic process of self-discovery and growth, where your well-being takes center stage, and your journey towards authenticity and fulfillment begins.
My Values as a Therapist
The focus of my process centers on the strengths of your personal power, truth, identity, healing, hope and transformation. In my years of practice I have learned that it is impossible to heal what you cannot /will not name, it is my passion to support others in finding, learning to trust and honor their own voices so that transformative healing can happen.

name: Brad Meadows
approaches:
My Background
Located in Portland, Oregon, I graduated with a Master of Science in Counseling, I went on to do Doctoral work at the California Institute of Integral Studies in East West Psychology and Tibetan Buddhism along with Archetypal Astrology with a diverse background ranging from 30 years of studying Tibetan Buddhism to in depth experience in different modalities of healing and philosophy. I maintain a compassionate setting for individuals to explore, understand and create meaning in their lives and heal. I utilize a framework from various domains to assist clients in making sense of their experiences in a collaborative and fruitful fashion.

name: Leslie Yeargers (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have a Master's in Applied Behavioral Science with an emphasis in Family Systems Counseling from the Leadership Institute of Seattle (LIOS) program. I also have a background as a job search coach and career counselor. I know the impact issues related to work and job-seeking can have on your self-confidence and your relationships. I apply trauma-informed, narrative family systems theory, internal family systems, emotion-focused techniques, and Gottman tools to help your intimate or family relationships feel more connected, grounded, and supportive. I have a client-centered, humanistic, collaborative, and when necessary, directive approach to support you and your loved ones on your journey to find more meaning in your lives together. I am also a certified sex informed couples therapist and am EMDRIA trained-- EMDR can be a powerful modality for resolving trauma and cognitive/emotional/behavioral patterns that impact your relationships.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe we are relational, spiritual beings in search of connection, expression, purpose and growth. As such, the quality of our relationships to our selves, partners, friends, extended family, pets, nature and work environments affect our personal identities and overall well-being. I see my role as helping people build deep connection with themselves and their loved ones to more deeply express their uniqueness and purpose in the world. As a cisgender, heterosexual provider with LGBTQIA friends and family, I want you and your family to know you are not alone in your experience of living with identities outside of societal norms. As a family systems counselor, I want to do what I can to help you and your family repair, and then build, your connection so that you can live authentically with mutual love and support.

name: Peggy Russell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
As a therapist and a human, I believe that being seen and heard in the caring non-judgmental presence of another creates an environment where deep healing can occur. I practice from a mind-body holism approach, meaning I honor the inseparable union of mind and body. Many of my clients benefit from somatic healing where years of previous talk therapy has only touched the surface. Together we will navigate a path towards understanding the natural somatic and emotional responses associated with difficult life events. I believe we all deserve to feel comfortable in our own skin and at home in our own life as we tend to our basic needs for love, acceptance, connection and safety. I hold a Master’s Degree in Counseling from Oregon State University, and have various continuing education coursework in trauma informed care, adverse childhood experiences, inner child work, grief and loss, somatic work. Currently I am working towards becoming a Certified Hakomi Therapist.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My own lived experience of grief, loss, intimate partner violence, and challenging life transitions found me at a breaking point. From this place I discovered the healing properties of therapy and somatic work. Through yoga, meditation, somatic experiencing, and being deeply heard by another, I began to grow and heal, turning pain into purpose I followed this passion into the field of counseling. I am grateful to pursue what I truly believe is my life’s purpose, and I am so glad you took this step to explore the gift of therapy. I am humbled and honored to share in your journey.

name: JESSICA VAN DER MERWE (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Therapy can feel heavy, but it doesn’t always have to. I use humor, creativity, and collaborate with you to achieve your goals. My goal is to establish an emotionally safe and a non-judgemental place for you to feel unburdened by expectations and get in touch with your most authentic self. Our work together will focus holistically on the body, mind, spirit, and cultural responses to stress to map a way toward healing. We might even laugh along the way. I am a certified Brainspotting therapist, which is a brain and body based approach to healing using points of vision to access and process and resolve "stuck" memories and persistent challenges to health. I am a certified in Trauma-Informed Stabilization Therapy (TIST), which uses "parts" work, somatic (body) responses, and mindfulness to reduce shame, excessive guilt, and reduce trauma-related responses that keep us stuck in unhelpful patterns.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe no matter what you face, or how long you've faced it, there is hope for change. I've come from my own struggles throughout childhood into adult life, faced eating disorders, low self esteem, panic and trauma responses, and lots of grief. In short, I'm not too different from you. There is a always an option for healing and change, we sometimes just need help finding it. I believe that everyone, no matter race, gender identity, religious beliefs, creed, or economic status deserve the best mental health care possible (in addition to any health care and human rights)! That means a couple things: I keep myself honest with ongoing cultural and ethical education and personal growth, as well as work to accommodate whatever financial challenge you may face in order to get the services you deserve. If we aren't a match, I will help you find someone who is. If you think this sounds good, send me a message and we can connect.

name: Joshua Bradshaw (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
The relationship we build as teammates through the process of therapy can establish trust and understanding that translates towards positive outcomes in your lived experience. I work to enhance that relationship through compassionate listening, validation, and encouragement through exploring the person within. As your therapist, I will create a space that feels safe, which will allow the journey of understanding your relationships and sexuality to feel protected. Our work together will allow you to be confronted with the difficult questions and experiences life has presented while navigating these with a therapist who looks to create wholeness with their client. Throughout our time together, you will feel accepted, appreciated, challenged, and validated. This journey will enhance your understanding of yourself, your relationships, and the intersectionality of who you are. I look forward to working with you.

name: Mattie Boucher (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is deeply relational and collaborative with a focus on attachment. I feel a safe and strong therapeutic relationship is the most important element of therapy to create an environment that makes growth possible. I currently use various interventions and techniques from different theories depending on what the client’s goals are in order to be client-centered, including DBT, CBT, EMDR, Ego State Therapy, Attachment theory and Polyvagal theory.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Many of the things we work on in the therapy room are directly related to the impacts of the external systems we are a part of, such as white supremacy and capitalism. For this reason I strongly believe an abolitionist, anti-racist and health at every size perspective is important to move toward healing and liberation in this work and aligns with my values outside of the therapy room as well. When I am not in session with clients I can be found puzzling, tinkering with my plants, reading, playing basketball or spending time with my senior dog on a very slow walk.

name: Heidi Savell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I will work with you at your pace to help you identify the goals most important to you, while also supporting you in letting go of whatever may be getting in your way. I find it can be most effective to focus on your areas of strength as well as areas where you are experiencing problems, as I believe your already existing strengths and skills can be used to help you bring your life more in line with how you would like it to be. My training and skills include a blend of attachment theory, compassion-focused therapy, non-violent communication, and cognitive behavioral therapy. I also incorporate parts work/Internal Family Systems.
More About the People I Treat
I provide counseling to those who: *Want to find better ways to cope with stress and anxiety *Are in a relationship that needs support around communication, connecting, and managing conflict *Identify as polyamorous or non-monogamous and are wanting experienced, competent care and support *Are dealing with low self-worth, perfectionism, or self-criticism *Are experiencing challenges with personal or professional relationships *Have experienced a life change and want to clarify how to handle it *Want to improve relationships with family members or other loved ones

name: Suzanne Sanchez (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I believe we all have the power to change habits and feelings through increasing our self-awareness. We often know what it is we need, but we need help establishing commitment to change. I believe all people are good, we just need help to see the good inside. Most importantly, I believe as a therapist, I am responsible for seeing a person’s entire environment as perpetuating a problem rather than a person wholly being at fault. I help my clients examine how their mental health and behavioral issues are affected on the different systemic levels of their life. I have much diverse experience as a therapist and have worked in residential settings, outpatient settings, schools, hospitals, and day treatment programs. I have a M.A. in counseling psychology from Lewis and Clark College, Portland (2007), and I have a B.A. in both psychology and in sociology from the University of Minnesota, Twin Cities (2000).
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I use a Health at Every Size (HAES) approach in counseling and am firmly anti-diet culture. I’ve served on boards focused on supporting individuals and families affected by eating disorders, and I teach an annual course on eating disorders in the certification program at Lewis & Clark College Graduate School, where I’m adjunct faculty. Cultural identity is also deeply important to me. I’m half-Asian and half-white (mostly Finnish), and I understand the experience of moving through the world as a multiracial person and a person of color in the Portland-metro area. I love working with cultural identity in all its forms, and I believe everyone—yes, even white folks—has culture that shapes their emotional health. I’m also a spiritual person in the sense that I believe we’re all connected. Practices like gratitude and meditation are key to mental and emotional balance, and I enjoy helping people find what that looks like for them.

name: Tanna TenHoopen Dolinsky
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My approach to therapy can be described as strength-based and focused. I draw from a foundational education in CBT and psychoanalysis, with the addition influence of Internal Family Systems, NVC, somatic experiencing, narrative therapy, mindfulness practices, and ACT. I view my role in our work together as a fellow traveler; it is an honor to be given the opportunity to earn your trust in our work together. I am committed to offer you warmth, kindness, and deep listening while respecting your own unique experiences and perspective; you are your own best resource. I aim to establish safety within our therapeutic relationship, set clear expectations, and collaboratively seek solutions as we approach your individual therapy goals.

name: Ivy Katz (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
I hold a masters degree in Counseling Psychology from Lewis & Clark College as well as certificate in Ecopsychology. This framework speaks to the recognition of the connections between the mental health of the client with the overall health of the culture and the planet. Along with this I have completed professional training from M.E.T.A in Hakomi Body Centered Psychotherapy, EMDR and Equine assisted psychotherapy through EAGALA certification and Natural Lifemanships Trauma focused equine assisted psychotherapy. I have worked in the mental health field since 2007, including wilderness therapy, residential treatment, private practice and outpatient counseling clinics. Along with my experience in counseling I am a certified yoga teacher and have traveled the country teaching trauma informed yoga trainings for many years. I received my bachelor’s degree from Prescott College in human development as well as dance.
My Approach to Helping
My approach is mindfulness-based, and experiential, meaning that I will help you deepen your awareness of the specific ways you experience yourself, others, and the world around you. I will guide and support you in cultivating compassion and acceptance towards yourself and others, so that you feel empowered to make the positive changes you desire in your life. If you are interested in becoming a client, please fill out the prescreening form that comes directly from the website of where I work : The Center at Heron Hill https://www.cognitoforms.com/TheCenterAtHeronHill/PrescreeningFormForNewOrReturningClientsV2022

name: Ann Krueger (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am trained in the Hakomi method, as well as in energetic movement in the body as understood through east asian medicine. I utilize an attachment and trauma-informed lens. A deep respect for the intelligence of nature is woven into my work. I offer Mindful Compassionate Dialogue skills, and offer space and support for psychedelic integration. I believe in equity, diversity, fairness, justice, respect, and teaching history that includes references to racism, sexism, gender and sexual preference discrimination, and education that aims to establish a more just and compassionate society. You are welcome here.
My Values as a Therapist
My values as a therapist include supporting your agency, including respecting your body's pace in healing. I bring and encourage authenticity, not at the expense of respect or safety. I value consistency and stability of care, and a commitment to your wellbeing. I am dedicated to the work of mindful somatic therapy - as it shifts our state, and shifts the story - this allows us to make different decisions that can change a life. I hope you'll discover a greater sense of ease through our work.

name: Neil Panchmatia (HE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Like acorns destined to become mighty oaks, I believe that we naturally gravitate towards more fulfilled and authentic versions of ourselves: thriving by achieving our highest potentials. I look forward to joining you on this journey by providing unconditional/unwavering support and empowerment. I am a Queer, person of color, kink/poly, and fat/body affirming, neurodiversity and disability centering intersectional feminist therapist who uses a non-hierarchal, collaborative and individually-tailored approach to therapy. I work from a place of cultural humility and an ever-improving understanding of my own intersections of privilege/marginalization. I believe that healing, growth and thriving result from a holistic integration of the mind, body and spirit. As my client, you will be in the driving seat of your therapeutic experience: You will have a say in determining the goals we work on, the pace we take, the approaches we adopt, and the outcomes we aspire towards.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I work with individuals and couples/unions from all sociocultural backgrounds. I specialize in therapy for: anxiety/panic, depression, post-trauma recovery (C-PTSD), body image issues/eating disorders (HAES), living with marginalization/oppression, learning allyship for marginalized groups, and living with disabilities/ chronic pain or illness. I graduated from Portland State University with a Masters in Counseling, and have accrued intensive experience working in different clinical settings over the last few years. I am multilingual (fluent in English, Gujarati, Hindi, Swahili, and Punjabi), and as a therapist, I draw heavily from my own experience as a transnational citizen: believing fully in the interconnected nature of our human experience. My theoretical orientation as a clinician is an integration of existential therapy, Internal Family Systems (IFS), and Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT).

name: Eric Abelow Blakeley (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Treatment is a collaborative endeavor in which emotional distress is increasingly captured in new understandings rather than in symptoms and in repetitive, problematic patterns of behavior. The work seeks to create new understandings that lead to emotional shifts that allow for new ways of relating to yourself and others.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I specialize in treating issues relating to adult children of narcissistic parents, separation issues, anxiety, grief and loss. I listen with an ear to uncover unconscious patterns that tend to repeat and create limitations, frustrations, and symptoms.

name: Caitlin Truitt (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Our bodies and minds are incredibly resourceful at adapting to our environment. When that environment is dangerous, our internal system creates powerful strategies to help us survive. Much of trauma work is about convincing our bodies that it's safe to let go of the survival strategies that are no longer helping us. When we feel safe enough to emerge from survival mode, we can uncover a stunning inner vibrancy and creativity that we never knew we had. My approach focuses on healing trauma using a somatic, relationship-based perspective. In session, I draw on attachment-based/psychodynamic therapies, somatic therapies, mindfulness, internal family systems, and nervous system regulation techniques. I am trained in somatic- and attachment-focused EMDR, which can be applied to childhood or adult trauma. In couples therapy, I also integrate concepts from the Gottman Method, as well as Relational Life Therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Because of the ways systems of oppression proliferate trauma, trauma therapy and anti-oppressive practice are one and the same. I am committed to keeping this idea at the heart of my work as a therapist. Working with LGBTQ folks in a variety of community-based and professional roles has been a meaningful part of my life for the last decade. My practice welcomes and affirms individuals of all sexual orientations, gender identities and representations, body sizes and types, cultural backgrounds, races, ethnicities, religions, spiritualities, and documentation statuses. I am affirming of ENM relationship structures and consensual nontraditional sexual practices.

name: Nicole Bongard (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I hope to help clients: -Build a strong therapeutic alliance to foster growth in relationships -Work through past traumas to heal the mind-body connection -Use mindfulness and grounding techniques to connect with the body -Identify inner strengths to empower and improve one's self-esteem
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Human beings drive connection and connection drives us to better understand ourselves. What I love about counseling is how it offers everyone a safe space to explore themselves, their relationships, and the world around them. This is so important to developing, growing, and becoming the best version of yourself possible.

name: Michael Unger (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have twenty years of experience as a professional counselor. Before starting a private practice I provided mental health counseling in university counseling centers, outpatient mental health clinics and health care clinics. I’m fortunate that my foundational clinical experiences were based in New York City. Due to the diversity of the population, I gained a great amount of experience counseling clients from a wide range of backgrounds. Clients that tend to benefit the most from my approach often struggle with excessive worry, rumination, self-judgment, irritability, low mood/motivation as well as increased stress due to relationship, work, academic or health related concerns. I utilize approaches such as Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy and Acceptance and Commitment Therapy that promote increased self-awareness and self-acceptance. Clients often feel more connected with their own strengths and empowered to cope with stressors through our work together.
My Values as a Therapist
Helping others and embracing diversity are core personal and professional values. I was fortunate to have been raised in a diverse environment in New York City and learned to recognize the strengths in differences. My grandparents participated in the civil rights movement and often cared for those in need. I knew that counseling was my career path soon after I started to take psychology courses in college. When I realized that I could make a living by actively listening, empathizing and utilizing counseling approaches to help others, I didn’t think twice.

name: Rebecca Youssef (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
I received my doctorate in clinical psychology from Midwestern University and completed my undergraduate education at Loyola University Chicago. I preach, teach, and practice mindfulness and valued living to help clients live a life of meaning despite dealing with physical and emotional distress. My work is grounded in Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT). I also incorporate solution focused counseling, motivational interviewing, mind/body work, and humor into the work I do to help clients improve their mood, functioning, quality of life, and overall health. Whether you’re looking for help with your mental health, your relationships, cultivating a sense of well-being, or you’re looking to feel more connected to yourself and present in your life, my intention is to show up each and every day with empathy, compassion, and authenticity to empower you to live a valued life of your own.
My Values as a Therapist
My approach to therapy is based in empathy, collaboration, authenticity, and compassion. I strive to create a safe space for you to explore where you are at in life, where you want to go, and how we can get you there together. My goals are to empower you to make changes based on your personal values and what you’re wanting for yourself, to help you connect to the things that are most important to you at this time in your life, and to walk alongside you (and cheer you on!) as you realize your own strengths. My passion is helping others live a life that is meaningful, purposeful, joyful, and fulfilling. This can oftentimes feel difficult when life throws pain and suffering our way, when we feel we have little control amidst chaos and stress, or when we feel stuck in routines and habits that are no longer working for us. My goal is to help you clarify what you’re wanting for yourself so you can make choices aligned with your values and live life YOUR way.

name: Emily Walker (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I utilize a relational approach with a creative flair; each session is catered to fit the needs of the individual. I am passionate about treating disordered eating and eating disorders, supporting people through life transitions and navigating the difficult choices that happen at critical periods of development. My use of art therapy in combination with traditional talk therapy allows clients to connect with themselves, their bodies, thoughts, emotions, and relationships at a deeper level without the limitations of words. Art therapy is accessible to anyone, not just an “artist”, as the purpose behind the art making is the process not the final product.
My Values as a Therapist
I offer deep and personal awareness of LGBTQ+ concerns and hold an anti-oppressive lens. I also work from a Health At Every Size approach and aim towards dismantling diet-culture narratives that are harmful and deeply internalized. We all deserve a safe space to process, explore, and challenge the impact of systemic beliefs while working towards self-acceptance and compassion.

name: Lauren Saville (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As each client is unique, I use a variety of skills and techniques to meet my clients' needs in the way that feels comfortable and empowering for them. My tendency is towards experiential methods, which can include incorporating somatic work, exploring different parts of self, and expressive arts therapies in addition to traditional talk therapy. My theoretical orientation draws largely from Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, Internal Family Systems, somatic experiencing, and anti-oppressive counseling. I have specialized training in trauma, sex therapy, and expressive arts therapies, I also offer ketamine assisted psychotherapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am an active member in queer communities, kink communities, and polyamorous communities. I am involved in work supporting transgender rights. I am also a part of the theatre community, focusing on uplifting/telling stories through the lens of trans, queer, female, fat, disabled, older, and BIPOC actors, and exploring issues of oppression with young people. As a university professor and community educator, I work to train and equip counselors in better serving diverse communities.

name: Demir Crone (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work at Bridges to Well-Being. I have done training in Emotional Focused Therapy for Couples. I am currently doing trainings in ISTDP for Couples, ATSIP (sex therapy), and in the Developmental Model. With individuals, I draw primarily on Intensive Short Term Dynamic Psychotherapy (ISTDP)- a powerful psychodynamic approach designed to create lasting change. Fun fact: ISTDP is supported by over 140 and counting high quality studies. My primary modality has been shown repeatedly to help everything from trauma to treatment resistant depression to mood disorders.

name: Grace Silvia, LCSW (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I help you become allies with your unconscious. I will support you to listen to, have compassion for, and honor what your symptoms and problems are trying to tell you.
