There are gifts in your challenges, and together we will find them and bring the exiled parts of yourself home. This work is creative, sacred, and changes your life from the inside out. There is never forcing an agenda or pace, or pushing past "no." Our deepest selves want to grow and heal--we just need to re-learn how to follow that impulse.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I trained 2-1/2 years through the Hakomi Institute in body-centered, experiential, mindfulness-based psychotherapy. Eduardo Duran's work inspires me--he's a Native American psychologist who validates historical trauma and spiritual healing. My work is also informed by having experienced years of deep depression and a time of "cracking open," and through both was able to find profound meaning. I support people healing through grief; depression; hearing voices; anxiety; mania/spiritual emergency; childhood sexual abuse; acute and chronic experiences of oppression; historical trauma; activist burnout; compulsive eating; and people wanting to reduce or stop taking psychiatric medications. Special sensitivity to Jewish, women's and LGBTTSAAQQ+ (Lesbian/ Gay/ Bisexual/ Trans/ Two Spirit/ Androgynous/ Asexual/ Queer/ Questioning) identity and oppression.

name: Jessi Huffman (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I come to this work with great optimism in the strength and goodness of humans, but not without an acknowledgement of the oppressive systems that create barriers to getting what we need, and which affect many populations disproportionately. With this awareness, I am oriented towards your internal resources and innate power to heal, and my work is built around encouraging those personal insights necessary for growth to happen. I work to create space that welcomes all emotions, encourages deep insight and self-compassion, and is curious about what unconscious dynamics may be shifted in order for transformation to occur. I do this through a psychodynamic orientation, which is focused on the unconscious processes that affect and guide our behavior. Using the wisdom of the body as a guide, we gently explore any areas that cause discomfort and pain, which often helps us better understand our behavior, thoughts, and feelings and ultimately integrate our understanding into a more whole self.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I find that we learn the most through our experiences, especially those that require tenacity and endurance to move through. My own healing work, which has included not only therapy but many other transformative practices, is ongoing and will never be finished. This work allows me to appreciate the therapy I provide not as an end to healing, but as one avenue along a journey with many potential roads. Therapy can be helpful in getting us unstuck and providing a new perspective, but we are whole beings who require social connection and support, causes we are passionate about, and a connection to that thread of "why are we here?" Supporting others is part of my "why", as is being present with and honoring myself and the humans in my life, caring for and spending time in nature, and creation in many forms (music, painting, clay, writing).

name: Ryan Dawson (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker (LCSW) based in Portland Oregon and have thirteen years experience working with people who struggle with symptoms of mental illness, alcohol and drug use, family and relationship troubles, chronic illness, complications of grief & loss, and other life challenging situations. I have a Masters of Social Work with a Minor in Mental Health & Chemical Dependency from Eastern Michigan University with expertise using Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavioral Therapy (DBT), Mindfulness practice and Motivational Interviewing (MI) to help clients work through difficult issues and to produce change. When working with clients, I believe it’s essential to develop a solid connection using a person-centered approach, meeting someone where they’re at, and providing a compassionate, honest and safe place to discuss what’s going on in their life.
Why I Became a Therapist
I was introduced to a counselor when I was in high school that impacted my life and helped me get through a very difficult time. Learning the attributes of healthy relationships, developing coping skills, and learning how to evaluate my decisions dynamically affected the course of my life and eventually led me to pursue a career helping others.

name: Sheri Bansemer (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I approach counseling with the belief that you are a whole person with unique experiences, strengths, and challenges. I trust in your intrinsic wisdom and will support you as you gain a deeper understanding of yourself and the systems in which we live. We will work together to name patterns that are helpful and unhelpful and work toward creating healthy interactions so you feel confident navigating life with insight and autonomy. In my work with individuals, couples, families, children, and adolescents, I aim to create a safe space where everyone feels heard and where authentic communication is valued. Cultivating trust, compassion, and respect and ensuring you feel seen, heard, and validated is of high priority to me. I invite you to approach this path with curiosity and courage. My commitment is to accompany you with empathy, respect, and steadfast support as you navigate life's complexities and celebrate the fullness of your being.

name: McKenzie Brock
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In my work I embrace a strengths-based, relational approach, incorporating trauma-informed care, utilizing an attachment lens, while working with individuals and family systems. I am guided by mindfulness-based and somatic practices, feminist multicultural therapy, Accelerate Experiential-Dynamic Psychotherapy, Internal Family Systems, and also offer EMDR and EMDR Flash. I believe that we all hold immense wisdom from our stories. It is my joy to partner alongside people as they rediscover and reclaim their power.
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a therapist because of the impact of my own healing journey. I believe that all therapists should be in therapy, doing their own work. The systems we live and work within are inherently traumatizing and we ALL deserve access to support and healing. As a member of the LGBTQIA+ community, it is my goal to bring more accessible service to our community.

name: Nina Landey
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I integrate mindful, experiential techniques into my work from attachment therapy, EMDR, and Hakomi. I believe all people come into the world with potential to be their best, authentic selves. We then have experiences from which we develop beliefs about the world and strategies for how to navigate through it. These become a template for living our lives and we often apply them to situations and relationships where they don't work. Together, we will work to reveal deeply held beliefs that may be blocking you. In my view, these beliefs solidified in response to emotional wounding in childhood, or to trauma. In a safe environment, I will provide support, empathy, and understanding. Our relationship can be a foundation for safe exploration and evolution. Together, we may be able to fill in missing experiences or reprocess traumas that have formed limiting beliefs. With experience and support, you can reset your patterns and experience more fulfilling ways of interacting with the world.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I earned an M.S from Portland State University, where I focused on marital, couples and family therapy. I have had professional training in the Hakomi Method, RC-S, and Attachment from M.E.T.A., where I also completed a year-long internship. I have completed full basic training in EMDR, along with Flash Technique and Attachment Focused EMDR. I also have training in grief counseling from The Dougy Center for Grieving Children and Families, where I was a volunteer facilitator. Before this, I earned a B.A. in English-Theatre from Barnard College and an M.F.A from the Yale School of Drama. I am a Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC).

name: Angelica Sanchez
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My style is non-judgmental and honoring of both individuality and cultural diversity. I take into consideration that we each have a unique story and path that has led us to where we are today. I maintain a strong belief in my client's inherent ability to move toward growth as we work collaboratively towards their goals. My therapeutic approach is integrative, as I adapt techniques from various evidence-based treatment methods including but not limited to: Cognitive-Behavioral, Solution-Focused Therapy, Narrative Therapy, Family Systems Therapy, and Gottman Method Couples Therapy. Sessions can be held in Spanish and English.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I hold a Master of Social Work from Portland State University. I have completed Level 1 Clinical Training of Gottman Method Couples Therapy and hold an Addictions therapy certification from PSU. Prior to private practice I worked as a therapist in a community based setting for over 5 years. I am trained and experienced in areas of anxiety, depression, trauma, addictions, life transitions, and relationships. Sliding scale fees are also available.

name: Jill Walsh (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to therapy is one of collaboration. I believe that clients are in the most successful position for positive and lasting change when they are effectively reinforced by an ally throughout their process. To achieve this transformation, I focus mainly on individual work by utilizing Strengths-Based Narratives, Cognitive-Behavioral modalities, Internal Family Systems techniques and Art Therapy. At all times, I strive to provide client-driven services with an emphasis on encouraging feedback regarding all aspects of my treatment approach. During sessions, I aim to create connection through open communication, warmth, laughter and authenticity.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a clinician, I derive my philosophy of care from a deep value of service, honoring diversity, empowerment, social justice, ethical practice and the importance of interpersonal relationships. Through years of work in community mental health, I've learned to understand people, families and the systems that impact the human experience.

name: Sydney Francis (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I utilize an internal family systems (IFS) and narrative approach, and strive to create a warm, safe, and collaborative environment.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that we are constantly evolving, and often the pain we experience can mirror the depth of joy and love that we are capable of. I'm a big proponent of befriending our younger selves, and providing them with what they need and/or may have never received. I believe that my role as a counselor is deeply sacred and I'm honored to be a part of your journey.

name: Kayleigh Rogalski (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I'm trained as a health psychologist, and I am well-versed in treating chronic conditions like POTS, hypermobile Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (hEDS), long-COVID, and overactive bladder. I have contributed to a published article on patients' experience of living with POTS. I also offer CBT-I, the gold-standard treatment for insomnia. If you have a chronic condition or belong to a marginalized community, healthcare interactions can be traumatic, and I am particularly versed in treating medical trauma. I offer both standard EMDR and intensive EMDR for people short on time or looking for big changes in a short period. I'm also passionate about weight stigma, having published, presented, and written a dissertation on the topic. I would love to support you healing your relationship with food (e.g., binge eating) and your body.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
This world is frightening for so many of us right now. We're regularly facing existential threats that impact our safety, housing, environment, healthcare, and overall wellbeing. And yet, we still have the agency to calm our nervous systems, to connect with ourselves and others, to get better sleep, to overcome our traumas, and feel better in our bodies. I would love to help get you back to you.

name: Gina DeLeo (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that therapy is a relational process where we work toward helping you find self-compassion, growth, and healing. I am trained in a systems approach, meaning that I look at the individuals, couples, and families I work with as being part of a meaningful web of connection with others in their lives. I believe that our histories are important to our emotional work in the here and now, and that it is empowering to explore what we value to help create a roadmap to a more purposeful and wholehearted life. I work mainly from an attachment lens, using emotion-focused and experiential approaches. My style is warm, non-judgmental, and engaged. I may bring in mindfulness practices in our work together to learn feelings of safety, process feelings and events, and ease the impact that stress and trauma has on the body.
My Values as a Therapist
I take a holistic perspective, considering the multiple facets of each person and their experiences, and view each of my clients as social and political beings whose health is impacted daily by the systems in which they live and interact. I recognize that systems of oppression affect us each differently and in specific ways. I also realize that they create privilege for some while making healthcare that much harder to access for others. I welcome people of all cultures, races, sizes, abilities, religions, sexual orientations, and gender identities in my counseling practice, and I seek to create a safe space that is tailored to my clients’ individual needs.

name: Jamie Krahulec (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My therapeutic approach represents my personal philosophy that we are all connected to and shaped by one another and that we each have our own perceptions of reality based on our unique experiences and observations. I consider myself a person-centered therapist and use psychoeducation to offer you understanding how brain development nuances show up in your life. This means that I consider it my role to offer you authentic and genuine reflections, help increase your self-confidence, and help strengthen your mental capacity to handle internal and external stressors (that you have likely already been facing daily). I have a master’s degree from George Fox University and my training includes expressive art therapy, interpersonal neurobiology and psychopharmacology, trauma-informed cognitive behavior therapy, psychopathology, child/parent relationship therapy, sand tray therapy, gender-affirming play therapy, and using narrative therapy to help a person live with grief and trauma.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Being alive can feel really hard sometimes and it is easy to feel stuck in sadness. I believe that every part of you has validity and worth and that even the most unhealthy moments do not mean you are a failure or lost forever. There are no time-limits or rules when it comes to emotions, especially grief. Our brains are incredibly complex and are experts at hiding and misdirection. Sometimes our “bad” thoughts and behavior occur because our minds are trying to protect us and survive. I believe that no one is defined by their mistakes. I think people are always in the process of rediscovering themselves. Some stories are intertwined with tragedy and challenges, and I think that our time together is for you to tell yours. I consider my role as a therapist to be a person who helps you explore the different parts of yourself and helps you look towards the future with purpose. I want to sit beside you, listen, and hopefully offer clarity/relief. I want you to know that YOU ARE ENOUGH.

name: Rachael Patoray (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC), and Registered Art Therapist (ATR),. I help clients identify and focus on their strengths using an IFS-informed Parts Perspective, Trauma-Informed Relational Therapy, Neurobiology Psychoeducation, and Person-Centered Approach. I employ the mind-body connection, apply Sensorimotor Psychotherapy, teach mindfulness techniques, review communication, address mood diagnosis, and use Art Therapy & Narrative Therapy. I provide a safe space to be heard without judgement and provide tools to make changes and to help folks get "unstuck". I have the pleasure of working with folks whose autoimmune systems have been affected or compromised, who are also struggling with physical illness, because of their trauma histories. I help clients understand the important connection between the body and the mind. I help my clients set down self-blame and help them focus on healing through self compassion and continued curiosity.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I'm a native Oregonian (though I've lived in many places, big & small, throughout the USA) but I'm happy to be back "home". I realized through my own journey, that art not only allowed me to express myself, but it allowed me to understand myself a little better too! I use art (as one modality) in my practice to help you do the same; to explore, understand, and heal. I do not expect artistic perfection, that is not what art therapy is about! It's about providing you another way to understand and express who you are, at any age! I'm also an animal lover and love witnessing my client's pets support them during processing work! I often encourage my clients to have their pets join them during sessions, for additional support! I received my Masters in Art Therapy/Counseling at Southwestern College in Santa Fe, NM. I'm a registered art therapist (ATR) and a licensed professional counselor (LPC).

name: Nuria Gomez
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
MY PRACTICE AND WAITLIST ARE CURRENTLY FULL. We'll work together to look at how your life and environment contribute to your mental and physical well-being, build on your strengths, and incorporate new tools. I've worked with people in some of their hardest moments to find their way through with balance, peace and resilience.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I’ve spent the last fifteen years getting to know bodies by trying on various professional and personal lenses including public health service, massage therapy, functional nutrition treatment, organic farming, outdoor education, myofascial release, meditation and mindfulness techniques, and body-oriented trauma treatment. I work with individual adults and adolescents as a Licensed Marriage and Family Therapist in Portland, Oregon. I completed a Master’s in Counseling Psychology with a specialization in Somatic Psychology from California Institute of Integral Studies in 2012. I came home to the Pacific Northwest so that I could do the work I love, providing counseling to people with healing bodies.

name: Crystal Covelle (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
My roles in the community have ranged from individual therapy, crisis line counselor, shelter worker, and support group facilitator, to yoga instructor and studio owner, as well as candlemaker and event organizer. \\\ I'm a Clinical Social Worker Associate (CSWA). I received my Master of Social Work (MSW) from LSU. I am honored and grateful to support folks on their path. /// In my free time, I enjoy living room dance parties with my partner and our five year old kiddo.

name: Katie Solomon (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a trauma-informed, somatic, and relational therapist, I approach therapy with a lot of curiosity, gentleness, and eagerness to explore what is happening for clients in the present moment. I often encourage clients to check in with the sensations in their bodies and may highlight patterns I notice. I strive to create a non-judgmental space where clients can access and explore their emotions and experiences, enabling a deeper connection to themselves and others. I also like to bring (appropriate) humor into sessions. I believe that building a strong therapeutic relationship is a key part of the process! I draw from Gestalt therapy, attachment theory, AEDP, and Internal Family Systems. I have a non-pathologizing approach rooted in the belief that capitalism, white supremacy, patriarchy, homophobia, transphobia, and other systems of oppression all impact our mental health.

name: Alana Harris
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe suffering is an aspect of human nature and everyone has the innate ability to tolerate and grow through difficult times, to find meaning and purpose in life. Sometimes we have to enter the paradox of pain to get relief from the pain itself. I have 8 years of experience in community clinics and have been intensively trained in mindfulness based stress reduction, CBT and DBT evidenced based treatment modalities. Prior to opening my own practice I was a DBT therapist for 3 years. Although I weave DBT skills into my practice, I value process based approaches as well as mindfulness and self-compassion work. My goal is to help plant seeds of self-awareness to propel perspective, introspection, agency and self-fulfillment. Every individual is unique and I tend to tailor client-centered relational and behavioral skill building techniques. With my approach I tend to work best with folks who are motivated and may want to do intentional work.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Human behavior is a fascinating thing. We hold a lot of truths about ourselves, sometimes opposing truths that coexist. We also have an innate ability to cultivate resiliency and develop new neural circuits in our brain chemistry. We can rewire faulty thinking patterns that for so long have influenced our sense of self and the ways we experience relationships and the world around us. If this feels like a journey you'd like to explore, feel free to reach out. A little about me, I live in SE Portland with my partner and our pup Doc. In my free time I enjoy gardening and throwing a random array of wildflower seeds all over the yard, camping, biking, climbing, skiing and all the outdoor pursuits the PNW has to offer. Music has saved me many of times so you may find me bouncing around to shows and concerts. https://www.inwardcounselingllc.com/

name: Alexandra Leichter LCSW, LICSW, MSW (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am eclectic in my approach - I am Gottman Level One certified and PrePare Enrich certified (premarital services). I am a Certified ND Compass therapist - this is specific to those couples who are neurodiverse. I am also an ADHD Certified Clinical Services Provider. My main focus is helping people tell their truth, what it is they are scared to admit to themselves and others, in a safe, supportive way.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker in the states of Oregon and California and a Licensed Independent Clinical Social Worker in the state of Washington. I have been in couples counseling myself and this is why I am passionate about what I do: I know it works. Call me, I offer free phone consultations.

name: Julian Morris (HE/ THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I specialize in working with clients who have big questions about life. Spiritual and existential quandaries welcome! I use a Gestalt and IFS framework that focuses on you becoming your own deepest resource. With couples I use this framework to peel back the layers of conflict to better understand each person's ground. The therapeutic goal is a stronger understanding of Self that brings a deeper connection to the experience of being alive. I use a dialogic approach in my work, which means I am listening to your language and the meaning that you make out of life. Together, we help bring unconscious beliefs, patterns, and habitual thoughts to the forefront to examine. This gives individuals and couples an increased awareness and an opportunity to make great change.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that every person is capable of growth and healing. The therapeutic process is much like fine tuning a radio. Our work is to lessen the static until core needs and emotions are clear enough to hear. As a therapist, I am a visitor in the overall journey of your healing. As such, I can provide a non-judgmental, compassionate, and caring environment for you to come into deeper relationship with yourself. Ultimately, I believe we are all equipped to be our own best healers. Therapy is a place to uncover this deep inner potential, as well as to grieve, to question, to explore, and ultimately to grow.

name: Lisa Sloan Strom (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work from a hybrid model, meaning I offer counseling sessions either in person, in the comfort of my well appointed SW Portland office (with plenty of free parking), or through the convenience of telehealth, through a secure video platform. In person or by telehealth. Your choice. My style is integrative, meaning that I utilize techniques tailored to fit your individual needs. My counseling approach utilizes cognitive behavioral, attachment, poly vagal theory, narrative, person centered, psycho-dynamic, mindfulness, neuroscience, and depth techniques, which include exploring dreams, metaphor and movement.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I've been around the block and have walked the walk. I have extensive training and the tools and skills for supporting your deep and long-lasting, sustained recovery and personal growth. My office is set up to meet you safely, with HIPAA compliant online counseling services. I am a Licensed Professional Counselor and Certified Alcohol and Drug Counselor. My Masters Degree in Counseling Psychology is from Pacifica Graduate Institute, with an emphasis in Depth Psychology. Give me a call, and let's chat to see if we're a good fit.

name: Jenn Garlock (SHE/HER)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I specialize in working with folks who have experienced trauma by the hands of another person, including sexual assault or abuse, physical abuse or neglect, emotional abuse or neglect, and verbal abuse. Many of my clients have experienced multiple incidents of trauma, of various types, by various people. It can be so difficult to trust others after being hurt by people in your life. I know that putting your trust in a therapist is no different, and I don't take that responsibility lightly. Trauma therapy is tough work, and it happens best in the context of a safe and supportive relationship between therapist and client. I am here to hold the unspeakable things with you so that you don't have to do this by yourself anymore. Together, we will work to build a trusting and authentic relationship where you can feel safe enough to explore what happened in your life, the ways you have survived, and how to get unstuck and move toward a life that is ruled by you, not the trauma.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a survivor of trauma myself, I am intimately familiar with the powerful impact therapy can have on a person’s life. I know what it is like to put your trust in a professional during your most raw and painful moments, and I treat this responsibility with a great deal of care. It is this personal experience that led me to focus my therapeutic work on helping others who have experienced trauma. You deserve to be heard, respected, and embraced for who you are, and your story deserves to be told. I truly love my work as a therapist, and I feel so fortunate to get to do this for a living. It is a privilege to walk alongside my clients as they take back their power from the memories that made them feel powerless. When I'm not with my clients, you can find me exploring the outdoors with my dog, Wally, curled up on the couch reading a good book on a rainy day, or spending time with friends exploring the Portland food and music scenes.

name: Mike Grant (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
I have training in trauma therapies including EMDR and Brainspotting which can be used to assist in processing trauma in a safe, effective way. I also teach HeartMath which can assist with managing stress and developing resiliency. I’m also an athlete and coach and understand the specific challenges that impede performance goals and work with a range of folks who want to be present so they can have peak performance.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I believe that therapy doesn’t always need to be so serious, laughter and joy are important to overall wellbeing. Let’s do some hard work but also enjoy the process some. I also believe that there needs to be more customer service in therapy, with flexibility and understanding. I try to meet you where you are and if mistakes happen, let’s chat and figure out the best solutions.

name: Brittany Eide (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is warm, attentive, curious, and sincere. I am invested in building a bridge quickly so as to foster connection and support the process. It is my intention to be real, vulnerable, and relatable. I am much more interested in connection than formality. I want people to feel comfortable and safe to explore anything and everything knowing that we are all human and doing the best we can.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I started in the field back in 1998 when I began my degree in psychology. From there, I got a Master's in Counseling Psychology and became a school counselor. I then became a Certified Relationship Coach and Certified Synergistic Play Therapist. I have served children, teens, and adults working through many different issues in my capacity as a therapist, school counselor, and coach.

name: Bailey Becker (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe we already possess the tools we need for healing and growth. My role as a therapist is to help you uncover those tools, encourage healthier attachments, and guide you as you create new pathways toward connection and fulfillment. My therapeutic approach relies primarily on Emotionally-Focused/Attachment Therapy (EFT) principles as well as elements of Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT). This means I tend to draw on the ways past experiences and relationships with others have shaped who we are today, and work to uncover your emotions that are hidden beneath patterns of behavior. Together we will blaze new trails that lead to increased connection and greater life satisfaction. I am happy to work with individuals and couples from all walks of life, and strive to make my office an affirming environment for all. I take particular interest in working with teens, new parents, women's issues, families, and couples facing life transitions.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
If I were to describe my therapeutic values in one word it would be 'balance.' Healing happens when we are able to hold acceptance and change, self-compassion and accountability, vulnerability and strength—making room for the full range of the human experience. Self exploration means getting comfortable with the uncomfortable. Struggling is a natural part of being human. Therapy isn't about "fixing" all of life's pain, but instead gives us a chance to grow around pain, understand our emotional experiences, and become who we want to be even in times of turmoil. I believe each client is a collection of their history, shaped by the imperfect world we navigate. I listen with curiosity and care, honoring how identities like race, gender, and sexuality impact lived experience. I want clients to leave my office feeling like their full, authentic self is seen and supported.

name: Allison Kennedy (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Background
I attended Florida State University to achieve my Masters of Science in Mental Health Counseling and attended Southern Illinois University-Carbondale to achieve my Bachelor of Arts in Psychology. I have always had a propensity for helping people mentally on a very deep level; it is my passion. I have experience in this field ranging from a residential eating disorder setting, to being a victim advocate and counselor for trauma survivors, to helping folks in a community mental health outpatient services setting serving just about every mental health diagnosis. I am now ecstatic about serving primarily adult individuals of all races, ethnicities, identities, sexual orientations, etc. within my telehealth private practice.
My Approach to Helping
I am committed to meeting you where you are by tailoring each therapeutic experience uniquely to your needs, strengths and abilities, goals, and within your boundaries. I am respectful, empathetic, kind, and curious. I aim to facilitate an open, warm, and trustworthy therapeutic relationship that will always keep your safety and best interest in mind. I hope you can find that we can be a good therapeutic fit and that you can place your trust in me on your journey to hope and healing. Let's set up an appointment to get you started on the path to reaching your goals. Call or email me to set up an appointment. I look forward to connecting with you!

name: Nicole Dove (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Counseling is a process by which we work together to identify and work on what you bring to our sessions. My approach to counseling involves helping you to identify strengths within yourself and develop self-awareness. My therapeutic approach is eclectic combining numerous theoretical approaches and techniques including mindfulness, CBT, person centered, and solution focused.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
As a working mom of of two children I can relate to the stressors of life. I also have been a labor and delivery nurse for over 15 years. I would love the opportunity to work with you and help navigate life.

name: Kelly Allen, LPC (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We live in a very difficult world and we could all use some extra support sometimes. I specialize in working with individuals and couples and helping them achieves their therapeutic goals. I have worked extensively with anxiety, depression, mood disorders, stress related issues, and couples concerns such as difficulty with communication or desire. I primarily work with adults aged 18 and up. I have been in practice for over ten years and have worked in many outpatient environments. I am trauma informed and LGBTQ+ allied.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that we can all change and grow when we feel ready and have the right support. Having an inclusive practice is very important to me and I welcome clients of all ethnicities, genders, and sexual orientations. I became a counselor to help others through their most difficult times in life and I am passionate about helping you become the best version of yourself.

name: jane Newman
approaches:
My goal is to provide a safe, supportive, confidential and non-judgmental environment- to serve you with presence and respect. While my work is predominantly humanistic and client-centered, I also draw from and rely on aspects of CBT (Cognitive Behavioral Therapy), Gestalt and Narrative Counseling techniques. My belief is that each individual has the ability to find his/her answers - my role is to help facilitate the process.
I have an MBA in Marketing and an MS in General Counseling. I am a Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC), NBCC Certified. Currently in Private Practice, I have worked in Hospice and an Outpatient Cancer Center. I have facilitated Support Groups (Grief, loss, chronic illness), provided peer supervision, educational workshops and ceremonies of remembrance.

name: Jenn Mohn (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have an M.S. degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from the University of North Texas and I am a professional counseling associate in supervision with Thomas Doherty, PsyD. I’m also a National Certified Counselor (NCC) as well as a member of the American Counseling Association (ACA) and the Oregon Counseling Association (ORCA.) I specialize in a client-centered, relational, walk and talk approach to therapy. This means that when we work together, we do so while walking on a local nature trail. Hiking therapy also has the potential to help clients prone to high anxiety or dysregulation feel more relaxed than in a typical clinical setting. Not to mention, that walking in nature just plain feels good because it increases endorphins, decreases stress hormones and blood pressure, decreases depression and anxiety, and promotes focus and self-regulation.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I choose to walk alongside my clients because I believe that it provides a relationally safer and natural opportunity to build genuine and collaborative relationships with them. Likewise, I believe that change happens when clients have the opportunity to heal and grow within safe, connected, and healthy relationships. When working with me, clients are supported by nature and movement as they process relational patterns, increase self awareness, build awareness of their nervous system states, and they are empowered to tap into their strengths and learn to trust their inner wisdom to build a more resilient, authentic life. When working with me, I utilize my knowledge of interpersonal neurobiology, polyvagal theory, family systems theory, attachment, human development, neurodiversity, and cultural diversity to create safe and neuroaffirming relationships with my clients.

name: Simone Gotter-Nagle (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I value a collaborative and supportive approach to working with individuals, adolescents, and families who are struggling with mental health issues, substance abuse/dependence, and abuse and trauma. I take a bio-psycho-social approach to addressing what you see as your top priorities, which means we look at the problems you are facing as complex, multi-faceted issues in need of creative and holistic approaches in resolving. When we work together, we create a plan that would include weekly or bi-weekly therapy meetings focusing on how to reach emotional well-being and include goals in areas like physical health and exercise, advocacy for support or resources at school or work, and goals around building friendships or increasing social activity. I work with you as your partner and hope to empower you through this process of growth and development to reach a place where you feel stable and supported in your life now, as well as hopeful and peaceful about your future.
My Values as a Therapist
I completed my MSW at USC in 2008 and was excited to move back home to Portland, where I began my career working with adults, children, and families who have faced mental health substance abuse struggles, stemming from life experiences of trauma, abuse, and lack of support. I have worked in many roles as a social worker, including in the corrections system and in mental health day treatment and interpersonal violence programs. I have been trained in EMDR, trauma-informed care, collaborative problem solving, restorative justice, neurobiology of trauma, cognitive behavioral therapy, and relapse prevention. It’s important to me to acknowledge how power dynamics and systemic oppression has impacted others and openly discuss how our relationship can be a restorative experience as you work towards your goals. I offer also mental health consulting and teach as an adjunct instructor in the PSU School of Social Work.

name: Madison Weiss (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Our work together will be creative, integrative and mindful. I have extensive training in psychotherapy and art therapy with a focus on social justice and diversity. Our sessions could include talk therapy, therapeutic art and sensory awareness. I believe change and healing develop within the bounds of a safe, trusted, ethical, and healthy therapeutic relationship. When you are ready, I can help you take back your power, build resiliency and remove roadblocks that are getting in the way.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I truly believe that both therapy and art saved my life. This is what motivated me to want to help others, as I can speak from experience. Going to therapy helped me manage feelings I just didn’t understand, and helped me to realize I didn’t have to carry around this pain all by myself. In therapy I was able to find relief, and compassionate support. I have always believed that everyone has the ability to grow and change, and as I continued in my own inner work, a healthier, happier person emerged. Having been there myself, I will be your ally. We will make a plan that's a road map that will keep us on course to uncovering, and working through what ever challenges life has thrown you. No problem is too big, or too small to address in therapy. I am here to listen, celebrate your triumphs and work on your issues with empathy and care. I value your privacy, and honesty, and building a collaboration that benefits you on your path of self-development.

name: Darin Bergen (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
We will work directly with your emotions. I will help you learn to navigate your feelings effectively, learning how to get beyond feeling "nothing" and increasing your capacity so painful feelings no longer overwhelm. We will heal the old emotional wounds that cause you so much pain. You will not be alone in this work, I will be there every step of the way. As much as I believe in the transformation that happens in therapy, I know that you don't have to go through it alone. I show up fully and help build a connected relationship so that I can help carry the burden, and you don't have to do it on your own. Last, therapy with me will be active and focused. Our work happens together and transformation happens in the therapy session. I won't be just telling you things and expecting you to go home and practice techniques. I won't sit quietly in the background. This isn't an intellectual exercise. We do the work together.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in you. I believe in people. I believe much of our suffering comes from feeling alone with very painful emotions that we didn't know what to do with. I believe that as a result, we tried to find ways to make ourselves feel better and unfortunately those patterns can lead to more pain later on. I believe that while we all have a worst version of ourself, activated by suffering, we also have a best version of ourself - and therapy should help you live that version of yourself. I believe in you. Beyond the small world of therapy, I recognize that there are systems of power that function to repress people. I know that those people, living in oppression, have been blamed for their own suffering. I will not perpetuate this. I recognize my privilege because I have nearly every privilege. I stand as an ally to those who are oppressed. I am committed to doing the work of undoing my own internalized biases and prejudices, and playing my part in dismantling systems of power.

name: Amy Ruben (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In your therapy, we will explore the language of your body to gain insight into parts of yourself previously kept in your subconscious or experienced through tension or pain. The goal of our work is for you to become an expert of your own nervous system, and build a foundation of ease within your body. The pace at which I integrate somatic techniques into your therapy is based on your level of comfort and familiarity with being in your body, and as trust and safety is established between us. I use trauma-informed somatic techniques, which primarily draw from Polyvagal Theory, Somatic Experiencing, Sensorimotor Psychotherapy, Yoga Therapy, EMDR, and Experiential Movement.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I humbly and continually strive to ground my practice in an anti-oppression/pro-liberation lens and work from feminist, queer, and consent theories. My work in the mental health field has taught me that trauma shows up in many forms, and that each of us has an incredible capacity for healing and an innate drive towards meaning and wholeness. I recognize that ancestral trauma, and/or the impacts of systems of oppression on our bodies and nervous systems are an essential part of therapy. I strive to hold myself and individuals with privileged identities accountable to the work of dismantling oppressive patterns, and to support individuals with marginalized identities in their work of healing. I recognize that working with intersectionality is vital so that healing can happen on individual and collective levels.

name: Jeremy Jones (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In the last 6 years, I've helped people cope with trauma, psychosis, depression, anxiety, health issues, and addiction. I take a holistic approach that considers all aspects of well-being, such as social, occupational, environmental, physical, intellectual, spiritual, and emotional. Through therapy, we will build protective supports and positive behaviors. I'm LGBTQIA+ affirming and committed to providing a safe, equitable space.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a Licensed Professional Counselor (OR License #C5359) registered with the O.B.L.P.C.T. I hold an MA in Applied Psychological Science from Pacific University. I worked as substance use disorder counselor with Cascadia Behavioral Healthcare. Up until recently I held employment as a dual diagnosis clinical supervisor with Columbia Community Mental Health. From my last two work placements I met the requirements to obtain my Certified Alcohol and Drug Counselor II (Certification #19-09-24) registered with M.H.A.C.B.O. Finally, I have Board Certified-TeleMental Health Provider credential which identifies those professionals who are uniquely trained in best practices for delivering traditional behavioral healthcare through technological means. BC-TMH credential holders have met nationally established telemental health criteria.

name: William Steiner (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I use a person centered approach with CBT and mindfulness based interventions to support client treatment goals. I take an eclectic approach to therapy, which allows for more flexibility in applying a variety of interventions that meet the specific needs of my clients . I believe the therapeutic alliance is the prerequisite to any success and I try to collaborate with my clients to build an authentic supportive relationship that leads to greater personal insight and growth.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
Working with a therapist can bring relief, but can also feel overwhelming and stressful. For that reason it is important to find a therapist that is a good fit and that is someone you feel like you can work with comfortably. I understand this is a difficult process and so offer a free initial check-in/ consult to see if we might work well together. Please feel free to reach out if you are interested in scheduling.

name: Kelli Warmouth (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My work is tailored to meeting you where you're at, and offering evidence-based compassionate services to help guide you toward your own powerful insight. It is your hour, and one that I strive to feel authentic, affirming, and non-judgmental. If you are trying to achieve better balance, peace, and clarity in life, I will provide the space and tools for you to be successful. In our sessions, we will use powerful tools, including Internal Family Systems, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, Feminist Multicultural Therapy, Dialectical Behavioral Therapy, and Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, to help you make positive changes and feel more joy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My work as a therapist is informed and oriented from a multicultural and social justice informed lens. I am a supporter and ally of the Black Lives Matter Movement and the LGBTQIA+ community, both in and out of my work as a mental health provider. From this lens, I believe we all operate within systems of the world around us. It is necessary then to consider the impact of these systems on our mental health and on our stories; whether these systems strengthen and instill power and wisdom within, or if they oppress and create barriers and pain. Together, we will zoom out and consider the ways your experiences are shaped by the world around you, and we will zoom in to understand the parts of yourself that are tired, hurting, and in need of compassion. By taking this 3-D view of yourself, you can expect to gain clarity, self-compassion, and direction toward greater happiness, healing, and peace.

name: Veronica Valenzuela
approaches:
Counseling provides the opportunity to create this connection and also allows you to explore concerns, fears, hopes and aspirations in a safe place. Together we can navigate significant life changes or events, your search for identity or meaning, as well as struggles with depression, anxiety or self-esteem. We will work collaboratively as I believe you are the expert in what you need and want. I incorporate mindfulness and attachment therapies into our work according to each client's unique preferences.
Master's degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from Portland State University. National Certified Counselor (NCC), member of the American Counseling Association (ACA) and the National Board for Certified Counselors (NBCC). Received additional in Hakomi Mindfulness and Attachment therapies through Mindful Experiential Therapy Approaches (M.E.T.A) in Portland, Oregon.

name: Katy Buddeke (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is rooted in warmth, authenticity, and a grounded presence. Our work together will begin with co-creating a safe and trusting therapeutic relationship. It is from that place of connection and safety that our work together will grow. I practice from a humanistic and relational-cultural perspective. What this means is that I hold a deep respect for the unique experiences, relationships, and contexts that have shaped the person you are. Beyond that foundation, I integrate other modalities, tools, and practices that align with your unique needs. I believe change and healing happens in the body and will help facilitate an ever growing mind-body connection. Throwing the same solution at a problem repeatedly will often yield the same results. I love to collaborate with clients in finding new and creative approaches that challenge stagnation and align with best practices.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe everyone has the strength and capacity within themselves to move towards their personal fulfillment and therapeutic goals. The search for a therapist who fits your unique needs can be a tiring endeavor. It is a brave and beautiful thing to seek support. I cannot overstate the true honor it will be to accompany you as you forge your path forward towards health, balance, stability, and pleasure.

name: Melanie Ramsey (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I provide psychiatric evaluations and medication management for children, adolescents and adults. I specialize in anxiety, mood disorders, and ADHD/ADD. I have experience in a multitude of settings and disorders including crisis management, depression/bipolar disorder, anxiety, ADHD, trauma, substance use disorder) across the lifespan. I enjoy collaborating with therapists and other providers in the community to ensure the most comprehensive care. I provide convenient videoconference appointments and I am in-network with most PPO insurance plans.
Why We are a Good Fit for You
Are you experiencing distress due to internal conflicts, trauma, relationships, fears, or feeling stuck? Do you feel anxious, depressed, and unmotivated? Some things are just too difficult to go through alone. Working with someone in a safe and compassionate environment without judgment can be the key to your understanding and growth. Your success and happiness are rooted in a partnership that we share. You do the hard part of being vulnerable; to the best of my ability, I will work to listen to and understand you, before developing a shared treatment plan. I am honored to support you on your journey to mental wellness.

name: AJ Bruce
approaches:
How I Can Help You
The great news is that growth and healing are within reach. I’ve supported many people in navigating their emotions, building stronger relationships, and gaining confidence in who they are. Work with me involves practical tools to understand your internal world, manage overwhelming feelings, and uncover patterns that may be holding you back. As you grow in emotional intelligence, you’ll deepen your connections and develop a stronger, more authentic sense of self.
More About the People I Treat
I work with people who are seeking to better understand and express their emotions. Whether you’re dealing with anger, depression, anxiety, relationship struggles, or personal growth challenges, I help people build emotional intelligence and improve communication. Many people come to me feeling stuck in unhealthy patterns, disconnected from their emotions, or struggling to navigate difficult conversations. Together, we’ll work to overcome these barriers, leading to healthier relationships and a deeper, more authentic sense of self.

name: Danielle Grover (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I am a Licensed Psychologist who has been working or training in an assessment capacity for over 8 years. My work has included positions in a variety of specialty and generalist capacities, such as ADHD and Autism clinics as well as primary care. I specialize in comprehensive testing for mood, anxiety, and neurodevelopmental conditions, as well as learning challenges. I am licensed to see individuals in OR.
My Approach to Helping
My goal is to promote positive growth through diagnostic clarification and education.

name: Caitlin Allaway (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I am a seasoned clinical psychologist with over a decade of specialized expertise in treating addiction and dual diagnoses. Dual diagnosis treatment is crucial because it addresses the complex, interwoven nature of mental health disorders and substance use disorders. These conditions often fuel each other, creating a challenging cycle where one worsens the other, making recovery seem out of reach. This personalized and integrated treatment plan ensures that underlying psychological issues are addressed alongside the substance use, leading to more effective and enduring recovery. You will learn healthier coping mechanisms to manage mental health issues, stress, triggers, and cravings. This approach reduces relapse rates, improves overall mental health, and significantly enhances the quality of life. I support all treatment goals, whether you’re focused on abstinence or harm reduction (moderation), and I tailor each treatment plan to meet your unique needs.
My Approach to Helping
I offer a personalized approach to recovery, meeting you exactly where you are. Together, we’ll explore your unique patterns, develop strategies to manage cravings, and prevent relapses—helping you achieve lasting recovery. My goal is to guide you toward healing, restoring balance to your life, and rebuilding the relationships that matter most. In addition to addiction, I provide support for mood disorders, ADHD, and family conflicts. I understand the deep impact addiction can have, and my compassionate approach is designed to heal not only the individual, but the whole system of relationships that shape your world. Together, we’ll gain valuable insights into your relationship with substances, identify triggers, and develop essential skills for craving management and relapse prevention. We’ll build a strong recovery community and foster the resilience you need to overcome challenges and maintain long-term success. I’m here to support you on your journey to lasting transformation.

name: Fran Witt (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Fran strongly believes that each client is the expert in their life and may utilize various interventions to meet each client’s individual needs. She primarily uses Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) and Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) from trauma and diversity-informed lenses. Learning and implementing Exposure and Response Prevention (ERP) and exposure-related trauma interventions are some of Fran’s passions. Leadership is another one of her passions, and she enjoys leading any chance she gets. Fran has worked with a variety of populations, including inpatient and nonprofit. She has experience treating domestic violence, depression, and anxiety. Her special interests include trauma, anxiety-related disorders (e.g., OCD, phobias), systems, marginalized populations, and gender/sexuality concerns. She is committed to providing unconditional positive regard, warmth, acceptance, safety, authenticity, and collaboration to clients.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe everyone has the right to show up in this challenging and messy world as their authentic self--especially marginalized populations, such as the BIPOC the LGBTQ+ communities. I enjoy providing support and working with marginalized populations to better embrace their genuine selves and navigate life. One way I assist clients from marginalized populations is by utilizing my own values such as anti-oppression, anti-racism, inclusion, diversity, equity, human rights, and social justice. In addition, some of my other values are empathy, open-mindedness, and kindness. Outside of the therapy room, you will likely find me spending time with my cat, making arts and crafts, hiking, advocating, thrifting, gardening, and engaging with my friends and loved ones.

name: Stefanie Cruz (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I consider therapy a collaborative process between a therapist and client(s). I work toward helping others develop more effective strategies that will result in more success as it relates to meeting their goals within the realm of the individual self and interpersonal relationships. I work with individuals and families from a systemic lens that allows exploration of issues around racism, sexism, and classism (to name a few). Overall, I utilize approaches rooted in a variety of areas depending on client needs. Much of my background stems from cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT), brief-solution focused therapy (BSF), acceptance and commitment therapy (ACT), and motivational interviewing (MI).
Why I Became a Therapist
I became a therapist to offer BIPOC representation to our local community. As I began my journey in mental health, I did not see very many providers that looked like me or represented cultural awareness I thought was key in understanding the complexities of an intersectional experience.

name: Jingyi Luan (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Jingyi is getting certified to become a Narcissistic Abuse Treatment Clinician who also works with people who are struggling with anxiety, depression, PTSD, intergenerational trauma, immigrant issues, academic stress, and relationship issues. She provides culturally trauma-informed Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectic Behavioral Therapy (DBT), and Polyvagal tailored to each client’s needs. Jingyi believes a good therapeutic relationship will enhance the effectiveness of the treatment modalities, so she focuses on relationship-building and supports each client with their self-discovery.

name: Naomi Painter (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Mindful, somatic experiential therapy: Hakomi is a form of sensorimotor psychotherapy (also called somatic therapy), consisting of guided self-study that uses mindfulness to access traumatic memories and harmful beliefs encoded in the body - those places in your body where you feel restless, uncomfortable, ill, or just plain “don’t go there.” Together, we will harness the power of mindfulness, creativity, curiosity, and presence to courageously dive in, unearth your pain, and reshape your experience of the world. Hakomi brings together the wisdom of Buddhist and Taoist teachings with modern scientific findings on how the brain changes itself through experience, not just talking about it.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Before becoming a counselor I was a family support worker and program manager for a home visiting program serving first-time parents, a special education paraeducator, a school counselor at the elementary, middle, and high school level, a case manager and program manager for a grant serving veterans experiencing homelessness, and an Aging and Disability case manager. My 15 years in social services and education gave me me a view of the rich tapestry of human experience, and a deep understanding of the toll working in the helping professions can take on people.

name: Christine Bethel (SHE/HER)
approaches:
Perhaps feeling lost or like a mess doesn't begin to describe your current struggle. Maybe you worry you're permanently damaged or that your relationship has experienced too much pain and resentment to recover. The isolation of keeping it together for the world makes the exhaustion, worry, and unhappiness even heavier. You are not alone and you are not doomed to this place forever. You can be ok, you can feel happier, your relationship can be fulfilling again. Common symptoms of these hidden struggles include: anxiety, depression, numbing, & PTSD. Whether you're an individual finding your way back to happiness, a couple adding new depth to your relationship, or you are just desperately trying to make things work, please call me. I'd love to help. What you see is what you get! My style is relaxed but direct, I want to optimize your time and growth.
I've been practicing therapy for 10 years and continue to be amazed by the clients I meet and the growth they allow me to witness, teach, and support. My background and special interests include: Successful couples on the rocks & successful individuals struggling with self, potentially experiencing symptoms of depression, anxiety, addiction and trauma. Ask me about EMDR! *Please check out my website, cbetherapy.com, for more info and to schedule an appointment.

name: Dr. Heidi Christensen (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Therapy is the place of healing and possibilities – where we can work together to resolve the pain that is present in your life. Our work will start with healing the current symptoms such as stress, depression, anxiety, or relationship issues. Imagine the freedom that comes from being able to understand yourself more deeply. Imagine giving yourself hope, freedom, and love. That is exactly what will happen when you embark on this therapeutic journey. The process of transformation and healing will bring up feelings and experiences that you haven’t felt in a while. It will not be easy. We will develop tools and support to process and heal these feelings and experiences in a place of care and safety. Some people find that this is enough and might be a good place to stop. For others, it might have incited curiosity about what is also present that might not be in their awareness, and this process can be longer.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My counseling philosophy honors the strengths you have, the wisdom of your experience, and connecting to your inner truth. I focus on bridging the outer part of yourself (that which we normally associate ourselves with) with the inner part of yourself (the more intuitive and spiritual part of our being). This focus is provided in an emotionally safe environment where you can explore whatever issue is present. As a depth-oriented therapist, my approach is a collaborative, related style. I use this style with individuals, couples, and groups. I also utilize current cutting edge research such as Gottman method and Brene Brown's Daring Way, and I take a Jungian approach to dreams, unconscious content, and creativity.

name: Judy Marantz-Herzberg (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that deep down, everyone possesses an inherent wisdom. Like a compass, it guides us. During challenging times- transitions, times of grief, loss, and change, we lose sight of our direction, and of what we think, feel, and value. We may act in ways that make things worse- procrastinating, developing unhealthy relationships with friends, lovers, family members, and our bodies. We disconnect from our inherent wisdom- the part that knows what we most need and want- and how to act on it. I work as a catalyst to guide you as you find your way back to your own path. I help by getting to know you, deeply understanding your perspective and life experiences, while helping you examine unnoticed possibilities. I help you flesh out a vision of how you want your life to be, and how to head in that direction. I listen strategically, noticing patterns. We collaboratively sift through these experiences, learn from them, and capitalize on your strengths and successes.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My BA in Psychology and Education is from Rutgers University; my Masters in Social Work is from Portland State University. As an elementary school teacher, I saw how my students’ emotional lives impacted their school functioning and well-being. I wanted to support them with their emotional needs, and help them lead with their strengths. I have found this opportunity through counseling. I have worked with high school and elementary school children as a school social worker and in private practice. I offer academic coaching for middle/high school students, enabling them to reach their potentials. Communicating with teachers and families, I foster a team approach to supporting students, alleviating family stress that arises when kids struggle with school and life. Various facets of my life inform my practice. Each dimension– teacher, mother, daughter, and partner - helps me appreciate the complexity of life and admire the ways people face adversity and change.

name: Hanna Maxwell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am trained in Story-Informed Trauma Therapy, a framework that helps us understand how your story - from birth to the present - informs your experience of life and the choices you make. I also come from a somatic perspective, and we’ll explore how you feel emotions in your body. In therapy, I’ll support you in developing your relationship with your body, your conscious awareness, and the wisdom that you have already inside you. By learning more about ourselves, our beliefs, and the strategies we've relied on to keep ourselves safe, we can begin to build a sense of trust in our selves and our bodies. This process isn't always easy or linear, and my role is to support you by providing presence and a safe relationship where change can occur. I believe you have immense value and a story that is waiting to unfold. It would be an honor and a privilege to sit with you in this next chapter of your life.
More About the People I Treat
Maybe you've thought about going to counseling but then paused because of thoughts like, "But my childhood wasn't that bad. Other people had it worse." Or you might feel that overall, life is going pretty well, even if you dealt with difficult things in the past. Somehow though, you still have symptoms that bother you - restlessness, difficulty in relationships, constant worry, periods of low mood and lack of motivation - and you might not know why. I love to help people not only resolve those symptoms, but help them understand where they come from. The comparison game of "who had it worse" doesn't have to prevent you from tending to the things in your life that were painful. I believe that it's important to understand yourself and your story so that you can experience health to the fullest.

name: Evoke Therapy LLC (THEY, THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I take a non-traditional approach, and that’s intentional. I’m here to help you break patterns, face what’s been getting in your way, and build a life that feels more aligned with who you really are. Whether it’s relationship challenges, anxiety, or just feeling stuck or disconnected, I’m focused on helping you create real, lasting change—not just talk about it. I blend practical tools with deeper, intuitive work—like dream analysis, tarot, and symbolic exploration. I also draw from mind-body-spirit practices rooted in my background as a Yoga Teacher, Herbalist, and Reiki Master. For couples, I use the Gottman Method to strengthen communication, rebuild trust, and create deeper connection. You don’t have to fit a mold to show up here. Wherever you’re at, I’ll meet you there—with honesty, intention, and maybe a little magic.
Social Justice Issues I Care About
I approach therapy with a deep awareness of the systems we’re all living under. As an anarchist, I believe in helping people understand and navigate the realities of class struggle and how systemic oppression impacts our mental, emotional, and physical well-being. Based in Philly, I run a community group that provides preventative care using accessible botanical medicine to those who can’t afford traditional healthcare. I’m actively involved in local mutual aid networks, committed to collective care, autonomy, and building alternatives to oppressive systems. Other parts of my identity include being neurodivergent, part of the LGBTQ community, and mixed-race--all lived experiences bring into my work. These identities shape how I navigate the world and the ways I connect with others, especially when it comes to recognizing and honoring the unique challenges we all face.

name: Emily Berry (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Background
During my difficult younger years, I discovered a longing to understand what humans need in order to live healthy, meaningful lives. I was often told I was "too sensitive," and while a part of me worried that that was true, in my gut I knew it wasn't a problem. It was a gift. I wanted to be a part of shifting our society’s narratives about mental health and emotional wellbeing by being someone who welcomes and connects with the humanity and vulnerability of others. Being a therapist is not just a job for me - this work is in my bones. Nothing is more meaningful to me than helping others discover the strength and wisdom within themselves to live their most authentic, invigorated life - a journey I have been on myself as well. I’m so grateful to be doing what I love in this amazing city.
My Values as a Therapist
First and foremost, I am a relational therapist. Earning my client’s trust is my number one priority. I challenge the conventional, medical model of psychotherapy which is rooted in seeing therapists as experts and their clients as patients with a disorder that needs to be "treated." I don’t see humans this way. People are way too complex and interesting for that. I understand the human condition in a holistic, strengths-based way - each of us is adaptive, resilient, and got to where we are for a reason. I value staying engaged in my personal and professional growth through advanced training and social justice oriented education. I am committed to anti-racism and anti-oppression and bring these values to my work. I genuinely invest in my clients and deeply honor the trust and bond that occurs in therapy.

name: Chad Ernest, MS, LPC (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe in a client/family centered approach with collaboration in treatment between the counselor and client/family. The goal of counseling overall is wellness and/or reconciliation with one’s self, family and the community. I am an integrationist in my counseling theory, which means that I believe in incorporating various theories into my practice to suit the needs of whom I am serving. My practice is based on the following theories and interventions within them: Various evidence based practices; the Family Checkup Model; Person-centered; Existential; Gestalt; Psychodynamic; Narrative; Strength Based; Cognitive Behavioral; Rational Emotive Behavioral; Reality; Feminist; Family Systems Therapy; and Collaborative Parenting. The approach utilized is based on your needs and facilitated with you or your family and your partner.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a BA in Psychology and Philosophy from the University of Wisconsin-Madison received in 2000. I have an MS in Mental Health Counseling from Capella University received in March 2011 and a Graduate Certificate in Marriage and Family Therapy from Capella University received in December 2010. I am a member of Counselors for Social Justice (CSJ) and American Counselors Association (ACA).

name: Karin Pfeiffer-Robinson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I graduated with an M.A. in Professional Mental Health Counseling from Lewis & Clark College. Prior to becoming a mental health counselor, I worked with Latina women and their children as a case manager in a transitional housing program as well as with Latinx farmers, both in Paraguay and in the Portland Metro area. My approach is guided by relational cultural theory, attachment theory, and ecotherapy. These philosophies recognize that each person is part of larger social, cultural, and environmental contexts that influence and impact them greatly. I utilize somatic therapy, parts work, EMDR, and mindful self-compassion in my work with clients. More important than a specific theory or approach, for counseling to be effective it is essential that you feel respected, safe, and understood by your therapist. I offer free 20-minute initial consultations for anyone interested in becoming a client, to see if this would be a good fit.
My Values as a Therapist
I believe that you know yourself better than anyone else does. Sometimes that inner wisdom can feel obscured. Painful life experiences and systemic oppression can lead to mental health challenges. Therapy is one avenue for reconnecting with yourself, with your values, and with others. I recognize that the field of mental health counseling has its roots in serving the dominant, white culture in the U.S. Access to nature has also traditionally been much more available for middle to upper-middle class, able-bodied white people than for anyone else. I started my private practice, Fern Path Counseling, to be one small part of changing mental health care to better align with social justice values and the rights of all people to access mental health care that they deserve.

name: Anna Belle Marin, Ph.D., LPC
approaches:
I provide therapy that is tailored to your unique situation, whether you experience depression, anxiety, unresolved trauma, relationship problems, or any other issues that keep you from being your best. You need good nervous system regulation to cope with life’s challenges. I use an eclectic, holistic approach with body-centered, mindful therapies so you can increase your capacity to manage life’s difficulties. Therapies I offer include: Somatic Experiencing: body-centered therapy for nervous system integration; EMDR (Eye Movement Desensitization Reprocessing): for trauma healing so you can move beyond past triggers; Depth Psychology and Dream work to uncover your unconscious strengths so you can navigate life with purpose and joy; and the Prepare-Enrich on-line inventory: a tool to help couples at any relationship stage including marriage preparation. My goal in therapy is to empower you to identify and use effective skills to enhance your daily life.
I am a life-long learner and infuse therapy sessions with curiosity and fresh approaches to help you identify, initiate and complete steps on your unique life path. I have developed my love and curiosity for people traveling the world from a very young age. So, I draw on a wealth of life experience, my passionate curiosity and diverse education to serve you. My experience and skills include: Licensed Professional Counselor, LPC, OR Board of Licensed Prof. Counselors & Therapists, #C3008; National Certified Counselor, #290484, M.A in Counseling Psychology from Pacifica Graduate Institute, Carpinteria, CA with a focus on marriage and family therapy and depth psychology, Ph.D. in Food Science with a minor in Psychology from Cornell U., Ithaca, NY.

name: Ranann Bell (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Outdoor therapy is centered around the belief, experience, and scientific backing that nature can offer grounding, calm, and clarity. Nature supports us in feeling more relaxed and open, allowing you to explore your emotions more freely. The rhythmic movement of walking releases built-up energy and tension, helping you process difficult feelings. In each session, we’ll take a walk in one of the beautiful parks Portland has to offer, using the natural world around us as a supportive backdrop for your healing process.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in a holistic approach to therapy, where nature becomes our guide. I created Bellflower Therapy to offer clients a unique experience—one where we step outside the walls of a traditional therapy room and into nature. My approach focuses on walking side by side, allowing the natural environment to be a healing partner as you explore your emotions and work through life’s challenges.

name: Misty Bottorff (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
I graduated with my master’s degree in social work in 2014 from the University of Kansas then went on to earn my clinical licensure. I also hold a graduate certificate from Portland State University in Infant/Toddler Mental Health. I am passionate about helping others and have several years of experience working with children, adolescents, families, and the perinatal population. My experiences include working in community mental health, short-term crisis stabilization, group practice, and the healthcare setting. I was born and raised in the Pacific Northwest and after a brief stint in Kansas, moved back to Oregon in 2017. When I am not working, I enjoy spending time in nature with my partner, live music, video games, camping, and spending time with family and friends. I also have an amazing daughter and daughter-in-law. I am a huge animal lover and have a rescue French Bulldog named Poppy and grumpy rescue cat named Max!

name: melissa sanders (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that the therapeutic relationship is an opportunity to gain a deeper understanding of the Self and make choices that are more aligned with personal goals. I use a Person Centered approach using Somatic awareness, Internal Family Systems, Narrative Therapy, DBT, CBT and creative expressive tools. Creating a deeper connection to body responses, and discovering different perspectives to develop more self awareness, I hope to work as a facilitator for your growth. I believe that you are always the expert in your own life, and I welcome feedback and collaboration about tools that you find most useful for your progress. At our initial sessions, we will explore what brought you to therapy and some potential types of therapies that may be beneficial for your treatment. I often use creative expressive activities during our sessions, with additional focus on somatic body awareness to bring more awareness to physical responses to thoughts and emotions.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As I child, I experienced traumatic events that I addressed through my own healing journey. I found that a combination of therapeutic interventions, including expressive therapies helped me identify the behaviors that were no longer serving me and strengthen my ability to access tools I was unaware of before.
