I believe that we all have the answers we seek already within us and the therapeutic relationship is a chance to have a supportive person to help guide you to understanding yourself on a deeper level of awareness. As humans, we develop behavior based on our experiences and what has provided us with our needs, even if this behavior is unhelpful or damaging to ourselves or our lives. I view therapy as a place where we can learn new ways to move closer to congruence with the people we want to be and the lives we want to lead.

name: Michael Viola (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background
I received my BA from Swarthmore College in Pennsylvania and my MA in Counseling from Southwestern College in Santa Fe, NM. I have also been educated and worked in the corporate and educational structures of NYC. I apprenticed with master herbalists in Ithaca, NY and Native healers in New Mexico and the Peruvian Amazon. I have been cultivating a life as a psychotherapist, shamanic practitioner, and artist. My journey has required me to explore and unify my inner Male and Female polarities along with the elemental energies of the directions (Earth, Air, Fire, Water). This union births the conscious and creative self within. We can then manifest in alignment with this Self, our Community, and The Earth, free from limiting energetic patterns. I work to assist others in this pursuit.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I view our patterns and habitual experiences as a function of vibration/frequency/resonance. We can hold the frequency patterns of earlier childhood traumas, cultural/religious narratives, ancestral wounds, and even past life tragedies. The beliefs that form from these experiences are stored within the body/mind frequency structure. When we liberate hidden patterns, purge them, and introduce new ones, we have effectively changed our resonant frequencies. The work is then to find the belief, in what it is rooted, and then to liberate the energy and introduce a new energetic imprint that is more appropriate. We can do this in many ways and I specialize in using shamanic techniques, including journey work, psychotherapy, and archetypal psychology to do this. My work has arisen from personal pursuits in the study of quantum physics/string theory, Jungian/Archetypal Psychology, Energy Psychology, Transpersonal Psychology, Somatic Body/Mind Psychology, Herbalism, and Vibrational Medicine.

name: Hanh Nguyen (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
As a counseling intern earning my Master of Arts in Counseling at Northwestern University, I’ve had the privilege of furthering my education and honing my skills working with adults navigating anxiety, depression, grief, and life transitions. My clinical experience includes supporting individuals in crisis settings, facilitating therapeutic groups, and offering one-on-one support to help clients feel grounded and empowered.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe healing happens in community and that therapy can be a space to come home to yourself. As a first-generation Vietnamese American woman, I understand how identity, culture, and intergenerational experiences shape the way we move through the world. I’m committed to practicing from an anti-oppressive, trauma-informed, and culturally responsive lens. I care deeply about advocating for marginalized communities, particularly BIPOC, LGBTQIA+ folks, and children of immigrants. Outside of the therapy room, I love movies, arts and crafts, and spending time with my dog Tofu and my cat Margot. I believe in the power of softness, laughter, and storytelling as part of the healing process.

name: Charlene McCleoud (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have gained collective knowledge and skills to provide the best service possible to individuals with mental illnesses. The tools that I'll provide I have been perfecting throughout my career. I will utilize and share these tools and techniques to assist individuals like yourself face the challenges you may be experiencing within this present moment. I am a cognitive behavioral and solution-focused therapist. I recognized the benefit of these tools and how they will positively impact your life. We will work collaboratively to address the difficulties that are bothering you. We will work towards gaining control over your body and thoughts that may be self-limiting now. I have assisted individuals within the BIPOC community. My experience is focused on trauma, recognizing culture as essential to successful engagement and treatment, and mental health issues with adults.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I believe therapy is about gaining insight into your true self. Many of us navigate this thing we call life expecting no challenges, fear, or worry. These aspects are constantly a part of our life, and we carry the inner power to face the difficulties we may experience. It's up to you to identify and unlock your inner strength and acceptance through therapy. Recognizing most of the work is completed between our sessions and outside of treatment. As your therapist, I serve as a facilitator throughout your healing process. It's up to you to take that first step, towards the direction you have envisioned for your life. Healing is a process and can happen in many ways. I enter this field with a non-judgmental and open mind. The client is the expert in their situation and holds the solution to their healing. I work in a collaborative environment and believe individuals should be involved throughout their treatment process. My work is built on empathy, respect, compassion, and empowerment.

name: Greg Peterson (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have a PhD in Counselor Education and Supervision and have been in professional counseling practice since 2012. I am licensed in both Washington and Oregon and have additional national credentials as an approved supervisor, board-certified tele-mental health provider, and certified counselor. I am a generalist counselor able to assist clients with quite a few issues. I specialize in working with young adults, mental health impacts of climate change, parenting issues, couples counseling, and men's issues. I approach counseling from a blended solutions-focused and acceptance and commitment therapy lens. I aim to make counseling as helpful as possible for my clients while assisting them in developing a flexible approach to life.

name: Julie Kirchner (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am here to listen and walk alongside you without judgment and help you work through life's many challenges. My goal is to create an atmosphere where you feel comfortable and safe. I use Strength-Based, Person-Centered, and Emotionally Focused therapies. I work with children, teenagers, adults, families, and couples with issues ranging from anxiety, depression, low self-esteem, grief, and difficult life transitions. I have strong foundational knowledge and have been trained in play therapy and parent-child relationships. I believe that everyone has the ability to heal, grow, and create a better life for themselves, and sometimes all they need is a little help.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As a Marriage and Family registered associate, I have experience working with children, families, and couples. My personal experience as a wife and a mother of four children has helped me gain insight into life’s challenges. I am especially interested in working with couples that need support with other life transitions and relationship problems. I am also passionate about working with children and helping them navigate challenging issues.

name: Scott Dupuis (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background
Scott earned a Master’s degree of Counseling Psychology from St. Thomas University in 2014. His previous experience includes: • Mental health therapist in a school setting working with kids age 5-11 in Minneapolis, MN • Mental health therapist working with adolescents in high school in Minneapolis, MN • Outpatient mental health therapist working with adults in Minneapolis, MN
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Scott was born and raised in Aurora, IL and has also lived in Minnesota before moving to Oregon. His interest are: • Bread making (Sourdough fanatic) • Cooking • Soccer • Reading • Gardening

name: Jeannie Balenger (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work mostly from a psychodynamic and existential lens. This means that I believe the concerns a person brings to therapy can often be linked to significant relationships and experiences in their past and present. I believe it is important to explore your past, in order to get to the root of the issue that keeps you feeling stuck in the same pattern or routine. I will also work with you to live in alignment with your values, find meaning in your life, address avoidance and build trust in decision making. If we work together, you can expect a collaborative partnership built on trust, empathy, and with humor and swearing in the mix. Additionally, I recognize how things like gender, sexuality, ethnicity, culture, class, and ability affect people's lives. I know that my views are shaped by my own experiences and identities, and I always have more to learn. I am committed to continuing my education and self-reflection.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My name is Jeannie (like “in a bottle”) I am in my late 30s, and originally from the Washington D.C. area but grew up here, in outer east Portland/Rockwood. Before starting college in my 30s, I worked in the restaurant industry for nearly 20 years. I've spent the last 5 years working in higher education and I did my clinical internship at the PSU Community Counseling Clinic, a low-cost community counseling clinic. My life and work experiences have significantly influenced my decision to become a counselor. I strive to be a genuine, trusting and safe person for clients. In my spare time, I advocate immigrant rights and for Universal Health Care in Oregon. I believe that all people deserve accessible health care as a basic human right. I am an member and supporter of the Health Care for All Oregon org and VOZ workers' Rights Education Project. Additionally, I enjoy banter, making things like food, art, and jokes, trivia, the 90s, and improving my 2nd grade level Spanish.

name: Shelby Houston (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
When someone comes to counseling it’s because they want something to change. I believe change happens by trying to be who we are rather than trying to be different. I'm trained in Gestalt therapy, which is a relational, experiential, and holistic approach. This means I'll offer an environment that's supportive of self-exploration and growth as we travel together on your path towards wholeness. By gaining awareness of how your relationships and environment guide the way you relate to the world, you'll be empowered to recognize choices you have available now to live life richly and authentically. As therapist, I show up as who I really am. I’m intentional with bringing my whole self into the therapy room because I believe this helps create space for you to do the same.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I’m committed to honoring client’s autonomy, intersecting identities, and the culturally-sensitive aspects of their experience. I create space to examine power and I promote client choice and solicit feedback throughout the therapy process. I believe it’s important to have a space to embody, explore and learn about core aspects of the human experience, including sexuality and gender. I value gender, sexual, relational, and affectional diversity. I'm a Professional Counselor Associate in Oregon and a National Certified Counselor. I earned a Master of Counseling degree from Oregon State University. I receive ongoing training in Gestalt therapy from Gestalt Therapy Associates Los Angeles, and also receive ongoing supervision in this modality. I'm certified in Emotion-Focused Therapy, Polyvagal Theory, and Interpersonal Neurobiology, and have engaged in professional counseling research in the topic areas of sizeism, kink, and neuroscience-informed approaches.

name: Annette Smith (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have been working with children and families in various capacities for over 20 years and one of the greatest things I have learned is that human connection is a conduit of healing. When there is a lack of connection, humans can feel despair, helplessness, fear, hopelessness, anxiety, and depression. Attachment disruptions, trauma, and lack of emotional attunement can create wounds that are felt for a lifetime. When those wounds go left untreated they can create problems and distress in our lives in the here and now. Healing those wounds become part of the process of gaining wellness, repairing relationships, and becoming fulfilled versions of ourselves. I work individually with adults 18+. I specialize in Attachment and trauma work using an Existential lens of discovering meaning in life. I work well with individuals who are experiencing existential crises that range from discovering one's authentic self to creating and accepting the meaning their life and experiences have.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I became a counselor to co-facilitate others' healing journeys. I believe that every individual has the ability to heal themselves and to find the peace and fulfillment they desire. I believe that my role as a counselor is to guide others on their journey. As a member of the BIPOC community, I am committed to serving those who also identify as BIPOC as well as other communities who have been marginalized in the mental health field. My position as a counselor also places me in the role of a social justice advocate seeking to support individuals in their empowerment. Within a collaborative environment, I work with clients in practical ways that give them real-world tools to navigate challenging experiences. I also work with clients to deepen their own understanding and meaning-making of their life and the world around them.

name: Sabrina Arnold (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I provide a safe space to talk about anything you want to. I use a mix of CBT, EMDR, and IFS, with little bits of other therapy approaches, depending on what works best for each person. I find that EMDR and IFS works great for trauma.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
As for who I am outside of being a therapist: I love tabletop roleplaying games, like Dungeons and Dragons and Ten Candles. I enjoy reading, karaoke, video games, and watching low budget sci-fi movies.

name: Anissa Bahrenburg (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I specialize in Sex and Relationship therapy and Clinical Supervision. Sex therapy addresses physical or psychological areas related to sexual health. It is for an individual or individuals in relationship seeking increased connection, pleasure intimacy and an increased knowledge of your sexual self. Sex therapy is entirely talk therapy. Relationship therapy is for any type of relationship (couples/dyads, triads, polycules and more) seeking increased ability to connect with each other. General areas of focus: -Improving communication -Building skills in conflict management -Increasing connection, vulnerability, and understanding -Increasing intimacy and sexual connection -Defining relationship values and developing a shared vision for the future -Addressing challenges emerging from breaches of trust, trauma, loss, and more within the relationship -Identifying and addressing old trauma that is getting in the way of connection
My Values as a Therapist
-I believe that people have good intentions and are doing the best that they can in the moment -I am present focused and have a collaborative and direct approach -I support all types of relationships including polyamory, ethical non-monogamy, monogamy and more -I am kink/fetish/BDSM affirming -I am body positive -I believe in CONSENT https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pZwvrxVavnQ -I believe in remaining open-minded and curious

name: Krista Schimmelbusch
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I primarily employ Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT) and Restoration Therapy (RT) techniques. EFT has proven the most effective success rate: 90% of couples who engage in EFT significantly improve their relationship and 70-75% of such couples no longer meet criteria for relationship distress after treatment. I am EMDR trained and regularly incorporate EMDR in my practice as desired by the client. Shame and trauma can keep us stuck in negative cycles of thought and behavior. I incorporate RT to address identity and safety wounds that feed into negative cycles and keep us disconnected from ourselves and others.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe in the innate power of an individual to grow and heal given the right conditions. An accepting, caring, warm and safe atmosphere helps my clients feel supported as they work through challenges and build satisfying relationships.

name: Katheryne Lewis (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a Licensed Mental Health Counselor in Washington (MHC.LH.61475994) and a Professional Counselor Associate in Oregon (R7357). My practice is oriented to Narrative Theory and Ecopsychology. Celebrating each individual’s uniqueness, I aim to help clients understand their stories within a larger sociocultural context while encouraging them to make room for meaningful narratives. Narrative theory emphasizes that our stories are not only our own, but are comprised of the narratives that society may have of us. It is important to embolden the voice of the client as they work to rewrite the storylines of their identity. I work to help clients create meaning while also acknowledging and holding the larger systems of oppression accountable for the barriers they continuously uphold. Additionally, I pull components from a variety of modalities and currently am in the process to become a certified ADHD clinical services provider.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
With a background in environmental conservation and currently in the process of re-indigenizing my understanding of conservation, I hold true that in order for our ecosystems to thrive, it embraces all that is queer, diverse, strange, unique, beautiful, and mystifying. The fact is, there is nothing queerer than nature. Imagine the thriving community we could cultivate if we choose to use nature as a mirror and model for success. As a queer Black woman and a clinician, my goal is to help my clients understand their unique existence in this greater ecosystem, ready to be embraced for all that they are. These identifies inform how I show up in the world and have always been affirmed in how I relate to our natural spaces. While that may not be everyone's cup of tea, it grounds me in my values and allows me to relate to others in the human and beyond human world.

name: Megan Sapp (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I integrate education, transparency, shared decision-making, and humor when meeting with clients. While I offer med management, I believe that meds are only one of the available tools, as behavioral and lifestyle changes are important for sustainable well-being. I offer med management with or without brief therapy but cannot offer stand-alone weekly therapy.

name: Christine Titus (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I work with adults (ages 18+ only) on a broad selection of mental health concerns including depression, anxiety, grief & loss, adjusting to life changes, and stress management. I have a special focus on pregnancy and postpartum mood and anxiety support. My treatment approach is holistic, meaning I treat the whole person and view the mind-body connection as important to our overall well-being. As a PMHNP, I have been trained to understand how genetic, developmental, biological, environmental, and psychosocial influences can significantly impact our health. As a therapist, I utilize a combination of therapeutic theories and techniques to help meet your individual needs. I often incorporate elements of cognitive-behavioral, psychodynamic, supportive, and interpersonal therapy techniques into our sessions. In addition to therapy, medication can be beneficial and we can discuss whether this option may be the best choice for you.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I received my Master's degree in Nursing after completing the Psychiatric Mental Health Nurse Practitioner program at Oregon Health & Sciences University. I am board certified by the American Nurses Credentialing Center and have been licensed and practicing since 2011. Prior to returning to graduate school, I worked as a Labor & Delivery RN caring for high-risk obstetric patients and their families. I have worked to unite my passion for reproductive health and mental health and have focused on the specialty of reproductive psychiatry since beginning my practice. I am passionate about supporting new parents and their families through the potential challenges experienced before, during, and after pregnancy and other reproductive life cycle changes.

name: Danielle Solari (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Everyone has the potential to grow and thrive, and my approach to therapy centers on empowering clients to develop the skills and insights necessary to access the changes they want to see. Through our work together you can expect to gain a deeper understanding of yourself and your needs, practical tools for managing difficult memories and current stressors, identify your strengths, and be provided with a supportive space to explore all the parts of you. My sincere hope for you is that therapy can help you reach your goals, increase your use of tools and resources, and ultimately stop coming to therapy!

name: Ally Willis (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I hold a Bachelor’s degree in Business from Western Oregon University and a Master of Arts in Marriage and Family Therapy from Syracuse University. I am dedicated to co-creating a warm, safe, and affirming environment where you can explore your deepest emotions, build hope, and tap into your inherent resilience. I place great emphasis on building rapport with clients and fostering a supportive relationship that is conducive to the healing process. I frequently draw from solution-focused therapy, recognizing that your individual and familial strengths hold the key to resolving the challenges you face. You are inherently resilient, and my role is to help you remember and harness your strengths and self-worth. I look forward to walking beside you as you navigate life's complexities and work towards your goals and aspirations. Together, we will create a path toward a more fulfilling and empowered future.
My Approach to Helping
In addition to these core values, I integrate evidence-based practices and tailor my approach to meet your unique needs. By incorporating mindfulness techniques, cognitive-behavioral strategies, and other therapeutic modalities, I aim to provide a well-rounded support system that enhances your overall well-being.

name: Miya Tischler (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My relational-cultural (RCT) and attachment-focused lenses are multiculturally inclusive, trauma-informed, body positive, LGBTQIA+, poly-supportive, and honor neurodiverse ways of thinking and being. This is combined with a nervous-system informed somatic approach to working with trauma. I also have training in non-directive and directive play therapy, Lifespan Integration, polyvagal-informed yoga, breath-based stress reduction, and TF-CBT.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My path toward becoming a therapist has taken me around the world, delving with humility into multicultural wisdom while returning again and again to the question of how healing happens, and what is really broken. This path has made it impossible to ignore how deeply culture-bound our struggles are and how pervasively impactful our culture is on the self-talk that we do. I support my clients in questioning thought patterns that are not in the best interest of their growth and uncovering parts that need to be heard for them to grow into the experience of wholeness.

name: Caitlin Gibb (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I have a Master's degree in Social Work from Columbia University as well as a Master's in Counseling Psychology. I completed an in-depth teacher training in Mindful Self Compassion with UC San Diego's Center for Mindfulness and participate in ongoing trainings in clinical application of mindfulness practices with UC Berkeley's Greater Good Science Center. I am an avid reader and lifelong learner and am committed to deepening and sharpening my skills and therapeutic presence on an ongoing basis. I draw from interpersonal neurobiology, Buddhist psychology, internal family systems, psychodynamic and modern attachment theory, among others. Our relationship will provide a foundation for growth outside of our sessions, creating an experiential roadmap for safe, authentic connection. I have been in private practice since 2020.
My Approach to Helping
Together, we will cultivate kindness and curiosity for your experiences, thoughts, and emotions. Self-exploration and honest feedback with an attuned listener can strengthen our capacity to be in fulfilling, authentic relationship with ourself and others. As you are ready, we can explore new narratives & develop practices that alleviate anxiety and depression, creating more space for acceptance, ease, and joy. I enjoy finding humor with my clients, and my style often moves between in depth explorations of the psyche to discussing practical strategies for nervous system regulation and management of distressing symptoms. Above all, I hope to bring gentle curiosity and compassion to the truly complex journey of being human.

name: Susan Gulka (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a Marriage and Family counselor, I provide professional mental health services to individuals and couples. I approach counseling as a joint endeavor in which you, as the client, and I work together to understand your goals, uncover and approach difficulties, and move towards your healthy functioning and personal fulfillment. I believe that inside each of us is a unique human being that has the resources for positive growth. Often our families of origin have influenced us in numerous ways the have created various ways of coping, some healthy and some not so healthy. Therefore, it is my goal with each client, couple, or family to tap into those inner resources of each individual and discover their unique feelings and perspective in order to create healthier and more effective ways of living, loving, and communicating in their lives. I use emotionally focused approaches including developmental models, along with Internal Family Systems approaches to therapy.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a Masters of Arts in Marriage, Couple, and Family Counseling from George Fox University. I am a member of the Oregon Counseling Association. I have worked as a Life Coach, and have a background in accounting and finance. Please check out my website to learn more about me and my approach.

name: Victoria Solon (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Originally trained as a psychotherapist in Brazil, I bring a multicultural lens to my work. I’m currently pursuing my MA in Marriage, Couple, and Family Therapy with a specialization in sexuality. My therapeutic approach is grounded in collaborative-dialogical and narrative practices, influenced by postmodern and social constructionist ideas. In my work, clients are the experts in their lives and the therapist is the expert in the process of doing therapy. I maintain a position of “not-knowing”—being genuinely curious and non-judgmental—by exploring possible routes in therapy in partnership with my clients. This allows me to cultivate a therapeutic relationship built on clients’ autonomy, with openness, collaboration and respect, creating a space where clients feel safe and supported to re-author their lives and embrace transformation.
My Values as a Therapist
As a queer, neurodivergent, and Latina therapist-in-training, I bring lived experience and deep care to my work. I approach therapy through a socially just lens, honoring the ways identity, culture, and systemic forces shape our lives. I strive to create an affirming space where clients — especially those from marginalized or underrepresented communities — feel seen, heard, and supported. My work is grounded in curiosity, collaboration, and a commitment to deconstructing dominant narratives. I believe healing happens in relationship, and I am dedicated to walking alongside clients as we open new possibilities for meaning, reclaim their voice, and move toward the life they aspire.

name: Ariana Ames
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
At the heart of my approach lies my belief that a sense of authenticity, choice, and belonging allows people to live full and meaningful lives. I develop an integrative and tailored approach to help individuals recognize their inherent strengths and realize their desired outcomes through the use of Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT), Mindfulness-Based and Compassion-Focused Therapies, Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) skills, EMDR, Attachment and Family Systems perspectives, Psychedelic Integration, and Humanistic-Existential Theories. I support individuals to arrive at their goals through a process of self-reflection to reveal unconscious beliefs, free oneself from unhelpful habitual patterns, repair past wounds, access innate potential for growth and enhance connections with others.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have 15+ years of progressive experience in mental health care in outpatient, community-based and school settings working with a wide variety of people of all ages from diverse backgrounds. I value cultural humility and strive to provide a welcoming and affirming space for individuals who identify with marginalized communities. I am a Licensed Clinical Social Worker in the states of Oregon and California and earned my Master’s Degree in Social Welfare (MSW) from the University of California, Berkeley.

name: Anya Valsamakis (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Each individual deserves therapy customized for them; the ‘right therapy’, is the one that empowers you to be the self you long to be; Trust is built on authenticity and honesty—you can expect that your openness will be reciprocated. My other core values include: Confidentiality – I create a safe environment where confidentiality is paramount. Your privacy will always be recognized and protected. Compassion – When you take a risk to disclose your concerns and open your life to a stranger I recognize your courage. I will treat you with respect and care. Equality – Your history and cultural identity is always present and makes you who you are; I will strive to understand your values and will always treat you with equality and dignity. Comprehensive Care – People are multifaceted and embody a variety of experiences and needs whether physical, emotional, mental, intellectual, relational and spiritual needs. My aim is keep all these needs in mind.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have both a Bachelor’s Degree a Master’s Degree (MSW) in social work, and I am licensed as a clinical social worker (LCSW). I have studied mediation and have had years of education at the Summer Institute of Intercultural Communication. More recently, I have acquired skills in the somatic, body-mind area of counseling: NeuroAffective Touch and Rubenfeld Synergy. These methods integrate conscious insights with unconscious discoveries that are communicated by the body when encouraged by awareness and gentle touch. I believe in this powerful type of treatment and see it as an adjunct to regular counseling work. For more information, please visit my web site at http://www.pravdacounseling.com

name: Yulia Tsarenko Carey (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In my approach to helping, I create a safe, culturally sensitive space where clients lead their mental health journey. Clients actively participate, and we build a roadmap to navigate challenges together. My stance encourages open exploration of thoughts and feelings. I'm dedicated to empowering clients to address unique mental health challenges. I specialize in helping clients transform challenges into strengths. For athletes, I address the psychological aspects of injury recovery and performance anxiety using evidence-based approaches. For non-athletes, I offer trauma-informed care and practical strategies for anxiety, depression, and emotional regulation. My approach is direct and collaborative. Collaboration is at the core of my therapeutic philosophy. I see my clients as active participants in their healing process.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My personal beliefs in therapy are grounded in the core values of empathy, collaboration, and respect for each individual's unique journey. I firmly believe that every person holds the key to their own well-being and is the expert of their own life. My role as a mental health professional is to provide a safe and supportive environment where clients can explore their thoughts, emotions, and behaviors without fear of judgment. Cultural sensitivity is a cornerstone of my practice. As an immigrant myself, I recognize the profound influence of cultural backgrounds on mental health. This awareness informs my integrative approach, where therapy is tailored to meet each client's specific needs and preferences. I see diversity as an asset and celebrate its richness in the therapeutic process.

name: Cameron Donley (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My goal as a therapist is to support your individual freedom - to help you become more fully yourself. I see you as an individual with your own unique experience and story, and your own path towards growth and healing. As a result, I do not have a "cookie cutter" approach to therapy. Instead, I blend several different approaches in my work to meet you where you are at and support you in your process. I am a trauma-informed therapist, and I implement a blend of EMDR, somatic work, mindfulness, and internal family systems/parts work to help clients process their trauma and unwind distressing patterns.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a Jungian lens, and my therapeutic perspective is deeply informed by feminism, social justice, and liberation psychology. My understanding of quarterlife is heavily influenced by the work of Satya Doyle Byock (who views quarterlife as a developmental stage of adulthood). My therapeutic approach is whole-hearted, fully engaged, and collaborative. My intention is to honor your path, and create a safe space in which you can be more fully yourself.

name: Linda Berlingen (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
I specialize in existensial therapy and Rogerian Therapy, assisting clients in finding meaning in life experiences while be unconditionally positively regarded.
Why We are a Good Fit for You
Sometimes we need a caring person, skilled at listening, who will act as a mirror, reflecting back our deepest knowing and personal truth. The act of entrusting another in this manner helps us to realign with our wisest self. We begin feeling more at ease and clearer about our intentions. Choices begin to reflect who we really want to be in the world. Suddenly, we are noticing and appreciating what is right and joyful in life more of the time.

name: Benné Gulick (HE/SHE/THEY/ALL)
approaches:
How I Can Help You
Let's explore your unique identity, coming to a better understanding of who you are. I'd like to help you unconditionally embrace and accept yourself. Therapy can bring up a lot of feelings, but they don't have to be overwhelming or scary. We can build a therapeutic relationship that supports you in meeting your goals in life. I hope to accompany you in your growth and healing as you laugh, cry, learn, explore, set goals, and gain skills along the way. I employ techniques from Motivational Interviewing, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Internal Family Systems, and Dialectical Behavioral Therapy. This can include education, self-exploration, goal-setting, coping strategies, and more.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
I believe that all people are worthy and valuable. I identify as mixed-race Asian-American and have been an active member of the Asian American Psychological Association since 2020. I am also a proud member of the Queer community. I am open to working with all adults, but particularly enjoy helping others in the AAPI and LGTBQIA2S+ communities. It's important to me that we elevate marginalized communities. #BLM #StopAsianHate

name: Galina Arintcina (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
You don’t have to carry this weight alone; together, we can move you forward. As a Registered Associate Counselor, I provide practical, evidence-based therapy for clients in Oregon via telehealth. My approach is direct yet supportive, grounded in EMDR and Internal Family Systems (IFS), both highly effective, research-backed methods. With these tools, we can work through the pain and help you build resilience, self-worth, and expand your toolkit for navigating life with greater ease and confidence. Imagine facing challenges from a place of strength and self-compassion, knowing you are loved and worthy just as you are. Even in the hard moments, you’ll feel equipped to handle what comes your way, trusting your capacity to move forward with peace and purpose. I’m here to support you every step of the way.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
Therapy is not one-size-fits-all; there are many approaches to understanding and supporting change. Some focus on behavior alone, offering new patterns to follow. While this can be helpful, I’ve seen how challenging it is for people to simply “change” without addressing the deeper reasons behind their actions. My approach recognizes that every behavior has a purpose, even when it’s not serving you in the way you’d hope. Instead of just focusing on what to change, we’ll explore why those behaviors exist, uncovering the underlying needs, emotions, or beliefs driving them. Together, we’ll identify alternative choices that feel aligned with your values and goals, keeping your integrity and individuality at the center of our work. This way, change becomes not just achievable but sustainable, empowering you to move forward in a way that feels authentic and meaningful.

name: Jonathan Chrystal
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My style of therapy is oriented from a Gestalt perspective, which emphasizes your unique individual experience, attention to the present moment, and focuses on bringing holistic awareness to many aspects of your life. Oftentimes, this awareness will bring understanding, and understanding can enable you to make the changes you want. I also incorporate contemporary Reichian somatic treatment, which can build insight into the full range of our lived experience. I believe that therapy helps us expand our options, make connections, and enable us to lead more fulfilling and satisfying lives.
My Approach to Helping
I work with adults 18 years and older in individual therapy. Many of my clients seek therapy due to anxiety, depression, relationship issues, recent and past traumatic experiences, difficulties adjusting to stressors, and challenging life transitions. I am LGBTQ+ affirming.

name: Raquel Mayer (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
As a therapist, I use a client-centered, trauma-informed, interpersonal, collaborative and transparent approach. I have experience in supporting clients with histories of complex and oppression-based trauma, domestic and sexual violence, and substance use. I am passionate about working with folks around body acceptance and/or LGBTQ+ identities and experiences. I approach clients with compassion, empathy, and unconditional positive regard, providing them with support and validation while helping them to meet their therapeutic goals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My journey as a therapist began with my experience as a client. Starting therapy, I didn’t know what to expect, but I left sessions feeling affirmed and supported. Through the powerful therapeutic relationship, I learned to have more compassion for myself and in turn, built my capacity to have more compassion for others. Working with a therapist changed me in profound ways and inspired my pursuit to provide counseling for others. I hold a master's degree in Professional Mental Health Counseling from Lewis & Clark College and a bachelor's degree in Psychology and Women's & Gender Studies from Sonoma State University, providing the foundation of my interest and knowledge in social justice and counseling. I am a white, queer, fat therapist with an awareness of how social identities impact life experiences and the therapeutic relationship. My hope is to hold space and offer tools along the way that help to build upon your unique identities and strengths.

name: Alicia Pasik (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I have specialized training in helping adults with disabilities and mental illness. Additionally, my undergraduate degree focused on expressive arts therapy, which informs my practice today. I believe that incorporating imagery and art can facilitate a deeper understanding of oneself, as well as get to the core issue a lot faster than talking can do. Sometimes, there aren't words, and that is ok. As a licensed counselor in the state of Oregon, I am required to complete ongoing education annually. My previous role in an inpatient crisis center included extensive trauma counseling, for which I saw first hand how powerful of a tool the sand tray can be in helping process and integrate trauma. I understand that some folks are not interested in working with this modality, and I always go at the pace of the client. Some folks will go through years of therapy without ever touching the sand, and that is ok.
My Values as a Therapist
The research shows that the biggest driving force for change is the therapeutic relationship. Thus, I prioritize our counseling relationship first and foremost. This includes having unconditional positive regard for you, as well as believing in your strengths and abilities. This also includes challenging you to see your own blind spots, or how you may be getting in your own way sometimes. I value authenticity, and have a warm, steady presence. Together, with our relationship as the safety net, we can dig deeper into what needs to be uncovered for your healing journey. I value uncovering all parts of ourselves, especially those parts that we may deem "bad" or "ugly" and having compassion for those parts.

name: Carly Henderson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My practice is guided by the belief that every adult and child strives to do the best they can. The individual or family system may need assistance in developing necessary skills and guidance in solving problems. Working collaboratively with clients I help them to narrate new ways of interacting with their environment.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I was born and raised in Oregon, and have spent several years overseas in the UK, Europe, and Africa. I have Master's degrees from Lewis & Clark College in Counseling Psychology and from University of Cambridge in Social and Developmental Psychology. I completed my undergraduate work in Psychology, also at Lewis & Clark in Portland. I have worked as a counselor since 2002, first as a Professional School Counselor in the US and overseas, and then in the private sector since 2011. Additionally, I spent two years working as a Behavior Consultant for children in a large public school district in Oregon. I have additional training in Interpersonal Neurobiology, EMDR, Somatic therapy, developmental/attachment trauma, Collaborative Problem Solving, and Positive Behavior Interventions and Supports.

name: Joey Tomlinson (THEY/HE)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I draw from narrative therapy and humanism in my work. Narrative therapy is all about your stories: the ones you’ve been told, and the ones that are yours to tell. I’ll work with you to understand and reimagine your stories, where they come from, what they mean, and what new stories might be possible. I bring humanism into this work by embracing you as a whole, messy person. I’ll offer empathy and reflection that celebrates your humanity and your innate capacity for growth, healing and connection. I’ll invite you to engage somatically, emotionally and relationally with feelings that come up in the moment and I’ll support you through the tender process of being with your radically authentic self.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe that our emotional and relational wellbeing is deeply entwined with our communities and social systems, and I know that most suffering in this world is a product of systemic oppression. My goal as a therapist is to support you as an individual while also building our collective capacity to resist oppression and cultivate liberation for all people. I draw from lineages and theories of abolitionism, disability justice, queer feminism and other resistance movements in this work. I’m genderqueer, non-monogamous, live with chronic illness, and love being outdoors and growing food. I’m sex positive, disability affirming, neurodiversity affirming, and believe that all people deserve dignity, freedom and to have their basic needs met.

name: Jonathan E Scarboro (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Sacrificing who we actually are in pursuit of who we think we should be can lead to alienation from ourselves, emotional distance from others, and a variety of behaviors in conflict with our actual goals and values. In some cases, parts of us may split off and become hidden beneath our awareness. Excavating and reintegrating these lost or buried aspects of ourselves can be a critical step towards overall well-being, and is crucial for our higher functions. I believe that forming deep connections with other people while remaining true to ourselves is essential to the changing and healing process. I will model this throughout our time together by bringing my authentic self into each session, talking with you frankly and openly, and looking for ways to draw out your fullness and complexity. My most important job as a counselor is to create a safe container for care and challenge to build upon each other at a pace that feels approachable to you.
Why I Became a Therapist
Perhaps you’re having trouble connecting with other people or you’re doing self-destructive things but can’t understand why. Or maybe you’re numb to both pleasure and pain like you’re living life on autopilot. Or you have no idea what direction you’re headed in and no clue how to know what makes sense for you. All of these were true for me when I first started seeing a counselor during a stressful period involving the near-simultaneous dissolution of my marriage and business partnership. Though I initially resisted the idea of being in therapy, I eventually came to see it as a powerful gift. It now brings me great joy to offer this gift in turn. My clinical approach to creative recovery and unblocking is informed by my counselor training and my experience as a visual artist. For what it’s worth, I hold a Bachelor of Fine Arts, co-founded an interdisciplinary artist collective, worked at a major museum in Manhattan, and served as an arts programming director.

name: Health Allies Counseling
approaches:
How We Can Help You
We have a full team of staff available to help you address issues related to narcissistic abuse, domestic violence, PTSD and trauma. Each team member brings unique experience and skills to these issues - from using somatic solutions such as EMDR and art therapy, to individuals trained in parenting, family or child therapy, CBT, DBT, and more.
Our Values as Mental Health Practitioners
We operate from a social justice lens and stand with People of Color, immigrants and the LGBTQ2SIA+ community. We consider ourselves a trans-affirming, anti-racist, and progressive leaning practice. We are committed to providing an inclusive and safe space for healing.

name: Emma Larson (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My style is eclectic and anchored in self-exploration, cultural awareness, and self-compassion. I draw from frameworks like IFS, Feminist, and Existential Therapy that explore how our different parts intersect and how the world around us shapes our reality, values, and relationships. Therapy with me highlights authenticity, depth, and laughter. I earned my Master’s in Counseling from Lewis & Clark College. Outside of therapy, you’ll likely find me knitting on the couch with a cat (or two) in my lap, catching a local drag show, or chasing a sunbeam to sit in.

name: Matthew McCullough (HE/HIS, THEY/THEIR)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach to counseling centers around identifying the things that matter the most to you and the strengths and abilities you’ve used to survive in this life so far, creating a safe space to look more closely at the difficult areas of life that don’t appear to have easy answers. I see therapy as a healing and creative process, where my training and knowledge act as a mirror to help you unlock your own expertise on yourself. I have over five years of outpatient counseling experience, including individual counseling and group therapy facilitation. My experience includes mental health counseling, substance abuse treatment and process addiction treatment (gambling, technology, etc.), and crisis management and intervention. I ascribe to a humanistic-existentialist philosophy and utilize Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, Solution-Focused Therapy, and Internal Family Systems. I also incorporate mindfulness and somatic-based skills from a neuroscience-informed basis.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My practice is affirming to all people, with particular awareness of the unique ways religion and spirituality have impacted LGBTQ people. I am familiar with the particular concerns transgender individuals face, and the transitioning process. I am open to clients who practice diverse relationships styles (polyamory, kink/BDSM). I strive to maintain cultural humility, both by remaining active in my education around diverse populations, and by being open to and welcoming critical feedback.

name: Elissa Barbee (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I utilize an integrative model drawing from components in Mindful Self-Compassion, Person-Centered therapy and Motivational Interviewing. I tend to work through an attachment lens and often utilize somatic interventions and 'parts' work. I support Harm Reduction practices where it is the appropriate fit for the client. As a dual-diagnosis counselor I am skilled in providing an effective healing space for those affected by mental health concerns, addiction, and trauma and am proficient in working with health care professionals.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My background and training as a dancer has guided my intrigue in the connection of the mind and body, particularly how we experience, store and express memories & emotions in the body. Through our work together we will explore this phenomenon and increase your awareness of bodily communications and how to utilize them to support your holistic well-being. I am a Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC) with the Oregon Board of Licensed Professional Counselors and Therapists. I also hold a Certification in Alcohol and Drug Counseling (CADC I) through the Addiction Counselor Certification Board of Oregon. I graduated from Lewis & Clark’s Professional Mental Health and Addiction’s Masters program and have been working in a variety of different settings ranging from residential to private practice since.

name: Phil Small (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Throughout more than a decade of dedicated clinical training, I learned to treat, guide, and support emerging adults and mid-life adults through understanding and coping with their specific circumstances and mental health concerns. I earned my PhD by valuing both clinical work as well as research -- a combination that led to me providing high quality, evidence-based care. I offer a warm, conversational therapy experience that is rooted in cognitive-behavioral and interpersonal practices. Treatment is structured into three phases: diagnosis and treatment, maintenance, and check-ups. This structure offers a road map to aid with expectations for your care, and will help support you moving beyond the concerns that have held you back.
Social Justice Issues I Care About
I am a life-long learner, and am committed to challenging myself and my beliefs every day. As such, I believe black lives matter, trans rights are human rights, and that everyone should have access to quality care. I have led trainings for other clinicians about how to work with LGBTQIA+ populations and how to provide gender affirming care. Additionally, I have volunteered at a 24-hour suicide phone center, which helped fuel my enthusiasm and commitment to supporting the mental health needs of anyone who needs it.

name: Jessica Besner (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I am a Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC) with over seven years of experience and a background in clinical psychology. I offer psychotherapy for healing from developmental trauma and attachment wounding from a holistic, feminist, and resilience-based lens, using Internal Family Systems (IFS, aka parts work), Dialectical-Behavior Therapy (DBT), and both mindfulness-based and somatic approaches. I have years of experience diagnosing and treating ADHD, and am currently offering ADHD evaluations in a limited capacity. I am CURRENTLY OPEN TO CLIENTS SEEKING Ketamine-Assisted Therapy (KAP) and PSYCHEDELIC INTEGRATION therapy. I am also currently enrolled in a Somatic Attachment Therapy Certificate program, and recently completed a certificate in Integrative Somatic Trauma Therapy. Past completed trainings include Internal Family Systems and Psychedelic Integration. I completed my doctoral internship at PSU, and hold a master’s degree in Clinical Psychology from Antioch University.
Why I Became a Therapist
Growing up as a deeply sensitive, neuro-divergent child in an invalidating environment, I know what it’s like to experience abandonment and feel like there is something wrong with you. I understand how difficult it can be to be open and vulnerable when your experiences and emotions have not been responded to adequately, resulting in attachment wounding. It is my belief that we all have the capacity for secure attachment, and that all healing occurs through safe connection with self, one another, and with nature. Being human can be both deeply painful and joyful, and I am here to hold space for it all. My own personal healing journey was propelled by spirituality, yoga, and psychedelics. I believe in the power of psychedelics and plant medicine as a pathway to healing for some, and am here to walk with you and guide you on this path, should you choose. My values and approach: - Heart-centered - Strengths-based/feminist - Cultural humility, anti-racist -LGBTQ+ affirming -HAES

name: Katie Kilbourn (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
In our work together, we'll focus on creating a safe, trusting, open relationship with one another. I use a highly relational approach in my work, because this is where the healing happens! I work from a compassionate, anti-oppressive, and evidence-based approach, recognizing that you are the expert in your own experience. We'll utilize techniques from a range of therapeutic methods like AEDP, Polyvagal Theory, CBT, Motivational Interviewing, Harm Reduction, and DBT. Therapy with me is a place where all emotions are welcome. We’ll explore tough feelings and memories, but we’ll also likely share some laughs along the way! Life is complex, and I’m here to support you as we navigate it together.
Why I Became a Therapist
I’ve worked with adults across all stages of life, and I truly believe there’s always room to grow and learn more about yourself! In my own history, I've navigated anxiety, depression, grief, sexuality, gender, and PTSD. One of the most supportive forces in my own healing and learning has been working with compassionate, warm, understanding therapists. (Shout out to all the amazing therapists I've had!) I entered this career field in the hopes that I could help others the way that I've received help, but I've stayed because this work of human connection and healing is so deeply beautiful and meaningful. This is a hard time to be a person, in a world that's already hard! If you need a therapist who understands that, and will support you without telling you to "logic your way out of it", then I might be your gal/they :)

name: Caroline Kinsley (SHE/HER)
approaches:
What I Specialize In
My work experience includes working with individuals over the past 5 years managing depression, anxiety, relational trauma, perfectionism, people pleasing, and difficult transitions. I approach these experiences with a variety of evidence-based lenses, adjusting to what best meets the needs of my clients, including dance/movement therapy, psychodynamic/psychoanalytic theory, internal family systems therapy, trauma informed practices, mindful self compassion, and mindfulness based cognitive behavioral therapy. I’ve worked with adolescents and adults in a variety of settings, including private practice, school-based, community mental health, and partial hospitalization. Just as I encourage my clients to do, I continue to seek opportunities to learn, and discover new approaches and ways of moving through my therapeutic practice.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My personal values are relationship building, promoting integration, and exploring through movement. I love understanding, supporting, and relating with people. I find the more we join, the more we know and discover common interests and humility. As something I value, I hope to share this very same with others as well. My value of integrating the body and mind throughout the whole therapeutic process is reflective in my life as well. A large part of what I love to do involves movement. I’m a dancer, yogi, skier, backpacker, and whitewater kayaker. These activities fill my cup-they allow me to move through wild places with others in many different ways. Being a member of these communities allows me to spend time with others in embodied and authentic ways.

name: Liz Ward (THEY/THEM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Therapy is a place where a person is provided the space to create change and growth in their emotional and mental wellbeing, which with time, can extend to other areas of their life. I believe that this is possible through fostering a therapeutic relationship where one feels safe to explore all the pieces of themself. As a therapist, I believe in the connection between our bodies and minds, and in allowing new experiences to propel growth. My work is grounded in queer theory, awareness of systemic injustice, and trauma-informed care. In the practice of therapy, I lean into somatic practices, mindfulness, emotions, and experiential components. I am DBT Trained. While I use some elements of DBT when appropriate, I also draw from other modes of therapy such as Emotionally Focused Therapy, Somatic Psychotherapy, Narrative Therapy, and Acceptance and Commitment therapy.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
As a non-binary, queer, non-monogamous, white, AuDHD, and invisibly disabled person, I aim to understand all the parts of your identity and how they may show up in your life. I seek to understand you as best as possible knowing that you are a unique person with a depth of life experience that has led you to where you are today. My best work is done collaboratively, supporting you in achieving the outcomes you want to see by bringing my knowledge to the room. My goal is to empower you to build and maintain the life that you want to be living. If you are interested in working with me, feel free to reach out at the contact form below. I offer free 20-minute consultations so we can determine if we'd be a good fit to begin therapy.

name: Cameron Panfely (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe that everyone is the expert of their own experience, and as your therapist, I am here to help you explore your experiences and achieve your goals in a way that makes sense to you. Our experiences shape us, and sometimes we lose sight of our worth and values, leading us to feel bad about, or distant from who we are. I want to take the time to really get to know you and understand your life experiences in a way that appreciates where you come from, and how you see the world. I view counseling as a collaborative process in which we will work together to address discomforts and focus on areas you want to grow in, or your goals and aspirations. I want to help you address the roots of your issues, while collaboratively developing solutions. It is my goal to provide you with both a safe, comfortable, and non-judgmental environment, and a genuine, positive, and empathetic relationship to work through these things.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
Beyond anything else, this process is about feeling comfortable with your therapist, and that is where my focus lies. You can expect my full attention, intention, and engagement at every step of this process. My emphasis is on provide an empathetic, trauma-informed, and non-judgemental space that is conducive to exploration and healing. While I utilize parts of several theoretical frameworks (IFS, PCT, CBT, ACT, DBT, etc.), I work with my clients to develop a uniquely tailored and collaborative approach that emphasizes their individual needs, goals, and strengths. I take OHP insurance, excluding Care Oregon Advantage, Health Share of Oregon, Jackson Care Connect, and Coluumbia Pacific CCO. To ensure that my services are kept affordable I offer a sliding-scale pricing model. I'm here to support you and believe in your ability to grow. Seeking counseling is exceptionally brave, and I hope to be alongside you in your journey.

name: Reid Zweifel (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
Originally from the red rock country of Southern Utah, I completed my undergraduate and medical school training in Salt Lake City. Following medical school, I completed my psychiatry residency at Oregon Health & Science University (OHSU), where I also served as a chief resident. After training in Utah and Oregon, I’ve made Portland my home and now practice privately in the community.
My Approach to Helping
I strive to offer thoughtful, individualized care through both medication management and psychodynamic psychotherapy. I work collaboratively with each person to understand the deeper meaning behind their symptoms, life experiences, and emotional patterns—while also providing evidence-based treatment to support symptom relief and long-term growth. I believe mental health and illness hold different meanings for each individual, and I strive to create a safe, respectful space where those meanings can be explored. My practice is grounded in curiosity, compassion, and a deep respect for each person’s unique story.

name: Adam Von Passow (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Background
I have 24 years of experience working with diverse populations young and old. I have worked with people to help them move through substance abuse, mental health issues, and coping with loss/grief. For the last 15 years, I have been working in hospice care, helping families transition through this life stage and related grief experience. While getting my master’s degree in social work, I was fortunate to have a great mentor who taught me about EMDR and hypnotherapy. EMDR is a specialized form of psychotherapy that uses eye movements to reprocess and reintegrate traumatic experiences. When we reprocess our trauma we can change how it affects our mind, body, emotions, and behavior. This is an elegant and powerful form of healing that I incorporate into my practice.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
When I am not working I feel it’s important to connect with nature so I explore all the Pacific Northwest has to offer. I also love writing songs, playing music, and recording music. I also get into exercise, so I run, play basketball, and skateboard with my kids. I also practice moving energy with Qigong, Tai Chi, and Yoga, which I have done for the last 20 years.

name: Ren Fonbuena (SHE/THEY)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
Ren is passionate about working with people to live their best life possible. She identifies as queer and a part of the LGBTQ+ community and works to be an inclusive therapist including being a poly and kink positive clinician. Rens work is rooted in the belief of the inherent worth and respect for all persons. Ren started at community college and went on to earn a Bachelors of Liberal Studies for California State University Stanislaus. After teaching elementary and English as a forgein language, Ren went back to school for her Masters of Social Work from Walden University. She has worked in a variety of settings in social work and counseling.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Ren is a newcomer to Oregon (2020) and has embraced the Pacific Northwest lifestyle. When not playing with her three dogs, she is usually reading Jane Austen fanfic or playing Dungeons and Dragons with her spouse.

name: Quinn Bailey (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I strive to create a warm, non-judgmental space where you can explore your emotions and experiences from a place of compassion and authenticity, fostering a genuine connection and opening up new possibilities for a more fulfilling and joyful life. I practice with an integrative approach that combines contemporary psychoanalytic, existential, and narrative frameworks—exploring internalized beliefs, the realities of life, and empowering clients to find new ways of relating to them. I blend these theoretical insights with relational, person-centered, and somatic approaches to create a sense of safety and empowerment for my clients. I also utilize cognitive behavioral (CBT) approaches such as Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) and incorporate mindfulness-based practices in order to provide clients with tangible tools to help facilitate their own healing.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
We live in a culture designed to exploit rather than support our mental health. We are constantly inundated with products designed to capture our attention by entertaining, angering, frightening, and making us generally feel inadequate. No wonder so many of us are struggling! I believe that it is only by reckoning honestly with the ways in which our culture manufactures an insecurity that it then exploits, can we begin to explore the options that have in navigating this toxicity. As a counselor, I identify as a Buddhist and as an existentialist, in the least pretentious sense. To me, this means that I recognize and accept that life entails suffering, and believe we also have freedom in choosing how we respond to it. The most insidious thing about the erosion of our attention is the way in which it limits our freedom to to choose how we respond. My clinical approach focuses on exploring values and utilizing mindfulness-based approaches to help restore this foundational human freedom.

name: ANDREW HAUCK (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I treat ADHD, anger/abuse, and anxiety/depression by incorporating CBT, ACT, and IFS into mindfulness-based existential therapy. Practical skills and coping mechanisms only go so far... By exploring your past, discussing the human condition, and examining your hopes and fears, you can find meaning & purpose, resilience, and enduring peace of mind. In vulnerability there is a deep well of strength. With self-awareness and greater focus, we can take back control of our own minds, forgive ourselves, and heal relationships. By reuniting with the lost and damaged parts of ourselves, we find authenticity, what matters most to us... our true self is revealed!
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I believe we need to show some compassion to ourselves, because life is often difficult and there is no guidebook! But we can endure, thrive, and find real, lasting happiness with the right tools and support. Whatever is going on, whatever happened, you don't have to bear the weight alone. We can reexamine our values and create new meaning for ourselves. Everyone deserves a chance to become their authentic self, to feel belonging and acceptance, to feel known and understood. Additionally, I understand that growing up as a white male in the USA has left me with plenty of blind spots. I embrace diversity, equity and inclusion as necessary parts of a strong community. I am open to feedback and willing to admit when I've misunderstood. From our differences, we can learn from each other, and by acknowledging the past, we can go together toward a better future. No matter our background, we all deserve love, respect and an equal seat at a round table.

name: Jennifer Stratton (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is highly relational and aimed to provide deeper awareness of relationship--with self, with others and with your place in the world at large. Patterns and ways of being are habituated and strengthened over time and repetition of experiences--even emotional experiences. The tendency to avoid pain is a core, fundamental human drive. As humans, we come up with some pretty effective and brilliant ways to avoid pain. However, much of the pain that we strive to avoid is historical pain, with origins in our relational history--re-enacted in both conscious and unconscious ways, presently. My aim is to support your sense of agency and self-compassion--to attend to you and your pain in collaboration in the present. Together, we uncover inner resources and new opportunities to resolve pain rather than avoiding! We find resolve amidst changing circumstances, habituated patterns and outdated ways of being and beliefs of self. In safety and in relationship.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
In addition to my training to become a licensed professional counselor in the state of Oregon, I have several years of experience working with folks on the substance use disorder spectrum and their loved ones. I have worked across various levels of care from in-patient and partial hospitalization to out-patient. My ongoing studies include training in Attachment-Focused EMDR, Mindful Self- Compassion Teacher-Training, Hakomi and Recreation of Self Models, and attachment-based, relational therapy. Interpersonal neurobiology, attachment & development, trauma and early experiences of stress inform the way I conceptualize human struggles and the way I approach care. I currently offer counseling via telehealth video services only.

name: Ursula Hurley (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I believe people can change and I choose to see the good in everyone. I want to help you with your struggles past or present, to learn to stay present and to keep moving forward. I have trained in Cognitive Processing Therapy to treat trauma. I have worked at a partial hospital level of care in the treatment of eating disorders and have experience using Cognitive Behavioral Therapy and Motivational Interviewing. I can provide education to family, friends, partners and parents for ways they can support their loved ones.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have an M.S. in Marriage and Family Therapy from Lewis & Clark College, as well as B.A.'s in Human Development and Family Sciences, and Education from Oregon State University. I have experience working with preschoolers through adults, and in partial hospital and intensive outpatient treatment. My work now is to focus on the struggles with anorexia/restrictive eating, bulimia, binge eating, exercise addiction, body image, comparisons and self-esteem. My goal is to incorporate Health At Every Size, Body Trust and Intuitive Eating to take some of the pressure off and get your supports on board in ways that you will find helpful.

name: Jake H. Jacobsen (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach is eclectic including: Psychodynamic Psychotherapy, Attachment theory, Harm Reduction and Creative Arts Therapy. My practice experience includes counseling people with anxiety, PTSD/trauma (AIDS pandemic, 9/11, Covid) depression and other mood disorders, addiction, adult children of alcoholics, neurodivergent adults/youth, sexuality, gender identity (medical and/or social gender transitioning) for teens and adults who identify as transgender or non-binary and those who are questioning their gender identity, LGBTQ family/partners, people living with HIV/AIDS, and other life challenging conditions.
Why I Became a Therapist
I've been in the LGBTIA+ community since I was a teenager, a main factor that led me into the field of psychology. In the late 1970's luckily I had a therapist that did not pathologize my coming out as queer and that meant the world to me. It meant that I could begin to envision a full life as a queer person in an era when everything I saw on TV, heard at school, or read in the newspaper told me that being queer was a pathology. Today we're in a time of profound socio-political change with a dramatic increase in anti-LGBT+ bills/laws attempting to stop trans youth rights to access healthcare in some states, racism and violence against POC, erasure of black history from education, anti-semitism, banning books, the effects of climate change and fears of rising autocracy. These issues are all interconnected and need to be seen and acknowledged in order to do the work towards the fight for social justice and our civil rights. Addressing these issues in therapy is fundamental for healing.

name: Emily Shiraishi (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I help people change their relationship with anxiety and self-doubt by guiding them to address the roots of the problem. By focusing on the past and the present as well as working to uncover more of your emotions and needs, we can better understand how things got stuck and empower you to chart a new path forward.
Why I Became a Therapist
I long found that people felt safe confiding in me. I became a therapist because it felt natural to me. I also get inspired by the strength and wisdom of my patients as well as my peers and mentors. I feel fortunate to do this work; to help improve the quality of people's lives.

name: Savannah Auster (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background
I have experience working with teens and young adults navigating life transitions, developing adult responsibilities, managing anxiety, body image concerns, self-esteem, and coping with grief and loss. In our work together, I will utilize evidence-based practices such as Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) to help you explore the thoughts, feelings, and emotions that are influencing your life. As a teen transitioning into young adulthood, you’re at a pivotal moment in life filled with change, uncertainty, and overwhelming emotions. My goal is to support you through this process by helping you uncover your best self, gain clarity, build confidence, set goals that align with your values, and learn to accept and embrace all aspects of who you are.
My Approach to Helping
My approach to counseling is comforting, non-judgmental, and goal-oriented. The type of therapy that I emphasize is a process of becoming whole – assessing all aspects of you as an individual. I believe that people have the means to make positive change in their lives when awareness is reached, goals are created, and consuming thoughts are reduced. I also believe that this type of change can occur most rapidly with the support of an empathetic, committed, and compassionate clinician. Additionally, I recognize that your experiences are shaped by a variety of factors, including societal influences, cultural and family backgrounds, gender roles, and the dynamics of your current relationships. My goal is to listen attentively and with an open heart, honoring your unique perspective and accepting you fully for who you are.

name: Christa Loveland (SHE HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I am an LCSW and received my education from the University of Oregon and Portland State University I graduated from the Graduate School of Social Work at PSU and am licensed as an LCSW with the state of Oregon . I have worked in many settings including community mental health and non profits serving children and families . family therapy. I have more recently over the last five years worked with families in need of re unification and I have also worked with addictions and strenght based approchs to supporting folks in recovery and have a CADC licence . I have worked and advocated for my clients within multiple systems including DHS child welfare, family court, and victims servicies , I have worked with children and families that have experienced multiple layers abuse.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a personal belief in the human spirit and the power of the individual to heal. I strongly support survivors of all traumas and feel honored to be trusted as part of that healing process. Under the right circumstances I believe that trauma and be transformed in to creativity and healing. I have training in DBT, CBT, Psychoanalysis, Dream work and expressive therapies. I look forward to using my combination of experience education and passion for healing to help you transform your trauma and begin to live the life that you want to live.

name: Nicole Falk (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
For many of us, the hardest challenge to overcome is to ask for help. If you're reading this, you've already found the courage to start becoming the best version of yourself. While as an individual you are unique, none of us are perfect and we all experience conflict, hardships, and a variety of emotions. I believe our life experiences as well as cultural background influence the way in which we interact with the world. I am a strong proponent of collaboration and I view the therapeutic relationship as a team.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have a gentle approach to building relationships combined with down to earth 'real talk' about what can help you feel better. I often incorporate mindfulness techniques into our work together, and am someone who's open to feedback and learning about your unique perspective on the world. Please keep in mind I offer TELEHEALTH appointments only at this time, with daytime availability Monday through Thursday.

name: Jenna LeJeune (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
My work is guided by what science has shown to be most effective. I specialize in Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) and incorporate elements of Emotion Focused Therapy (EFT), evidence-based approaches that help people navigate life's challenges with greater flexibility. Therapy is a significant investment. To help you make the most of your investment, I work in a focused, time-limited model of up to 12 sessions centered on a particular struggle or theme. This approach allows us to dive deeply into making important changes in your life with clear direction and purpose. We can work together to identify unworkable patterns and develop concrete tools for lasting change. I also have extensive specialized training in psychedelic assisted therapy.
