I provide harm reduction and integration services for those exploring psychedelic journeys. I'm also a research therapist at Portland Psychotherapy, where I work on clinical trials investigating the therapeutic uses of MDMA and psychedelics.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
A fundamental belief in my practice: You are not broken. You are stuck. We all struggle, at times deeply struggle. And it's often during those times when it's most difficult to stay connected with what matters most. But often, it's finding a way to stay connected with our values and what truly matters, that enables us to navigate life's inevitable challenges with greater flexibility and purpose. As for what is most important to me and how I choose to be in this world, I seek to walk through this world open-heartedly. I bring this value of open-heartedness into every area of my life, including my work where I have deep care for the clients I serve. I also find value in the simplicity I find with being in nature, including going on long walks with my dog. I find that adding a bit of whimsy to everyday moments brings me joy. Finally, I love being curious and exploring other cultures and perspectives, especially through food and travel.

name: Dr. Sarah Kendrick (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Background and Approach
I’ve helped families cultivate post-traumatic growth, compassion, safety, connection, & resilience since 2002. I offer concierge-level support & carefully curated, accessible. short-term services with lasting transformation. I provide Therapy Intensives for rapid healing & resilience, Accelerated Resolution Therapy to transform sensations of suffering, Safe & Sound Listening Program for a calm, connected nervous system, Birth-Mapping for confident, safe, meaningful birth, and Postpartum & Parenting Support for compassionate grit to bear hard shit. My approach is rooted in a unique blend of clinical expertise and deep, personal understanding of the complexities of parenthood. I am a Certified Clinical Trauma Specialist (CCTS), Perinatal Mental Health provider (PMH-C), Master Accelerated Resolution Therapy provider, Meditation & Mindfulness teacher, Safe & Sound Protocol provider, and Childbirth Educator and bring highly specialized knowledge to guide clients from striving to thriving.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
“If we have no peace, it is because we have forgotten that we belong to each other” (Agnes Gonxha Bojaxhiu). At its heart, Toward Freedom is about reminding you that you’re not alone, crazy, broken, or disordered. That your symptoms make perfect sense and will change once you don’t need them anymore. That you are innately good, already whole, and worthy of belonging. That there’s no room for shame or time for hiding. That while our surroundings are increasingly unsafe or bewildering, we are inherently wired for safety & connection (and even flourishing!) Affective neuroscience offers us simple, gentle, innovative, & profoundly powerful ways to rewire & return to those states right in the middle of the chaos. Safe connection is the compass for all services. Other values include resisting duality, learning continually, affirming embodied wisdom, braving vulnerability, welcoming what is, and embracing ambiguity. Also- to transparency, a healthy dose of dark humor, & absolutely no bullshit

name: Amanda Roberson (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
My approach integrates several different modalities based on your preference and individualized needs. My primary therapy modality is EMDR due to it's efficacy in reducing physical, emotional, and cognitive distress as a result of past hurts. I also use CBT, DBT, ACT, and Mindfulness regularly in my practice.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
My background includes in depth work with survivors of domestic violence as well as longterm treatment of anxiety, depression, and trauma/stressor disorders. I have been in practice for 5 years and enjoy working with folks from all walks of life who are currently experiencing barriers to living their most fulfilling life. I am an expert cheerleader, accountability partner, and heart holder. Therapy with me is more than "just listening" and isn't about giving you unwanted advice. I seek to establish an authentic and deep connection built on trust, safety, and compassion. I look forward to meeting you!

name: Alex Bistroi
approaches:
How I Can Help You
Whether you’re struggling with depression or anxiety, are concerned about your life choices, or have difficulty finding joy, therapy can be a valuable tool towards self-discovery and positive change. My goal is to create a safe space in which you can be yourself without fear of judgment. I view the quality of the relationships I have with clients as crucial towards progress. I also aim to provide honest feedback to encourage exploration of painful emotions and memories which may have been obscured or hidden from awareness. I believe good therapy is a mixture of empathy, support, encouragement, and drive towards honest self-discovery. While I believe in the power of therapy, I try to not take myself too seriously and work to cultivate a sense of humility and fun about life in general.
My Approach to Helping
I am a licensed professional counselor (LPC) in Oregon. I have completed post-licensure training in EMDR, Emotion-Focused Therapy (EFT) for individuals and couples. I have a wide-range of experience working in crisis centers, addictions treatment, family therapy, and outpatient settings. I have a strong interest in working with clients who struggle with self-criticism and/or low self-worth.

name: Rhea Morais (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I genuinely care about each of my clients and believe that the therapeutic relationship can be a powerful tool to help facilitate growth and change. I work with clients collaboratively and approach our work through an integrative lens, meaning I don't believe in a one-size-fits-all approach to therapy and instead draw from a number of different therapeutic frameworks based on individual clients' needs. I primarily come from a depth-oriented approach and my work is influenced by existential-humanism, gestalt therapy, somatic therapy, and psychodynamic frameworks. What this looks like in practice is that I may bring attention to your emotions in the present moment, your felt sense of emotions in your body, exploration of your past and its influence on the present, exploration of what gives your life meaning, and your experience of what it's like to be you as a complex individual. I work with clients of all backgrounds and take into account the cultural contexts clients are coming from.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I aim to deeply listen to and understand my clients, and strive to create a safe and supportive environment where you are free to explore and heal from your pain with someone on your side. I believe humans are capable of growth, change, resilience, and tremendous strength and courage in the face of life's sufferings. I love empowering clients to find this within themselves as well as a sense of hope, joy, creativity, choice, meaning, connection to themselves and others, self-compassion, and authenticity.

name: Zachary Ash (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I've worked in the field of mental health for over 10 years, serving clients at every level from inpatient psychiatric care, to community mental health, and now outpatient mental health services. I've been fortunate to have the opportunity to help people from all backgrounds manage and resolve struggles around depression, anxiety, and trauma. During my time in practice, I have treated clients suffering from issues such as post-traumatic stress disorder, anxiety, ADD & ADHD, and depression.
Why I am a Good Fit for You
One of the biggest and hardest step that one can take in their mental health journey is that first phone call or email to reach out for professional health. My practice is based around a relational approach, building a strong partnership to process, strategize, and lift the weight of unresolved trauma and self doubt that many of us carry everyday. My practice is also based on a racial justice and equality framework and follow the NASW (National Organization of Social Workers) Code of Ethics. I believe that mental health care is a human right, which is why I am currently accepting OHP insurance as a way to ensure that there is no cost to you when seeking treatment.

name: Brittney Dane (SHE/HER/HERS)
approaches:
Our Background and Approach
I am a Masters Student Intern and QMHP under the supervision of Justin Little, LMFT and Judith Faustima, LMFT. I am currently completing my master’s degree in Clinical Mental Health Counseling from Oregon State University – Cascades. I am a Certified Level I Restoration Therapist. I also have training in a variety of other counseling theories and techniques, including crisis management. I have experience working with youth and adults living with severe anxiety symptoms and obsessive compulsive disorder. With almost a decade of wilderness guiding experience and a lifetime of finding joy and comfort in nature, I love to blend my personal passion with the empirical support behind nature-based counseling techniques. I believe that nature-based counseling should be accessible to everyone and am able to provide a virtual nature-based experience through video and phone mediums while you enjoy a private natural setting in your favorite park or even in your own backyard.
Why I Became a Therapist
I was drawn to the counseling field because I cherish the journey of self-discovery and the transformative potential within each of us. To me, being a counselor is to walk the path alongside another person, being a support in navigating life’s challenges. We live in a society that values individual strength and self-reliance, yet the profound impact of collaboration and community support in fostering resilience and growth is not often recognized. It is an honor to be brought into your community, your system, and to share in this work with you.

name: Christopher Bartel (HE/HIM/HIS)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
I strive to establish a supportive, respectful, and affirming environment in which clients can identify and explore thoughts, feelings, beliefs, and recurring patterns of behavior that may be interfering with their capacity to experience a fuller and more satisfying life. I place a high emphasis on the importance of the therapeutic relationship, and I offer a collaborative, relational, and culturally sensitive approach in which both therapist and client are actively working together to deepen self-understanding and promote positive change.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I am a psychologist licensed by the Oregon Board of Psychologist Examiners. I earned a Master's and Doctor of Psychology Degrees in Clinical Psychology from The Chicago School of Professional Psychology (fully accredited by the American Psychological Association), and completed an APA-approved internship as well as a post-doctoral residency. I also completed additional postdoc training in psychodynamic psychotherapy at the Oregon Psychoanalytic Center and I am currently a candidate in psychoanalysis at the Oregon Psychoanalytic Institute. I am trained in psychoanalytic, humanistic/existential, and cognitive-behavioral techniques. I work with a wide array of issues surrounding anxiety, depression, interpersonal struggles, grief/loss, identity (including LGBTQ), and post-trauma concerns, including working with adult survivors of childhood abuse.

name: Khatya Albano (SHE/HER)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Your emotional journey is uniquely yours, and I honor the creative coping strategies that have brought you this far. I’m not here to erase what’s helped you survive; instead, I offer new tools to navigate the complexities of your experience. While talking can be powerful, sometimes words aren’t enough. In our sessions, we blend talk therapy with experiential and somatic techniques—whether that’s writing, art, movement, or guided visualizations—to help you tap into your body’s wisdom. Rooted in Somatic Experiencing, EMDR, Parts Work, and Attachment Theory, my approach is trauma-informed and holistic, drawing on my background in psychology, massage therapy, and Buddhist studies. Together, we’ll explore new pathways toward clarity, balance, and authenticity.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
Ever since I was young, I’ve been driven to help others heal, inspired by the impacts of intergenerational trauma. I studied psychology in high school and college, later training in massage therapy to explore the mind-body connection. My journey led me to the University of the West—a Buddhist graduate school—where I deepened my understanding of mindfulness, spirituality, and faith. As a certified Somatic Experiencing Practitioner, I integrate Somatic Experiencing and Parts Work to help you forge healthier connections with yourself and others. I proudly support LGBTQ+ communities and stand for inclusivity, social justice, and authentic self-expression. I’m dedicated to empowering young adults, especially women, to overcome self-doubt and emotional overwhelm and embrace a life of balance, clarity, and genuine connection.

name: Patrick Bluett (HE/HIM)
approaches:
My Approach to Helping
Specializing in relationship and sexual issues, I work with both couples and individuals to treat anxiety and other factors impacting their ability to connect securely and deeply with themselves and others. My approach to therapy is relational, inclusive and trauma-informed. I serve as an ally to my clients and provide them with a space to explore their deepest truths without fear of judgment. I work with my clients to cultivate deep insights, self-compassion and to accept and embrace their humanity. Our therapeutic journey will be one of courage, constructive discomfort, and ultimately, clarity. My role as a therapist is to accompany you on this journey, to guide, encourage and, most importantly, protect you as you grow in your awareness and understanding. As your therapist, I place great emphasis on our connection and rapport; I cherish laughter as well as tears; and I will ask of you only what is safe to explore.
My Personal Beliefs and Interests
I have lived in Oregon almost my whole life and am deeply rooted in the Pacific Northwest. In my free time I am training for half marathons, planning my next backpacking trip and hosting board game nights. I hold a BA from the University of Oregon and an MS in Mental Health Counseling from Capella University and am a Licensed Professional Counselor under the Oregon Board of Licensed Professional Counselors and Therapists. I am also a member of the American Association of Sexuality Educators, Counselors and Therapists (AASECT) completing my requirements to become a Certified Sex Therapist. My practice is an inclusive and safe space for all - no exceptions. My practice is diverse, sex positive, LGBTQ-allied, kink-friendly and poly / ENM competent. I offer a free 20 minute phone consultation to ensure we are a good fit. If not, I will happily point you towards other therapists and resources to help you on your journey.
